---
okf_version: "0.1"
---

# Knowledge Bundle

## Concepts

* [@distributed Decorator](/concepts/distributed-decorator.md) - A decorator from the serverless_gpu library that annotates functions to run across multiple GPUs, accepting parameters for GPU count and type.
* [@distributed Decorator Data Loading](/concepts/distributed-decorator-data-loading.md) - Best practice to load data inside the @distributed decorator to avoid pickle serialization issues when datasets are too large.
* [@distributed decorator for distributed training](/concepts/distributed-decorator-for-distributed-training.md) - A Python decorator from the serverless_gpu library that handles data parallelism across multiple GPUs, automatically managing rank, local rank, and world size environment variables.
* [@distributed Decorator Pattern](/concepts/distributed-decorator-pattern.md) - A Python decorator from Databricks serverless_gpu library that provisions GPU resources and handles distributed training setup automatically
* [@mlflow.trace decorator](/concepts/mlflowtrace-decorator.md) - A Python decorator that wraps any function to automatically capture its inputs, outputs, and execution metadata as a span in an MLflow trace.
* [@mlflow.trace Function Decorator](/concepts/mlflowtrace-function-decorator.md) - A Python decorator that creates a span for any function, automatically logging function name, inputs, outputs, and execution time with minimal code changes.
* [@scorer decorator](/concepts/scorer-decorator.md) - The primary way to define custom code-based scorers for MLflow GenAI evaluation, using a decorator-based function signature with keyword-only arguments.
* [%run command in Databricks](/concepts/run-command-in-databricks.md) - A magic command that imports and executes another notebook inline, making its functions and variables available in the calling notebook.
* [$CODE_SOURCE_PATH](/concepts/code_source_path.md) - A Databricks-resolved environment variable that points to the uploaded code location on the remote compute node, used to reference training scripts without hardcoding paths.
* [20B to 120B+ Parameter Model Training](/concepts/20b-to-120b-parameter-model-training.md) - The parameter range where FSDP is particularly useful, enabling training of models between 20 billion and 120+ billion parameters
* [30-Day Lookback Window](/concepts/30-day-lookback-window.md) - A profiling constraint where for time series or inference profiles, the analysis looks back 30 days from profile creation, potentially causing partial windows in the first analysis.
* [30-Day Lookback Window Limitation](/concepts/30-day-lookback-window.md) - For time series or inference profiles, the profile looks back 30 days from when it is created, which may cause the first analysis to include a partial window (e.g., cutting mid-week or mid-month).
* [403 PERMISSION_DENIED Serverless Budget Policy Error](/concepts/403-permission_denied-serverless-budget-policy-error.md) - An error that occurs when a workspace disables the default serverless budget policy and no fallback policy is available for MLflow serverless workloads.
* [8xH100 Single-Node Configuration](/concepts/8xh100-single-node-configuration.md) - A Databricks Serverless GPU configuration providing 8 H100 GPUs on a single node for distributed workloads
* [A/B Comparison of Agent Configurations](/concepts/ab-comparison-of-agent-configurations.md) - Using custom judges to compare different agent configurations (e.g., toggling a behavior flag) by running `mlflow.genai.evaluate()` separately on each variant and comparing judge ratings across the same evaluation dataset.
* [A100 GPU Support on Databricks](/concepts/a100-gpu-support-on-databricks.md) - Databricks supports A100 GPUs across all clouds for deep learning tasks like LLM training, NLP, object detection, and recommendation engines, though availability may be limited
* [ABAC (Attribute-Based Access Control)](/concepts/abac-attribute-based-access-control.md) - An access-control model in Unity Catalog that uses governed tags and policies to grant permissions based on object attributes rather than per-object grants.
* [ABAC Built-in Tag Functions](/concepts/abac-built-in-tag-functions.md) - The has_tag() and has_tag_value() functions used in policy conditions to match tables and columns based on governed tags, supporting logical operators and snake_case naming.
* [ABAC Column Mask Policies](/concepts/abac-column-mask-policy.md) - A Unity Catalog policy type that masks column values in query results using a UDF, applicable to columns matched by governed tags or custom expressions.
* [ABAC Column Mask Policy](/concepts/abac-column-mask-policy.md) - A Unity Catalog ABAC policy that masks column values by applying a masking function to columns matched by governed tags or custom expressions.
* [ABAC Column-Level Mask (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/abac-column-level-mask-unity-catalog.md) - Attribute-based access control policy that can be automatically applied to columns tagged by custom classifiers, enabling dynamic data masking based on user attributes.
* [ABAC Compute Requirements](/concepts/abac-compute-requirements.md) - Compute types and runtime versions required to use attribute-based access control row filter and column mask policies in Unity Catalog
* [ABAC Conditions and Built-in Functions](/concepts/abac-conditions-and-built-in-functions.md) - Policy conditions use has_tag() and has_tag_value() built-in functions in WHEN (table) and MATCH COLUMNS (column) clauses to match objects based on governed tags, with support for logical operators and aliases.
* [ABAC Dynamic Policy Evaluation and Transparency](/concepts/abac-dynamic-policy-evaluation-and-transparency.md) - The query-time nature of ABAC policy evaluation and practices to improve visibility for data consumers and table owners
* [ABAC Governance based on Data Classification](/concepts/abac-governance-from-classification.md) - Using attribute-based access control (ABAC) policies in Unity Catalog to mask sensitive data based on classification tags, with the ability to create policies directly from the Data Classification UI.
* [ABAC Governance Controls from Classification](/concepts/abac-governance-from-classification.md) - Using Attribute-based Access Control (ABAC) policies in Unity Catalog to mask sensitive columns based on detected classification tags, optionally combining multiple tags in a single policy.
* [ABAC Governance from Classification](/concepts/abac-governance-from-classification.md) - Using attribute-based access control policies in Unity Catalog to mask or restrict sensitive columns based on data classification tags detected by the classification engine.
* [ABAC GRANT Policies](/concepts/abac-grant-policy.md) - Attribute-based access control policies that dynamically grant Unity Catalog privileges to securable objects whose governed tags match a condition, rather than granting on explicitly named objects.
* [ABAC GRANT Policies and Direct Grants Auditing](/concepts/abac-grant-policies-and-direct-grants-auditing.md) - Auditing both direct grants and ABAC GRANT policies together to uncover unintended permissions
* [ABAC GRANT Policy](/concepts/abac-grant-policy.md) - An attribute-based access control policy that dynamically grants Unity Catalog privileges to securable objects whose governed tags match a condition, evaluated at access time.
* [ABAC GRANT Policy and Direct Grant Auditing](/concepts/abac-grant-policy-and-direct-grant-auditing.md) - Effective privileges on a data object are the union of both direct grants and ABAC GRANT policies, requiring auditors to check both surfaces to avoid hidden unintended permissions.
* [ABAC on Materialized Views and Streaming Tables](/concepts/abac-on-materialized-views-and-streaming-tables.md) - Limitations and requirements for applying ABAC policies to materialized views and streaming tables, including pipeline owner exemption
* [ABAC Policies and AI Search Indexes](/concepts/abac-policies-and-ai-search-indexes.md) - AI Search indexes created from ABAC-secured tables do not enforce row filter or column mask policies when serving queries
* [ABAC Policies from Data Classification](/concepts/abac-policies-from-data-classification.md) - The practice of creating attribute-based access control policies that mask or restrict sensitive columns based on the classification tags detected by the Data Classification engine.
* [ABAC Policies in Unity Catalog](/concepts/abac-policies-in-unity-catalog.md) - Policies attached at a level in the Unity Catalog hierarchy (catalog, schema, table) that are evaluated dynamically — when a securable object has targeted attributes, the policy takes effect automatically.
* [ABAC Policies on SAP Governance Tags](/concepts/abac-policies-on-sap-governance-tags.md) - Using attribute-based access control (ABAC) policies in Unity Catalog that reference SAP system-governed tags to control access to sensitive SAP BDC data.
* [ABAC Policies on Shared Data](/concepts/abac-policies-on-shared-data.md) - Attribute-based access control (ABAC) policies such as row filters and column masks can be applied to shared tables, schemas, and catalogs but not to shared streaming tables or materialized views.
* [ABAC policies vs table-level row filters and column masks](/concepts/abac-policies-vs-table-level-row-filters-and-column-masks.md) - Comparison of attribute-based access control (schema/catalog-level, tag-driven) versus per-table manual row filters and column masks in Unity Catalog.
* [ABAC Policy Audit Logging](/concepts/abac-policy-audit-logging.md) - Databricks logs all governed tag and ABAC policy operations (create, delete, list, get) in the system.access.audit table for compliance and monitoring.
* [ABAC Policy Conditions and Built-in Functions](/concepts/abac-policy-conditions-and-built-in-functions.md) - Tag-based expressions using has_tag() and has_tag_value() functions to match tables (WHEN clause) and columns (MATCH COLUMNS clause) within a policy's scope, supporting up to 3 column expressions.
* [ABAC Policy CRUD Operations](/concepts/abac-policy-crud-operations.md) - Operations to create, edit, view (SHOW/DESCRIBE), and delete ABAC row filter and column mask policies in Unity Catalog using Catalog Explorer UI, SQL, Python SDK, REST APIs, SDKs, or Terraform.
* [ABAC Policy Dependency Lifecycle Management](/concepts/abac-policy-dependency-lifecycle-management.md) - Handling of removed policy dependencies (governed tags, UDFs, tagged columns) including error codes, fail-closed behavior, and resolution steps required when dependencies are deleted while policies still reference them.
* [ABAC Policy Evaluation and Enforcement](/concepts/abac-policy-evaluation-and-enforcement.md) - Two-stage process where Unity Catalog evaluates which policies apply based on user identity and tags, then Databricks Runtime enforces row filters and column masks during query execution.
* [ABAC Policy Integration with Classification](/concepts/abac-policy-integration-with-classification.md) - Integration between Data Classification and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) allowing users to create masking policies directly from classification results, protecting sensitive data by masking columns based on detected classification tags.
* [ABAC Policy Quotas and Service Limits](/concepts/abac-policy-quotas-and-service-limits.md) - Quotas governing the number and scope of ABAC row filter and column mask policies in Unity Catalog
* [ABAC Policy Requirements](/concepts/abac-policy-requirements.md) - Prerequisites for creating and managing ABAC policies including MANAGE permission on the securable object, EXECUTE on the UDF, Databricks Runtime 16.4+ or serverless compute, and governed tags on target objects.
* [ABAC Policy Requirements and Permissions](/concepts/abac-policy-requirements.md) - Prerequisite permissions (MANAGE, object ownership, EXECUTE on UDFs) and compute requirements (Databricks Runtime 16.4+ or serverless) for creating and managing ABAC policies.
* [ABAC Policy Requirements and Prerequisites](/concepts/abac-policy-requirements.md) - Requirements for creating ABAC policies including MANAGE privilege or ownership on the securable object, Databricks Runtime 16.4+ or serverless compute, EXECUTE on a UDF, and governed tags applied to target objects.
* [ABAC Policy Scoping (Catalog vs Schema vs Table)](/concepts/policy-scoping-at-catalog-or-schema-level.md) - Attaching ABAC policies at the highest applicable scope (catalog or schema) rather than table level to maximize reuse and simplify management.
* [ABAC Policy Scoping and Sprawl Prevention](/concepts/abac-policy-scoping-and-sprawl-prevention.md) - Attaching policies at catalog or schema level rather than table level, starting with a small number of broad policies, and periodically consolidating overlapping ones to avoid complex authorization checks.
* [ABAC Policy Scoping at Catalog and Schema Level](/concepts/policy-scoping-at-catalog-or-schema-level.md) - Attaching ABAC policies at the highest applicable scope (catalog or schema) rather than table level for simpler management.
* [ABAC Policy Scoping Hierarchy](/concepts/abac-policy-scoping-hierarchy.md) - Principle of attaching ABAC policies at the highest applicable scope (catalog or schema level) rather than table level to maximize reusability
* [ABAC Policy Sprawl Prevention](/concepts/abac-policy-sprawl-prevention.md) - Avoiding excessive tags and policies by starting with a small set of broad policies, consolidating overlapping ones, and periodic review.
* [ABAC Policy Structure](/concepts/abac-policy-structure.md) - Every ABAC policy specifies a scope (catalog/schema/table), principals (TO/EXCEPT clauses), actions (row filter, column mask, or grant), and tag-based conditions (WHEN and MATCH COLUMNS clauses).
* [ABAC Policy Types](/concepts/abac-policy-types.md) - Three policy types in Unity Catalog ABAC: row filter policies, column mask policies, and GRANT policies (Beta), each enforcing different access controls based on tag conditions.
* [ABAC policy UDF complexity and performance](/concepts/abac-policy-udf-complexity-and-performance.md) - How the complexity of UDFs in row filter and column mask policies directly affects query execution time, with guidance on keeping UDFs simple, avoiding external calls, and favoring built-in SQL functions.
* [ABAC Row Filter Policies](/concepts/abac-row-filter-policy.md) - A Unity Catalog policy type that filters rows from query results based on a user-defined function (UDF), where rows returning FALSE are excluded.
* [ABAC Row Filter Policy](/concepts/abac-row-filter-policy.md) - A Unity Catalog ABAC policy that filters rows from query results using a boolean user-defined function (UDF), excluding rows where the function returns FALSE.
* [ABAC Separation of Duties](/concepts/abac-separation-of-duties.md) - The role-based permission model for ABAC that distributes responsibilities across specialized teams (tag taxonomy creators, data stewards, governance admins, data creators, data consumers) with distinct permission requirements per role.
* [ABAC Tag Governance and Standardization](/concepts/abac-tag-governance-and-standardization.md) - Establishing a consistent, controlled tagging taxonomy for ABAC policies with restricted tag permissions
* [ABAC Tagging as a Security Boundary](/concepts/abac-tagging-as-a-security-boundary.md) - The principle that tag mutation permissions are a security boundary since tags determine which ABAC policies apply to a data asset
* [ABAC Tagging Taxonomy and Governance](/concepts/abac-tagging-taxonomy-and-governance.md) - Establishing consistent, controlled tag taxonomies with standardized key names and allowed values, and restricting tag modification to authorized stewards.
* [ABAC User-Defined Functions (UDFs)](/concepts/abac-user-defined-functions-udfs.md) - Row filter and column mask policies rely on UDFs (SQL or Python) registered in Unity Catalog to implement filtering or masking logic, with SQL UDFs recommended for better performance.
* [ABAC View Sharing Ownership Change (April 2026)](/concepts/abac-view-sharing-ownership-change-april-2026.md) - A policy change effective April 23, 2026, shifting the exemption requirement from the view owner to the share owner for sharing views protected by ABAC policies, with a grace period until July 22, 2026.
* [Acceptable Use Models Policy](/concepts/acceptable-use-models-policy.md) - Databricks policy governing which AI/ML models are allowed to be served on the Databricks Model Serving platform
* [Acceptable Use Policy for ML Models](/concepts/acceptable-use-policy-for-ml-models.md) - Policies and terms governing which types of machine learning models are permissible for deployment on Databricks Model Serving.
* [Access Control Differences Between Unity Catalog and Hive Metastore](/concepts/access-control-differences-between-unity-catalog-and-hive-metastore.md) - Unity Catalog and the legacy Hive metastore have differing access control models, including metastore scope (account-level vs workspace-level), permission requirements (USE CATALOG/USE SCHEMA), view ownership rules, and support for DENY, ANY FILE, ANONYMOUS FUNCTION, and READ_METADATA.
* [Access Control for Data Quality Monitoring Results](/concepts/access-control-for-data-quality-monitoring-results.md) - Only account admins can access the system.data_quality_monitoring.table_results table and must explicitly grant access to other users.
* [Access Control for MLflow System Tables](/concepts/access-control-for-mlflow-system-tables.md) - By default only account admins can access system tables; they can grant USE and SELECT permissions to other users, who then see metadata across all experiments in the account.
* [Access Control Separation for PII-Containing Tables](/concepts/access-control-separation-for-pii-containing-tables.md) - A security pattern in Unity Catalog where raw tables containing unredacted PII have tightly restricted access (only pipeline service principals and administrators), while redacted tables are broadly available for analytics, dashboards, and observability.
* [Access Expiration via Mapping Tables](/concepts/access-expiration-via-mapping-tables.md) - Using an expires_on date column in a mapping table to automatically revoke access without manual intervention, useful for contractors and time-limited data sharing.
* [Access Request Approval Methods](/concepts/access-request-approval-methods.md) - Two methods for approving requests: adding the requester to an existing group with privileges, or directly granting privileges to the principal.
* [Access Request Destination Inheritance](/concepts/access-request-destination-inheritance.md) - Destinations configured at higher levels of the Unity Catalog hierarchy automatically apply to child objects that lack explicit destinations.
* [Access Request Destinations](/concepts/access-request-destinations.md) - Configured endpoints (email, Slack, Teams, webhook, redirect URL) that receive privilege requests for Unity Catalog securable objects.
* [Access Requests in Unity Catalog](/concepts/access-requests-in-unity-catalog.md) - Configurable destinations (email, Slack, Teams, webhooks) for access requests on Unity Catalog securable objects.
* [ACCESS_DENIED (Delta Uniform error)](/concepts/access_denied-delta-uniform-error.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_UNIFORM_COMPATIBILITY_MISSING_OR_INVALID_LOCATION indicating the location cannot be accessed.
* [ACCESS_DENIED sub-error (Delta Uniform Compatibility)](/concepts/access_denied-sub-error-delta-uniform-compatibility.md) - A specific reason code under the DELTA_UNIFORM_COMPATIBILITY_MISSING_OR_INVALID_LOCATION error class indicating the configured location cannot be accessed due to permissions
* [Accessing Data Profiling Dashboards in Databricks](/concepts/accessing-data-profiling-dashboards-in-databricks.md) - Two navigation paths to locate the auto-generated data profiling dashboard: via Catalog Explorer (Quality tab on a table) or via the Dashboards sidebar listing.
* [Accessing Databricks secrets in scorers](/concepts/accessing-secrets-in-scorers.md) - Custom scorers can securely use Databricks secrets by importing dbutils from databricks.sdk.runtime inside the scorer function.
* [Accessing Databricks Utilities via Databricks Connect](/concepts/accessing-databricks-utilities-via-databricks-connect.md) - How to use dbutils.fs and dbutils.secrets utilities through Databricks Connect, including file copy operations between local and remote filesystems and accessing secrets.
* [Accessing secrets in scorers](/concepts/accessing-secrets-in-scorers.md) - How to securely use Databricks secrets within custom scorers by importing dbutils from databricks.sdk.runtime.
* [Accessing the Data Profiling Dashboard](/concepts/accessing-the-data-profiling-dashboard.md) - Two methods to view the dashboard: via Catalog Explorer (Quality tab on the primary table) or via the Dashboards sidebar.
* [Account Admin (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/account-admin-unity-catalog.md) - A highly privileged role operating at the Databricks account level, responsible for creating and linking metastores and workspaces, and assigning admin roles.
* [Account Admin Role (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/account-admin-unity-catalog.md) - A highly privileged role operating at the Databricks account level, responsible for creating and linking metastores and workspaces, and assigning admin roles.
* [Account Admin Role in Unity Catalog](/concepts/account-admin-unity-catalog.md) - A highly privileged role operating at the Databricks account level, responsible for creating and linking metastores and workspaces, and assigning admin roles.
* [Account admins (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/account-admin-unity-catalog.md) - Highly privileged role operating at the Databricks account level, responsible for creating and linking metastores and workspaces, and assigning admin roles.
* [Account Admins in Unity Catalog](/concepts/account-admin-unity-catalog.md) - Account-level admin role in Databricks that creates and links metastores and workspaces, assigns admin roles, and operates across the entire Databricks account.
* [Account-Level Identity Federation for Unity Catalog](/concepts/account-level-identity-management-for-unity-catalog.md) - Enabling Unity Catalog shifts identity management from workspace-level to account-level interfaces, requiring SCIM provisioning connectors and automation to target account endpoints.
* [Account-Level Identity Management for Unity Catalog](/concepts/account-level-identity-management-for-unity-catalog.md) - The shift from workspace-level to account-level management of users, groups, and service principals when Unity Catalog is enabled, requiring updates to SCIM and automation.
* [Account-Level Identity Provisioning for Unity Catalog](/concepts/account-level-identity-provisioning-for-unity-catalog.md) - The requirement to provision users, groups, and service principals at the Databricks account level rather than workspace level for Unity Catalog compatibility.
* [Account-Level Legacy Feature Settings](/concepts/account-level-legacy-feature-settings.md) - An account-level setting to disable legacy features, including the Hive metastore, for new workspaces before they are created
* [ACID Transactions in Data Lakes](/concepts/acid-transactions-in-data-lakes.md) - The capability of Delta Lake to provide ACID (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) transaction guarantees on data stored in data lakes.
* [ACID Transactions on Data Lakes](/concepts/acid-transactions-in-data-lakes.md) - Delta Lake provides ACID (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) transaction guarantees on top of existing data lakes, bringing database-like reliability to data lake storage.
* [ACL_ENABLED Error on Delta Writes](/concepts/acl_enabled-error-on-delta-writes.md) - A DELTA_METADATA_MISMATCH sub-error that occurs when Table ACLs are enabled on a cluster, preventing automatic schema migration and requiring ALTER TABLE commands instead.
* [ACL_ENABLED sub-error](/concepts/acl_enabled-sub-error.md) - A DELTA_METADATA_MISMATCH sub-error that occurs when table ACLs are enabled, preventing automatic schema migration
* [Activation Checkpointing](/concepts/activation-checkpointing.md) - A memory optimization technique that trades compute for memory by not storing intermediate activations during forward pass and recomputing them during backward pass.
* [Activation Link (Delta Sharing)](/concepts/activation-link-delta-sharing.md) - A secure URL generated when creating an Open Sharing recipient, used by non-Databricks recipients to download their credential file once for authentication.
* [Activation Link and Credential File Distribution](/concepts/activation-link-and-credential-file-distribution.md) - The mechanism by which a Delta Sharing data provider shares connection information with a recipient via a secure activation link that allows one-time download of a credential file containing the bearer token.
* [Activation Link for Delta Sharing Recipients](/concepts/activation-link-delta-sharing.md) - A one-time-use URL generated by Databricks when creating or rotating a recipient token, allowing the recipient to download their credential file and access shared data.
* [Active experiment configuration in MLflow](/concepts/active-experiment-in-mlflow.md) - Methods to control where MLflow runs are logged by setting the active experiment using set_experiment(), start_run(), or environment variables.
* [Active Experiment in MLflow](/concepts/active-experiment-in-mlflow.md) - Mechanism to control where MLflow runs are logged by setting an active experiment via set_experiment(), start_run(), or environment variables.
* [Active Maintenance Definition for Libraries](/concepts/active-maintenance-definition-for-libraries.md) - Criteria Databricks uses to determine if a library is actively maintained: commits within 3 months and releases within 9 months, no archived repository, and no announced stop of maintenance.
* [Active MLflow run management](/concepts/active-mlflow-run-management.md) - The practice of wrapping fit() calls inside a 'with mlflow.start_run():' statement to ensure tuning information is logged under its own MLflow main run for better organization.
* [Active MLflow Run Management in Tuning](/concepts/active-mlflow-run-management-in-tuning.md) - Best practice of wrapping CrossValidator or TrainValidationSplit fit() calls inside a 'with mlflow.start_run():' context to ensure clean parent runs and avoid UUID name conflicts
* [Active MLflow Run Management with fit()](/concepts/active-mlflow-run-management-with-fit.md) - The recommended practice of wrapping CrossValidator/TrainValidationSplit fit() calls inside a 'with mlflow.start_run():' statement to ensure proper logging under a dedicated MLflow main run and enable additional custom logging.
* [Active MLflow run management with Hyperopt](/concepts/active-mlflow-run-management-with-hyperopt.md) - Databricks recommends wrapping fmin() calls in an active MLflow run to ensure each call is logged to a separate main run and avoid name conflicts.
* [Adapting Single-Node PyTorch to Distributed Training](/concepts/adapting-single-node-pytorch-to-distributed-training.md) - The process of modifying PyTorch code originally written for single-node training to run in a distributed manner across multiple nodes
* [Add Traces to Labeling Session](/concepts/add-traces-to-labeling-session.md) - The workflow for adding MLflow Traces (via UI selection, search results, or individual trace objects) into a labeling session for expert review.
* [addCompiledArtifacts API](/concepts/addcompiledartifacts-api.md) - API in Databricks Connect for Scala that uploads compiled classes and JARs from local development to a remote Databricks cluster so that UDFs and typed Dataset operations can execute on the server side.
* [Adding Traces to Labeling Sessions](/concepts/adding-traces-to-labeling-sessions.md) - The process of selecting and associating existing MLflow Traces (via UI or API) with a labeling session for expert review, supporting bulk selection from search results and individual trace objects.
* [Admin Consent for OIDC Delta Sharing Apps](/concepts/admin-consent-for-oidc-delta-sharing-apps.md) - One-time admin approval required in Entra ID tenant to authorize the Databricks multi-tenant app for OIDC federation
* [Admin-Side Data Discovery](/concepts/admin-side-data-discovery.md) - Administrator tools and configurations in Unity Catalog that shape data discoverability through metadata, trust signals, and organization, directly determining the quality of the user discovery experience.
* [Admin-side Metadata Governance](/concepts/admin-side-metadata-governance.md) - Administrators shape data discoverability by applying metadata, trust signals, and organizational structures to catalog objects.
* [Administrator's Role in Data Discoverability](/concepts/administrators-role-in-data-discoverability.md) - Administrators shape discoverability by applying metadata, trust signals, and organization to make it easier for users to locate and assess data quality.
* [Advanced AutoML Configuration Options](/concepts/advanced-automl-configuration-options.md) - Advanced parameters for AutoML experiments including evaluation metrics, training framework exclusion, stopping conditions (time-based or early stopping), and time-based data splitting.
* [Advanced Workflow Tracing](/concepts/advanced-workflow-tracing.md) - Manual tracing support for complex production scenarios including multi-threaded operations, async workflows, streaming responses with custom aggregation, and complex nested operations.
* [AG2 Multi-Agent Termination with Message Content](/concepts/ag2-multi-agent-termination-with-message-content.md) - A pattern where a conversational agent workflow terminates when a specific string (e.g., 'TERMINATE') appears in a message's content, controlled via the is_termination_msg callback.
* [Agent Bricks Knowledge Assistant Incompatibility](/concepts/agent-bricks-knowledge-assistant-incompatibility.md) - A known limitation where Knowledge Assistant does not support foundation model Unity Catalog permissions, requiring users to contact their account team before enabling the feature.
* [Agent Evaluation Dataset Design](/concepts/agent-evaluation-dataset-design.md) - Evaluation datasets for GenAI agents consist of input message histories and optional `expectations` dictionaries that define expected agent behaviors, enabling both input/output evaluation and correctness checking.
* [Agent Function Interface](/concepts/agent-function-interface.md) - The predict_fn contract for conversational agents being evaluated, accepting conversation history as a list of message dicts and returning a string response.
* [Agent Function Interface for MLflow Evaluation](/concepts/agent-function-interface.md) - The predict_fn contract that wraps a conversational agent for use with MLflow evaluation, accepting conversation history and returning responses.
* [Agent Function Interface for Simulated Conversations](/concepts/agent-function-interface-for-simulated-conversations.md) - The predict_fn interface that receives conversation history and context (including mlflow_session_id and custom context fields) and returns assistant responses.
* [Agent Function Interface for Simulation](/concepts/agent-function-interface.md) - The predict_fn interface that conversational AI agents must implement to participate in simulation, accepting conversation history as a list of message dicts (supports 'input' or 'messages' parameter names) and returning a string response.
* [Agent Labeling and Review](/concepts/agent-labeling-and-review.md) - Workflows for viewing labeling sessions, assigning reviewers to traces, and inspecting labeling schemas for feedback criteria such as ratings and comments
* [Agent Mode](/concepts/agent-mode.md) - A toggle in the Genie Code pane that enables interactive, prompt-driven troubleshooting and analysis of model serving endpoints.
* [Agent Tool Registration Pattern](/concepts/agent-tool-registration-pattern.md) - A design pattern in AG2 where a function is registered twice — once for the LLM to call (register_for_llm) and once for execution (register_for_execution) — decoupling tool discovery from secure execution.
* [Agent-Based System Observability](/concepts/agent-based-system-observability.md) - Observability techniques tailored for complex agent-based AI systems, including tracking intermediate steps and decision chains.
* [Aggregate Metrics](/concepts/aggregate-metrics.md) - Summary statistics computed across all evaluated examples in an evaluation run, such as mean correctness or safety pass rate.
* [Aggregate Metrics (MLflow Evaluation)](/concepts/aggregate-metrics.md) - Summary statistics computed across all evaluated examples in an evaluation run, such as mean correctness or pass rates.
* [Aggregate Metrics in MLflow Evaluation](/concepts/aggregate-metrics.md) - Summary statistics computed across all evaluated examples in an evaluation run, such as mean correctness, mean relevance, and safety pass rate.
* [Aggregation Functions and Column Selection](/concepts/aggregation-functions-and-columnselection.md) - Features can be computed via aggregation functions (Sum, Avg, Count, ApproxCountDistinct, etc.) wrapped in AggregationFunction with a time window, or pass-through via ColumnSelection for direct value lookup.
* [Aggregation Functions and ColumnSelection](/concepts/aggregation-functions-and-columnselection.md) - Two function types for feature computation: AggregationFunction (wrapping operators like Sum, Avg, Count with time windows) for time-series aggregations, and ColumnSelection for pass-through features without aggregation.
* [AggregationFunction and ColumnSelection](/concepts/aggregationfunction-and-columnselection.md) - Two types of function wrappers for feature computation: AggregationFunction for window-based aggregation (Sum, Avg, Count, ApproxCountDistinct) and ColumnSelection for pass-through features
* [AI Assets as Securable Objects](/concepts/ai-assets-as-securable-objects.md) - AI resources (models, functions, connections, hosted foundation models) managed as securable objects in Unity Catalog with standard privileges for grant and revoke operations.
* [AI Code Generation Human Review Requirement](/concepts/ai-code-generation-human-review-requirement.md) - A safety requirement that all AI-generated code must undergo human review before production use, with additional restrictions for external users.
* [AI Functions](/concepts/ai-functions.md) - SQL-based functions tightly integrated with Model Serving for batch inference, allowing users to call models directly from SQL analytics workflows without configuring endpoints for pre-provisioned models.
* [AI Functions (Databricks)](/concepts/ai-functions-databricks.md) - Built-in SQL functions on Databricks that enable applying AI models directly to data using task-specific or general-purpose functions like ai_query
* [AI Functions and Batch Inference](/concepts/ai-functions.md) - Databricks SQL functions (including ai-query) tightly integrated with Model Serving to perform batch inference using pre-provisioned or custom models directly within analytics workflows.
* [AI Functions for Batch Inference](/concepts/ai-functions-for-batch-inference.md) - Batch inference mode using AI Functions within Databricks, recommended for running inference on large datasets using generative AI or ML models.
* [AI Functions for PII Redaction](/concepts/ai-functions-for-pii-redaction.md) - Built-in Databricks SQL functions (ai_mask) that use foundation models to detect and mask personally identifiable information in free-text columns.
* [AI Functions on Databricks](/concepts/ai-functions.md) - SQL-callable functions tightly integrated with Model Serving for batch inference, allowing users to enrich data using pre-provisioned or custom models directly from analytics workflows.
* [AI Gateway](/concepts/ai-gateway.md) - A feature for centrally managing permissions, usage limits, and monitoring quality across all model endpoints including externally hosted ones
* [AI Gateway (Databricks)](/concepts/ai-gateway-databricks.md) - Centralized governance layer for managing, monitoring, and setting usage limits on all model endpoints (including externally hosted ones) from the Serving UI.
* [AI Gateway Inference Tables](/concepts/ai-gateway-inference-tables.md) - Automatically logs online prediction requests and responses into Unity Catalog Delta tables for monitoring, debugging, training data generation, and compliance audits.
* [AI Gateway on Databricks](/concepts/ai-gateway-on-databricks.md) - Databricks service to govern and monitor access to deployed models with usage tracking, payload logging, and security controls.
* [AI Governance](/concepts/ai-governance.md) - Extending data governance frameworks to AI resources, applying access control, lineage, and audit models to AI assets and AI traffic.
* [AI Governance (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/ai-governance-unity-catalog.md) - Extension of Unity Catalog's data governance capabilities to AI assets, applying access control, lineage, and audit models to AI resources.
* [AI Governance on Databricks](/concepts/ai-governance-on-databricks.md) - Extends data governance capabilities of Unity Catalog to AI resources, applying access control, lineage, and audit models to AI assets and AI traffic.
* [AI Governance with Unity AI Gateway](/concepts/ai-governance-with-unity-ai-gateway.md) - Extension of Unity Catalog governance to AI, providing enterprise governance for LLM endpoints, agents, and MCP servers
* [AI Governance with Unity Catalog](/concepts/ai-governance-unity-catalog.md) - Extending Databricks' data governance capabilities (access control, lineage, audit) to AI assets such as models, functions, connections, and hosted foundation models using Unity Catalog.
* [AI Playground](/concepts/ai-playground.md) - A Databricks interface for interactively experimenting with supported foundation models before deploying them into production workloads.
* [AI Playground (Databricks)](/concepts/ai-playground-databricks.md) - Chat-like environment within the Databricks workspace for testing, prompting, and comparing supported large language models before deployment.
* [AI Playground on Databricks](/concepts/ai-playground-databricks.md) - A chat-like environment within Databricks workspaces for testing, prompting, and comparing large language models interactively before deploying them via Model Serving.
* [AI Runtime](/concepts/ai-runtime.md) - Databricks' on-demand serverless GPU compute platform for distributed training workloads.
* [AI Runtime (AI v5) on Databricks](/concepts/ai-runtime-ai-v5-on-databricks.md) - Databricks' pre-configured environment for AI workloads, bundling Unsloth, TRL, PEFT, bitsandbytes, xformers, and other LLM finetuning dependencies.
* [AI Runtime (air) CLI Distributed Workflows](/concepts/ai-runtime-air-cli-distributed-workflows.md) - Databricks' CLI tool and YAML-based workload configuration system for defining and submitting multi-node distributed training jobs, with built-in secret injection, MLflow integration, Unity Catalog volume access, and automatic rendezvous variable injection.
* [AI Runtime (air) CLI Workload YAML](/concepts/ai-runtime-air-cli-workload-yaml.md) - A YAML configuration format for the Databricks AI Runtime CLI that defines distributed training jobs with compute requirements, environment dependencies, code sources, and inline bootstrap commands.
* [AI Runtime (Databricks)](/concepts/ai-runtime.md) - Databricks' on-demand serverless GPU compute platform designed for machine learning training workloads
* [AI Runtime and Databricks Runtime for Machine Learning](/concepts/ai-runtime-and-databricks-runtime-for-machine-learning.md) - Customizable training environments with GPU support that enable training and fine-tuning across classic ML, deep learning, and GenAI paradigms on Databricks.
* [AI Runtime Base Environments](/concepts/ai-runtime-base-environments.md) - The two base environment options for AI Runtime: None (default environment without AI libraries) and the AI environment (databricks_ai_v4/v5) which includes pre-installed AI and ML libraries.
* [AI Runtime CLI](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli.md) - A Databricks command-line interface for submitting, monitoring, and managing machine learning training workloads on serverless GPU infrastructure.
* [AI Runtime CLI (air)](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli-air.md) - Command-line interface for submitting and managing machine learning workloads on Databricks, currently in Beta.
* [AI Runtime CLI (Databricks)](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli.md) - A command-line interface for submitting and managing machine learning training workloads on Databricks serverless GPU infrastructure, currently in Beta.
* [AI Runtime CLI authentication profiles](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli-authentication-profiles.md) - The air CLI uses Databricks authentication profiles via the -p/--profile flag, allowing users to select a named credential profile instead of the default.
* [AI Runtime CLI Authentication via Databricks CLI Profiles](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli-authentication-via-databricks-cli-profiles.md) - The air CLI reuses Databricks CLI authentication profiles, which can be passed via the -p flag or the DATABRICKS_CONFIG_PROFILE environment variable.
* [AI Runtime CLI Commands](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli-commands.md) - The set of CLI commands (air run, air logs, air list runs, air cancel) used to submit, monitor, and manage training jobs.
* [AI Runtime CLI global flags](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli-global-flags.md) - Cross-cutting flags available to all air CLI commands, including --version, --profile (-p), and --help (-h).
* [AI Runtime CLI Installation via uv](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli-installation-via-uv.md) - The recommended method to install the air CLI is using the uv Python package manager, which isolates the tool in its own environment.
* [AI Runtime CLI Log Management](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli-log-management.md) - Commands and patterns for streaming, filtering by node, downloading to local directory, and inspecting logs from AI Runtime training runs.
* [AI Runtime CLI YAML configuration help system](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli-yaml-configuration-help-system.md) - Built-in help system that provides per-field documentation for YAML configuration sections via 'air -h config' and 'air -h config.compute'.
* [AI Runtime CLI YAML workload definition](/concepts/ai-runtime-cli-yaml-workload-definition.md) - Workloads for the AI Runtime CLI are defined via YAML configuration files, with the CLI offering field-level help (-h config) and per-section help (-h config.compute) for authoring these files.
* [AI Runtime connection modes](/concepts/ai-runtime-connection-modes.md) - Three primary ways to connect to AI Runtime: interactive notebooks, scheduled jobs, and Jobs API/Databricks Asset Bundles.
* [AI Runtime Connectivity Methods](/concepts/ai-runtime-connectivity-methods.md) - Ways to connect to AI Runtime including interactive notebooks, scheduled jobs, Jobs API, and Databricks Asset Bundles.
* [AI Runtime Data Access and Integration](/concepts/ai-runtime-data-access-and-integration.md) - Data loading patterns and native integration points for AI Runtime including notebooks, jobs, Unity Catalog, MLflow experiment tracking, and Genie Code AI assistant for deep learning workloads.
* [AI Runtime Data Loading](/concepts/ai-runtime-data-loading.md) - The mechanism for reading data into AI Runtime workloads, requiring an understanding of how data access works on the serverless platform for a smooth experience.
* [AI Runtime Distributed Training](/concepts/ai-runtime-distributed-training.md) - A Beta API for multi-GPU training on AI Runtime using the @distributed decorator from the serverless_gpu Python package, supporting PyTorch DDP, FSDP, and DeepSpeed with minimal configuration on a single multi-GPU node.
* [AI Runtime Distributed Training API](/concepts/ai-runtime-distributed-training.md) - A Beta API using the @distributed decorator for multi-GPU workloads on single-node AI Runtime with PyTorch DDP, FSDP, or DeepSpeed.
* [AI Runtime environment configuration](/concepts/ai-runtime-environment-configuration.md) - Configuration of AI Runtime involves selecting a serverless GPU compute, an accelerator type, and a base environment (None/default or AI v4/v5).
* [AI Runtime Environments](/concepts/ai-runtime-environments.md) - Two managed Python environments for AI Runtime: a minimal default base environment for maximum dependency flexibility, and a full-featured Databricks AI environment pre-loaded with popular ML frameworks like PyTorch and Transformers.
* [AI Runtime example notebooks](/concepts/ai-runtime-example-notebooks.md) - Curated collection of notebook examples demonstrating AI Runtime usage for LLMs, computer vision, recommender systems, classic ML, and distributed training.
* [AI Runtime Experiment Tracking and Observability](/concepts/ai-runtime-experiment-tracking-and-observability.md) - Integration with MLflow for experiment tracking, log viewing, and model checkpoint management on AI Runtime
* [AI Runtime for Computer Vision](/concepts/ai-runtime-for-computer-vision.md) - Databricks AI Runtime provides a managed environment with pre-configured libraries and serverless GPUs for training and fine-tuning computer vision models on AWS.
* [AI Runtime for Recommender Systems](/concepts/ai-runtime-for-recommender-systems.md) - Databricks AI Runtime provides infrastructure for building deep learning based recommendation systems, supporting both single-node (Public Preview) and multi-GPU distributed training (Beta).
* [AI Runtime for single-node tasks](/concepts/ai-runtime-for-single-node-tasks.md) - Public Preview feature of Databricks AI Runtime for running single-node machine learning workloads on GPU instances.
* [AI Runtime Genie Code Support](/concepts/ai-runtime-genie-code-support.md) - Integration with Genie Code to assist with deep learning code generation, library debugging, optimization suggestions on AI Runtime.
* [AI Runtime Hardware Accelerators](/concepts/ai-runtime-hardware-options.md) - The GPU hardware options available for AI Runtime on AWS, including A10 and H100 accelerators (single-node, multi-GPU), with region-specific availability and capacity considerations.
* [AI Runtime Hardware Options](/concepts/ai-runtime-hardware-options.md) - The supported GPU accelerators for AI Runtime, currently limited to A10 and H100 types, each provisioned on a single node with a specific number of GPUs depending on the accelerator type.
* [AI Runtime idle auto-termination](/concepts/ai-runtime-idle-auto-termination.md) - AI Runtime compute auto-terminates after 60 minutes of inactivity to preserve resources.
* [AI Runtime Interactive Notebook Connection](/concepts/ai-runtime-interactive-notebook-connection.md) - How to connect a Databricks notebook to an AI Runtime environment using serverless GPU compute via the Environment side panel.
* [AI Runtime Jobs API and Asset Bundles](/concepts/ai-runtime-jobs-api-and-asset-bundles.md) - Programmatic creation and management of AI Runtime jobs using the Databricks Jobs API or Databricks Asset Bundles with YAML configuration for serverless GPU compute.
* [AI Runtime Limitations and Constraints](/concepts/ai-runtime-limitations-and-constraints.md) - Key operational constraints for AI Runtime including no support for compliance security profiles (HIPAA/PCI), maximum 7-day runtime, limited accelerator types (A10/H100 only), potential capacity constraints, and cross-region egress costs.
* [AI Runtime Local Caching for Volumes](/concepts/ai-runtime-local-caching-for-volumes.md) - Mechanism where files from Unity Catalog volumes are copied to local storage on first access, avoiding repeated FUSE reads across epochs.
* [AI Runtime Logging](/concepts/ai-runtime-logging.md) - AI Runtime provides two logging channels: notebook cell output for stdout/stderr and the MLflow experiment UI for training metrics, parameters, and artifacts.
* [AI Runtime Managed Environments](/concepts/ai-runtime-environments.md) - Two Python environments offered by AI Runtime: a minimal default base environment and a full-featured Databricks AI environment pre-loaded with ML frameworks
* [AI Runtime MLflow Integration](/concepts/ai-runtime-mlflow-integration.md) - Native integration of MLflow with Databricks AI Runtime for experiment tracking, model logging, and metric visualization, including autologging, custom run naming, and experiment configuration.
* [AI Runtime Model Checkpointing](/concepts/ai-runtime-model-checkpointing.md) - Manual checkpointing mechanism using UCVolumeWriter and UCVolumeReader from the serverless_gpu.data module to enable resumption of workloads that may exceed the 7-day maximum runtime.
* [AI Runtime model checkpointing with Unity Catalog](/concepts/model-checkpointing-with-unity-catalog.md) - For long-running workloads exceeding 7 days, implement manual checkpointing using UCVolumeWriter and UCVolumeReader from serverless_gpu.data with Unity Catalog volumes.
* [AI Runtime on Databricks](/concepts/ai-runtime-on-databricks.md) - A Databricks runtime optimized for AI and machine learning workloads, supporting single-node tasks (Public Preview) and multi-GPU distributed training (Beta).
* [AI Runtime programmatic job management](/concepts/ai-runtime-programmatic-job-management.md) - AI Runtime jobs can be created and managed programmatically using the Databricks Jobs API or Databricks Asset Bundles with YAML configuration specifying serverless GPU compute and environment settings.
* [AI Runtime Python API](/concepts/ai-runtime-python-api.md) - The in-notebook Python API (`@distributed` and `@ray_launch`) for multi-GPU workload training, an alternative to the CLI.
* [AI Runtime Regional Availability and Cross-Region GPUs](/concepts/ai-runtime-regional-availability-and-cross-region-gpus.md) - AI Runtime availability limited to specific AWS regions (us-west, us-east, ca-central-1, sa-east-1) with potential cross-region GPU spillover during high demand, which may incur egress costs.
* [AI Runtime scheduled job limitations](/concepts/ai-runtime-scheduled-job-limitations.md) - Scheduled jobs using serverless GPU have key limitations: no Environments panel for dependencies (must use %pip install), no auto-recovery on failure, and a 7-day maximum runtime requiring manual checkpointing.
* [AI Runtime Scheduled Jobs](/concepts/ai-runtime-scheduled-jobs.md) - Scheduling notebooks that use serverless GPU as recurring jobs with limitations on dependency management and auto-recovery.
* [AI Runtime Tabular Data Loading with Spark Connect](/concepts/ai-runtime-tabular-data-loading-with-spark-connect.md) - Loading tabular ML data from Delta tables using Spark Connect, with conversion to pandas/NumPy via toPandas() and to_numpy().
* [AI Runtime Usage Constraints](/concepts/ai-runtime-usage-constraints.md) - Key operational constraints for AI Runtime: 60-minute auto-termination on inactivity, no Environments panel for scheduled jobs, no auto-recovery on failure, 7-day maximum job runtime, and required manual checkpointing for long-running workloads.
* [AI Runtime Use Cases](/concepts/ai-runtime-use-cases.md) - Recommended workloads for AI Runtime including LLM fine-tuning, computer vision, recommender systems, reinforcement learning, and time series forecasting
* [AI Runtime Workload YAML Configuration](/concepts/ai-runtime-workload-yaml-configuration.md) - A YAML-based configuration format that defines training workloads including experiment name, compute specs, code source, environment dependencies, and execution command.
* [AI Traffic Governance](/concepts/ai-traffic-governance.md) - The practice of controlling access, enforcing rate limits, and tracking usage and costs for LLM endpoints and MCP servers through a central gateway.
* [ai_mask Function](/concepts/ai_mask-function.md) - An LLM-backed SQL function in Databricks that detects and masks standard PII types (email, phone, name, address, SSN, credit card, IP address, date of birth) across varied formats without requiring separate regex patterns.
* [ai_query function](/concepts/ai_query-function.md) - A general-purpose AI function in Databricks SQL for running batch inference against supported foundation models
* [AI-Assisted Development (Genie Code)](/concepts/ai-assisted-development-genie-code.md) - An integrated AI assistant for development, debugging, and operations across the ML workflow on Databricks
* [AI-Assisted Development with Genie Code](/concepts/ai-assisted-development-genie-code.md) - An AI assistant integrated across Databricks notebooks and workspace that helps with development, debugging, and operations using enterprise-context knowledge at every step of the ML workflow.
* [air cancel command](/concepts/air-cancel-command.md) - Cancels a running workload, enabling lifecycle management of submitted jobs
* [air CLI](/concepts/air-cli.md) - Command-line interface for submitting and managing distributed training workloads on Databricks AI Runtime, using `air run -f train.yaml` as the primary submission command.
* [air CLI (Databricks AI Runtime CLI)](/concepts/air-cli-databricks-ai-runtime-cli.md) - Command-line tool for submitting and managing distributed AI/ML training workloads on Databricks infrastructure
* [air get run / air list runs commands](/concepts/air-get-run-air-list-runs-commands.md) - Subcommands for querying run metadata, status, and configuration; list supports --limit and --active flags to filter results.
* [air get run and air list runs commands](/concepts/air-get-run-and-air-list-runs-commands.md) - Commands to query run metadata, status, and configuration, with filtering options like --limit and --active
* [air get run command](/concepts/air-get-run-command.md) - Displays metadata, status, and configuration details for a specific AI Runtime run.
* [air list runs command](/concepts/air-list-runs-command.md) - Lists recent AI Runtime runs with optional filters for result count and active-only status.
* [AIR Logs and Run Inspection](/concepts/air-logs-and-run-inspection.md) - Commands and patterns for inspecting training runs: checking status, streaming/downloading logs (with per-node support), listing active/recent runs, and canceling runs.
* [air logs command](/concepts/air-logs-command.md) - Streams or downloads logs for a run, with targeting of specific nodes via --node and directory download via --download-to
* [air run - Run management commands](/concepts/air-run-run-management-commands.md) - Commands for inspecting, listing, canceling, and retrieving logs from AI Runtime runs, including 'air get run', 'air list runs', 'air logs', and 'air cancel'.
* [air run - Workload submission](/concepts/air-run-workload-submission.md) - The primary CLI command for submitting AI workloads defined by YAML configuration files to Databricks AI Runtime.
* [air run -f train.yaml](/concepts/air-run-f-trainyaml.md) - Primary command pattern for launching distributed training workloads via the Databricks AI Runtime CLI using a YAML workload definition file
* [air run command](/concepts/air-run-command.md) - The primary CLI command used to submit AI training workloads, with support for YAML config files, field overrides, dry-run validation, idempotency keys, and log watching.
* [AIR Run Submission and Lifecycle](/concepts/air-run-submission-and-lifecycle.md) - The workflow for submitting training runs via `air run`, including idempotency keys, dry-run validation, field overrides, and run lifecycle tracking (status, logs, cancellation).
* [air run workflow](/concepts/air-run-workflow.md) - Mechanism to submit distributed training jobs by specifying a YAML configuration file via the `air run -f train.yaml` command
* [AIR Workload YAML Configuration](/concepts/air-workload-yaml-configuration.md) - A YAML-based configuration format (train.yaml) that defines experiment name, compute spec, command, environment dependencies, and code source for AI Runtime CLI training jobs.
* [Alert Notification Channels](/concepts/alert-notification-channels.md) - Supported destinations for profile alert notifications including email, webhook, Slack, and PagerDuty.
* [Alert Query Parameter Defaults](/concepts/alert-query-parameter-defaults.md) - The behavior where profile alerts use default parameter values when the underlying query includes parameters, requiring confirmation that defaults reflect alert intent.
* [Alert Rule Permissions Model](/concepts/alert-rule-permissions-model.md) - Privilege requirements for creating and managing anomaly detection alert rules: MANAGE privilege on the schema for schema-level alerts, MANAGE privilege on the catalog for catalog-level alerts.
* [Alert Rule Scope](/concepts/alert-rule-scope.md) - Alert rules in Databricks are scoped to either a specific schema or an entire catalog; when a monitored table within the scope becomes unhealthy, notifications are sent to designated recipients.
* [Alert Rule Scoping (Catalog vs Schema)](/concepts/alert-rule-scoping-catalog-vs-schema.md) - Alert rules in Unity Catalog Data Quality Monitoring can be scoped to an entire catalog (all schemas) or a specific schema, controlling which monitored tables are covered.
* [Alert Rule Scoping and Permissions](/concepts/alert-rule-scoping-and-permissions.md) - Alert rules in Databricks Data Quality Monitoring are scoped to a catalog or schema, with MANAGE privilege required on the scoped object to create or manage rules.
* [Alerting on Fairness Metric Thresholds](/concepts/alerting-on-fairness-metric-thresholds.md) - The practice of setting up automated alerts when fairness metrics exceed defined thresholds, enabling teams to investigate and remediate bias
* [Alias-based model lifecycle in Unity Catalog](/concepts/alias-based-model-lifecycle-in-unity-catalog.md) - Replacement for fixed stages (Staging, Production, etc.) in the Workspace Model Registry — up to 10 custom, reassignable aliases can be used to reference specific model versions.
* [Aligning Judges with Human Experts](/concepts/aligning-judges-with-human-experts.md) - The process of improving LLM judge accuracy by iteratively aligning judge behavior with human expert feedback on application outputs.
* [Aligning LLM Judges with Human Experts](/concepts/aligning-judges-with-human-experts.md) - A process to improve judge accuracy by aligning LLM judge behavior with expert human feedback on application outputs.
* [Alignment Optimizer Framework](/concepts/alignment-optimizer-framework.md) - An extensible framework for judge alignment including the default MemAlign optimizer and the AlignmentOptimizer base class for custom optimization strategies.
* [Alignment Optimizers](/concepts/alignment-optimizers.md) - Optimization algorithms (e.g., MemAlign) and the AlignmentOptimizer base class used to align judges, supporting both default and custom optimizer implementations.
* [Alignment Validation](/concepts/alignment-validation.md) - The practice of comparing original and aligned judge performance against human ground truth to measure improvement in accuracy after alignment.
* [AlignmentOptimizer Base Class](/concepts/alignmentoptimizer-base-class.md) - An extensible base class in mlflow.genai.judges.base for implementing custom alignment strategies, providing a template with an align() method for specialized optimization logic.
* [Allotted Time Exceeded in Delta Conflict Resolution](/concepts/allotted-time-for-conflict-resolution.md) - A timeout condition where Delta Lake's row-level conflict resolution exceeds the allowed time, requiring a retry of the operation.
* [Allotted Time Exceeded in Delta Lake](/concepts/allotted-time-exceeded-in-delta-lake.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CONCURRENT_APPEND indicating row-level conflict resolution exceeded the allowed time limit, requiring a retry.
* [Allotted Time for Conflict Resolution](/concepts/allotted-time-for-conflict-resolution.md) - A timeout condition (ALLOTTED_TIME_EXCEEDED) indicating row-level conflict resolution exceeded the maximum allowed time, requiring a retry
* [ALLOTTED_TIME_EXCEEDED in Delta Lake](/concepts/allotted_time_exceeded-sub-error.md) - A specific sub-error of DELTA_CONCURRENT_DELETE_READ indicating that row-level conflict resolution exceeded its allotted time and the operation should be retried.
* [ALLOTTED_TIME_EXCEEDED Sub-error](/concepts/allotted_time_exceeded-sub-error.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CONCURRENT_DELETE_DELETE indicating that row-level conflict resolution exceeded the allotted time and the operation should be retried.
* [Allow-list Model Access Pattern](/concepts/allow-list-model-access-pattern.md) - A security pattern for foundation model governance where default EXECUTE permission is revoked from the system.ai schema and then selectively granted on approved individual models.
* [Allowlist (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/allowlist-unity-catalog.md) - A Databricks Unity Catalog feature that controls which libraries (JARs, Maven artifacts) and init scripts can run on standard access mode compute, enhancing security and isolation.
* [Allowlist Path Permission Enforcement](/concepts/allowlist-path-permission-enforcement.md) - The mechanism by which allowlist entries use prefix matching on paths in Unity Catalog volumes or object storage, supporting permissions at base path, nested directory, or single file levels, with trailing slashes controlling prefix matching scope.
* [Allowlist Path Permissions and Prefix Matching](/concepts/allowlist-path-permissions-and-prefix-matching.md) - Rules governing how file paths and directories are matched in the allowlist, including prefix matching, trailing slash semantics, and the requirement for READ VOLUME permissions on volumes.
* [Allowlist Path Prefix Matching](/concepts/allowlist-path-prefix-matching.md) - The mechanism by which allowlist permissions propagate to contained files and directories, with trailing slashes controlling whether prefix matching is performed at a given directory level.
* [Allowlist Security Best Practices](/concepts/allowlist-security-best-practices.md) - Recommended practices for managing the Unity Catalog allowlist, including granting MANAGE ALLOWLIST sparingly, using specific paths, read-only storage, and formal approval processes.
* [Allowlisting Maven Coordinates](/concepts/allowlisting-maven-coordinates.md) - The process of adding Maven coordinates (groupId:artifactId:version) to the Unity Catalog allowlist, including wildcard patterns for versions and group IDs.
* [ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED](/concepts/alter-table-set-managed.md) - A Databricks SQL DDL command to convert an external table into a managed (Metastore-owned) table, which can fail with metadata cleanup errors.
* [ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED (Databricks)](/concepts/alter-table-set-managed.md) - Databricks SQL command to convert an external (unmanaged) Delta table to a managed table, with associated error conditions
* [ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED (Delta Lake)](/concepts/alter-table-set-managed.md) - A Delta Lake SQL command that attempts to convert an unmanaged (external) table to a managed table in Databricks.
* [ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED Command](/concepts/alter-table-set-managed.md) - Databricks SQL command to convert an external Delta table into a managed (internal) table
* [ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED operation](/concepts/alter-table-set-managed.md) - A Delta Lake DDL operation that migrates an external (unmanaged) table to a managed table, which can fail due to concurrent operations or file validation issues.
* [ALTER TABLE UNSET MANAGED command](/concepts/alter-table-unset-managed-command.md) - A Databricks SQL command used to roll back a Unity Catalog managed table to its original external table state.
* [ALTER VIEW as Alternative to DROP](/concepts/alter-view-as-alternative-to-drop.md) - When a view cannot be deleted because it is shared, Databricks recommends using ALTER VIEW to update it instead of dropping and recreating it.
* [Alternatives to DDP for large models](/concepts/alternatives-to-ddp-for-large-models.md) - FSDP and DeepSpeed are alternatives to DDP when models do not fit in single GPU memory.
* [Alternatives to DDP: FSDP and DeepSpeed](/concepts/alternatives-to-ddp-fsdp-and-deepspeed.md) - Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP) and DeepSpeed as alternatives to DDP for models that don't fit in a single GPU's memory
* [Amazon DynamoDB Online Store Integration](/concepts/amazon-dynamodb-online-store-integration.md) - Details of using Amazon DynamoDB as a third-party online store with Feature Store, including combined primary keys, automatic table creation via publish_table(), and instance profile authentication.
* [Anaconda License Impact on MLflow](/concepts/anaconda-license-impact-on-mlflow.md) - Changes to Anaconda's terms of service that led Databricks to stop using the defaults channel as default for MLflow models, switching to conda-forge, and implications for models logged before MLflow v1.18.
* [Anaconda Licensing for Legacy MLflow Models](/concepts/anaconda-licensing-for-legacy-mlflow-models.md) - Notice that MLflow models logged before v1.18 (Databricks Runtime 8.3 ML or earlier) may have a dependency on the Anaconda 'defaults' channel, which now requires a commercial license; newer models default to the community-managed 'conda-forge' channel.
* [Anaconda Licensing Impact on Legacy MLflow Models](/concepts/anaconda-licensing-for-legacy-mlflow-models.md) - MLflow models logged before v1.18 may have an unintended dependency on Anaconda's 'defaults' channel, requiring a commercial license per Anaconda's updated terms of service; affected customers can re-register models with a new conda.yaml.
* [Anaconda licensing implications for legacy MLflow models](/concepts/anaconda-licensing-for-legacy-mlflow-models.md) - MLflow models logged before v1.18 defaulted to Anaconda's 'defaults' conda channel; Anaconda's updated terms of service may require a commercial license. Affected models can be re-registered with a new conda.yaml specifying 'conda-forge' instead.
* [Analyzing GenAI Feedback with MLflow SDK](/concepts/analyzing-genai-feedback-with-mlflow-sdk.md) - Using MlflowClient to search traces, retrieve assessments, and compute feedback metrics (feedback rate, positive rate, per-dimension averages) from production data.
* [Anomaly Detection](/concepts/anomaly-detection.md) - One-click scalable data quality monitoring that uses intelligent scanning to prioritize important tables, analyzing historical patterns to detect freshness and completeness issues.
* [Anomaly Detection (Databricks)](/concepts/anomaly-detection-databricks.md) - A Databricks feature that automatically monitors data quality across schema tables by analyzing historical patterns of freshness and completeness.
* [Anomaly Detection Alert](/concepts/anomaly-detection-alert.md) - A Databricks feature that notifies users when anomaly detection identifies data quality issues such as stale tables or unexpected drops in row count.
* [Anomaly Detection Alerts](/concepts/anomaly-detection-alert.md) - Notifications triggered when Databricks anomaly detection identifies data quality issues such as stale tables or unexpected drops in row count.
* [Anomaly Detection Alerts in Databricks Unity Catalog](/concepts/anomaly-detection-alerts-in-databricks-unity-catalog.md) - Overview of alerting mechanisms that notify users when anomaly detection identifies data quality issues in Unity Catalog, supporting both UI-based and SQL-based configuration.
* [Anomaly detection expense tracking](/concepts/anomaly-detection-expense-tracking.md) - Technique to isolate anomaly detection costs by filtering on a non-null schema_id in the billing usage table.
* [Anomaly Detection for Table Completeness](/concepts/anomaly-detection-for-table-completeness.md) - A monitoring feature that analyzes historical row counts to predict an expected range of rows over 24 hours and marks a table as incomplete if the actual count falls below the lower bound.
* [Anomaly Detection for Table Freshness](/concepts/anomaly-detection-for-table-freshness.md) - An intelligent scanning feature that predicts the expected time of the next table commit using historical patterns and marks a table as stale if a commit is unusually late.
* [Anomaly Detection Output System Table](/concepts/anomaly-detection-output-system-table.md) - The system table system.data_quality_monitoring.table_results that stores anomaly detection output results and can be queried for alert configuration, with legacy beta jobs using an alternative table path.
* [Anomaly Detection Result Schema](/concepts/anomaly-detection-result-schema.md) - Schema of the anomaly detection results table, with rows representing scanned tables and columns including commit_freshness, total_row_count, daily_row_count, upstream_jobs, and downstream_impact.
* [Answer Sheet Evaluation](/concepts/answer-sheet-evaluation.md) - An evaluation mode for scoring pre-computed outputs or existing traces without running the GenAI app directly during evaluation.
* [Answer Sheet Evaluation Mode](/concepts/answer-sheet-evaluation.md) - Evaluation mode for scoring pre-computed outputs or existing traces without running the app directly, useful for external systems or historical data.
* [AnswerRelevancy Scorer](/concepts/answerrelevancy-scorer.md) - A DeepEval scorer that evaluates how relevant a model's answer is to the given input query, with configurable thresholds and scoring models.
* [Anthropic Messages API (on Databricks)](/concepts/anthropic-messages-api-on-databricks.md) - Databricks-hosted access to Anthropic's Messages API for Claude models, supporting text and image inputs.
* [Anthropic Tool Calling Trace with MLflow](/concepts/anthropic-tool-calling-trace-with-mlflow.md) - Capturing and annotating Anthropic function/tool calling responses in MLflow Traces, including decorating tool functions with @mlflow.trace to create spans
* [ANY FILE Securable](/concepts/any-file-securable.md) - A special Hive metastore securable object type that controls access to files at arbitrary paths, going beyond standard table-level permissions.
* [Apache Airflow Orchestration for Databricks Pipelines](/concepts/apache-airflow-orchestration-for-databricks-pipelines.md) - Triggering pipeline updates from Apache Airflow DAGs using the DatabricksSubmitRunOperator with a pipeline_task configuration.
* [Apache Iceberg Metadata Version Parsing](/concepts/apache-iceberg-metadata-version-parsing.md) - The requirement that Apache Iceberg metadata file names follow a specific version convention; failures in parsing the version from metadata locations can cause ingress violations.
* [Apache Iceberg to Delta Lake Conversion](/concepts/iceberg-to-delta-conversion.md) - Converting Iceberg tables (with Parquet file format) to Delta Lake by using the Iceberg manifest for metadata, schema, and partitioning information.
* [Apache Software Foundation Trademark Attribution](/concepts/apache-software-foundation-trademark-attribution.md) - Standard attribution notice for Apache, Apache Spark, Spark and the Spark logo as trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.
* [Apache Spark API compatibility with Delta Lake](/concepts/apache-spark-api-compatibility-with-delta-lake.md) - Full compatibility of Delta Lake with Apache Spark APIs, allowing users to leverage existing Spark knowledge and codebases when working with Delta Lake.
* [Apache Spark Compatibility](/concepts/apache-spark-compatibility.md) - Delta Lake is fully compatible with Apache Spark APIs, allowing users to leverage existing Spark code and workflows when working with Delta Lake tables.
* [Apache Spark DataFrames to TFRecord Conversion](/concepts/apache-spark-dataframes-to-tfrecord-conversion.md) - The recommended approach for saving Apache Spark DataFrames as TFRecord files for use in distributed deep learning, as documented by Databricks.
* [Apache Spark MLlib](/concepts/apache-spark-mllib.md) - The scalable machine learning library in Apache Spark, providing common algorithms for classification, regression, clustering, collaborative filtering, dimensionality reduction, and optimization primitives.
* [Apache Spark Server Incompatibility with Databricks Connect](/concepts/apache-spark-server-incompatibility-with-databricks-connect.md) - Databricks Connect is built against Databricks Runtime and is incompatible with open-source Apache Spark servers due to differing dependencies such as the json4s library version, causing class-not-found, API mismatch, or serialization errors.
* [Apache Spark Trademark Attribution](/concepts/apache-spark-trademark-attribution.md) - Legal attribution notice that Apache, Apache Spark, Spark, and the Spark logo are trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation, as required by Databricks documentation.
* [Apache Spark Trademark Attribution in Databricks Documentation](/concepts/apache-spark-trademark-attribution.md) - Databricks documentation includes standard attribution for Apache Spark, Spark, and the Spark logo as trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.
* [Apache Spark Trademark Usage](/concepts/apache-spark-trademark-usage.md) - Guidelines and legal terms for using Apache Spark trademarks, as referenced in Databricks documentation
* [API Response Structure for Reasoning Models](/concepts/api-response-structure-for-reasoning-models.md) - The chat completions API response for reasoning models includes both a reasoning block (with summary text and signature) and a text block (the final answer), allowing clients to access the model's step-by-step thinking.
* [API-Based Endpoint Configuration with Instance Profile](/concepts/api-based-endpoint-configuration-with-instance-profile.md) - Using the Databricks Serving Endpoints API (POST/PUT /api/2.0/serving-endpoints) with the instance_profile_arn field to assign an instance profile during creation or update.
* [App Resource Environment Variable Injection](/concepts/app-resource-environment-variable-injection.md) - Pattern in Databricks Apps where resource identifiers (e.g., MLflow experiment IDs) are automatically exposed as environment variables at deployment, referenced via the valueFrom field in app.yaml configuration.
* [App Resource Permission Levels](/concepts/app-resource-permission-levels.md) - Three-tiered permission model (Can read, Can edit, Can manage) for granting Databricks app service principals access to MLflow experiment resources.
* [APPLY (CHECKPOINT)](/concepts/apply-checkpoint.md) - A REORG TABLE subcommand that performs Delta checkpointing on the table's latest Delta version to improve metadata management.
* [APPLY (PURGE)](/concepts/apply-purge.md) - A REORG TABLE subcommand that rewrites files containing soft-deleted data (e.g., from ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN) to physically remove it from files.
* [APPLY (UPGRADE UNIFORM)](/concepts/apply-upgrade-uniform.md) - A REORG TABLE subcommand that upgrades a Delta table to a specified Apache Iceberg version (1 or 2) for Iceberg compatibility.
* [apply_fsdp Sharding Pattern](/concepts/apply_fsdp-sharding-pattern.md) - A design pattern that applies FSDP sharding to individual transformer layers using fully_shard() before sharding the entire model, enabling distributed training of modular architectures.
* [AppState Pattern for Distributed Checkpointing](/concepts/appstate-pattern-for-distributed-checkpointing.md) - A Stateful-protocol-compliant wrapper class that encapsulates model and optimizer state dict logic for use with PyTorch's distributed checkpoint save/load APIs in FSDP contexts.
* [AppState Stateful Wrapper for Checkpointing](/concepts/appstate-stateful-wrapper-pattern.md) - A PyTorch Stateful protocol-compliant wrapper class that encapsulates model and optimizer state, automatically managing FSDP fully qualified names and sharded state dict types during dcp.save/load operations.
* [AppState Stateful Wrapper Pattern](/concepts/appstate-stateful-wrapper-pattern.md) - A design pattern implementing PyTorch's Stateful protocol to wrap model and optimizer state for use with Distributed Checkpoint API, handling FSDP fully qualified names and SHARDED_STATE_DICT automatically.
* [Archival and the Delta Transaction Log](/concepts/archival-and-the-delta-transaction-log.md) - The critical constraint that the Delta transaction log directory (_delta_log/) must never be archived, as doing so makes the entire table inaccessible.
* [Archival support for Delta tables](/concepts/archival-support-in-databricks.md) - A Databricks feature enabling Delta tables to coexist with cloud object lifecycle policies by ignoring archived files during queries.
* [Archival support for Delta tables in Databricks](/concepts/archival-support-in-databricks.md) - A Databricks feature that allows Delta tables to use cloud lifecycle policies on object storage, enabling queries to ignore files older than a specified period and fail early when archived files must be scanned.
* [Archival Support in Databricks](/concepts/archival-support-in-databricks.md) - A Databricks feature that enables cloud-based lifecycle policies on Delta tables, allowing queries to safely coexist with archived cloud object storage data.
* [Archival support limitations](/concepts/archival-support-limitations.md) - The documented restrictions on Delta Lake archival support including inapplicability to transaction logs, exclusion of non-creation-time-based policies, and unsupported operations on archived data.
* [Archival support sampling behavior](/concepts/archival-support-sampling-behavior.md) - The mechanism by which Databricks samples files older than the retention period to determine if restored data is available before scanning a table.
* [Archival-aware query optimization in Delta Lake](/concepts/archival-aware-query-optimization-in-delta-lake.md) - Optimizations that allow queries to succeed without touching archived files by using metadata-only queries or filters that avoid archived data.
* [Archival-compatible S3 storage tiers](/concepts/archival-compatible-s3-storage-tiers.md) - Archival support requires S3 Glacier Deep Archive or Glacier Flexible Retrieval; Glacier Instant Retrieval works without configuration, and Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering async archive tiers are incompatible due to access-time-based archiving.
* [Archived Data Sampling Mechanism](/concepts/archived-data-sampling-mechanism.md) - Databricks' approach to detecting restored archived files by sampling files older than the retention period; if sampled files appear restored, all archived files for the query are assumed restored.
* [Arize Phoenix Scorers](/concepts/arize-phoenix-scorers.md) - Built-in scorers from Arize Phoenix for evaluating LLM outputs, integrated with MLflow GenAI evaluation
* [Arize Phoenix Scorers Integration](/concepts/arize-phoenix-scorers-integration.md) - MLflow integration with Arize Phoenix offering a lightweight set of scorers for hallucination detection, relevance, toxicity, QA correctness, and summarization quality
* [AS OF join](/concepts/as-of-join.md) - A temporal join that matches each row in the label DataFrame with the most recent feature value from a time series feature table whose timestamp is not later than the label's timestamp.
* [Assessment Data (Feedback vs Expectation)](/concepts/assessments-feedback-vs-expectation.md) - The two types of human-generated assessments collected during labeling sessions: Feedback (subjective quality ratings) and Expectations (ground-truth reference responses).
