* [Assessment Objects](/concepts/assessment-objects.md) - MLflow entity objects that store collected labels as structured assessments attached to traces within labeling sessions, retrievable via the MLflow SDK.
* [Assessment Objects in MLflow Tracing](/concepts/assessment-objects-on-traces.md) - Labels collected from domain experts are stored as Assessment objects on each Trace within a labeling session, retrievable programmatically for analysis or evaluation dataset creation
* [Assessment Objects on Traces](/concepts/assessment-objects-on-traces.md) - Labels collected from domain experts are stored as Assessment objects on each Trace within a labeling session, retrievable via MLflow SDK for analysis and evaluation dataset creation.
* [Assessment Source Tracking in MLflow](/concepts/assessment-source-tracking-in-mlflow.md) - Tagging feedback with AssessmentSource and AssessmentSourceType to distinguish feedback provenance (end users, developers, or expert reviewers) for traceability
* [Assessment types: Feedback vs Expectation](/concepts/assessment-types-feedback-vs-expectation.md) - Two distinct categories of assessments in labeling schemas: subjective feedback (ratings, opinions) and objective ground-truth expectations (correct answers, expected behavior).
* [Assessments](/concepts/assessments.md) - Quality measurements attached to a trace, comprising feedback (judgments from scorers, users, or experts) and expectations (ground-truth labels from domain experts).
* [Assessments (Feedback & Expectations)](/concepts/assessments-feedback-expectations.md) - The human-generated labels collected through labeling sessions, consisting of either Feedback (opinion-based ratings) or Expectations (ground-truth data) that can drive evaluation and improvement workflows.
* [Assessments (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/assessments.md) - Quality measurements and ground truth labels attached to traces, divided into feedback (judgments) and expectations (ground truth).
* [Assessments on Traces](/concepts/assessments-on-traces.md) - Traces support assessments (feedback and expectations) searchable by name, type, span_id, with details like value, source, rationale, and metadata.
* [Assessments: Feedback vs Expectation](/concepts/assessments-feedback-vs-expectation.md) - The two types of human-generated labels that can be captured in labeling sessions: Feedback (subjective evaluation) and Expectation (ground-truth reference data).
* [AssessmentSource Entity](/concepts/assessmentsource-entity.md) - An MLflow entity that identifies who or what provided feedback, supporting source_type (HUMAN or LLM_JUDGE) and source_id fields
* [AssessmentSource Entity for Feedback Attribution](/concepts/assessmentsource-entity.md) - The AssessmentSource object identifies who or what provided feedback, supporting HUMAN, LLM_JUDGE, or CODE source types with source_id for specific attribution.
* [AssessmentSource in MLflow](/concepts/assessmentsource-entity.md) - Entity that identifies the origin of feedback, supporting source types like HUMAN for user feedback and LLM_JUDGE for automated evaluation.
* [Assigned User Management for Labeling](/concepts/assigned-user-management-for-labeling.md) - The process of adding, replacing, and managing domain experts assigned to labeling sessions, including automatic WRITE permission grants on the MLflow experiment.
* [Assigned User Management for Labeling Sessions](/concepts/assigned-user-management-for-labeling.md) - The process of adding, replacing, and configuring permissions for domain expert reviewers in labeling sessions, including automatic WRITE permission grants on the underlying MLflow Experiment.
* [Assigning a Metastore Admin](/concepts/assigning-a-metastore-admin.md) - The process by which account admins assign the metastore admin role to a user, service principal, or group (groups recommended), with changes taking up to 30 seconds plus caching delays.
* [Assigning the Metastore Admin Role](/concepts/assigning-a-metastore-admin.md) - Process by which account admins assign the metastore admin role to a user, service principal, or group in the account console; Databricks recommends using a group. Changes may take up to 30 seconds to propagate due to caching.
* [Async Anthropic Tracing in MLflow](/concepts/async-anthropic-tracing-in-mlflow.md) - Support for tracing asynchronous Anthropic SDK calls (AsyncAnthropic) added in MLflow 2.21.0, enabling non-blocking concurrent invocation tracing
* [Async-to-Sync Wrapper Pattern for Agent Evaluation](/concepts/async-to-sync-wrapper-pattern-for-agent-evaluation.md) - A pattern wrapping asynchronous Claude agent execution inside a synchronous predict function to enable compatibility with MLflow's evaluation API.
* [Asynchronous Checkpoint I/O with DCP](/concepts/asynchronous-checkpoint-io-with-dcp.md) - Using dcp.async_save with UCVolumeWriter to upload checkpoints in the background while training continues, requiring a CPU backend on the process group with gloo and nccl.
* [Asynchronous Checkpoint Save](/concepts/asynchronous-checkpoint-save.md) - AI Runtime supports dcp.async_save with a UCVolumeWriter to upload checkpoints in the background while training continues, requiring a CPU backend on the process group.
* [Asynchronous Iceberg Metadata Generation](/concepts/asynchronous-iceberg-metadata-generation.md) - The process by which Databricks automatically generates Iceberg metadata (commit metadata JSON files) in the background after each Delta Lake write transaction, using the same compute resource without blocking writes.
* [Asynchronous Model and Code Lifecycles](/concepts/asynchronous-model-and-code-lifecycles.md) - The fundamental concept in MLOps that model artifacts and the code that produces them evolve on different cadences, enabling scenarios like weekly model retraining without code changes or code updates without retraining.
* [Asynchronous Pipeline Update Handling](/concepts/asynchronous-pipeline-update-handling.md) - Managing downstream task dependencies when pipeline update requests return asynchronously, requiring polling or until-loops to wait for completion.
* [Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC)](/concepts/attribute-based-access-control-abac.md) - Dynamic, fine-grained access policies based on attributes of data and users, enabling row-level filtering and column-level masking without managing permissions table by table.
* [Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) in Unity Catalog](/concepts/attribute-based-access-control-abac-in-unity-catalog.md) - Tag-driven centralized policies that dynamically filter and mask data across catalogs, recommended over per-table filters for scaling access control.
* [Audio/Video Input Methods for Foundation Models](/concepts/audiovideo-input-methods-for-foundation-models.md) - Two supported methods for providing media to Gemini models on Databricks: publicly accessible URLs and base64-encoded inline data URIs.
* [Audit Log Access Requirements](/concepts/audit-log-access-requirements.md) - Prerequisites for accessing OpenSharing audit logs, including enabling system tables, account admin privileges, and access to system.access.audit.
* [Audit Log System Table Requirements](/concepts/audit-log-system-table-requirements.md) - Prerequisites for accessing OpenSharing audit logs, including enabling the audit log system table and granting access to system.access.audit for non-admin users.
* [Audit Logging for ABAC Policy Operations](/concepts/audit-logging-for-abac-policy-operations.md) - Databricks logs governed tag assignment/deletion events and ABAC policy CRUD operations (create, delete, get, list) in the system.access.audit table for monitoring and compliance.
* [Auditing ABAC GRANT Policies with Direct Grants](/concepts/auditing-abac-grant-policies-with-direct-grants.md) - Auditing both direct grants and ABAC GRANT policies together since effective privileges are the union of both.
* [Auditing Direct Grants and ABAC GRANT Policies](/concepts/auditing-direct-grants-and-abac-grant-policies.md) - The need to audit both direct grants and ABAC GRANT policies together since effective privileges are the union of both
* [Authentication for Databricks remote MLflow tracking](/concepts/mlflow-authentication-methods.md) - Methods for authenticating with a remote Databricks MLflow tracking server, including Personal Access Tokens (PAT) and OAuth via service principals.
* [Authentication methods for Databricks MLflow remote tracking](/concepts/mlflow-authentication-methods.md) - Two authentication options for remote MLflow tracking: Personal Access Tokens (PAT) for simple development setups and OAuth using service principals for production.
* [Authentication methods for remote MLflow tracking](/concepts/mlflow-authentication-methods.md) - Two authentication approaches for remote tracking server connections: Personal Access Tokens (PAT) for simple token-based auth and OAuth using service principals.
* [Authentication via DatabricksSession for Scala Utilities](/concepts/authentication-via-databrickssession-for-scala-utilities.md) - Authentication for the Databricks Utilities for Scala library is determined through initializing the DatabricksSession class in a Databricks Connect project
* [Auto Loader for Image Ingestion](/concepts/auto-loader-for-image-ingestion.md) - Using Databricks Auto Loader to incrementally ingest large volumes of images from cloud object storage into Delta tables, handling continuously arriving new files.
* [Auto Loader for Parquet Migration](/concepts/auto-loader-for-parquet-migration.md) - Using Databricks Auto Loader's incremental ingestion capabilities to migrate Parquet data to Delta Lake with exactly-once guarantees.
* [Auto Time-to-Live (Auto-TTL) for Unity Catalog Tables](/concepts/auto-time-to-live-auto-ttl-for-unity-catalog-tables.md) - A configurable automatic retention mechanism in Databricks Unity Catalog that deletes trace data older than a specified number of days, helping comply with GDPR and other data protection regulations.
* [Auto Time-to-Live for Compliance](/concepts/auto-time-to-live-for-compliance.md) - Automatic row deletion based on a time column in Unity Catalog managed Delta tables, used to enforce data retention policies without scheduled jobs.
* [Auto-ARIMA and Algorithm Selection in Serverless Forecasting](/concepts/auto-arima-and-algorithm-selection-in-serverless-forecasting.md) - The use of the Auto-ARIMA algorithm (via pmdarima) and the ability to choose training frameworks for AutoML to explore during model tuning.
* [Auto-ARIMA and Regular Frequency Requirement](/concepts/auto-arima-and-regular-frequency-requirement.md) - Requirement that time series data must have a regular, consistent frequency for Auto-ARIMA, with AutoML automatically filling in missing time steps using forward fill.
* [Auto-ARIMA for Forecasting](/concepts/auto-arima-for-forecasting.md) - An algorithm used by Databricks AutoML that requires time series data with a regular frequency; missing time steps are filled with the previous value.
* [Auto-ARIMA Frequency Requirements](/concepts/auto-arima-frequency-requirements.md) - Constraint in AutoML forecasting requiring time series to have a regular frequency with consistent intervals between data points, matching the frequency unit specified in the UI or API.
* [Auto-ARIMA in Databricks AutoML](/concepts/auto-arima-in-databricks-automl.md) - An algorithm option for forecasting that requires regularly-spaced time series data; Databricks AutoML handles missing time steps by forward-filling with the previous value.
* [Auto-assign metastores for workspace provisioning](/concepts/auto-assign-metastores-for-workspace-provisioning.md) - Admins must set a Unity Catalog metastore to auto-assign for every region where workspaces are deployed, ensuring new workspaces automatically receive a metastore.
* [Auto-tagging for Detected Classifications](/concepts/auto-tagging-for-detected-classifications.md) - Automatic tagging feature that applies classification tags to all existing and future detections of a classification type. Configurable at both metastore level and catalog level, with catalog-level settings taking precedence.
* [Auto-Tracing for Sequential LLM Calls](/concepts/auto-tracing-for-sequential-llm-calls.md) - How MLflow automatic tracing captures multiple sequential LLM calls within a single workflow, creating a parent span with child spans for each OpenAI invocation.
* [Auto-Tracing with Databricks Foundation Model APIs](/concepts/auto-tracing-with-databricks-foundation-model-apis.md) - Configuring OpenAI client to call Databricks Foundation Model APIs (e.g., Llama 4 Maverick) while mlflow.openai.autolog() automatically traces those calls using Databricks authentication.
* [AutoGen Auto-Tracing](/concepts/autogen-auto-tracing.md) - MLflow's automatic tracing capability for AutoGen agent workflows, enabled via mlflow.autogen.autolog()
* [Autologging Administration in Databricks](/concepts/autologging-administration-in-databricks.md) - Workspace-level control for administrators to enable or disable Databricks Autologging across all clusters from the admin settings page.
* [Autologging Configuration for Databricks Environments](/concepts/autologging-configuration-for-databricks-environments.md) - Prerequisites and configuration steps for enabling MLflow Tracing on Databricks Foundation Models, including installing mlflow[databricks]>=3.1 and the OpenAI SDK, setting environment variables (DATABRICKS_HOST, DATABRICKS_TOKEN), and noting that serverless compute clusters require explicit autolog() calls.
* [Autologging for GenAI Tracing](/concepts/autologging-for-genai-tracing.md) - One-line code pattern using mlflow.<library>.autolog() to automatically instrument and trace 20+ supported AI libraries and frameworks without manual instrumentation.
* [Autologging Limitations and Constraints](/concepts/autologging-limitations-and-constraints.md) - MLflow Gemini autolog has known constraints: not auto-enabled on serverless compute clusters, only supports synchronous text interactions (no async), and may not record full inputs for multi-modal inputs.
* [Autologging Limitations and Edge Cases](/concepts/autologging-limitations-and-edge-cases.md) - Known constraints of Databricks Autologging, including driver-node-only execution, incompatibility with explicit mlflow.start_run(), and unsupported XGBoost scikit-learn integration.
* [Autologging on Serverless Compute](/concepts/autologging-on-serverless-compute.md) - Databricks Autologging is not automatically enabled on serverless compute; users must explicitly call mlflow.autolog() to use it
* [Autologging Security and Administration](/concepts/autologging-security-and-administration.md) - Security via MLflow Experiment permissions and workspace-level admin controls to enable or disable Databricks Autologging for all interactive notebooks
* [Autologging Security and Data Management](/concepts/autologging-security-and-data-management.md) - The security model for autologged data, which is stored in MLflow Tracking and secured by MLflow Experiment permissions, allowing sharing, modification, or deletion via MLflow APIs or UI.
* [Automated Container Compliance Rebuilding](/concepts/automated-container-compliance-rebuilding.md) - Databricks automatically rebuilds outdated served containers within 30 days to maintain compliance, with event log alerts when the automated job fails.
* [Automated Data Classification with AI](/concepts/automated-data-classification-with-ai.md) - Databricks Data Classification uses a large language model (LLM) agent to automatically classify and tag tables in Unity Catalog, identifying sensitive data such as PII, GDPR, HIPAA, and DPDPA-related content.
* [Automated Evaluation and Monitoring](/concepts/automated-evaluation-and-monitoring.md) - Using the same LLM judges and scorers during development and production to replace manual testing and continuously improve GenAI app quality
* [Automatic Container Rebuilding for Compliance](/concepts/automatic-container-rebuilds-for-compliance.md) - Compliance standards require served containers to be rebuilt within the last 30 days; Databricks automatically rebuilds outdated containers, with failure notifications via event logs.
* [Automatic Container Rebuilds for Compliance](/concepts/automatic-container-rebuilds-for-compliance.md) - Compliant Model Serving requires containers built within the last 30 days; Databricks automatically rebuilds outdated containers, with event log notifications if the automated rebuild fails.
* [Automatic Dependency Declaration for Databricks Utilities Scala Library](/concepts/automatic-dependency-declaration-for-databricks-utilities-scala-library.md) - Databricks Connect for Scala automatically declares a dependency on the Databricks Utilities for Scala library, so no explicit build file configuration is needed
* [Automatic Exception Capture in Spans](/concepts/automatic-exception-capture-in-spans.md) - When an exception is raised during a traced operation, MLflow captures partial data and records exception details as span events, indicated in the UI as an unsuccessful invocation.
* [Automatic Exception Handling in MLflow Traces](/concepts/automatic-exception-handling-in-mlflow-traces.md) - Built-in capture of exceptions during trace-instrumented operations, showing failure indicators in the UI and recording exception details as span events.
* [Automatic exception handling in traces](/concepts/automatic-exception-capture-in-spans.md) - If an Exception is raised during a trace-instrumented operation, MLflow captures the partial trace data and records exception details as span events for debugging.
* [Automatic Feature Lineage](/concepts/automatic-feature-lineage.md) - Models trained using Databricks Feature Store automatically track lineage to the features used during training, enabling reproducibility and governance.
* [Automatic Feature Lineage Capture](/concepts/automatic-feature-lineage-capture.md) - Lineage information tracking feature tables and Python UDFs used in models is automatically captured when calling log_model
* [Automatic Feature Lineage Tracking](/concepts/automatic-feature-lineage.md) - Models trained using Databricks features automatically track lineage to the features they were trained on, enabling runtime feature lookup from online stores.
* [Automatic Feature Lookup for Model Serving](/concepts/automatic-feature-lookup-for-model-serving.md) - Databricks Model Serving can automatically look up feature values from online feature stores (Databricks or third-party) during model inference, eliminating the need for manual feature engineering in the serving pipeline.
* [Automatic Input/Output Parsing for Evaluation](/concepts/automatic-inputoutput-parsing-for-evaluation.md) - How the Guidelines judge automatically extracts request and response fields from traces, supporting OpenAI-format messages, ChatCompletion objects, and arbitrary dicts.
* [Automatic Instrumentation with mlflow.openai.autolog()](/concepts/automatic-instrumentation-with-mlflowopenaiautolog.md) - A feature that automatically captures trace data from OpenAI SDK calls (including Databricks-hosted LLMs) without requiring manual instrumentation of each API invocation.
* [Automatic Provider and Recipient Objects](/concepts/automatic-provider-and-recipient-objects.md) - Two objects automatically created when an SAP BDC connection is established, enabling bidirectional sharing
* [Automatic Provider Object Creation](/concepts/automatic-provider-object-creation.md) - For Databricks-to-Databricks sharing, provider objects are created automatically in the recipient's Unity Catalog metastore, so recipients typically do not need to manually create them.
* [Automatic Request/Response Parsing](/concepts/automatic-requestresponse-parsing.md) - How the Guidelines judge automatically extracts request and response data from traces, handling OpenAI format messages, ChatCompletions, and arbitrary dicts.
* [Automatic System Metrics Capture](/concepts/automatic-system-metrics-capture.md) - GPU, CPU, and memory system metrics are captured automatically for every run without configuration, viewable in the MLflow run's System metrics tab.
* [Automatic Tagging](/concepts/automatic-tagging.md) - A mechanism in Databricks Data Classification that automatically applies governed tags to detected columns, configurable at both metastore and catalog levels with inheritance semantics.
* [Automatic Tagging in Data Classification](/concepts/automatic-tagging.md) - The ability to automatically apply governed tags to all existing and future column detections of a given classification type, configurable at catalog and metastore levels.
* [Automatic Tagging with Governed Tags](/concepts/automatic-tagging.md) - A mechanism to automatically apply governed classification tags to detected columns; configurable at metastore and catalog levels with inherited, active, or inactive states.
* [Automatic Tracing](/concepts/automatic-tracing.md) - An MLflow tracing approach that uses a single line of code (mlflow.<library>.autolog()) to automatically capture traces for 20+ supported libraries without manual instrumentation.
* [Automatic Tracing (autolog)](/concepts/automatic-tracing.md) - One-line `mlflow.<library>.autolog()` approach to automatically capture traces for 20+ supported libraries
* [Automatic Tracing (MLflow)](/concepts/automatic-tracing.md) - One-line mlflow.<library>.autolog() approach to automatically capture traces for 20+ supported libraries without manual instrumentation.
* [Automatic Tracing for Databricks Foundation Model APIs](/concepts/auto-tracing-with-databricks-foundation-model-apis.md) - Using mlflow.openai.autolog() to trace calls to Databricks Foundation Model APIs via the OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
* [Automatic Tracing for OpenAI](/concepts/automatic-tracing-for-openai.md) - One-line integration that automatically traces OpenAI SDK calls by wrapping the client with the tracedOpenAI function from the mlflow-openai package.
* [Automatic Transformer Block Detection for FSDP Wrapping](/concepts/automatic-transformer-block-detection-for-fsdp-wrapping.md) - A technique to programmatically infer which module classes in a model correspond to transformer decoder layers, enabling correct FSDP auto-wrap configuration without hardcoding class names.
* [Automatic Type Casting for Column Mask Functions](/concepts/automatic-type-casting-for-column-mask-functions.md) - Databricks automatically casts input column values to match the mask function's parameter type and the function output to match the target column's data type, with special support for struct-to-VARIANT casting on Runtime 18.1+.
* [Automatic UDF Dependency Discovery with withAutoDependencies](/concepts/automatic-udf-dependency-discovery-with-withautodependencies.md) - Databricks Connect can automatically discover and upload local modules and public PyPI dependencies used in UDF imports, eliminating manual specification via the withAutoDependencies() API.
* [Automatic Unity Catalog Enablement](/concepts/automatic-unity-catalog-enablement.md) - For workspaces created after November 8, 2023, Unity Catalog is auto-enabled with a metastore, eliminating the need for a separate metastore admin by granting workspace admins sufficient metastore-level privileges.
* [Automatic Unity Catalog Enablement and Workspace Admin Default Privileges](/concepts/automatic-unity-catalog-enablement.md) - For workspaces created after November 8, 2023, Unity Catalog is auto-enabled and workspace admins receive default metastore-level privileges (CREATE CATALOG, CREATE EXTERNAL LOCATION, etc.) represented by the auto-generated _workspace_admins_databricks system group.
* [Automatic Unity Catalog Workspace Admin Privileges](/concepts/automatic-unity-catalog-workspace-admin-privileges.md) - Default metastore-level privileges (CREATE CATALOG, CREATE EXTERNAL LOCATION, etc.) automatically granted to workspace admins in workspaces created after November 8, 2023, making the metastore admin role optional.
* [Automatic vs Manual Tracing Instrumentation](/concepts/automatic-vs-manual-tracing-instrumentation.md) - Two approaches to instrumenting GenAI applications for tracing: automatic (framework-integrated) and manual (developer-defined).
* [Automatically Populated Trace Metadata](/concepts/automatically-populated-trace-metadata.md) - MLflow automatically setting certain standard metadata fields based on the execution environment, with the ability to override them via mlflow.update_current_trace().
* [Automatically Populated Trace Metadata in MLflow](/concepts/automatically-populated-trace-metadata.md) - MLflow's automatic population of certain standard metadata fields based on execution environment, with the ability to override via update_current_trace()
* [AutoML Advanced Configuration Options](/concepts/automl-advanced-configuration-options.md) - Optional parameters for AutoML classification experiments including evaluation metrics, framework exclusion, stopping conditions, and time column splitting
* [AutoML Advanced Configuration Parameters](/concepts/automl-advanced-configuration-options.md) - Configurable options for AutoML experiments including evaluation metrics, training framework exclusions, stopping conditions, time-based data splitting, and data directory security.
* [AutoML API Feature Store Configuration](/concepts/automl-api-feature-store-configuration.md) - The AutoML API accepts a `feature_store_lookups` parameter, a list of dictionaries specifying table names and lookup keys to join feature tables to the training dataset.
* [AutoML API feature_store_lookups Parameter](/concepts/automl-run-api-feature-store-parameters.md) - The feature_store_lookups parameter in the AutoML run specification allows programmatic configuration of feature table joins.
* [AutoML Classification (classify)](/concepts/automl-classification-classify.md) - Method to configure and train classification models via AutoML, supporting parameters like target_col, primary_metric, timeout_minutes, and pos_label
* [AutoML Classification Data Preparation](/concepts/automl-classification-data-preparation.md) - How Databricks AutoML prepares datasets for classification model training, including feature type detection, imputation, sampling, and splitting.
* [AutoML Classification Experiment Setup](/concepts/automl-classification-experiment-setup.md) - Step-by-step process for configuring and launching a classification experiment using the Databricks AutoML UI
* [AutoML Classification Experiment Setup on Databricks](/concepts/automl-classification-experiment-setup.md) - GUI-based workflow to configure and launch a classification experiment using AutoML on Databricks, including dataset selection, prediction target, and compute specification.
* [AutoML Classification on Databricks](/concepts/automl-classification-on-databricks.md) - Automated machine learning service on Databricks that automatically finds the best classification algorithm and hyperparameter configuration to predict a label or category from input data.
* [AutoML Classify API](/concepts/automl-classify-api.md) - Databricks AutoML method to configure and run classification model training
* [AutoML classify()](/concepts/automl-classify.md) - Python API method to start an AutoML classification run, supporting parameters such as target_col, primary_metric, imputers, pos_label, and more.
* [AutoML Column Selection](/concepts/automl-column-selection.md) - Ability to specify which columns AutoML uses for training, including excluding columns via UI checkbox or API parameter, with restrictions on dropping target or time columns.
* [AutoML Data Preparation](/concepts/automl-data-preparation.md) - Data preparation features for AutoML including column selection for training, null value imputation, and chronological data splitting
* [AutoML Data Preparation and Column Configuration](/concepts/automl-data-preparation.md) - Capabilities to specify which columns AutoML uses for training, configure null value imputation strategies, and control chronological data splitting using a time column.
* [AutoML Data Preparation for Classification](/concepts/automl-data-preparation-for-classification.md) - Overview of how Databricks AutoML prepares data for classification model training, including configurable settings via UI and API.
* [AutoML Data Preparation Settings](/concepts/automl-data-preparation.md) - Configuration options for data preparation in AutoML including column selection, null value imputation, and chronological data splitting
* [AutoML Data Splitting Strategies](/concepts/automl-data-splitting-strategies.md) - Methods for dividing datasets into training, validation, and test sets in Databricks AutoML, including random, chronological, and manual splits.
* [AutoML Dataset Sampling](/concepts/automl-dataset-sampling.md) - Automatic stratified (classification) or random (regression) sampling of large datasets in Databricks AutoML to fit within single-worker-node memory limits.
* [AutoML Dataset Warnings and Quality Checks](/concepts/automl-dataset-warnings-and-quality-checks.md) - Feature in Databricks Runtime 10.1 ML+ that displays warnings for potential dataset issues such as unsupported column types or high cardinality columns during AutoML experiments.
* [AutoML Experiment Advanced Configuration](/concepts/automl-experiment-advanced-configuration.md) - Additional AutoML configuration options including evaluation metrics selection, framework exclusion, stopping conditions, early stopping, chronological data splitting, and data directory security.
* [AutoML Experiment Configuration](/concepts/automl-experiment-configuration.md) - Setup process for configuring an AutoML experiment including compute, dataset, prediction target, and advanced options
* [AutoML Experiment Configuration and Stopping Conditions](/concepts/automl-experiment-configuration-and-stopping-conditions.md) - Standard configuration parameters for AutoML experiments including compute selection, dataset browsing, prediction target, time column, evaluation metrics, and stopping conditions (default 120 minutes for forecasting).
* [AutoML Experiment Configuration UI](/concepts/automl-experiment-configuration-ui.md) - User interface workflow for configuring AutoML classification experiments including dataset selection, prediction target specification, compute cluster choice, and experiment naming.
* [AutoML Experiment Configuration via UI](/concepts/automl-experiment-configuration-via-ui.md) - Step-by-step process for setting up a classification experiment using the AutoML user interface in Databricks
* [AutoML Experiment Monitoring and Notebooks](/concepts/automl-experiment-monitoring-and-notebooks.md) - Tools for monitoring AutoML experiment progress, viewing dataset warnings (e.g., unsupported column types, high cardinality), accessing data exploration notebooks, and reviewing trial run notebooks stored as MLflow artifacts.
* [AutoML Experiment Monitoring and Results](/concepts/automl-experiment-monitoring-and-results.md) - How to monitor AutoML experiment progress, view warnings, explore generated notebooks, and interpret trial run results
* [AutoML Experiment Requirements by Runtime](/concepts/automl-experiment-requirements-by-runtime.md) - Classification and regression AutoML experiments with feature store integration require Databricks Runtime 11.3 LTS ML+, while forecasting experiments require 12.2 LTS ML+.
* [AutoML Experiment Results and Monitoring](/concepts/automl-experiment-results-and-monitoring.md) - How to view, monitor, and interpret results of an AutoML experiment including warnings, runs table, and generated notebooks
* [AutoML Experiment with feature_store_lookups](/concepts/automl-experiment-with-feature_store_lookups.md) - The mechanism to pass Feature Store tables as covariates to an AutoML forecasting experiment via the feature_store_lookups parameter
* [AutoML Feature Store Integration](/concepts/automl-feature-store-integration.md) - How Databricks AutoML can augment input datasets with features from Unity Catalog or legacy Workspace Feature Store tables
* [AutoML Feature Store Lookups](/concepts/automl-feature-store-lookups.md) - A mechanism in Databricks AutoML to pass external feature tables as covariates to forecasting experiments using the feature_store_lookups parameter.
* [AutoML Feature Store Lookups Parameter](/concepts/automl-feature-store-lookups.md) - The feature_store_lookups parameter in Databricks AutoML that links one or more Feature Store tables (keyed by lookup_key) as covariates in a forecasting experiment.
* [AutoML feature_store_lookups Parameter](/concepts/automl-feature-store-lookups.md) - A configuration parameter in Databricks AutoML forecasting API that accepts a list of feature table lookups to include covariates in the experiment.
* [AutoML Forecast API](/concepts/automl-forecast-api.md) - Databricks AutoML method to configure and run time-series forecasting model training
* [AutoML forecast()](/concepts/automl-forecast.md) - Python API method to start an AutoML forecasting run with parameters for time column, frequency, horizon, identity column, and country code.
* [AutoML Forecasting (Classic Compute)](/concepts/automl-forecasting-classic-compute.md) - Databricks AutoML's classic compute mode for automatically finding the best forecasting algorithm and hyperparameters for time-series data, requiring Databricks Runtime 10.0 ML or above.
* [AutoML Forecasting (Databricks Classic Compute)](/concepts/automl-forecasting-classic-compute.md) - Automated machine learning workflow on Databricks for time-series forecasting using classic (non-serverless) compute, requiring Databricks Runtime 10.0 ML+
* [AutoML Forecasting (forecast)](/concepts/automl-forecasting-forecast.md) - Method for time series forecasting model training, requiring target_col, time_col, frequency, horizon, and supporting identity_col for multiple time series
* [AutoML Forecasting Data Preparation](/concepts/automl-forecasting-data-preparation.md) - How Databricks AutoML prepares data for forecasting training, including supported feature types, imputation, splitting, and aggregation.
* [AutoML Forecasting Data Splitting Strategy](/concepts/automl-forecasting-data-splitting-strategy.md) - How Databricks AutoML partitions forecasting data into train, validation, and test sets using time series cross-validation, where the number of folds depends on input table characteristics such as number of time series, covariates presence, and series length.
* [AutoML Forecasting Experiment (Classic Compute)](/concepts/automl-forecasting-experiment-classic-compute.md) - Setting up a time-series forecasting experiment using Databricks AutoML with user-managed compute clusters, including dataset configuration, target column selection, and launch workflow.
* [AutoML Forecasting Experiment Configuration](/concepts/automl-forecasting-experiment-configuration.md) - Configuring and launching an automated forecasting experiment in Databricks AutoML with parameters for target column, time column, frequency, horizon, and timeout.
* [AutoML Forecasting Experiment Lifecycle](/concepts/automl-forecasting-experiment-stages.md) - The three-stage pipeline of a Databricks forecasting experiment: Preprocessing (validation, imputation, feature engineering), Tuning (algorithm selection and hyperparameter search), and Training (final model training and registration).
* [AutoML Forecasting Experiment Stages](/concepts/automl-forecasting-experiment-stages.md) - The three-phase pipeline of a Databricks forecasting experiment: Preprocessing (validation, imputation, feature generation), Tuning (algorithm exploration), and Training (final model training and registration).
* [AutoML Import Notebook](/concepts/automl-import-notebook.md) - Method to import a notebook saved as an MLflow artifact
* [AutoML import_notebook()](/concepts/automl-import-notebook.md) - Method to import a notebook saved as an MLflow artifact from an AutoML run, returning an ImportNotebookResult.
* [AutoML Imputation Strategies](/concepts/automl-imputation-strategies.md) - How Databricks AutoML handles missing values in classification datasets, including default type-based imputation and custom imputation methods.
* [AutoML Missing Value Imputation](/concepts/automl-missing-value-imputation.md) - Configurable strategies for handling null values in AutoML regression training, including default per-column-type imputation and custom overrides via UI or API.
* [AutoML Model Registration and Deployment](/concepts/automl-model-registration-and-deployment.md) - Process for registering AutoML-generated models to Unity Catalog or Model Registry and deploying to model serving endpoints
* [AutoML on Databricks](/concepts/automl-on-databricks.md) - A no-code UI and Python API feature that automatically finds the best algorithm and hyperparameter configuration for machine learning datasets.
* [AutoML Pandas Compatibility Issue](/concepts/automl-pandas-compatibility-issue.md) - Known pandas version incompatibility error when serving AutoML models, with resolution via a dependency-fix script
* [AutoML Python API](/concepts/automl-python-api.md) - Programmatic interface for Databricks AutoML that provides methods to start classification, regression, and forecasting runs.
* [AutoML Regress API](/concepts/automl-regress-api.md) - Databricks AutoML method to configure and run regression model training
* [AutoML regress()](/concepts/automl-regress.md) - Python API method to start an AutoML regression run, using r2 as the default primary metric and supporting similar parameters to classification.
* [AutoML Regression (regress)](/concepts/automl-regression-regress.md) - Method to configure and train regression models via AutoML, with primary_metric defaulting to r2 and supporting similar parameters to classify
* [AutoML Result Monitoring and Model Deployment](/concepts/automl-result-monitoring-and-model-deployment.md) - Workflow for monitoring experiment progress, viewing warnings, reviewing best model notebooks, and registering/deploying models to Unity Catalog or Model Registry via the AutoML UI.
* [AutoML Run API Feature Store Parameters](/concepts/automl-run-api-feature-store-parameters.md) - The feature_store_lookups parameter in the AutoML run specification API that enables programmatic feature table joins
* [AutoML Run Duration Control](/concepts/automl-run-duration-control.md) - Controlling AutoML run duration via timeout_minutes, with max_trials deprecated
* [AutoML Sampling for Large Datasets](/concepts/automl-sampling-for-large-datasets.md) - Automatic memory-aware dataset sampling in AutoML, using stratified sampling for classification and random sampling for regression.
* [AutoML Stopping Conditions](/concepts/automl-stopping-conditions.md) - Default stopping conditions for AutoML experiments including time limits, trial limits, and early stopping based on validation metric improvement
* [AutoML Supported Feature Types](/concepts/automl-supported-feature-types.md) - The specific Spark data types supported as features in Databricks AutoML classification, including numeric, boolean, string, timestamps, arrays, and decimal types.
* [AutoML Trial Notebooks](/concepts/automl-trial-notebooks.md) - Generated notebooks per model trial during an AutoML run, importable via import_notebook from MLflow artifact URIs
* [AutoML UI Feature Selection](/concepts/automl-ui-feature-selection.md) - The graphical workflow in the Databricks AutoML UI for selecting, configuring, and joining multiple feature tables to an experiment's training dataset.
* [AutoML UI Feature Table Selection](/concepts/automl-ui-feature-selection.md) - The workflow in the Databricks AutoML UI for selecting and joining feature tables to an experiment through the 'Join features' option
* [AutoML Warnings and Diagnostics](/concepts/automl-warnings-and-diagnostics.md) - AutoML feature that displays warnings for potential dataset issues such as unsupported column types or high cardinality columns
* [AutoMLSummary](/concepts/automlsummary.md) - Summary object returned by AutoML runs that describes metrics, parameters, and trial details, and provides methods to load trained models.
* [AutoModel and AutoTokenizer for Fine-tuning](/concepts/automodel-and-autotokenizer-for-fine-tuning.md) - Using Hugging Face AutoModelForSequenceClassification and AutoTokenizer to load pre-trained models and tokenize data for fine-tuning tasks.
* [AutoModelForSequenceClassification](/concepts/automodelforsequenceclassification.md) - A Hugging Face Auto Model class that loads a pre-trained transformer model with a classification head for text classification fine-tuning.
* [AutoModelForSequenceClassification for Fine-tuning](/concepts/automodelforsequenceclassification-for-fine-tuning.md) - Loading and configuring pre-trained sequence classification models with label mappings for fine-tuning tasks
* [Autoscaling Limitation with ray.data.from_spark()](/concepts/autoscaling-limitation-with-raydatafrom_spark.md) - Autoscaling Spark clusters (including spot instances) must set use_spark_chunk_api=False when using ray.data.from_spark() to avoid cache misses caused by executor termination.
* [Autoscaling Restriction for Distributed XGBoost](/concepts/autoscaling-restriction-for-distributed-xgboost.md) - Distributed XGBoost training cannot be used on clusters with autoscaling enabled because new worker nodes remain idle
* [AutoTokenizer and Tokenization for Fine-tuning](/concepts/autotokenizer-and-tokenization-for-fine-tuning.md) - Using AutoTokenizer with Hugging Face datasets to tokenize text for model training
* [AutoTokenizer for Transformers](/concepts/autotokenizer-for-transformers.md) - A Hugging Face Auto class that loads the tokenizer corresponding to a pre-trained model, ensuring consistent tokenization across training and inference datasets.
* [Available DeepEval Metric Categories](/concepts/deepeval-metric-categories.md) - DeepEval provides five categories of metrics: RAG, Agentic, Conversational, Safety, and Non-LLM metrics
* [Avoid Spark Caching with Delta Lake](/concepts/avoid-spark-caching-with-delta-lake.md) - Databricks recommends against using Spark caching for Delta tables because it breaks data skipping optimizations and can serve stale data if the table is accessed via a different identifier.
* [Avoiding Data Silos in Lakehouse](/concepts/avoiding-data-silos-in-lakehouse.md) - Principles and best practices for preventing data silos by using Unity Catalog views, OpenSharing, single sources of truth, and lineage tracking instead of copying or duplicating datasets across departments.
* [AWS Graviton Support for Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/aws-graviton-support-in-databricks-runtime-ml.md) - Support for Graviton instance types starting from Databricks Runtime 15.4 LTS ML, offering improved price-to-performance for Spark, Photon, and ML libraries
* [AWS Graviton Support in Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/aws-graviton-support-in-databricks-runtime-ml.md) - Support for AWS Graviton (ARM-based) instance types in Databricks Runtime 15.4 LTS ML and above, offering improved performance for Spark, Photon, feature engineering, XGBoost, LightGBM, and Spark MLlib gradient boosting.
* [AWS S3 Bucket Naming Restrictions for Delta Sharing](/concepts/aws-s3-bucket-naming-restrictions-for-delta-sharing.md) - Dots (periods) in S3 bucket names cause SSL/certificate errors when Delta Sharing generates pre-signed URLs, due to an AWS limitation on bucket naming conventions.
* [Axolotl](/concepts/axolotl.md) - A high-performance framework for LLM fine-tuning that supports QLoRA and other efficient training techniques, with integration for MLflow, HuggingFace, and distributed training
* [Axolotl Framework for LLM Fine-Tuning](/concepts/axolotl.md) - A high-performance framework for post-training large language models, supporting QLoRA and multi-GPU distributed training
* [Azure Data Factory Orchestration for Databricks Pipelines](/concepts/azure-data-factory-orchestration-for-databricks-pipelines.md) - Running pipeline updates from Azure Data Factory using Web activities that call the Databricks Pipelines REST API endpoint.
* [Backfill Scorers](/concepts/backfill-scorers.md) - Technique to retrospectively apply evaluation scorers to historical MLflow experiment traces within a specified date range.
* [Backfill Scorers (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/backfill-scorers-mlflow-genai.md) - Technique for retroactively applying scoring functions to historical trace data within a specified time range using the databricks.agents.scorers.backfill_scorers() API.
* [BackfillScorerConfig](/concepts/backfillscorerconfig.md) - Configuration object used to specify custom sample rates for individual scorers during a backfill operation.
* [BackfillScorers](/concepts/backfillscorers.md) - A Databricks function that applies scorers to historical trace data over a specified time range, returning a job ID.
* [Base Environment vs AI Environment in AI Runtime](/concepts/default-vs-ai-environments-in-ai-runtime.md) - Two environment options for AI Runtime: the default base environment (None) and the pre-configured AI environment (AI v4 / databricks_ai_v5)
* [BASE_INDEX in Delta Streaming Offsets](/concepts/base_index-in-delta-streaming-offsets.md) - A required offset index format for Delta streaming skip offsets, validated as part of the INVALID_INDEX subcondition of the DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE error.
* [BASE_INDEX Offset Index Requirement](/concepts/base_index-offset-index-requirement.md) - The requirement that when an invalid offset index occurs in a Delta streaming skip offset range, the index must be of the form BASE_INDEX (baseIndex)
* [BASE_INDEX Offset Reference](/concepts/base_index-offset-reference.md) - A requirement that Delta streaming skip offset indices must be expressed as BASE_INDEX, a specific reference format for identifying offsets in a Delta source stream.
* [Base64 Encoding for Media in ML Inference](/concepts/base64-encoding-for-media-in-ml-inference.md) - The technical process of encoding local audio or video files as base64 strings for inline transmission to model serving endpoints, using Python's base64 library.
* [Baseline Drift](/concepts/baseline-drift.md) - A drift comparison method that compares a data window to a baseline distribution defined by a separate baseline table, used when a static reference distribution exists.
* [Baseline Table](/concepts/baseline-table.md) - An optional reference table in Databricks data profiling used to compute drift metrics relative to expected data distributions and quality standards.
* [Baseline Table Comparison for Data Drift](/concepts/baseline-table-comparison-for-data-drift.md) - The practice of comparing current data distribution against a baseline table to detect statistical drift and trigger retraining of models.
* [Baseline Table for Data Profiling](/concepts/baseline-table-for-data-profiling.md) - An optional reference table used in data profiling to measure drift; it should reflect expected data quality, distributions, and match the primary table's schema.
* [Batch Inference on Databricks](/concepts/batch-inference-on-databricks.md) - Databricks' recommended approach for running batch predictions on large datasets using AI Functions and SQL queries
* [Batch Inference Pipelines](/concepts/batch-inference-pipelines.md) - A Databricks pattern for deploying and orchestrating batch inference workloads as pipelines, enabling scalable processing of large datasets.
* [Batch Inference with ai_query on Databricks](/concepts/batch-inference-with-ai_query-on-databricks.md) - Databricks recommends using the ai_query AI function as the modern replacement for legacy Hugging Face batch inference patterns
* [Batch Processing with Nested Span Hierarchies](/concepts/batch-processing-with-nested-span-hierarchies.md) - Pattern for tracking complex multi-level workflows by creating nested span hierarchies for each item in a batch, with structured error handling per item.
* [Bearer Token Authentication for Delta Sharing](/concepts/bearer-token-authentication-for-open-sharing.md) - A token-based authentication method for Open Sharing recipients where Databricks generates a bearer token, a credential file, and an activation link for the recipient to download credentials and access shared data.
* [Bearer Token Authentication for Delta Sharing Recipients](/concepts/bearer-token-authentication-for-open-sharing.md) - A Databricks-to-Open sharing authentication method where the provider creates a recipient object, Databricks generates a bearer token and credential file, and the recipient authenticates via an activation link.
* [Bearer Token Authentication for Open Sharing](/concepts/bearer-token-authentication-for-open-sharing.md) - A token-based authentication method for open sharing where Databricks generates a bearer token, a credential file, and an activation link for the recipient to download and use for read access to shared data.
* [Bearer Token Authentication for OpenSharing](/concepts/bearer-token-authentication-for-opensharing.md) - Alternative authentication method for non-Databricks users accessing Databricks-to-Open sharing, contrasted with OIDC federation in this guide.
* [Best Practices for ABAC GRANT Policies](/concepts/best-practices-for-abac-grant-policies.md) - Design recommendations for GRANT policies including using groups over individual users, attaching at smallest scope, using tag inheritance, and avoiding mixing with direct grants.
* [Best Practices for Adding Context to MLflow Traces](/concepts/best-practices-for-adding-context-to-mlflow-traces.md) - Guidelines including consistent ID formats, session boundaries, environment variables for metadata, combining context types, and regular analysis
* [Best Practices for Deep Learning on Databricks](/concepts/best-practices-for-deep-learning-on-databricks.md) - Optimization guidelines and recommended workflows for developing and fine-tuning deep learning models efficiently on the Databricks platform.
* [Best Practices for Judge Instructions](/concepts/best-practices-for-judge-instructions.md) - Guidelines for writing effective LLM judge prompts including specifying output format (categorical, boolean, or numeric) and breaking down complex evaluations into structured sections.
* [Best practices for labeling schema design](/concepts/best-practices-for-labeling-schema-design.md) - Guidelines for writing clear questions, providing reviewer context, setting reasonable input limits, ensuring mutually exclusive categorical options, and managing schema lifecycles.
* [Best practices for LLM judge instructions](/concepts/best-practices-for-judge-instructions.md) - Guidelines for writing effective judge prompts: specifying output format (categorical, boolean, or numeric), breaking complex evaluations into clear sections, and aligning with human expert feedback.
* [Best Practices for MLflow Experiment Organization](/concepts/best-practices-for-mlflow-experiment-organization.md) - Recommendations for structuring MLflow experiments in Databricks apps including logical grouping by project or model type, consistent naming conventions, and retention policy management.
* [Best Practices for MLflow Trace Context](/concepts/best-practices-for-mlflow-trace-context.md) - Guidelines for adding context to traces including consistent ID formats, defining session boundaries, using environment variables for metadata, combining context types, and setting up dashboards.
* [Best Practices for mlflow.search_traces()](/concepts/best-practices-for-mlflowsearch_traces.md) - Recommended practices including using keyword arguments, proper filter_string formatting (prefixes, single quotes, backticks, timestamps), and avoiding unsupported operators like OR.
* [Best Practices for Ray on Databricks](/concepts/best-practices-for-ray-on-databricks.md) - Collection of optimization and troubleshooting guidance including GPU allocation, pickling errors, OOM errors, transformers MLflow integration, and batch data processing
* [Best Practices for Trace Context Management](/concepts/best-practices-for-trace-context-management.md) - Guidelines for consistent ID formats, session boundary definitions, environment variable usage for metadata, combining context types, and regular trace analysis.
* [Best Practices for Writing Evaluation Guidelines](/concepts/best-practices-for-writing-evaluation-guidelines.md) - Principles for crafting effective pass/fail natural language criteria including specificity, clear conditions, context references, and structured multi-constraint guidelines.
* [Best Practices for Writing Judge Instructions](/concepts/best-practices-for-judge-instructions.md) - Guidelines for crafting effective judge prompts, including specifying expected output formats (categorical, boolean, numeric), and breaking complex evaluations into clear sections covering what to evaluate, what information to examine, how to judge, and what format to return.
* [Beta Feature Lifecycle at Databricks](/concepts/beta-feature-lifecycle-at-databricks.md) - The Beta release stage for Databricks features, indicating the AI Runtime CLI is not yet GA and may have limited availability.
* [Beta features in Databricks](/concepts/beta-features-in-databricks.md) - Databricks release process designates certain features as Beta, indicating they are not yet generally available and may have limitations.
* [Beta Scope and Limitations of GRANT Policies](/concepts/beta-scope-and-limitations-of-grant-policies.md) - In Beta, GRANT policies only support EXECUTE privilege on models (MLflow and foundation models), cannot grant USE CATALOG/SCHEMA, and policies can only be attached at catalog or schema level, not model level.
* [Billing portal LakehouseMonitoring tag](/concepts/billing-portal-lakehousemonitoring-tag.md) - A method to view data profiling expenses via the Databricks account console billing portal using the LakehouseMonitoring tag key set to true.
* [Bin-packing optimization (Delta Lake)](/concepts/bin-packing-optimization-delta-lake.md) - An idempotent compaction technique that balances data files by size on disk, producing evenly-sized files without effect on re-run.
* [Bloom Filter Index](/concepts/bloom-filter-index.md) - A legacy indexing technique for Delta tables in Databricks used to skip irrelevant data files during query execution.
* [Bloom Filter Index (Databricks)](/concepts/bloom-filter-index.md) - A deprecated indexing feature in Databricks that used Bloom filters to speed up data skipping during queries on Delta tables.
* [Bloom Filter Index (Deprecated)](/concepts/bloom-filter-index.md) - A deprecated indexing feature in Databricks that used Bloom filters to optimize data skipping during queries, now replaced by alternative optimizations.
* [Bloom Filter Index Cleanup Semantics](/concepts/bloom-filter-index-cleanup-semantics.md) - When a Bloom filter index is dropped, all related metadata is removed from specified columns and underlying index files are cleaned only after a vacuum
* [Bloom Filter Index Deprecation](/concepts/bloom-filter-index-deprecation.md) - Bloom filter indexes are deprecated in Databricks and should not be created anymore.
* [Bloom Filter Index Deprecation (Databricks)](/concepts/bloom-filter-index-deprecation.md) - Bloom filter indexes are deprecated in Databricks, with migration guidance to predictive I/O or liquid clustering.
* [Bloom filter index deprecation in Databricks](/concepts/bloom-filter-index-deprecation.md) - Bloom filter indexes are deprecated in Databricks; users are recommended to drop all existing Bloom filter indexes
* [Bloom Filter Index Deprecation on Databricks](/concepts/bloom-filter-index-deprecation.md) - Official recommendation from Databricks to drop all existing Bloom filter indexes and migrate to recommended alternatives.
* [Bloom Filter Index Lifecycle and Cleanup](/concepts/bloom-filter-index-lifecycle-and-cleanup.md) - Bloom filter index files on disk are only physically removed when the table is vacuumed after dropping the index.
* [Bloom filter index metadata cleanup on DROP](/concepts/bloom-filter-index-metadata-cleanup-on-drop.md) - Dropping a Bloom filter index removes all Bloom filter related metadata from specified columns or entire table
* [Bloom Filter Index Metadata Removal](/concepts/bloom-filter-index-metadata-removal.md) - All Bloom filter-related metadata is removed from specified columns when the DROP BLOOMFILTER INDEX command succeeds.
* [Bloom filter indexes](/concepts/bloom-filter-index.md) - A deprecated indexing feature in Delta Lake used to skip data files during reads; users are advised to migrate to Predictive I/O or Liquid Clustering.
* [Bloom Filter Indexes (Databricks)](/concepts/bloom-filter-index.md) - A deprecated indexing technique in Databricks that uses Bloom filters to skip non-matching files during query execution, improving performance on equality predicates.
* [Bloom Filter Metadata Cleanup](/concepts/bloom-filter-metadata-cleanup.md) - Behavior of the DROP command to remove all Bloom filter related metadata from specified columns, with index file cleanup occurring on VACUUM.
* [Boolean Slice Expressions for Protected Group Definition](/concepts/boolean-slice-expressions-for-protected-group-definition.md) - A technique for defining protected and unprotected groups in fairness analysis using Boolean expressions that partition data into slices
* [brickster package db_repl function](/concepts/brickster-package-db_repl-function.md) - Alternative approach using the brickster package's db_repl function to use all sparklyr's Spark MLlib functions when Databricks Connect's DataFrame-API-only limitation is a blocker.
* [Budget Policy Permission Requirements](/concepts/budget-policy-permission-requirements.md) - Users and service principals must have explicit entitlement to use a budget policy before they can assign it to an MLflow experiment.
* [Build Logs for Model Serving](/concepts/build-logs-for-model-serving.md) - Displays output from the process that creates a production-ready Python environment for model serving endpoints, retained for up to 30 days.
* [Building a Scala JAR with sbt for Databricks](/concepts/building-a-scala-jar-with-sbt-for-databricks.md) - Step-by-step process to create a Scala project with sbt, configure build.sbt with correct versions, add sbt-assembly plugin, and produce a fat JAR for Databricks serverless compute.
* [Building JARs with sbt for Databricks Serverless](/concepts/building-jars-with-sbt-for-databricks-serverless.md) - Step-by-step process to create a Scala JAR compatible with Databricks serverless using sbt, with sbt-assembly plugin, matching JDK 17, Scala 2.13, and Databricks Connect versions.
* [Building Python Wheels with setuptools](/concepts/building-python-wheels-with-setuptools.md) - The process of using setuptools and the wheel package to compile Python source code into a .whl distribution file via the 'python3 setup.py bdist_wheel' command.
* [Building Scala JARs with sbt-assembly for Serverless](/concepts/building-scala-jars-with-sbt-assembly-for-serverless.md) - Step-by-step process to build a serverless-compatible Scala JAR using sbt and sbt-assembly plugin
* [Built-in Conversation Scorers](/concepts/built-in-conversation-scorers.md) - Predefined multi-turn judges like ConversationCompleteness, UserFrustration, and Safety provided by MLflow for evaluating conversation quality without custom development.
* [Built-in Hive Metastore](/concepts/built-in-hive-metastore.md) - A per-workspace managed service in Databricks that deploys an instance to each cluster and provides centralized metadata storage for the workspace's Hive tables.
* [Built-in Judges](/concepts/built-in-judges.md) - Pre-defined evaluation judges in MLflow/Databricks such as RelevanceToQuery, Safety, and Correctness, which can be used directly or aligned to a domain.
* [Built-in Judges (MLflow)](/concepts/built-in-judges-mlflow.md) - Pre-configured LLM-based judges for assessing GenAI quality dimensions including safety, relevance, groundedness, and correctness.
* [Built-in Judges vs Custom Judges](/concepts/built-in-judges-vs-custom-judges.md) - The distinction between predefined MLflow judges (like RelevanceToQuery, Safety, Correctness) and user-defined judges created with make_judge(), both of which support the alignment workflow.
* [Built-in LLM Judge Labeling Schemas](/concepts/built-in-llm-judge-schemas.md) - Predefined schema names (e.g., EXPECTED_FACTS, GUIDELINES, EXPECTED_RESPONSE) provided by MLflow for compatibility with built-in LLM Judges that use the Expectation type.
* [Built-in LLM Judge schemas](/concepts/built-in-llm-judge-schemas.md) - Predefined labeling schema names (EXPECTED_FACTS, GUIDELINES, EXPECTED_RESPONSE) provided by MLflow for compatibility with built-in LLM Judges.
* [Built-in LLM Judges](/concepts/built-in-llm-judges.md) - Predefined scorers that use Databricks-hosted LLMs to evaluate common quality dimensions of GenAI applications such as relevance, safety, groundedness, and correctness.
* [Built-in LLM judges predefined schemas](/concepts/built-in-llm-judge-schemas.md) - Predefined labeling schema names (EXPECTED_FACTS, GUIDELINES, EXPECTED_RESPONSE) that ensure compatibility with MLflow's built-in LLM evaluation judges.
* [Built-in Tools (Responses API)](/concepts/built-in-tools-responses-api.md) - Platform-provided tool capabilities like apply_patch that return structured outputs and are fully managed by the platform, requiring no backend implementation from the user.
* [Built-in vs Custom Judge Alignment](/concepts/built-in-vs-custom-judge-alignment.md) - The distinction that alignment applies to both built-in judges (generic criteria adapted to domain) and custom judges (refined specialized evaluation logic), using the same align() method.
* [BY POSITION Column Mapping](/concepts/by-position-column-mapping.md) - Syntax for matching source columns to target Delta table columns by ordinal position, primarily for headerless CSV files.
* [BY POSITION Column Matching](/concepts/by-position-column-matching.md) - A syntax option for matching source columns to target table columns by ordinal position, supporting headerless CSV files
* [BY_CLEAN_ROOMS Sub-Error](/concepts/by_clean_rooms-sub-error.md) - A sub-condition of DELTA_SHARING_SECURABLE_DELETE_BLOCKED that appears when the securable is shared through Clean Rooms, listing the central clean room IDs.
* [CACHE SELECT](/concepts/cache-select.md) - A Databricks SQL construct that caches data accessed by a simple SELECT query in the disk cache, supporting column and row subsets.
* [CACHE SELECT (Databricks SQL)](/concepts/cache-select.md) - SQL command that caches data from a SELECT query into the Databricks disk cache, supporting column and row subset selection for Delta and Parquet tables.
* [CACHE SELECT with Views](/concepts/cache-select-with-views.md) - Support for caching views with CACHE SELECT, but with the restriction that the expanded view query must be a simple SELECT as defined by the syntax.
* [Cache Selectivity (Databricks)](/concepts/cache-selectivity-databricks.md) - The ability to cache only a subset of columns (via column list) and rows (via WHERE predicate) from a table, reducing cache footprint and improving performance.
* [CAN EDIT METADATA Permission](/concepts/can-edit-metadata-permission.md) - A permission level allowing users to view and edit a feature table's description and metadata
* [CAN MANAGE Permission](/concepts/can-manage-permission.md) - The highest permission level for feature tables, allowing management of other users' permissions and deletion of the table
* [CAN VIEW METADATA Permission](/concepts/can-view-metadata-permission.md) - A permission level allowing users to view a feature table in the UI and see its metadata
* [CANNOT_FINALIZE_REDIRECT](/concepts/cannot_finalize_redirect.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED indicating the migration cannot finalize because the redirect configuration does not exist, possibly due to a concurrent rollback to external.
* [CANNOT_FINALIZE_REDIRECT error](/concepts/cannot_finalize_redirect.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED raised when the redirect configuration for an external location does not exist, often during a rollback-to-external scenario.
* [CANNOT_FINALIZE_REDIRECT Sub-Error](/concepts/cannot_finalize_redirect.md) - Sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED triggered when redirect configuration is missing during table migration
* [Capybara Dataset](/concepts/capybara-dataset.md) - A conversational AI training dataset from the TRL library used for supervised fine-tuning of language models
* [Capybara Dataset for Conversational AI Training](/concepts/capybara-dataset.md) - A dataset from the TRL library used for conversational AI training, employed as the training dataset in the supervised fine-tuning example for Llama 3.2 1B.
* [Cast-compatible masking functions](/concepts/cast-compatible-masking-functions.md) - Patterns for designing row filter and column mask UDFs where the return type is automatically cast to match the target column's data type, including techniques to avoid numeric overflow and return compatible types in all branches.
* [Catalog and Schema](/concepts/catalog-and-schema.md) - Catalogs are the first-level and schemas the second-level container objects in Unity Catalog's three-level namespace, used to organize data and AI assets by organizational unit, project, or lifecycle scope.
* [Catalog Explorer](/concepts/catalog-explorer.md) - The Databricks UI tool for navigating, discovering, and managing Unity Catalog assets including tables and their quality configurations.
* [Catalog Explorer for OpenSharing](/concepts/catalog-explorer-for-opensharing.md) - The Databricks UI tool used to visually manage OpenSharing shares, recipients, and grants through the Catalog pane.
* [Catalog Explorer Lineage Graph](/concepts/catalog-explorer-lineage-tab.md) - Visual UI in Databricks Catalog Explorer showing lineage relationships between feature tables, models, and functions with an interactive graph view
* [Catalog Explorer Lineage Tab](/concepts/catalog-explorer-lineage-tab.md) - The UI surface in Databricks Catalog Explorer where users can view lineage for tables, model versions, and functions, including a 'See lineage graph' option for a visual graph view.
* [Catalog Explorer Lineage Visualization](/concepts/catalog-explorer-lineage-tab.md) - Interactive graph interface in Databricks Catalog Explorer showing table, job, dashboard, and column-level lineage with expandable nodes and a Lineage details panel.
* [Catalog Explorer Upgrade Wizard](/concepts/catalog-explorer-upgrade-wizard.md) - UI-based tool in Databricks Catalog Explorer for upgrading Hive schemas and tables to Unity Catalog external tables with destination mapping, ownership assignment, and notebook generation.
* [Centrality Measures (Degree and Betweenness)](/concepts/centrality-measures-degree-and-betweenness.md) - Metrics for measuring the importance of a node within a network, including degree centrality and betweenness centrality
* [Centralized Feature Store Architecture](/concepts/centralized-feature-store-architecture.md) - Architectural pattern designating a single Databricks workspace to store all feature store metadata, with multiple workspaces accessing it for creating, writing to, or reading from feature tables.
* [Certification Status System Tag](/concepts/certification-status-system-tag.md) - A system-governed tag (system.certification_status) in Unity Catalog that marks data assets as certified or deprecated to indicate data quality and lifecycle status.
* [Certified and Deprecated Data Flags](/concepts/certified-and-deprecated-data-flags.md) - System tags that mark data assets as certified (trusted, meeting quality standards) or deprecated (outdated, unsuitable for new use), influencing search and inline display.
* [Certified and Deprecated Data Signals](/concepts/certified-and-deprecated-data-flags.md) - System tags applied by admins to mark data assets as certified (trusted, meeting quality standards) or deprecated (outdated, unsuitable for new use), influencing search and inline display.
* [Certified Data (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/certified-data-unity-catalog.md) - System tags applied to data assets to mark them as trusted and meeting quality standards in Unity Catalog.
* [Chaining MLflow Evaluation Results](/concepts/chaining-mlflow-evaluation-results.md) - A pattern using evaluation results to filter problematic traces and re-evaluate them with different scorers or an updated application for targeted iteration.
* [Champion/Challenger Model Deployment Strategy](/concepts/championchallenger-model-deployment-strategy.md) - A pattern where a newly trained and validated model is given the 'Challenger' alias and compared against the current production model (the 'Champion') before being promoted to production status.
* [Change Data Feed (CDC) Column Conflicts](/concepts/change-data-feed-column-conflict.md) - A specific conflict scenario where a table contains a user-defined column named '_change_type' that interferes with Delta Lake's Change Data Feed metadata, preventing row-level conflict detection.
* [Change Data Feed (CDF) for Efficient Profiling](/concepts/change-data-feed-cdf-for-efficient-profiling.md) - Best practice to enable change data feed on tables to process only newly appended data during TimeSeries and Inference profile refreshes instead of re-processing the entire table.
* [Change Data Feed (CDF) for Recipient-Side Replication](/concepts/change-data-feed-cdf-for-recipient-side-replication.md) - Enabling CDF on shared tables allows recipients to access changes and merge them into a local copy, limiting egress to the incremental refresh rather than full query access across regions.
* [Change Data Feed (CDF) Metadata Column Conflict](/concepts/change-data-feed-column-conflict.md) - A specific conflict scenario where a table column named '_change_type' conflicts with CDF metadata columns, preventing row-level conflict detection.
* [Change Data Feed (CDF) Metadata Column Conflicts](/concepts/change-data-feed-column-conflict.md) - A conflict scenario where a table contains a column named '_change_type' that interferes with Delta Lake's Change Data Feed metadata columns, preventing row-level conflict detection.
* [Change Data Feed (CDF) metadata conflicts](/concepts/change-data-feed-column-conflict.md) - A conflict scenario where a user-defined column named '_change_type' collides with Change Data Feed metadata columns, preventing row-level conflict detection in Delta Lake.
* [Change Data Feed Column Conflict](/concepts/change-data-feed-column-conflict.md) - A conflict condition where a user-defined column named '_change_type' clashes with Delta Lake's Change Data Feed metadata, preventing row-level conflict detection.
* [Change Data Feed Column Conflicts](/concepts/change-data-feed-column-conflict.md) - An error condition where a user-created column named '_change_type' conflicts with Change Data Feed (CDF) metadata columns, preventing row-level conflict detection in Delta Lake.
* [Change Data Feed for Delta Streaming](/concepts/change-data-feed-for-streaming.md) - Approach to use Delta Lake change data feed to capture inserts, updates, and deletes from source tables in streaming queries for robust change data capture (CDC).
* [Change Data Feed for Incremental Profiling](/concepts/change-data-feed-for-incremental-profiling.md) - A best practice to enable change data feed (CDF) on tables so profiling processes only newly appended data instead of re-processing the entire table on every refresh.
* [Change Data Feed for Streaming](/concepts/change-data-feed-for-streaming.md) - Using Delta Lake change data feed to stream all change types (inserts, updates, deletes) and handle them explicitly in downstream tables
* [Change Data Feed for streaming all change types](/concepts/change-data-feed-for-streaming.md) - Using Delta Lake change data feed to stream inserts, updates, and deletes from a source table, providing row-level change tracking for robust downstream processing.
* [Change Data Feed Metadata Column Conflict](/concepts/change-data-feed-column-conflict.md) - A conflict condition where a table contains a column named '_change_type' that clashes with Change Data Feed (CDC) metadata columns, preventing row-level conflict detection.
* [Change Data Feed Metadata Column Conflicts](/concepts/change-data-feed-column-conflict.md) - A conflict where user-defined columns named '_change_type' interfere with Change Data Feed (CDF) metadata columns, preventing row-level conflict detection in Delta Lake.
* [Change Data Feed Metadata Conflict](/concepts/change-data-feed-column-conflict.md) - A conflict condition where a user-defined column named '_change_type' collides with Change Data Feed (CDC) metadata columns, preventing row-level conflict detection in Delta Lake; resolution requires renaming the column or disabling CDC.
* [Change Data Feed Metadata Conflicts](/concepts/change-data-feed-column-conflict.md) - A conflict that occurs when a user-defined column named '_change_type' collides with Delta Lake's Change Data Feed metadata columns, preventing row-level conflict detection
* [Change Data Feed with OpenSharing](/concepts/change-data-feed-with-opensharing.md) - Reading incremental change data from OpenSharing tables using the readChangeFeed option in Spark DataFrames and Structured Streaming.
* [Chart Customization in MLflow](/concepts/chart-customization-in-mlflow.md) - Ability to add, move, resize, edit, delete, and download charts in the MLflow Chart View, including full-screen mode and kebab menu controls.
* [Chat Completion Response Format](/concepts/chat-completion-response-format.md) - Standard structure of responses from Databricks chat model endpoints, including choices, usage tokens, and metadata.
* [Chat Completions API](/concepts/chat-completions-api.md) - An API on Databricks for multi-turn conversations using 'messages' array, supporting content types including text, image, reasoning, and document content.
* [Chat Completions API (Databricks)](/concepts/chat-completions-api-databricks.md) - Multi-turn conversation API compatible with OpenAI's Chat Completions format, supporting system/assistant/user messages, tool calls, and streaming.
* [Chat Completions API for Multimodal Media Input](/concepts/chat-completions-api-for-multimodal-media-input.md) - How to use the OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions API on Databricks to pass video_url and audio_url content types in the messages array.
* [Chat Completions API for Vision Models](/concepts/chat-completions-api-for-vision-models.md) - The OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions API used to send text and image content to vision models hosted on Databricks, supporting multiple image inputs.
* [Chat Template Formatting for Fine-Tuning](/concepts/chat-template-formatting-for-fine-tuning.md) - The practice of applying a chat template (e.g., ChatML format) to conversational datasets when fine-tuning causal language models, ensuring proper structured conversation formatting for assistant-style responses.
* [Chat Template Processing with Unsloth](/concepts/chat-template-processing-with-unsloth.md) - The pipeline for converting conversational datasets (ShareGPT format) into model‑specific chat template text using Unsloth's get_chat_template and standardize_sharegpt utilities.
* [Checkpoint CRC Corruption Detection](/concepts/crc-checksum-corruption-detection.md) - Detection of CRC checksum corruption in Delta Lake checkpoint files, identified by the fileCrcCorrupt column in FSCK REPAIR TABLE output.
* [Checkpoint Model Ranking via MLflow API](/concepts/checkpoint-model-ranking-via-mlflow-api.md) - Using mlflow.search_logged_models to rank checkpoint models by metrics like accuracy, enabling programmatic selection of the best-performing model version.
* [Checkpoint V2 requirement](/concepts/checkpoint-v2-requirement.md) - APPLY (CHECKPOINT) requires the table to have Checkpoint V2 enabled to prevent corruption caused by race conditions.
* [Choosing Parallelism Techniques for Multi-GPU Training](/concepts/choosing-parallelism-techniques-for-multi-gpu-training.md) - The process of selecting between DDP, FSDP, and DeepSpeed based on model size, GPU memory capacity, and performance requirements.
* [Chronological Data Splitting in AutoML](/concepts/chronological-data-splitting-in-automl.md) - Using a time column to split data chronologically into training, validation, and test sets for classification and regression experiments, ensuring temporal order is preserved.
* [CI/CD for Machine Learning](/concepts/cicd-for-machine-learning.md) - Automated process for developing, deploying, monitoring, and maintaining ML applications, combining MLOps, DataOps, ModelOps, and DevOps principles.
* [CI/CD for Machine Learning (Databricks)](/concepts/cicd-for-machine-learning.md) - Automated process for developing, deploying, monitoring, and maintaining ML applications, applied to code, data pipelines, and model artifacts.
* [CI/CD for ML Pipelines](/concepts/cicd-for-ml-pipelines.md) - Integration of GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps with MLOps Stacks to automatically test and deploy ML code and resources across workspaces, ensuring only tested code reaches production.
* [Class-based Scorer Definition](/concepts/class-based-scorer-definition.md) - A stateful, Pydantic-based approach to defining MLflow scorers by subclassing the Scorer base class, supporting additional fields and complex logic (offline evaluation only).
* [Class-based Scorer for stateful evaluation](/concepts/class-based-scorer-for-stateful-evaluation.md) - A Pydantic-based Scorer base class for defining evaluation scorers that require state (additional fields), supported only for offline evaluation with mlflow.genai.evaluate()
* [Class-based Scorer limitations in production monitoring](/concepts/class-based-scorer-limitations-in-production-monitoring.md) - Class-based Scorer subclasses are not supported for production monitoring in Databricks MLflow; only @scorer-decorated functions work.
* [Class-based Scorer subclass limitation](/concepts/class-based-scorer-subclass-limitation.md) - Class-based Scorer subclasses are not supported for production monitoring in Databricks; only @scorer-decorated functions work
* [Class-based Scorer subclasses limitation](/concepts/class-based-scorer-subclass-limitation.md) - Class-based Scorer subclasses are not supported for production monitoring in Databricks
* [Class-based vs decorator-based scorer definitions](/concepts/class-based-vs-decorator-based-scorer-definitions.md) - Two approaches to defining MLflow scorers: the @scorer decorator for stateless functions (supported in both offline and production monitoring) and the Scorer base class for stateful Pydantic objects (offline evaluation only).
* [Classic Compute Forecasting](/concepts/classic-compute-forecasting.md) - The non-serverless alternative to Databricks Serverless Forecasting that requires managing compute resources, presented as a comparison to the serverless approach.
* [Classic ML on AI Runtime](/concepts/classic-ml-on-ai-runtime.md) - Notebook examples for traditional ML tasks including XGBoost training and time series forecasting on AI Runtime
* [Classic ML on Databricks AI Runtime](/concepts/classic-ml-on-databricks-ai-runtime.md) - Support for traditional machine learning tasks like XGBoost training and time series forecasting within the AI Runtime environment.
