* [Classic ML on Databricks GPU](/concepts/classic-ml-on-databricks-gpu.md) - Notebook examples for traditional machine learning tasks on Databricks AI Runtime, including XGBoost model training and time series forecasting.
* [Classic ML vs Deep Learning vs GenAI](/concepts/classic-ml-vs-deep-learning-vs-genai.md) - The distinctions and overlaps between classic machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI paradigms, and the platform features that support all three.
* [Classification Results UI](/concepts/classification-results-ui.md) - The Databricks user interface that displays per-classification detection counts, auto-tagging status, user access patterns, and provides review panels for detected columns and user access details.
* [Classification Tags and Governed Tags System](/concepts/classification-tags-and-governed-tags-system.md) - A system of governed tags (personal_name, email_address, phone_number, etc.) organized by global, regional, and compliance frameworks (PII, GDPR, HIPAA, DPDPA) that are applied to columns when sensitive data is detected.
* [Classify](/concepts/classify.md) - The databricks.automl.classify method configures an AutoML run to train a classification model.
* [Claude Agent SDK (ClaudeSDKClient)](/concepts/claude-agent-sdk-claudesdkclient.md) - Anthropic's SDK client for building agentic interactions with Claude, supporting async query/response streaming patterns.
* [Clean Rooms in Databricks](/concepts/clean-rooms-in-databricks.md) - A data collaboration environment where shared objects are protected from deletion; the error message distinguishes clean room sharing from standard share-based sharing.
* [Client Options for Querying Models](/concepts/client-options-for-querying-models.md) - Multiple available client interfaces for querying Databricks models including OpenAI client, REST API, SQL, MLflow Deployments SDK, Databricks Python SDK, and LangChain.
* [Client Request IDs vs MLflow Trace IDs for Feedback Correlation](/concepts/client-request-ids-vs-mlflow-trace-ids-for-feedback-correlation.md) - Two approaches for linking user feedback to specific traces: using MLflow's auto-generated trace IDs or using application-generated client request IDs.
* [Client vs Server Concurrency Ratio](/concepts/client-vs-server-concurrency-ratio.md) - The optimal performance point for an ML serving system is determined by the ratio of client-side concurrency (number of simultaneous requesters) to server-side concurrency (provisioned parallel capacity), which load testing aims to identify.
* [Client-Server Architecture for Spark](/concepts/client-server-architecture-for-spark.md) - A decoupled architecture for Apache Spark using Spark Connect that allows remote connectivity to Spark clusters via unresolved logical plans and Apache Arrow over gRPC, enabling thin embedded clients.
* [Client-Server Architecture for Spark Jobs](/concepts/client-server-architecture-for-spark-jobs.md) - The architectural pattern where Databricks Connect sends logical representations of Spark DataFrame commands to a remote Spark server running in Databricks for execution, rather than running locally.
* [Client-Server Architecture of Databricks Connect](/concepts/client-server-architecture-of-databricks-connect.md) - The architectural pattern where Databricks Connect separates code execution into local (general code), remote cluster (DataFrame APIs), and cluster-side (UDFs), enabling interactive debugging on the client while leveraging Spark on Databricks for data transformations.
* [Client-Server Decoupled Architecture](/concepts/client-server-decoupled-architecture.md) - The architectural pattern where Databricks Connect separates local client code execution from remote Spark DataFrame API execution on Databricks compute clusters.
* [Client-Server Execution Model of Databricks Connect](/concepts/client-server-execution-model-of-databricks-connect.md) - A decoupled architecture where general code runs locally for interactive debugging, DataFrame APIs execute remotely on Databricks compute, and UDFs are serialized and transmitted to the cluster.
* [Client-Side Serving Optimization](/concepts/client-side-serving-optimization.md) - Strategies for client applications to improve interaction with serving endpoints, including connection pooling, error handling with retry logic and exponential backoff, and payload size minimization.
* [Client-Side Timeout – MLflow Configuration](/concepts/client-side-timeout-mlflow-configuration.md) - Timeouts caused by MLflow environment variables (MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT, MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_MAX_RETRIES) that can be tuned for long-running model inference.
* [Client-Side Timeout – Third Party Client APIs](/concepts/client-side-timeout-third-party-client-apis.md) - Timeouts from third-party API clients (e.g., OpenAI SDK) used in custom PyFunc model pipelines, and how to configure their timeout parameters.
* [CLONE Limitations for Streaming Tables and Materialized Views](/concepts/clone-limitations-for-streaming-tables-and-materialized-views.md) - Streaming tables and materialized views cannot be used as source or target tables for the CLONE operation.
* [CLONE Parquet](/concepts/clone-parquet.md) - A Databricks SQL command to incrementally copy data from Parquet to Delta Lake, supporting both shallow and deep clones.
* [CLONE Restrictions with Streaming Tables and Materialized Views](/concepts/clone-limitations-for-streaming-tables-and-materialized-views.md) - Streaming tables and materialized views are not supported as source or target tables for CLONE operations.
* [Clone Source Table Compatibility](/concepts/clone-source-table-compatibility.md) - Delta, Parquet, and foreign Iceberg tables support both deep and shallow cloning; managed Iceberg tables support only deep cloning.
* [CLONE Syntax and Parameters](/concepts/clone-syntax-and-parameters.md) - The SQL syntax for cloning tables, including CREATE TABLE, REPLACE TABLE, IF NOT EXISTS, SHALLOW/DEEP CLONE, TBLPROPERTIES, and LOCATION clauses.
* [CLONE Table Limitations](/concepts/clone-table-limitations.md) - Streaming tables and materialized views cannot be used as source or target tables for CLONE operations in Databricks.
* [CLONE with IF NOT EXISTS](/concepts/clone-with-if-not-exists.md) - A SQL syntax modifier for CLONE that causes the operation to be silently ignored if the target table already exists.
* [CLONE with Unity Catalog](/concepts/clone-with-unity-catalog.md) - Support for shallow cloning Unity Catalog managed tables in Databricks SQL and Runtime 13.3 LTS+, with no shallow clone support in Runtime 12.2 LTS and below.
* [Clone Workflow Pattern for UC Migration](/concepts/clone-workflow-pattern-for-uc-migration.md) - Pattern of cloning existing training, deployment, and inference jobs as a starting point for building Unity Catalog-compatible parallel pipelines
* [Cloud Storage and Service Access Governance](/concepts/cloud-storage-and-service-access-governance.md) - Unity Catalog provides storage credentials (for cloud storage authentication), external locations (storage path + credential pairs), service credentials (for external cloud services), and connections (for external databases and services), governing access to external resources.
* [Cloud Token Access (Directory-based Access Mode)](/concepts/cloud-token-access-directory-based-access.md) - A mechanism where Databricks uses temporary, path-scoped cloud credentials to give recipients direct read access to shared Delta table files, bypassing the need for long-lived bearer tokens.
* [Cloud Token Access (Directory-Based Access)](/concepts/cloud-token-access-directory-based-access.md) - A mechanism using temporary, path-scoped cloud credentials to give recipients direct read access to shared Delta table files. Applicable to Databricks-to-Databricks and Databricks-to-Open sharing under specific conditions (full history, no partition filter, etc.). Grants read access to data files and the Delta log.
* [Cloud Token Access for Delta Sharing](/concepts/cloud-token-access-for-delta-sharing.md) - Temporary, path-scoped cloud credentials that give recipients direct read access to shared Delta table files, improving performance by avoiding server-side materialization.
* [Cloud Token Eligibility (Directory-Based Access Mode)](/concepts/cloud-token-access-directory-based-access.md) - A mechanism that uses temporary, path-scoped cloud credentials to give recipients direct read access to shared Delta table files without long-lived bearer tokens.
* [Cloudflare R2 Integration for Zero-Egress Sharing](/concepts/cloudflare-r2-integration-for-zero-egress-sharing.md) - Using Cloudflare R2 object storage as the backing store for OpenSharing shares to eliminate cloud vendor egress fees, including setup steps for mounting R2 buckets as external locations in Unity Catalog.
* [Cluster Connectivity Troubleshooting for Databricks Connect](/concepts/cluster-connectivity-troubleshooting-for-databricks-connect.md) - Common connectivity failures when Databricks Connect cannot reach a cluster, indicated by errors like StatusCode.UNAVAILABLE, DNS resolution failure, or HTTP 500 status codes.
* [Cluster ID configuration in Databricks Connect](/concepts/cluster-id-configuration-in-databricks-connect.md) - Methods for specifying cluster_id when connecting to classic compute, including inline, in config profiles, or via DATABRICKS_CLUSTER_ID environment variable.
* [Cluster-level Hive Metastore Access Control](/concepts/cluster-level-hms-access-control.md) - Using Spark configurations (spark.databricks.unityCatalogOnlyMode and spark.databricks.sql.initial.catalog.namespace) to disable Hive metastore access on individual compute clusters
* [Cluster-Level Hive Metastore Disablement](/concepts/cluster-level-hive-metastore-disablement.md) - Gradually disabling direct Hive metastore access on individual compute clusters via Spark configurations during Unity Catalog migration.
* [Cluster-Level HMS Access Control](/concepts/cluster-level-hms-access-control.md) - The ability to disable Hive metastore access on a per-cluster basis using Spark configurations (spark.databricks.unityCatalogOnlyMode and spark.databricks.sql.initial.catalog.namespace).
* [Cluster-Scoped Data Access Permissions](/concepts/cluster-scoped-data-access-permissions.md) - When using the Hive metastore alongside Unity Catalog, cluster-scoped credentials are used for Hive metastore data access but not for Unity Catalog data, and are also used for paths outside Unity Catalog.
* [Clustering columns](/concepts/clustering-columns.md) - Designated columns in a Liquid table used for data organization; certain commands on Liquid tables may only reference clustering columns in their predicates.
* [Clustering Columns in Delta Lake](/concepts/clustering-columns-in-delta-lake.md) - Columns designated for data clustering in Delta Lake Liquid tables; commands on Liquid tables may only reference these columns in predicates, with specific error sub-conditions for violations.
* [CNN Architecture for MNIST Classification in PyTorch](/concepts/cnn-architecture-for-mnist-classification-in-pytorch.md) - A simple CNN with two convolutional layers (kernel size 5), max pooling, dropout, and two fully connected layers, ending with log_softmax for 10-class digit classification.
* [COCO128 dataset](/concepts/coco128-dataset.md) - COCO128 is a subset of the COCO dataset used for training object detection models such as YOLO11n on Databricks serverless GPUs.
* [Code Path Parameter for Shared Modules](/concepts/code-path-parameter-for-shared-modules.md) - MLflow pyfunc's code_path parameter allows logging shared utility code alongside a model, making it importable from within custom pyfunc classes.
* [Code Path Sharing with mlflow pyfunc](/concepts/code-path-sharing-with-mlflow-pyfunc.md) - Using the code_path parameter in mlflow.pyfunc.log_model to share and re-use custom Python modules across logged pyfunc models
* [Code Source and Environment Management in AIR](/concepts/code-source-and-environment-management-in-air.md) - Mechanisms for packaging local code (snapshot with root_path) and declaring Python dependencies (environment block with version and dependency list) for serverless GPU training runs.
* [Code Source Management](/concepts/code-source-management.md) - Uploading local code to remote training nodes via the `code_source` block, supporting plain tarball snapshots, git-versioned snapshots, and folder filtering with `include_paths`.
* [Code Source Management in AI Runtime](/concepts/code-source-management-in-ai-runtime.md) - Mechanism for uploading local code directories to remote execution nodes using the code_source block and $CODE_SOURCE_PATH variable in workload configurations.
* [Code Source Snapshot](/concepts/code-source-snapshot.md) - Mechanism to upload local code to the remote execution node using a snapshot block in the YAML config, with $CODE_SOURCE_PATH as the recommended reference path.
* [Code source snapshot pattern](/concepts/code-source-snapshot-pattern.md) - Pattern for uploading local training code to AI Runtime via code_source.snapshot configuration, where $CODE_SOURCE_PATH resolves to the remote upload directory.
* [code_path for Shared Module Dependencies](/concepts/code_path-for-shared-module-dependencies.md) - The code_path parameter in mlflow.pyfunc.log_model() allows logging full code references (shared modules) that load into the model's context and are usable from custom pyfunc models.
* [code_path parameter for shared modules](/concepts/code_path-parameter-for-shared-modules.md) - MLflow pyfunc log_model's code_path parameter allows packaging shared code modules alongside custom models for reuse across deployments
* [code_paths Parameter for Custom Libraries](/concepts/code_paths-parameter-for-custom-libraries.md) - MLflow log_model parameter that allows inclusion of custom libraries stored outside Unity Catalog volumes or DBFS by embedding them in the model artifact's code directory
* [Code-based Scorers](/concepts/code-based-scorers.md) - Custom programmatic scorers offering maximum flexibility for defining evaluation metrics through heuristics, advanced logic, or custom LLM integration beyond what custom judges provide.
* [Code-based Scorers (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/code-based-scorers-in-mlflow.md) - Custom evaluation metrics for GenAI applications defined using the @scorer decorator in MLflow Evaluation.
* [Code-based Scorers for MLflow GenAI](/concepts/code-based-scorers-in-mlflow.md) - Custom evaluation metrics defined as Python functions using the @scorer decorator pattern for evaluating GenAI applications in MLflow.
* [Code-based Scorers for MLflow GenAI Evaluation](/concepts/code-based-scorers-in-mlflow.md) - Custom Python functions decorated with @scorer that define flexible evaluation metrics for AI agents and applications in MLflow Evaluation for GenAI.
* [Code-based scorers in MLflow](/concepts/code-based-scorers-in-mlflow.md) - Custom Python functions or classes that define flexible evaluation metrics for GenAI agent evaluation, supporting primitive returns, Feedback objects, trace data access, and error handling.
* [Code-based scorers in MLflow GenAI](/concepts/code-based-scorers-in-mlflow.md) - Custom Python functions or classes decorated with @scorer that define flexible evaluation metrics for AI agents and applications in MLflow Evaluation.
* [Collaborative Editing via Group Ownership](/concepts/collaborative-editing-via-group-ownership.md) - Transferring ownership of a view or metric view to a group enables collaborative editing, where all group members can edit the definition while data access is limited to what the group has permission to see.
* [Column mapping in Delta Lake](/concepts/column-mapping-in-delta-lake.md) - A Delta Lake feature enabling column aliasing and rename without breaking existing data files, required for alias-based reconciliation queries.
* [Column Mapping Requirement for Delta Reconciliation Queries](/concepts/column-mapping-requirement-for-delta-reconciliation-queries.md) - Column mapping must be enabled on a Delta table to use an alias in a reconciliation query, otherwise a COLUMN_RENAME_WITHOUT_COLUMN_MAPPING error occurs.
* [Column Mask (CM) policies in Delta Lake](/concepts/column-mask-cm-policies-in-delta-lake.md) - A Delta table security feature that masks column values at query time, unsupported by external metadata sources.
* [Column Mask for Nested STRUCT Fields](/concepts/column-mask-for-nested-struct-fields.md) - A technique to apply column masks to individual fields within a STRUCT column by reconstructing the STRUCT with named_struct(), conditionally redacting sensitive sub-fields.
* [Column Mask Policies](/concepts/column-mask-policies.md) - ABAC policy type that enforces column-level security on tables, materialized views, and streaming tables by masking column values based on attributes
* [Column mask policies in Unity Catalog](/concepts/column-mask-policies.md) - ABAC policy type that supports column-level security on tables, materialized views, and streaming tables.
* [Column masks (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/column-masks-unity-catalog.md) - SQL UDFs applied to specific columns that transform or redact column values at query time based on user identity or other attributes.
* [Column Masks in Delta Lake](/concepts/column-masks-in-delta-lake.md) - A Delta Lake table feature for dynamically masking column values at read time, which is incompatible with REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM.
* [Column Masks in Unity Catalog](/concepts/column-masks-unity-catalog.md) - A column-level security feature that uses SQL UDFs to mask or transform column values at query time based on user identity or group membership.
* [Column masks with Delta Uniform](/concepts/column-masks-with-delta-uniform.md) - Column mask table features are incompatible with REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM operations on Delta tables
* [Column Selection Control in AutoML](/concepts/column-selection-in-automl.md) - Ability to specify which columns to include or exclude from training in Databricks AutoML via UI checkboxes or the exclude_cols API parameter.
* [Column Selection for AutoML Training](/concepts/column-selection-for-automl-training.md) - Ability to specify which columns AutoML should use for training by excluding columns via the UI or the exclude_cols API parameter; the prediction target and time column cannot be dropped.
* [Column Selection in AutoML](/concepts/column-selection-in-automl.md) - The ability to include or exclude specific columns from AutoML training via UI checkboxes or the exclude_cols API parameter.
* [Column-Level External Lineage](/concepts/column-level-external-lineage.md) - The ability to map columns between external metadata objects and Unity Catalog objects when configuring lineage relationships, enabling fine-grained field-level lineage tracking.
* [Column-Level Lineage](/concepts/column-level-lineage.md) - Granular tracking of data at the column level within Unity Catalog, showing how specific columns in downstream tables derive from upstream columns, with limitations for path-based references and UDFs.
* [Column-only predicate row filtering](/concepts/column-only-predicate-row-filtering.md) - Pattern for row filter policies that use simple boolean logic referencing only table columns, enabling predicate pushdown for better query performance on protected tables.
* [Column-only predicates for row filtering with predicate pushdown](/concepts/column-only-predicates-for-row-filtering-with-predicate-pushdown.md) - Using simple boolean expressions that reference only table columns in row filter UDFs to enable predicate pushdown, allowing the engine to skip irrelevant data during scans.
* [ColumnSelection (Pass-through Features)](/concepts/columnselection-pass-through-features.md) - A non-aggregating function that selects a single column from a data source, returning the latest value per entity via point-in-time join or passing through inference-time request data.
* [ColumnSelection Materialization](/concepts/columnselection-materialization.md) - Declarative features that select the latest single-column value per entity key without aggregation; can only materialize to online stores (not offline).
* [Combined Automatic and Manual Tracing](/concepts/combined-automatic-and-manual-tracing.md) - Hybrid approach that mixes automatic autolog() tracing with manual tracing for complete coverage
* [Combined Manual and Automatic Tracing](/concepts/combined-manual-and-automatic-tracing.md) - Pattern of using @mlflow.trace decorator and @mlflow.trace(span_type=...), alongside mlflow.autolog() to create unified traces that capture both library-internal calls and custom business logic.
* [Combined Tracing](/concepts/combined-tracing.md) - A hybrid MLflow tracing approach that mixes automatic and manual instrumentation for complete coverage of generative AI application logic.
* [Combined Tracing (MLflow)](/concepts/combined-tracing-mlflow.md) - Strategy that mixes automatic and manual tracing approaches for complete coverage of application traces.
* [Combined Tracing Approach](/concepts/combined-tracing.md) - Mixing automatic and manual tracing to achieve complete coverage of generative AI application traces.
* [Combining Autologging with Manual MLflow Tracking](/concepts/combining-autologging-with-manual-mlflow-tracking.md) - A pattern using mlflow.autolog(exclusive=False) and mlflow.start_run() to augment automatically captured autologging data with additional custom metrics, parameters, and artifacts.
* [Combining Manual and Automatic MLflow Tracing](/concepts/combining-manual-and-automatic-tracing.md) - Using @mlflow.trace decorators alongside autolog() to create unified traces that capture custom business logic and auto-traced LLM calls together.
* [Combining Manual and Automatic Tracing](/concepts/combining-manual-and-automatic-tracing.md) - Using `@mlflow.trace` decorator alongside automatic autolog tracing to create unified traces covering both LLM calls and custom business logic.
* [Combining Manual and Automatic Tracing in MLflow](/concepts/combining-manual-and-automatic-tracing.md) - Using @mlflow.trace decorators alongside mlflow.autolog() to create unified traces that capture both automatic LLM calls and custom business logic as a single trace hierarchy.
* [commit_freshness Struct](/concepts/commit_freshness-struct.md) - A struct in the anomaly detection results table that captures data freshness metrics for each scanned table.
* [Common AutoML Parameters](/concepts/common-automl-parameters.md) - Shared configuration parameters across classify, regress, and forecast methods including dataset, target_col, primary_metric, timeout_minutes, exclude_cols, exclude_frameworks, and feature_store_lookups.
* [Common Pitfalls of Low-Level Tracing APIs](/concepts/common-pitfalls-of-low-level-tracing-apis.md) - List of frequent mistakes when using client APIs including forgetting to end spans, incorrect parent-child relationships, mixing API levels, hardcoding IDs, and thread safety issues.
* [Common Table Expressions (CTE) in DELETE](/concepts/common-table-expressions-ctes-in-dml.md) - Optional use of CTEs with DELETE FROM to reuse named queries and improve complex deletion logic
* [Common Table Expressions (CTE) in DML](/concepts/common-table-expressions-cte-in-dml.md) - Use of named CTE queries within DELETE statements on Delta Lake to improve readability and avoid repeated computation.
* [Common Table Expressions (CTE) in UPDATE](/concepts/common-table-expressions-cte-in-update.md) - Named queries that can be reused within the UPDATE statement to avoid repeated computations or improve readability.
* [Common Table Expressions (CTE) with DELETE](/concepts/common-table-expressions-cte-in-dml.md) - A CTE can optionally prefix a DELETE FROM statement to define named subqueries that improve readability and avoid repeated computation.
* [Common Table Expressions (CTEs) in DML](/concepts/common-table-expressions-ctes-in-dml.md) - CTEs can be used with DELETE FROM statements to reuse named subqueries for improved readability and performance.
* [Common Table Expressions with DELETE FROM](/concepts/common-table-expressions-with-delete-from.md) - Databricks SQL allows using common table expressions (CTEs) as named queries that can be reused within the DELETE FROM statement to improve readability or avoid repeated computations.
* [Comparative Agent Evaluation with Toggles](/concepts/comparative-agent-evaluation-with-toggles.md) - A technique using global configuration flags (e.g., RESOLVE_ISSUES) to switch between agent behaviors and compare evaluation results across different agent configurations.
* [Comparative Prompt Evaluation](/concepts/comparative-prompt-evaluation.md) - The systematic process of evaluating multiple prompt versions against a shared dataset and scoring them to identify the best-performing version.
* [Comparative Prompt Evaluation Methodology](/concepts/comparative-prompt-evaluation.md) - The practice of evaluating multiple prompt versions against identical datasets and using weighted composite scores to objectively identify the best-performing version.
* [Comparative Prompt Evaluation Workflow](/concepts/comparative-prompt-evaluation-workflow.md) - Systematic methodology for evaluating multiple prompt versions against the same dataset using consistent scorers to identify the best performer.
* [Comparing Runs from Multiple Experiments](/concepts/comparing-runs-from-multiple-experiments.md) - Ability to display and compare MLflow runs across different experiments for broader analysis.
* [Compatibility matrix for Databricks Feature Engineering](/concepts/compatibility-matrix-for-databricks-feature-engineering.md) - A table mapping Databricks Runtime ML versions to the appropriate databricks-feature-engineering or databricks-feature-store package versions
* [Compatible Cloud Storage Tiers for Delta Archival](/concepts/compatible-cloud-storage-tiers-for-delta-archival.md) - The specific AWS S3 storage tiers compatible with Databricks archival support: S3 Glacier Deep Archive and Glacier Flexible Retrieval; excluding Glacier Instant Retrieval (no config needed) and Intelligent-Tiering asynchronous archive tiers.
* [Completeness (data quality metric)](/concepts/completeness-data-quality.md) - A metric measuring expected row counts in the last 24 hours, with anomalous low counts flagging a table as incomplete, and optionally augmented by percent-null analysis per column.
* [Completeness (data quality)](/concepts/completeness-data-quality.md) - A data quality metric tracking expected row count over 24 hours; anomaly detection predicts a range and flags tables falling below the lower bound as incomplete.
* [Completeness Monitoring (Databricks Anomaly Detection)](/concepts/completeness-monitoring-databricks-anomaly-detection.md) - A quality dimension that monitors the expected number of rows written to a table in the last 24 hours by analyzing historical row counts and flagging tables as incomplete if row counts fall below predicted bounds.
* [Completions API](/concepts/completions-api.md) - Text completion API for generating responses to single prompts, supporting batched independent prompts in one request.
* [Completions API (Databricks)](/concepts/completions-api-databricks.md) - Text completion API for single prompts supporting batched inputs of multiple independent prompts in one request.
* [Complex RAG Pipeline Tracing](/concepts/complex-rag-pipeline-tracing.md) - Comprehensive pattern for instrumenting a full RAG pipeline with multiple span types (CHAIN, RETRIEVER, CHAT_MODEL, TOOL), custom tags, Document objects, chat tools, and token usage tracking.
* [Compliance Framework Reference (Databricks)](/concepts/compliance-framework-reference-databricks.md) - A mapping of classification tags to common regulatory compliance frameworks including PII, PCI DSS, GDPR, HIPAA, GLBA, DPDPA, and PIPEDA.
* [Compliance Security Profile (Databricks on AWS)](/concepts/compliance-security-profile-databricks-on-aws.md) - A compliance framework for Databricks Model Serving that enforces standards like HIPAA, PCI-DSS, FedRAMP, IRAP, CCCS, and UK Cyber Essentials Plus, requiring container rebuilds within the last 30 days.
* [Compliance Security Profile Standards for Model Serving](/concepts/compliance-security-profile-standards-for-model-serving.md) - Compliance standards (e.g., UK Cyber Essentials Plus) that apply to served model containers, requiring containers to be rebuilt within the most recent 30 days.
* [Composite Scoring for Prompt Selection](/concepts/composite-scoring-for-prompt-selection.md) - A technique for combining multiple evaluation metrics (e.g., correctness and compliance) with weighted scores to produce a single quantitative ranking for prompt version selection.
* [Comprehensive Trace Analysis Utility](/concepts/comprehensive-trace-analysis-utility.md) - Pattern for building a complete trace analyzer that extracts basic info, tags, token usage, span types, retrieval metrics, assessments, performance breakdown, and data flow from a trace.
* [Compute configuration for Databricks Connect](/concepts/compute-configuration-for-databricks-connect.md) - Configuration details for setting up Databricks compute clusters to work with Databricks Connect.
* [Compute Migration for Unity Catalog](/concepts/compute-migration-for-unity-catalog.md) - The requirement to upgrade all-purpose and job compute resources to Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS or above and supported access modes for Unity Catalog compatibility.
* [Compute Options for dbt Tasks in Databricks](/concepts/compute-options-for-dbt-tasks-in-databricks.md) - dbt tasks can run against serverless SQL warehouses, pro SQL warehouses, or Databricks all-purpose compute, but not job compute; SQL warehouse is recommended for most use cases.
* [Compute Resource Specification](/concepts/compute-resource-specification.md) - Configuration of GPU resources for a training workload using `num_accelerators` and `accelerator_type` fields, supporting types like GPU_1xA10 and GPU_8xH100.
* [Compute Scale-Out and Scale-to-Zero](/concepts/compute-scale-out-and-scale-to-zero.md) - Model serving endpoints support Small/Medium/Large compute scale-out sizes (0-4, 8-16, 16-64 concurrent requests) and optional scale-to-zero, though scale-to-zero incurs cold-start latency and is not recommended for production.
* [Compute Scale-out Configuration](/concepts/compute-scale-out-configuration.md) - Configuring concurrency and scaling for serving endpoints using Small (0-4), Medium (8-16), or Large (16-64) scale-out sizes, with optional scale-to-zero for non-production use.
* [Compute type options for Model Serving endpoints](/concepts/compute-type-options-for-model-serving-endpoints.md) - Model Serving provides CPU and GPU compute types including CPU_MEDIUM and CPU_LARGE variants that trade concurrency for higher memory per worker; GPU requires explicit code setup for GPU-based predictions, though MLflow handles this automatically for PyTorch and Transformers flavors.
* [Compute Types for Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/compute-types-for-databricks-model-serving.md) - Variety of CPU and GPU workload types for model deployment, including CPU_MEDIUM and CPU_LARGE which trade concurrency for more memory per worker on the same CPU hardware.
* [Compute-Scoped vs Notebook-Scoped Library Installation](/concepts/compute-scoped-vs-notebook-scoped-library-installation.md) - Two methods for installing additional libraries on Databricks: compute-scoped libraries (available to all notebooks on a compute resource) and notebook-scoped Python libraries (available only to a specific notebook session).
* [Computer Vision Model Training with RetinaNet on Databricks](/concepts/computer-vision-model-training-with-retinanet-on-databricks.md) - A tutorial demonstrating how to train a RetinaNet object detection model on serverless GPUs using Databricks AI Runtime.
* [Computer Vision Model Training with YOLO on Databricks](/concepts/computer-vision-model-training-with-yolo-on-databricks.md) - A tutorial demonstrating how to train a YOLO11n object detection model on COCO128 using Databricks AI Runtime, with MLflow tracking and Model Serving deployment.
* [Computer vision on Databricks](/concepts/computer-vision-on-databricks.md) - Notebook examples for computer vision tasks on Databricks AI Runtime, including object detection and image classification.
* [Computer Vision on Databricks AI Runtime](/concepts/computer-vision-on-databricks-ai-runtime.md) - Capabilities for computer vision tasks such as object detection and image classification using GPU-accelerated AI Runtime notebooks.
* [Concurrency in LLM Serving Endpoints](/concepts/concurrency-in-llm-serving-endpoints.md) - The number of parallel requests sent to an LLM endpoint; controlling concurrency is central to balancing latency and throughput, with low concurrency for real-time uses and high concurrency for batch/offline uses.
* [Concurrent COPY INTO Invocations](/concepts/concurrent-copy-into-invocations.md) - COPY INTO supports concurrent runs on distinct file sets, with transaction conflicts on overlapping files. Single invocation with many files outperforms concurrent single-file runs.
* [Concurrent notebook execution in Databricks](/concepts/concurrent-notebook-execution-in-databricks.md) - Running multiple notebooks in parallel using Python or Scala threading/futures constructs alongside dbutils.notebook.run().
* [Concurrent Request Saturation](/concepts/concurrent-request-saturation.md) - The phenomenon where increasing parallel requests beyond an endpoint's provisioned capacity causes throughput to plateau while latency continues to rise due to queueing.
* [Condition Functions for ABAC](/concepts/condition-functions-for-abac.md) - Built-in functions such as has_tag and has_tag_value used in GRANT policy WHEN clauses to match governed tags on securable objects and determine policy applicability.
* [Conditional Column Masking](/concepts/conditional-column-masking.md) - A column masking technique where the mask applied to PII data depends on the value of another column on the same row (e.g., order_priority), allowing confidential rows to override user clearance levels.
* [Config Override and Dry-Run Patterns](/concepts/config-override-and-dry-run-patterns.md) - Common CLI patterns for overriding YAML configuration fields at submission time and validating configurations without actually submitting a job using --dry-run.
* [Configurable Forecasting Data Settings in AutoML](/concepts/configurable-forecasting-data-settings-in-automl.md) - The ability for users to adjust data preparation options (imputation, aggregation) during AutoML experiment setup via UI dropdowns or the Python API.
* [Configurable Judge Models](/concepts/configurable-judge-models.md) - MLflow built-in judges can be backed by different LLMs specified in LiteLLM-compatible format, defaulting to a Databricks-hosted model
* [Configuring a Lakeflow Job for Python Wheels](/concepts/configuring-a-lakeflow-job-for-python-wheels.md) - The step-by-step process of creating a Databricks job that uses a Python wheel task, including specifying package name, entry point, compute resources, and the wheel file itself.
* [Configuring Feature Table Permissions via UI](/concepts/configuring-feature-table-permissions-via-ui.md) - Users with CAN MANAGE permission can configure permissions on individual feature tables through a drop-down menu on the feature table page in the Databricks UI.
* [Configuring Judge LLMs in MLflow](/concepts/configuring-judge-llms-in-mlflow.md) - The ability to customize which LLM powers the built-in judges by specifying a model in '<provider>:/<model-name>' format, supporting LiteLLM-compatible providers including Databricks serving endpoints.
* [Configuring Permissions Across All Feature Tables](/concepts/configuring-permissions-across-all-feature-tables.md) - Workspace admins and Feature Store CAN MANAGE holders can set base permissions for all current and future feature tables from the Feature Store page
* [Configuring Permissions on an Individual Feature Table](/concepts/configuring-permissions-on-an-individual-feature-table.md) - How to set or override permissions for a specific feature table via its Permissions dropdown.
* [Configuring Permissions on Individual Feature Tables](/concepts/configuring-permissions-on-individual-feature-tables.md) - Procedure to edit permissions on a single feature table via the UI, requiring CAN MANAGE to modify
* [Conflict handling during metadata upgrade](/concepts/conflict-handling-during-metadata-upgrade.md) - Behavior when source and target table metadata differs: comments are skipped with a warning (or cause an error in older versions), other mismatches cause errors unless overwrite=True is passed.
* [Conflict resolution by data migration](/concepts/conflict-resolution-by-data-migration.md) - Resolution strategy for storage path conflicts where data must be physically moved because managed tables exist directly under the root bucket path that conflicts with the workspace default storage path.
* [Conflict resolution by path narrowing](/concepts/conflict-resolution-by-path-narrowing.md) - Resolution strategy for storage path conflicts where the external location path is updated to a more specific path (e.g., s3://bucket/region/workspace-id) without moving data, resolving the overlap while retaining data access.
* [Connecting to AI Runtime from Interactive Notebooks](/concepts/connecting-to-ai-runtime-from-interactive-notebooks.md) - Step-by-step process to attach a notebook to Serverless GPU compute with accelerator selection and environment configuration
* [Connecting to AI Runtime via Interactive Notebooks](/concepts/connecting-to-ai-runtime-via-interactive-notebooks.md) - Primary method to use AI Runtime by attaching a notebook to Serverless GPU compute and selecting an accelerator and base environment.
* [Connection Identifier Exchange](/concepts/connection-identifier-exchange.md) - The handshake process where a Databricks workspace generates a connection identifier, shares it with an SAP BDC admin who produces a connection link, which is then used to finalize the connection on Databricks.
* [Connection Identifier Exchange Protocol](/concepts/connection-identifier-exchange.md) - A two-phase handshake where a Databricks-generated connection identifier is provided to an SAP BDC admin, who returns a connection link to finalize the connection.
* [Connection Ownership and Deletion Prerequisites](/concepts/connection-ownership-and-deletion-prerequisites.md) - Connection ownership is restricted to individual users (not groups or service principals), and deleting a connection requires first unmounting all shared catalogs and being the connection owner.
* [Connection Ownership Constraints](/concepts/connection-ownership-constraints.md) - Rules governing SAP BDC connection ownership, including requirement for individual user owners and ownership transfer restrictions
* [Connection Timeout](/concepts/connection-timeout.md) - Network-level timeout where a client waits to establish a connection with the server, commonly seen with JDBC/SQL endpoints.
* [Connection Timeouts in Model Serving](/concepts/connection-timeouts-in-model-serving.md) - Timeouts related to establishing a network connection between a client and the model serving server, commonly seen with JDBC/SQL endpoints and socket connections.
* [Connection Timeouts in Model Serving Pipelines](/concepts/connection-timeouts-in-model-serving.md) - Timeouts related to establishing network connections (e.g., JDBC SocketTimeout) between model serving endpoints and downstream services like SQL endpoints or AI Search indices.
* [Consecutive and Baseline Drift](/concepts/consecutive-and-baseline-drift.md) - Two drift comparison methods: consecutive drift compares a window to the previous time window, while baseline drift compares against a user-provided baseline table.
* [Consecutive Drift](/concepts/consecutive-drift.md) - A drift comparison method that compares a data window to the immediately previous time window to detect changes over sequential time periods.
* [Consecutive vs Baseline Drift](/concepts/consecutive-vs-baseline-drift.md) - Two types of drift computed in Databricks profiling: consecutive drift compares a window to the previous time window, while baseline drift compares to a baseline distribution from a baseline table.
* [Consistent hashing (deterministic pseudonymization)](/concepts/consistent-hashing-deterministic-pseudonymization.md) - Replacing sensitive data with a deterministic hash (e.g., SHA2) via a DETERMINISTIC UDF so the same input always produces the same pseudonym across tables, with a version parameter to support key rotation.
* [Consistent hashing for deterministic pseudonymization](/concepts/consistent-hashing-deterministic-pseudonymization.md) - Replacing sensitive data with a deterministic hash that is consistent across tables using DETERMINISTIC UDFs, with a version parameter to support key rotation without breaking historical data.
* [Consistent Training and Serving Environments](/concepts/consistent-training-and-serving-environments.md) - A key benefit of express deployments where the model serving environment matches the training environment, avoiding divergence issues common in non-express deployments.
* [Contacting Databricks support to repair Delta tables](/concepts/contacting-databricks-support-to-repair-delta-tables.md) - Databricks recommends contacting their support team to repair tables affected by the DELTA_VERSIONS_NOT_CONTIGUOUS error.
* [Container Build Debugging for Model Serving](/concepts/container-build-debugging-for-model-serving.md) - Debugging container build failures and successes using event logs, including interpreting SERVED_ENTITY_CONTAINER_EVENT types and resolving build errors like OSError and GPU unavailability.
* [Container Build Event Logs](/concepts/container-build-event-logs.md) - Event logs available in the workspace UI's Events tab that track container build progress, with successful builds emitting a SERVED_ENTITY_CONTAINER_EVENT with a completion message.
* [Containerized Deployment of MLflow Traced Agents](/concepts/containerized-deployment-of-mlflow-traced-agents.md) - Patterns for passing MLflow tracing configuration environment variables to Docker and Kubernetes deployments so external GenAI agents can send traces to Databricks.
* [Content Block Types (Databricks APIs)](/concepts/content-block-types-databricks-apis.md) - The supported content types in messages across Databricks APIs: TextContent, ImageContent, ReasoningContent, DocumentContent, and FileContent.
* [Context Features](/concepts/context-features.md) - Features provided at inference time (not stored in feature tables) that serve as runtime inputs to feature functions for dynamic computation
* [Context Variable Extraction in LLM Judges](/concepts/context-variable-extraction-in-llm-judges.md) - The Guidelines judge automatically parses trace inputs and outputs into `request` and `response` context keys, handling OpenAI-format messages, ChatCompletion objects, and arbitrary dicts with specific extraction rules.
* [Context Variable Referencing in Guidelines](/concepts/context-variable-referencing-in-guidelines.md) - Guidelines can reference any key from the evaluation context dictionary (e.g., request, response, retrieved_documents, user_preferences) to create dynamic, data-aware pass/fail rules.
* [Context Variables in Guidelines](/concepts/context-variables-in-guidelines.md) - A mechanism allowing guidelines to reference any key from the evaluation context dictionary (e.g. request, response, retrieved_documents, user_preferences) directly in natural language criteria.
* [ContextPrecision Scorer](/concepts/contextprecision-scorer.md) - A RAGAS scorer that evaluates retrieval quality by measuring how much of the retrieved context is relevant to the query.
* [Contrast with Row Filter and Column Mask Policies](/concepts/contrast-with-row-filter-and-column-mask-policies.md) - GRANT policies differ from row filter/column mask policies: they determine whether a user can access an object at all (vs restricting content), and they use inline conditions (vs requiring a UDF).
* [Control Tag Pattern for Secure Defaults](/concepts/control-tag-pattern-for-secure-defaults.md) - A governance pattern using a schema-level control tag (e.g., review_status) to automatically lock down new tables by default until a data steward reviews and releases them.
* [ConversableAgent](/concepts/conversableagent.md) - The fundamental agent class in AG2 (AutoGen 0.2) used to define conversational agents with system messages, LLM configs, and termination logic.
* [Conversation Assessment Storage](/concepts/conversation-assessment-storage.md) - The design principle of storing multi-turn assessment results on the first trace of each session to ensure stability when new turns are added.
* [Conversation Assessment Storage on First Trace](/concepts/conversation-assessment-storage-on-first-trace.md) - Design pattern where multi-turn assessment results are stored on the first (chronologically earliest) trace in each session to ensure stability and efficient UI display.
* [Conversation Evaluation](/concepts/conversation-evaluation.md) - Assessing entire conversation sessions rather than individual turns to capture multi-interaction quality like user frustration, completeness, and coherence.
* [Conversation Evaluation (Multi-turn Evaluation)](/concepts/conversation-evaluation.md) - Assessment of entire conversation sessions rather than individual turns, capturing session-level quality like user frustration, conversation completeness, knowledge retention, and dialogue coherence.
* [Conversation Simulation](/concepts/conversationsimulator.md) - Generating synthetic multi-turn conversations programmatically for testing conversational AI agents using defined goals, personas, and context, without relying on manual test creation or production data.
* [Conversation Simulation (MLflow)](/concepts/conversationsimulator.md) - A technique in MLflow for programmatically generating synthetic multi-turn conversations to test conversational AI agents without manual creation or production data.
* [Conversation Simulation for Evaluation](/concepts/conversation-simulation-for-evaluation.md) - Approach that generates new conversations by simulating user interactions with defined test cases, goals, and personas to test agent versions systematically.
* [Conversation Stopping Conditions](/concepts/conversation-stopping-conditions.md) - Rules that determine when a simulated conversation ends: reaching the max_turns limit or detecting the user's goal has been accomplished.
* [Conversation Termination Conditions](/concepts/conversation-termination-conditions.md) - Rules that stop simulated conversations when max turns are reached or the simulator detects the user's goal has been achieved.
* [ConversationSimulator](/concepts/conversationsimulator.md) - MLflow API class that generates synthetic multi-turn conversations for testing conversational AI agents by defining test scenarios and simulating realistic user interactions.
* [ConversationSimulator API](/concepts/conversationsimulator.md) - The MLflow class (mlflow.genai.simulators.ConversationSimulator) that orchestrates multi-turn conversation simulation, accepting test cases and configuration parameters like max_turns, temperature, and user_model.
* [Conversion Caveats and File Visibility](/concepts/conversion-caveats-and-file-visibility.md) - Post-conversion, untracked Parquet files become invisible to Delta Lake and can be deleted by VACUUM; writes must go through Delta Lake; multiple external tables sharing the same directory face access issues.
* [CONVERT TO DELTA](/concepts/convert-to-delta.md) - A SQL command that transforms an existing directory of Parquet files into a Delta table in a single operation.
* [CONVERT TO DELTA Command](/concepts/convert-to-delta-command.md) - A SQL command that converts existing Apache Parquet or Iceberg tables to Delta tables in-place by creating a Delta Lake transaction log.
* [CONVERT TO DELTA SQL Command](/concepts/convert-to-delta-sql-command.md) - A one-time SQL command that converts Parquet and Apache Iceberg tables to Delta Lake tables on Databricks.
* [CONVERT TO DELTA Statistics and NO STATISTICS Option](/concepts/convert-to-delta-statistics-and-no-statistics-option.md) - The command automatically collects statistics on data files during conversion for query performance; the NO STATISTICS keyword bypasses this to finish faster.
* [CONVERT_TO_DELTA Duplicate Column Validation](/concepts/convert_to_delta-duplicate-column-validation.md) - The validation that occurs when converting an existing data source to Delta format, which checks for duplicate columns and raises DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND if found.
* [Converting Iceberg tables to Delta Lake](/concepts/converting-iceberg-tables-to-delta-lake.md) - One-time conversion of Apache Iceberg tables to Delta Lake with specific version and feature limitations
* [Converting managed and external tables in Unity Catalog](/concepts/converting-managed-and-external-tables-in-unity-catalog.md) - Process for converting Hive metastore Parquet tables to Unity Catalog Delta Lake tables using CONVERT TO DELTA or CTAS
* [Converting Parquet directories to Delta Lake](/concepts/converting-parquet-directories-to-delta-lake.md) - Converting directories of Parquet data files stored in external locations into Delta Lake tables
* [COPY INTO (Databricks SQL)](/concepts/copy-into-delta-lake.md) - A SQL command for loading data from file locations into Delta tables, supporting retryable and idempotent ingestion.
* [COPY INTO (Databricks)](/concepts/copy-into-delta-lake.md) - A SQL command for idempotently loading data from file storage into Delta tables, skipping previously loaded files.
* [COPY INTO (Delta Lake)](/concepts/copy-into-delta-lake.md) - A SQL command in Databricks that loads data from file locations into Delta tables with idempotent, retryable semantics.
* [COPY INTO BY POSITION](/concepts/copy-into-by-position.md) - A syntax option for matching source columns to target Delta table columns by ordinal position rather than by name, only supported for headerless CSV files.
* [COPY INTO Column Mapping (BY POSITION)](/concepts/copy-into-column-mapping-by-position.md) - Syntax for matching source columns to target Delta table columns by ordinal position, supporting headerless CSV and explicit column name lists.
* [COPY INTO Command](/concepts/copy-into-command.md) - A SQL command in Databricks that loads data from file locations into Delta tables with retryable and idempotent semantics.
* [COPY INTO Concurrency Model](/concepts/copy-into-concurrency-model.md) - Concurrent COPY INTO invocations are supported against the same table as long as they operate on distinct input file sets
* [COPY INTO COPY_OPTIONS](/concepts/copy-into-copy_options.md) - Configuration options for COPY INTO including force (disable idempotency) and mergeSchema (evolve schema for incoming data).
* [COPY INTO File Selection (FILES and PATTERN)](/concepts/copy-into-file-selection-files-and-pattern.md) - Two mutually exclusive mechanisms for selecting which files to ingest: listing specific file names (FILES, up to 1000) or using a glob pattern (PATTERN).
* [COPY INTO FORCE and mergeSchema Options](/concepts/copy-into-force-and-mergeschema-options.md) - Copy options to disable idempotency (force) or enable automatic schema evolution (mergeSchema) during ingestion.
* [COPY INTO Source Access with Credentials](/concepts/copy-into-source-access-with-credentials.md) - Mechanisms for providing access to source file locations via named storage credentials, inline temporary credentials, or Unity Catalog external locations.
* [COPY INTO Source Credential Options](/concepts/copy-into-source-credential-options.md) - Mechanisms for providing access to source file locations including named credentials, inline temporary credentials, and external locations via Unity Catalog
* [COPY INTO Source Credentials](/concepts/copy-into-source-credentials.md) - Methods for providing access credentials to source storage locations in COPY INTO, including named credentials, inline temporary credentials, and external locations via Unity Catalog.
* [COPY INTO Source Credentials & Encryption](/concepts/copy-into-source-credentials-encryption.md) - Mechanisms for providing temporary or named credentials and encryption options to access source file locations during COPY INTO operations.
* [COPY INTO VALIDATE](/concepts/copy-into-validate.md) - A validation-only mode of COPY INTO that checks whether data can be parsed, schema matches, and constraints are met without writing to the table.
* [COPY INTO VALIDATE Mode](/concepts/copy-into-validate-mode.md) - A validation mode that checks parseability, schema compatibility, and constraints without writing data; supports row-limited previews.
* [COPY INTO with SELECT Expressions](/concepts/copy-into-with-select-expressions.md) - The ability to specify SELECT expressions including window operations and global aggregates when loading source data into a Delta table
* [copy_model_version API](/concepts/copy_model_version.md) - An MLflow API method used to copy a specific model version from a source registry (workspace) to a destination registry (Unity Catalog)
* [copy_model_version()](/concepts/copy_model_version.md) - An MLflow API function (≥3.4.0) that copies a model version from the Workspace Model Registry to a destination Unity Catalog model, creating the destination model if it doesn't exist.
* [Copying Experiments Between Databricks Workspaces](/concepts/copying-experiments-between-databricks-workspaces.md) - MLflow experiments can be migrated between workspaces using the community-driven MLflow Export-Import open source tool, enabling cross-workspace collaboration, cloning experiments, and backup of experiments and models.
* [Core Schema Notation (CSN)](/concepts/core-schema-notation-csn.md) - A schema description format used to describe Databricks Delta Shares to SAP BDC so that SAP BDC users can read and understand the shared data assets.
* [Core Schema Notation (CSN) for SAP BDC](/concepts/core-schema-notation-csn.md) - A schema notation used to describe data shares to SAP BDC so that SAP BDC users can read and interpret the shared data
* [Correctness Judge](/concepts/correctness-judge.md) - An MLflow built-in LLM judge that evaluates whether a GenAI application's response is factually correct by comparing it against ground truth information (expected_facts or expected_response).
* [Correctness LLM Judge](/concepts/correctness-judge.md) - A built-in LLM judge that evaluates whether a GenAI application's response is factually correct by comparing it against provided ground truth information (expected_facts or expected_response).
* [Correctness Scorer](/concepts/correctness-scorer.md) - An MLflow GenAI evaluation scorer that measures the correctness of model outputs against expected responses, used as a feedback signal during prompt optimization.
* [Correctness Scorer for Fact Coverage](/concepts/correctness-scorer-for-fact-coverage.md) - A built-in MLflow scorer that evaluates whether LLM outputs contain expected facts defined in the evaluation dataset, measuring factual completeness.
* [Correctness Scorer for Factual Accuracy](/concepts/correctness-scorer-for-factual-accuracy.md) - A built-in MLflow scorer that checks whether generated output covers expected facts defined in the evaluation dataset.
* [Correctness Scorer for GenAI Evaluation](/concepts/correctness-scorer-for-genai-evaluation.md) - MLflow scorer that compares GenAI app outputs against expert-provided expected responses to produce quantitative correctness metrics.
* [Correctness Scorer in MLflow GenAI](/concepts/correctness-scorer.md) - An evaluation scorer that assesses whether model outputs match expected responses based on provided ground-truth labels and facts
* [CORS Configuration for Managed Volumes](/concepts/cors-configuration-for-managed-volumes.md) - Cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) configuration required on the S3 bucket to enable Databricks to upload data to managed volumes in Unity Catalog.
* [CORS configuration for Unity Catalog managed volumes](/concepts/cors-configuration-for-managed-volumes.md) - Cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) settings that must be applied to the S3 bucket to enable Databricks to upload data to managed volumes in Unity Catalog.
* [Cost Optimization for Online Feature Stores](/concepts/cost-optimization-for-online-feature-stores.md) - Best practices for managing online store costs including reusing stores across projects, right-sizing capacity (starting at CU_2), and deleting unused stores that continuously incur costs.
* [Covariates (External Regressors) for Forecasting](/concepts/covariates-external-regressors-for-forecasting.md) - Additional variables outside the target time series that can improve forecasting model accuracy by providing external context.
* [Covariates (External Regressors) in Time Series Forecasting](/concepts/covariates-external-regressors-for-forecasting.md) - Additional variables outside the target time series that can improve forecasting model accuracy by providing exogenous information.
* [Covariates in Time Series Forecasting](/concepts/covariates-in-time-series-forecasting.md) - External regressors — additional variables outside the target time series that can improve forecasting model accuracy.
* [CPU Offloading](/concepts/cpu-offloading.md) - A memory optimization technique in DeepSpeed that offloads optimizer states or parameters from GPU memory to CPU memory to reduce GPU memory pressure
* [CPU Offloading in DeepSpeed](/concepts/cpu-offloading.md) - A feature of DeepSpeed ZeRO that allows offloading optimizer states, gradients, or parameters to CPU memory to reduce GPU memory usage during large model training.
* [CPU Resource Planning for Locust Tests](/concepts/cpu-resource-planning-for-locust-tests.md) - Locust performance scales at approximately 4000 requests per second per CPU core, and the framework auto-detects available cores via the --processes -1 flag to maximize throughput.
* [CPU-Constrained Load Test Orchestration](/concepts/cpu-constrained-load-test-orchestration.md) - Locust's throughput is limited by CPU resources at roughly 4000 requests per second per core; the test notebook uses the --processes -1 flag to auto-detect and utilize all available CPU cores on the driver, and recommends CPU-optimized instances with at least 32 cores for the test cluster.
* [CRC Checksum Corruption Detection](/concepts/crc-checksum-corruption-detection.md) - The ability of FSCK REPAIR TABLE to detect CRC (Cyclic Redundancy Check) checksum corruption in Delta Lake checkpoint files (.checkpoint.parquet).
* [CRC checksum corruption in Delta Lake](/concepts/crc-checksum-corruption-in-delta-lake.md) - Corruption of CRC checksum in Delta Lake checkpoint files detected by FSCK REPAIR TABLE
* [CREATE BLOOM FILTER INDEX](/concepts/create-bloom-filter-index.md) - The deprecated SQL command for creating Bloom filter indexes on Delta tables; no longer recommended for use.
* [CREATE BLOOM FILTER INDEX (Databricks SQL)](/concepts/create-bloom-filter-index.md) - The SQL command syntax and usage for creating Bloom filter indexes in Databricks, now deprecated and not recommended for new use.
* [CREATE BLOOM FILTER INDEX Statement](/concepts/create-bloom-filter-index.md) - The deprecated SQL statement used to create Bloom filter indexes on Delta tables in Databricks.
* [CREATE OR REPLACE CLONE](/concepts/create-or-replace-clone.md) - A variant of the CLONE statement that replaces an existing table if it exists or creates a new one if it does not (without CREATE OR, the target table must already exist).
* [CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE CLONE](/concepts/create-or-replace-table-clone.md) - A DDL syntax variant that creates a new cloned table or replaces an existing one at the target location.
* [CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE CLONE Syntax](/concepts/create-or-replace-table-clone-syntax.md) - A SQL syntax variant for cloning that either replaces an existing target table or creates a new one if it does not exist.
* [CREATE RECIPIENT Privilege](/concepts/create-recipient-privilege.md) - A Unity Catalog privilege required to create recipient objects in OpenSharing, granted at the metastore level.
* [CREATE RECIPIENT Privilege and Command](/concepts/create-recipient-privilege-and-command.md) - A privilege required to create recipients in Unity Catalog, and the SQL command used to create recipient objects for Delta Sharing
* [CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) for Migration](/concepts/create-table-as-select-ctas-for-migration.md) - Alternative method to create Unity Catalog managed tables by querying Hive tables with SELECT, allowing column/row filtering during migration.
* [CREATE TABLE AS SELECT Workaround](/concepts/create-table-as-select-workaround.md) - The CREATE TABLE AS SELECT statement is not supported in Databricks Connect for Scala; the recommended alternative is spark.sql(...).write.saveAsTable(...).
* [CREATE TABLE CLONE for Migration](/concepts/create-table-clone-for-migration.md) - Method using DEEP CLONE to copy managed Delta tables from Hive metastore to Unity Catalog managed tables; table history and time travel are not preserved.
* [CREATE TABLE CLONE Syntax](/concepts/create-table-clone-syntax.md) - The SQL syntax for cloning tables in Databricks, including CREATE TABLE, CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE, IF NOT EXISTS, SHALLOW/DEEP CLONE, TBLPROPERTIES, and LOCATION clauses.
* [Create Training Set with Point-in-Time Features](/concepts/create-training-set-with-point-in-time-features.md) - Workflow for using declaratively defined features to compute point-in-time aggregated training datasets via create_training_set, ensuring temporal consistency between feature values and labels.
* [create_training_set for Point-in-Time Features](/concepts/create_training_set-for-point-in-time-features.md) - An API that computes point-in-time correct feature values for ML training by joining labeled data with feature definitions, ensuring no data leakage by aligning timestamps.
* [Creating and Managing Tables with Databricks Connect](/concepts/creating-and-managing-tables-with-databricks-connect.md) - The pattern of creating in-memory DataFrames locally, saving them as managed tables on a remote Databricks cluster, querying them, and cleaning up via Databricks Connect for Scala.
* [Creating and persisting DataFrames to Databricks tables](/concepts/creating-and-persisting-dataframes-to-databricks-tables.md) - The end-to-end workflow of creating in-memory Spark DataFrames, saving them as managed tables via saveAsTable(), querying them with Spark SQL, and cleaning up with DROP TABLE.
* [Creating and Running JAR Tasks in Databricks Jobs](/concepts/creating-and-running-jar-tasks-in-databricks-jobs.md) - How to configure a Databricks job to run a JAR as a serverless task, including main class, parameters, and environment setup
* [Creating and saving DataFrames as tables with Databricks Connect](/concepts/creating-and-saving-dataframes-as-tables-with-databricks-connect.md) - Pattern for creating in-memory DataFrames, saving them as persistent tables on a Databricks cluster, querying them, and cleaning up
* [Creating MLflow Experiments on Databricks](/concepts/creating-mlflow-experiments-on-databricks.md) - Experiments can be created via the Databricks workspace UI, the Experiments page (with presets for Foundation Model Fine-tuning, AutoML forecasting/classification/regression, or Custom), the MLflow API, or the Databricks Terraform provider.
* [Creator Identity for Model Serving](/concepts/creator-identity-for-model-serving.md) - The recorded identity (typically a service principal) that created an endpoint, used to access Unity Catalog resources; cannot be changed after creation and must remain a workspace member.
* [Creator Identity for Serving Endpoints](/concepts/creator-identity-for-serving-endpoints.md) - The recorded caller identity (typically a service principal) that owns a model serving endpoint, used for Unity Catalog access; it is immutable after creation and must remain a workspace member for the endpoint lifetime.
* [Credential File (Delta Sharing)](/concepts/credential-file-delta-sharing.md) - A file containing the bearer token that a recipient downloads once via an activation link, treated as a secret and used to authenticate and get read access to shared tables.
* [Credential file (OpenSharing)](/concepts/credential-file-opensharing.md) - A file downloaded via an activation link in the Databricks-to-Open sharing model, used for bearer token authentication; downloadable only once and should be stored securely.
* [Credential File Authentication](/concepts/credential-file-authentication.md) - A bearer-token-based authentication method for Databricks-to-Open sharing, where recipients download a credential file (one-time) from an activation URL to access shared data; tokens are valid for up to one year.
* [Credential File-Based Access Model](/concepts/credential-file-based-access-model.md) - A security model where data providers share a credential file containing endpoints and bearer tokens, which recipients use to gain read access to shared datasets.
* [Credential Injection for External Model Endpoints](/concepts/credential-injection-for-external-model-endpoints.md) - Using secrets-based environment variables in Databricks Model Serving to pass credentials to external services like OpenAI and LangChain models, enabling secure access from serving endpoints.
* [CrewAI Knowledge Source Integration](/concepts/crewai-knowledge-source-integration.md) - CrewAI agents can attach knowledge sources (e.g., StringKnowledgeSource) with metadata for contextual awareness
* [CrewAI Synchronous Execution Limitation](/concepts/crewai-synchronous-execution-limitation.md) - MLflow CrewAI integration only supports synchronous task execution; async tasks and kickoff are not traced
* [CrewAI Task and Agent Tracing](/concepts/crewai-task-and-agent-tracing.md) - MLflow captures nested traces for CrewAI tasks, agents, LLM calls, memory ops, and exceptions
* [Cross-Catalog Joins Between Hive Metastore and Unity Catalog](/concepts/cross-catalog-joins-between-hive-metastore-and-unity-catalog.md) - Using three-level namespace notation, users can join tables across the legacy Hive metastore and Unity Catalog, though such joins only work on the workspace where the Hive metastore data resides.
* [Cross-Framework Trace Consolidation](/concepts/cross-framework-trace-consolidation.md) - The practice of routing traces from multiple instrumentation frameworks (Langfuse, OpenTelemetry SDKs, etc.) to a single Databricks MLflow backend for unified querying, comparison, and governance.
* [Cross-Geo Data Processing](/concepts/cross-geo-data-processing.md) - Databricks may process Foundation Model API request data outside the originating region/cloud provider; workspaces in non-EU/US regions must enable cross-Geo data processing to access certain models.
* [Cross-Geo Data Processing (Databricks)](/concepts/cross-geo-data-processing.md) - A mechanism allowing Foundation Model API requests to be processed outside the workspace's home region and cloud provider, required for workspaces in non-US/non-EU Model Serving regions.
* [Cross-Geo Data Processing for Foundation Model APIs](/concepts/cross-geo-data-processing.md) - Databricks may process Foundation Model API request data outside the region and cloud provider where it originated; workspaces outside US/EU regions must enable cross-Geo data processing to access certain models.
* [Cross-Geography Routing for Global Endpoints](/concepts/cross-geography-routing-for-global-endpoints.md) - Some hosted models require enabling cross-geography routing to be accessible via global endpoints, as noted for OpenAI GPT-5.1 Codex models and Google Gemini 3 Pro Preview.
* [Cross-model hyperparameter comparison](/concepts/cross-model-hyperparameter-comparison.md) - Technique of tuning hyperparameters for multiple model types (e.g., different scikit-learn models) in parallel to select the best overall model
* [Cross-model hyperparameter optimization](/concepts/cross-model-hyperparameter-comparison.md) - The technique of tuning hyperparameters across multiple distinct model types (e.g., different scikit-learn classifiers) and comparing their best configurations to select an overall best model.
* [Cross-Model Hyperparameter Tuning](/concepts/cross-model-hyperparameter-tuning.md) - A workflow that tunes hyperparameters for multiple distinct model types simultaneously and selects the best overall model, rather than tuning a single model type.
* [Cross-Workspace Access for Feature Tables](/concepts/cross-workspace-access-for-feature-tables.md) - Feature tables, functions, and models are automatically available across any workspace that has access to the Unity Catalog.
* [Cross-workspace Feature Access](/concepts/cross-workspace-feature-access.md) - Feature tables, functions, and models are automatically available across any workspace that has access to the Unity Catalog catalog
* [Cross-Workspace Feature Sharing](/concepts/cross-workspace-feature-sharing.md) - The ability to discover and share feature tables across different Databricks workspaces through Unity Catalog integration.
* [Cross-workspace Feature Store Access with Tokens](/concepts/cross-workspace-feature-store-access-with-tokens.md) - Mechanism for accessing a centralized feature store from a local workspace using personal access tokens stored in the local workspace's secret manager, with each API request including the token.
* [Cross-Workspace Model Performance](/concepts/cross-workspace-model-performance.md) - Capability to view model performance metrics across all MLflow experiments and workspaces on a single page within the model registry UI.
* [CrossValidator](/concepts/crossvalidator.md) - A Spark MLlib model tuning tool that, when used with MLlib automated MLflow tracking, automatically logs hyperparameter settings and evaluation metrics to MLflow as nested runs.
* [CrossValidator and TrainValidationSplit](/concepts/crossvalidator.md) - Spark MLlib model tuning utilities whose hyperparameter search results are automatically logged as nested MLflow runs when automated tracking is enabled.
* [CrossValidator and TrainValidationSplit MLflow Integration](/concepts/crossvalidator.md) - The two Spark MLlib model tuning estimators that, when used with MLlib automated MLflow tracking, automatically log tuning results as nested MLflow runs without requiring explicit API calls.
* [CTAS for Migrating Hive Tables to Unity Catalog Managed Delta Tables](/concepts/ctas-for-migrating-hive-tables-to-unity-catalog-managed-delta-tables.md) - Using CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) to convert legacy Hive metastore managed Parquet tables to managed Unity Catalog Delta Lake tables.
* [CUDA Error Troubleshooting for Training](/concepts/cuda-error-troubleshooting-for-training.md) - Common CUDA errors during model training (out-of-memory, kernel errors) and recommended solutions such as reducing batch size, lower precision, and GPU memory cleanup.
* [CUDA Out of Memory (OOM) Mitigation for Transformers Training](/concepts/cuda-out-of-memory-oom-mitigation-for-transformers-training.md) - A collection of strategies to resolve CUDA out-of-memory errors during single-GPU training, including reducing batch size, using FP16 precision, gradient accumulation steps, 8-bit Adam optimizer, and GPU memory cleanup.
* [CUDA Out of Memory Troubleshooting for Transformers Training](/concepts/cuda-out-of-memory-troubleshooting-for-transformers-training.md) - Common CUDA out-of-memory errors during Hugging Face training and resolution strategies
* [CUDA Peer Access Error on AWS G5 GPUs](/concepts/cuda-peer-access-error-on-aws-g5-gpus.md) - A specific distributed training error (peer access not supported) encountered on AWS G5 GPU instances, resolved by disabling NCCL P2P
* [CURRENT_METASTORE](/concepts/current_metastore.md) - A Databricks default SQL function that returns the sharing identifier of the attached Unity Catalog metastore
* [CURRENT_METASTORE SQL Function](/concepts/current_metastore.md) - A Databricks SQL function that returns the sharing identifier of the current Unity Catalog metastore, used by recipients to discover their metastore identity for sharing.
* [Custom Agents Deployment for GenAI on Databricks](/concepts/custom-agents-deployment-for-genai-on-databricks.md) - The recommended deployment method for GenAI applications on Databricks that provides automatic MLflow Tracing with minimal configuration by wrapping agent code with MLflow ResponsesAgent and deploying via agents.deploy().
* [Custom Alert Notification Templates](/concepts/custom-alert-notification-templates.md) - The ability to customize email notification templates for anomaly detection alerts in Databricks SQL using HTML, allowing detailed table-level information to be included in alert emails.
* [Custom Alignment Optimizer](/concepts/custom-alignment-optimizer.md) - An extensible framework allowing users to implement specialized alignment strategies by extending the AlignmentOptimizer base class with custom optimization logic.
* [Custom Classifier (Databricks Unity Catalog)](/concepts/custom-classifier-databricks.md) - A custom classifier extends Databricks' built-in data classification system to detect organization-specific sensitive data (e.g., employee IDs, product codes) by associating a governed tag with example columns.
* [Custom Classifier (Databricks)](/concepts/custom-classifier-databricks.md) - User-defined classifier that extends Databricks Data Classification to detect organization-specific sensitive data using governed tags and example columns.
* [Custom Classifier Lifecycle and Limits](/concepts/custom-classifier-lifecycle-and-limits.md) - Rules governing custom classifier creation, editing, deletion, and enforcement — including the 50-classifier metastore limit, 1-10 example column requirement, immutability of governed tag after creation, and metastore-wide scope.
* [Custom Classifier Lifecycle Management](/concepts/custom-classifier-lifecycle-management.md) - The process of creating, editing, deleting, and troubleshooting custom classifiers, including handling suspended classifiers and understanding detection timing.
* [Custom Classifier Limitations](/concepts/custom-classifier-limitations.md) - Technical constraints include a maximum of 50 classifiers per metastore, 1-10 example columns each, no per-catalog scoping, immutable tag association after creation, and application only to subsequent scans.
* [Custom Classifier Limitations (Databricks)](/concepts/custom-classifier-limitations-databricks.md) - Constraints on custom classifiers including a 50-classifier-per-metastore cap, 1-10 example column requirement, immutable tag assignment, and metastore-wide application scope.
* [Custom classifier limitations and scoping](/concepts/custom-classifier-limitations-and-scoping.md) - Operational constraints including 50-classifier metastore limit, 1-10 example column requirement, global metastore-wide application, and tag immutability after creation.
* [Custom Classifier Requirements and Permissions](/concepts/custom-classifier-requirements-and-permissions.md) - To use custom classifiers, users must be metastore admins, have ASSIGN privileges on the governed tag, SELECT on example columns, and have Data Classification enabled with serverless compute available.
* [Custom Classifier Suspension](/concepts/custom-classifier-suspension.md) - Automatic suspension mechanism that halts detection for a custom classifier when rule generation or validation fails due to inaccessible columns, unrepresentative data, or invalid governed tags.
* [Custom Classifier Suspension and Troubleshooting](/concepts/custom-classifier-suspension-and-troubleshooting.md) - When rule generation or validation fails, classifiers are suspended with no new detections; common causes include deleted tables, unrepresentative examples, or invalid tags.
* [Custom Classifiers](/concepts/custom-classifiers.md) - User-defined classifiers that extend Databricks' built-in data classification to detect organization-specific sensitive data types like internal employee IDs, proprietary product codes, and partner account numbers.
* [Custom Classifiers for Data Classification](/concepts/custom-classifiers.md) - User-defined classifiers that extend Databricks' built-in data classification system to detect organization-specific sensitive data types such as internal employee IDs, proprietary product codes, or partner account numbers.
* [Custom classifiers for Databricks Data Classification](/concepts/custom-classifiers.md) - User-defined classifiers that extend built-in data classification to detect organization-specific sensitive data by associating governed tags with example columns.
* [Custom Code-Based Scorers (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/code-based-scorers-in-mlflow.md) - User-defined Python functions annotated with @scorer that compute flexible evaluation metrics for GenAI applications in MLflow.
* [Custom CPU Model Serving as Alternative Deployment](/concepts/custom-cpu-model-serving-as-alternative-deployment.md) - Alternative deployment method for GenAI agents on Databricks when Custom Agents cannot be used, requiring manual environment variable configuration (ENABLE_MLFLOW_TRACING, MLFLOW_EXPERIMENT_ID) and credential setup.
* [Custom dbt Profiles in Databricks Jobs](/concepts/custom-dbt-profiles-in-databricks-jobs.md) - Using a custom profiles.yml file to configure dbt task connections to SQL warehouses or all-purpose compute, with dynamic credential injection via environment variables.
* [Custom Dependency Management in Model Serving Containers](/concepts/custom-dependency-management-in-model-serving-containers.md) - Ensuring correct specification of model dependencies including custom .whl artifacts, GPU libraries (CUDA, cuDNN), and flash-attn wheel versions to avoid build and runtime errors like ModuleNotFoundError.
* [Custom Email Notification Templates for Anomaly Alerts](/concepts/custom-email-notification-templates-for-anomaly-alerts.md) - Feature allowing customization of email notification templates for Databricks SQL alerts, using Handlebars-style template syntax with query result row iteration.
* [Custom Email Templates for Databricks Alerts](/concepts/custom-email-templates-for-databricks-alerts.md) - The ability to customize email notification templates for anomaly detection alerts using HTML and query result placeholders.
* [Custom Framework Instrumentation](/concepts/custom-framework-instrumentation.md) - The capability to add tracing to proprietary or internal frameworks, custom LLM wrappers, and new libraries that lack official MLflow integration.
* [Custom HTTP Headers for Proxy in Databricks Connect](/concepts/custom-http-headers-in-databricks-connect.md) - Using the `header()` method on DatabricksSession.builder to add custom HTTP headers for proxy services that sit between the client and the Databricks cluster.
* [Custom HTTP Headers in Databricks Connect](/concepts/custom-http-headers-in-databricks-connect.md) - Using the `header()` method on DatabricksSession.builder to add custom HTTP headers to gRPC requests, useful when a proxy is placed between the client and the Databricks cluster.
