* [Custom HTTP Headers in Databricks Connect Proxy Configuration](/concepts/custom-http-headers-in-databricks-connect.md) - Using the header() method to add custom HTTP headers for proxy servers when using Databricks Connect with gRPC over HTTP/2.
* [Custom Hyperparameters (HYPERPARAMETERS_PATH)](/concepts/custom-hyperparameters-hyperparameters_path.md) - Passing structured YAML configuration to training scripts via the `parameters` field in the workload YAML, accessible at runtime through the `HYPERPARAMETERS_PATH` environment variable.
* [Custom Judge Creation with make_judge](/concepts/custom-judge-creation-with-make_judge.md) - API for building custom multi-turn LLM judges using make_judge, with access to the full conversation history via the {{ conversation }} template variable.
* [Custom Judge Metrics with make_judge](/concepts/custom-judge-metrics-with-make_judge.md) - Creating domain-specific evaluation criteria using the make_judge API to assess LLM outputs against custom requirements like sentence count compliance.
* [Custom Judge Model Selection](/concepts/custom-judge-model-selection.md) - The ability to override MLflow's default judge LLM with any LiteLLM-compatible model, specified using the format '<provider>:/<model-name>', enabling tailored evaluation.
* [Custom Judge Scorers](/concepts/custom-judge-scorers.md) - Custom evaluation metrics created using MLflow's make_judge API with custom prompt instructions to assess specific criteria like sentence count compliance.
* [Custom Judge Scorers with make_judge](/concepts/custom-judge-scorers-with-make_judge.md) - Creating bespoke evaluation metrics by defining a judge LLM with custom instructions and feedback types via mlflow.genai.make_judge.
* [Custom Judges](/concepts/custom-judges.md) - Domain-specific evaluation judges created with make_judge() that define specialized evaluation logic and can be aligned to human preferences.
* [Custom Judges (LLM-based Scorers)](/concepts/custom-judges-llm-based-scorers.md) - LLM-based scorers that evaluate GenAI agent outputs for specific quality criteria like issue resolution, expected behaviors, and tool usage
* [Custom Judges (make_judge)](/concepts/custom-judges-make_judge.md) - LLM-based scorers created with `make_judge()` that evaluate GenAI agent outputs against user-defined quality criteria, returning `mlflow.entities.Feedback` objects.
* [Custom Labeling Schema Creation](/concepts/custom-labeling-schema-creation.md) - The process of defining custom labeling schemas via the MLflow UI or Python API, specifying name, type, title, input type, and optional instructions for tailored human feedback collection.
* [Custom Libraries for Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/custom-libraries-for-databricks-model-serving.md) - Pattern for including custom Python libraries or libraries from private mirrors when deploying ML models to Databricks Model Serving endpoints
* [Custom LLM Entrypoint and MLflow Logging](/concepts/custom-llm-entrypoint-and-mlflow-logging.md) - The technique of logging a custom LLM with a custom entrypoint in MLflow, specifying the task as llm/v1/chat and configuring the vLLM server to listen on port 8080 for Model Serving compatibility.
* [Custom LLM Judge](/concepts/custom-llm-judge.md) - User-defined evaluators created when built-in LLM judges do not fit a specific use case, offering more control over evaluation criteria and behavior.
* [Custom LLM Judges](/concepts/custom-llm-judges.md) - User-defined judges created with custom prompts and instructions for specialized evaluation tasks, with support for judge alignment to match human standards.
* [Custom LLM Judges (make_judge)](/concepts/custom-llm-judges-make_judge.md) - A function in MLflow that lets you define custom evaluation criteria for GenAI applications using natural language prompts, extending beyond built-in judge metrics.
* [Custom LLM Serving on Databricks](/concepts/custom-llm-serving-on-databricks.md) - A workflow for deploying custom large language models on Databricks Model Serving using a vLLM engine, supporting fine-tuned models, PEFT variants, multimodal models, and foundation models not available in FMAPI.
* [Custom LLM Serving Telemetry and Monitoring](/concepts/custom-llm-serving-telemetry-and-monitoring.md) - Observability infrastructure for custom LLM serving endpoints, including live logs, persisted logs and metrics to Unity Catalog Delta tables, and automatic scraping of vLLM Prometheus metrics.
* [Custom LLM-as-a-Judge in scorers](/concepts/custom-llm-as-a-judge-in-scorers.md) - Integrating a custom or externally hosted LLM within a scorer to evaluate response quality, with full control over prompts, model selection, and feedback generation.
* [Custom LLM-as-a-Judge Integration](/concepts/custom-llm-as-a-judge-integration.md) - Using an external or custom LLM within a scorer by making API calls and parsing structured JSON output for evaluation feedback.
* [Custom LLM-based Judges (make_judge)](/concepts/custom-judges-make_judge.md) - Factory function in MLflow for creating LLM-based scorers that evaluate GenAI agents against custom quality criteria.
* [Custom Metrics and Slicing Expressions](/concepts/custom-metrics-and-slicing-expressions.md) - Advanced profiling options allowing users to define custom SQL-based metrics and slice data into subsets for granular analysis alongside default metrics.
* [Custom Metrics for Data Profiling](/concepts/custom-metrics-for-data-profiling.md) - User-defined SQL-based metrics that augment built-in profiling metrics, with configurable types (Aggregate, Derived, Drift), input columns, output types, and SQL definitions.
* [Custom Metrics in Data Profiling](/concepts/custom-metrics-in-data-profiling.md) - A feature allowing users to define custom metrics beyond the built-in summary statistics when profiling tables in Databricks.
* [Custom Metrics in Databricks Data Profiling](/concepts/custom-metrics-in-databricks-data-profiling.md) - User-defined SQL-based metrics (Aggregate, Derived, or Drift) that appear alongside built-in metrics in profiling output tables for specialized data quality analysis.
* [Custom MLflow Logging via MLFLOW_RUN_ID](/concepts/custom-mlflow-logging-via-mlflow_run_id.md) - The platform exposes the MLflow run ID via the `MLFLOW_RUN_ID` environment variable, allowing users to log custom parameters, metrics, and artifacts using the MLflow tracking API.
* [Custom MLflow PyFunc Model](/concepts/custom-mlflow-pythonmodel.md) - A pattern for packaging arbitrary Python code as an MLflow model using mlflow.pyfunc.PythonModel, requiring implementations of load_context and predict methods.
* [Custom MLflow PythonModel](/concepts/custom-mlflow-pythonmodel.md) - Pattern for packaging arbitrary Python code as an MLflow model by implementing load_context and predict methods in a class that extends mlflow.pyfunc.PythonModel
* [Custom Model Instrumentation Patterns](/concepts/custom-model-instrumentation-patterns.md) - Best practices for adding logging and OpenTelemetry instrumentation to MLflow pyfunc models, including Python logging capture, logging level configuration, and model code organization.
* [Custom Model Serving Endpoint (Databricks)](/concepts/custom-model-serving-endpoint-databricks.md) - A serving endpoint that hosts custom ML models for real-time inference on Databricks, created via UI, REST API, MLflow Deployments SDK, or Workspace Client.
* [Custom Model Serving Endpoint Support](/concepts/custom-model-serving-endpoint-support.md) - Genie Code for observability currently only supports custom model serving endpoints, not all endpoint types.
* [Custom Models (Databricks Model Serving)](/concepts/custom-models-databricks.md) - Any Python ML model or custom code deployed as a production-grade API on Databricks using CPU or GPU compute resources, logged in MLflow format and registered in Unity Catalog or workspace registry.
* [Custom Models (Databricks)](/concepts/custom-models-databricks.md) - Traditional ML models or customized Python models packaged in MLflow format, including scikit-learn, XGBoost, PyTorch, and Hugging Face transformers, registered in Unity Catalog or workspace model registry
* [Custom Models on Databricks](/concepts/custom-models-databricks.md) - Python models packaged in MLflow format (scikit-learn, PyTorch, Hugging Face, etc.) registered in Unity Catalog or Workspace Model Registry for serving
* [Custom models on Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/custom-models-databricks.md) - Any Python ML model or custom code deployable as a production-grade API on Databricks using CPU or GPU compute, logged in MLflow format and registered in Unity Catalog or workspace registry.
* [Custom Models on Model Serving](/concepts/custom-models-on-model-serving.md) - Python MLflow-packaged models (scikit-learn, PyTorch, Hugging Face, etc.) registered in Unity Catalog or Workspace Model Registry that can be deployed on Databricks for real-time and batch inference, including agent serving.
* [Custom Multi-turn Judges with make_judge](/concepts/custom-judge-creation-with-make_judge.md) - The ability to create custom multi-turn evaluation judges using MLflow's make_judge API, leveraging the {{ conversation }} template variable to inject full conversation history.
* [Custom OTel Span Attribute Visibility Limitations in MLflow](/concepts/custom-otel-span-attribute-visibility-limitations-in-mlflow.md) - Custom OTel span attributes set via span.set_attribute() outside recognized mappings are not surfaced as MLflow trace tags, though preserved on the underlying span.
* [Custom Python Code Dependencies with code_paths](/concepts/custom-python-code-dependencies-with-code_paths.md) - Including custom .py files or Python wheel files as model artifacts using the code_paths parameter so they are added to the Python path when the model is loaded.
* [Custom request and response previews](/concepts/custom-request-and-response-previews.md) - Ability to customize the Request and Response column previews in the MLflow Tracing UI using mlflow.update_current_trace() with request_preview and response_preview parameters.
* [Custom Review App Template](/concepts/custom-review-app-template.md) - An open-source, customizable frontend template for the MLflow Review App that allows teams to build tailored trace visualization, labeling interfaces, and workflows while using the same MLflow backend APIs.
* [Custom Review App UI](/concepts/custom-review-app-ui.md) - An open-source deployable template for customizing the Review App frontend with specialized trace visualizations, tailored labeling interfaces, and domain-specific workflows
* [Custom Scorer Definition in MLflow](/concepts/custom-scorer-definition-in-mlflow.md) - Pattern for defining user-provided evaluation functions using the @scorer decorator with aggregation metrics (mean, min, max) for GenAI trace evaluation.
* [Custom Scorer Definition with @scorer Decorator](/concepts/custom-scorer-definition-with-scorer-decorator.md) - The pattern of defining custom evaluation functions in MLflow GenAI using the @scorer decorator, including aggregation configuration and manual registration/starting lifecycle.
* [Custom Scorer Serialization Constraints](/concepts/custom-scorer-serialization-constraints.md) - Constraints for custom @scorer functions in production monitoring: must be defined in notebooks, self-contained with inline imports, no class-based scorers, and no type hints requiring imports.
* [Custom Scorer with Aggregations](/concepts/custom-scorer-with-aggregations.md) - Pattern to define user-defined evaluation scorers using the @scorer decorator with aggregate statistics like mean, min, and max.
* [Custom Scorers (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/custom-scorers-mlflow-genai.md) - User-defined scoring functions created with the @scorer decorator that compute metrics on model outputs (e.g., response length) and can be registered and started with sampling configs.
* [Custom scorers for LLM evaluation](/concepts/custom-scorers-for-llm-evaluation.md) - User-defined evaluation functions that go beyond built-in scorers to assess LLM outputs on criteria specific to a given use case, such as markdown formatting quality.
* [Custom Scorers for Prompt Evaluation](/concepts/custom-scorers-for-prompt-evaluation.md) - MLflow's support for custom evaluation scorers (e.g., Correctness, Safety) and aggregation functions to guide prompt optimization based on specific quality metrics.
* [Custom Spark Batch Migration](/concepts/custom-spark-batch-migration.md) - Using custom Apache Spark batch logic to migrate Parquet data to Delta Lake, offering flexibility but requiring manual tracking and handling of incremental updates.
* [Custom SSL/TLS Certificates for Databricks Connect](/concepts/ssltls-certificates-for-databricks-connect.md) - Configuring custom SSL/TLS certificates in Databricks Connect via the GRPC_DEFAULT_SSL_ROOTS_FILE_PATH environment variable
* [Custom Tools in Foundation Model APIs](/concepts/custom-tools-in-foundation-model-apis.md) - A tool type in Databricks Foundation Model APIs that allows models to return arbitrary string output (e.g., code, patches) instead of JSON-formatted function arguments, supporting grammar-based output formats for GPT-5 series models.
* [Custom Tools in Responses API](/concepts/custom-tools-in-responses-api.md) - GPT-5 series model feature allowing models to return arbitrary plain text output instead of JSON-formatted function arguments, with optional grammar-based format constraints.
* [Custom Trace ID Generation](/concepts/custom-trace-id-generation.md) - Technique for generating business-specific trace IDs by integrating MLflow tracing with existing observability systems using custom naming schemes.
* [Custom Trace UI Previews](/concepts/custom-trace-ui-previews.md) - The ability to set custom request_preview and response_preview strings via mlflow.update_current_trace() to control how traces are summarized in the MLflow UI's trace list view.
* [Custom Transformers in MLlib](/concepts/custom-transformers-in-mllib.md) - The ability to create custom pipeline transformers in Apache Spark MLlib for domain-specific feature transformations.
* [Custom vs Built-in Scorers](/concepts/custom-vs-built-in-scorers.md) - The distinction between predefined LLM judges provided by Databricks/MLflow and custom alternatives (custom LLM judges or Python code-based scorers) for more controlled evaluation.
* [Customer Service Chatbot Evaluation Patterns](/concepts/customer-service-chatbot-evaluation-patterns.md) - Real-world patterns for evaluating customer service chatbots using guidelines judges, covering tone, policy compliance, urgent situations, cancellations, and billing disputes.
* [Customer-Managed Key (CMK) Encryption for Online Feature Stores](/concepts/customer-managed-keys-for-online-feature-stores.md) - Encryption at rest for online feature stores using customer-managed keys, automatically applied when the workspace has CMK configured and the backing Lakebase project was created after CMK support became available.
* [Customer-Managed Key Encryption for Online Feature Stores](/concepts/customer-managed-keys-for-online-feature-stores.md) - Online feature stores support encryption at rest with customer-managed keys (CMK) via underlying Lakebase Autoscaling support, applying automatically when workspace CMK is configured and the store was created after CMK became available in the region.
* [Customer-Managed Key Encryption for Online Stores](/concepts/customer-managed-keys-for-online-feature-stores.md) - Encryption at rest for online feature stores using customer-managed keys, automatically applied when the workspace has CMK configured and the backing Lakebase project was created after CMK support.
* [Customer-Managed Keys (CMK) for Online Feature Stores](/concepts/customer-managed-keys-for-online-feature-stores.md) - Encryption at rest for online feature store data using a workspace-level customer-managed key, automatically applying when the workspace, online store, and region all support CMK.
* [Customer-Managed Keys for Online Feature Stores](/concepts/customer-managed-keys-for-online-feature-stores.md) - Encryption at rest support using customer-managed keys (CMK) via underlying Lakebase Autoscaling, requiring specific workspace and regional prerequisites.
* [Customizable Judge Models](/concepts/customizable-judge-models.md) - The ability to change which LLM powers a built-in judge by specifying a model in '<provider>:/<model-name>' format, compatible with LiteLLM providers including Databricks serving endpoints.
* [Customizable Judge Models in MLflow](/concepts/customizable-judge-models.md) - The ability to select which LLM powers a built-in MLflow judge by specifying a model in `<provider>:/<model-name>` format, with support for LiteLLM-compatible providers including Databricks serving endpoints.
* [Customizable Review App Template](/concepts/custom-review-app-template.md) - An open-source, deployable template that provides full frontend control over trace visualization, labeling interfaces, and workflows while using the same MLflow backend APIs and data model.
* [Customizing Autologging with Exclusive Mode](/concepts/customizing-autologging-with-exclusive-mode.md) - Technique to track additional metrics and parameters alongside autologged content by calling mlflow.autolog(exclusive=False) combined with explicit MLflow tracking calls
* [Customizing Databricks Data Profiling Dashboards](/concepts/customizing-databricks-data-profiling-dashboards.md) - The data profiling dashboard is fully customizable and shareable — users can edit date ranges, data slices, models, modify charts, add new charts, and adjust dashboard-level and chart-level parameters.
* [Customizing LLM Judge Models](/concepts/customizing-llm-judge-models.md) - The ability to swap the underlying LLM powering a built-in judge by specifying a different model via the 'model' argument using LiteLLM-compatible provider format, enabling use of custom or proprietary models for evaluation.
* [Customizing MLflow Judge Models](/concepts/customizing-mlflow-judge-models.md) - The ability to replace the default Databricks-hosted LLM powering MLflow's built-in judges with any LiteLLM-compatible model, specified in the format <provider>:/<model-name>.
* [Customizing the Judge LLM](/concepts/customizing-the-judge-llm.md) - The ability to override the default Databricks-hosted LLM that powers built-in MLflow judges by specifying a different model via the 'model' argument in a provider:/model-name format.
* [Customizing Trace Request/Response Previews](/concepts/customizing-trace-requestresponse-previews.md) - Using mlflow.update_current_trace() with request_preview and response_preview parameters to control how traces appear in the MLflow UI's trace list view.
* [Cut Cross Entropy](/concepts/cut-cross-entropy.md) - A memory-efficient loss computation technique for large language models that reduces GPU memory usage during training by avoiding materialization of the full logit matrix
* [Cut Cross-Entropy Loss](/concepts/cut-cross-entropy-loss.md) - A memory-efficient loss computation technique for large language models, reducing GPU memory usage during training
* [Dashboard access methods](/concepts/dashboard-access-methods.md) - Two ways to access the data profiling dashboard: via the Catalog Explorer UI from a table's Quality tab, or via the Dashboards sidebar listing.
* [Dashboard Customization and Parameters](/concepts/dashboard-customization-and-parameters.md) - User-editable parameters for both the entire dashboard and individual charts, allowing customization of date range, data slices, models, and chart modifications.
* [Dashboard customization and sharing](/concepts/dashboard-customization-and-sharing.md) - The ability to edit, add new charts, modify parameters, and share the data profiling dashboard like any other Databricks dashboard.
* [Dashboard Customization with Parameters (Databricks)](/concepts/dashboard-customization-and-parameters.md) - User-editable parameters at both the dashboard and individual chart level in Databricks profiling dashboards, allowing customization of date range, data slices, models, and chart selection.
* [Dashboard for Single Run Details](/concepts/dashboard-for-single-run-details.md) - A pre-built dashboard pattern that replicates the MLflow run details page, allowing filtering by experiment ID, run ID, and metric name to display tags, parameters, and metric graphs.
* [Dashboard Import and Export in Databricks](/concepts/dashboard-import-and-export-in-databricks.md) - Ability to export dashboards as JSON files and import them into a Databricks workspace for reuse and customization
* [Dashboard Refresh Mechanism](/concepts/profile-refresh-mechanism.md) - A two-step process where profile calculations must be refreshed to update metric tables, then the dashboard must be separately refreshed to reflect the new data.
* [Dashboard Refresh vs Profile Refresh](/concepts/dashboard-refresh-vs-profile-refresh.md) - A key architectural distinction in Databricks data profiling where the profile recalculation and the dashboard query are separate operations that must each be triggered independently.
* [Dashboard Widget Selectors for Data Filtering](/concepts/dashboard-widget-selectors-for-profile-data.md) - Widgets at the top of the profiling dashboard that control which data is displayed, with different selectors appearing depending on the profile type (Snapshot, Timeseries, or InferenceLog).
* [Dashboard Widget Selectors for Profile Data](/concepts/dashboard-widget-selectors-for-profile-data.md) - Interactive widgets at the top of the dashboard that control which data is displayed, with different selectors for Snapshot, Timeseries, and InferenceLog analysis types.
* [Data Certification](/concepts/data-certification.md) - The practice of labeling data assets (catalogs, schemas, tables, etc.) as meeting internal standards for accuracy, completeness, and trust, indicated by a checkmark icon in the Databricks workspace.
* [Data Certification and Deprecation](/concepts/data-certification-and-deprecation.md) - A system in Unity Catalog that allows administrators to flag data assets as certified (trusted, meeting quality standards) or deprecated (outdated, unsuitable for new use), with signals appearing in the workspace and influencing search results.
* [Data Certification and Deprecation Signals](/concepts/data-certification-and-deprecation.md) - System tags that mark data assets as certified (trusted, meeting quality standards) or deprecated (outdated, unsuitable for new use) to provide inline trust signals.
* [Data Certification in Unity Catalog](/concepts/data-certification-in-unity-catalog.md) - The practice of labeling Unity Catalog objects as 'certified' to indicate they meet internal standards for accuracy, completeness, and trust, displayed with a check mark icon.
* [Data Classification](/concepts/data-classification.md) - AI-agent-based automatic scanning of catalog data to tag sensitive information such as PII, financial data, and credentials, which can integrate with ABAC policies.
* [Data Classification (Databricks)](/concepts/data-classification-databricks.md) - A Databricks feature that detects sensitive data in tables and automatically assigns system tags to matching columns.
* [Data Classification (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/data-classification-unity-catalog.md) - Built-in system in Databricks Unity Catalog that scans tables and automatically detects sensitive data using built-in and custom classifiers.
* [Data Classification Billing and Cost Monitoring](/concepts/data-classification-billing-and-cost-tracking.md) - How to view and understand Databricks Data Classification expenses using the system.billing.usage table and usage dashboards, with initial scans costing more than incremental scans.
* [Data Classification Billing and Cost Tracking](/concepts/data-classification-billing-and-cost-tracking.md) - The ability to query Data Classification expenses via the system.billing.usage system table and usage dashboard, with initial scans costing more than subsequent incremental scans.
* [Data Classification Billing and Usage Tracking](/concepts/data-classification-billing-and-usage-tracking.md) - Usage and billing for Data Classification can be tracked via the system.billing.usage table or a usage dashboard, with initial scans being more costly than subsequent incremental scans.
* [Data Classification in Unity Catalog](/concepts/data-classification-unity-catalog.md) - The broader Databricks data classification system that automatically scans tables across catalogs to detect sensitive data using built-in and custom classifiers.
* [Data Classification Results Page](/concepts/data-classification-results-page.md) - The UI surface in Databricks Catalog Explorer where built-in and custom classifier detections are displayed and where custom classifiers are managed.
* [Data Classification Results System Table](/concepts/data-classification-system-table.md) - A system table (system.data_classification.results) storing all classification results across the metastore, including sample values, accessible only via serverless compute.
* [Data Classification Scans](/concepts/data-classification-scans.md) - The recurring scan process in Unity Catalog that detects built-in and custom classifications; new or updated classifiers only apply to subsequent scans, and results typically appear within a few hours.
* [Data Classification System Table](/concepts/data-classification-system-table.md) - The system.data_classification.results table that stores all classification results across the metastore, including sample values, accessible only via serverless compute.
* [Data Collation for Transformers](/concepts/data-collation-for-transformers.md) - Using DataCollatorWithPadding to batch input sequences of varying lengths for training and evaluation of transformer models.
* [Data Collation with DataCollatorWithPadding](/concepts/data-collation-with-datacollatorwithpadding.md) - Using Hugging Face data collators to batch input sequences with dynamic padding for training and evaluation
* [Data Completeness](/concepts/data-completeness.md) - A data quality metric measuring whether the expected number of rows were written to a table in the last 24 hours, based on historical row count analysis; tables are marked incomplete if row count falls below predicted lower bound.
* [Data Completeness (Anomaly Detection)](/concepts/data-completeness.md) - A quality metric that measures expected row counts over 24 hours using historical analysis, flagging tables with unusually low row additions
* [Data Completeness (Table Monitoring)](/concepts/data-completeness.md) - A monitoring metric that tracks the expected number of rows written to a table in a 24-hour window; tables are marked incomplete if row commits fall below the predicted lower bound.
* [Data Deprecation](/concepts/data-deprecation.md) - The practice of warning that a data asset is outdated, no longer reliable, or should not be used in new workflows, indicated by a restricted icon in the Databricks workspace.
* [Data Deprecation in Unity Catalog](/concepts/data-deprecation-in-unity-catalog.md) - The practice of labeling Unity Catalog objects as 'deprecated' to warn users that a data asset is outdated, unreliable, or should not be used in new workflows, displayed with a restricted icon.
* [Data Discovery in Unity Catalog](/concepts/data-discovery-in-unity-catalog.md) - The capability for users across an organization to find, understand, and trust data assets in Databricks Unity Catalog, shaped by administrator-defined metadata, trust signals, and organization.
* [Data Drift Detection](/concepts/data-drift-detection.md) - The practice of measuring changes in data distributions over time using Databricks profiling, with optional baseline tables and drift metrics for comparison.
* [Data Drift Measurement](/concepts/data-drift-measurement.md) - The process of measuring statistical changes in data distributions over time or relative to a baseline, including drift between successive time windows and between current data and expected values.
* [Data Drift Monitoring](/concepts/data-drift-monitoring.md) - The practice of tracking changes in data distributions over time against a baseline, detecting when data integrity, statistical distributions, or model performance shifts from expected norms.
* [Data Feature Types Supported by Databricks AutoML Forecasting](/concepts/data-feature-types-supported-by-databricks-automl-forecasting.md) - The specific column types (Numeric, Boolean, String, Timestamps, ArrayType[Numeric], DecimalType) that are supported for forecasting tasks in Databricks AutoML.
* [Data File Readability Verification](/concepts/data-file-readability-verification.md) - Direct read validation of data files to detect corruption or unreadable files in Delta Lake tables, performed by VERIFY ALL FILES mode.
* [Data Freshness](/concepts/data-freshness.md) - A data quality metric measuring how recently a table has been updated, determined by analyzing commit history to predict next commit timing; tables are marked stale if commits are unusually late.
* [Data Freshness (Anomaly Detection)](/concepts/data-freshness.md) - A quality metric that measures how recently a table has been updated, using historical commit patterns to predict expected next commit times
* [Data Freshness (Table Monitoring)](/concepts/data-freshness.md) - A monitoring metric that tracks how recently a table has been updated by analyzing commit history to predict the next commit time; tables are marked stale if a commit is unusually late.
* [Data Ingestion Strategy for Lakehouse](/concepts/data-ingestion-strategy-for-lakehouse.md) - Methods and patterns for ingesting data into the bronze layer, including Lakeflow Connect, partner tools, custom pipelines, batch/streaming ingestion, and change data capture (CDC).
* [Data leakage in feature engineering](/concepts/data-leakage-in-feature-engineering.md) - A modeling error that occurs when feature values unavailable at prediction time (e.g., future data) are included in the training set, degrading model performance.
* [Data Lineage](/concepts/data-lineage.md) - Automatic column-level tracking of how data flows across tables, notebooks, jobs, and pipelines, enabling impact analysis and origin tracing.
* [Data Lineage in Unity Catalog](/concepts/data-lineage-in-unity-catalog.md) - Automatic capture of data flow across tables, notebooks, jobs, and pipelines at the column level for tracing origins and impact analysis
* [Data Loading with Delta Lake for Deep Learning](/concepts/data-loading-with-delta-lake-for-deep-learning.md) - Using Delta Lake tables and streaming approaches (PyTorch IterableDataset, Hugging Face datasets, Ray Data) to optimize data throughput for large deep learning datasets.
* [Data Parallelism (Spark)](/concepts/data-parallelism-spark.md) - Spark excels at data parallelism — applying the same operation to each element of a large dataset — making it ideal for ETL, analytics, feature engineering, and large-scale data processing.
* [Data Pipeline Checkpointing](/concepts/data-pipeline-checkpointing.md) - Limitations of model checkpointing for data pipeline state and strategies for resuming training from the correct position in the dataset.
* [Data Pipeline Checkpointing Gap](/concepts/data-pipeline-checkpointing-gap.md) - Model checkpoints capture model and optimizer state but not the data pipeline position, so resumed runs cannot fast-forward to the exact sample where training stopped.
* [Data Pipeline State vs Model Checkpointing](/concepts/data-pipeline-state-vs-model-checkpointing.md) - Model checkpoints capture model and optimizer state but not data pipeline position, requiring explicit tracking of processed shards or epoch-boundary resumption strategies.
* [Data preparation for distributed training](/concepts/data-preparation-for-distributed-training.md) - The step of preparing data before running distributed training notebooks with HorovodRunner on Databricks.
* [Data Preparation for LLM Fine-Tuning with Hugging Face](/concepts/data-preparation-for-llm-fine-tuning-with-hugging-face.md) - Preparing data for fine-tuning open-source large language models using Hugging Face Transformers and Datasets libraries on Databricks.
* [Data Profile (Databricks)](/concepts/data-profile-databricks.md) - A monitoring construct on a Unity Catalog Delta table that tracks data quality metrics over time, supporting three profile types: TimeSeries, InferenceLog, and Snapshot.
* [Data Profile Refresh and Metric Tables](/concepts/data-profile-refresh-and-metric-tables.md) - The process of refreshing data profile metrics using create_refresh, viewing refresh history, and accessing metric tables stored as Unity Catalog tables for querying.
* [Data Profile Scheduling and Notifications](/concepts/data-profile-scheduling-and-notifications.md) - Options to run data profiling on a recurring schedule and configure email notifications for profile events, supporting up to 5 emails per event type.
* [Data Profiling](/concepts/data-profiling.md) - Summary statistics and historical metrics tracking for table data distributions, ML model performance, and inference endpoints; formerly known as Lakehouse Monitoring.
* [Data Profiling (Databricks)](/concepts/data-profiling-databricks.md) - A feature in Databricks that computes summary statistics for tables over time, enabling data quality monitoring, drift detection, and ML model performance tracking.
* [Data Profiling (Lakehouse Monitoring)](/concepts/data-profiling.md) - A feature that captures historical metrics of a table's data distribution or ML model performance, providing summary statistics, drift detection, and custom metrics to track data quality over time.
* [Data Profiling Analysis Types](/concepts/profiling-analysis-modes.md) - The three modes of analysis in Databricks data profiling: time series (temporal metrics over windows), inference (ML model performance tracking), and snapshot (single-point-in-time profiling).
* [Data profiling dashboard](/concepts/data-profiling-dashboard.md) - A dashboard automatically created by Databricks when a data profile is run, displaying key metrics computed by the profile in customizable visualizations.
* [Data Profiling Dashboard (Databricks)](/concepts/data-profiling-dashboard.md) - An auto-generated dashboard in Databricks that displays key metrics computed by a data profile run, organized into sections based on profile type.
* [Data Profiling Dashboard Filters](/concepts/data-profiling-dashboard-filters.md) - Widgets at the top of the dashboard that control which data is displayed, with different selectors for Snapshot, Timeseries, and InferenceLog analysis profiles.
* [Data profiling expense tracking](/concepts/data-profiling-expense-tracking.md) - Technique to isolate data profiling costs by filtering on a non-null table_id in the billing usage table.
* [Data Profiling in Databricks](/concepts/data-profiling-in-databricks.md) - A feature in Databricks Unity Catalog that analyzes table data to compute summary statistics, detect anomalies, and monitor data quality over time, configurable via the UI or API.
* [Data Profiling in Databricks Unity Catalog](/concepts/data-profiling-in-databricks.md) - The process of analyzing table statistics and data quality metrics using the Databricks UI within Unity Catalog, supporting profile types for general analysis, time series, and ML inference monitoring.
* [Data Profiling in Unity Catalog](/concepts/data-profiling-in-databricks.md) - The practice of analyzing and generating statistical summaries of table data within Databricks Unity Catalog to monitor data quality over time.
* [Data Profiling Limitations](/concepts/data-profiling-limitations.md) - Constraints for Databricks data profiling including supported table types (only Delta tables), regional availability, 30-day time window for time series/inference profiles, and 4TB maximum for snapshot profiles.
* [Data Profiling Limitations and Requirements](/concepts/data-profiling-limitations.md) - Constraints for Databricks data profiling including Unity Catalog enablement, supported table types (Delta only), regional availability, and size limits for snapshot profiles.
* [Data Profiling UI Workflow](/concepts/data-profiling-ui-workflow.md) - The step-by-step process in Databricks Catalog Explorer to enable, configure, schedule, refresh, view, and delete data profiles through the Quality tab interface.
* [Data provider and data recipient roles](/concepts/data-provider-and-data-recipient-roles.md) - In OpenSharing, a data provider (a Databricks user) shares data with a data recipient (a person or group outside their organization), with distinct responsibilities and workflows for each.
* [Data Quality in the Medallion Pipeline](/concepts/data-quality-in-the-medallion-pipeline.md) - A strategy for progressively enforcing data quality through constraints, primary/foreign keys, expectations, and monitoring as data moves from bronze (lenient) to silver and gold (strict).
* [Data Quality Monitoring](/concepts/data-quality-monitoring.md) - Anomaly detection across all tables in a schema and data profiling at the table level, automatically monitoring freshness and completeness using historical data patterns.
* [Data quality monitoring (Databricks)](/concepts/data-quality-monitoring-databricks.md) - A Unity Catalog feature that ensures the quality of data assets through anomaly detection and data profiling capabilities.
* [Data Quality Monitoring (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/data-quality-monitoring-databricks.md) - A Databricks capability in Unity Catalog that ensures data asset quality through anomaly detection and data profiling without modifying tables or adding job overhead.
* [Data Quality Monitoring Alert Rules](/concepts/data-quality-monitoring-alert-rules.md) - Rules that notify workspace users by email when anomaly detection identifies unhealthy tables within a catalog or schema scope.
* [Data Quality Monitoring Alerts](/concepts/data-quality-monitoring-alerts.md) - Notification system that alerts workspace users when anomaly detection identifies data quality issues like stale tables or unexpected drops in row count.
* [Data Quality Monitoring Incidents Management](/concepts/data-quality-monitoring-incidents-management.md) - Workflow for reviewing, assigning, and dismissing data quality anomalies from the UI, including auto-resolved incidents that self-heal without manual intervention.
* [Data Quality Monitoring UI](/concepts/data-quality-monitoring-ui.md) - A Databricks interface (Beta) for creating and managing anomaly detection alert rules directly within the Data Quality Monitoring page, including scope and recipient configuration.
* [Data Quality Monitoring UI (Beta)](/concepts/data-quality-monitoring-ui-beta.md) - A Beta UI feature in Databricks Catalog Explorer that provides a visual interface for viewing data quality results and managing alert rules without leaving the page.
* [Data Quality Monitoring UI (Databricks)](/concepts/data-quality-monitoring-ui-databricks.md) - A user interface for viewing anomaly detection results, incidents per schema or catalog, with tabs for unhealthy, healthy, or error tables and features for assigning and dismissing incidents.
* [Data Quality Monitoring UI Alert Management](/concepts/data-quality-monitoring-ui.md) - Beta feature for creating and managing anomaly detection alert rules directly from the Data Quality Monitoring UI in Catalog Explorer, scoped to catalogs or schemas.
* [Data Recency Constraint for MLflow System Tables](/concepts/data-recency-constraint-for-mlflow-system-tables.md) - The MLflow system tables began recording data from all regions on September 2, 2025; data before that date may not be available.
* [Data Recipient](/concepts/data-recipient.md) - A person or group outside the data provider's organization who receives and accesses shared data via OpenSharing, with read-only access.
* [Data Recipient (OpenSharing)](/concepts/data-recipient-opensharing.md) - A named object in OpenSharing that represents the identity of a user or group of users who consume shared data, identified by a sharing identifier for Databricks-to-Databricks mode.
* [Data Slicing Expressions](/concepts/data-slicing-expressions.md) - A mechanism to compute per-segment statistics by partitioning data based on column values or Boolean expressions, generating multiple slice keys and values.
* [Data Slicing in Databricks Profiling](/concepts/data-slicing-in-databricks-profiling.md) - A technique for computing profile metrics on subsets of data defined by boolean expressions or column values, enabling segmented analysis of data quality.
* [Data Sources for Features](/concepts/data-sources-for-features.md) - DeltaTableSource and RequestSource define where feature data originates: Delta tables (with optional filtering and transformations) or inference-time request payloads with a defined schema.
* [Data Splitting Strategies](/concepts/data-splitting-strategies.md) - Methods for splitting data into train, validation, and test sets: random stratified split, chronological split by time column, and manual split using a designated column.
* [Data Splitting Strategies for AutoML](/concepts/data-splitting-strategies.md) - Methods to divide data into training, validation, and test sets in AutoML: random split (default, 60/20/20), chronological split (by time column), and manual split (by designated split column).
* [Data Splitting Strategies in AutoML](/concepts/data-splitting-strategies.md) - Methods for splitting data into training, validation, and test sets, including random split, chronological split, and manual split options.
* [Data type mismatch in row filters and column masks](/concepts/data-type-mismatch-in-row-filters-and-column-masks.md) - Risks and unexpected behavior when UDF parameter types do not match table column types, including silent NULL conversion with ANSI mode disabled.
* [Databricks @distributed Decorator](/concepts/databricks-distributed-decorator.md) - A Python decorator in Databricks Serverless GPU Compute that orchestrates multi-GPU distributed training by launching a function across all GPUs with proper distributed setup.
* [Databricks @distributed Decorator for GPU Training](/concepts/databricks-distributed-decorator-for-gpu-training.md) - A decorator from the serverless_gpu library that enables execution of GPU workloads on Databricks AI Runtime, automatically provisioning GPUs (e.g., 8xH100) and handling distributed training setup.
* [Databricks @distributed Decorator for Multi-GPU Training](/concepts/databricks-distributed-decorator.md) - A decorator-based API in Databricks Serverless GPU Compute that automatically orchestrates distributed training across multiple GPUs, handling provisioning, data distribution, and synchronization.
* [Databricks Acceptable Use Policy for Models](/concepts/databricks-acceptable-use-policy-for-models.md) - A governing policy that defines acceptable and prohibited uses of models served through Databricks Model Serving.
* [Databricks Agent Evaluation Scorers](/concepts/databricks-agent-evaluation-scorers.md) - Framework from Databricks for evaluating AI agent outputs, including built-in scorers like Safety() and a lifecycle of register/start.
* [Databricks AI Runtime](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime.md) - A serverless GPU compute offering on Databricks for deep learning workloads, providing fully managed GPU infrastructure with native integration across notebooks, jobs, Unity Catalog, and MLflow.
* [Databricks AI Runtime (AI v5)](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-ai-v5.md) - A Databricks environment image pre-packaged with AI/ML libraries including Unsloth, TRL, PEFT, bitsandbytes, xformers, and einops, eliminating manual installations.
* [Databricks AI Runtime (AI v5) for LLM Workloads](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-ai-v5-for-llm-workloads.md) - A curated Databricks runtime environment (AI v5) that bundles Unsloth, TRL, PEFT, bitsandbytes, xformers, and other LLM finetuning dependencies, accessible via serverless GPU clusters with accelerator selection.
* [Databricks AI Runtime CLI (air)](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-cli-air.md) - Command-line interface for submitting and managing distributed AI/ML workloads on Databricks clusters, using commands like `air run -f train.yaml`
* [Databricks AI Runtime CLI (air) workload submission](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-cli-air.md) - CLI tool for submitting distributed training workloads to Databricks using YAML configuration with inline dependencies, compute specs, and command scripts.
* [Databricks AI Runtime CLI YAML config](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-cli-yaml-config.md) - The YAML-based configuration system used by the air CLI to define workload specifications, with field-level help accessible via '-h config'.
* [Databricks AI Runtime Environment](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-environment.md) - A pre-configured runtime environment (AI v5) on Databricks that bundles all required libraries for running serverless GPU compute workloads with H100 accelerators.
* [Databricks AI Runtime Environments](/concepts/ai-runtime-environments.md) - Two managed Python environments offered by AI Runtime: a minimal default base environment and a full-featured Databricks AI environment pre-loaded with ML frameworks like PyTorch and Transformers.
* [Databricks AI Runtime for Distributed Training](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-for-distributed-training.md) - A Databricks platform runtime that supports distributed training frameworks including DeepSpeed, DDP, and FSDP on AWS.
* [Databricks AI Runtime for GPU Fine-Tuning](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-for-gpu-fine-tuning.md) - A pre-configured Databricks environment (AI v5) that bundles Unsloth, bitsandbytes, TRL, xformers, MLflow and other dependencies, optimized for running GPU-accelerated LLM fine-tuning workloads on serverless compute with A10 accelerators.
* [Databricks AI Runtime GPU training](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-gpu-training.md) - Databricks' managed environment supporting distributed GPU training with DDP, PyTorch Lightning, and serverless GPU resources.
* [Databricks AI Runtime Observability](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-observability.md) - Overview of observability features in Databricks AI Runtime including MLflow logging, notebook output, GPU monitoring, and checkpoint management.
* [Databricks AI Runtime on AWS](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-on-aws.md) - A managed AI runtime environment on AWS for running distributed machine learning workloads, including LLM fine-tuning and GPU-based training.
* [Databricks AI Runtime release stages](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-release-stages.md) - AI Runtime features are released in different stages: single-node tasks in Public Preview, multi-GPU distributed training in Beta.
* [Databricks AI Runtime Requirements](/concepts/databricks-ai-runtime-requirements.md) - Prerequisites for using AI Runtime, including a Databricks workspace with AI Runtime enabled and Python 3.10+.
* [Databricks AI v5 Environment](/concepts/databricks-ai-v5-environment.md) - A Databricks runtime environment pre-packaged with libraries for AI/ML GPU workloads on Serverless GPU compute
* [Databricks Anomaly Detection](/concepts/databricks-anomaly-detection.md) - Automated monitoring of data quality across tables in a schema using historical pattern analysis to detect freshness and completeness anomalies
* [Databricks App Resource Permission Levels](/concepts/app-resource-permission-levels.md) - Three permission levels (Can read, Can edit, Can manage) that control an app's service principal access to a resource like an MLflow experiment.
* [Databricks App Resource Permissions](/concepts/app-resource-permission-levels.md) - Permission levels (Can read, Can edit, Can manage) that govern an app's service principal access to bound resources like MLflow experiments.
* [Databricks App Service Principal Authorization](/concepts/databricks-app-service-principal-authorization.md) - The mechanism by which Databricks grants a Databricks App's service principal specific permissions on resources like MLflow experiments, scoping access to only the explicitly added resources.
* [Databricks Apps MLflow Experiment Resource](/concepts/databricks-apps-mlflow-experiment-resource.md) - An MLflow experiment added as a first-class resource in a Databricks App, enabling experiment tracking, agent debugging, prompt management, and model training logging directly from the app.
* [Databricks Apps Resource Permission Levels](/concepts/app-resource-permission-levels.md) - Three permission tiers (Can read, Can edit, Can manage) that control what a Databricks App can do with a bound resource like an MLflow experiment, from viewing metadata to full administrative access.
* [Databricks Apps Service Principal Authorization](/concepts/databricks-apps-service-principal-authorization.md) - The mechanism by which Databricks Apps use a service principal to obtain permissions on resources — when a resource is added, the app's service principal is granted the specified permissions on that resource.
* [DATABRICKS Authentication Type](/concepts/databricks-authentication-type.md) - An authentication type for recipient objects that restricts data access to a specific Unity Catalog metastore identified by a sharing identifier, used in Databricks-to-Databricks sharing.
* [Databricks Autologging](/concepts/databricks-autologging.md) - A Databricks feature that enables automatic MLflow tracking by default on supported runtimes, including MLflow PySpark ML autologging.
* [Databricks Autologging Administration](/concepts/databricks-autologging-administration.md) - Workspace-level controls allowing administrators to enable or disable Databricks Autologging for all interactive notebook sessions across their workspace via the admin settings page, requiring cluster restart to take effect.
* [Databricks Autologging Limitations](/concepts/databricks-autologging-limitations.md) - Key constraints including that autologging is only enabled on the driver node, must be explicitly enabled on worker nodes, and the XGBoost scikit-learn integration is not supported.
* [Databricks AutoML](/concepts/databricks-automl.md) - Automated machine learning system on Databricks that trains models and generates trial notebooks for classification, regression, and forecasting tasks.
* [Databricks AutoML Experiment Lifecycle for Forecasting](/concepts/databricks-automl-experiment-lifecycle-for-forecasting.md) - The three-stage pipeline (Preprocessing, Tuning, Training) that Databricks Serverless Forecasting follows when running an AutoML experiment.
* [Databricks AutoML Feature Store Lookups](/concepts/automl-feature-store-lookups.md) - Mechanism to pass external feature tables as covariates to AutoML forecasting experiments via the feature_store_lookups parameter
* [Databricks AutoML for Classification](/concepts/databricks-automl-for-classification.md) - How to use Databricks AutoML to automatically find the best classification algorithm and hyperparameter configuration
* [Databricks AutoML Forecasting API](/concepts/databricks-automl-forecasting-api.md) - The automl.forecast() Python function that automates time series model selection, training, and evaluation on Databricks.
* [Databricks AutoML Forecasting Data Preparation](/concepts/databricks-automl-forecasting-data-preparation.md) - Process and configurable settings for preparing time series data for automated forecasting model training on Databricks.
* [Databricks AutoML Forecasting Experiment](/concepts/databricks-automl-forecasting-experiment.md) - The automated machine learning workflow in Databricks for forecasting tasks, supporting parameters like target_col, time_col, frequency, horizon, and identity_col.
* [Databricks AutoML Python API](/concepts/databricks-automl-python-api.md) - Programmatic Python interface for Databricks AutoML, providing methods to start classification, regression, and forecasting runs
* [Databricks AutoML Regression](/concepts/databricks-automl-regression.md) - Automated machine learning process for predicting continuous numeric values using Databricks AutoML
* [Databricks AutoML UI Experiment Workflow](/concepts/databricks-automl-ui-experiment-workflow.md) - End-to-end process for setting up, running, and monitoring a forecasting AutoML experiment through the Databricks UI, including compute selection, dataset browsing, and progress tracking
* [Databricks Budget Policy Permission Model](/concepts/databricks-budget-policy-permission-model.md) - The requirement that users and service principals can only assign a budget policy they are entitled to use, governing who can set serverless budget policies on experiments.
* [Databricks Built-in SQL Functions and Operators](/concepts/databricks-built-in-sql-functions-and-operators.md) - The comprehensive reference of SQL functions and operators available in Databricks, from which a security-restricted subset is chosen for use in Databricks-to-Databricks view sharing.
* [Databricks Chat Completions API](/concepts/databricks-chat-completions-api.md) - A unified API on Databricks that works across all model providers, mentioned as an alternative to the Anthropic-specific Messages API.
* [Databricks Claude Model Naming Convention](/concepts/databricks-claude-model-naming-convention.md) - The naming pattern for Anthropic Claude models hosted on Databricks, using the prefix 'databricks-claude-' followed by the model tier and version (e.g., databricks-claude-sonnet-4-5).
* [Databricks Clean Rooms](/concepts/databricks-clean-rooms.md) - Secure collaboration environments within Databricks that allow multiple parties to share and jointly analyze data, which can cause deletion blocks on shared securables.
* [Databricks CLONE SQL Syntax](/concepts/databricks-clone-sql-syntax.md) - The SQL language syntax for the CREATE TABLE CLONE and REPLACE TABLE CLONE statements, including parameters such as IF NOT EXISTS, SHALLOW/DEEP, TBLPROPERTIES, and LOCATION.
* [Databricks Cluster Access Modes and Table ACLs](/concepts/databricks-cluster-access-modes-and-table-acls.md) - Table access control is enabled by default on clusters with Standard access mode, and is not supported on Machine Learning Runtime.
* [Databricks Cluster Access Modes for Table ACLs](/concepts/databricks-cluster-access-modes-for-table-acls.md) - How table access control is enabled by default in Standard access mode clusters and configured via Spark conf settings.
* [Databricks Commitment to Open-Source ML Ecosystem](/concepts/databricks-open-source-ml-ecosystem.md) - Full support for open-source ML frameworks (scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow, etc.) with open formats via Delta Lake, open-source MLflow, and open-source Unity Catalog APIs for portability.
* [Databricks Configuration Profiles](/concepts/databricks-configuration-profiles.md) - Named configuration files used to store connection properties (host, token, cluster_id, etc.) for Databricks authentication and compute targeting
* [Databricks configuration profiles for authentication](/concepts/databricks-configuration-profiles-for-authentication.md) - Using profiles (in .databrickscfg) to store authentication fields (host, token, client_id, client_secret) and cluster_id for Databricks Connect connections.
* [Databricks Configuration Profiles for Connect](/concepts/databricks-configuration-profiles-for-connect.md) - Named configuration profiles (including DEFAULT) store connection fields like host, token, cluster_id, client_id, and client_secret for Databricks Connect authentication.
* [Databricks Connect](/concepts/databricks-connect.md) - A client library for the Databricks Runtime that enables remote Spark execution from IDEs, notebooks, and custom applications using Spark Connect.
* [Databricks Connect (Legacy)](/concepts/databricks-connect-legacy.md) - A client library that allows users to connect local IDEs and applications to Databricks clusters, running Spark jobs remotely instead of locally.
* [Databricks Connect API Portability](/concepts/databrickssession-api-portability.md) - Databricks Connect APIs are available in Databricks notebooks as part of the corresponding Databricks Runtime, allowing code to run in notebooks without any changes after local development.
* [Databricks Connect Authentication](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication.md) - Supported authentication methods for Databricks Connect including OAuth user-to-machine (U2M) and OAuth machine-to-machine (M2M), with specific configuration requirements.
* [Databricks Connect authentication defaults](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication-defaults.md) - The default authentication mechanism used by Databricks Connect client setup, configurable via environment variables such as SPARK_REMOTE
* [Databricks Connect Authentication for R](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication-for-r.md) - The authentication mechanism for Databricks Connect for R, which currently only supports Databricks personal access tokens (PAT) stored in environment variables.
* [Databricks Connect Authentication Methods](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication.md) - Supported authentication mechanisms for Databricks Connect including OAuth user-to-machine (U2M) and machine-to-machine (M2M) authentication via the Databricks SDK for Python.
* [Databricks Connect Authentication Requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication-requirements.md) - Databricks Connect supports OAuth U2M and M2M authentication; U2M requires prior authentication via Databricks CLI, and both are supported on Databricks SDK for Python 0.19.0+.
* [Databricks Connect Authentication Type Requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication-type-requirements.md) - The specific configuration fields required for each authentication type (PAT, OAuth M2M, OAuth U2M) when connecting via Databricks Connect.
* [Databricks Connect Authentication Types](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication.md) - Supported authentication methods for Databricks Connect including personal access tokens, OAuth M2M, and OAuth U2M
* [Databricks Connect Authentication via PAT](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication-via-pat.md) - Databricks Connect for R currently only supports Databricks personal access token (PAT) authentication for workspace and cluster access.
* [Databricks Connect Authentication via Personal Access Tokens](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication-via-pat.md) - The authentication mechanism for Databricks Connect for R, which currently only supports Databricks personal access tokens (PAT) for workspace authentication.
* [Databricks Connect authentication with personal access tokens](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication-via-pat.md) - Databricks Connect for R currently only supports authentication via Databricks personal access tokens (PAT), configured through environment variables DATABRICKS_HOST, DATABRICKS_TOKEN, and DATABRICKS_CLUSTER_ID.
* [Databricks Connect build file configuration](/concepts/databricks-connect-build-file-configuration.md) - How to configure build.sbt, pom.xml, or build.gradle to add the Databricks Connect library dependency matching the target Databricks Runtime version.
* [Databricks Connect Client Setup](/concepts/databricks-connect-client-setup.md) - Step-by-step process to install the databricks-connect package, uninstall conflicting PySpark, configure connection properties (workspace URL, personal access token, cluster ID, port), and test connectivity.
* [Databricks Connect Client Setup and Configuration](/concepts/databricks-connect-client-setup.md) - The step-by-step process of installing the Databricks Connect client package, configuring connection properties (workspace URL, token, cluster ID, port), and testing connectivity to a Databricks cluster.
* [Databricks Connect Client-Server Architecture](/concepts/databricks-connect-client-server-architecture.md) - Architecture where the local client sends logical command representations (e.g., DataFrames) to a Spark server on the remote Databricks cluster for execution, decoupling the client from the cluster.
* [Databricks Connect Cluster Access Modes](/concepts/databricks-connect-cluster-access-modes.md) - Target clusters for Databricks Connect must use either Assigned or Shared cluster access mode.
* [Databricks Connect cluster configuration requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-cluster-configuration-requirements.md) - The target Databricks workspace and cluster must meet specific compute configuration requirements for Databricks Connect to work, including appropriate runtime versions and cluster settings.
* [Databricks Connect cluster connectivity troubleshooting](/concepts/databricks-connect-cluster-connectivity-troubleshooting.md) - Common errors and fixes when Databricks Connect cannot reach a cluster, including checking workspace instance name, cluster ID, and cluster version compatibility.
* [Databricks Connect Cluster Disabling](/concepts/databricks-connect-service-disabling.md) - Databricks Connect and Spark Connect services can be disabled on a cluster by setting spark.databricks.service.server.enabled false in Spark configuration.
* [Databricks Connect Cluster Requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-cluster-requirements.md) - Requirements for target compute resources including cluster access modes (Assigned or Shared) and serverless compute support (starting from Databricks Connect 15.1)
* [Databricks Connect code examples and tutorials](/concepts/databricks-connect-code-examples-and-tutorials.md) - Tutorials and example applications demonstrating Databricks Connect for Python, including ETL, Plotly, and PySpark AI examples.
* [Databricks Connect Code Portability](/concepts/databrickssession-api-portability.md) - The principle that all Databricks Connect APIs are available in Databricks notebooks as part of the corresponding Databricks Runtime, enabling seamless transitions from local development to deployment without code changes.
* [Databricks Connect compute configuration](/concepts/databricks-connect-compute-configuration.md) - Configuration options for classic compute and serverless compute when using Databricks Connect.
* [Databricks Connect Configuration](/concepts/databricks-connect-configuration.md) - The process of configuring Databricks Connect including workspace URL, personal access token, cluster ID, and port settings via CLI, SQL configs, or environment variables.
* [Databricks Connect Configuration and Setup](/concepts/databricks-connect-client-setup.md) - Step-by-step process for installing the Databricks Connect client, configuring connection properties (workspace URL, personal access token, cluster ID, port), and testing connectivity.
* [Databricks Connect Configuration Precedence](/concepts/databricks-connect-configuration-precedence.md) - Databricks Connect searches for configuration properties in a defined order (remote() method, config profile, env vars, DEFAULT profile) and uses the first match.
* [Databricks Connect Configuration Profiles](/concepts/databricks-connect-configuration-profiles.md) - Using .databrickscfg configuration profiles (including DEFAULT) to store connection properties like host, token, cluster_id, and serverless_compute_id.
* [Databricks Connect Configuration Resolution Order](/concepts/databricks-connect-configuration-resolution-order.md) - The ordered sequence (5 levels) in which Databricks Connect searches for connection configuration properties, using the first match found.
* [Databricks Connect Connection String Configuration](/concepts/databricks-connect-connection-string-configuration.md) - How to configure Databricks Connect using the Spark Connect connection string via the remote() function or the SPARK_REMOTE environment variable
* [Databricks Connect Connection Validation](/concepts/databricks-connect-connection-validation.md) - Methods to validate that the environment, credentials, and compute connection are correctly configured, including the databricks-connect test command and validateSession().
* [Databricks Connect Credential Resolution](/concepts/databricks-connect-credential-resolution.md) - The mechanism by which the PySpark shell picks up default credentials from environment variables (DATABRICKS_* vars) or a DEFAULT configuration profile when started without additional parameters.
* [Databricks Connect Custom Proxy Headers](/concepts/databricks-connect-custom-proxy-headers.md) - Using the header() method to add custom HTTP headers to gRPC requests when a proxy service sits between the client and the Databricks cluster
* [Databricks Connect DataFrame Logical Plan Size Limit](/concepts/databricks-connect-dataframe-logical-plan-size-limit.md) - Databricks Connect cannot create DataFrames with an unresolved logical plan larger than 128 MB (applies to plan size, not data) for Runtime 13.3 LTS and below.
* [Databricks Connect Dependency Management](/concepts/databricks-connect-dependency-management.md) - The approach to handling dependencies where application dependencies are installed locally on the client machine and UDF dependencies are installed on the Databricks cluster.
* [Databricks Connect End-of-Support Lifecycle](/concepts/databricks-connect-support-lifecycle.md) - Databricks Connect follows the Databricks Runtime support lifecycle policy; versions that have reached end-of-support should be upgraded to a currently supported version.
* [Databricks Connect Environment Variable Configuration](/concepts/databricks-connect-environment-variable-configuration.md) - Environment variables such as DATABRICKS_CONFIG_PROFILE, DATABRICKS_CLUSTER_ID, DATABRICKS_HOST, DATABRICKS_TOKEN can configure the connection without hardcoding values.
* [Databricks Connect Execution Model](/concepts/databricks-connect-execution-model.md) - The architecture where general Python/Scala code runs locally on the client, DataFrame APIs execute on the remote Databricks compute, and UDF code is serialized and transmitted to the cluster for execution.
* [Databricks Connect Feature Limitations by Runtime Version](/concepts/databricks-connect-feature-limitations-by-runtime-version.md) - Databricks Connect functionality is gated by Databricks Runtime version, with certain features unavailable depending on the runtime version (13.3 LTS, 15.3, 16.3).
* [Databricks Connect for Python](/concepts/databricks-connect-for-python.md) - A Databricks SDK feature enabling IDEs, notebook servers, and custom applications to connect to Databricks clusters using PySpark.
* [Databricks Connect for Python Volume File Operations](/concepts/databricks-connect-for-python-volume-file-operations.md) - Example pattern demonstrating how to use Databricks SDK's WorkspaceClient and dbutils.fs to create, read, and delete files in Unity Catalog volumes.
* [Databricks Connect for R](/concepts/databricks-connect-for-r.md) - A feature enabling R IDEs like RStudio Desktop to connect to Databricks clusters using sparklyr for remote Spark execution.
* [Databricks Connect for R (sparklyr)](/concepts/databricks-connect-for-r.md) - A feature that enables connecting R IDEs like RStudio Desktop to Databricks clusters using the sparklyr package, allowing remote execution of Spark code from local R environments.
* [Databricks Connect for Scala](/concepts/databricks-connect-for-scala.md) - A tool for connecting Scala IDEs, notebook servers, and custom applications to Databricks clusters, introduced for Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS and above.
* [Databricks Connect for Scala Compatibility](/concepts/databricks-connect-for-scala-compatibility.md) - This version of Databricks Connect for Scala requires Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS and above
* [Databricks Connect for Scala installation](/concepts/databricks-connect-for-scala-installation.md) - Installation process for setting up Databricks Connect for Scala on a local development machine.
* [Databricks Connect for Scala installation methods](/concepts/databricks-connect-for-scala-installation-methods.md) - The procedures for adding Databricks Connect as a dependency in Scala projects using sbt, Maven, or Gradle build tools.
* [Databricks Connect for Scala Runtime Compatibility](/concepts/databricks-connect-for-scala-compatibility.md) - The described Databricks Utilities integration works with Databricks Connect for Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS and above.
* [Databricks Connect GitHub Examples](/concepts/databricks-connect-github-examples.md) - A GitHub repository by Databricks containing example applications demonstrating Databricks Connect usage, including ETL, Plotly visualizations, and PySpark AI.
* [Databricks Connect GitHub Examples Repository](/concepts/databricks-connect-github-examples.md) - A curated collection of example applications demonstrating Databricks Connect usage, including ETL, Plotly visualizations, and PySpark AI integrations
* [Databricks Connect GitHub repository](/concepts/databricks-connect-github-examples.md) - A GitHub repository (dbconnect-examples) containing example applications for Databricks Connect including ETL and Plotly-based interactive apps
* [Databricks Connect gRPC Communication](/concepts/databricks-connect-grpc-communication.md) - Databricks Connect communicates with Databricks Clusters via gRPC over HTTP/2, which is the underlying protocol for Spark Connect connections.
* [Databricks Connect gRPC Communication Layer](/concepts/databricks-connect-grpc-communication-layer.md) - Databricks Connect communicates with Databricks clusters via gRPC over HTTP/2, enabling advanced proxy and header customization
* [Databricks Connect gRPC Communication Protocol](/concepts/databricks-connect-grpc-communication.md) - Databricks Connect communicates with Databricks Clusters via gRPC over HTTP/2, which underlies the need for proxy configuration and certificate management.
* [Databricks Connect gRPC over HTTP/2 Communication](/concepts/databricks-connect-grpc-communication.md) - The underlying protocol used by Databricks Connect to communicate with Databricks clusters via gRPC over HTTP/2
* [Databricks Connect Hybrid Session Pattern](/concepts/databricks-connect-hybrid-session-pattern.md) - A design pattern where code checks for the DATABRICKS_RUNTIME_VERSION environment variable to decide whether to reuse an existing SparkSession (on-cluster) or create a new DatabricksSession with artifact uploads (local development).
* [Databricks Connect IDE Integration](/concepts/databricks-connect-ide-integration.md) - Integration of Databricks Connect with popular IDEs and tools including Visual Studio Code, PyCharm, JupyterLab, Classic Jupyter Notebook, RStudio Desktop, IntelliJ, Eclipse, PyDev, SBT, and Spark shell for remote development and debugging.
* [Databricks Connect IDE Integration Patterns](/concepts/databricks-connect-ide-integration.md) - The various methods for integrating Databricks Connect with popular development environments including JupyterLab, PyCharm, VS Code, IntelliJ, Eclipse, RStudio, and Spark shells.
* [Databricks Connect incompatibility with open source Apache Spark](/concepts/databricks-connect-incompatibility-with-open-source-apache-spark.md) - Databricks Connect is built against Databricks Runtime and cannot connect to open source Apache Spark servers due to dependency differences such as json4s library versions.
* [Databricks Connect Interactive Development](/concepts/databricks-connect-interactive-development.md) - The capability to interactively develop and debug Spark code from IDEs (VS Code, PyCharm, IntelliJ) and build interactive data applications using Databricks compute instead of local Spark sessions.
* [Databricks Connect Java 17 compatibility](/concepts/databricks-connect-java-17-compatibility.md) - Java 17's security restrictions block Apache Arrow reflection access needed by Databricks Connect; the fix requires setting --add-opens JVM flag.
* [Databricks Connect Language Support](/concepts/databricks-connect-language-support.md) - Databricks Connect is available for Python, R, and Scala, allowing developers to write code using Spark APIs in their preferred language and run it remotely on Databricks compute.
* [Databricks Connect library distribution via Maven Central](/concepts/databricks-connect-library-distribution-via-maven-central.md) - The Databricks Connect client library is distributed through Maven Central repository, with separate artifact coordinates for different Databricks Runtime versions.
* [Databricks Connect Limitations](/concepts/databricks-connect-limitations.md) - Known limitations of Databricks Connect including lack of support for Unity Catalog, Structured Streaming, certain Delta table operations, Koalas, table access control, and various dbutils modules.
* [Databricks Connect Limitations and Unsupported Features](/concepts/databricks-connect-limitations.md) - The documented set of features and APIs that are not supported when using Databricks Connect, including Unity Catalog, Structured Streaming, Delta table native APIs, dbutils subsets, and table access control.
* [Databricks Connect Local Environment Requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-local-environment-requirements.md) - Local development prerequisites for Databricks Connect including Python version, authentication setup (OAuth U2M/M2M), and Scala compatibility.
* [Databricks Connect Local Environment Setup](/concepts/databricks-connect-local-environment-setup.md) - Local development environment prerequisites including Python 3 installation, Python version matching for UDFs, and Scala requirements
* [Databricks Connect Local Python Environment Requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-local-python-environment-requirements.md) - Python 3 must be installed locally, and the minor version must satisfy the version compatibility table; the document references both Python and Scala APIs.
* [Databricks Connect Logging and Debug Logs](/concepts/databricks-connect-logging-and-debugging.md) - Using the SPARK_CONNECT_LOG_LEVEL environment variable and standard Python logging to enable debug-level log output from Databricks Connect.
* [Databricks Connect Logging and Debugging](/concepts/databricks-connect-logging-and-debugging.md) - Enabling debug logging in Databricks Connect for Python via the SPARK_CONNECT_LOG_LEVEL environment variable
* [Databricks Connect Logging Configuration](/concepts/databricks-connect-logging-and-debugging.md) - Enabling debug logs in Databricks Connect for Python by setting the `SPARK_CONNECT_LOG_LEVEL=debug` environment variable, which sends logs to stderr using standard Python logging.
* [Databricks Connect Long-lived Sessions](/concepts/databricks-connect-long-lived-sessions.md) - On serverless compute (DBR 16.4+), Databricks Connect best-effort preserves session state — including configs, temporary views, UDFs, temp variables, and uploaded files — across idle timeouts, so users don't need to re-run setup commands.
* [Databricks Connect MemoryUtil initialization error](/concepts/databricks-connect-memoryutil-initialization-error.md) - An error occurring when building a DatabricksSession due to Apache Arrow's reflection access to private Java methods being blocked in Java 17.
* [Databricks Connect migration](/concepts/databricks-connect-migration.md) - Process for migrating from Databricks Connect for Databricks Runtime 12.2 LTS and below to Databricks Connect for Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS and above.
* [Databricks Connect MLlib Compatibility Limitations](/concepts/databricks-connect-mllib-limitation.md) - A limitation where Databricks Connect has limited compatibility with Apache Spark MLlib because MLlib uses RDDs while Databricks Connect only supports the DataFrame API.
* [Databricks Connect MLlib Limitation](/concepts/databricks-connect-mllib-limitation.md) - Databricks Connect has limited compatibility with Apache Spark MLlib because MLlib uses RDDs while Databricks Connect only supports the DataFrame API.
* [Databricks Connect MLlib limitations in R](/concepts/databricks-connect-mllib-limitation.md) - Spark MLlib functions that rely on RDDs are incompatible with Databricks Connect, which only supports the DataFrame API, limiting sparklyr's MLlib capabilities.
* [Databricks Connect Project Setup with renv](/concepts/databricks-connect-project-setup-with-renv.md) - A reproducible R project workflow using RStudio, renv, and environment variables (.Renviron) to configure Databricks Connect connections.
* [Databricks Connect public preview](/concepts/databricks-connect-public-preview.md) - The Public Preview status of Databricks Connect for Scala on Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS and above.
* [Databricks Connect PySpark Shell](/concepts/databricks-connect-pyspark-shell.md) - A PySpark REPL (Spark shell) bundled with Databricks Connect for Python that connects to a remote Databricks cluster for interactive analysis.
* [Databricks Connect Python Environment Requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-python-environment-requirements.md) - Local environment requires Python 3 with correct minor version matching the Databricks Runtime, especially for UDF compatibility.
* [Databricks Connect Python Version Matching for UDFs](/concepts/databricks-connect-python-version-matching-for-udfs.md) - When using user-defined functions (UDFs) with Databricks Connect, the local Python minor version must match the minor version on the target Databricks Runtime cluster or serverless compute.
* [Databricks Connect Query Interruption via interruptTag API](/concepts/databricks-connect-query-interruption-via-interrupttag-api.md) - Databricks Connect provides an interruptTag() API that allows users to tag DataFrames and then interrupt long-running queries by tag, useful for cost control.
* [Databricks Connect Query Timeout](/concepts/databricks-connect-query-timeout.md) - Long queries over 3600 seconds are not available in Databricks Connect for Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS and below.
* [Databricks Connect Remote Execution Model](/concepts/databricks-connect-remote-execution-model.md) - The architectural pattern where logical representations of Spark commands (DataFrame operations) are sent to a remote Spark server on the Databricks cluster rather than executed locally.
* [Databricks Connect remote table operations](/concepts/databricks-connect-remote-table-operations.md) - The ability to read, create, query, and manage tables on a remote Databricks cluster using standard Spark DataFrame and SQL APIs through Databricks Connect.
* [Databricks Connect remote() method](/concepts/databricks-connect-remote-method.md) - The remote() method allows connecting to Databricks compute not attached to the notebook or job using explicit configuration
* [Databricks Connect Requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-requirements.md) - Supported Databricks Runtime versions (7.3 LTS through 12.2 LTS), matching Python minor versions, matching databricks-connect package versions, and JRE 8.
* [Databricks Connect Resilient Query Execution](/concepts/databricks-connect-resilient-query-execution.md) - Databricks Connect (DBR 14.0+) automatically reconnects to running queries after network interruptions or OS pauses (up to 5 min), and supports longer query execution beyond the previous 1-hour limit.
* [Databricks Connect Serverless Compute](/concepts/databricks-connect-serverless-compute.md) - Feature allowing Databricks Connect to connect to serverless compute resources instead of classic clusters
