* [Databricks Connect Serverless Compute Connection](/concepts/databricks-connect-serverless-compute-connection.md) - Configuration methods for connecting Databricks Connect to serverless compute instead of classic clusters, including environment variables, config profiles, and builder methods.
* [Databricks Connect Serverless Compute Support](/concepts/databricks-connect-serverless-compute-support.md) - Support for connecting Databricks Connect to serverless compute starting from version 15.1, with workspace-level prerequisites for serverless compute.
* [Databricks Connect Serverless UDF Limitations](/concepts/databricks-connect-serverless-udf-limitations.md) - UDF support on serverless compute always follows the initial corresponding minor release of Databricks Connect, with version compatibility defined in the official compatibility table.
* [Databricks Connect service disablement](/concepts/databricks-connect-service-disabling.md) - Method to disable Databricks Connect (Spark Connect) on a cluster by setting spark.databricks.service.server.enabled false in Spark configuration.
* [Databricks Connect Service Disabling](/concepts/databricks-connect-service-disabling.md) - How to disable the Databricks Connect (Spark Connect) service on a cluster using the spark.databricks.service.server.enabled Spark configuration property.
* [Databricks Connect setup and requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-setup-and-requirements.md) - Prerequisites, installation steps, and compatibility requirements for using Databricks Connect in a local development environment.
* [Databricks Connect Spark Connect Connection String](/concepts/databricks-connect-spark-connect-connection-string.md) - Advanced method to connect Databricks Connect to a cluster using a Spark Connect connection string, either via the remote() function or the SPARK_REMOTE environment variable.
* [Databricks Connect Spark Connect Server](/concepts/databricks-connect-spark-connect-server.md) - Running Databricks Connect against an open source Spark Connect server instead of a Databricks cluster, with caveats about exclusive features
* [Databricks Connect SSL/TLS Certificate Configuration](/concepts/databricks-connect-ssltls-certificate-configuration.md) - Configuring custom SSL/TLS certificates for Databricks Connect by setting the GRPC_DEFAULT_SSL_ROOTS_FILE_PATH environment variable
* [Databricks Connect Streaming and Distributed ML Limitations](/concepts/databricks-connect-streaming-and-distributed-ml-limitations.md) - Streaming foreachBatch, distributed ML training, and ApplyInPandas/Cogroup with standard access mode are unsupported in certain Databricks Connect runtime versions.
* [Databricks Connect Support Lifecycle](/concepts/databricks-connect-support-lifecycle.md) - Databricks Connect follows the Databricks Runtime support lifecycles; end-of-support versions should be upgraded to supported versions.
* [Databricks Connect Table CRUD Operations](/concepts/databricks-connect-table-crud-operations.md) - Common patterns for reading, creating, querying, and dropping tables on a Databricks cluster through a remote Databricks Connect session.
* [Databricks Connect Table Operations](/concepts/databricks-connect-table-operations.md) - Code patterns for reading, creating, and managing tables in Databricks using Databricks Connect for Scala, including CRUD operations and SQL queries.
* [Databricks Connect Testing with pytest](/concepts/databricks-connect-testing-with-pytest.md) - How to write and run pytest tests for PySpark code using Databricks Connect, where tests execute on a remote Databricks cluster or serverless compute.
* [Databricks Connect Troubleshooting](/concepts/databricks-connect-troubleshooting.md) - Common issues encountered when using Databricks Connect such as Python version mismatches, conflicting PySpark installations, SPARK_HOME conflicts, serialization settings, and Windows-specific problems.
* [Databricks Connect troubleshooting and limitations](/concepts/databricks-connect-troubleshooting-and-limitations.md) - Known troubleshooting guidance and limitations when using Databricks Connect.
* [Databricks Connect tutorial (IntelliJ IDEA)](/concepts/databricks-connect-tutorial-intellij-idea.md) - A walkthrough guide for running Scala code from IntelliJ IDEA on classic Databricks compute using Databricks Connect.
* [Databricks Connect UDF Custom Library Restriction on Serverless Compute](/concepts/databricks-connect-udf-custom-library-restriction-on-serverless-compute.md) - On serverless compute with Databricks Connect for Runtime 16.3 and below, UDFs cannot include custom libraries.
* [Databricks Connect UDF Serialization](/concepts/databricks-connect-udf-serialization.md) - The mechanism by which locally-defined user-defined functions (UDFs) are serialized and transmitted to the remote Databricks cluster for execution, along with related APIs like foreach, foreachBatch, and transformWithState.
* [Databricks Connect Unsupported APIs and Libraries](/concepts/databricks-connect-unsupported-apis-and-libraries.md) - Several Spark APIs and third-party libraries are unsupported in Databricks Connect, including RDDs, Spark Context, data frame display, certain Databricks Utilities, and libraries like Mosaic, GraphFrames, and GreatExpectations.
* [Databricks Connect usage requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-usage-requirements.md) - Prerequisites and compatibility requirements for workspace and local development environment to use Databricks Connect.
* [Databricks Connect usage workflow](/concepts/databricks-connect-usage-workflow.md) - The typical three-step workflow for getting started with Databricks Connect: confirming requirements, installing the package, and walking through a tutorial.
* [Databricks Connect Version Compatibility](/concepts/databricks-connect-version-compatibility.md) - The version alignment rules between Databricks Connect packages and Databricks Runtime versions, including support lifecycles and end-of-support policies.
* [Databricks Connect Version Pinning with Dot-Asterisk Notation](/concepts/databricks-connect-version-pinning-with-dot-asterisk-notation.md) - The recommended practice of specifying databricks-connect versions using X.Y.* notation to ensure the latest patch version is installed.
* [Databricks Connect Windows path space restriction](/concepts/databricks-connect-windows-path-space-restriction.md) - Databricks Connect fails on Windows when installed into a directory path containing spaces; workarounds include using short name forms or space-free paths.
* [Databricks Connect with Classic Compute](/concepts/databricks-connect-with-classic-compute.md) - Support for running code from IDEs like PyCharm on classic (non-serverless) Databricks compute clusters
* [Databricks Connect with Open Source Spark Connect Server](/concepts/databricks-connect-spark-connect-server.md) - Running Databricks Connect against a local open source Spark Connect server instead of a Databricks cluster, with the caveat that some Databricks-exclusive features may fail.
* [Databricks Connect with Serverless Compute](/concepts/databricks-connect-with-serverless-compute.md) - Support for running Python code on Databricks serverless compute via Databricks Connect
* [Databricks Connect Workaround for CREATE TABLE AS SELECT](/concepts/databricks-connect-workaround-for-create-table-as-select.md) - In Databricks Connect, CREATE TABLE AS SELECT is not supported; the workaround is to use spark.sql('SELECT ...').write.saveAsTable('table') instead.
* [Databricks Connect Workspace Requirements](/concepts/databricks-connect-workspace-requirements.md) - Prerequisites for using Databricks Connect including Unity Catalog enablement, compute version compatibility, and cluster access mode requirements.
* [Databricks Dashboard Customization and Sharing](/concepts/databricks-dashboard-customization-and-sharing.md) - User-editable parameters for the entire dashboard and individual charts, allowing customization of date range, data slices, models, and modifications to charts.
* [Databricks Dashboard Import/Export](/concepts/databricks-dashboard-importexport.md) - The ability to import and export Databricks dashboards as JSON files, enabling sharing and reproduction of dashboard definitions across workspaces.
* [Databricks Data Classification](/concepts/databricks-data-classification.md) - An AI agent in Unity Catalog that automatically classifies and tags sensitive data across tables using large language models and intelligent scanning.
* [Databricks Data Preparation for Distributed ML](/concepts/databricks-data-preparation-for-distributed-ml.md) - The overall workflow and best practices for preparing data specifically on the Databricks platform to support distributed machine learning training.
* [Databricks Data Profile (Data Profiling in Unity Catalog)](/concepts/databricks-data-profile-data-profiling-in-unity-catalog.md) - A feature in Databricks Unity Catalog that tracks statistical properties and quality metrics of Delta tables over time, supporting three profile types: TimeSeries, InferenceLog, and Snapshot.
* [Databricks Data Profile Metric Tables](/concepts/databricks-data-profile-metric-tables.md) - Unity Catalog tables created by data profiling that store computed statistics and can be queried via SQL or Catalog Explorer
* [Databricks Data Profile Refresh Management](/concepts/databricks-data-profile-refresh-management.md) - Creating, listing, and monitoring refresh runs for data profiles using the API
* [Databricks Data Profile Scheduling and Notifications](/concepts/databricks-profile-scheduling-and-notifications.md) - Configuring cron-based schedules and email notifications for data profile refreshes
* [Databricks Data Profiling](/concepts/databricks-data-profiling.md) - A feature in Databricks Unity Catalog that analyzes table statistics, distributions, and quality metrics to help users understand their data.
* [Databricks Data Profiling (API)](/concepts/databricks-data-profiling-api.md) - Creating and managing data profiles on Unity Catalog tables using the Databricks SDK or REST API to monitor data quality and distribution over time.
* [Databricks Data Profiling (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/databricks-data-profiling-unity-catalog.md) - API-driven data profiling service for Delta tables in Unity Catalog, supporting TimeSeries, InferenceLog, and Snapshot profile types
* [Databricks Data Profiling Dashboard](/concepts/databricks-data-profiling-dashboard.md) - An automatically generated, customizable dashboard in Databricks that visualizes profiling metric and drift results.
* [Databricks Data Profiling Metric Tables](/concepts/databricks-data-profiling-metric-tables.md) - Two metric tables (profile metrics and drift metrics) created when a data profile runs on a Databricks table, containing summary statistics and distribution-change tracking data.
* [Databricks Data Profiling SDK and API](/concepts/databricks-sdk-data-profiling-api.md) - Python SDK and REST API for creating and managing data profiles on Unity Catalog tables programmatically.
* [Databricks Default Storage vs Customer-Provisioned Managed Storage](/concepts/databricks-default-storage-vs-customer-provisioned-managed-storage.md) - Two flavors of managed storage: Databricks-provisioned default storage and customer-provided cloud storage; same lifecycle but different billing and post-purge retention behavior.
* [Databricks Delta Lake documentation](/concepts/databricks-delta-lake-documentation.md) - The Databricks-specific documentation and tutorials for using Delta Lake on the Databricks platform on AWS.
* [Databricks Designated Service with Geos](/concepts/databricks-designated-service-with-geos.md) - Foundation Model APIs is a Databricks Designated Service that uses Databricks Geos to manage data residency when processing customer content.
* [Databricks Development Workflow for Distributed ML](/concepts/databricks-development-workflow-for-distributed-ml.md) - Recommended methodology on Databricks for iteratively developing machine learning models by starting with single-node training before scaling to distributed execution
* [Databricks DevOps for ML](/concepts/databricks-devops-for-ml.md) - Production and automation capabilities including model serving, Jobs for scheduling, Git folders for version control, and infrastructure-as-code tools like Bundles and Terraform.
* [Databricks Disk Cache](/concepts/databricks-disk-cache.md) - Local caching layer on Databricks compute nodes that accelerates data access by storing frequently read data files in SSD-based local storage.
* [Databricks Distributed Deep Learning Deprecation](/concepts/databricks-distributed-deep-learning-deprecation.md) - Databricks deprecated Horovod and HorovodRunner after ML 15.4 LTS, recommending TorchDistributor for PyTorch and tf.distribute.Strategy for TensorFlow.
* [Databricks distributed training on AWS](/concepts/databricks-distributed-training-on-aws.md) - Reference architecture for running distributed TensorFlow training on Databricks clusters in AWS
* [Databricks Documentation Lifecycle](/concepts/databricks-documentation-lifecycle.md) - The practice of Databricks marking documentation as 'retired' when products or services are no longer supported or superseded, serving as an archival notice for users.
* [Databricks Documentation Structure for Model Serving](/concepts/databricks-documentation-structure-for-model-serving.md) - The URL path convention used by Databricks to organize model-serving documentation under /aws/en/machine-learning/model-serving/.
* [Databricks Embedding Model Querying](/concepts/databricks-embedding-model-querying.md) - How to write and send query requests to foundation models optimized for embeddings tasks via Databricks model serving endpoints.
* [Databricks Environment Configuration for MLflow Tracing](/concepts/databricks-environment-configuration-for-mlflow-tracing.md) - Required environment setup including DATABRICKS_HOST, DATABRICKS_TOKEN, and LLM provider API keys for MLflow Tracing with Smolagents
* [Databricks Error Class System](/concepts/databricks-error-class-system.md) - Databricks organizes runtime errors into named error classes with unique identifiers for structured error handling
* [Databricks Error Classes](/concepts/databricks-error-classes.md) - A system in Databricks that organizes error conditions into classes and sub-conditions, each with specific SQLSTATE codes and resolution guidance.
* [Databricks error classes pattern](/concepts/databricks-error-classes.md) - The structured error classification system used in Databricks, where error classes group multiple specific error conditions under a single identifier with SQLSTATE codes
* [Databricks Error Message Documentation](/concepts/databricks-error-message-documentation.md) - Databricks provides structured documentation for error conditions, including error classes and their arguments, to help users troubleshoot issues on the Databricks platform
* [Databricks Error Message Framework](/concepts/databricks-error-message-framework.md) - The structured error classification system used by Databricks that organizes runtime errors by class name (e.g., DELTA_UNIFORM_REFRESH_INVALID_ARGUMENT) with associated cause and resolution information.
* [Databricks Error Message Reference](/concepts/databricks-error-message-reference.md) - The curated collection of documented error conditions in Databricks, each with a unique error class, description, cause, and remediation
* [Databricks Feature Engineering Client](/concepts/databricks-feature-engineering-client.md) - Python API (FeatureEngineeringClient) for creating, managing, and storing feature tables in Unity Catalog for use in machine learning workflows.
* [Databricks Feature Engineering Compatibility Matrix](/concepts/feature-engineering-compatibility-matrix.md) - A reference mapping package versions (databricks-feature-engineering and databricks-feature-store) to Databricks Runtime ML versions.
* [Databricks Feature Engineering package installation](/concepts/databricks-feature-engineering-package-installation.md) - Instructions for installing databricks-feature-engineering via pip in Databricks Runtime or local environments
* [Databricks Feature Engineering Python API](/concepts/databricks-feature-engineering-client.md) - The Python client library (databricks-feature-engineering) for working with feature tables and online stores on Databricks
* [Databricks Feature Engineering Python Client](/concepts/databricks-feature-engineering-client.md) - The databricks-feature-engineering Python package that provides APIs for working with feature tables and online stores in Unity Catalog and Workspace Feature Store.
* [Databricks Feature Serving](/concepts/databricks-feature-serving.md) - A managed serving infrastructure that exposes Databricks feature data (from Unity Catalog) to external models and applications via high-availability, low-latency, auto-scaling endpoints.
* [Databricks Feature Store](/concepts/databricks-feature-store.md) - A managed feature store for batch and real-time serving of ML features with a single governed source of truth under Unity Catalog.
* [Databricks Feature Store Access Control (Legacy)](/concepts/feature-store-access-control-legacy.md) - Granular permission system for feature table metadata in Databricks workspaces not enabled for Unity Catalog
* [Databricks Feature Store for Covariate Lookups](/concepts/databricks-feature-store-for-covariate-lookups.md) - Using Databricks Feature Engineering Client to create and store feature tables that can be joined with primary training data in AutoML experiments.
* [Databricks Feature Store for Covariate Management](/concepts/databricks-feature-store-for-covariates.md) - Using the Databricks Feature Store to store, version, and serve covariate feature tables for AutoML forecasting
* [Databricks Feature Store for Covariates](/concepts/databricks-feature-store-for-covariates.md) - Using Databricks Feature Store (FeatureEngineeringClient) to register covariate feature tables that can be joined with primary training data for AutoML forecasting.
* [Databricks Feature Store Lineage](/concepts/databricks-feature-store-lineage.md) - Automatic tracking of relationships between feature tables, functions, and models used to build ML models in Databricks
* [Databricks Feature Store Workflow](/concepts/databricks-feature-store-workflow.md) - The end-to-end pipeline from raw data transformation through feature table creation, model training with feature logging, model registration, to batch and real-time inference.
* [Databricks Forecasting Advanced Options](/concepts/databricks-forecasting-advanced-options.md) - Additional configuration parameters for forecasting experiments including holiday region covariates, weight columns, split columns, training framework selection, and primary metrics.
* [Databricks Foundation Model Access Enforcement Tiers](/concepts/databricks-foundation-model-access-enforcement-tiers.md) - The three tiers of model access in Databricks — pay-per-token, batch inference (AI Functions), and provisioned throughput — each with different permission enforcement behaviors.
* [Databricks Foundation Model Agent Tracing Pattern](/concepts/databricks-foundation-model-agent-tracing-pattern.md) - A design pattern for building tool-calling agents with Databricks Foundation Models where MLflow Tracing automatically captures the full agent loop including model invocations, tool function execution (decorated with @mlflow.trace), and final responses.
* [Databricks Foundation Model API Pattern](/concepts/databricks-foundation-model-api-pattern.md) - The 'databricks:/model-name' URI pattern used to reference Databricks-hosted foundation models for LLM-as-a-judge scoring
* [Databricks Foundation Model API Tracing via MLflow](/concepts/databricks-foundation-model-api-tracing-via-mlflow.md) - Using MLflow's OpenAI autolog to trace calls to Databricks Foundation Model APIs by configuring an OpenAI client with Databricks host and token, then querying models like Llama 4 Maverick.
* [Databricks Foundation Model API Tracing via OpenAI Client](/concepts/databricks-foundation-model-api-tracing-via-mlflow.md) - Technique of using the OpenAI Python client configured with Databricks credentials and endpoints to query Databricks-hosted models (e.g., Llama 4 Maverick) while MLflow automatically traces those calls.
* [Databricks Foundation Model APIs](/concepts/databricks-foundation-model-apis.md) - Databricks-hosted foundation models made available through APIs that can be governed via Unity Catalog permissions as securable AI objects.
* [Databricks Foundation Model Endpoints](/concepts/databricks-foundation-model-endpoints.md) - Databricks-hosted model endpoints accessed via 'databricks:/' URIs for use as evaluation judges
* [Databricks Function Calling](/concepts/databricks-function-calling.md) - An OpenAI-compatible mechanism on Databricks that lets LLMs generate structured JSON function-call outputs based on user-defined tool schemas, without the model executing the functions itself.
* [Databricks Function Calling Limitations](/concepts/databricks-function-calling-limitations.md) - Current Public Preview restrictions: single-turn optimization for most models, no parallel function calling, max 32 functions, no support on existing provisioned-throughput endpoints, and no 'id' field for Gemini models.
* [Databricks Function Calling Workflow](/concepts/databricks-function-calling-workflow.md) - The four-step sequence for using function calling on Databricks: submit query with tools, model returns JSON arguments, parse and execute function, append results for final summary.
* [Databricks Gateway Model as Judge](/concepts/databricks-gateway-model-as-judge.md) - Using Databricks-hosted LLM models (e.g., databricks-gpt-5-mini) as the evaluator model for scoring LLM outputs
* [Databricks GenAI Agent Evaluation Workflow](/concepts/databricks-genai-agent-evaluation-workflow.md) - End-to-end workflow for evaluating GenAI agents on Databricks, including creating agents, defining judges, building datasets, and running evaluations.
* [Databricks GenAI notebook development](/concepts/databricks-genai-notebook-development.md) - A development workflow pattern where Databricks notebooks serve as the IDE for building GenAI applications with built-in MLflow experiment tracking, auto-linked experiments, and inline trace visualization.
* [Databricks GenAI Tracing for Local Models](/concepts/databricks-genai-tracing-for-local-models.md) - Pattern for tracing locally-run Ollama models within Databricks by setting the MLflow tracking URI to 'databricks' and enabling OpenAI autologging on serverless compute clusters.
* [Databricks Genie Code](/concepts/databricks-genie-code.md) - An AI assistant within Databricks that performs automated exploratory data analysis, natural language AutoML, code generation for data preparation and feature pipelines, and diagnosis of model serving endpoints.
* [Databricks Genie Code for Deep Learning](/concepts/genie-code-for-deep-learning.md) - An AI assistant feature that supports deep learning workloads on AI Runtime by generating training code, resolving library errors, suggesting optimizations, and debugging issues.
* [Databricks Geos and Data Residency](/concepts/databricks-geos-and-data-residency.md) - Databricks' framework for managing data residency when processing customer content, applicable to Foundation Model APIs as a Designated Service.
* [Databricks Git Folders and CI/CD Integration](/concepts/databricks-git-folders-for-cicd.md) - Git integration in the Databricks workspace enabling version control, code sharing, and automated CI/CD pipelines with tools like GitHub Actions and Jenkins.
* [Databricks Git Folders for CI/CD](/concepts/databricks-git-folders-for-cicd.md) - Built-in Git integration in the Databricks workspace that enables version control, code sharing, and automated CI/CD workflows via APIs and third-party tools.
* [Databricks Inbound IP Allowlisting](/concepts/databricks-inbound-ip-allowlisting.md) - The practice of adding Databricks-managed IP addresses to a firewall allowlist for the provider's cloud and region to enable SecureConnect access
* [Databricks Lakebase Autoscaling](/concepts/databricks-lakebase-autoscaling.md) - The underlying infrastructure for Online Feature Stores—a Lakebase Autoscaling project that provisions highly available managed compute for real-time feature serving.
* [Databricks Legacy Feature Store (databricks-feature-store)](/concepts/databricks-legacy-feature-store-databricks-feature-store.md) - The deprecated legacy Python client package (databricks-feature-store) for workspace-level feature store operations, superseded by databricks-feature-engineering as of version 0.17.0.
* [Databricks LLM Endpoint Benchmarking Methodology](/concepts/llm-endpoint-benchmarking-methodology.md) - Databricks recommends maximizing throughput within a given latency budget using a benchmarking harness that measures total latency, throughput, and the throughput-latency curve across varying concurrency levels.
* [Databricks LLM Serving Endpoint Autoscaling](/concepts/databricks-llm-serving-endpoint-autoscaling.md) - Databricks' strategy for dynamically scaling LLM serving endpoints that balances latency and throughput, where throughput plateaus at provisioned capacity limits as concurrency increases.
* [Databricks LLM Serving Endpoints](/concepts/databricks-llm-serving-endpoints.md) - An OpenAI-compatible API for accessing Databricks-hosted LLMs (e.g., Claude Sonnet) using Databricks credentials, enabling seamless integration with MLflow evaluation.
* [Databricks LLM Serving Endpoints via OpenAI SDK](/concepts/databricks-llm-serving-endpoints.md) - Using the OpenAI Python SDK client configured with Databricks credentials to call Databricks-hosted LLMs (e.g., Claude Sonnet) as serving endpoints.
* [Databricks ML Deprecation Policy](/concepts/databricks-ml-deprecation-policy.md) - The practice of removing pre-installed packages like Horovod after specific ML runtime versions (15.4 LTS ML and later).
* [Databricks ML Lifecycle Platform](/concepts/databricks-ml-platform.md) - A unified platform that spans the full data science and machine learning lifecycle from raw data ingestion through feature engineering, model training, deployment, and production monitoring, integrating open-source frameworks with enterprise governance and MLOps tools.
* [Databricks ML Platform](/concepts/databricks-ml-platform.md) - Unified platform for the full data science and machine learning lifecycle on Databricks, integrating open-source frameworks with enterprise MLOps tooling.
* [Databricks MLflow 3 for GenAI](/concepts/databricks-mlflow-3-for-genai.md) - Databricks' MLflow 3 platform tailored for Generative AI development, providing tracing, evaluation with LLM-as-a-judge, human feedback collection, and production monitoring capabilities.
* [Databricks MLflow Agent Evaluation Workflow](/concepts/databricks-mlflow-agent-evaluation-workflow.md) - End-to-end workflow on Databricks for evaluating Claude agent responses using MLflow datasets, custom scorers, and experiment tracking.
* [Databricks MLflow Credential Propagation](/concepts/databricks-mlflow-credential-propagation.md) - Method for passing Databricks MLflow tracking credentials to remote Ray workers using get_databricks_env_vars and environment variable updates.
* [Databricks MLflow Experiment Setup](/concepts/databricks-mlflow-experiment-setup.md) - Required setup: install mlflow-tracing, create a GenAI Experiment in Databricks, configure authentication via environment variables, and initialize the SDK with a tracking URI and experiment ID.
* [Databricks MLflow GenAI Evaluation Framework](/concepts/databricks-mlflow-genai-evaluation-framework.md) - A comprehensive evaluation framework on Databricks that includes built-in LLM judges, custom judge creation, and the mlflow.genai.evaluate() API for running evaluations.
* [Databricks MLflow OTLP Endpoint](/concepts/databricks-mlflow-otlp-endpoint.md) - A Databricks REST API endpoint at /api/2.0/otel/v1/traces that ingests OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) spans and routes them to a specified Unity Catalog table via the X-Databricks-UC-Table-Name header.
* [Databricks MLflow PySpark ML autologging](/concepts/mlflow-pyspark-ml-autologging.md) - The replacement for deprecated MLlib automated MLflow tracking, enabled by default in Databricks Runtime 10.2 ML and above via mlflow.pyspark.ml.autolog() and Databricks Autologging.
* [Databricks MLflow REST API 2.0](/concepts/databricks-mlflow-rest-api-20.md) - Databricks' managed version of the MLflow API, documented separately from the open-source reference
* [Databricks MLflow Serverless Workloads](/concepts/databricks-mlflow-serverless-workloads.md) - Serverless workloads managed by MLflow on Databricks, including scheduled scorers, synthetic evaluation set generation, and agent evaluation.
* [Databricks MLflow Tracking Integration](/concepts/databricks-mlflow-tracking-integration.md) - Configuration to route MLflow experiment data to a Databricks workspace using mlflow.set_tracking_uri('databricks') and mlflow.set_experiment().
* [Databricks MLlib MLflow Tracking Deprecation](/concepts/databricks-mllib-mlflow-tracking-deprecation.md) - MLlib automated MLflow tracking is deprecated on Databricks Runtime 10.1 ML and above, disabled by default from 10.2 ML onwards, with mlflow.pyspark.ml.autolog() as the recommended replacement.
* [Databricks MLlib Support](/concepts/databricks-mllib-support.md) - Databricks supports Apache Spark MLlib on serverless, standard, and dedicated compute, with integration for Python, Scala, Java, and R.
* [Databricks MLOps Stacks](/concepts/databricks-mlops-stacks.md) - Templates for enabling automated, repeatable model promotion from development to production using infrastructure-as-code, forming part of Databricks' MLOps and governance suite.
* [Databricks model endpoint references in evaluators](/concepts/databricks-model-endpoint-references-in-evaluators.md) - The pattern of referencing Databricks-hosted foundation models (e.g., databricks-gpt-5-mini) via the 'databricks:/' prefix as the evaluation model for Phoenix scorers in the MLflow GenAI evaluation framework.
* [Databricks Model Registry with Unity Catalog](/concepts/databricks-model-registry-with-unity-catalog.md) - A centralized model repository integrated with Unity Catalog providing governance, cross-workspace access, lineage tracking, and model discovery for managing the model deployment lifecycle.
* [Databricks Model Retirement Policy](/concepts/databricks-model-retirement-policy.md) - The overall policy governing how and when Databricks notifies customers about model retirements, covering transition periods, retirement dates, and migration recommendations.
* [Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/databricks-model-serving.md) - Databricks' real-time model serving solution, distinct from batch inference, for deploying models for low-latency predictions
* [Databricks Model Serving Acceptable Use Policy](/concepts/databricks-model-serving-acceptable-use-policy.md) - A policy framework governing which machine learning models can be served on Databricks Model Serving, though the source page contains no substantive policy content.
* [Databricks Model Serving Data Protection](/concepts/databricks-model-serving-data-protection.md) - Security controls for Model Serving including logical isolation per customer, AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.2+ in transit, no training on user data, and temporary retention of logs and metrics.
* [Databricks Model Serving Endpoint](/concepts/databricks-model-serving-endpoint.md) - A REST API endpoint (POST /api/2.0/serving-endpoints) for deploying and managing machine learning models for inference on Databricks.
* [Databricks Model Serving Endpoint Load Testing](/concepts/databricks-model-serving-endpoint-load-testing.md) - Process of configuring and running load tests on Databricks Model Serving endpoints to optimize performance, latency, and throughput
* [Databricks Model Serving Endpoint Load Testing Methodology](/concepts/databricks-model-serving-endpoint-load-testing-methodology.md) - A step-by-step process for configuring, running, and analyzing load tests on Databricks custom model serving endpoints using Locust.
* [Databricks Model Serving Endpoints](/concepts/databricks-model-serving-endpoints.md) - The underlying serving infrastructure on Databricks that hosts Foundation Model APIs, supporting both pay-per-token and provisioned throughput endpoint modes.
* [Databricks Model Serving for computer vision](/concepts/databricks-model-serving-for-computer-vision.md) - Databricks supports deploying trained computer vision models (e.g., YOLO11n) to production using Model Serving after training on AI Runtime.
* [Databricks Model Serving Foundation Model APIs (FMAPI)](/concepts/databricks-model-serving-foundation-model-apis-fmapi.md) - A Databricks model serving capability for foundation models that is explicitly incompatible with express deployments, which require custom models instead.
* [Databricks Model Serving Monitoring](/concepts/databricks-model-serving-monitoring.md) - Overview of monitoring tools available for model quality and endpoint health on Databricks Model Serving.
* [Databricks Model Serving on AWS](/concepts/databricks-model-serving-on-aws.md) - A Databricks platform service for deploying and serving machine learning models on AWS infrastructure.
* [Databricks Model Training](/concepts/databricks-model-training.md) - A unified platform for training and deploying ML and AI models through AutoML and Foundation Model Fine-tuning workloads
* [Databricks model training with MLlib](/concepts/databricks-model-training-with-mllib.md) - Using Apache Spark MLlib on Databricks for binary classification, decision trees, GBT regression, structured streaming, and custom transformers.
* [Databricks model training with scikit-learn and MLflow](/concepts/databricks-model-training-with-scikit-learn-and-mlflow.md) - End-to-end machine learning on Databricks using scikit-learn with Unity Catalog, MLflow tracking, and automated hyperparameter tuning via Hyperopt/Optuna.
* [Databricks model training with XGBoost](/concepts/databricks-model-training-with-xgboost.md) - Training XGBoost models on Databricks using Python, PySpark, and Scala, supporting both single-node and distributed workloads.
* [Databricks Model URI for Scorers](/concepts/databricks-model-uri-for-scorers.md) - The use of databricks:/ model URIs (e.g., databricks:/databricks-gpt-5-mini) to specify LLM judge models for Phoenix scorers within Databricks-managed MLflow environments.
* [Databricks NLP Platform](/concepts/databricks-nlp-platform.md) - Databricks' overall support for natural language processing tasks using open-source libraries (Spark ML, Spark NLP, Hugging Face) and proprietary integrations (John Snow Labs), enabling distributed NLP workloads.
* [Databricks notebook code modularization](/concepts/databricks-notebook-code-modularization.md) - The practice of organizing reusable code across notebooks using %run and workspace files to encapsulate functions and logic.
* [Databricks notebook integration for MLflow](/concepts/databricks-notebook-integration-for-mlflow.md) - Databricks notebooks automatically connect to the MLflow server, support notebook and workspace experiments, enable autologging for MLflow tracking, and provide an inline tracing UI for GenAI interactive analysis.
* [Databricks notebook requirement for scorer registration](/concepts/databricks-notebook-requirement-for-scorer-registration.md) - Scorer functions used in production monitoring must be defined and registered from a Databricks notebook because the monitoring service serializes function code for remote execution.
* [Databricks notebook serialization requirement for scorers](/concepts/databricks-notebook-serialization-requirement-for-scorers.md) - Custom scorer functions used in production monitoring must be defined and registered from a Databricks notebook because the monitoring service requires the notebook environment for code serialization.
* [Databricks Notebook Widgets for Configuration](/concepts/databricks-notebook-widgets-for-configuration.md) - Using dbutils.widgets.text to create parameterized notebook inputs for Unity Catalog paths and model names, enabling reusable training workflows.
* [Databricks OIDC Profile Portal](/concepts/databricks-oidc-profile-portal.md) - A portal URL provided by the Databricks provider where recipients download an Iceberg profile file containing catalog endpoint, share name, and placeholders for OAuth credentials.
* [Databricks on AWS](/concepts/databricks-on-aws.md) - Databricks platform deployed on Amazon Web Services infrastructure for data analytics and machine learning
* [Databricks on AWS Platform](/concepts/databricks-on-aws.md) - Databricks' unified analytics platform deployed on Amazon Web Services infrastructure.
* [Databricks Online Feature Store](/concepts/databricks-online-feature-store.md) - A high-performance, low-latency service for serving ML feature data to real-time applications, powered by Databricks Lakebase.
* [Databricks Online Feature Store Cost Optimization](/concepts/databricks-online-feature-store-cost-optimization.md) - Best practices for managing costs including reusing online stores across projects, right-sizing capacity from CU_2, and deleting unused stores to avoid continuous charges.
* [Databricks Online Tables](/concepts/databricks-online-tables.md) - Read-only, serverless copies of Delta Tables stored in row-oriented format, optimized for low-latency online access from Model Serving, Feature Serving, and RAG applications.
* [Databricks Online Tables (Legacy)](/concepts/databricks-online-tables.md) - Serverless, read-only copies of Delta Tables stored in row-oriented format, optimized for low-latency online access and designed for use with Model Serving, Feature Serving, and RAG applications.
* [Databricks Open Source ML Ecosystem](/concepts/databricks-open-source-ml-ecosystem.md) - Databricks' full support for the open-source ML ecosystem including frameworks like scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Hugging Face, with open formats for artifacts (MLflow), governance (Unity Catalog APIs), and storage (Delta Lake).
* [Databricks OpenAI Client for Embeddings](/concepts/databricks-openai-client-for-embeddings.md) - A Databricks-specific OpenAI-compatible client library (databricks_openai) used to send embedding requests to Databricks-hosted models using the OpenAI embeddings API format.
* [Databricks OpenAI Client for Foundation Models](/concepts/databricks-openai-client-for-foundation-models.md) - The databricks-openai package provides an OpenAI-compatible API client for calling Databricks-hosted Foundation Model endpoints, enabling standard OpenAI SDK patterns against Databricks models.
* [Databricks OpenAI client integration with MLflow](/concepts/databricks-openai-client-integration-with-mlflow.md) - Using Databricks-hosted OpenAI-compatible models (via DatabricksOpenAI client) within MLflow prompt optimization workflows for prediction and evaluation.
* [Databricks OpenSharing](/concepts/databricks-opensharing.md) - A Databricks feature that enables organizations to share data across platforms using Delta Sharing protocols
* [Databricks OpenSharing Audit Log System Table](/concepts/databricks-opensharing-audit-log-system-table.md) - The system.access.audit table stores OpenSharing audit events, accessible to account admins and authorized users when system tables are enabled.
* [Databricks OTLP Trace Endpoint](/concepts/databricks-otlp-trace-endpoint.md) - The /api/2.0/otel/v1/traces endpoint that accepts OpenTelemetry traces via a BatchSpanProcessor and OTLPSpanExporter, routing them to a Unity Catalog table via the X-Databricks-UC-Table-Name header.
* [Databricks Personal Access Token (PAT) Authentication](/concepts/databricks-personal-access-token-pat-authentication.md) - An authentication method for connecting local development tools to Databricks workspaces, using DATABRICKS_TOKEN and DATABRICKS_HOST environment variables.
* [Databricks personal access token authentication for R](/concepts/databricks-connect-authentication-for-r.md) - The only supported authentication method for Databricks Connect for R, requiring a PAT stored in the DATABRICKS_TOKEN environment variable.
* [Databricks Pipeline Task for Jobs](/concepts/databricks-pipeline-task-for-jobs.md) - Using the Pipeline task type in Databricks Jobs to include a Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipeline as a step in a multi-task job workflow.
* [Databricks Pipelines REST API Update Endpoint](/concepts/databricks-pipelines-rest-api-update-endpoint.md) - The REST API endpoint at /api/2.0/pipelines/{pipeline_id}/updates used to trigger pipeline updates programmatically from external orchestrators.
* [Databricks Premium Plan Requirement](/concepts/databricks-premium-plan-requirement.md) - The pricing tier prerequisite stating that Hive metastore table access control requires the Premium plan or above on Databricks.
* [Databricks Profile Metrics 30-Day Lookback Window](/concepts/databricks-profile-metrics-30-day-lookback-window.md) - For TimeSeries and InferenceLog profiles, metrics look back 30 days from profile creation time, which may cause partial windows (e.g., mid-week/month boundaries) in the first analysis.
* [Databricks Profile Notifications](/concepts/databricks-profile-notifications.md) - Configurable email notifications that alert users when a data profiling refresh fails or times out.
* [Databricks Profile Scheduling and Notifications](/concepts/databricks-profile-scheduling-and-notifications.md) - Configuring cron-based schedules and email notifications for automated data profile refreshes in Databricks.
* [Databricks Profile Types: Snapshot, TimeSeries, InferenceLog](/concepts/databricks-profile-types-snapshot-timeseries-inferencelog.md) - Three analysis types exist: Snapshot (single point-in-time window), TimeSeries (windows based on timestamp granularities), and InferenceLog (adds model accuracy, fairness, and bias statistics).
* [Databricks Ray-Spark Integration](/concepts/databricks-ray-spark-integration.md) - Databricks provides native integration for running Ray and Spark in the same environment, including utilities like databricks.ray.data.from_spark for seamless data passing between the frameworks.
* [Databricks Release Stages: Public Preview vs Beta](/concepts/databricks-release-stages-public-preview-vs-beta.md) - Databricks distinguishes between Public Preview (feature-complete and ready for broader adoption) and Beta (still under active development) release stages for its AI Runtime capabilities.
* [Databricks Release Tiers: Public Preview vs Beta](/concepts/databricks-release-tiers-public-preview-vs-beta.md) - AI Runtime features are split across two release stages: single-node serverless GPU tasks are in Public Preview, while the distributed multi-GPU training API remains in Beta.
* [Databricks release types for ML features](/concepts/databricks-release-types-for-ml-features.md) - The distinction between Public Preview and Beta release stages for Databricks machine learning features, indicating maturity and support levels
* [Databricks Release Types: Public Preview and Beta](/concepts/databricks-release-types-public-preview-and-beta.md) - Databricks categorizes features by maturity: Public Preview (generally available for evaluation) and Beta (still under active development, for early adopters).
* [Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS](/concepts/databricks-runtime-133-lts.md) - The minimum supported Databricks Runtime version (13.3 LTS and above) for the current generation of Databricks Connect for Scala.
* [Databricks Runtime 14.0 ML](/concepts/databricks-runtime-140-ml.md) - The ML runtime version on Databricks that introduced support for the DeepSpeed library, enabling advanced distributed training capabilities.
* [Databricks Runtime Compatibility](/concepts/databricks-runtime-compatibility.md) - Version compatibility requirements between Databricks Runtime and Databricks Connect client packages.
* [Databricks Runtime for Machine Learning](/concepts/databricks-runtime-for-machine-learning.md) - A pre-built runtime environment for deep learning that includes TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras, pre-configured GPU support, and integrated MLflow tracking.
* [Databricks Runtime for Machine Learning (Databricks Runtime ML)](/concepts/databricks-runtime-for-machine-learning.md) - A pre-configured Databricks runtime environment that automates compute resource creation with built-in machine learning and deep learning libraries and tools.
* [Databricks Runtime for Machine Learning (ML)](/concepts/databricks-runtime-for-machine-learning-ml.md) - A pre-configured Databricks runtime that includes PyTorch and other ML libraries
* [Databricks Runtime for ML](/concepts/databricks-runtime-for-ml.md) - Pre-configured compute clusters for machine learning on Databricks, bundling libraries like scikit-learn, XGBoost, MLflow, and deep learning frameworks.
* [Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml.md) - A Databricks runtime variant that includes pre-installed popular machine learning and deep learning libraries for ML workloads.
* [Databricks Runtime ML deprecation of Hyperopt](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-deprecation-of-hyperopt.md) - Hyperopt removed from Databricks Runtime for Machine Learning after version 16.4 LTS ML
* [Databricks Runtime ML Deprecation Policy](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-deprecation-policy.md) - Databricks Runtime for Machine Learning drops Hyperopt after version 16.4 LTS ML, reflecting a shift toward Optuna and RayTune.
* [Databricks Runtime ML for Deep Learning](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-for-deep-learning.md) - Databricks Runtime ML includes deep learning libraries such as TensorFlow and PyTorch, and supports featurization at scale by distributing computation across clusters.
* [Databricks Runtime ML for DeepSpeed](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-support.md) - Databricks Runtime 14.0 ML or above is required to use the DeepSpeed library and distributor for distributed training.
* [Databricks Runtime ML for Distributed Training](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-for-distributed-training.md) - Databricks Runtime 13.0 ML or above as the required environment for running TorchDistributor with Spark 3.4
* [Databricks Runtime ML for XGBoost](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-for-xgboost.md) - The pre-configured Databricks Runtime ML environment that includes XGBoost libraries and dependencies for Scala-based training.
* [Databricks Runtime ML Library Continuity Options](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-library-continuity-options.md) - Options available to customers after a library is removed from Databricks Runtime ML pre-installation: manually install the library or use an earlier runtime version.
* [Databricks Runtime ML Library Deprecation Policy](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-library-deprecation-policy.md) - The criteria and process by which Databricks removes pre-installed libraries from Databricks Runtime ML, including conditions like inactivity, low usage, or replacement.
* [Databricks Runtime ML Library Removal Notification](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-library-removal-notification.md) - The multi-channel notification process Databricks uses to inform customers before removing a library, including release notes, import warnings, and documentation updates.
* [Databricks Runtime ML model training examples](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-model-training-examples.md) - Databricks provides notebook examples for training models including hyperparameter tuning documentation within the Databricks Runtime ML ecosystem
* [Databricks Runtime ML Mosaic Streaming Integration](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-mosaic-streaming-integration.md) - Mosaic Streaming is pre-installed in Databricks Runtime 15.2 ML and higher; authentication for Unity Catalog volumes requires setting DATABRICKS_HOST and DATABRICKS_TOKEN environment variables, especially for distributed training with TorchDistributor.
* [Databricks Runtime ML on AWS Graviton](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-on-aws-graviton.md) - Support for Graviton instance types starting from Databricks Runtime 15.4 LTS ML, offering improved performance for Spark, Photon, feature engineering, and ML libraries like XGBoost and LightGBM with better price-to-performance.
* [Databricks Runtime ML Support](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-support.md) - DeepSpeed is available in Databricks Runtime 14.0 ML and above, enabling distributed training with DeepSpeed on the Databricks platform.
* [Databricks Runtime ML Top-tier Libraries](/concepts/databricks-runtime-ml-top-tier-libraries.md) - A designated subset of pre-installed ML/DL libraries in Databricks Runtime ML that receive faster updates, advanced testing, and embedded optimizations.
* [Databricks Runtime Support Lifecycles](/concepts/databricks-connect-support-lifecycle.md) - The policy that Databricks Connect follows Databricks Runtime support lifecycles, with end-of-support versions requiring upgrades to supported versions.
* [Databricks Runtime Support Lifecycles for Databricks Connect](/concepts/databricks-connect-support-lifecycle.md) - End-of-support policy where Databricks Connect versions follow Databricks Runtime support lifecycles, requiring upgrades when versions reach end-of-support.
* [Databricks Runtime Version Alignment for Model Deployment](/concepts/databricks-runtime-version-alignment-for-model-deployment.md) - The recommendation to run inference on the same Databricks Runtime version used during training, leveraging the saved databricks_runtime field in MLmodel metadata to ensure dependency compatibility.
* [Databricks Runtime Version Compatibility](/concepts/databricks-runtime-version-compatibility.md) - Annotations (DBR tags) indicating which Databricks Runtime ML version introduced each parameter, helping users determine feature availability.
* [Databricks S3 Commit Service and Multi-cluster Writes](/concepts/databricks-s3-commit-service-and-multi-cluster-writes.md) - Databricks provides an S3 commit service to support multi-cluster writes within a single workspace; certain features like SSE-C and STS-dependent credential paths are unsupported in this mode.
* [Databricks SDK Authentication Methods](/concepts/databricks-sdk-authentication-methods.md) - Multiple ways to authenticate the WorkspaceClient: hard-coded credentials, configuration profiles, or environment variables (DATABRICKS_HOST and DATABRICKS_TOKEN).
* [Databricks SDK Data Profiling API](/concepts/databricks-sdk-data-profiling-api.md) - The Python SDK and REST API for creating, refreshing, viewing, and deleting data profiles on Unity Catalog tables, including support for scheduling and notifications.
* [Databricks SDK for Python Authentication Methods](/concepts/databricks-sdk-authentication-methods.md) - Three ways to authenticate the WorkspaceClient: hard-coded host/token, configuration profiles, or environment variables (DATABRICKS_HOST and DATABRICKS_TOKEN). The SPARK_REMOTE environment variable is not supported.
* [Databricks SDK for Python Integration](/concepts/databricks-sdk-for-python-integration.md) - The Databricks SDK for Python, included with Databricks Connect, provides not only dbutils access via WorkspaceClient but also full access to any Databricks REST API at both workspace and account levels.
* [Databricks SDK for Python REST API Access](/concepts/databricks-sdk-for-python-rest-api-access.md) - The included Databricks SDK for Python can be used to access any available Databricks REST API, beyond just the dbutils utilities
* [Databricks SDK serving endpoint query](/concepts/databricks-sdk-serving-endpoint-query.md) - Using the Databricks Python SDK's WorkspaceClient.serving_endpoints_data_plane.query() method to directly query route-optimized endpoints with service principal credentials.
* [Databricks Secret Scopes](/concepts/databricks-secret-scopes.md) - A secure storage mechanism for credentials that can be referenced by model serving endpoints using the {{secrets/scope/key}} syntax
* [Databricks Secret Scopes for Model Serving](/concepts/databricks-secret-scopes-for-model-serving.md) - Creating and managing secret scopes (via CLI or API) that model serving endpoints read from at serving time to populate secrets-based environment variables.
* [Databricks Secrets for ML Model Credentials](/concepts/databricks-secrets-for-ml-model-credentials.md) - A pattern for securely injecting Hugging Face access tokens (or other sensitive credentials) into distributed training workloads by storing them as Databricks secrets and referencing them in the workload YAML rather than hard-coding them in scripts.
* [Databricks Secrets for Online Store Credentials](/concepts/databricks-secrets-for-online-store-credentials.md) - Storing read-only and read-write credentials for third-party online stores in Databricks secret scopes, organized by prefix-based naming conventions for DynamoDB and SQL stores.
* [Databricks Secrets for Third-Party Online Store Credentials](/concepts/databricks-secrets-for-online-store-credentials.md) - Storing read-only and read-write credentials (access keys, passwords, usernames) in Databricks secret scopes with a consistent prefix naming scheme for authenticating to online stores.
* [Databricks Security Perimeter](/concepts/databricks-security-perimeter.md) - A security boundary within which Databricks hosts third-party foundation model endpoints, ensuring data does not leave the Databricks-controlled environment.
* [Databricks Serverless Budget Policy 403 Error](/concepts/databricks-serverless-budget-policy-403-error.md) - A 403 Forbidden error (PERMISSION_DENIED) that occurs when no fallback budget policy is available and no policy is manually set on an MLflow experiment.
* [Databricks Serverless Forecasting](/concepts/databricks-serverless-forecasting.md) - A fully-managed AutoML forecasting capability on Databricks that automatically selects algorithms and hyperparameters for time-series prediction using serverless compute.
* [Databricks Serverless Forecasting with AutoML](/concepts/databricks-serverless-forecasting.md) - A UI-driven feature in Databricks that automates time series forecasting by selecting optimal algorithms and hyperparameters on fully-managed compute resources.
* [Databricks Serverless GPU](/concepts/databricks-serverless-gpu.md) - A serverless compute environment on Databricks that provides on-demand GPU resources (e.g., 8×H100) for distributed training workloads without requiring manual cluster management.
* [Databricks Serverless GPU Cluster](/concepts/databricks-serverless-gpu-cluster.md) - On-demand GPU compute infrastructure on Databricks used for training deep learning forecasting models
* [Databricks serverless GPU clusters](/concepts/databricks-serverless-gpu-clusters.md) - A compute environment on Databricks (AWS) that enables serverless GPU-accelerated workloads for classic machine learning tasks
* [Databricks Serverless GPU Compute](/concepts/databricks-serverless-gpu.md) - Databricks managed compute service that automatically scales GPU resources and handles provisioning, data distribution, and synchronization for distributed training workloads.
* [Databricks Serverless GPU Compute Configuration](/concepts/databricks-serverless-gpu-compute-configuration.md) - The workflow to configure a Databricks notebook with serverless GPU compute, including selecting accelerator type (8xH100) and environment (AI v5)
* [Databricks Serverless GPU Compute for LLM Training](/concepts/databricks-serverless-gpu-compute-for-llm-training.md) - Databricks AI Runtime environment providing serverless GPU compute with A10 accelerators, pre-installed with Unsloth and deep learning libraries for fine-tuning large language models.
* [Databricks Serverless GPU Compute for ML](/concepts/databricks-serverless-gpu-compute-for-ml.md) - A Databricks compute environment (e.g., 1xA10 GPU) optimized for running GPU-accelerated machine learning workloads with Unity Catalog integration.
* [Databricks Serverless GPU Distributed Training](/concepts/databricks-serverless-gpu-distributed-training.md) - A Databricks compute paradigm that provisions on-demand GPU clusters (e.g., 8xH100) for distributed training using the @distributed decorator, handling data parallelism automatically.
* [Databricks Serverless GPU for Deep Learning](/concepts/databricks-serverless-gpu-for-deep-learning.md) - A cloud compute environment on Databricks that provides on-demand GPU acceleration (e.g., 1xA10) for training deep learning models without managing infrastructure.
* [Databricks Serverless GPU with @distributed Decorator](/concepts/databricks-serverless-gpu-with-distributed-decorator.md) - Databricks serverless GPU compute with a Python decorator (`@distributed`) that transparently launches training across multiple H100 GPUs on a single node.
* [Databricks Spark Configuration for MLflow Tracking](/concepts/databricks-spark-configuration-for-mlflow-tracking.md) - Spark configuration flags spark.databricks.mlflow.trackMLlib.enabled and spark.databricks.mlflow.autologging.enabled used to enable or disable legacy MLlib automated MLflow tracking in Databricks Runtime 10.2 ML and above.
* [Databricks Spark Job Project Format](/concepts/databricks-spark-job-project-format.md) - A specialized MLflow Project type for Databricks Spark Jobs clusters (Runtime 13.0+), using databricks_spark_job section with python_file or entry_points settings and python_libraries dependencies.
* [Databricks SQL Alert Configuration for Anomaly Detection](/concepts/databricks-sql-alert-configuration-for-anomaly-detection.md) - A method for creating anomaly detection alerts by querying the system.data_quality_monitoring.table_results system table with custom SQL, supporting advanced filtering, trigger conditions, and custom notification templates.
* [Databricks SQL Alert on Anomaly Detection Results](/concepts/databricks-sql-alert-on-anomaly-detection-results.md) - Method for creating advanced alerts by querying the system.data_quality_monitoring.table_results system table with Databricks SQL, supporting custom filtering and notification templates.
* [Databricks SQL Alerts](/concepts/databricks-sql-alerts.md) - The underlying alerting framework used to create profile alerts, supporting scheduled evaluation of queries and multi-channel notifications.
* [Databricks SQL Anomaly Alert Configuration](/concepts/databricks-sql-anomaly-alert-configuration.md) - An approach to configure anomaly detection alerts by querying the system.data_quality_monitoring.table_results system table with custom SQL, supporting advanced filtering and custom notification templates.
* [Databricks SQL Views for Trace Data](/concepts/databricks-sql-views-for-trace-data.md) - Databricks SQL views (trace_unified and trace_metadata) that transform OpenTelemetry table data into MLflow format for SQL-based trace querying.
* [Databricks SQLSTATE 0AKDC](/concepts/databricks-sqlstate-0akdc.md) - A SQLSTATE code in Databricks indicating a 'feature not supported' condition, associated with the DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE error class.
* [Databricks SQLSTATE KD00C](/concepts/databricks-sqlstate-kd00c.md) - The SQLSTATE class KD00C that categorizes datasource-specific errors within Databricks, including the DELTA_VERSIONS_NOT_CONTIGUOUS error.
* [Databricks table history operations](/concepts/databricks-table-history-operations.md) - Broader topic covering how to work with Delta table history, including DESCRIBE HISTORY and related tools, documented separately in the Databricks guide.
* [Databricks Token-Based Authentication for External API Calls](/concepts/databricks-token-based-authentication-for-external-api-calls.md) - Using a Databricks personal access token (DATABRICKS_TOKEN) as a Bearer token in HTTP headers to authenticate requests made to Databricks serving endpoints from external clients or SDKs.
* [Databricks Trace Archival](/concepts/databricks-trace-archival.md) - Mechanism for persisting MLflow traces and their assessments to Unity Catalog Delta tables for long-term storage and advanced analysis.
* [Databricks Unified ML Platform](/concepts/databricks-ml-platform.md) - A single, integrated platform covering the full data science and machine learning lifecycle from raw data ingestion through feature engineering, model training, deployment, and production monitoring.
* [Databricks Unity Catalog Data Classification](/concepts/databricks-unity-catalog-data-classification.md) - The data classification subsystem within Unity Catalog that uses system tags to identify and label sensitive data across tables.
* [Databricks Utilities (dbutils) via Connect](/concepts/databricks-utilities-dbutils-via-connect.md) - Accessing dbutils.fs and dbutils.secrets through Databricks Connect, including file copying between local and remote filesystems and secret management.
* [Databricks Utilities (dbutils) via Databricks Connect](/concepts/databricks-utilities-dbutils-via-connect.md) - The supported subset of Databricks Utilities commands accessible through Databricks Connect, including dbutils.fs for file operations and dbutils.secrets for secrets management.
* [Databricks Utilities Availability via Databricks Connect](/concepts/limited-dbutils-availability-in-databricks-connect.md) - Only dbutils.fs (file system) and dbutils.secrets are available through WorkspaceClient.dbutils in Databricks Connect, not the full set of Databricks Utilities
* [Databricks Utilities for Scala (dbutils-scala)](/concepts/databricks-utilities-for-scala-dbutils-scala.md) - A Scala library (com.databricks:databricks-dbutils-scala_2.12) that provides access to DBFS and secrets utilities via DBUtils.getDBUtils in Databricks Connect for Scala projects.
* [Databricks Utilities for Scala library](/concepts/databricks-utilities-for-scala-library.md) - A published library (com.databricks/databricks-dbutils-scala_2.12) that provides Scala bindings for Databricks Utilities, with a dependency automatically declared by Databricks Connect for Scala.
* [Databricks Utilities with Databricks Connect](/concepts/databricks-utilities-with-databricks-connect.md) - Using Databricks Utilities (dbutils) within a Databricks Connect session for Python.
* [Databricks Utilities with Unity Catalog Volumes](/concepts/databricks-utilities-with-unity-catalog-volumes.md) - Using the DBUtils.fs API in Scala to programmatically create, read, and delete files in Unity Catalog volume paths within Databricks Connect projects.
* [Databricks Visual Studio Code Extension](/concepts/databricks-visual-studio-code-extension.md) - A VS Code extension that uses Databricks Connect to provide built-in debugging of user code on Databricks compute.
* [Databricks vs OSS MLflow Search Differences](/concepts/databricks-vs-oss-mlflow-search-differences.md) - Databricks-managed MLflow shares most search query syntax with open-source MLflow but has field-level differences in supported filters and capabilities.
* [Databricks Workspace Feature Store (Deprecated)](/concepts/databricks-workspace-feature-store-deprecated.md) - The legacy databricks-feature-store Python package, deprecated as of version 0.17.0, with all modules moved to databricks-feature-engineering.
* [Databricks Workspace Feature Store (Legacy)](/concepts/databricks-workspace-feature-store-legacy.md) - A legacy feature store UI in Databricks on AWS for managing, searching, and tracking feature tables used in machine learning workflows.
* [Databricks Workspace Feature Store UI](/concepts/workspace-feature-store-ui.md) - A user interface within Databricks on AWS for managing, searching, and governing feature tables used in machine learning workflows.
* [Databricks Workspace-level Table Access Control Setting](/concepts/databricks-workspace-level-table-access-control-setting.md) - An admin setting on the Security tab of the Databricks workspace settings page that must be enabled before users can configure Python and SQL table access control on clusters.
* [databricks-feature-store (legacy) deprecation](/concepts/databricks-feature-store-legacy-deprecation.md) - The legacy databricks-feature-store package is deprecated as of version 0.17.0 in favor of databricks-feature-engineering
* [databricks-gpt-5-mini Model](/concepts/databricks-gpt-5-mini-model.md) - A Databricks-hosted GPT model used as the judge/grader for Arize Phoenix scorers in MLflow evaluations
* [Databricks-hosted Claude model integration](/concepts/databricks-hosted-claude-model-integration.md) - Use of Databricks-hosted Claude models (via databricks:/ paths) as judges or generation models within MLflow GenAI evaluation workflows.
* [Databricks-hosted foundation models](/concepts/databricks-hosted-foundation-models.md) - State-of-the-art open models hosted and managed by Databricks, available as preconfigured endpoints through Foundation Model APIs for pay-per-token and provisioned throughput modes.
* [Databricks-hosted foundation models via OpenAI client](/concepts/databricks-hosted-foundation-models-via-openai-client.md) - The databricks_openai.DatabricksOpenAI client allows calling Databricks-hosted LLMs (e.g., Claude Sonnet 4) using the standard OpenAI SDK interface.
* [Databricks-Hosted GPT-5 Foundation Models](/concepts/databricks-hosted-gpt-5-foundation-models.md) - A family of pay-per-token foundation model endpoints (databricks-gpt-5 through databricks-gpt-5-5-pro) hosted on Databricks that support the OpenAI Responses API with various model sizes and capabilities.
* [Databricks-Hosted Judge LLM](/concepts/databricks-hosted-judge-llm.md) - The default LLM used by built-in judges in Databricks, designed specifically for performing GenAI quality assessments and accessible via Databricks serving endpoints.
* [Databricks-hosted LLM for Judges](/concepts/databricks-hosted-llm-for-judges.md) - The default LLM model provided by Databricks that powers built-in GenAI quality assessment judges, with the option to switch to other models via the 'model' argument.
* [Databricks-hosted LLM Serving](/concepts/databricks-hosted-llm-serving.md) - Using Databricks-hosted foundation models (e.g., Claude Sonnet) via OpenAI-compatible API endpoints with Databricks credentials for GenAI application development.
* [Databricks-hosted LLMs](/concepts/databricks-hosted-llms.md) - Large Language Models served directly from Databricks workspaces, accessible via the databricks-openai library which provides a compatible OpenAI client interface for models like databricks-claude-sonnet-4.
* [Databricks-hosted LLMs with MLflow](/concepts/databricks-hosted-llms-with-mlflow.md) - Integration of Databricks-hosted foundation models (via the databricks-openai package) with MLflow Tracing and Evaluation for GenAI applications.
* [Databricks-hosted LLMs with OpenAI-compatible API](/concepts/databricks-hosted-llms-with-openai-compatible-api.md) - Using the databricks-openai package to create an OpenAI-compatible client that connects to Databricks-hosted foundation models for GenAI application development.
* [Databricks-hosted MLflow tracking server](/concepts/databricks-hosted-mlflow-tracking-server.md) - Default managed MLflow tracking server provided by Databricks that stores experiment data in the workspace with no additional setup required.
* [Databricks-Managed MLflow](/concepts/databricks-managed-mlflow.md) - Databricks' managed version of the open-source MLflow platform providing experiment tracking, model registry, and audit trails integrated with Unity Catalog and Git.
* [Databricks-to-Databricks OpenSharing Protocol](/concepts/databricks-to-databricks-opensharing-protocol.md) - A Databricks-managed secure data sharing protocol that does not require credential files, relying instead on Unity Catalog metastore identifiers for establishing connections between provider and recipient workspaces.
* [Databricks-to-Databricks Sharing](/concepts/databricks-to-databricks-sharing.md) - A sharing model where the recipient must be a Databricks user with Unity Catalog; the provider uses the recipient's metastore identifier to create a secure connection.
* [Databricks-to-Databricks Sharing Model](/concepts/databricks-to-databricks-sharing.md) - A sharing model where both the data provider and recipient use Databricks workspaces with Unity Catalog, using a metastore sharing identifier to establish a secure connection.
* [Databricks-to-Databricks View Sharing](/concepts/databricks-to-databricks-view-sharing.md) - A Delta Sharing mode that allows sharing views between Databricks workspaces while restricting SQL functions for security
* [Databricks-to-Databricks vs Databricks-to-Open Sharing](/concepts/databricks-to-databricks-vs-databricks-to-open-sharing.md) - Two OpenSharing protocols: Databricks-to-Databricks (DATABRICKS auth) for cross-Databricks sharing, and Databricks-to-Open (TOKEN/OAUTH/OIDC auth) for sharing with non-Databricks recipients.
* [Databricks-to-Open Sharing](/concepts/databricks-to-open-sharing.md) - A sharing model where the recipient can use any tool (including Databricks) to access shared data via an activation URL, portal link, credential file, or OIDC federation.
* [Databricks-to-Open Sharing (Bearer Token Authentication)](/concepts/databricks-to-open-sharing-bearer-token-authentication.md) - An authentication flow in Delta Sharing where data providers create recipient objects using bearer tokens for non-Databricks users to access shared data without a Unity Catalog workspace.
* [Databricks-to-Open Sharing Model](/concepts/databricks-to-open-sharing.md) - A sharing model where recipients can use any tool (including Databricks) to access shared data via an activation URL or portal link to download a credential file or URL.
* [Databricks-to-Open Sharing Protocol](/concepts/databricks-to-open-sharing-protocol.md) - Delta Sharing protocol variant on Databricks that enables data providers to share data with recipients using the open-source Delta Sharing standard, supporting features like IP access lists and SecureConnect.
* [Databricks-to-Open Sharing vs Databricks-to-Databricks Sharing](/concepts/databricks-to-open-sharing-vs-databricks-to-databricks-sharing.md) - Distinction between sharing data to recipients without a Unity Catalog-enabled Databricks workspace (Open Sharing) and recipients with such a workspace, with OIDC federation being an alternative to bearer tokens for the former.
* [databricks.automl.classify](/concepts/databricksautomlclassify.md) - AutoML method that trains classification models, returning an AutoMLSummary with trial notebooks and model metrics.
* [databricks.automl.forecast](/concepts/databricksautomlforecast.md) - AutoML method that trains forecasting models with required time_col, frequency, horizon, and optional identity_col for time series data.
* [databricks.automl.import_notebook](/concepts/databricksautomlimport_notebook.md) - Method to import a notebook saved as an MLflow artifact, returning an ImportNotebookResult with path and URL.
* [databricks.automl.regress](/concepts/databricksautomlregress.md) - AutoML method that trains regression models, returning an AutoMLSummary with per-trial notebooks and metrics; primary metric defaults to r2.
* [databricks.migrations.migrate_traces_to_uc](/concepts/databricksmigrationsmigrate_traces_to_uc.md) - A Python SDK module providing the run() function used to perform the migration from a source MLflow experiment to a target Unity Catalog-backed experiment.
* [DatabricksSession](/concepts/databrickssession.md) - The primary entry point for creating a SparkSession when using Databricks Connect, bridging local Python code to a remote Databricks compute environment.
* [DatabricksSession API](/concepts/databrickssession.md) - The primary entry-point class in Databricks Connect that creates a SparkSession bound to a remote Databricks cluster.
* [DatabricksSession API Portability](/concepts/databrickssession-api-portability.md) - Databricks Connect APIs are available in Databricks notebooks as part of the runtime, enabling seamless code transitions
* [DatabricksSession Authentication for DBUtils](/concepts/databrickssession-authentication-for-dbutils.md) - Authentication for the Databricks Utilities for Scala library is determined through initialization of the DatabricksSession class in a Databricks Connect for Scala project.
* [DatabricksSession authentication for Scala Utilities](/concepts/databrickssession-authentication-for-dbutils.md) - Authentication for the Databricks Utilities for Scala library is determined through initializing the DatabricksSession class in the Databricks Connect project for Scala.
* [DatabricksSession behavior differences](/concepts/databrickssession-behavior-differences.md) - The behavior of DatabricksSession.builder.getOrCreate() and create() differs between local development environments and Databricks workspace notebooks/jobs, particularly regarding default session handling and error behavior.
* [DatabricksSession Builder API](/concepts/databrickssession-builder-api.md) - The programmatic API (remote(), profile(), serverless(), sdkConfig(), getOrCreate()) for configuring and initializing a Databricks Connect session.
* [DatabricksSession builder configuration chain](/concepts/databrickssession-builder-configuration-chain.md) - The ordered precedence of configuration methods for DatabricksSession: remote() method, config profile, DATABRICKS_CONFIG_PROFILE env var, individual env vars, and DEFAULT profile.
* [DatabricksSession Builder Configuration Methods](/concepts/databrickssession-builder-configuration-methods.md) - The DatabricksSession class provides builder methods (remote(), profile(), sdkConfig(), serverless(), getOrCreate()) to configure connections to Databricks compute.
* [DatabricksSession Builder Patterns](/concepts/databrickssession-builder-patterns.md) - The getOrCreate() and create() methods on DatabricksSession.builder behave differently depending on whether code runs in a local IDE (Databricks Connect) or in the Databricks workspace (notebooks/jobs).
* [DatabricksSession class](/concepts/databrickssession-class.md) - The class used to initialize authentication for Databricks Connect projects in Scala, which also determines authentication for the Databricks Utilities for Scala library.
* [DatabricksSession initialization for authentication](/concepts/databrickssession-initialization-for-authentication.md) - Authentication for the Databricks Utilities for Scala library is determined through initializing the DatabricksSession class in a Databricks Connect for Scala project.
* [DatabricksSession local development behavior](/concepts/databrickssession-local-development-behavior.md) - In local IDE environments, getOrCreate() reuses existing sessions while create() always creates new sessions with connection params from code, env vars, or config files
* [DatabricksSession remote() Method](/concepts/databrickssession-remote-method.md) - A method that allows DatabricksSession to connect to Databricks compute not attached to the current notebook or job by specifying configuration kwargs or individual configuration methods like host() or token().
* [DatabricksSession workspace behavior](/concepts/databrickssession-workspace-behavior.md) - In Databricks notebooks/jobs, getOrCreate() returns the default pre-configured Spark session, while create() requires explicit connection parameters
* [DatabricksSession Workspace vs Local Behavior](/concepts/databrickssession-workspace-vs-local-behavior.md) - Key behavioral differences of DatabricksSession.getOrCreate() and create() when run in a local IDE versus a Databricks notebook or job, including default session handling and connection parameter requirements.
* [DatabricksSession.getOrCreate() vs create()](/concepts/databrickssessiongetorcreate-vs-create.md) - Behavioral differences between getOrCreate() and create() methods when running locally vs in Databricks workspace
* [DataCollatorWithPadding](/concepts/datacollatorwithpadding.md) - A Hugging Face data collator that dynamically pads input sequences to the longest sequence in each batch during training, providing a memory-efficient alternative to static padding.
* [DataOps on Databricks](/concepts/dataops-on-databricks.md) - Practice of applying CI/CD principles to data pipelines within the Databricks platform, ensuring reliable and secure data for ML models.
* [Dataset Lineage Tracking](/concepts/dataset-lineage-tracking.md) - The mechanism for tracking provenance of evaluation dataset records using source fields that identify whether a record came from a human, a document, or a production trace.
* [Dataset Record Tagging](/concepts/dataset-record-tagging.md) - The ability to add and edit metadata tags on individual evaluation dataset records through the MLflow UI for organization and filtering.
* [Dataset Size Estimation with load_dataset_builder](/concepts/dataset-size-estimation-with-load_dataset_builder.md) - Using Hugging Face's load_dataset_builder to inspect download and dataset sizes before committing to a large download
* [Dataset Source Lineage](/concepts/dataset-source-lineage.md) - The source field in evaluation datasets for tracking record provenance via human, document, or trace origin types.
* [Dataset Synchronization](/concepts/dataset-synchronization.md) - A mechanism to sync collected Expectations from labeling sessions to Evaluation Datasets via an intelligent upsert operation, using trace inputs as unique keys.
* [db_repl and brickster Package](/concepts/db_repl-and-brickster-package.md) - The brickster R package provides a db_repl function as an alternative to sparklyr for using full Spark MLlib capabilities within Databricks.
* [DBFS Access Patterns for Shared Clusters](/concepts/dbfs-access-patterns-for-shared-clusters.md) - DBFS FUSE paths fail on shared clusters in Unity Catalog; alternatives include using dbutils, spark readers, or Unity Catalog Volumes.
* [DBFS Migration to Unity Catalog Volumes](/concepts/dbfs-migration-to-unity-catalog-volumes.md) - The process of migrating raw files, notebooks, scripts, and init scripts from DBFS root and mounts to governed Unity Catalog volumes and workspace files.
* [DBFS root and DBFS mounts deprecation](/concepts/dbfs-root-and-dbfs-mounts-deprecation.md) - DBFS root storage and DBFS mounts will no longer be available in new workspaces created after September 30, 2026, requiring migration of files to volumes and external locations.
* [DBFS root location](/concepts/dbfs-root-location.md) - A sibling path under a workspace's default storage path that does not create a conflict when used as an external location, as it is not the same as the internal storage path.
* [dbt Python Models on Databricks](/concepts/dbt-python-models-on-databricks.md) - Running dbt Python models (beta, requires dbt >= 1.3) as part of a Databricks job, noting that SQL warehouses are not supported for Python models and all-purpose compute must be used.
* [dbt Task Artifact and Output Retrieval](/concepts/dbt-task-artifact-and-output-retrieval.md) - Retrieving dbt task output, inline logs, and archived artifacts (logs, results, manifests, configuration) programmatically via the Databricks CLI or Jobs API.
* [dbt Task Configuration via UI and API](/concepts/dbt-task-configuration-via-ui-and-api.md) - Step-by-step configuration of a dbt task in the Databricks Jobs UI and via the Jobs API, including source repo, commands, SQL warehouse, compute, and environment settings.
