* [dbt Task in Databricks Jobs](/concepts/dbt-task-in-databricks-jobs.md) - Running dbt Core projects as a task within Databricks Lakeflow Jobs, enabling automation, monitoring, multi-step workflows, and artifact archiving.
* [dbt-databricks Adapter Package](/concepts/dbt-databricks-adapter-package.md) - The recommended dbt adapter package for Databricks, a fork of dbt-spark optimized for Databricks, used to connect dbt Core to Databricks compute and SQL warehouses.
* [dbutils.fs and dbutils.secrets in Databricks Connect](/concepts/dbutilsfs-and-dbutilssecrets-in-databricks-connect.md) - The only two Databricks Utilities accessible through the dbutils variable in Databricks Connect for Python
* [dbutils.fs via Databricks Connect](/concepts/dbutilsfs-via-databricks-connect.md) - The file system utility from Databricks Utilities accessible through WorkspaceClient.dbutils.fs for file operations on Databricks volumes.
* [dbutils.fs Volume Operations Example](/concepts/dbutilsfs-volume-operations-example.md) - A concrete Python example demonstrating file operations (put, head, rm) on Unity Catalog volumes using dbutils.fs via Databricks Connect's WorkspaceClient.
* [DBUtils.getDBUtils](/concepts/dbutilsgetdbutils.md) - The Scala API entry point in the Databricks Utilities for Scala library used to access DBFS and secrets functionality within Databricks Connect projects.
* [DBUtils.getDBUtils for Scala](/concepts/dbutilsgetdbutils.md) - The method to access Databricks Utilities (DBFS and secrets) in Scala via Databricks Connect
* [DBUtils.getDBUtils in Databricks Connect for Scala](/concepts/dbutilsgetdbutils.md) - The entry point for accessing Databricks Utilities (DBFS and Secrets) in Scala-based Databricks Connect projects via the DBUtils.getDBUtils() method from the com.databricks.sdk.scala.dbutils package.
* [DBUtils.getDBUtils() method](/concepts/dbutilsgetdbutils.md) - The entry point method in the dbutils-scala library that returns a DBUtils instance for accessing Databricks File System (DBFS) and secrets utilities within a Databricks Connect for Scala project.
* [dbutils.notebook.run()](/concepts/dbutilsnotebookrun.md) - An API method to call a Databricks notebook as a separate job, supporting parameter passing via widgets and return values via dbutils.notebook.exit().
* [dbutils.secrets via Databricks Connect](/concepts/dbutilssecrets-via-databricks-connect.md) - The secrets utility from Databricks Utilities accessible through WorkspaceClient.dbutils.secrets for managing sensitive information.
* [DDP (Distributed Data Parallel) on Databricks](/concepts/ddp-distributed-data-parallel-on-databricks.md) - A parallelism technique for multi-GPU training that replicates the model on each GPU and synchronizes gradients across devices.
* [DDP Applicability Criteria](/concepts/ddp-applicability-criteria.md) - Conditions under which DDP is appropriate: model fits in single GPU memory, scaling data throughput is the goal, and simplest distributed approach is desired
* [DDP Gradient Checkpointing for Distributed LLM Training](/concepts/ddp-gradient-checkpointing-for-distributed-llm-training.md) - The requirement to use non-reentrant gradient checkpointing (use_reentrant=False) when combining Unsloth with Distributed Data Parallel (DDP) to avoid variable-ready errors during multi-GPU training.
* [DDP gradient checkpointing with non-reentrant mode](/concepts/ddp-gradient-checkpointing-with-non-reentrant-mode.md) - A distributed training fix required when combining PyTorch DDP with gradient checkpointing to avoid 'mark a variable ready only once' errors.
* [Debugging Databricks Connect R Code in RStudio](/concepts/debugging-databricks-connect-r-code-in-rstudio.md) - The ability to set breakpoints and debug R code that runs against Databricks clusters through Databricks Connect within RStudio Desktop.
* [Decision Framework: DDP vs FSDP vs DeepSpeed](/concepts/decision-framework-ddp-vs-fsdp-vs-deepspeed.md) - Guidelines for choosing between DDP (simpler use cases), FSDP (PyTorch-native large model training), and DeepSpeed (advanced memory optimization with ZeRO stages) on Databricks.
* [Declarative Automation Bundles](/concepts/declarative-automation-bundles.md) - A collection of source files that serves as the end-to-end definition of a Databricks project, including ML resources like workspaces and pipelines defined as code for version control and testing.
* [Declarative Automation Bundles (Databricks)](/concepts/declarative-automation-bundles-databricks.md) - Databricks' recommended tool for building and deploying Scala JARs, providing correct Scala, JDK, and Databricks Connect versions pre-configured for serverless compute.
* [Declarative Automation Bundles for Scala JARs](/concepts/declarative-automation-bundles-databricks.md) - Databricks recommends Declarative Automation Bundles over manual JAR building; bundles handle correct version configuration, templating, and easy deployment to the workspace.
* [Declarative Feature Engineering API](/concepts/declarative-feature-engineering-api.md) - A Python-based API for defining, registering, and using machine learning features declaratively (vs. imperatively) with Unity Catalog on Databricks.
* [Declarative Feature Engineering APIs](/concepts/declarative-feature-engineering-apis.md) - A Databricks framework for defining and computing ML features using declarative objects rather than imperative data pipelines
* [Declarative Feature Lifecycle Workflows](/concepts/declarative-feature-lifecycle-workflows.md) - The end-to-end workflow covering feature development, model training, and feature materialization/serving with declarative APIs
* [Declarative Feature Limitations and Constraints](/concepts/declarative-feature-limitations-and-constraints.md) - Key constraints of declarative features including restrictions on entity column types, supported functions, source column uniqueness, and request source capabilities
* [Declarative Feature Serving Limitation](/concepts/declarative-feature-serving-limitation.md) - Feature Serving endpoints are not supported for Declarative Feature Engineering; models must be deployed via model serving endpoints using models logged through Unity Catalog.
* [Declarative Features Limitations and Constraints](/concepts/declarative-features-limitations-and-constraints.md) - Known limitations of the declarative feature APIs including entity column type restrictions, RequestSource scalar-only support, globally unique column names across sources, and limited UDAF function support.
* [Declarative YAML Job Configuration for ML Training](/concepts/declarative-yaml-job-configuration-for-ml-training.md) - The pattern of defining ML training jobs declaratively in YAML configuration files that can be checked into source control
* [Decorator Ordering Best Practices](/concepts/decorator-ordering-best-practices.md) - When combining @mlflow.trace with other decorators (e.g., from web frameworks), @mlflow.trace must be the outermost decorator to ensure complete observability of the function's execution.
* [Decorator Ordering for @mlflow.trace](/concepts/decorator-ordering-for-mlflowtrace.md) - The rule that @mlflow.trace must be the outermost decorator when combined with other decorators to ensure complete observability of function execution.
* [Decorator ordering with @mlflow.trace](/concepts/decorator-ordering-for-mlflowtrace.md) - The @mlflow.trace decorator must be the outermost decorator when combined with other decorators to ensure complete observability of function execution.
* [Decoupled Client-Server Architecture](/concepts/decoupled-client-server-architecture.md) - The architectural pattern underlying Databricks Connect where the client runs general code locally while Spark DataFrame operations are executed remotely on Databricks compute via Spark Connect.
* [Dedicated Access Mode for Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/dedicated-access-mode-for-databricks-runtime-ml.md) - A required access mode for Unity Catalog access on Databricks Runtime ML, automatically set when selecting Machine Learning compute, which can be scoped to a single user or group.
* [Dedicated Access Mode for ML Compute](/concepts/dedicated-access-mode-for-ml-compute.md) - Databricks Runtime ML requires Dedicated access mode for Unity Catalog data access, assigning compute to a single user or group with automatic permission down-scoping, with fine-grained access control available from Runtime 15.4 LTS ML.
* [Dedicated access mode with fine-grained access control](/concepts/dedicated-access-mode-with-fine-grained-access-control.md) - Restrictions and caveats when querying tables with row filters or column masks from dedicated access compute resources, including serverless FGAC enforcement and Cloud Fetch storage implications.
* [Deep Clone](/concepts/deep-clone.md) - A cloning strategy that creates a complete, independent copy of a source table, including all data and metadata.
* [Deep Clone (Databricks)](/concepts/deep-clone.md) - A complete, independent copy of a source Delta, Iceberg, or Parquet table, copying both data and metadata to a target location.
* [Deep Clone (Delta Lake)](/concepts/deep-clone.md) - A complete, independent copy of a source Delta, Iceberg, or Parquet table, including both data and metadata.
* [Deep Clone (Delta Table)](/concepts/deep-clone.md) - A complete, independent copy of a source Delta, Iceberg, or Parquet table, including both data and metadata.
* [Deep Clone Limitations for Streaming Tables](/concepts/deep-clone-limitations-for-streaming-tables.md) - Streaming tables have specific restrictions when used as the source of a deep clone operation
* [Deep Clone of Streaming Tables](/concepts/deep-clone-of-streaming-tables.md) - The operation of creating a deep copy of a Delta streaming table, which has specific constraints and requirements in Databricks
* [Deep clone restrictions for streaming tables](/concepts/deep-clone-restrictions-for-streaming-tables.md) - Databricks imposes multiple restrictions on deep cloning streaming tables: no custom LOCATION, only default publishing mode, WITH HISTORY required, no scheduled tables, no time travel.
* [Deep Clone Restrictions on Streaming Tables](/concepts/deep-clone-restrictions-on-streaming-tables.md) - A set of constraints preventing deep clone operations on Delta streaming tables, including location, architecture, history, scheduling, and time travel limitations.
* [Deep learning based recommender systems](/concepts/deep-learning-based-recommender-systems.md) - Notebook examples for building recommendation systems on Databricks AI Runtime using modern deep learning approaches like two-tower models.
* [Deep Learning Based Recommender Systems on Databricks](/concepts/deep-learning-based-recommender-systems-on-databricks.md) - The overall pattern of building modern recommendation systems using deep learning approaches within the Databricks platform, leveraging AI Runtime and GPU acceleration.
* [Deep learning best practices on Databricks](/concepts/deep-learning-best-practices-on-databricks.md) - General guidelines and optimization strategies for developing and fine-tuning deep learning models on the Databricks platform.
* [Deep Learning Data Loading with Delta Lake](/concepts/deep-learning-data-loading-with-delta-lake.md) - Best practices for using Delta Lake tables and streaming approaches (PyTorch IterableDataset, Hugging Face datasets, Ray Data) to optimize data throughput for deep learning workloads on Databricks.
* [Deep Learning Inference on Databricks](/concepts/deep-learning-inference-on-databricks.md) - Strategies for online, batch, and streaming inference including Model Serving, Pandas UDFs, and considerations for CPU vs GPU selection.
* [Deep Learning on Databricks](/concepts/deep-learning-on-databricks.md) - Overview of using PyTorch, TensorFlow, and distributed training to develop and fine-tune deep learning models on the Databricks platform.
* [Deep Learning Performance Optimization on Databricks](/concepts/deep-learning-performance-optimization-techniques.md) - Techniques including early stopping, batch size tuning, transfer learning, and GPU monitoring to optimize deep learning training performance.
* [Deep Learning Performance Optimization Techniques](/concepts/deep-learning-performance-optimization-techniques.md) - Key optimization strategies for deep learning on Databricks including early stopping, batch size tuning (with learning rate adjustment by sqrt(n)), transfer learning, and GPU scheduling to maximize utilization.
* [Deep Learning Performance Optimization Techniques on Databricks](/concepts/deep-learning-performance-optimization-techniques.md) - Techniques including early stopping, batch size tuning, learning rate adjustment, and transfer learning to optimize deep learning model training on Databricks
* [Deep Learning Recommender Systems (Databricks)](/concepts/deep-learning-recommender-systems-databricks.md) - Examples for building recommendation systems using deep learning approaches like two-tower models on AI Runtime
* [Deep learning recommender systems on Databricks](/concepts/deep-learning-recommender-systems-databricks.md) - Examples for building recommendation systems using modern deep learning approaches like two-tower models on AI Runtime.
* [Deep Learning Training Best Practices on Databricks](/concepts/deep-learning-training-best-practices-on-databricks.md) - Key training optimization techniques including early stopping, batch size tuning with learning rate adjustment, transfer learning, and moving to distributed training.
* [DeepAR](/concepts/deepar.md) - A recurrent neural network (RNN) model for probabilistic forecasting that produces prediction intervals alongside point forecasts.
* [DeepAR Estimator](/concepts/deepar.md) - A probabilistic forecasting model in GluonTS using recurrent neural networks (RNNs) to produce prediction intervals and point forecasts for time series data.
* [DeepAR Model](/concepts/deepar-model.md) - A probabilistic forecasting model from GluonTS used for time-series prediction of electricity consumption
* [DeepAR Probabilistic Forecasting Model](/concepts/deepar-probabilistic-forecasting-model.md) - A neural network-based model from GluonTS that produces probabilistic forecasts for time series data, demonstrated in an electricity consumption forecasting workflow.
* [DeepEval Evaluation Framework](/concepts/deepeval-evaluation-framework.md) - A comprehensive evaluation framework for LLM applications that provides metrics for RAG systems, agents, conversational AI, and safety evaluation.
* [DeepEval Framework](/concepts/deepeval-framework.md) - A comprehensive evaluation framework for LLM applications that provides metrics for RAG systems, agents, conversational AI, and safety evaluation.
* [DeepEval Integration with MLflow](/concepts/deepeval-integration-with-mlflow.md) - MLflow integrates with DeepEval so that DeepEval metrics can be used as scorers for LLM evaluation
* [DeepEval Metric Categories](/concepts/deepeval-metric-categories.md) - DeepEval provides five categories of metrics: RAG metrics, Agentic metrics, Conversational metrics, Safety metrics, and Non-LLM metrics.
* [DeepEval Scorer API](/concepts/deepeval-scorer-api.md) - Python API for invoking DeepEval scorers directly or via mlflow.genai.evaluate(), with threshold, model and metric-specific parameters
* [DeepEval Scorer Categories](/concepts/deepeval-metric-categories.md) - DeepEval provides five categories of scorers: RAG metrics, agentic metrics, conversational metrics, safety metrics, and non-LLM metrics.
* [DeepEval Scorer Configuration](/concepts/deepeval-scorer-configuration.md) - DeepEval scorers accept metric-specific keyword parameters including model, threshold, include_reason, window_size, and strict_mode for customizing evaluation behavior.
* [DeepEval Scorer Configuration Parameters](/concepts/deepeval-scorer-configuration.md) - DeepEval scorers accept metric-specific parameters like threshold, model, include_reason, window_size, and strict_mode to customize evaluation behavior.
* [DeepEval Scorers Integration](/concepts/deepeval-scorers-integration.md) - MLflow integration with DeepEval offering the broadest metric coverage across RAG, agents, conversational AI, and safety including agent plan quality and multi-turn conversation completeness
* [DeepEval-MLflow Integration](/concepts/deepeval-mlflow-integration.md) - MLflow natively integrates DeepEval scorers for evaluating LLM applications within the MLflow evaluation framework.
* [DeepSpeed](/concepts/deepspeed.md) - A deep learning optimization library by Microsoft that provides advanced memory optimization techniques for distributed training of large models
* [DeepSpeed Distributor](/concepts/deepspeed-distributor.md) - A Databricks distributed training solution built on TorchDistributor, using Microsoft's DeepSpeed library for optimized memory usage, reduced communication overhead, and advanced pipeline parallelism.
* [DeepSpeed Distributor (Databricks)](/concepts/deepspeed-distributor.md) - A Databricks solution for distributed PyTorch training built on top of TorchDistributor, using the DeepSpeed library to overcome GPU memory constraints and scale large model training.
* [DeepSpeed Distributor on Databricks](/concepts/deepspeed-distributor.md) - A Microsoft open-source library integrated with Databricks for distributed training with optimized memory usage, reduced communication overhead, and pipeline parallelism.
* [DeepSpeed Library](/concepts/deepspeed-library.md) - An open-source library developed by Microsoft that offers optimized memory usage, reduced communication overhead, and advanced pipeline parallelism for scaling model training on standard hardware.
* [DeepSpeed on Databricks](/concepts/deepspeed-on-databricks.md) - A parallelism technique from Microsoft that provides ZeRO optimization stages and other memory-saving strategies for large-scale training on Databricks.
* [DeepSpeed Use Cases](/concepts/deepspeed-use-cases.md) - The DeepSpeed distributor is beneficial for scenarios with low GPU memory, large model training, and large input data such as batch inference.
* [DeepSpeed ZeRO Stage 1](/concepts/deepspeed-zero-stage-2.md) - ZeRO stage that shards optimizer states across GPUs, reducing memory consumption while gradients and model parameters remain fully replicated.
* [DeepSpeed ZeRO Stage 2](/concepts/deepspeed-zero-stage-2.md) - ZeRO stage that shards both optimizer states and gradients across GPUs, further reducing memory compared to Stage 1.
* [DeepSpeed ZeRO Stage 3](/concepts/deepspeed-zero-stage-3.md) - A memory optimization technique that partitions model parameters, gradients, and optimizer states across GPUs to enable training of large models
* [DeepSpeed ZeRO Stage 3 Optimization](/concepts/deepspeed-zero-stage-3.md) - A memory optimization technique that partitions model parameters, gradients, and optimizer states across all GPUs to reduce per-GPU memory consumption, enabling training of large models that wouldn't fit on a single GPU.
* [Default Budget Policy Fallback Failure](/concepts/default-budget-policy-fallback-failure.md) - When a Databricks workspace disables the default serverless budget policy, MLflow fails to register scorers or run evaluations unless a policy is explicitly set on the experiment.
* [Default Catalog Configuration in Unity Catalog](/concepts/default-catalog-configuration-in-unity-catalog.md) - When a workspace is enabled for Unity Catalog, a default catalog is configured; automatic enablement sets the workspace catalog as default, while manual enablement sets `hive_metastore` as default, affecting how unqualified queries resolve.
* [DEFAULT Configuration Profile Constraint](/concepts/default-configuration-profile-constraint.md) - A constraint that pytest with Databricks Connect only works with the DEFAULT configuration profile, which must include the target cluster or serverless compute.
* [Default Email Destinations](/concepts/default-email-destinations.md) - A metastore-wide setting that routes access requests to the object owner's email when no explicit destination is configured.
* [DEFAULT Expression in UPDATE](/concepts/default-expression-in-update.md) - Sets a column to its defined default value (or NULL if no default is defined) during an UPDATE operation. Available in Databricks Runtime 11.3 LTS and above.
* [Default Feature Table Permissions](/concepts/default-feature-table-permissions.md) - Upon creation, the creator and workspace admins automatically receive CAN MANAGE permission on a feature table, while all other users have NO PERMISSIONS.
* [DEFAULT Keyword in MERGE Actions](/concepts/default-keyword-in-merge-actions.md) - The DEFAULT keyword can be used as an expression in UPDATE SET and INSERT VALUES clauses to explicitly set a column to its default value (Databricks Runtime 11.3 LTS+).
* [Default Permission Behavior on Feature Table Creation](/concepts/default-permission-behavior-on-feature-table-creation.md) - Default permissions assigned when a feature table is created: creator and workspace admins get CAN MANAGE, all others get NO PERMISSIONS
* [Default Permissions for Feature Tables](/concepts/default-permissions-for-feature-tables.md) - By default, the creator and workspace admins get CAN MANAGE permission while all other users have no permissions on a new feature table
* [Default Permissions on Feature Table Creation](/concepts/default-permissions-on-feature-table-creation.md) - When a feature table is created in Databricks Feature Store, the creator and workspace admins get CAN MANAGE, while other users get NO PERMISSIONS by default.
* [Default Serverless Budget Policy Fallback Error](/concepts/databricks-serverless-budget-policy-403-error.md) - A 403 Forbidden error that occurs when a workspace has disabled the default serverless budget policy and MLflow cannot automatically select a policy for an experiment.
* [Default Spark Session in Databricks Workspace](/concepts/default-spark-session-in-databricks-workspace.md) - In Databricks notebooks and jobs, the `spark` variable provides a pre-configured default Spark session that connects to the attached compute, accessible via DatabricksSession.builder.getOrCreate() without extra configuration.
* [Default values for feature lookups](/concepts/default-values-for-feature-lookups.md) - A mechanism in FeatureLookup that supplies fallback values when a feature column has no value, with the restriction that renamed outputs must use the new name in default_values.
* [Default vs AI Environments in AI Runtime](/concepts/default-vs-ai-environments-in-ai-runtime.md) - Two base environment options: 'None' (default environment) and 'AI v4/v5' (Databricks AI environment), configurable in notebook and job definitions.
* [Default workspace admin privileges (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/default-workspace-admin-privileges-on-metastore.md) - A set of metastore-level privileges (CREATE CATALOG, CREATE EXTERNAL LOCATION, CREATE SHARE, etc.) auto-granted to workspace admins in automatically-enabled Unity Catalog workspaces, represented via a system group.
* [Default Workspace Admin Privileges on Metastore](/concepts/default-workspace-admin-privileges-on-metastore.md) - The set of privileges (CREATE CATALOG, CREATE EXTERNAL LOCATION, etc.) automatically granted to workspace admins on the attached metastore in auto-enabled workspaces.
* [Degree and Degree Distribution](/concepts/degree-and-degree-distribution.md) - Degree is the number of edges incident to a node (with in-degree and out-degree for directed graphs); degree distribution describes the count of nodes at each degree level, revealing network structure and organization.
* [DELETE FROM (Delta Lake)](/concepts/delete-from-delta-lake.md) - SQL statement to delete rows from Delta Lake tables, optionally filtered by a predicate
* [DELETE FROM for Delta Lake](/concepts/delete-from-for-delta-lake.md) - The DELETE FROM statement in Databricks SQL is used to delete rows from Delta Lake tables, with optional predicate filtering and support for subqueries in the WHERE clause.
* [Delete Operations on Prompt Registry](/concepts/delete-operations-on-prompt-registry.md) - Deleting prompts requires deleting all versions first for Unity Catalog registries; other registry types allow direct prompt deletion without version cleanup.
* [Deleting Materialized Features](/concepts/deleting-materialized-features.md) - Process for removing materialized features by passing the appropriate materialized feature object; aggregation features require deleting the offline version to propagate to both stores, while ColumnSelection features are deleted directly via online materialized feature.
* [Deletion Vector constraints for Iceberg compat](/concepts/deletion-vectors-and-icebergcompat.md) - IcebergCompatV requires that Deletion Vectors be either disabled or fully purged from a Delta table before Uniform Iceberg compatibility can be enabled.
* [Deletion Vector corruption detection](/concepts/deletion-vector-corruption-detection.md) - Capability of FSCK REPAIR TABLE to detect corruption in deletion vectors of Delta Lake tables
* [Deletion Vector Integrity Checking](/concepts/deletion-vector-integrity-checking.md) - Detection of missing or corrupted deletion vector files associated with data files in Delta Lake tables, surfaced by FSCK REPAIR TABLE commands.
* [Deletion Vectors](/concepts/deletion-vectors.md) - A Delta Lake feature for marking rows as deleted without rewriting data files; conflicts with symlink manifest generation and has Parquet-only persistence constraints
* [Deletion Vectors and Column Mapping in Shared Tables](/concepts/deletion-vectors-and-column-mapping-in-shared-tables.md) - Storage optimization features (deletion vectors for efficient deletes/updates, column mapping for renaming/dropping columns) that can be enabled on shared Delta tables. Requires history sharing and recipients must use Databricks Runtime 14.1+ or open source delta-sharing-spark 3.1+ to query such tables.
* [Deletion Vectors and IcebergCompat](/concepts/deletion-vectors-and-icebergcompat.md) - IcebergCompatV requires Deletion Vectors to be either disabled or fully purged from a Delta table before compatibility can be enabled.
* [Deletion Vectors and IcebergCompat conflict](/concepts/deletion-vectors-and-icebergcompat.md) - IcebergCompatV requires Deletion Vectors to be either disabled or completely purged from a Delta table before enabling Iceberg compatibility, with specific REORG TABLE APPLY (PURGE) commands needed.
* [Deletion Vectors in Delta Lake](/concepts/deletion-vectors-in-delta-lake.md) - A Delta Lake table feature that must be purged or disabled before enabling IcebergCompat, as unresolved deletion vectors cause compatibility violations.
* [Deletion Vectors in Delta-Iceberg Compatibility](/concepts/deletion-vectors-and-icebergcompat.md) - Deletion Vectors must be purged or disabled before enabling IcebergCompatV on a Delta table, as they are incompatible with the Iceberg read protocol.
* [Delta ACID Transactions](/concepts/delta-acid-transactions.md) - Delta Lake provides ACID transaction support to data lakes, ensuring data reliability and consistency.
* [Delta Cache](/concepts/delta-cache.md) - A Databricks caching feature for Delta Lake tables that materializes selected query results in the disk cache to avoid rescanning source files.
* [Delta Cache Supported Data Sources](/concepts/delta-cache-supported-data-sources.md) - The Delta Cache (disk cache) supports Delta tables, Parquet tables, and views, with views restricted to simple queries upon expansion.
* [Delta Change Data Feed (CDF)](/concepts/delta-change-data-feed-cdf.md) - A Delta table feature that records row-level changes and is required for Triggered and Continuous online table sync modes.
* [Delta Change Data Feed for Streaming](/concepts/delta-change-data-feed-for-streaming.md) - Using the Delta Lake change data feed to capture and stream all types of row-level changes (inserts, updates, deletes) from a source table, providing the most robust approach for handling upstream modifications.
* [Delta Clone](/concepts/delta-clone.md) - A Databricks operation to clone an existing table (including Iceberg or other formats) into a Delta table, with specific compatibility constraints.
* [Delta Clone (Databricks)](/concepts/delta-clone.md) - A Databricks operation that creates a deep or shallow clone of a Delta table; certain source table features like indexes or partition evolution can cause incompatibility errors.
* [Delta Clone with History](/concepts/delta-clone-with-history.md) - A Databricks Delta Lake operation that clones a Delta table including its full version history, which has restrictions on source table types.
* [Delta Clone with History (Deep Clone)](/concepts/delta-clone-with-history-deep-clone.md) - A Databricks operation that creates a deep copy of a Delta table including its full commit history, as opposed to a shallow clone.
* [Delta CLONE with history operation](/concepts/delta-clone-with-history.md) - A Delta Lake operation that clones a table including its full version history, which has restrictions on supported source types and time travel methods
* [Delta CLONE WITH HISTORY semantics](/concepts/delta-clone-with-history.md) - The broader Databricks/Delta Lake feature of cloning a table including its full change history, which imposes restrictions on source format and access methods.
* [Delta Column Mask Compatibility with External Metadata](/concepts/delta-column-mask-compatibility-with-external-metadata.md) - Tables with Column Mask (CM) policies are unsupported sources for Delta external metadata operations.
* [Delta Conversion Caveats and Constraints](/concepts/delta-conversion-caveats-and-constraints.md) - Important limitations: file visibility after conversion, shared external directories across tables, and metadata mismatch exceptions when catalog and log diverge.
* [Delta Conversion Metadata and Partitioning](/concepts/delta-conversion-metadata-and-partitioning.md) - How CONVERT TO DELTA handles partition specifications from the metastore or explicit PARTITIONED BY clauses, with validation against directory structure.
* [Delta Conversion Metadata Check](/concepts/delta-conversion-metadata-check.md) - A validation mechanism in CONVERT TO DELTA that compares catalog metadata with existing Delta Lake transaction log metadata, throwing a convertMetastoreMetadataMismatchException on mismatch, which can be overridden via Spark configuration.
* [Delta Conversion Statistics Collection](/concepts/delta-conversion-statistics-collection.md) - The automatic process during CONVERT TO DELTA that collects statistics to improve query performance on the converted Delta table, which can be bypassed with the NO STATISTICS option.
* [Delta Deep Clone for Cross-Region Replication](/concepts/delta-deep-clone-for-cross-region-replication.md) - Technique using DEEP CLONE to replicate Delta tables to external locations across regions, enabling incremental updates and avoiding egress costs when recipients access local replicas.
* [Delta error SQLSTATE codes](/concepts/delta-error-sqlstate-codes.md) - Standardized SQLSTATE codes used by Databricks Delta to classify error conditions, such as class 0A for feature not supported
* [Delta Error Sub-Conditions](/concepts/delta-error-sub-conditions.md) - The pattern of categorized sub-conditions (NON_CLUSTERING_COLUMN, NO_CLUSTERING_COLUMNS, UNRESOLVED_COLUMN, UNSUPPORTED_EXPRESSION) within Delta error classes to provide granular error diagnostics.
* [Delta External Metadata Projection Restrictions](/concepts/delta-external-metadata-projection-restrictions.md) - The reconciliation query for external metadata operations has unsupported projection expressions that trigger a PROJECTION_NOT_SUPPORTED error.
* [Delta External Metadata Support for Streaming Tables and Materialized Views](/concepts/delta-external-metadata-support-for-streaming-tables-and-materialized-views.md) - Only streaming tables and materialized views are supported sources for Delta external metadata operations; other table types trigger an error.
* [Delta Iceberg Partition Spec Constraints](/concepts/delta-iceberg-partition-spec-constraints.md) - A restriction that replacing partitioned tables with differently-named partition specs is not supported under IcebergCompatV due to Iceberg-Spark 1.1.0 limitations.
* [Delta Lake](/concepts/delta-lake.md) - An open source storage layer that brings reliability, ACID transactions, scalable metadata handling, and unified streaming/batch processing to data lakes.
* [Delta Lake ACL Restrictions on Schema Migration](/concepts/delta-lake-acl-restrictions-on-schema-migration.md) - When Table ACLs are enabled on a cluster, automatic schema migration via DataFrameWriter options is blocked; users must use ALTER TABLE instead.
* [Delta Lake Apache Spark Compatibility](/concepts/apache-spark-compatibility.md) - Delta Lake runs on top of existing data lakes and is fully compatible with Apache Spark APIs.
* [Delta Lake API](/concepts/delta-lake-api.md) - The programming interface for interacting with Delta Lake, with API references available for Scala, Java, and Python on the Delta Lake website.
* [Delta Lake API Reference](/concepts/delta-lake-api.md) - The official API documentation for Delta Lake, providing Scala, Java, and Python programming interfaces for interacting with Delta Lake tables and operations.
* [Delta Lake APIs](/concepts/delta-lake-api.md) - Programmatic interfaces for Scala, Java, and Python that allow developers to interact with Delta Lake tables using Apache Spark APIs.
* [Delta Lake Archival Support](/concepts/delta-lake-archival-support.md) - A Databricks feature that enables Delta Lake tables to work with cloud-based lifecycle policies by ignoring files older than a specified threshold, preventing query failures against archived data.
* [Delta Lake as a Streaming Sink](/concepts/delta-lake-as-a-streaming-sink.md) - Using Delta Lake tables as sinks for Spark Structured Streaming with exactly-once processing guarantees
* [Delta Lake as a Streaming Source and Sink](/concepts/delta-lake-as-a-streaming-source-and-sink.md) - Overview of using Delta Lake tables with Spark Structured Streaming readStream/writeStream, including exactly-once guarantees and performance benefits.
* [Delta Lake Automated Metadata Management](/concepts/delta-lake-automated-metadata-management.md) - Delta Lake automatically handles REFRESH TABLE, partition management, and data file tracking via the transaction log, making manual operations unnecessary and dangerous.
* [Delta Lake Automatic Partition Management](/concepts/delta-lake-automatic-partition-management.md) - Delta Lake automatically tracks partitions and returns up-to-date information, eliminating the need for manual REFRESH TABLE, ALTER TABLE ADD/DROP PARTITION, or MSCK commands.
* [Delta Lake Bucket Versioning Limitations on S3](/concepts/delta-lake-s3-bucket-versioning-limitations.md) - Databricks recommends against enabling S3 bucket versioning for Delta Lake tables because it interferes with Delta Lake's own versioning and garbage collection, leading to retained metadata and data files.
* [Delta Lake Change Data Feed (CDF)](/concepts/delta-lake-change-data-feed-cdf.md) - The underlying Delta Lake feature that table_changes relies on, which only works on Delta tables, not views.
* [Delta Lake Cloning Constraints from External Sources](/concepts/delta-lake-cloning-constraints-from-external-sources.md) - The concept that Delta Lake imposes specific compatibility constraints when cloning from external table formats like Iceberg or tables with database indexes, requiring certain conditions to be met.
* [Delta Lake CLUSTER BY Duplicate Constraint](/concepts/delta-lake-cluster-by-duplicate-constraint.md) - A constraint in Delta Lake that the CLUSTER BY clause must not contain duplicate column references, enforced by the CLUSTER_BY sub-reason of DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND.
* [Delta Lake Clustering Columns](/concepts/delta-lake-clustering-columns.md) - Columns designated as clustering columns in Liquid tables; only these columns may be referenced in certain command predicates.
* [Delta Lake Column Mask (CM) Policies](/concepts/delta-lake-column-mask-cm-policies.md) - A column-level security feature in Delta Lake that restricts visibility of sensitive columns, and is unsupported for external metadata operations.
* [Delta Lake Column Masking](/concepts/delta-lake-column-masking.md) - A Delta table security feature that masks column values; incompatible with external metadata sources in Databricks.
* [Delta Lake column masks](/concepts/delta-lake-column-masks.md) - A Delta Lake table feature that restricts column visibility; incompatible with REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM.
* [Delta Lake Compatibility with Apache Spark](/concepts/delta-lake-compatibility-with-apache-spark.md) - Delta Lake runs on top of existing data lakes and is fully compatible with Apache Spark APIs.
* [Delta Lake Concurrent Operation Retry Strategy](/concepts/delta-lake-concurrent-operation-retry-strategy.md) - The recommended resolution pattern for DELTA_CONCURRENT_APPEND errors: retrying the failed operation after a concurrent modification completes.
* [Delta Lake Conversion from Apache Iceberg](/concepts/delta-lake-conversion-from-apache-iceberg.md) - Process and limitations for converting Iceberg tables to Delta Lake, including version requirements and unsupported features like partition evolution
* [Delta Lake data ingestion duplicate detection](/concepts/delta-lake-data-ingestion-duplicate-detection.md) - Sub-conditions under DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND that detect duplicate columns during data ingestion operations, including DATA (saving data) and CONVERT_TO_DELTA (converting formats to Delta).
* [Delta Lake Deletion Vectors](/concepts/delta-lake-deletion-vectors.md) - A Delta Lake feature that marks rows as logically deleted rather than physically removing them, enabling efficient operations like MERGE and UPDATE without rewriting entire Parquet files.
* [Delta Lake DML Operations](/concepts/delta-lake-dml-operations.md) - Delta Lake supports standard DML operations including DELETE, INSERT, MERGE, and UPDATE with SQL syntax.
* [Delta Lake DML Restrictions](/concepts/delta-lake-dml-restrictions.md) - Constraints on Delta Lake DML operations including DELETE restrictions on temporal specifications, foreign tables, and subquery nesting.
* [Delta Lake DML statements](/concepts/delta-lake-dml-operations.md) - Set of data modification operations supported on Delta Lake tables, including DELETE, INSERT, MERGE, UPDATE, and COPY INTO.
* [Delta Lake DML Support](/concepts/delta-lake-dml-support.md) - DELETE FROM is only supported for Delta Lake tables in Databricks, not other table formats
* [Delta Lake Duplicate Columns in Data Ingestion](/concepts/delta-lake-duplicate-columns-in-data-ingestion.md) - Scenarios where duplicate columns are detected during data ingestion operations such as saving data or converting to Delta format.
* [Delta Lake Duplicate Columns in Partitioning](/concepts/delta-lake-duplicate-columns-in-partitioning.md) - Scenarios where duplicate columns are detected in PARTITION BY, CLUSTER BY, or partition schema specifications in Delta Lake.
* [Delta Lake Duplicate Columns in Schema Operations](/concepts/delta-lake-duplicate-columns-in-schema-operations.md) - Scenarios where duplicate columns are detected during schema-related Delta Lake operations including adding columns, replacing columns, reading schemas, and updating metadata.
* [Delta Lake External Metadata](/concepts/delta-lake-external-metadata.md) - A mechanism for storing Delta table metadata externally; has compatibility constraints with certain Delta features like column masking, RLS, and unsupported table types.
* [Delta Lake File Compaction](/concepts/delta-lake-file-compaction.md) - The practice of running OPTIMIZE to compact small files into larger ones, improving read throughput and query performance
* [Delta Lake File Compaction (OPTIMIZE)](/concepts/delta-lake-file-compaction.md) - Best practice of running OPTIMIZE to compact small files into larger ones for improved read throughput, with VACUUM to remove old files.
* [Delta Lake for Deep Learning Data Loading](/concepts/delta-lake-for-deep-learning-data-loading.md) - Using Delta Lake tables to optimize data throughput for deep learning workloads, particularly for image ETL and large-scale training.
* [Delta Lake General Best Practices](/concepts/delta-lake-general-best-practices.md) - High-level recommendations for Delta Lake including Unity Catalog managed tables, predictive optimization, liquid clustering, and safe table replacement patterns.
* [Delta Lake Integration for Ray via Third-Party Libraries](/concepts/delta-lake-integration-for-ray-via-third-party-libraries.md) - Use the deltalake (delta-rs) and deltaray (Delta Incubator) libraries to write Ray Core data to Delta Lake tables, though deltalake currently works only with Hive metastore tables (not Unity Catalog) and is not officially supported by Databricks.
* [Delta Lake Legacy Configuration Removal](/concepts/legacy-delta-configuration-removal.md) - Recommendation to remove explicit legacy Delta configurations from Spark configs and table properties when upgrading Databricks Runtime to enable new optimizations and default values
* [Delta Lake Limitations on S3](/concepts/delta-lake-limitations-on-s3.md) - Warning against simultaneously modifying the same Delta table stored on S3 from multiple workspaces or systems due to concurrency limitations.
* [Delta Lake Liquid Clustering](/concepts/delta-lake-liquid-clustering.md) - A data organization technique in Delta Lake where data is clustered by specified columns, and operations like certain commands may only reference those clustering columns.
* [Delta Lake manifest files](/concepts/delta-lake-manifest-files.md) - External metadata files generated from a Delta table that list its data files for use by query engines that do not natively support Delta Lake.
* [Delta Lake Manifest Files for External Query Engines](/concepts/delta-lake-manifest-files-for-external-query-engines.md) - Generated files that allow external query engines like Presto and Amazon Athena to read Delta Lake tables without native Delta Lake support.
* [Delta Lake MERGE INTO (Upsert)](/concepts/delta-lake-merge-into-upsert.md) - A SQL statement that merges updates, insertions, and deletions from a source table into a target Delta table, supporting WHEN MATCHED, WHEN NOT MATCHED, and WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE clauses.
* [Delta Lake MERGE Performance Optimization](/concepts/delta-lake-merge-performance-tuning.md) - Strategies to speed up MERGE operations including reducing search space with partition constraints, file compaction, controlling shuffle partitions, enabling optimized writes, and tuning file sizes.
* [Delta Lake MERGE Performance Tuning](/concepts/delta-lake-merge-performance-tuning.md) - Techniques to reduce merge operation time including reducing search space, compacting files, controlling shuffle partitions, enabling optimized writes, and tuning file sizes
* [Delta Lake metadata update duplicate detection](/concepts/delta-lake-metadata-update-duplicate-detection.md) - A sub-condition under DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND specifically for detecting duplicate columns when updating table metadata, distinct from schema or partition validation.
* [Delta Lake multi-cluster write configuration](/concepts/delta-lake-multi-cluster-write-configuration.md) - The spark.databricks.delta.multiClusterWrites.enabled setting controls whether multiple clusters can concurrently write to the same Delta Lake table; disabling it requires all writes to originate from a single cluster.
* [Delta Lake multi-cluster write restrictions on S3](/concepts/delta-lake-multi-cluster-write-restrictions-on-s3.md) - S3's eventual consistency model limits multi-cluster writes to a single Databricks workspace; cross-workspace writes to the same Delta table on S3 risk data corruption.
* [Delta Lake Multi-Cluster Writes Configuration](/concepts/delta-lake-multi-cluster-write-configuration.md) - The spark.databricks.delta.multiClusterWrites.enabled setting controls multi-cluster writes; when disabled, writes to a single Delta table must originate from exactly one cluster to avoid corruption.
* [Delta Lake Named Table References in DDL](/concepts/delta-lake-named-table-references-in-ddl.md) - The DROP BLOOMFILTER INDEX command requires a table name without temporal or options specifications, and fails if the table or specified columns do not exist.
* [Delta Lake on Databricks](/concepts/delta-lake-on-databricks.md) - Databricks' integration and documentation of Delta Lake, including tutorials for creating and managing Delta Lake tables within the Databricks environment on AWS.
* [Delta Lake on Databricks SQL](/concepts/delta-lake-on-databricks-sql.md) - Databricks SQL and Databricks Runtime support for Delta Lake DELETE operations with full SQL DML semantics.
* [Delta Lake Optimistic Concurrency Control](/concepts/delta-lake-optimistic-concurrency-control.md) - The concurrency model underlying Delta Lake that detects conflicts at commit time rather than locking resources upfront, leading to error conditions like DELTA_CONCURRENT_DELETE_DELETE.
* [Delta Lake Optimized Writes](/concepts/delta-lake-optimized-writes.md) - A feature that reduces the number of small files produced by MERGE operations on partitioned tables by reducing the number of files each shuffle task writes across multiple partitions
* [Delta Lake partition duplicate detection](/concepts/delta-lake-partition-duplicate-detection.md) - Sub-conditions under DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND that detect duplicate columns in partitioning contexts, including PARTITION_COLUMNS, PARTITION_SCHEMA, and CLUSTER_BY clauses.
* [Delta Lake Partition Spec Immutability in IcebergCompatV1](/concepts/delta-lake-partition-spec-immutability-in-icebergcompatv1.md) - The restriction that replacing a partitioned table with a differently-named partition spec is disallowed when IcebergCompatV1 is enabled, mirroring a limitation in Iceberg-Spark 1.1.0.
* [Delta Lake Partitioning Considerations](/concepts/delta-lake-partitioning-considerations.md) - Guidance on avoiding over-partitioned tables when migrating from Parquet to Delta Lake, as over-partitioning is a common cause of slow workloads.
* [Delta Lake Partitioning Constraints](/concepts/delta-lake-partitioning-constraints.md) - The requirement that partition columns and partition schemas in Delta Lake must not contain duplicate column definitions, enforced by the PARTITION_COLUMNS and PARTITION_SCHEMA sub-reasons of DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND.
* [Delta Lake Predictive Optimization](/concepts/delta-lake-predictive-optimization.md) - A Databricks feature that automatically runs OPTIMIZE and VACUUM commands on Unity Catalog managed tables to maintain performance
* [Delta Lake Protocol Change Conflicts](/concepts/delta-lake-protocol-change-conflicts.md) - A transaction conflict triggered when a concurrent operation upgrades the table protocol in Delta Lake, requiring the conflicting operation to be retried.
* [Delta Lake protocol changes and table metadata conflicts](/concepts/delta-lake-protocol-changes-and-table-metadata-conflicts.md) - A concurrency conflict type where a concurrent operation upgrades the table protocol or changes table metadata (schema, partitioning), invalidating an in-progress transaction.
* [Delta Lake Protocol Upgrade Conflicts](/concepts/delta-lake-protocol-change-conflicts.md) - A transaction conflict triggered when a concurrent operation upgrades the Delta Lake table protocol version, forcing other transactions to retry.
* [Delta Lake Reader Table Features](/concepts/delta-lake-reader-table-features.md) - Extensibility features in Delta Lake that affect how a table is read, and can cause incompatibility with UniForm metadata refresh.
* [Delta Lake Reconciliation Queries](/concepts/delta-lake-reconciliation-queries.md) - Queries used to compare or reconcile data between sources in Delta Lake, with restrictions on projections and column aliasing when external metadata is involved.
* [Delta Lake Row-Level Conflict Detection](/concepts/delta-lake-row-level-conflict-detection.md) - A mechanism used by Delta Lake to detect and resolve transaction conflicts at the granularity of individual rows, with constraints on time, predicates, and metadata.
* [Delta Lake Row-Level Conflict Resolution](/concepts/row-level-conflict-resolution.md) - A mechanism in Delta Lake that detects and resolves conflicts at the row level when concurrent transactions delete data being read by another transaction.
* [Delta Lake row-level security](/concepts/delta-lake-row-level-security.md) - A Delta Lake table feature that restricts row visibility; incompatible with REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM.
* [Delta Lake Row-Level Security (RLS)](/concepts/delta-lake-row-level-security-rls.md) - A Delta table security feature that restricts row access; incompatible with external metadata sources in Databricks.
* [Delta Lake Row-Level Security (RLS) Policies](/concepts/delta-lake-row-level-security-rls.md) - A row-level security feature in Delta Lake that restricts data access at the row level, and is unsupported for external metadata operations.
* [Delta Lake S3 bucket versioning conflict](/concepts/delta-lake-s3-bucket-versioning-conflict.md) - S3 bucket versioning should not be enabled for Delta Lake tables because it conflicts with Delta Lake's own versioning and garbage collection, causing retained data files and performance degradation.
* [Delta Lake S3 bucket versioning limitations](/concepts/delta-lake-s3-bucket-versioning-limitations.md) - Databricks recommends against enabling S3 bucket versioning for Delta Lake tables due to conflicts with Delta's own versioning and garbage collection, suggesting a max of 3 versions retained for ≤7 days if enabled.
* [Delta Lake Schema Migration](/concepts/delta-lake-schema-migration.md) - The process of evolving a Delta table's schema to match incoming data, controlled via DataFrameWriter options (mergeSchema, overwriteSchema) or session configurations.
* [Delta Lake schema modification duplicate detection](/concepts/delta-lake-schema-modification-duplicate-detection.md) - Sub-conditions under DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND that detect duplicate columns during schema-altering operations such as ADDING_COLUMNS, REPLACING_COLUMNS, or when validating EXISTING_SCHEMA, TABLE_SCHEMA, SPECIFIED_COLUMNS, and READ_SCHEMA.
* [Delta Lake Schema Operations and Duplicate Column Detection](/concepts/delta-lake-schema-operations-and-duplicate-column-detection.md) - The set of Delta Lake operations (schema changes, data ingestion, partitioning, clustering, table conversion) that trigger duplicate column validation and raise DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND.
* [Delta Lake schema validation rules for empty schemas](/concepts/delta-lake-schema-validation-rules-for-empty-schemas.md) - The four specific sub-conditions (STRUCT_ALL_VOID_FIELDS, STRUCT_NO_FIELDS, TABLE_ALL_VOID_COLUMNS, TABLE_NO_COLUMNS) under which Delta Lake refuses to write because the schema is effectively empty.
* [Delta Lake SQLSTATE 2D521](/concepts/delta-lake-sqlstate-2d521.md) - The SQL state code (2D521) indicating an invalid transaction termination due to a transaction conflict in Delta Lake.
* [Delta Lake Stream Input Rate Limiting](/concepts/delta-lake-stream-input-rate-limiting.md) - Options maxFilesPerTrigger and maxBytesPerTrigger to control how much data each micro-batch processes, helping manage memory, latency, and cloud storage costs
* [Delta Lake Streaming and Batch Unification](/concepts/delta-lake-streaming-and-batch-unification.md) - Delta Lake unifies streaming and batch data processing within a single storage layer.
* [Delta Lake streaming retention window and failure handling](/concepts/delta-lake-streaming-retention-window-and-failure-handling.md) - Streaming queries from Delta tables must run within the VACUUM retention window (7 days for data files, 30 days for transaction logs) or they fail with DELTA_FILE_NOT_FOUND_DETAILED.
* [Delta Lake Streaming Sink Modes](/concepts/delta-lake-streaming-sink-modes.md) - Using Delta Lake tables as streaming sinks with append mode (adding new records) and complete mode (replacing the entire table each batch)
* [Delta Lake Table](/concepts/delta-lake-table.md) - The storage format required for UPDATE, DELETE, and MERGE operations in Databricks; standard tables do not support UPDATE.
* [Delta Lake Table Deletion on S3](/concepts/delta-lake-table-deletion-on-s3.md) - Using rm -rf to drop a Delta Lake table on S3 can result in stale data; users should use proper table drop operations instead.
* [Delta Lake table deletion risks on S3](/concepts/delta-lake-table-deletion-on-s3.md) - Using rm -rf or direct file deletion to drop Delta Lake tables on S3 causes stale data issues; proper drop or replace table operations should be used instead.
* [Delta Lake Table Feature Dependencies](/concepts/delta-lake-table-feature-dependencies.md) - The concept that Delta Lake table features have dependency relationships where enabling a compatibility mode like IcebergCompatV1 requires certain features to be enabled and prohibits others.
* [Delta Lake table features and Iceberg compatibility](/concepts/delta-lake-table-features-compatibility.md) - IcebergWriterCompatV requires specific Delta Lake table features to be enabled and supported, and is incompatible with certain features
* [Delta Lake Table Features Compatibility](/concepts/delta-lake-table-features-compatibility.md) - The set of table features that are required, incompatible, or must be enabled/disabled for IcebergCompatV to function correctly.
* [Delta Lake table history retention](/concepts/delta-lake-table-history-retention.md) - Delta Lake retains table history metadata for 30 days by default
* [Delta Lake Table Protocol Changes](/concepts/delta-lake-table-protocol-changes.md) - A concurrent operation upgrading the table protocol version, causing transaction conflicts that require retrying the operation
* [Delta Lake Table Protocol Upgrade Conflicts](/concepts/delta-lake-protocol-change-conflicts.md) - A conflict scenario where a concurrent operation upgrades a Delta table's protocol version, requiring the conflicting transaction to retry.
* [Delta Lake Table Validation Strategies](/concepts/delta-lake-table-validation-strategies.md) - Three modes of table validation: METADATA ONLY (lightweight CRC check), DRY RUN (file existence checks), and VERIFY ALL FILES (deep data validation including file readability and deletion vector integrity).
* [Delta Lake Time Travel](/concepts/delta-lake-time-travel.md) - Ability to query, restore, or access previous versions of a Delta table using timestamps or version numbers
* [Delta Lake Time Travel and VACUUM](/concepts/delta-lake-time-travel-and-vacuum.md) - Running VACUUM on a Delta table removes the ability to time travel to versions older than the specified data retention period.
* [Delta Lake Transaction Log](/concepts/delta-lake-transaction-log.md) - The metadata structure created during CONVERT TO DELTA that tracks all Parquet files, infers schema from file footers, and enables Delta Lake features on the table.
* [Delta Lake Transaction Log Generation](/concepts/delta-lake-transaction-log-generation.md) - During conversion, CONVERT TO DELTA scans existing Parquet files (or Iceberg manifests) and creates a Delta Lake transaction log that tracks those files, with no data rewriting required.
* [Delta Lake Transaction Log Integrity](/concepts/delta-lake-transaction-log-integrity.md) - Delta Lake uses a transaction log to commit changes atomically; users must never manually modify, add, or delete Parquet data files directly as it can cause data loss or corruption.
* [Delta Lake Transaction Protocol Changes](/concepts/delta-lake-table-protocol-changes.md) - A conflict scenario where a concurrent operation upgrades the table protocol, which can invalidate ongoing transactions and require retries.
* [Delta Lake Universal Format (delta.universalFormat.enabledFormats)](/concepts/delta-lake-universal-format-deltauniversalformatenabledformats.md) - A Delta Lake table property that enables multi-format support, requiring explicit opt-in for features like SYNC UNIFORM
* [Delta Lake Universal Format (UniForm)](/concepts/delta-lake-universal-format-uniform.md) - A Databricks feature that enables Delta Lake tables to be read by Apache Iceberg clients by automatically generating Iceberg metadata asynchronously without rewriting data.
* [Delta Lake VACUUM and Garbage Collection](/concepts/delta-lake-vacuum-and-garbage-collection.md) - Delta Lake's VACUUM command permanently deletes data files and transaction logs; enabling S3 bucket versioning causes S3 to retain these files, undermining Delta's own garbage collection.
* [Delta Lake VACUUM and garbage collection on S3](/concepts/delta-lake-vacuum-and-garbage-collection.md) - Delta Lake implements its own versioning and garbage collection (including VACUUM), which conflicts with S3 bucket versioning and lifecycle policies that retain deleted files.
* [Delta Lake VACUUM and S3 Garbage Collection](/concepts/delta-lake-vacuum-and-garbage-collection.md) - Delta Lake's VACUUM command permanently deletes old data files as part of its garbage collection, but S3 bucket versioning retains these deleted files, undermining the cleanup process.
* [Delta Lake VACUUM and S3 Versioning Interaction](/concepts/delta-lake-vacuum-and-garbage-collection.md) - When S3 bucket versioning is enabled, VACUUM operations and automatic transaction log cleanup in Delta Lake do not permanently delete files; S3 retains older versions.
* [Delta Lake view restrictions](/concepts/delta-lake-view-restrictions.md) - Constraints on which views can be used with table_changes, including requiring single-relation, deterministic, Delta-based views without subqueries
* [Delta Lake vs Parquet on Apache Spark](/concepts/delta-lake-vs-parquet-on-spark.md) - Key differences where Delta Lake automatically handles REFRESH TABLE, partition management, partition reading, and data file modifications that must be done manually with Parquet
* [Delta Lake vs Parquet on Spark](/concepts/delta-lake-vs-parquet-on-spark.md) - Key operational differences: Delta Lake automatically handles REFRESH TABLE, partition management, partition loading, and data file modifications, whereas Parquet on Spark requires manual management.
* [Delta Lake vs Parquet Operational Differences](/concepts/delta-lake-vs-parquet-operational-differences.md) - Key operational differences where Delta Lake automates table management tasks that require manual steps with Parquet, including partition management, data file integrity, and caching.
* [Delta Lake-Iceberg column mapping constraints](/concepts/delta-lake-iceberg-column-mapping-constraints.md) - IcebergWriterCompatV requires column mapping physical names to follow the 'col-[fieldId]' convention, enforced via the FIELD_ID_DOES_NOT_MATCH_PHYSICAL_NAME condition
* [Delta Lake-Iceberg map key constraints](/concepts/delta-lake-iceberg-map-key-constraints.md) - IcebergWriterCompatV prohibits changing map keys that are structs, enforced via the CANNOT_CHANGE_MAP_STRUCT_KEY condition
* [Delta Log File Removal](/concepts/delta-log-file-removal.md) - Manual deletion of transaction log files from the Delta log directory, a primary cause of the non-contiguous version error across all cloud platforms.
* [Delta log gap](/concepts/delta-log-gap.md) - A missing version sequence number in the Delta transaction log, often caused by manual file deletion or S3 eventual consistency issues.
* [Delta Log Location](/concepts/delta-log-location.md) - The metadata directory (_delta_log) for a Delta table; errors occur when this location is missing or unexpected during Uniform Ingress operations.
* [Delta log non-contiguity due to manual file deletion](/concepts/delta-log-non-contiguity-due-to-manual-file-deletion.md) - Manually removing files from the Delta transaction log can create gaps in version numbering, triggering the non-contiguous error.
* [Delta log non-contiguity due to S3 eventual consistency](/concepts/delta-log-non-contiguity-due-to-s3-eventual-consistency.md) - On AWS, eventual consistency of S3 when a Delta table is deleted and recreated at the same location can cause non-contiguous version gaps.
* [Delta Log Version Mismatch on Concurrent Rollback](/concepts/deltalog-version-mismatch-in-managed-rollback.md) - A concurrency conflict where the managed and external DeltaLog versions diverge, typically because a concurrent ALTER TABLE UNSET MANAGED command already succeeded.
* [Delta Row Filter (RLS) Compatibility with External Metadata](/concepts/delta-row-filter-rls-compatibility-with-external-metadata.md) - Tables with Row-level Security (RLS) policies are unsupported sources for Delta external metadata operations.
* [Delta Schema Migration via mergeSchema](/concepts/delta-schema-migration-via-mergeschema.md) - The mechanism to enable automatic schema migration when writing to Delta tables using the mergeSchema option or session configuration.
* [Delta Sharing](/concepts/delta-sharing.md) - An open protocol for secure real-time data sharing across platforms, foundational to Databricks-to-Databricks view sharing
* [Delta Sharing Asset Types](/concepts/delta-sharing-asset-types.md) - Types of assets that can be shared via OpenSharing including tables, views, materialized views, streaming tables, and volumes
* [Delta Sharing Clean Rooms](/concepts/delta-sharing-clean-rooms.md) - A mechanism within Delta Sharing where securable objects are shared via clean rooms, with central clean room IDs tracked as a dependency that blocks deletion.
* [Delta Sharing on AWS Databricks](/concepts/delta-sharing-on-aws-databricks.md) - An open protocol for secure data sharing across Databricks workspaces on AWS, enabling cross-workspace collaboration without copying data.
* [Delta Sharing Open Sharing](/concepts/delta-sharing-open-sharing.md) - An authentication flow for sharing data via Delta Sharing with recipients who do not have access to a Unity Catalog-enabled Databricks workspace, using either bearer tokens or OIDC federation.
* [Delta Sharing Provider/Recipient Error Resolution](/concepts/delta-sharing-providerrecipient-error-resolution.md) - The bilateral error resolution approach in Delta Sharing where providers verify recipient property existence and recipients contact the data provider when property mismatches occur.
* [Delta Sharing Recipient Object](/concepts/delta-sharing-recipient-object.md) - A named object in Unity Catalog that represents the identity of a user or group who consumes shared data, with authentication types depending on the recipient's environment.
* [Delta Sharing Recipient Permissions Model](/concepts/delta-sharing-recipient-permissions-model.md) - The hierarchy of privileges required to create, describe, manage, and grant access to recipients and shares in Unity Catalog, including metastore admin, USE RECIPIENT, CREATE RECIPIENT, and share-level permissions.
* [Delta Sharing recipient properties](/concepts/delta-sharing-recipient-properties.md) - Configurable properties on Delta Sharing recipients that can restrict or control data access
* [Delta Sharing Resource Limits (Active Files & RemoveFile Actions)](/concepts/delta-sharing-resource-limits-active-files-removefile-actions.md) - Hard limits on shared Delta tables: 400K active files (AddFile) and 100K RemoveFile entries in the Delta log, which if exceeded produce RESOURCE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED errors.
* [Delta Sharing Securable Protection](/concepts/delta-sharing-securable-protection.md) - The security principle that securable objects (tables, views) actively shared via OpenSharing or used in clean rooms cannot be deleted until sharing is revoked.
* [Delta Sharing Security Model](/concepts/delta-sharing-security-model.md) - The overarching security approach in Delta Sharing that uses function whitelisting, column-level security, and view definitions rather than raw table access to protect shared data.
* [Delta Source Streaming Offsets](/concepts/delta-source-streaming-offsets.md) - Conceptual mechanism by which Delta streaming sources track and skip processing positions via offset ranges
* [Delta Streaming Offset Validation Constraints](/concepts/delta-streaming-offset-validation-rules.md) - Validations that streaming offsets must pass when used with skip offset ranges in Delta sources, including restrictions on event time, initial snapshot status, and index format
* [Delta Streaming Offset Validation Rules](/concepts/delta-streaming-offset-validation-rules.md) - Three validation constraints for Delta streaming skip offsets: no event time allowed, no initial snapshot offsets permitted, and offsets must reference a valid BASE_INDEX.
* [Delta Streaming Skip Offsets](/concepts/delta-streaming-skip-offsets.md) - A mechanism in Databricks Delta Lake streaming that allows users to specify an offset range to skip when reading from a Delta source, enabling selective processing of data.
* [Delta Streaming Tables and Materialized Views](/concepts/delta-streaming-tables-and-materialized-views.md) - The only table types supported by Delta external metadata; other table types trigger the DELTA_EXTERNAL_METADATA_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE error.
* [Delta Table as Feature Table](/concepts/delta-table-as-feature-table.md) - Any Delta table in Unity Catalog with a primary key constraint can be used as a feature table for machine learning.
* [Delta Table Caching](/concepts/delta-table-caching.md) - The application of CACHE SELECT specifically to Delta Lake tables, enabling faster subsequent queries by avoiding full file scans.
* [Delta Table Cloning](/concepts/delta-table-cloning.md) - The ability to create deep or shallow copies of Delta tables, copying data and/or metadata to a target location for purposes like archiving, ML workflows, and data migration.
* [Delta Table Column Mapping](/concepts/delta-table-column-mapping.md) - A Delta Lake feature that must be enabled to use column aliases in reconciliation queries; related to external metadata constraints.
* [Delta Table Features and IcebergCompat dependencies](/concepts/delta-table-features-and-icebergcompat-dependencies.md) - IcebergCompatV requires specific Delta table features to be enabled and supported, and prevents dropping required features or enabling incompatible features simultaneously.
* [Delta table features compatibility with Iceberg](/concepts/delta-table-features-compatibility-with-iceberg.md) - Constraints enforced by IcebergWriterCompatV that require specific Delta table features to be enabled and prohibit incompatible features
* [Delta Table for Image Metadata Management](/concepts/delta-table-for-image-metadata-management.md) - Using Delta tables to store and manage image metadata, file paths, and inference results, enabling ACID transactions and schema enforcement for ML image workloads.
* [Delta Table History Retention](/concepts/delta-table-history-retention.md) - The period (30 days) for which Delta Lake retains write history for a table.
* [Delta table name restrictions for index commands](/concepts/delta-table-name-restrictions-for-index-commands.md) - The table name in DROP BLOOM FILTER INDEX must not include temporal or options specifications
* [Delta Table Partitioning Constraints](/concepts/delta-table-partitioning-constraints.md) - Delta Lake enforces that partition columns specified during a write must match the existing table's partition columns; a mismatch raises the PARTITIONING_MISMATCH error.
* [Delta Table Partitioning Mismatch](/concepts/delta-table-partitioning-mismatch.md) - An error subtype indicating that the partition columns provided during a write do not match the existing partition columns of the Delta table.
* [Delta Table Provenance](/concepts/delta-table-provenance.md) - Metadata captured for every write operation on a Delta table, including operation type, user, and timestamp.
* [Delta Table Repair](/concepts/delta-table-repair.md) - The recommended remediation for a non-contiguous Delta log, requiring contacting Databricks support to repair the table.
* [Delta table repair via Databricks support](/concepts/delta-table-repair.md) - The recommended remediation for a Delta table with non-contiguous log versions, requiring contacting Databricks support to repair the table.
* [Delta table schema requirements](/concepts/delta-table-schema-requirements.md) - The requirement that a Delta table must have at least one non-void column and any struct columns must have at least one non-void field to be writable.
* [Delta Table Streaming Start Position](/concepts/delta-table-streaming-start-position.md) - Using startingVersion and startingTimestamp options to define the initial read position for a streaming query on a Delta table
* [Delta table versioning](/concepts/delta-table-versioning.md) - The mechanism by which Delta Lake maintains sequential, atomic versions of a table through ordered commits in the transaction log.
* [Delta Tables with Primary Key as Feature Tables](/concepts/delta-tables-with-primary-key-as-feature-tables.md) - Any Delta table in Unity Catalog with a primary key constraint can serve as a feature table
* [Delta transaction log](/concepts/delta-transaction-log.md) - The commit log that records all changes made to a Delta table in sequential version order.
* [Delta transaction log archival constraint](/concepts/delta-transaction-log-archival-constraint.md) - Archiving any files in the _delta_log/ directory makes the entire Delta table inaccessible; cloud lifecycle policies must exclude the transaction log path from archival rules.
* [Delta transaction log archival prohibition](/concepts/delta-transaction-log-archival-prohibition.md) - The Delta transaction log (_delta_log/) must never be archived by cloud lifecycle policies, or the table becomes entirely inaccessible.
* [Delta transaction log archival restriction](/concepts/delta-transaction-log-archival-restriction.md) - The Delta transaction log (_delta_log/ directory) must never be archived; doing so makes the entire table inaccessible.
* [Delta Uniform](/concepts/delta-uniform.md) - A Databricks feature that enables Delta Lake tables to be readable as Apache Iceberg tables, requiring specific table properties like tableId, snapshotId, and metadataLocation.
* [Delta Uniform (Apache Iceberg Ingress)](/concepts/delta-uniform-apache-iceberg-ingress.md) - A Databricks feature that allows reading Apache Iceberg tables using Delta Lake via a uniform ingress table configuration
* [Delta Uniform (Databricks)](/concepts/delta-uniform-databricks.md) - A Databricks feature enabling Delta Lake tables to be read by other query engines through a unified format
* [Delta Uniform (Delta Lake Uniform)](/concepts/delta-uniform.md) - A Databricks feature that provides interoperability between Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg formats, allowing tables to be read using Iceberg readers while maintaining Delta Lake as the primary format.
* [Delta UniForm (Delta Universal Format)](/concepts/delta-uniform-universal-format.md) - A Databricks feature that enables Delta Lake tables to be read by Iceberg, Hudi, and other formats through automatic metadata conversion and refresh operations.
* [Delta Uniform (UniForm)](/concepts/delta-uniform.md) - A Databricks feature that allows Delta Lake tables to be read natively as Apache Iceberg or Apache Hudi tables without conversion.
* [Delta UniForm (Universal Format)](/concepts/delta-uniform-universal-format.md) - A Databricks Delta Lake feature enabling interoperability with other table formats like Apache Iceberg and Apache Hive by writing metadata in those formats alongside Delta metadata.
* [Delta Uniform (Universal Format) Compatibility](/concepts/delta-uniform-universal-format-compatibility.md) - A Databricks feature that enables Delta tables to be read by non-Delta engines through a compatibility format, requiring a dedicated empty directory location
* [Delta Uniform Compatibility Format](/concepts/delta-uniform-compatibility-format.md) - A Delta Lake feature in Databricks requiring a dedicated empty directory for compatibility format configuration.
* [Delta UniForm compatibility location requirements](/concepts/delta-uniform-compatibility-location-requirements.md) - The set of conditions an error location must satisfy for Delta UniForm compatibility: must exist, must be a directory, must not be blank, must be empty, and must be accessible.
* [Delta Uniform compatibility requirement](/concepts/delta-uniform-compatibility-requirement.md) - The delta.universalFormat.enabledFormats config must include 'compatibility' for REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM to work
* [Delta Uniform Delta Log Location](/concepts/delta-uniform-delta-log-location.md) - The delta_log directory within a Delta Uniform table that is critical for ingress operations; errors occur when it is missing, unexpected, or when the metadata path cannot be found.
* [Delta Uniform delta_log location errors](/concepts/delta-uniform-delta_log-location-errors.md) - Errors related to missing, unexpected, or incorrect delta_log location metadata when working with Delta Uniform ingress tables
* [Delta Uniform Iceberg Metadata Location File Naming Convention](/concepts/delta-uniform-iceberg-metadata-location-file-naming-convention.md) - The expected file naming convention for Apache Iceberg metadata files that must be followed by the Iceberg writer for Delta Uniform to correctly parse version information from metadata locations.
* [Delta Uniform Iceberg metadata versioning](/concepts/delta-uniform-iceberg-metadata-versioning.md) - The requirement that Iceberg metadata locations for a Delta Uniform table must follow a monotonically increasing version sequence, and refreshes must point to a higher version than the existing metadata.
* [Delta Uniform Ingress](/concepts/delta-uniform-ingress.md) - The pattern of reading or importing Apache Iceberg tables through Delta Uniform on Databricks, supporting only CREATE and REFRESH operations.
* [Delta Uniform Ingress Table](/concepts/delta-uniform-ingress.md) - A Databricks table concept that supports reading Apache Iceberg metadata via Delta Uniform, requiring Unity Catalog and supporting only CREATE and REFRESH operations.
* [Delta Uniform Metadata Conversion](/concepts/delta-uniform-metadata-conversion.md) - The process of converting metadata between source formats and Delta Lake during Delta Uniform ingress operations, which is the central failure point for the DELTA_UNIFORM_INGRESS_VIOLATION error.
* [Delta Uniform Metadata Conversion Failure](/concepts/delta-uniform-metadata-conversion-failure.md) - A failure scenario where converting source format metadata to Delta format fails during a Delta Uniform read operation, reported under the DELTA_UNIFORM_INGRESS_VIOLATION error class.
* [Delta Uniform metadata versioning constraints](/concepts/delta-uniform-metadata-versioning-constraints.md) - The requirement that Iceberg metadata locations being refreshed must have a strictly higher version number than the existing metadata location.
* [Delta Uniform Refresh](/concepts/delta-uniform-refresh.md) - A Databricks operation to synchronize or update the metadata of a Delta table's Uniform representation so external engines can read the latest data.
* [Delta Uniform Required Table Properties](/concepts/delta-uniform-required-table-properties.md) - The set of Delta table properties (tableId, snapshotId, metadataLocation) that must be present for Delta Uniform to function correctly when bridging between Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg.
* [Delta Uniform Sub-Error Conditions](/concepts/delta-uniform-sub-error-conditions.md) - A set of specific sub-errors under DELTA_UNIFORM_INGRESS_VIOLATION: DELTA_LOG_LOCATION_NOT_FOUND, NOT_UNIFORM_INGRESS_TABLE, OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED, UNEXPECTED_DELTA_LOG_LOCATION, and UNITY_CATALOG_NOT_ENABLED.
* [Delta Uniform Unity Catalog Requirement](/concepts/delta-uniform-unity-catalog-requirement.md) - Unity Catalog must be enabled to read Apache Iceberg tables using Delta Uniform; absence triggers the UNITY_CATALOG_NOT_ENABLED error.
* [Delta Uniform with IcebergCompat](/concepts/delta-uniform-with-icebergcompat.md) - The Uniform feature in Delta Lake enables Apache Iceberg compatibility, and upgrading or enabling it requires all existing files to be Iceberg-compatible, enforced via IcebergCompatV.
* [Delta Universal Format (Uniform)](/concepts/delta-universal-format-uniform.md) - A Delta Lake feature for format compatibility that requires a valid empty directory location configuration
* [Delta Unsupported Clustering Column Predicates Error Class](/concepts/delta_unsupported_clustering_column_predicates-error-class.md) - A Delta Lake error class (DELTA_UNSUPPORTED_CLUSTERING_COLUMN_PREDICATES) raised when a command on a Liquid table uses predicates that reference non-clustering columns, missing clustering columns, unresolved columns, or unsupported expressions.
* [DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED](/concepts/delta_alter_table_set_managed_failed.md) - A Databricks error class that occurs when ALTER TABLE ... SET MANAGED fails, with multiple sub-error types indicating specific failure modes during external-to-managed table migration.
* [DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED Error Class](/concepts/delta_alter_table_set_managed_failed.md) - Error class raised when ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED fails on a Delta table in Databricks
* [DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_TABLE_NOT_MIGRATABLE](/concepts/delta_alter_table_set_managed_table_not_migratable.md) - A Databricks error raised when ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED fails to migrate a table to managed storage.
* [DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_TABLE_NOT_MIGRATABLE error](/concepts/delta_alter_table_set_managed_table_not_migratable.md) - A Databricks error condition that occurs when ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED fails to migrate a table to managed storage
* [DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_TABLE_NOT_MIGRATABLE error class](/concepts/delta_alter_table_set_managed_table_not_migratable.md) - A Databricks error class indicating that ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED failed to migrate the given table to a managed table.
* [DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_UNSET_MANAGED_FAILED](/concepts/delta_alter_table_unset_managed_failed.md) - Databricks error class raised when a managed Delta table cannot be rolled back to an external table
* [DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_UNSET_MANAGED_FAILED error class](/concepts/delta_alter_table_unset_managed_failed.md) - A Databricks error class (SQLSTATE 42809) raised when a table cannot be rolled back from managed to external in Unity Catalog.
* [DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA](/concepts/delta_cannot_write_empty_schema.md) - A Delta Lake error raised when attempting to write to a table whose schema is empty or contains only unsupported (VOID) types.
* [DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA error](/concepts/delta_cannot_write_empty_schema.md) - A Delta Lake error raised when a table schema is empty or consists entirely of unsupported VOID-type columns or empty structs, preventing writes.
* [DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA error class](/concepts/delta_cannot_write_empty_schema.md) - A Databricks Delta Lake error raised when a table cannot be written because its schema is empty or composed entirely of VOID-type columns
