* [DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE](/concepts/delta_clone_incompatible_source.md) - A Databricks error class raised when attempting to clone a table into Delta format from a source that has valid format but unsupported features.
* [DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE Error](/concepts/delta_clone_incompatible_source.md) - A Databricks error that occurs when cloning a Delta table from a source that has a valid format but unsupported features, including sub-classes like HAS_INDEXES, ICEBERG_MISSING_PARTITION_SPECS, and ICEBERG_UNDERGONE_PARTITION_EVOLUTION.
* [DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE error class](/concepts/delta_clone_incompatible_source.md) - A Databricks error condition raised when a Delta clone operation fails because the source table has a valid format but uses unsupported features.
* [DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE error class](/concepts/delta_clone_with_history_unsupported_target.md) - A Databricks error class indicating that a Delta table CLONE operation with history is attempted on an unsupported source table
* [DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_TARGET](/concepts/delta_clone_with_history_unsupported_target.md) - A Databricks error raised when cloning a Delta table with history into an unsupported target table type.
* [DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_TARGET error class](/concepts/delta_clone_with_history_unsupported_target.md) - A Databricks error that occurs when attempting to clone a Delta table with history to an unsupported target type.
* [DELTA_CONCURRENT_APPEND Error](/concepts/delta_concurrent_append-error.md) - A Delta Lake error indicating a transaction conflict was detected because a concurrent operation added data to the table.
* [DELTA_CONCURRENT_APPEND Error Class](/concepts/delta_concurrent_append-error.md) - A Delta Lake error class indicating transaction conflicts when concurrent operations modify the same table or partition
* [DELTA_CONCURRENT_APPEND Error Condition](/concepts/delta_concurrent_append-error.md) - A Delta Lake transaction conflict detected when a concurrent operation adds data to a table, described by SQLSTATE 2D521 with multiple sub-conditions.
* [DELTA_CONCURRENT_DELETE_DELETE Error Class](/concepts/delta_concurrent_delete_delete-error-class.md) - A Databricks error class for transaction conflicts where a concurrent delete operation conflicts with another delete on the same Delta table.
* [DELTA_CONCURRENT_DELETE_READ Error](/concepts/delta_concurrent_delete_read-error.md) - A transaction conflict error in Delta Lake on Databricks triggered when a concurrent operation deletes data that another transaction is reading.
* [DELTA_CONCURRENT_DELETE_READ Error Class](/concepts/delta_concurrent_delete_read-error.md) - A Delta Lake transaction conflict error that occurs when a concurrent operation deletes data that another transaction has read, with multiple sub-types describing specific conflict scenarios.
* [DELTA_DEEP_CLONE_STREAMING_TABLE_ERROR](/concepts/delta_deep_clone_streaming_table_error.md) - An error class in Databricks that occurs when attempting to deep clone a streaming table, with multiple specific sub-error conditions.
* [DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND](/concepts/delta_duplicate_columns_found.md) - A Delta Lake error class raised when duplicate column names are detected in a table operation, with sub-reasons identifying the specific operation context.
* [DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND Error (Databricks)](/concepts/delta_duplicate_columns_found.md) - A Delta Lake error class raised when duplicate column names are detected during table operations, with sub-classifications for different operation contexts.
* [DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND error class](/concepts/delta_duplicate_columns_found.md) - A Databricks Delta Lake error (SQLSTATE 42711) raised when duplicate column names are detected in a Delta operation, with multiple sub-condition variants pinpointing the exact operation context.
* [DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND error condition](/concepts/delta_duplicate_columns_found.md) - A Delta Lake error (SQLSTATE 42711) raised when duplicate column names are detected during various table operations such as schema changes, data writes, or metadata updates.
* [DELTA_EXTERNAL_METADATA_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE](/concepts/delta_external_metadata_unsupported_source.md) - A Databricks error condition raised when external metadata operations are attempted on unsupported Delta table sources or configurations.
* [DELTA_EXTERNAL_METADATA_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE error](/concepts/delta_external_metadata_unsupported_source.md) - A Databricks error class raised when external metadata operations are attempted on unsupported Delta table features.
* [DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_V1_VIOLATION](/concepts/delta_iceberg_compat_v1_violation.md) - A Delta Lake error class indicating that validation of IcebergCompatV1 has failed on a table operation
* [DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_V1_VIOLATION error class](/concepts/delta_iceberg_compat_v1_violation.md) - A Delta Lake error class raised when IcebergCompatV1 validation fails on a table operation
* [DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_VIOLATION error class](/concepts/delta_iceberg_compat_v1_violation.md) - A Databricks Delta Lake error class (SQLSTATE KD00E) raised when IcebergCompatV validation fails during table operations.
* [DELTA_ICEBERG_WRITER_COMPAT_VIOLATION](/concepts/delta_iceberg_writer_compat_violation.md) - A Databricks error class raised when Delta Lake write operations violate Iceberg-compatible writer constraints
* [DELTA_ICEBERG_WRITER_COMPAT_VIOLATION error class](/concepts/delta_iceberg_writer_compat_violation.md) - A Databricks error class raised when IcebergWriterCompatV validation fails on a Delta table, with multiple specific sub-error conditions.
* [DELTA_METADATA_MISMATCH Error Class](/concepts/delta_metadata_mismatch-error-class.md) - A Databricks error condition triggered when a metadata mismatch is detected during writes to a Delta table, with several specific subtypes.
* [DELTA_METADATA_MISMATCH Error Condition](/concepts/delta_metadata_mismatch-error-class.md) - A Databricks error condition (SQLSTATE 42KDG) raised when a metadata mismatch is detected while writing to a Delta table, with multiple subtypes for different root causes.
* [DELTA_SHARING_CURRENT_RECIPIENT_PROPERTY_UNDEFINED](/concepts/delta_sharing_current_recipient_property_undefined.md) - A Databricks error condition raised when a Delta Sharing recipient property referenced in a query does not apply to the current recipient in the session.
* [DELTA_SHARING_CURRENT_RECIPIENT_PROPERTY_UNDEFINED error](/concepts/delta_sharing_current_recipient_property_undefined.md) - A Databricks error raised when a Delta Sharing recipient property restricting data access does not apply to the current recipient session
* [DELTA_SHARING_CURRENT_RECIPIENT_PROPERTY_UNDEFINED Error Condition](/concepts/delta_sharing_current_recipient_property_undefined.md) - A Databricks error (SQLSTATE 42704) raised when a Delta Sharing query references a recipient property that does not apply to the current recipient in the session.
* [DELTA_SHARING_SECURABLE_DELETE_BLOCKED Error](/concepts/delta_sharing_securable_delete_blocked-error.md) - A Databricks error (SQLSTATE 55006) raised when a securable object cannot be deleted because it is actively shared via Delta Sharing (OpenSharing).
* [DELTA_SHARING_SECURABLE_DELETE_BLOCKED Error Class](/concepts/delta_sharing_securable_delete_blocked-error.md) - A Databricks error that prevents deletion of a securable object (e.g., table, view) that is currently being shared via Delta Sharing's OpenSharing protocol.
* [DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE](/concepts/delta_streaming_skip_offsets_invalid_offset_range.md) - A Databricks error class (SQLSTATE 42616) raised when a user-specified skip offset range for a Delta streaming source is invalid.
* [DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE Error Class](/concepts/delta_streaming_skip_offsets_invalid_offset_range.md) - A Databricks error class raised when an invalid skip offset range is specified for a Delta streaming source, with SQLSTATE 42616
* [DELTA_TABLE_CHANGES_VIEW_UNSUPPORTED](/concepts/delta_table_changes_view_unsupported.md) - An error class in Databricks that occurs when table_changes is called on a view that does not meet the required criteria (single relation, deterministic, references a Delta table, etc.)
* [DELTA_TABLE_CHANGES_VIEW_UNSUPPORTED error](/concepts/delta_table_changes_view_unsupported.md) - A Databricks error raised when the table_changes function is used on a view instead of a Delta table.
* [DELTA_TABLE_CHANGES_VIEW_UNSUPPORTED error class](/concepts/delta_table_changes_view_unsupported.md) - A Databricks error indicating that the table_changes function cannot be applied to views, with multiple specific sub-reasons.
* [DELTA_UNIFORM_COMPATIBILITY_MISSING_OR_INVALID_LOCATION](/concepts/delta_uniform_compatibility_missing_or_invalid_location.md) - A Databricks error class raised when the Delta UniForm compatibility location configuration is missing, empty, inaccessible, or not a valid empty directory.
* [DELTA_UNIFORM_COMPATIBILITY_MISSING_OR_INVALID_LOCATION Error Class](/concepts/delta_uniform_compatibility_missing_or_invalid_location.md) - A Databricks error class raised when the delta.universalFormat.compatibility.location configuration is missing, blank, inaccessible, or points to an invalid path
* [DELTA_UNIFORM_ICEBERG_INGRESS_VIOLATION](/concepts/delta_uniform_iceberg_ingress_violation.md) - A Databricks error class that occurs when reading Apache Iceberg via Delta Uniform fails due to version parsing problems in metadata locations.
* [DELTA_UNIFORM_ICEBERG_INGRESS_VIOLATION error class](/concepts/delta_uniform_iceberg_ingress_violation.md) - A Databricks error class (SQLSTATE KD00E) that occurs when reading an Apache Iceberg table via Delta Uniform fails, typically due to metadata parsing failures or misconfigured table properties.
* [DELTA_UNIFORM_INGRESS_VIOLATION](/concepts/delta_uniform_ingress_violation.md) - An error class in Databricks raised when reading Delta Uniform tables fails due to metadata conversion or configuration issues.
* [DELTA_UNIFORM_INGRESS_VIOLATION Error Class](/concepts/delta_uniform_ingress_violation.md) - A Databricks error class (SQLSTATE KD00E) raised when reading a Delta Uniform table fails due to metadata conversion or ingress configuration issues.
* [DELTA_UNIFORM_REFRESH_INVALID_ARGUMENT Error](/concepts/delta_uniform_refresh_invalid_argument-error.md) - A Databricks error class raised when invalid arguments are provided during a Delta Uniform refresh operation
* [DELTA_UNIFORM_REFRESH_INVALID_ARGUMENT Error Class](/concepts/delta_uniform_refresh_invalid_argument-error.md) - A Databricks error condition raised when an invalid argument is passed to a Delta UniForm refresh operation on AWS.
* [DELTA_UNIFORM_REFRESH_INVALID_ARGUMENT Error Condition](/concepts/delta_uniform_refresh_invalid_argument-error.md) - A Databricks error class indicating an invalid argument was provided during a Delta Uniform refresh operation
* [DELTA_UNIFORM_REFRESH_NOT_SUPPORTED error class](/concepts/delta_uniform_refresh_not_supported-error-class.md) - A Databricks error class raised when REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM cannot be performed due to various incompatibilities
* [DELTA_UNSUPPORTED_CLUSTERING_COLUMN_PREDICATES error class](/concepts/delta_unsupported_clustering_column_predicates-error-class.md) - A Delta Lake error class raised when commands on Liquid tables use predicates that are not supported, involving clustering column restrictions.
* [DELTA_VERSIONS_NOT_CONTIGUOUS error](/concepts/delta_versions_not_contiguous-error.md) - A Databricks Delta Lake error that occurs when versions in the delta transaction log are not sequential, indicating a gap in the log.
* [DELTA_VERSIONS_NOT_CONTIGUOUS error condition](/concepts/delta_versions_not_contiguous-error.md) - A Delta Lake error that occurs when version numbers in the transaction log are not sequential, indicating a gap in the delta log.
* [DELTA_VIOLATE_TABLE_PROPERTY_VALIDATION_FAILED](/concepts/delta_violate_table_property_validation_failed.md) - A Delta Lake error class indicating that table property validation has been violated due to incompatible feature combinations
* [DELTA_VIOLATE_TABLE_PROPERTY_VALIDATION_FAILED Error Class](/concepts/delta_violate_table_property_validation_failed-error-class.md) - A Databricks error class (SQLSTATE 0A000) triggered when table property validation constraints are violated, with specific sub-cases related to deletion vectors and manifest generation.
* [Delta-Iceberg Column Mapping Field Physical Name Constraint](/concepts/delta-iceberg-column-mapping-field-physical-name-constraint.md) - A requirement of IcebergWriterCompatV that column mapping field physical names must follow the pattern 'col-[fieldId]' for Iceberg-compatible Delta writes.
* [Delta-Iceberg column mapping field physical name convention](/concepts/delta-iceberg-column-mapping-field-physical-name-constraint.md) - The requirement enforced by IcebergWriterCompatV that column mapping field physical names follow the pattern 'col-[fieldId]'
* [Delta-Iceberg Map Key Struct Restriction](/concepts/delta-iceberg-map-key-struct-restriction.md) - A restriction enforced by IcebergWriterCompatV that prevents changing map keys that are structs, because Iceberg does not support struct-typed map keys.
* [Delta-Iceberg Schema Compatibility Constraints](/concepts/delta-iceberg-schema-compatibility-constraints.md) - A set of restrictions on Delta table schemas when using IcebergCompatV, including unsupported data types, partition column types, and schema evolution rules.
* [Delta-Iceberg Table Feature Compatibility](/concepts/delta-lake-table-features-compatibility.md) - Constraints enforced by IcebergWriterCompatV requiring specific table features to be enabled or disabled, and preventing incompatible features from coexisting on a Delta table intended for Iceberg compatibility.
* [Delta-Iceberg Unsupported Data Types and Properties](/concepts/delta-iceberg-unsupported-data-types-and-properties.md) - The set of data types and Apache Iceberg table properties that IcebergWriterCompatV rejects, ensuring only Iceberg-compatible schema types and table configurations are used in Delta tables.
* [Delta-Iceberg Version Alignment and Metadata Tracking](/concepts/delta-iceberg-version-alignment-and-metadata-tracking.md) - Delta Lake and Iceberg table versions do not directly align by commit timestamp or version ID; metadata fields track the relationship, accessible via DESCRIBE EXTENDED or Catalog Explorer.
* [Delta-Iceberg writer compatibility constraints](/concepts/delta-iceberg-writer-compatibility-constraints.md) - The set of specific schema and table property constraints enforced by IcebergWriterCompatV, including field naming, data types, table features, and configuration requirements.
* [Delta-Uniform Managed Table Requirement](/concepts/delta-uniform-managed-table-requirement.md) - The IcebergCompatV feature can only be enabled on managed Delta tables, not external or unmanaged tables.
* [delta.deletedFileRetentionDuration Table Property](/concepts/deltadeletedfileretentionduration-table-property.md) - A Delta table property that controls the retention window for VACUUM, overriding the default 7-day threshold to support longer time travel capabilities.
* [delta.timeUntilArchived](/concepts/deltatimeuntilarchived.md) - A Delta table property that specifies the archival interval, telling Databricks to ignore files older than the given time period when querying.
* [delta.timeUntilArchived table property](/concepts/deltatimeuntilarchived.md) - A Delta table property that specifies the archival threshold, telling Databricks to ignore files older than a given duration.
* [delta.universalFormat.compatibility.location](/concepts/deltauniversalformatcompatibilitylocation.md) - A configuration property in Databricks Delta Lake that must point to an empty directory for Delta UniForm compatibility format to work.
* [delta.universalFormat.compatibility.location Configuration](/concepts/deltauniversalformatcompatibilitylocation.md) - A Databricks Delta Lake configuration property that must point to an empty directory for Uniform compatibility format
* [delta.universalFormat.enabledFormats configuration](/concepts/deltauniversalformatenabledformats-configuration.md) - A Spark configuration that controls which UniForm formats (Iceberg, Hudi) are enabled for a Delta table.
* [DeltaLog Version Mismatch](/concepts/deltalog-version-mismatch.md) - A condition where the internal managed DeltaLog and external DeltaLog have diverging versions, preventing table rollback
* [DeltaLog version mismatch during managed migration](/concepts/deltalog-version-mismatch-during-managed-migration.md) - A version conflict in the managed DeltaLog when a concurrent ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED command has already migrated the table, leading to a VERSION_MISMATCH error.
* [DeltaLog Version Mismatch in Managed Rollback](/concepts/deltalog-version-mismatch-in-managed-rollback.md) - A VERSION_MISMATCH error occurs when the managed DeltaLog and external DeltaLog versions diverge, often due to a concurrent ALTER TABLE UNSET MANAGED command succeeding first.
* [DeltaTableSource](/concepts/deltatablesource.md) - An ephemeral Python object defining how features are computed from a Delta table in Unity Catalog; supports filter_condition, transformation_sql, schema_json, and helper methods like from_sql() and to_dataframe().
* [DeltaTableSource and RequestSource](/concepts/deltatablesource-and-requestsource.md) - Two data source types for feature computation: DeltaTableSource reads from Unity Catalog Delta tables (with optional filtering and transformation), and RequestSource defines inference-time schema for pass-through features.
* [Dependency management for Databricks Connect Scala Utilities](/concepts/dependency-management-for-databricks-connect-scala-utilities.md) - Databricks Connect for Scala automatically declares a dependency on the databricks-dbutils-scala library, so users do not need to add it explicitly in build.sbt, pom.xml, or build.gradle.
* [Dependency Management for Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/dependency-management-for-databricks-model-serving.md) - Best practices for managing dependencies in model serving containers, including custom libraries, flash-attn, and version pinning.
* [Dependency Management in Databricks Connect](/concepts/dependency-management-in-databricks-connect.md) - The dual dependency model where application dependencies are installed locally while UDF dependencies must be managed separately on the Databricks cluster.
* [Dependency Management Strategies for Serverless JARs](/concepts/dependency-management-strategies-for-serverless-jars.md) - Three strategies to make libraries available on serverless compute: declare as provided, attach as environment libraries, or use JDBC connections for external databases.
* [Dependency Management with uv](/concepts/dependency-management-with-uv.md) - Python dependency specification for AI Runtime workloads using `environment.dependencies`, supporting pip-style specs, requirements files, wheels, index URLs, and install flags, all installed via the uv package manager.
* [Dependency Version Matching for Serverless Compute](/concepts/dependency-version-matching-for-serverless-compute.md) - JAR Scala and JDK versions must exactly match the serverless runtime versions; mismatches cause failures. Example uses Scala 2.13, JDK 17, Databricks Connect 17.3.
* [Deploy Code Pattern (recommended)](/concepts/deploy-code-pattern-recommended.md) - A model deployment pattern where training code is promoted through development, staging, and production environments, and the model is retrained in each environment using that environment's data.
* [Deploy Code, Not Models](/concepts/deploy-code-not-models.md) - A MLOps principle recommending that during ML development, code (rather than models) should be promoted from one environment to the next, ensuring all code undergoes code review and integration testing.
* [Deploy Models Pattern](/concepts/deploy-models-pattern.md) - A model deployment pattern where the model artifact is trained in development, then tested in staging and promoted to production without retraining, separating model artifact lifecycle from code lifecycle.
* [Deploying Code Instead of Models](/concepts/deploying-code-instead-of-models.md) - The MLOps Stacks philosophy of deploying the code that builds the model rather than the model artifact itself, simplifying retraining by automating model construction from source.
* [Deploying Non-Natively Supported Frameworks with MLflow](/concepts/deploying-non-natively-supported-frameworks-with-mlflow.md) - Using MLflow pyfunc to deploy models whose frameworks are not natively supported by MLflow's built-in flavors
* [Deployment Container and Dependencies](/concepts/deployment-container-and-dependencies.md) - Production-grade container built during deployment includes MLflow-captured or specified libraries; dependencies can be added via pip_requirements, conda_env, extra_pip_requirements, and code_path.
* [Deployment container and dependency packaging](/concepts/deployment-container-and-dependency-packaging.md) - Model Serving builds a production-grade container from the MLflow model log; native flavor models capture dependencies automatically while pyfunc models require explicit specification via pip_requirements, conda_env, extra_pip_requirements, or code_path parameters.
* [Deployment Job Approval Mechanism](/concepts/deployment-job-approval-mechanism.md) - An experimental human-in-the-loop approval process for deployment jobs that uses Unity Catalog tags to gate progression from evaluation to deployment, with automatic job repair on approval.
* [Deployment Job Auto-Triggering and Credential Security](/concepts/deployment-job-auto-triggering-and-credential-security.md) - Deployment jobs auto-trigger using the model owner's credentials when a new model version is created, with recommendations to use service principals to prevent privilege escalation.
* [Deployment Job Connection to Unity Catalog Models](/concepts/deployment-job-connection-to-unity-catalog-models.md) - The process of linking a deployment job to a Unity Catalog registered model via the MLflow client or UI, enabling automatic triggering when new model versions are created.
* [Deployment Job Template Notebooks](/concepts/deployment-job-template-notebooks.md) - Reference notebooks for evaluation, approval, deployment, and job creation that serve as customizable templates for building deployment jobs on Databricks.
* [Deployment Jobs for model lifecycle](/concepts/deployment-jobs-for-model-lifecycle.md) - Automated jobs that trigger on new model versions to manage evaluation, approval, and deployment workflows in Unity Catalog, replacing stage transitions from the Workspace Model Registry.
* [Deprecated Data (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/deprecated-data-unity-catalog.md) - System tags applied to data assets to mark them as outdated or unsuitable for new use in Unity Catalog.
* [Deprecation and Migration Guidance (Databricks)](/concepts/deprecation-and-migration-guidance-databricks.md) - Databricks documentation providing migration paths away from deprecated Bloom filter indexes toward recommended alternatives like predictive I/O and liquid clustering.
* [Deprecation Comments and Genie Code Integration](/concepts/deprecation-comments-and-genie-code-integration.md) - Mechanism to annotate deprecated Hive tables with structured comments, triggering strikethrough display and Quick Fix links in notebooks, plus Genie Code auto-replacement of table references.
* [Deprecation Notification Process](/concepts/deprecation-notification-process.md) - The multi-channel notification process Databricks follows when planning to remove a library, including release notes warnings, import-time notifications, and documentation updates.
* [Deprecation of MLlib Automated MLflow Tracking](/concepts/deprecation-of-mllib-automated-mlflow-tracking.md) - MLlib automated MLflow tracking is deprecated on Databricks Runtime 10.1 ML and above, disabled by default on 10.2 ML+, with a configuration escape hatch to re-enable the old behavior
* [Deprecation of sparkdl.xgboost in Favor of xgboost.spark](/concepts/deprecation-of-sparkdlxgboost-in-favor-of-xgboostspark.md) - The sparkdl.xgboost module is deprecated since Databricks Runtime 12.0 ML, with recommendation to migrate to the xgboost.spark module
* [DESC EXTENDED diagnostic command](/concepts/desc-extended-diagnostic-command.md) - A Databricks SQL command used to inspect a table's metadata and verify whether an ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED migration has already completed.
* [DESCRIBE HISTORY](/concepts/describe-history.md) - A Delta Lake SQL command that returns provenance information (operation, user, etc.) for each write to a Delta table.
* [DESCRIBE HISTORY Command](/concepts/describe-history-command.md) - Command used to retrieve the version history of a Delta table, enabling selection of a specific version for restore
* [DESCRIBE HISTORY syntax constraints](/concepts/describe-history-syntax-constraints.md) - The table name used with DESCRIBE HISTORY must not include a temporal specification or options specification
* [Detection Exclusion](/concepts/detection-exclusion.md) - A beta feature allowing users to exclude individual column detections, which removes existing tags, prevents reapplication, and provides feedback to improve future classification accuracy.
* [Detection Exclusion and Feedback Loop](/concepts/detection-exclusion.md) - A Beta feature allowing users to exclude individual column detections, which removes existing tags, prevents reapplication, and provides feedback to improve future classification accuracy.
* [Detection Exclusion Feedback Loop](/concepts/detection-exclusion.md) - A Beta feature allowing users to exclude individual column detections, which removes existing classification tags, prevents future reapplication, and provides feedback that improves the accuracy of future classification results.
* [Detection Exclusions](/concepts/detection-exclusion.md) - A beta feature that allows users to exclude individual column detections, removing tags and preventing reapplication while providing feedback to improve future classification accuracy.
* [Detection Exclusions in Data Classification](/concepts/detection-exclusion.md) - A Beta feature allowing users to exclude individual column detections, removing existing tags, preventing reapplication, and providing feedback to improve future classification accuracy.
* [DetectJailbreak Scorer](/concepts/detectjailbreak-scorer.md) - A Guardrails AI scorer for detecting jailbreak attempts in LLM interactions, configurable with custom sensitivity thresholds.
* [DetectPII Scorer](/concepts/detectpii-scorer.md) - A Guardrails AI scorer for detecting personally identifiable information such as credit card numbers, SSNs, and email addresses in LLM outputs.
* [Deterministic and error-safe expressions in policy UDFs](/concepts/deterministic-and-error-safe-expressions-in-policy-udfs.md) - Using DETERMINISTIC functions and error-safe alternatives (try_divide, try_cast, try_to_number) in policy UDFs enables the optimizer to cache results, apply constant folding, and safely rearrange query plans without risking side-channel data leakage.
* [Developer Annotations in MLflow UI](/concepts/developer-annotations-on-traces.md) - Adding developer feedback, ratings, and notes directly through the MLflow UI by creating assessments on trace spans with custom names, numeric values, and rationales
* [Developer Annotations on Traces](/concepts/developer-annotations-on-traces.md) - Interactive workflow for developers to add custom feedback assessments (e.g., accuracy scores) directly in the MLflow UI on individual trace spans.
* [Developer Annotations via MLflow UI](/concepts/developer-annotations-on-traces.md) - Interactive method for developers to add feedback scores and rationale directly in the MLflow UI's Assessments tab on individual trace spans.
* [Developer iteration workflow for scorers](/concepts/developer-iteration-workflow-for-scorers.md) - A 4-step workflow to rapidly iterate on code-based scorers without rerunning the entire AI application by generating, storing, and reusing traces.
* [Development and Production Workflow for dbt on Databricks](/concepts/development-and-production-workflow-for-dbt-on-databricks.md) - Recommended workflow of developing dbt projects against a Databricks SQL warehouse for testing and debugging, then running in production via the dbt task in a Databricks job.
* [Development Workflow for Distributed Training](/concepts/development-workflow-for-distributed-training.md) - Recommended workflow of first training a model on a single node, then adapting the code for distributed training using tools like HorovodRunner
* [Development Workflow for HorovodRunner](/concepts/development-workflow-for-horovodrunner.md) - The recommended development workflow on Databricks for preparing data and code before running distributed training with HorovodRunner.
* [Development-Production Unified Tracing](/concepts/development-production-unified-tracing.md) - A property of MLflow Tracing where instrumentation is written once and works consistently across development environments (IDE, notebooks) and production monitoring dashboards.
* [Development-time Annotation](/concepts/development-time-annotation.md) - Adding assessment labels (feedback or expectations) to MLflow traces during GenAI application development, before formal evaluation is set up.
* [DevOps for ML on Databricks](/concepts/devops-for-ml-on-databricks.md) - Production and automation practices including model serving, jobs orchestration, Git folders, declarative automation bundles, and Terraform infrastructure-as-code.
* [Difference Between ML, Deep Learning, and GenAI](/concepts/difference-between-ml-deep-learning-and-genai.md) - Conceptual boundaries between classic machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI, noting that deep learning and GenAI are technically types of ML with fuzzy boundaries.
* [Direct Evaluation](/concepts/direct-evaluation.md) - An evaluation mode where MLflow calls the GenAI app directly via a predict_fn, generates traces, and applies scorers in parallel to assess quality.
* [Direct Evaluation Mode](/concepts/direct-evaluation.md) - Evaluation mode where MLflow calls the GenAI app directly via a predict_fn to generate and evaluate traces, recommended for reusing scorers in production monitoring.
* [Direct Grant vs GRANT Policy Interaction](/concepts/direct-grant-vs-grant-policy-interaction.md) - The effective privileges on an object are the union of direct grants and applicable GRANT policies; mixing both for the same privilege complicates reasoning about access.
* [Direct Model Query](/concepts/direct-model-query.md) - The ability to bypass traffic splitting rules and query a specific served model directly on a multi-model endpoint using a dedicated invocation URL.
* [Directed and Undirected Graphs](/concepts/directed-and-undirected-graphs.md) - Classification of graphs based on whether edges represent one-way or two-way relationships between nodes
* [Directed vs Undirected Networks](/concepts/directed-vs-undirected-networks.md) - Classification of graphs based on edge directionality: directed graphs represent one-way relationships (e.g., follower/followee), while undirected graphs represent two-way relationships (e.g., coworkers).
* [DIRECTORY_NOT_EMPTY (Delta Uniform error)](/concepts/directory_not_empty-delta-uniform-error.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_UNIFORM_COMPATIBILITY_MISSING_OR_INVALID_LOCATION indicating the specified directory already contains files.
* [DIRECTORY_NOT_EMPTY sub-error (Delta Uniform Compatibility)](/concepts/directory_not_empty-sub-error-delta-uniform-compatibility.md) - A specific reason code under the DELTA_UNIFORM_COMPATIBILITY_MISSING_OR_INVALID_LOCATION error class indicating the configured directory must be empty
* [Disable legacy features setting](/concepts/disable-legacy-features-setting.md) - An account console setting that allows organizations to test the new UC-only default behavior before the September 30, 2026 deadline by disabling legacy features in new workspaces.
* [Disable Legacy Hive Metastore Access](/concepts/disable-legacy-hive-metastore-access.md) - Workspace admin setting to prevent users from bypassing Unity Catalog and directly accessing the legacy Hive metastore in Databricks.
* [Disable Legacy Hive Metastore Access (Workspace Setting)](/concepts/disable-legacy-hive-metastore-access.md) - A workspace admin setting that prevents users from bypassing Unity Catalog and directly accessing tables registered in the legacy Hive metastore.
* [disable_databricks_trace_archival API](/concepts/disable_databricks_trace_archival-api.md) - An MLflow API function used to stop archiving traces for a specific experiment.
* [Disabling Databricks Connect on a Cluster](/concepts/disabling-databricks-connect-on-a-cluster.md) - Disabling the Databricks Connect and Spark Connect services on a cluster by setting spark.databricks.service.server.enabled to false.
* [Disabling Direct Access to the Legacy Hive Metastore](/concepts/disabling-direct-access-to-the-legacy-hive-metastore.md) - After migrating tables to Unity Catalog, Databricks recommends explicitly disabling direct Hive metastore access (workspace-wide or per-cluster) to avoid unnecessary connections and resource consumption.
* [Disabling Legacy Features After Unity Catalog Migration](/concepts/disabling-legacy-features-after-unity-catalog-migration.md) - Post-migration steps to disable legacy DBFS root and mounts, the Hive metastore, and no-isolation shared compute resources to enforce Unity Catalog governance.
* [Disabling Legacy Hive Metastore Access in Databricks](/concepts/disable-legacy-hive-metastore-access.md) - A workspace admin setting that prevents users from bypassing Unity Catalog and accessing tables registered in the Hive metastore
* [Disabling MLflow Auto Tracing](/concepts/disabling-mlflow-auto-tracing.md) - Auto-tracing for Amazon Bedrock can be disabled globally by calling mlflow.bedrock.autolog(disable=True) or mlflow.autolog(disable=True).
* [Disabling MLflow Autologging](/concepts/disabling-mlflow-autologging.md) - The mechanism to globally disable MLflow auto-tracing for Ollama/OpenAI interactions by calling mlflow.openai.autolog(disable=True) or mlflow.autolog(disable=True).
* [DISABLING_REQUIRED_TABLE_FEATURE](/concepts/missing_required_table_feature.md) - Error sub-type indicating a required table feature for IcebergCompatV1 cannot be dropped without first disabling IcebergCompatV1
* [DISABLING_REQUIRED_TABLE_FEATURE (Delta Iceberg error)](/concepts/missing_required_table_feature.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_V1_VIOLATION that occurs when attempting to drop a table feature required by IcebergCompatV1
* [DISABLING_REQUIRED_TABLE_FEATURE error](/concepts/missing_required_table_feature.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_V1_VIOLATION indicating an attempt to drop a table feature that IcebergCompatV1 requires.
* [Disk Cache Compatibility](/concepts/disk-cache-compatibility.md) - The Databricks disk cache (and therefore CACHE SELECT) is applicable only to Delta tables and Parquet tables; views are supported but with expanded query restrictions.
* [Distributed Checkpointing with PyTorch DCP](/concepts/distributed-checkpointing-with-pytorch-dcp.md) - Saving and loading model and optimizer state during distributed training using PyTorch's distributed checkpoint (dcp) API, enabling fault tolerance and continued training or inference.
* [Distributed Data Parallel (DDP)](/concepts/distributed-data-parallel-ddp.md) - A parallelism technique for distributed training where the full model is replicated on each GPU and data batches are split across GPUs.
* [Distributed Data Parallel (DDP) on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-data-parallel-ddp-on-databricks.md) - PyTorch's standard multi-GPU data parallelism integrated with the serverless_gpu @distributed API for distributed training.
* [Distributed Data Parallel (DDP) Training](/concepts/distributed-data-parallel-ddp.md) - A parallelism technique for distributed training where the full model is replicated on each GPU and data batches are split across GPUs
* [Distributed data parallelism (DDP) on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-data-parallelism-ddp-on-databricks.md) - Training approach that shards mini-batches across multiple GPUs with synchronized gradients, automated via Databricks' @distributed decorator
* [Distributed Data Parallelism Training](/concepts/distributed-data-parallelism-training.md) - Training technique that distributes model training across multiple GPUs using data parallelism, handling gradient synchronization and batching across devices
* [Distributed Data Parallelism with @distributed Decorator](/concepts/distributed-data-parallelism-with-distributed-decorator.md) - Pattern for distributing training across multiple GPUs using Databricks' serverless_gpu library, handling data parallelism, environment variables, and process coordination
* [Distributed data-parallel fine-tuning of LLMs with Ray Train](/concepts/distributed-data-parallel-fine-tuning-of-llms-with-ray-train.md) - End-to-end pattern for fine-tuning a large language model (e.g., Qwen2.5-3B) using Ray Train's TorchTrainer across multiple GPUs with DDP, covering model loading, dataset preparation, training loop, and metric reporting.
* [Distributed Deep Learning Data Preparation on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-deep-learning-data-preparation-on-databricks.md) - Strategies for preparing data specifically for distributed deep learning training workloads on Databricks clusters.
* [Distributed deep learning on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-deep-learning-on-databricks.md) - The practice of scaling deep learning model training across multiple nodes using HorovodRunner within the Databricks platform.
* [Distributed Deep Learning Training on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-deep-learning-training-on-databricks.md) - Best practices and examples for developing and fine-tuning deep learning models at scale using distributed training on the Databricks platform.
* [Distributed execution architecture on Databricks Serverless GPU](/concepts/distributed-execution-architecture-on-databricks-serverless-gpu.md) - The underlying compute manager, runtime environment, and launcher components that orchestrate distributed training across multiple GPUs.
* [Distributed Featurization with Pandas UDFs](/concepts/distributed-featurization-with-pandas-udfs.md) - A pattern for scaling featurization computations across a Spark cluster by using pandas UDFs (including Scalar Iterator pandas UDFs) to apply pre-trained deep learning models to large datasets in a distributed manner.
* [Distributed Fine-tuning with Databricks Serverless GPU](/concepts/distributed-fine-tuning-with-databricks-serverless-gpu.md) - A training pattern that uses the @distributed decorator to automatically provision multiple H100 GPUs, handle data distribution, and orchestrate synchronized distributed training of LLMs.
* [Distributed Image Inference on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-image-inference-on-databricks.md) - Architecture and workflow for scaling deep learning model inference across large image collections using Spark, pandas UDFs, and DL frameworks like PyTorch and TensorFlow on Databricks.
* [Distributed Multi-GPU Training API](/concepts/distributed-multi-gpu-training-api.md) - A Databricks API (currently in Beta) for orchestrating training workloads across multiple GPUs, enabling larger-scale model training.
* [Distributed Multi-GPU Training for LLMs](/concepts/distributed-multi-gpu-training-for-llms.md) - Training approach that distributes LLM workloads across multiple GPUs using Databricks' distributed training API
* [Distributed Multi-GPU Training on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-multi-gpu-training-api.md) - A Beta API in AI Runtime for distributing training workloads across multiple GPUs, separate from the single-node preview.
* [Distributed PyTorch Training Development Workflow](/concepts/distributed-pytorch-training-development-workflow.md) - A recommended workflow pattern for distributed deep learning that starts with single-node training before adapting code to distributed execution using HorovodRunner
* [Distributed PyTorch Training on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-pytorch-training-on-databricks.md) - Approaches and best practices for running distributed PyTorch training workloads on Databricks clusters
* [Distributed PyTorch training with Horovod](/concepts/distributed-pytorch-training-with-horovod.md) - Technique for scaling PyTorch model training across multiple nodes using Horovod, demonstrated via an MNIST example on Databricks.
* [Distributed training adaptation workflow](/concepts/distributed-training-adaptation-workflow.md) - The recommended development workflow of first training a model on a single node, then adapting it using HorovodRunner for distributed training.
* [Distributed training API (Databricks)](/concepts/distributed-training-api-databricks.md) - A Beta API for multi-GPU distributed training workloads on Databricks, scoped to the Serverless GPU API.
* [Distributed training API for multi-GPU workloads](/concepts/distributed-training-api-for-multi-gpu-workloads.md) - Beta-stage API in Databricks AI Runtime that enables scaling training workloads across multiple GPUs and nodes using the Serverless GPU API.
* [Distributed Training Development Workflow](/concepts/distributed-training-development-workflow.md) - A four-step process for converting single-node PyTorch code to distributed training using TorchDistributor on Databricks notebooks.
* [Distributed training development workflow on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-training-development-workflow-on-databricks.md) - The recommended workflow pattern for developing and running distributed deep learning jobs on Databricks using HorovodRunner, including data preparation and notebook-based execution.
* [Distributed Training for Large Models and Data](/concepts/distributed-training-for-large-models-and-data.md) - A strategy on Databricks for training neural networks when model or data exceeds single-machine memory capacity, using distributed frameworks
* [Distributed Training Import Placement for Pickling](/concepts/distributed-training-import-placement-for-pickling.md) - Best practice of placing all imports inside the training function to avoid pickling errors when distributing code to executors
* [Distributed Training on AI Runtime](/concepts/distributed-training-on-ai-runtime.md) - A Beta API using the @distributed decorator from the serverless_gpu Python package that enables multi-GPU training on a single node with PyTorch DDP, FSDP, or DeepSpeed and minimal configuration.
* [Distributed Training on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-training-on-databricks.md) - Moving from single-node to distributed deep learning training using built-in tools like TorchDistributor, DeepSpeed, and Ray.
* [Distributed Training Strategies Comparison](/concepts/distributed-training-strategies-comparison.md) - A taxonomy of distributed training approaches including DDP (Data Distributed Parallel), FSDP (Fully Sharded Data Parallel), and DeepSpeed, each suited to different model sizes and use cases.
* [Distributed Training Tools on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-training-tools-on-databricks.md) - Built-in distributed training support via TorchDistributor, DeepSpeed, and Ray, plus adaptive hyperparameter tuning with Optuna.
* [Distributed Training with @distributed Decorator](/concepts/distributed-training-with-distributed-decorator.md) - A Python API (Beta) for multi-GPU distributed training on AI Runtime using PyTorch DDP, FSDP, or DeepSpeed via a @distributed decorator
* [Distributed training with Ray Train](/concepts/distributed-training-with-ray-train.md) - A distributed data-parallel fine-tuning pattern using Ray Train's TorchTrainer across 8 H100 GPUs on a single node, with one worker per GPU.
* [Distributed training with Ray Train on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-training-with-ray-train.md) - Distributed data-parallel fine-tuning using Ray Train's TorchTrainer across 8 H100 GPUs on a single node, with one worker per GPU.
* [Distributed training with TensorFlow 2](/concepts/distributed-training-with-tensorflow-2.md) - Approach to scaling TensorFlow model training across multiple workers using tf.distribute.Strategy
* [Distributed Training with the @distributed Decorator](/concepts/distributed-training-with-the-distributed-decorator.md) - A Databricks Serverless GPU Compute API that uses the @distributed decorator to orchestrate multi-GPU training across a specified number of accelerators, handling distributed setup coordination automatically.
* [Distributed Training with xgboost.spark](/concepts/distributed-training-with-xgboostspark.md) - Distributed XGBoost training on Spark using the num_workers parameter, with restrictions on autoscaling and MLflow autologging
* [Distributed Training Workflow for Notebooks](/concepts/distributed-training-workflow-for-notebooks.md) - A four-step workflow to convert single-node PyTorch training code for distributed execution using TorchDistributor on Databricks
* [Distributed Training Workloads on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-training-workloads-on-databricks.md) - Multi-GPU training workloads submitted and managed via the AI Runtime CLI or the in-notebook Python API (@distributed and @ray_launch).
* [Distributed XGBoost Training on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-xgboost-training-on-databricks.md) - Running XGBoost model training at scale across a Spark cluster using Scala on the Databricks platform
* [Distributed XGBoost Training on Databricks with Scala](/concepts/distributed-xgboost-training-on-databricks.md) - Using Scala API on Databricks to perform distributed training of XGBoost models
* [Distributed XGBoost Training with num_workers](/concepts/distributed-xgboost-training-with-num_workers.md) - Distributed training capability in sparkdl.xgboost using the num_workers parameter, which can be set to use all Spark task slots via sc.defaultParallelism
* [Distributed XGBoost training with Scala on Databricks](/concepts/distributed-xgboost-training-on-databricks.md) - Using XGBoost for distributed model training within Scala notebooks on Databricks, integrated with Apache Spark MLlib.
* [DistributedSampler Pattern for Sharded Data Loading](/concepts/distributedsampler-pattern-for-sharded-data-loading.md) - A PyTorch pattern using DistributedSampler with DataLoader to partition dataset batches across multiple GPU workers in a distributed training setup, with epoch-based shuffling via sampler.set_epoch().
* [DL Training Scenarios on Databricks](/concepts/dl-training-scenarios-on-databricks.md) - The three supported deep learning training scenarios on Databricks: single-node training, distributed hyperparameter tuning, and distributed training.
* [Document Extraction Accuracy Evaluation](/concepts/document-extraction-accuracy-evaluation.md) - Patterns for evaluating document extraction applications with guidelines judges, covering field extraction completeness, numerical handling, entity recognition, and document-type-specific criteria.
* [Documentation archive on Databricks](/concepts/documentation-archive-on-databricks.md) - The archive section of Databricks documentation containing legacy or older versions of machine learning and training guides.
* [Downstream Impact Metrics in Anomaly Detection](/concepts/downstream-impact-metrics-in-anomaly-detection.md) - Concept of measuring the impact of data quality issues by tracking the number of queries affected on unhealthy tables, used as a trigger threshold for alerts.
* [Downstream Impact Tracking in Data Quality](/concepts/downstream-impact-tracking-in-data-quality.md) - A metric (num_queries_on_affected_tables) that measures how many queries are affected by unhealthy tables, used to prioritize alerting based on business impact.
* [downstream_impact Struct](/concepts/downstream_impact-struct.md) - A struct column in the anomaly detection results table that captures downstream impact information for each scanned table.
* [Drift detection (data quality)](/concepts/drift-detection-data-quality.md) - A monitoring capability that identifies statistical drift between current data and a known baseline, between successive time windows, or in ML model inputs and predictions over time.
* [Drift Metrics](/concepts/drift-metrics.md) - Statistics produced by data profiling that quantify how a table's data distribution changes over time or relative to a baseline table, used to detect data and model drift.
* [Drift Metrics Table](/concepts/drift-metrics-table.md) - A table that stores statistics tracking changes in data distribution over time, enabling alerts on data drift.
* [Drift Metrics: Consecutive vs Baseline Drift](/concepts/drift-metrics-consecutive-vs-baseline-drift.md) - Two types of drift computed for monitoring data distribution changes: consecutive drift compares a window to the previous time window, while baseline drift compares a window to a baseline distribution from a baseline table.
* [DROP BLOOM FILTER INDEX Command](/concepts/drop-bloom-filter-index-syntax.md) - SQL syntax and parameters for dropping a Bloom filter index on a Delta table column in Databricks
* [DROP BLOOM FILTER INDEX syntax](/concepts/drop-bloom-filter-index-syntax.md) - SQL command syntax and parameters for dropping Bloom filter indexes on Delta tables in Databricks
* [Drop Published Online Table](/concepts/drop-published-online-table.md) - The drop_online_table() operation to delete a published table from an online store provider, along with irreversible warning about downstream dependencies and the suggested key-rotation safety practice.
* [Dropping Delta Tables with rm -rf](/concepts/dropping-delta-tables-with-rm-rf.md) - Using rm -rf to drop a Delta Lake table on S3 leads to stale data issues; Databricks recommends using proper table drop or replace operations instead.
* [DRY RUN mode](/concepts/dry-run-mode.md) - Preview mode for FSCK REPAIR TABLE that reports detected issues without making any changes
* [Dual Export (MLflow + OpenTelemetry)](/concepts/dual-export-mlflow-opentelemetry.md) - A mode where traces are sent simultaneously to both the MLflow Tracking Server and an OpenTelemetry Collector, enabled via the MLFLOW_ENABLE_DUAL_EXPORT environment variable.
* [Dual Run Model (Job Run + MLflow Run)](/concepts/dual-run-model-job-run-mlflow-run.md) - Each `air run` submission creates both a Databricks job run (execution tracking) and an MLflow run (experiment tracking), with retries creating new MLflow runs.
* [Duplicate column error scenarios in Delta Lake](/concepts/duplicate-column-scenarios-in-delta-lake.md) - The specific operational contexts — ADDING_COLUMNS, CLUSTER_BY, CONVERT_TO_DELTA, DATA, EXISTING_SCHEMA, PARTITION_COLUMNS, PARTITION_SCHEMA, READ_SCHEMA, REPLACING_COLUMNS, SPECIFIED_COLUMNS, TABLE_SCHEMA — in which duplicate column detection can trigger the DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND error.
* [Duplicate column scenarios in Delta Lake](/concepts/duplicate-column-scenarios-in-delta-lake.md) - The specific operational contexts in which the DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND error can be triggered, including adding columns, partitioning, schema operations, data reads/writes, and table conversions.
* [Dynamic ABAC Policy Evaluation](/concepts/dynamic-abac-policy-evaluation.md) - ABAC policies evaluate at query time based on user identity, group memberships, and object tags, requiring tools like SHOW EFFECTIVE POLICIES and documentation for transparency.
* [Dynamic Partition Overwrites with partitionOverwriteMode](/concepts/dynamic-partition-overwrites-with-partitionoverwritemode.md) - Legacy approach using spark.sql.sources.partitionOverwriteMode=dynamic to replace only affected partitions during INSERT OVERWRITE operations.
* [Dynamic Policy Evaluation and Transparency in ABAC](/concepts/dynamic-policy-evaluation-and-transparency-in-abac.md) - ABAC policies evaluate at query time based on user identity, group memberships, and object tags; tools like SHOW EFFECTIVE POLICIES help data consumers understand which rules apply to a given table.
* [Dynamic Policy Evaluation in ABAC](/concepts/dynamic-policy-evaluation-in-abac.md) - ABAC policies evaluate at query time based on user identity, group memberships, and object tags, making transparency a challenge
* [Dynamic Scorer Creation](/concepts/dynamic-scorer-creation.md) - DeepEval scorers can be created dynamically at runtime using get_scorer() by passing the metric name as a string, enabling flexible evaluation pipelines.
* [Dynamic Scorer Creation via get_scorer](/concepts/dynamic-scorer-creation.md) - DeepEval scorers can be created dynamically at runtime by passing the metric name as a string to the get_scorer function.
* [Dynamic Scorer Creation with get_scorer](/concepts/dynamic-scorer-creation-with-get_scorer.md) - A method to dynamically create Guardrails AI scorers at runtime by passing the validator name as a string to the get_scorer function.
* [Dynamic Scorer Factory (get_scorer)](/concepts/dynamic-scorer-factory-get_scorer.md) - MLflow's get_scorer() function enables dynamic creation of DeepEval scorers by passing the metric name as a string parameter.
* [Dynamic Scorer Resolution in MLflow](/concepts/dynamic-scorer-resolution-in-mlflow.md) - MLflow's get_scorer factory function allows creating DeepEval scorers dynamically by passing a metric name as a string parameter.
* [Dynamic Views for Column/Row-Level Permissions](/concepts/dynamic-views-for-columnrow-level-permissions.md) - Using dynamic view functions (current_user() and is_member()) to implement column-level permissions, row-level permissions, and data masking in Hive metastore-managed views.
* [Dynamic Views for Fine-Grained Access](/concepts/dynamic-views-for-fine-grained-access.md) - Dynamic views can be created to restrict MLflow system table access to specific experiment IDs or other custom criteria, providing finer-grained control than granting full table access.
* [Dynamic Views for Fine-Grained Access Control](/concepts/dynamic-views-for-fine-grained-access-control.md) - Using dynamic views with is_member() and current_user() functions in view definitions to enforce column-level, row-level, and data-masking permissions in the Hive metastore.
* [Dynamic Views for Recipient-Scoped Access Control](/concepts/dynamic-views-for-recipient-scoped-access-control.md) - Using dynamic views with the CURRENT_RECIPIENT() function to configure fine-grained access control at row and column levels based on recipient properties. Enables data masking and security by restricting recipient access according to properties specified in the recipient definition. Requires Databricks Runtime 14.2+.
* [Dynamic Views with CURRENT_RECIPIENT()](/concepts/dynamic-views-with-current_recipient.md) - Using dynamic views with the CURRENT_RECIPIENT() SQL function to enforce row-level and column-level security based on recipient properties, enabling fine-grained access control in shared data.
* [DynamoDB Permission Requirements for Databricks Feature Store](/concepts/dynamodb-permission-requirements-for-databricks-feature-store.md) - The comprehensive set of DynamoDB IAM permissions (e.g., DeleteItem, PutItem, Query, Scan) that an instance profile or IAM user must have to publish features to a DynamoDB online store.
* [DynamoDB Permissions for Feature Store Operations](/concepts/dynamodb-permissions-for-feature-store-operations.md) - Complete set of 24 required DynamoDB IAM permissions needed for publishing feature tables to third-party DynamoDB online stores via Databricks Feature Store.
* [DynamoDB Permissions Required for Feature Store Operations](/concepts/dynamodb-permissions-for-feature-store-operations.md) - Complete list of DynamoDB IAM permissions needed for publishing feature tables and looking up features from DynamoDB online stores.
* [DynamoDB Required IAM Permissions for Feature Store](/concepts/dynamodb-required-iam-permissions-for-feature-store.md) - The complete set of IAM permissions (dynamodb: actions) required for write authentication when publishing feature tables to DynamoDB online stores from Databricks.
* [DynamoDB Required Permissions for Feature Store](/concepts/dynamodb-required-permissions-for-feature-store.md) - The comprehensive list of DynamoDB IAM permissions (e.g., PutItem, GetItem, CreateTable, Query) required for a Databricks Feature Store integration with DynamoDB as an online store.
* [Early failure and error reporting for archived queries](/concepts/early-failure-and-error-reporting-for-archived-queries.md) - Archival support enables queries that need archived files to fail early with descriptive errors, reducing wasted compute.
* [Early failure for archived queries](/concepts/early-failure-for-archived-queries.md) - A behavior where queries requiring archived data fail immediately with clear error messages, avoiding wasted compute and enabling rapid recovery via file restoration.
* [Early Failure for Queries Requiring Archived Data](/concepts/early-failure-for-queries-requiring-archived-data.md) - A Databricks optimization that causes queries needing archived files to fail immediately with clear error messages, rather than waiting for unavailable data.
* [Early Failure on Archived Queries](/concepts/early-failure-on-archived-queries.md) - An optimization where queries that must scan archived files fail early with clear error messages instead of running into missing-data issues, reducing wasted compute.
* [Effective Policy Evaluation in Unity Catalog](/concepts/effective-policy-evaluation-in-unity-catalog.md) - Policies applied at a catalog or schema scope are inherited by child objects (tables); SHOW EFFECTIVE POLICIES reveals all applicable policies including those from parent scopes, enabling table admins to see rules without requiring parent-level read access.
* [Effective Policy Visibility via SHOW EFFECTIVE POLICIES](/concepts/effective-policy-visibility-via-show-effective-policies.md) - The ability to view all policies affecting a securable object including inherited policies from parent scopes (catalog → schema → table) without requiring permissions on parent objects.
* [Effective Privileges Union Model](/concepts/effective-privileges-union-model.md) - The principle that effective privileges on a securable object are the union of direct grants and any applicable GRANT policies, meaning a more selective policy does not override broader direct grants.
* [Effects of Disabling Legacy Hive Metastore](/concepts/effects-of-disabling-legacy-hive-metastore.md) - The observable changes in a Databricks workspace after disabling legacy Hive metastore access, including job failures, UI changes, and SQL command impacts.
* [Electricity Consumption Forecasting](/concepts/electricity-consumption-forecasting.md) - Use case of applying probabilistic time-series forecasting to predict electricity demand
* [Embarrassingly Parallel Image Inference](/concepts/embarrassingly-parallel-image-inference.md) - Characterization of image model inference as an embarrassingly parallel workload that can be easily distributed across Spark cluster nodes for near-linear scaling.
* [Embedding Models for RAG](/concepts/text-embedding-models.md) - Text embedding models like GTE, BGE, and Qwen3-Embedding that map text to dense vectors for semantic search, retrieval, and RAG use cases, available alongside generative LLMs in Foundation Model APIs.
* [Embedding Models Response Format](/concepts/embedding-models-response-format.md) - Standard JSON response structure for embedding model queries including object type, embedding vector, model name, and token usage metadata.
* [Embedding Normalization Check](/concepts/embedding-normalization-check.md) - A technique to verify whether embeddings generated by a model are normalized by checking if the L2 norm is approximately equal to 1.
* [Embeddings API](/concepts/embeddings-api.md) - An API endpoint on Databricks for mapping input strings into embedding vectors, supporting batched inputs and model-specific instruction strings.
* [Embeddings API (Databricks)](/concepts/embeddings-api.md) - API for mapping input strings into embedding vectors with batching support, where instructions are model-specific (e.g., BGE, Qwen3-Embedding, Instructor-XL).
* [Empty DataFrame Creation Without sc.emptyRDD](/concepts/empty-dataframe-creation-without-scemptyrdd.md) - Creating empty DataFrames by passing an empty ArrayList (Scala) or empty list (Python) to spark.createDataFrame instead of using sc.emptyRDD.
* [Empty Read Predicates and Whole Table Scan Conflicts](/concepts/empty-read-predicates-and-whole-table-scan-conflicts.md) - A conflict scenario where a transaction reads an entire table without filters while a concurrent operation deletes data, requiring filter predicates to narrow the read scope and resolve the conflict.
* [Empty Source Query Semantics in Delta Overwrites](/concepts/empty-source-query-semantics-in-delta-overwrites.md) - Behavioral differences between REPLACE WHERE (may delete data), REPLACE USING (no deletion), and REPLACE ON (no deletion) when the source query produces zero rows.
* [enable_databricks_trace_archival API](/concepts/enable_databricks_trace_archival-api.md) - An MLflow API function used to begin archiving traces for an experiment by specifying a target Unity Catalog Delta table and optional experiment ID.
* [ENABLE_FEATURE_TRACING for Inference Tables](/concepts/feature-tracing-for-inference-tables.md) - A special environment variable that logs automatic feature lookup DataFrames to inference tables on a model serving endpoint, requiring MLflow 2.14.0 or above.
* [ENABLE_LIQUID sub-error](/concepts/enable_liquid-sub-error.md) - A DELTA_METADATA_MISMATCH sub-error that occurs when trying to enable liquid clustering on an existing table without using overwrite mode
* [Enabling Liquid Clustering on Existing Delta Tables](/concepts/enabling-liquid-clustering-on-existing-delta-tables.md) - The requirement to use overwrite mode with overwriteSchema option to enable clustering on an existing Delta table.
* [Encryption of Classification Metadata](/concepts/encryption-of-classification-metadata.md) - Custom classifier configuration and detection metadata are encrypted at rest, and a customer-managed key (CMK) on the system catalog can be used to manage the encryption key.
* [Encryption of custom classifier configuration](/concepts/encryption-of-custom-classifier-configuration.md) - Custom classifier metadata and detection patterns are encrypted at rest, with optional customer-managed key (CMK) support via the system catalog.
* [End User Feedback Collection Patterns](/concepts/end-user-feedback-collection-patterns.md) - Two approaches for linking user feedback to GenAI traces: using MLflow's auto-generated trace IDs or custom client request IDs.
* [End-to-End Forecasting Pipeline](/concepts/end-to-end-forecasting-pipeline.md) - A complete workflow for time-series forecasting covering data ingestion, resampling, model training, prediction, visualization, and evaluation stages.
* [End-to-end forecasting workflow](/concepts/end-to-end-forecasting-pipeline.md) - A complete pipeline for time series forecasting covering data ingestion, resampling, training, prediction, visualization, and evaluation
* [End-to-End Forecasting Workflow on Databricks](/concepts/end-to-end-forecasting-workflow-on-databricks.md) - Complete pipeline from data ingestion to visualization and evaluation for time-series forecasting on Databricks
* [End-to-End Latency Components](/concepts/end-to-end-latency-components.md) - Total latency for a model inference request consists of two main parts: network overhead (communication time) and model execution time (inference computation), both of which must be accounted for when setting realistic performance requirements.
* [End-to-End ML Workflow on Databricks](/concepts/end-to-end-ml-workflow-on-databricks.md) - A complete machine learning pipeline pattern covering data ingestion, preprocessing, model training, prediction, visualization, and evaluation within the Databricks environment.
* [End-to-End Trace Recording](/concepts/end-to-end-trace-recording.md) - The practice of capturing complete execution flows including inputs, outputs, intermediate steps, and metadata for GenAI applications.
* [End-User Feedback Collection via SDK](/concepts/end-user-feedback-collection-via-sdk.md) - Using mlflow.log_feedback() to programmatically collect end-user feedback (like thumbs up/down) and associate it with specific traces for quality monitoring
* [Endpoint Configuration for Load Testing](/concepts/endpoint-configuration-for-load-testing.md) - Specific configuration recommendations for starting load tests: Small CPU endpoint with concurrency of 4, route optimization enabled, and scale to zero disabled
* [Endpoint Configuration Lifecycle](/concepts/endpoint-configuration-lifecycle.md) - The process of creating, modifying, and updating Databricks model serving endpoints, including immutable properties, in-progress update cancellation, and safe rollback to previous configuration on failure.
* [Endpoint Configuration Restrictions](/concepts/endpoint-configuration-restrictions.md) - Constraints on endpoint configuration updates: external_model endpoints can only have one served entity, and the external_model type cannot be added or removed after creation.
* [Endpoint Configuration Updates and Rollback Behavior](/concepts/endpoint-configuration-updates-and-rollback-behavior.md) - Process of modifying an existing endpoint's compute configuration; old configuration continues serving traffic until new config is ready, with cancel capability
* [Endpoint Creation Interfaces](/concepts/endpoint-creation-interfaces.md) - Three options for creating model serving endpoints: Serving UI (graphical), REST API, and MLflow Deployments SDK (programmatic).
* [Endpoint Creation, Update, and Maintenance Expectations](/concepts/endpoint-creation-update-and-maintenance-expectations.md) - Key operational characteristics: ~10-minute deployment time, zero-downtime updates by keeping old config until new one is ready, 597-second request timeout, and occasional zero-downtime system maintenance where models are reloaded.
* [Endpoint Health Metrics](/concepts/endpoint-health-metrics.md) - Infrastructure metrics including latency, request rate, error rate, CPU usage, and memory usage for model serving endpoints.
* [Endpoint Lifecycle and Instance Profile Staleness](/concepts/endpoint-lifecycle-and-instance-profile-staleness.md) - Changes to an instance profile IAM role or deletion of the profile from Databricks settings do not affect running endpoints until the endpoint is updated or restarted.
* [Endpoint Lifecycle Management](/concepts/endpoint-lifecycle-management.md) - Operations to stop, start, and delete model serving endpoints, including constraints on when each operation is allowed.
* [Endpoint Sizing and Concurrency Planning](/concepts/endpoint-sizing-and-concurrency-planning.md) - Methodology for testing on small endpoints and scaling up based on linear relationships between concurrency, latency, and RPS to determine production endpoint size.
* [Endpoint Sizing from Load Test Results](/concepts/endpoint-sizing-from-load-test-results.md) - A methodology that uses a small concurrency endpoint for load testing, then calculates the production endpoint size needed to meet target requests-per-second and latency requirements based on observed metrics.
* [Endpoint Sizing via Load Test Results](/concepts/endpoint-sizing-via-load-test-results.md) - Methodology for calculating required endpoint concurrency from load test results by correlating client-side concurrency with latency percentiles and RPS targets to recommend final endpoint size.
* [Endpoint Telemetry Configuration](/concepts/endpoint-telemetry-configuration.md) - Process for enabling telemetry on custom model serving endpoints through either the Databricks UI or REST API, including specifying Unity Catalog destination and table prefix.
* [Endpoint Telemetry Limitations and Constraints](/concepts/endpoint-telemetry-limitations-and-constraints.md) - Boundaries of the telemetry feature including no schema evolution, managed Delta tables only, region restrictions, at-least-once delivery, size limits, and QPS thresholds.
* [Endpoint Update and Configuration Constraints](/concepts/endpoint-update-and-configuration-constraints.md) - Rules governing updates to model serving endpoints, including compute configuration changes, the inability to add/remove external_model from an endpoint, and cancellation of in-progress updates.
* [Endpoint Update Semantics](/concepts/endpoint-update-semantics.md) - Rules governing how model serving endpoints can be updated, including constraints on external_model presence and configuration change handling.
* [Enforce Table Access Control](/concepts/enforce-table-access-control.md) - The practice of restricting users to clusters with table access control enabled by denying cluster creation permissions and CAN ATTACH TO permissions for non-compliant clusters.
* [Enforcing Table Access Control in Databricks](/concepts/enforcing-table-access-control-in-databricks.md) - Workspace-level enforcement mechanisms to ensure users only access data through clusters that have table access control enabled.
* [Enforcing Table Access Control via Cluster Permissions](/concepts/enforcing-table-access-control-via-cluster-permissions.md) - The practice of denying users the ability to create clusters or attach to clusters without table access control enabled, to ensure data access restrictions are consistently applied.
* [Enforcing Table Access Control via Workspace Settings](/concepts/enforcing-table-access-control-via-workspace-settings.md) - Workspace-level administrative controls to enable table access control and restrict users to secured clusters with table access control enabled.
* [Entity Columns vs Filter Conditions](/concepts/entity-columns-vs-filter-conditions.md) - A design pattern decision guide for choosing between entity columns (for different aggregation levels) and filter_condition on DeltaTableSource (for row filtering at the same aggregation level).
* [Entity vs Filter Condition Decision Guide](/concepts/entity-vs-filter-condition-design-pattern.md) - Design pattern distinguishing when to use entity columns for different aggregation levels vs filter conditions for row-level filtering at the same aggregation level
* [Entity vs Filter Condition Design Pattern](/concepts/entity-vs-filter-condition-design-pattern.md) - Decision guide for structuring feature definitions: use entity columns for different aggregation levels (e.g., customer-level vs customer-merchant) and filter_condition for filtering rows within the same aggregation level.
* [Entra ID Configuration for Delta Sharing Recipients](/concepts/entra-id-configuration-for-delta-sharing-recipients.md) - Configuration steps for Microsoft Entra ID users to provide issuer, subject claim, subject, and audience values for OIDC federation setup
* [Entry Points in Python Wheels](/concepts/entry-points-in-python-wheels.md) - A mechanism in setup.py that maps a named entry point to a specific function in the package, used to define which code runs when a wheel task executes.
* [env_pack Parameter](/concepts/env_pack-parameter.md) - A parameter used with mlflow.register_model() to trigger packaging of model artifacts and the serverless notebook environment during model registration for express deployments.
* [Environment and Version Tracking in MLflow Traces](/concepts/environment-and-version-tracking-in-mlflow-traces.md) - Tracking execution environment (dev/staging/prod) and application version metadata for debugging and performance analysis across deployments
* [Environment and Version Tracking in Traces](/concepts/environment-and-version-tracking-in-traces.md) - Capturing execution environment (development, staging, production) and application version metadata via environment variables and standardized MLflow fields like mlflow.source.type.
* [Environment Packaging (env_pack)](/concepts/environment-packaging-env_pack.md) - A mechanism in MLflow's register_model function that packages and stages model artifacts along with the serverless notebook environment during model registration, enabling express deployments.
* [Environment Variable Configuration for Databricks Connect](/concepts/environment-variable-configuration-for-databricks-connect.md) - Using environment variables such as DATABRICKS_CLUSTER_ID, DATABRICKS_HOST, DATABRICKS_TOKEN, etc. to configure Databricks Connect without code changes.
* [Environment Variable Injection for App Resources](/concepts/environment-variable-injection-for-app-resources.md) - Databricks Apps automatically injects resource metadata (such as experiment ID) into the app runtime via environment variables, configured using the valueFrom field in app.yaml.
* [Environment Variable Injection for Databricks App Resources](/concepts/environment-variable-injection-for-app-resources.md) - The mechanism by which Databricks exposes resource identifiers (such as experiment IDs) as environment variables in deployed apps, accessible via the valueFrom field in app.yaml.
* [Environment Variable Injection from App Resources](/concepts/environment-variable-injection-for-app-resources.md) - The mechanism by which Databricks exposes resource IDs (e.g., MLflow experiment IDs) as environment variables to app code via the 'valueFrom' field in app.yaml configuration.
* [Environment Variable Injection from Databricks App Resources](/concepts/environment-variable-injection-from-databricks-app-resources.md) - When a Databricks App is deployed with a resource like an MLflow experiment, the resource ID is exposed via environment variables for use in application code.
* [Environment variables for Databricks Connect authentication](/concepts/environment-variables-for-databricks-connect-authentication.md) - Using environment variables (DATABRICKS_HOST, DATABRICKS_TOKEN, DATABRICKS_CLIENT_ID, DATABRICKS_CLIENT_SECRET, DATABRICKS_CLUSTER_ID) to configure connections without code-level config.
* [EnvPackConfig](/concepts/envpackconfig.md) - A configuration class with an install_dependencies parameter that controls whether model dependencies are installed during the env_pack process to validate the environment.
* [Ephemeral Service Logs](/concepts/ephemeral-service-logs.md) - Real-time stdout/stderr logs from model serving endpoints, useful for debugging during deployment.
* [Equal Opportunity](/concepts/equal-opportunity.md) - A fairness metric that measures whether a label is predicted equally well for both protected and unprotected groups (true positive rate parity)
* [Error handling and chaining in MLflow scorers](/concepts/error-handling-and-chaining-in-mlflow-scorers.md) - Patterns for handling scorer errors gracefully via AssessmentError and try/except, and for chaining evaluation results to iteratively refine datasets and applications.
* [Error handling in code-based scorers](/concepts/error-handling-in-scorers.md) - Two approaches for handling scorer errors: letting exceptions propagate for automatic capture, or handling exceptions explicitly with Feedback objects and AssessmentError.
* [Error handling in Databricks notebook workflows](/concepts/error-handling-in-databricks-notebook-workflows.md) - Strategies for handling errors and implementing retry logic when using dbutils.notebook.run(), which throws WorkflowException on failure.
* [Error handling in MLflow scorers](/concepts/error-handling-in-scorers.md) - Two approaches for handling scorer errors: letting exceptions propagate (recommended) for automatic capture, or handling exceptions explicitly with Feedback and AssessmentError.
* [Error handling in scorers](/concepts/error-handling-in-scorers.md) - Two approaches for handling scorer errors: letting exceptions propagate (recommended) or handling exceptions explicitly with AssessmentError and Feedback
* [Error handling patterns for MLflow scorers](/concepts/error-handling-patterns-for-mlflow-scorers.md) - Approaches for handling failures in custom scorers: returning AssessmentError via Feedback for graceful degradation, or raising exceptions to let MLflow handle errors, ensuring evaluation continues even when some scorers fail.
* [Error handling patterns for scorers](/concepts/error-handling-in-scorers.md) - MLflow supports two error handling approaches: letting exceptions propagate (recommended) or catching exceptions and returning Feedback with AssessmentError details.
* [Error handling strategies for MLflow scorers](/concepts/error-handling-in-scorers.md) - Two approaches for handling errors in custom scorers: returning structured AssessmentError within a Feedback object, or letting exceptions propagate for MLflow to handle gracefully
* [Error Monitoring with MLflow Traces](/concepts/error-monitoring-with-mlflow-traces.md) - Pattern for detecting and analyzing errors in production GenAI traces using mlflow.search_traces() with status and time-window filters.
* [Evaluation Chaining and Conditional Guidelines](/concepts/evaluation-chaining-and-conditional-guidelines.md) - A workflow pattern where evaluation results are reused to filter problematic traces and re-evaluate with different scorers or guidelines based on context.
* [Evaluation Dataset](/concepts/evaluation-dataset.md) - A curated, versioned collection of test cases with inputs and optional ground truth expectations, used for systematically testing and validating a GenAI application's quality.
* [Evaluation Dataset Constraints](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-limitations.md) - Technical limitations for MLflow evaluation datasets including a maximum of 2000 rows per dataset, 20 expectations per record, and incompatibility with CMK-encrypted Unity Catalog catalogs.
* [Evaluation Dataset for GenAI Agents](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-for-genai-agents.md) - Dataset structure for evaluation containing inputs (conversation messages), optional expectations, and test cases for scoring GenAI agent responses
* [Evaluation Dataset for GenAI Applications](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-for-genai-applications.md) - Structured input datasets used in MLflow evaluation, containing template-based examples with multiple test cases for assessing GenAI app performance.
* [Evaluation Dataset for Prompts](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-for-prompts.md) - Structured dataset containing input examples and expected facts used to objectively measure prompt quality and fact coverage across different versions.
* [Evaluation Dataset Limitations](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-limitations.md) - Known constraints on MLflow evaluation datasets including a maximum of 2000 rows per dataset, 20 expectations per record, and incompatibility with CMK-encrypted catalogs.
* [Evaluation Dataset Schema](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-schema.md) - The structured schema for MLflow evaluation datasets, defining core fields like inputs, expectations, and lineage metadata for GenAI app evaluation.
* [Evaluation Dataset SDK](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-sdk.md) - The programmatic Python API (mlflow.genai.datasets) for creating, retrieving, and deleting evaluation datasets, including the EvaluationDataset class.
