* [Evaluation Dataset Source Lineage](/concepts/dataset-source-lineage.md) - A field tracking the origin of each dataset record, supporting exactly one source type: human, document, or trace.
* [Evaluation Dataset Storage in Unity Catalog](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-storage-in-unity-catalog.md) - MLflow evaluation datasets are stored as tables in Unity Catalog, which provides versioning, lineage tracking, sharing across workspaces, and governance controls.
* [Evaluation Dataset Structure for GenAI](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-structure-for-genai.md) - A structured dataset format with inputs, outputs, and expectations (context) fields used for MLflow GenAI evaluation
* [Evaluation Dataset Sync](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-sync.md) - A process that synchronizes Expectations collected in a labeling session to an Evaluation Dataset using an intelligent upsert operation keyed on trace inputs.
* [Evaluation Dataset UI](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-ui.md) - A visual interface in MLflow experiments for managing evaluation datasets and records without writing code, including create, edit, delete, tagging, and trace viewing.
* [Evaluation Dataset with Expected Facts](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-with-expected-facts.md) - A structured dataset containing input-output pairs with expected facts that allow measuring correctness of LLM responses by checking fact coverage.
* [Evaluation Datasets](/concepts/evaluation-datasets.md) - Structured test data used as input to `mlflow.genai.evaluate()`, supporting EvaluationDataset objects, lists of dicts, Pandas DataFrames, and Spark DataFrames.
* [Evaluation Datasets and Runs](/concepts/evaluation-datasets-and-runs.md) - Evaluation datasets are curated test cases (with optional ground-truth expectations) and evaluation runs are the results of testing an app version against them using scorers, producing traces and aggregated metrics.
* [Evaluation Datasets for GenAI](/concepts/evaluation-datasets-for-genai.md) - Structured datasets used to evaluate GenAI applications, consisting of input templates or prompts organized as records with consistent schema for batch evaluation.
* [Evaluation Datasets for GenAI Agents](/concepts/evaluation-datasets-for-genai-agents.md) - Structured test datasets containing inputs (conversation messages) and optional expectations fields used to evaluate GenAI agents with custom judges.
* [Evaluation Datasets with Expected Facts](/concepts/evaluation-datasets-with-expected-facts.md) - Building structured evaluation datasets that pair input content with expected facts to serve as ground truth for prompt performance measurement.
* [Evaluation Feedback](/concepts/evaluation-feedback.md) - Quality assessments produced by scorers and attached to individual traces within an evaluation run.
* [Evaluation Harness](/concepts/evaluation-harness.md) - Testing framework for evaluating traces during the development phase of GenAI applications.
* [Evaluation Harness (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/evaluation-harness-mlflow-genai.md) - SDK (mlflow.genai.evaluate()) for systematically testing an app version against an evaluation dataset using scorers, producing traces with feedback assessments.
* [Evaluation Harness and Production Monitoring](/concepts/evaluation-harness-and-production-monitoring.md) - The evaluation harness (mlflow.genai.evaluate()) runs applications against evaluation datasets with scorers for iterative quality improvement; production monitoring schedules scorers on live traces for ongoing quality oversight.
* [Evaluation Run](/concepts/evaluation-run.md) - The results of testing an application version against an evaluation dataset using scorers, containing the generated traces, their assessments, and aggregated quality metrics.
* [Evaluation Run (MLflow)](/concepts/evaluation-run-mlflow.md) - An MLflow run that organizes and stores the results of evaluating a GenAI application, including traces, feedback, metrics, and metadata.
* [Evaluation Run Structure](/concepts/evaluation-run-structure.md) - The hierarchical organization of an evaluation run comprising run info, traces with feedback, aggregate metrics, and configuration parameters.
* [Evaluation Runs](/concepts/evaluation-runs.md) - Test reports that capture aggregate metrics and all per-row traces annotated with scorer feedback, viewable in the MLflow UI for investigation.
* [Evaluation Runs and Results UI](/concepts/evaluation-runs-and-results-ui.md) - The structured test report that captures all evaluation results, including per-row traces annotated with feedback from scorers, viewable in the Databricks UI with pass/fail assessments.
* [Evaluation Traces](/concepts/evaluation-traces.md) - Per-input records within an evaluation run, each containing inputs, outputs, and associated feedback for one example in the evaluation dataset.
* [Evaluation-Production Consistency](/concepts/evaluation-production-consistency.md) - The principle that same evaluation logic and scorers used in offline development can be reused in production monitoring, providing consistent quality views across the AI lifecycle.
* [EVENT_TIME_PRESENT](/concepts/event_time_present.md) - A sub-condition of the DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE error stating that skip offsets must not contain event timestamps
* [EVENT_TIME_PRESENT sub-condition](/concepts/event_time_present.md) - A specific reason for the DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE error, indicating that offsets in the skip range must not have event time metadata (eventTimeMillis must be empty).
* [EVENT_TIME_PRESENT Subcondition](/concepts/event_time_present.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE indicating skip offset range is invalid because the offset contains an event time (eventTimeMillis must be empty).
* [Event-Time Ordered Initial Snapshot Processing](/concepts/event-time-ordered-initial-snapshot-processing.md) - The withEventTimeOrder option processes initial Delta snapshot data in event-time order buckets to prevent watermark-based data drops in stateful streaming queries.
* [Eventual Consistency Impact on Delta Lake on S3](/concepts/s3-eventual-consistency-impact-on-delta-lake.md) - Amazon S3's eventually consistent model can cause data corruption and data loss when multiple systems or clusters modify the same Delta Lake table simultaneously, especially across workspaces.
* [Eventually consistent storage impact on Delta Lake](/concepts/eventually-consistent-storage-impact-on-delta-lake.md) - Amazon S3's eventually consistent model introduces risks for Delta Lake operations like concurrent writes and table modifications, requiring workarounds and single-workspace isolation.
* [Example applications for Databricks Connect](/concepts/example-applications-for-databricks-connect.md) - Reference code examples, including a simple ETL application and charts visualizations with JFreeChart, demonstrating Databricks Connect usage.
* [Example Column (Custom Classifier)](/concepts/example-column-custom-classifier.md) - A column selected from a Unity Catalog table that provides representative values for training a custom classifier's detection rules in Databricks Data Classification.
* [Example column selection for custom classifiers](/concepts/example-column-selection-for-custom-classifiers.md) - Process of choosing 1-10 representative table columns per classifier to train detection rules, where broader and more varied examples improve accuracy.
* [Example Columns Pattern](/concepts/example-columns-pattern.md) - The technique of providing 1–10 representative example columns from existing tables to teach a custom classifier what values to detect; broader and more varied examples produce more accurate detection rules.
* [EXCEPT clause in Unity Catalog access policies](/concepts/except-clause-in-unity-catalog-access-policies.md) - Using EXCEPT in policy definitions to exempt specific groups from masking or filtering rules while applying the policy to all other principals, enabling owner-group exceptions.
* [Expectation vs Feedback Label Types](/concepts/expectation-vs-feedback-labels.md) - Two distinct label types in MLflow labeling: 'expectation' labels capture ground truth / correct answers for evaluation datasets, while 'feedback' labels capture subjective ratings, classifications, or assessments.
* [Expectation vs Feedback Labels](/concepts/expectation-vs-feedback-labels.md) - Two types of labeling schemas: expectation type captures ground truth or correct answers (usable in evaluation datasets), while feedback type captures subjective assessments, ratings, or classifications.
* [Expectations in MLflow evaluation](/concepts/expectations-in-mlflow-evaluation.md) - Ground-truth values or labels provided in evaluation datasets (via an 'expectations' column) that scorers can use for offline evaluation, with graceful degradation for production monitoring where expectations are absent.
* [Expectations Reserved Keys](/concepts/expectations-reserved-keys.md) - Built-in reserved keys in the expectations field (guidelines, expected_facts, expected_response) used by MLflow's LLM judges for structured ground-truth evaluation.
* [Expectations-Based Evaluation](/concepts/expectations-based-evaluation.md) - A pattern for offline evaluation where ground-truth 'expectations' are supplied alongside inputs and passed to custom scorers for comparison.
* [Expectations-based LLM Evaluation Dataset](/concepts/expectations-based-llm-evaluation-dataset.md) - An evaluation dataset structure for MLflow GenAI that includes 'inputs', 'outputs', and 'expectations' (with 'context') to provide ground-truth reference for scorers.
* [ExpectationsGuidelines() Judge (Per-Row Guidelines)](/concepts/expectationsguidelines-judge-per-row-guidelines.md) - A built-in MLflow scorer that evaluates against row-specific guidelines provided by domain experts in the evaluation dataset, enabling different criteria for each example.
* [Expected Behaviors Evaluation](/concepts/expected-behaviors-evaluation.md) - Evaluation technique where judges verify that agent responses demonstrate predefined expected behaviors by comparing outputs against expectations.
* [expected_facts vs expected_response](/concepts/expected_facts-vs-expected_response.md) - Two alternative ground-truth parameters for the Correctness judge: expected_facts allows flexible fact-checking without requiring word-for-word matching, while expected_response requires a closer match to a reference answer.
* [Experiment Artifact Storage Locations](/concepts/experiment-artifact-storage-locations.md) - MLflow experiment artifacts can be stored in MLflow-managed DBFS (default), Unity Catalog volumes (recommended), or S3 (not recommended). S3-stored artifacts don't appear in the MLflow UI and cannot be registered in Model Registry.
* [Experiment-Level Trace Archival Configuration](/concepts/experiment-level-trace-archival-configuration.md) - The pattern of enabling or disabling trace archival on a per-experiment basis using experiment_id parameters.
* [Expert Labeling Sessions for GenAI Review](/concepts/expert-labeling-sessions-for-genai-review.md) - Creating structured expert review workflows using create_labeling_session() and label schemas (categorical and text inputs) to collect authoritative ground-truth feedback on trace responses
* [Expert Trace Labeling Workflow](/concepts/expert-trace-labeling-workflow.md) - The end-to-end process of identifying traces for expert review, creating labeling sessions with schemas, adding traces, having domain experts label them via the Review App, and retrieving labels for evaluation datasets.
* [Explicit Scorer Selection](/concepts/explicit-scorer-selection.md) - MLflow 3 requires users to explicitly list all scorers for evaluation, unlike MLflow 2.x which automatically selected applicable judges based on data fields.
* [Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) on Databricks](/concepts/exploratory-data-analysis-eda-on-databricks.md) - The process of summarizing and visualizing datasets to surface distributions, correlations, missing values, and outliers, supported by Databricks tools including notebooks, dashboards, Genie Chat, and Genie Code.
* [Express Deployment Requirements and Constraints](/concepts/express-deployment-requirements-and-constraints.md) - The set of conditions necessary for express deployments including UC model registry, CPU serving, custom models (not FMAPI), max 1GB environment size, mlflow>=3.1, and serverless notebooks.
* [Express Deployments (Databricks Model Serving)](/concepts/express-deployments-databricks.md) - A deployment method for model serving endpoints that dramatically reduces deployment times by packaging and staging model artifacts during model registration in serverless notebooks.
* [Express Deployments (Databricks)](/concepts/express-deployments-databricks.md) - A deployment approach for Databricks model serving that accelerates endpoint creation and ensures environment consistency between training and serving by packaging model artifacts during registration.
* [Express Deployments for Model Serving](/concepts/express-deployments-databricks.md) - A Databricks feature that packages and stages model artifacts during model registration to dramatically reduce endpoint deployment times and maintain environment consistency between training and serving.
* [Extending Autologged Runs with Additional Tracking](/concepts/extending-autologged-runs-with-additional-tracking.md) - Pattern for combining automatic autologging with manual MLflow tracking calls (log_param, log_metric) by setting exclusive=False and wrapping training code inside mlflow.start_run().
* [Extending lifecycle management transition rules](/concepts/extending-lifecycle-management-transition-rules.md) - A risk scenario: extending the cloud archival interval without caution may cause errors because cloud providers do not auto-restore already-archived files.
* [Extensible Optimization Framework](/concepts/extensible-optimization-framework.md) - MLflow's prompt optimization framework allows plugging in custom optimization algorithms by extending the base class, making it framework-agnostic and compatible with any agent system.
* [External and Foreign Object Deletion Behavior](/concepts/external-and-foreign-object-deletion-behavior.md) - Deleting external tables/volumes removes metadata but leaves data files intact in cloud storage; deleting foreign/federated catalogs removes connection metadata without affecting source data.
* [External API Bottlenecks in Model Inference](/concepts/external-api-bottlenecks-in-model-inference.md) - Performance bottlenecks caused by models calling external APIs during inference, manifested as high latency, throughput limits, and error rates. Mitigation includes caching frequently accessed data and monitoring external API performance.
* [External assets in Unity Catalog](/concepts/external-assets-in-unity-catalog.md) - Data assets (tables and volumes) where Unity Catalog controls governance only and the file storage lifecycle is controlled by the user
* [External JAR Upload for Databricks Connect](/concepts/external-jar-upload-for-databricks-connect.md) - Uploading private or third-party JARs from local directories (e.g., a lib/ folder) to Databricks clusters using the addCompiledArtifacts() API to resolve ClassNotFoundException during UDF execution.
* [External Lineage](/concepts/external-lineage.md) - Extension of Unity Catalog lineage beyond Databricks by registering external assets like Salesforce, MySQL, Tableau, or Power BI to appear alongside Unity Catalog tables in a single lineage graph.
* [External Lineage (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/external-lineage-unity-catalog.md) - The ability to manually or automatically add lineage metadata for data sources and consumers outside of Databricks, enabling end-to-end data lineage views in Unity Catalog.
* [External Lineage API](/concepts/external-lineage-api.md) - A REST API for programmatically creating lineage relationships between external metadata objects and Unity Catalog objects (tables, models, paths, or other external metadata), with optional column-level mappings and custom metadata.
* [External Lineage APIs and SDK](/concepts/external-lineage-apis-and-sdk.md) - Programmatic interfaces including the External Metadata API, External Lineage API, and Databricks SDK for Python for managing external lineage without the UI.
* [External Lineage APIs and SDKs](/concepts/external-lineage-apis-and-sdk.md) - Programmatic interfaces for adding external lineage, including the External Metadata API, External Lineage API, and Databricks SDK for Python, in addition to the manual Catalog Explorer UI.
* [External Lineage in Unity Catalog](/concepts/external-lineage-unity-catalog.md) - A feature in Databricks Unity Catalog that allows users to add lineage metadata for workloads running outside Databricks (e.g., ETL tools, BI tools) to augment automatically captured runtime lineage.
* [External Lineage Limitations](/concepts/external-lineage-limitations.md) - Constraints on external lineage including non-recording in system lineage tables, and resource limits of 10,000 external metadata objects and 100,000 relationships per metastore.
* [External Lineage Limitations and Quotas](/concepts/external-lineage-limitations.md) - Resource limits and constraints for external lineage: a maximum of 10,000 external metadata objects and 100,000 external lineage relationships per metastore, and external lineage is not recorded in lineage system tables.
* [External Lineage Privileges (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/external-lineage-privileges-unity-catalog.md) - The specific Unity Catalog privileges required to create external metadata objects and define lineage relationships, including CREATE EXTERNAL METADATA and MODIFY privileges.
* [External Lineage Relationship Patterns](/concepts/lineage-relationship-patterns.md) - Patterns for modeling complex external lineage: connecting two Unity Catalog tables via an intermediary external metadata object, adding multiple levels of lineage across systems, and specifying column-level lineage.
* [External location](/concepts/external-location.md) - A Unity Catalog object that defines a cloud storage path and its access credentials, which must not overlap with any workspace's default storage path in the same account.
* [External Location Creation via AWS Quickstart](/concepts/external-location-creation-via-aws-quickstart.md) - A streamlined method using AWS CloudFormation Quickstart templates to create external locations and storage credentials in Unity Catalog for S3 bucket access, involving token-based authentication between Databricks and AWS.
* [External locations and credentials in COPY INTO](/concepts/external-locations-and-credentials-in-copy-into.md) - Mechanisms for providing storage access to COPY INTO via Unity Catalog external locations, named credentials, or inline temporary credentials.
* [External Metadata API](/concepts/external-metadata-api.md) - A REST API for programmatically creating and managing external metadata objects in Unity Catalog, including specifying names, system types, entity types, URLs, column definitions, and custom properties.
* [External metadata in Databricks](/concepts/external-metadata-in-databricks.md) - A Databricks concept for managing table metadata externally, with limited support only for streaming tables and materialized views.
* [External Metadata Object](/concepts/external-metadata-object.md) - A securable object in Unity Catalog that represents an entity in an external system (e.g., a Tableau dashboard, MySQL table, or Power BI report), which can be connected to Unity Catalog objects via lineage relationships.
* [External Model Configuration](/concepts/external-model-configuration.md) - Configuration details required to set up an external model endpoint, including model provider selection, task type (chat/completion/embeddings), API keys via secrets, and model name selection.
* [External Model Credential Management via Environment Variables](/concepts/external-model-credential-management-via-environment-variables.md) - Pattern of storing API keys and tokens as Databricks secrets and exposing them as environment variables to serve OpenAI, LangChain, and other SaaS models.
* [External Model Endpoint Constraints](/concepts/external-model-endpoint-constraints.md) - Restrictions on external model endpoints: only one served_entity per endpoint when external_model is present, and external_model cannot be added or removed after creation.
* [External Model Multi-Serving](/concepts/external-model-multi-serving.md) - Serving multiple external (third-party provider) models such as OpenAI GPT-4 and Anthropic Claude on the same endpoint, provided they share the same task type.
* [External Model Routing via Unified AI Gateway](/concepts/external-model-routing-via-unified-ai-gateway.md) - The pattern of routing requests to external model providers (such as Google Gemini) through Databricks serving endpoints, enabling centralized auth, logging, and governance.
* [External Models](/concepts/external-models.md) - Foundation models hosted outside Databricks (e.g., GPT-4, Claude) served through Databricks endpoints with centralized governance, rate limits, and access control.
* [External Models (Databricks)](/concepts/external-models.md) - A Model Serving feature allowing centrally governed access to foundation models hosted outside Databricks, such as GPT-4 from OpenAI and Anthropic, streamlining multi-provider LLM management.
* [External Models in Databricks](/concepts/external-models.md) - Foundation models hosted outside Databricks (e.g., GPT-4, Claude) served through centrally governed endpoints with rate limits and access control.
* [External Models in Databricks Responses API](/concepts/external-models-in-databricks-responses-api.md) - Support for externally hosted models (OpenAI provider, Azure OpenAI provider) that can be queried via the Responses API with full parameter and tool support, unlike Databricks-hosted pay-per-token models.
* [External Models on Databricks](/concepts/external-models-on-databricks.md) - Foundation models hosted outside of Databricks but integrated through Databricks model serving endpoints.
* [External Models vs Foundation Model APIs on Databricks](/concepts/external-models-vs-foundation-model-apis-on-databricks.md) - The distinction between Databricks-hosted foundation models (with specific image handling and token billing) and externally hosted models (where provider documentation governs behavior).
* [External Ray Application Connectivity to Databricks](/concepts/external-ray-applications-connecting-to-databricks.md) - Connect Ray applications outside Databricks to Unity Catalog tables using ray.data.read_databricks_tables() for SQL warehouse queries (Ray 2.8.0+) and ray.data.read_delta_sharing_tables() for Delta Sharing tables (Ray 2.33+).
* [External Ray Applications Connecting to Databricks](/concepts/external-ray-applications-connecting-to-databricks.md) - Connecting Ray applications running outside of Databricks to Unity Catalog tables using ray.data.read_databricks_tables() or Delta Sharing with ray.data.read_delta_sharing_tables().
* [External Ray Applications to Databricks](/concepts/external-ray-applications-to-databricks.md) - Connecting Ray applications running outside Databricks to Databricks data sources using ray.data.read_databricks_tables (SQL warehouse) and ray.data.read_delta_sharing_tables (Delta Sharing) APIs.
* [External Ray Applications to Databricks Connectivity](/concepts/external-ray-applications-to-databricks.md) - Connecting Ray applications running outside Databricks to Unity Catalog tables via SQL warehouses (ray.data.read_databricks_tables) and Delta Sharing (ray.data.read_delta_sharing_tables)
* [External Ray Applications with Databricks](/concepts/external-ray-applications-to-databricks.md) - Connecting external Ray applications to Databricks using ray.data.read_databricks_tables() (via SQL warehouse) and ray.data.read_delta_sharing_tables() (via Delta Sharing/OpenSharing).
* [External Sharing Restriction for Mounted Genie Spaces](/concepts/external-sharing-restriction-for-mounted-genie-spaces.md) - Mounted Genie Spaces cannot be shared with users outside the recipient's organization using OpenSharing; OpenSharing recipients cannot re-share data assets received from a provider.
* [External Table Conversion Caveats](/concepts/external-table-conversion-caveats.md) - Risks and limitations when converting external tables, including shared directory visibility issues and data integrity concerns during the conversion process.
* [External Tables in Unity Catalog](/concepts/external-tables-in-unity-catalog.md) - Tables whose data lifecycle, file layout, and storage are not managed by Unity Catalog; support multiple formats and allow direct non-Databricks compute access to data.
* [External User Authentication for AI Systems](/concepts/external-user-authentication-for-ai-systems.md) - Requirements for authenticating or monitoring users outside an organization who interact with AI applications, including multi-factor authentication and activity logging.
* [Fail-Closed Access Control in Unity Catalog](/concepts/fail-closed-access-control-in-unity-catalog.md) - A security design principle where users not found in an access mapping table, or whose access has expired, see no rows by default rather than all rows.
* [Fail-Closed Design for ABAC Policies](/concepts/fail-closed-design-for-abac-policies.md) - Security model where Databricks denies access to ABAC-secured tables if it cannot safely verify and enforce all applicable policies, including on unsupported compute, specific operations, or when policy dependencies are removed.
* [Fairness and Bias Monitoring for Classification Models](/concepts/fairness-and-bias-monitoring-for-classification-models.md) - Using data profiling to detect whether a classification model performs differently across demographic or protected groups
* [Faithfulness Scorer](/concepts/faithfulness-scorer.md) - A RAGAS scorer that measures the factual consistency between the generated answer and the retrieved context, producing a score between 0.0 and 1.0 with an explanation.
* [Fallback Behavior After Hive Metastore Disablement](/concepts/fallback-behavior-after-hive-metastore-disablement.md) - When legacy Hive metastore access is disabled, fallback to hive_metastore catalog is disabled and jobs referencing legacy tables fail.
* [Fallback from DatabricksSession to SparkSession](/concepts/fallback-from-databrickssession-to-sparksession.md) - The pattern of using SparkSession.builder().getOrCreate() directly when the DatabricksSession class is unavailable, relying on the SPARK_REMOTE environment variable for authentication.
* [Fallback Rules for Unclassified Data](/concepts/fallback-rules-for-unclassified-data.md) - Applying default restrictive tags and policies to unclassified or unverified data objects as a safety net
* [Fallback Rules for Unclassified Data in ABAC](/concepts/fallback-rules-for-unclassified-data.md) - Implementing default restrictive tags and policies for unclassified or untagged data objects to prevent unprotected data exposure.
* [Fallback Rules for Untagged Data in ABAC](/concepts/fallback-rules-for-untagged-data-in-abac.md) - Applying default restrictive tags like 'classification:unverified' to new objects and creating policies that restrict access to untagged data until a data steward reviews the classification.
* [Feature Construction and Registration Workflows](/concepts/feature-construction-and-registration-workflows.md) - Two complementary approaches to creating features: two-step (local Feature construction + register_feature) for experimentation, and single-step (create_feature) for direct registration in Unity Catalog.
* [Feature Definition and Registration Lifecycle](/concepts/feature-definition-and-registration-workflow.md) - Workflow for defining Feature objects locally or via create_feature, then persisting them to Unity Catalog using register_feature for reuse in training, serving, and model governance.
* [Feature Definition and Registration Workflow](/concepts/feature-definition-and-registration-workflow.md) - Workflow for creating Feature objects locally or directly in Unity Catalog using create_feature and register_feature, which can then be used in training and serving.
* [Feature Discovery in Unity Catalog](/concepts/feature-discovery-in-unity-catalog.md) - The ability to browse and search for feature tables by name, feature, comment, or tag within Unity Catalog.
* [Feature Discovery with Genie Code](/concepts/feature-discovery-with-genie-code.md) - Using Databricks' Genie Code assistant to find features and feature tables via natural language /findTables queries
* [Feature Engineering Compatibility Matrix](/concepts/feature-engineering-compatibility-matrix.md) - A reference mapping Databricks Runtime ML versions to the corresponding pre-installed versions of databricks-feature-engineering and databricks-feature-store packages.
* [Feature Engineering for Time Series Forecasting](/concepts/feature-engineering-for-time-series-forecasting.md) - Creating derived features from date/time columns — such as is_weekend binary flags using dayofweek — to improve forecasting model accuracy.
* [Feature Engineering in Unity Catalog](/concepts/feature-engineering-in-unity-catalog.md) - Databricks' feature store solution that integrates feature tables with Unity Catalog for governance, lineage, and discovery
* [Feature Engineering on Databricks](/concepts/feature-engineering-on-databricks.md) - Databricks feature store service for creating, managing, and serving ML features with automated data pipelines and feature discovery.
* [Feature Engineering Python API (FeatureEngineeringClient)](/concepts/feature-engineering-python-api-featureengineeringclient.md) - The Python client library (databricks-feature-engineering) providing methods like write_table, read_table, get_table, create_table, drop_table, and tag management for interacting with feature tables in Unity Catalog.
* [Feature Engineering with Unity Catalog](/concepts/feature-engineering-with-unity-catalog.md) - Integration of Databricks Feature Store with Unity Catalog to provide built-in governance, lineage, point-in-time joins, and cross-workspace feature sharing.
* [Feature flag A/B performance analysis using traces](/concepts/feature-flag-ab-performance-analysis-using-traces.md) - Pattern for comparing latency distributions between control and treatment groups in feature flag experiments by filtering traces on metadata tags.
* [Feature Flag Performance Analysis with Traces](/concepts/feature-flag-performance-analysis-with-traces.md) - Pattern for comparing GenAI application performance across different feature flag states by filtering and aggregating trace latency data.
* [Feature Freshness Monitoring](/concepts/feature-freshness-monitoring.md) - Tracking the last write time and scheduled job status for feature tables to verify data recency.
* [Feature Function](/concepts/feature-function.md) - A user-defined scalar function registered in Unity Catalog that computes feature values on-demand at inference time using both stored and context-provided inputs.
* [Feature Function (UDF)](/concepts/feature-function.md) - A user-defined function in Unity Catalog that computes on-demand features at inference time using input bindings from retrieved and context features
* [Feature Function Integration](/concepts/feature-function-integration.md) - The capability to define and include Python UDFs within a FeatureSpec to compute derived features on the fly, using input bindings from other features or request parameters.
* [Feature Governance and Lineage](/concepts/feature-governance-and-lineage.md) - Built-in tracking of the origin, transformations, and usage of features across model training and inference workflows, enabled by Unity Catalog.
* [Feature Lifecycle Workflows](/concepts/feature-lifecycle-workflows.md) - The end-to-end pipeline covering feature development (create/register), model training (create_training_set), and materialization/serving in Databricks.
* [Feature Lineage and Online Workflows](/concepts/feature-lineage-and-online-workflows.md) - Models trained using Databricks features automatically track lineage to those features; when deployed as serving endpoints, they use Unity Catalog to resolve and look up the correct features in online stores for real-time inference.
* [Feature Lineage and Training/Serving Skew Elimination](/concepts/feature-lineage-and-trainingserving-skew-elimination.md) - Models automatically track lineage to training features and look up latest values at inference, ensuring identical feature computations between training and serving.
* [Feature Lineage in Databricks](/concepts/feature-lineage-in-databricks.md) - Lineage tracking for feature tables that records data sources used to create the table and links to models, notebooks, jobs, and endpoints that consume each feature.
* [Feature Lineage in Unity Catalog](/concepts/feature-lineage-in-unity-catalog.md) - Tracking of data sources used to create feature tables and tracking of downstream consumers including models, notebooks, jobs, and endpoints that use each feature.
* [Feature Lineage Tracking](/concepts/feature-lineage-tracking.md) - Automatic tracking of which features from the Feature Store were used to train a model, enabling governance and reproducibility.
* [Feature Lookup](/concepts/feature-lookup.md) - A mechanism to retrieve precomputed features from a feature table by primary key at serving time
* [Feature Lookup Keys](/concepts/feature-lookup-keys.md) - When joining a feature table to an AutoML training dataset, each feature table primary key must be mapped to a corresponding lookup key column in the training dataset.
* [Feature Lookup with Timestamp Keys](/concepts/feature-lookup-with-timestamp-keys.md) - Using FeatureLookup with a timestamp_lookup_key parameter to perform point-in-time lookups of feature values based on a time column in the label DataFrame.
* [Feature Materialization](/concepts/feature-materialization.md) - The process of persisting computed features to offline or online stores for efficient reuse in training and serving
* [Feature Materialization for Offline and Online Serving](/concepts/feature-materialization-for-offline-and-online-serving.md) - Process of materializing defined features to offline stores for efficient batch reuse or online stores for low-latency model serving using materialize_features.
* [Feature Materialization with Online and Offline Stores](/concepts/feature-materialization-with-online-and-offline-stores.md) - The process of persisting computed features to offline stores (for batch training) or online stores (for low-latency serving) using materialize_features with scheduling triggers like CronSchedule or TableTrigger.
* [Feature Naming and Auto-Generated Names](/concepts/feature-naming-and-auto-generated-names.md) - A naming convention system where features can be explicitly named or auto-named based on function, window, and column properties for traceability.
* [Feature Object and Registration Workflow](/concepts/feature-object-and-registration-workflow.md) - Two-step workflow for defining features locally with the Feature class and persisting them to Unity Catalog via register_feature() or create_feature(), supporting experimentation before registration.
* [Feature Publishing to Online Stores](/concepts/feature-publishing-to-online-stores.md) - The process of publishing Databricks feature tables to third-party online stores (DynamoDB, SQL stores) for low-latency, real-time serving of machine learning features.
* [Feature Registration and Lifecycle](/concepts/feature-registration-and-lifecycle.md) - Two workflows for persisting features to Unity Catalog: a two-step approach (construct Feature locally, then register_feature) and a single-step approach (create_feature that validates and registers immediately).
* [Feature Registration and Lifecycle in Unity Catalog](/concepts/feature-registration-and-lifecycle-in-unity-catalog.md) - Process of persisting locally-defined Feature objects into Unity Catalog using register_feature or create_feature, enabling governance, discoverability, and reuse across teams.
* [Feature Registration Workflows](/concepts/feature-registration-workflows.md) - Two approaches for registering features in Unity Catalog: a two-step workflow (construct Feature locally, then register_feature) for experimentation, or single-step create_feature for immediate registration
* [Feature Serving and Model Serving Integration](/concepts/feature-serving-and-model-serving-integration.md) - Online feature stores integrate with Feature Serving Endpoints and Model Serving Endpoints to automatically look up and serve features for real-time inference and applications.
* [Feature Serving Default Values](/concepts/feature-serving-default-values.md) - A mechanism to supply fallback values for feature lookups when keys are not found in the online store, requiring renamed feature names when using rename_outputs.
* [Feature Serving Endpoint](/concepts/feature-serving-endpoint.md) - A Databricks endpoint that serves precomputed and on-demand features for real-time inference using a FeatureSpec
* [Feature Serving Endpoint Lifecycle](/concepts/feature-serving-endpoint-lifecycle.md) - The create/read/update/delete lifecycle for Feature Serving endpoints, emphasizing update-over-recreate to avoid downtime, and supporting UI, REST API, Python API, and Databricks SDK.
* [Feature Serving endpoint lifecycle management](/concepts/feature-serving-endpoint-lifecycle-management.md) - APIs and UI workflows for creating, retrieving, updating (never delete-and-recreate), and deleting Feature Serving endpoints, with warnings against downtime caused by deletion.
* [Feature Serving Endpoints (Databricks)](/concepts/feature-serving-endpoints-databricks.md) - Feature and function serving endpoints on Databricks that support route optimization using FeatureSpecs.
* [Feature Serving Endpoints for Online Tables](/concepts/feature-serving-endpoints-for-online-tables.md) - A REST API endpoint that serves features from online tables at low latency for models and applications hosted outside Databricks, created via a Feature Spec linked to a Delta table.
* [Feature Serving vs Model Serving](/concepts/feature-serving-vs-model-serving.md) - The distinction between serving raw features directly via Feature Serving versus serving ML models that autonomously look up features via Model Serving, as depicted in the document's decision diagram.
* [Feature Serving with Online Tables](/concepts/feature-serving-with-online-tables.md) - Architecture for serving online table data via REST API endpoints using a feature serving endpoint, enabling low-latency feature lookups for models and applications hosted outside Databricks.
* [Feature Store](/concepts/feature-store.md) - A governed system for defining, managing, and reusing ML features as assets, with the same feature definitions used for both training and serving, supporting batch and real-time ingestion.
* [Feature Store (Machine Learning)](/concepts/feature-store-machine-learning.md) - A centralized repository that enables data scientists to find, share, and reuse features, ensuring the same feature computations are used during model training and inference.
* [Feature Store Access Control (Legacy)](/concepts/feature-store-access-control-legacy.md) - A permission system for controlling access to feature table metadata in Databricks workspaces not enabled for Unity Catalog
* [Feature Store Access Control Permission Levels](/concepts/feature-store-access-control-permission-levels.md) - Three permission levels for feature table metadata: CAN VIEW METADATA, CAN EDIT METADATA, and CAN MANAGE.
* [Feature Store Access Control Permissions (Legacy)](/concepts/feature-store-access-control-permissions-legacy.md) - Three permission levels (CAN VIEW METADATA, CAN EDIT METADATA, CAN MANAGE) that control granular access to feature table metadata in Databricks Feature Store workspaces not using Unity Catalog.
* [Feature Store Access Control vs Unity Catalog](/concepts/legacy-access-control-vs-unity-catalog.md) - Legacy access control for feature tables in workspaces not enabled for Unity Catalog; workspaces with Unity Catalog should use UC privileges instead.
* [Feature Store and Feature Engineering](/concepts/feature-store-and-feature-engineering.md) - Managed Feature Store in Unity Catalog providing a single governed source of truth for features, supporting both batch and real-time serving with Genie Code-assisted discovery and transformation.
* [Feature Store Client API for Remote Operations](/concepts/feature-store-client-api-for-remote-operations.md) - The set of FeatureStoreClient methods (create_table, read_table, write_table, publish_table, create_training_set, get_table) that operate on remote feature stores when configured with a feature_store_uri.
* [Feature Store Client URI (databricks://)](/concepts/feature-store-client-uri-databricks.md) - A URI scheme of the form 'databricks://<scope>:<prefix>' used to specify a remote feature store or model registry when instantiating a FeatureStoreClient in Databricks.
* [Feature Store Lookup Keys](/concepts/feature-lookup-keys.md) - The mechanism for joining feature tables to training datasets by mapping feature table primary keys to columns in the training dataset
* [Feature Store Lookups in AutoML](/concepts/feature-store-lookups-in-automl.md) - The mechanism for passing Databricks Feature Store tables as covariates to AutoML forecasting experiments using the feature_store_lookups parameter.
* [Feature Store Online Store Authentication](/concepts/feature-store-online-store-authentication.md) - Overview of authentication methods (instance profiles and Databricks secrets) for publishing to and reading from third-party online stores in Databricks Feature Store.
* [Feature Store Permission Levels (Legacy)](/concepts/feature-table-permission-levels.md) - Three permission levels for feature table metadata in Databricks Feature Store: CAN VIEW METADATA, CAN EDIT METADATA, and CAN MANAGE.
* [Feature Store Resource Cleanup](/concepts/feature-store-resource-cleanup.md) - Background asynchronous cleanup process that removes tables, pipelines, and jobs when the last materialized feature referencing those resources is deleted, using a Databricks-managed system service principal.
* [Feature Store-Wide Permission Configuration](/concepts/feature-store-wide-permission-configuration.md) - Workspace administrators and users with CAN MANAGE on the Feature Store can set default permission levels on all current and future feature tables from the Feature Store page.
* [Feature Store-Wide Permission Inheritance](/concepts/feature-store-wide-permission-inheritance.md) - Permissions set at the Feature Store level apply to all feature tables (current and future) and can be overridden per-table to add but not restrict permissions
* [Feature Store-Wide Permissions](/concepts/feature-store-wide-permissions.md) - Workspace administrators can set permissions for all current and future feature tables from the Feature Store UI.
* [Feature Table](/concepts/feature-table.md) - An organized collection of features backed by a Delta table with a primary key and additional metadata tracking data sources, notebooks, and jobs.
* [Feature Table Access Control](/concepts/feature-table-access-control.md) - A mechanism within the Databricks Workspace Feature Store for restricting which users or groups can view, modify, or use feature tables.
* [Feature Table Access Control (Legacy)](/concepts/feature-table-access-control-legacy.md) - Mechanisms to control user access to feature tables within the Databricks Workspace Feature Store, documented in a separate dedicated page.
* [Feature Table Default Permissions](/concepts/feature-table-default-permissions.md) - By default, the creator and workspace admins get CAN MANAGE, while other users have NO PERMISSIONS on a new feature table.
* [Feature Table Governance](/concepts/feature-table-governance.md) - Unity Catalog governs feature tables, functions, and models; models inherit permissions from the data they were trained on.
* [Feature Table Governance and Tagging](/concepts/feature-table-governance-and-tagging.md) - Feature tables in Unity Catalog can be browsed, searched, and managed via the Features UI and Catalog Explorer, with support for tags (key-value pairs) for categorization.
* [Feature Table Lookup Keys](/concepts/feature-lookup-keys.md) - Mechanism for joining feature tables to AutoML training datasets by mapping primary keys from feature tables to corresponding lookup key columns in the training data.
* [Feature table management and update strategies](/concepts/feature-table-management-and-update-strategies.md) - Methods for updating feature tables including merge mode for upserts, streaming pipelines for real-time updates, scheduled jobs for periodic refreshes, and composite primary keys (date + ID) for storing historical feature values.
* [Feature Table Permission Levels](/concepts/feature-table-permission-levels.md) - Databricks Feature Store defines three fine-grained permission levels for feature table metadata: CAN VIEW METADATA, CAN EDIT METADATA, and CAN MANAGE.
* [Feature Table Producers](/concepts/feature-table-producers.md) - Notebooks and jobs that write to a feature table, visible in the Producers table on the feature table detail page.
* [Feature Table Producers Table](/concepts/feature-table-producers-table.md) - A UI component in the feature table page that lists all notebooks and jobs that write to a feature table, enabling freshness and job status verification.
* [Feature Table Publishing](/concepts/feature-table-publishing.md) - The process of synchronizing offline feature tables to an online store using the publish_table API, with support for CONTINUOUS, TRIGGERED, and SNAPSHOT publish modes.
* [Feature Table Search](/concepts/feature-table-search.md) - A case-insensitive search capability in the Databricks Feature Store UI allowing users to find feature tables by name, feature name, data source, or tag key-value pairs.
* [Feature Table Search and Discovery](/concepts/feature-table-search.md) - Capability to search feature tables by name, feature name, data source, or tag with case-insensitive matching.
* [Feature Table Tagging](/concepts/feature-table-tagging.md) - Adding key-value tag pairs to feature tables for organization and searchability, with edit and delete capabilities.
* [Feature table tagging and governance](/concepts/feature-table-tags-and-governance.md) - Using key-value tags to categorize, search, and manage feature tables and individual features in Unity Catalog, supported via Catalog Explorer, SQL, and the Feature Engineering Python API.
* [Feature Table Tags and Governance](/concepts/feature-table-tags-and-governance.md) - Tags (key-value pairs) can be applied to feature tables and individual features via Catalog Explorer, SQL, or Python API for categorization and governance.
* [Feature Table Update Strategies](/concepts/feature-table-update-strategies.md) - Methods for updating feature tables including adding new features, merging specific rows, scheduling periodic jobs, and streaming feature computation pipelines.
* [Feature table upgrade to Unity Catalog](/concepts/feature-table-upgrade-to-unity-catalog.md) - Two-step process of first upgrading the underlying Delta table to Unity Catalog, then migrating feature table metadata using upgrade_workspace_table.
* [Feature Tables](/concepts/feature-table.md) - Organized collections of features backed by Delta tables with primary keys, tracked with metadata about data sources, notebooks, and jobs that created them.
* [Feature Tables in Unity Catalog](/concepts/feature-tables-in-unity-catalog.md) - Any Delta table with a primary key constraint in Unity Catalog can serve as a feature table for machine learning, accessible via a three-level namespace.
* [Feature Tracing for Inference Tables](/concepts/feature-tracing-for-inference-tables.md) - Logging automatic feature lookup DataFrames to inference tables by setting the ENABLE_FEATURE_TRACING environment variable on a model serving endpoint
* [Feature Tracing with ENABLE_FEATURE_TRACING](/concepts/feature-tracing-with-enable_feature_tracing.md) - Logging automatic feature lookup DataFrames to inference tables by setting the ENABLE_FEATURE_TRACING environment variable
* [Feature Tracing with Inference Tables](/concepts/feature-tracing-for-inference-tables.md) - Logging automatic feature lookup DataFrames to inference tables by setting ENABLE_FEATURE_TRACING environment variable to true, requiring MLflow 2.14.0 or above.
* [FEATURE_SOURCE Environment Variable](/concepts/feature_source-environment-variable.md) - An environment variable set on serving endpoints to switch their feature data source from legacy online tables to the Databricks Online Feature Store.
* [FeatureEngineeringClient (Python client)](/concepts/featureengineeringclient-python-api.md) - A Python client (databricks-feature-engineering package) for creating, reading, updating, and deleting feature tables in Unity Catalog programmatically.
* [FeatureEngineeringClient API](/concepts/featureengineeringclient-api.md) - The Python client class for creating, writing, and managing Feature Store tables in Databricks
* [FeatureEngineeringClient for Covariate Tables](/concepts/featureengineeringclient-for-covariate-tables.md) - Databricks API client used to create and register feature tables with a primary key (e.g., date) for use as covariates in AutoML
* [FeatureEngineeringClient Python API](/concepts/featureengineeringclient-python-api.md) - A Python client API (`FeatureEngineeringClient`) for creating, reading, updating, and managing feature tables in Unity Catalog, pre-installed in Databricks Runtime ML editions.
* [FeatureEngineeringClient vs FeatureStoreClient](/concepts/featureengineeringclient-vs-featurestoreclient.md) - Two distinct clients: FeatureEngineeringClient for Unity Catalog feature tables and FeatureStoreClient for the legacy Workspace Feature Store.
* [FeatureEngineeringClient.log_model](/concepts/featureengineeringclientlog_model.md) - API method that automatically captures lineage of features, feature tables, and Python UDFs when logging an ML model
* [FeatureEngineeringClient.log_model / FeatureStoreClient.log_model](/concepts/featureengineeringclientlog_model-featurestoreclientlog_model.md) - Models must be logged using FeatureEngineeringClient.log_model (Unity Catalog) or FeatureStoreClient.log_model (legacy) at v0.3.5+ to enable automatic feature lookup at serving time.
* [FeatureFunction](/concepts/featurefunction.md) - A mechanism for on-demand feature computation that combines real-time inference inputs with stored feature values at scoring time.
* [FeatureFunction (On-Demand Feature Computation)](/concepts/featurefunction-on-demand-feature-computation.md) - A Unity Catalog function used in FeatureSpecs to compute feature values at inference time using runtime-provided context data
* [FeatureFunction API](/concepts/featurefunction-api.md) - API construct in Databricks Feature Engineering that binds Python UDF inputs to columns/feature lookups for on-demand feature computation during training and inference.
* [FeatureFunction for On-Demand Features](/concepts/featurefunction-for-on-demand-features.md) - A construct used with FeatureEngineeringClient to register Python UDFs that compute features on-demand at inference time, with lineage tracked like feature tables.
* [FeatureLookup](/concepts/featurelookup.md) - A construct that specifies which features to select from a feature table and defines the join keys used to link feature tables with label data during training set creation.
* [FeatureLookup and FeatureFunction API](/concepts/featurelookup-and-featurefunction-api.md) - Features for a training set can be defined via FeatureLookup (table-based) or FeatureFunction (UDF-based) to compute on-demand features
* [FeatureLookup default values](/concepts/featurelookup-default-values.md) - Mechanism to specify default values for feature lookups when lookup keys are missing, with special handling when using rename_outputs.
* [Features UI and Catalog Explorer](/concepts/features-ui-and-catalog-explorer.md) - User interfaces for exploring and managing feature tables, including metadata like ownership, online stores, last write time, tags, and comments.
* [Features UI in Databricks](/concepts/features-ui-in-databricks.md) - A dedicated user interface for browsing, searching, and managing feature tables within Unity Catalog
* [FeatureSpec](/concepts/featurespec.md) - A Unity Catalog entity that bundles FeatureLookups and FeatureFunctions into a reusable logical unit for model training and Feature Serving endpoints.
* [FeatureSpec Default Values](/concepts/featurespec-default-values.md) - The ability to specify fallback default values for feature lookups within a FeatureSpec, which are used when a lookup key is not found in the feature table.
* [Featurization for Transfer Learning](/concepts/featurization-for-transfer-learning.md) - Using pre-trained deep learning models to compute feature vectors from data, which can then be used as input for downstream models in a different problem domain.
* [Feedback (MLflow Evaluation)](/concepts/feedback-mlflow-evaluation.md) - Quality assessments from scorers attached to individual traces during an evaluation run, representing per-example scores.
* [Feedback Analysis and Monitoring with MLflow SDK](/concepts/feedback-analysis-and-monitoring-with-mlflow-sdk.md) - Using the MLflow SDK to query traces, extract feedback assessments, calculate metrics like feedback rate and positive rate, and analyze multi-dimensional ratings over time windows.
* [Feedback Analysis via MLflow SDK](/concepts/feedback-analysis-via-mlflow-sdk.md) - Programmatic analysis of collected user feedback by querying traces, extracting assessments, and computing quality metrics
* [Feedback Collection Approaches: Trace IDs vs Client Request IDs](/concepts/feedback-collection-approaches-trace-ids-vs-client-request-ids.md) - Two methods for linking user feedback to specific traces: using MLflow's auto-generated trace IDs or generating unique client request IDs.
* [Feedback Collection Patterns in GenAI Applications](/concepts/feedback-collection-patterns-in-genai-applications.md) - Two approaches for linking user feedback to MLflow traces: using MLflow-generated trace IDs or custom client request IDs
* [Feedback Collection with Streaming Responses](/concepts/feedback-collection-with-streaming-responses.md) - Techniques for collecting user feedback when GenAI responses are streamed via SSE or WebSockets, where trace IDs are only available after stream completion.
* [Feedback in MLflow Evaluation](/concepts/feedback-mlflow-evaluation.md) - Quality assessments from scorers attached to each trace within an evaluation run, providing per-example quality scores.
* [Feedback Object](/concepts/feedback-object.md) - The return type of the Correctness judge containing a binary 'value' ('yes'/'no') indicating correctness and a 'rationale' string explaining which facts are supported or missing.
* [Feedback object for rich assessments](/concepts/feedback-object-for-rich-assessments.md) - Scorers can return Feedback objects with value, rationale, source, and metadata for detailed multi-metric evaluations.
* [Feedback Object in MLflow Evaluation](/concepts/feedback-object-in-mlflow-evaluation.md) - A structured result type carrying a value, rationale, optional name, error info, and assessment source metadata for scorer outputs.
* [Feedback Object Return Format](/concepts/feedback-object-return-format.md) - Guidelines judges return an `mlflow.entities.Feedback` object containing a pass/fail value ('yes'/'no'), detailed rationale, assessment name, and optional error details.
* [Feedback Object Schema](/concepts/feedback-object-schema.md) - The structured output format returned by MLflow LLM judges, containing a 'value' field (yes/no) and a 'rationale' field explaining the judge's decision.
* [Feedback Object Structure](/concepts/feedback-object-structure.md) - The output format of MLflow relevance judges, consisting of a 'value' field (yes/no) indicating relevance and a 'rationale' field explaining the judge's reasoning.
* [Feedback objects](/concepts/feedback-objects.md) - Rich return type for scorers enabling detailed assessments with scores, rationales, metadata, and error information, supporting multiple feedback per scorer.
* [Feedback Objects in MLflow](/concepts/feedback-objects-in-mlflow.md) - Return type of make_judge() judges, represented as mlflow.entities.Feedback objects containing evaluation results with typed ratings
* [Feedback Value Types](/concepts/feedback-value-types.md) - A typing mechanism using Python's Literal and bool to define the output format and possible values returned by custom judges, such as categorical labels or boolean assessments.
* [Feedback Value Types for Judges](/concepts/feedback-value-types-for-judges.md) - Custom judges can return various feedback value types including boolean (`bool`), string literals (e.g., `fully_resolved`, `partially_resolved`, `needs_follow_up`), and other types defined via Python's `Literal` type annotation.
* [Feedback Value Types in Judges](/concepts/feedback-value-types-in-judges.md) - The return types for custom judges, supporting Literal string types (e.g., 'fully_resolved') and bool types for different grading scales.
* [Feedback vs Expectation Assessment Types](/concepts/feedback-vs-expectation-assessment-types.md) - Two distinct assessment types in MLflow: Feedback for subjective quality ratings/comments, and Expectation for recording what output should have been produced (ground truth).
* [Feedback vs Expectation in MLflow](/concepts/feedback-vs-expectation-in-mlflow.md) - Two distinct assessment types: Feedback evaluates the app's actual outputs (ratings, comments on what was generated), while Expectation defines the desired or correct outcome (ground truth) that the app should have produced, useful for creating evaluation datasets.
* [Feedback vs Expectation Labels](/concepts/feedback-vs-expectation-labels.md) - The two types of human-generated assessments captured in labeling sessions — Feedback (subjective ratings) and Expectation (ground-truth annotations) — used to improve GenAI apps through systematic evaluation.
* [Feedback vs Expectation schema types](/concepts/feedback-vs-expectation-schema-types.md) - Two distinct schema types: feedback for subjective assessments (ratings, preferences) and expectation for objective ground truth (correct answers, required facts).
* [feedback_value_type](/concepts/feedback_value_type.md) - Parameter in make_judge() that defines the return type of a custom judge, supporting Literal string unions or bool
* [Feedback-Driven Evaluation with MLflow Correctness Scorer](/concepts/feedback-driven-evaluation-with-mlflow-correctness-scorer.md) - Using expert-provided expected_response labels to quantitatively evaluate GenAI app outputs against ground truth using MLflow's Correctness scorer within mlflow.genai.evaluate()
* [Feedback-to-score mapping for prompt optimization](/concepts/feedback-to-score-mapping-for-prompt-optimization.md) - A technique where categorical or qualitative feedback from LLM judges is mapped to numerical scores (e.g., high→1.0, medium→0.5, low→0.0) for use by prompt optimizers.
* [FILE_VALIDATION_FAILED](/concepts/file_validation_failed.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED indicating that one or more files could not be migrated during the external-to-managed table transition.
* [FILE_VALIDATION_FAILED error](/concepts/file_validation_failed.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED raised when one or more files cannot be migrated from external to managed storage during table conversion.
* [FILE_VALIDATION_FAILED error (Delta managed migration)](/concepts/file_validation_failed.md) - Sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED indicating one or more files could not be migrated during the external-to-managed transition
* [FILE_VALIDATION_FAILED Sub-Error](/concepts/file_validation_failed.md) - Sub-error raised when one or more files cannot be migrated during ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED
* [Filter Conditions and SQL Transformations](/concepts/filter-conditions-and-sql-transformations.md) - Mechanisms to filter source rows before aggregation (filter_condition as SQL WHERE clause) and transform columns (transformation_sql for renaming, casting, arithmetic) within DeltaTableSource, with schema_json for type safety.
* [Filter Conditions and SQL Transformations on Source Data](/concepts/filter-conditions-and-sql-transformations.md) - Mechanisms to filter rows (filter_condition) and transform columns (transformation_sql) at the DeltaTableSource level before aggregation, supporting row-wise operations like casts and arithmetic.
* [Filter Predicates in Conflict Detection](/concepts/filter-predicates-in-conflict-detection.md) - The requirement that filter predicates used by a transaction must be applicable for row-level conflict detection, otherwise the operation may fail with PREDICATES_NEED_REWRITE
* [Filter-Based Conflict Resolution Strategy](/concepts/filter-based-conflict-resolution-strategy.md) - The recommended approach to resolve certain Delta Lake concurrent delete conflicts by adding filter predicates to narrow the data scope of operations, reducing the chance of whole-table conflicts and enabling row-level detection.
* [Filtering and Sorting MLflow Runs](/concepts/filtering-and-sorting-mlflow-runs.md) - Ability to filter runs by parameter, metric, or tag values, and sort runs by selected parameters to control which runs appear on charts.
* [Filtering MLflow experiment data](/concepts/filtering-mlflow-experiment-data.md) - Using Spark DataFrame filter expressions on metrics and params columns (e.g., 'metrics.loss < 0.01') after loading experiment data via the mlflow-experiment data source.
* [Fine-tune Llama 2 with DeepSpeedTorchDistributor](/concepts/fine-tune-llama-2-with-deepspeedtorchdistributor.md) - An example notebook demonstrating fine-tuning of the Llama 2 7B Chat model using the DeepSpeedTorchDistributor on Databricks.
* [Fine-tuned and custom model support via Provisioned Throughput](/concepts/fine-tuned-and-custom-model-support-via-provisioned-throughput.md) - Provisioned throughput endpoints support base models of all sizes, fine-tuned variants, fully custom weights and tokenizers, and architectural variations like CodeLlama.
* [Fine-tuning large language models with AI Runtime](/concepts/fine-tuning-large-language-models-with-ai-runtime.md) - Notebook examples for fine-tuning LLMs on Databricks AI Runtime, including parameter-efficient methods.
* [Fine-tuning Llama 2 7B with DeepSpeed on Databricks](/concepts/fine-tuning-llama-2-7b-with-deepspeed-on-databricks.md) - An example notebook demonstrating how to fine-tune the Llama 2 7B Chat model using the DeepSpeedTorchDistributor on Databricks.
* [FineTome-100k Dataset](/concepts/finetome-100k-dataset.md) - A large-scale instruction-following dataset (100k examples) created by mlabonne, used for supervised fine-tuning of language models, formatted in the ShareGPT conversation format.
* [Fixed-Size vs Auto-Scaling Ray Clusters](/concepts/fixed-size-vs-auto-scaling-ray-clusters.md) - Ray cluster sizing strategies on Databricks, including fixed-size clusters with explicit worker counts and auto-scaling clusters that adjust capacity dynamically.
* [Flash Attention](/concepts/flash-attention.md) - An optimized attention mechanism that accelerates transformer training and inference by reducing memory reads/writes, distributed as a prebuilt wheel to avoid slow compilation from source.
* [Flash-Attention Model Logging for Serving](/concepts/flash-attention-model-logging-for-serving.md) - Best practices for logging MLflow models that require flash-attn, including custom wheel versions and compatible CUDA/pytorch dependency specification.
* [flash-attn Dependency Management in Model Serving](/concepts/flash-attn-dependency-management-in-model-serving.md) - Best practices for logging models requiring flash-attn, including using custom wheel versions, specifying compatible PyTorch/CUDA versions, and listing all pip requirements explicitly in mlflow.transformers.log_model.
* [foreachBatch for Idempotent and Upsert Writes](/concepts/foreachbatch-for-idempotent-and-upsert-writes.md) - Using foreachBatch with txnAppId/txnVersion for idempotent writes to multiple Delta tables, and employing MERGE within foreachBatch for continuous upserts from streaming queries.
* [Forecast](/concepts/forecast.md) - The databricks.automl.forecast method configures an AutoML run for training a forecasting model with support for Auto-ARIMA.
* [Forecast Frequency and Horizon](/concepts/forecast-frequency-and-horizon.md) - Two key parameters that define the granularity (frequency) and duration (horizon) of a time series forecast in Databricks AutoML.
* [Forecast Frequency and Horizon Configuration](/concepts/forecast-frequency-and-horizon-configuration.md) - Key parameters for defining a forecasting problem: forecast frequency (granularity like hours/days/months) and forecast horizon (number of future time units to predict).
* [Forecast Horizon and Frequency Configuration](/concepts/forecast-horizon-and-frequency-configuration.md) - Configuration parameters in AutoML forecasting that specify the number of future time periods to predict and the time units (e.g., days, weeks, months).
* [Foreign Asset Sharing via Lakehouse Federation](/concepts/foreign-asset-sharing-via-lakehouse-federation.md) - The ability to share foreign schemas and tables (data managed by external systems) through OpenSharing without copying data or transferring credentials. Data is queried and temporarily materialized on the provider's side. Requires Lakehouse Federation Sharing preview, serverless compute, and default storage. Recommended for ad-hoc exploratory queries with result sizes under 10 GB.
* [Foreign Iceberg Table Sharing](/concepts/foreign-iceberg-table-sharing.md) - Sharing Iceberg tables federated from foreign Iceberg catalogs via Lakehouse Federation, supporting both Databricks-to-Databricks and open Iceberg client recipients.
* [Foreign Schema and Table Sharing (Lakehouse Federation)](/concepts/foreign-table-sharing-via-lakehouse-federation.md) - Sharing data from external systems (foreign tables/schemas) via Lakehouse Federation without copying data into Databricks, using on-demand materialization on the provider side.
* [Foreign Table and Schema Sharing](/concepts/foreign-table-and-schema-sharing.md) - Sharing data from external systems via Lakehouse Federation without copying data into Databricks, using serverless compute for on-demand materialization on the provider side.
* [Foreign Table Sharing via Lakehouse Federation](/concepts/foreign-table-sharing-via-lakehouse-federation.md) - Securely sharing data from external database systems through Lakehouse Federation without copying data, using on-demand query materialization on the provider side.
* [Foundation Model Access Governance Alternatives](/concepts/foundation-model-access-governance-alternatives.md) - Alternative governance mechanisms for Databricks foundation models including system.billing for cost tracking, AI Gateway for rate limits, Private Link for secure connectivity, and egress controls — to be used for day-to-day governance rather than model-specific restriction.
* [Foundation Model Access Governance Use Cases](/concepts/foundation-model-access-governance-use-cases.md) - The distinction between when to use foundation model Unity Catalog permissions (export controls, vendor restrictions, corporate policies) versus other governance tools like billing, AI Gateway, and network controls.
* [Foundation Model API Multimodal Support](/concepts/foundation-model-api-multimodal-support.md) - The capability of Databricks-hosted foundation models to accept diverse input types including text, image, video, and audio across multiple model providers.
* [Foundation Model API Offerings](/concepts/foundation-model-api-offerings.md) - The three Databricks Foundation Model API service tiers — pay-per-token, provisioned throughput, and fine-tuning — each with distinct model retirement timelines and policies.
* [Foundation Model API Security Best Practices](/concepts/foundation-model-api-security-best-practices.md) - Security measures include workspace-level access controls, IP allowlists, PrivateLink, network policies, and automatic container rebuilding every 30 days for compliance.
* [Foundation Model API Service Offerings](/concepts/foundation-model-api-offerings.md) - Three tiers of Databricks generative AI model access — pay-per-token, provisioned throughput, and fine-tuning — each with distinct retirement timelines and policies.
* [Foundation Model APIs](/concepts/foundation-model-apis.md) - Databricks-curated set of state-of-the-art open foundation model architectures (e.g., Meta-Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct) supporting optimized inference with pay-per-token or provisioned throughput pricing.
* [Foundation Model APIs (Databricks)](/concepts/foundation-model-apis-databricks.md) - Databricks-managed API infrastructure for hosting and querying foundation models including vision models via Model Serving.
* [Foundation Model APIs Access Control (Databricks)](/concepts/foundation-model-apis-access-control-databricks.md) - Workspace-level access controls for Foundation Model API endpoints, including admin-only governance modifications, IP allowlists, PrivateLink support, and outbound network policy configuration.
* [Foundation Model APIs Compliance Standards](/concepts/foundation-model-apis-compliance-standards.md) - Enterprise compliance certifications (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, FedRAMP, IRAP, CCCS, UK Cyber Essentials Plus) supported by Databricks Foundation Model APIs, with availability depending on deployment mode (pay-per-token vs provisioned throughput).
* [Foundation Model APIs Limits and Compliance](/concepts/foundation-model-apis-limits-and-compliance.md) - Rate limits, quotas, and compliance standards specific to Foundation Model APIs workloads including provisioned throughput, pay-per-token, and batch inference.
* [Foundation Model APIs Pay-per-Token](/concepts/foundation-model-apis-pay-per-token.md) - A Databricks offering that provides access to generative AI models with pay-per-token pricing, governed by a specific model retirement policy with defined transition periods.
* [Foundation Model APIs Provisioned Throughput](/concepts/foundation-model-apis-provisioned-throughput.md) - A Databricks offering for dedicated model serving capacity with provisioned throughput, governed by a specific model retirement policy with defined transition periods.
* [Foundation Model APIs Rate Limits](/concepts/foundation-model-apis-rate-limits.md) - Rate limits enforced on Databricks Foundation Model APIs to ensure reliable performance and fair resource allocation, varying by workspace tier, model type, and deployment method.
* [Foundation Model APIs Security Best Practices](/concepts/foundation-model-apis-security-best-practices.md) - Security measures for Foundation Model APIs including workspace-level access controls, IP allowlists, PrivateLink configurations, network policy restrictions, and endpoint governance by workspace admins.
* [Foundation Model Context Windows](/concepts/foundation-model-context-windows.md) - The maximum token limits for model inputs and outputs across supported models, ranging from 128K to 1M tokens, with common configurations like 400K input / 128K output for OpenAI GPT models.
* [Foundation Model Fine-tuning (Databricks)](/concepts/foundation-model-fine-tuning-databricks.md) - A Databricks offering for fine-tuning foundation models, governed by a specific model retirement policy with defined transition periods and recommended replacement models.
* [Foundation Model Fine-tuning on Databricks](/concepts/foundation-model-fine-tuning-on-databricks.md) - A feature within Databricks Model Training that lets users customize large language models using their own data via instruction fine-tuning, continued pre-training, and chat completion.
* [Foundation Model Fine-tuning Retirement Policy](/concepts/foundation-model-fine-tuning-retirement-policy.md) - The specific model retirement timelines, migration windows, and replacement recommendations for Databricks' Foundation Model Fine-tuning offering.
* [Foundation Model Permission Enforcement Scope](/concepts/foundation-model-permission-enforcement-scope.md) - The principle that foundation model Unity Catalog permissions are automatically enforced for pay-per-token and batch inference (AI Functions) but not for provisioned throughput endpoints.
* [Foundation Model Retirement and Migration Policy](/concepts/foundation-model-retirement-and-migration-policy.md) - Policies and timelines for retired foundation models including migration guidance and temporary re-routing strategies.
* [Foundation Model Serving Endpoints](/concepts/foundation-model-serving-endpoints.md) - Databricks Model Serving endpoints that deploy and serve foundation models, supporting external models and Foundation Model APIs.
* [Foundation Model Serving Endpoints (Databricks)](/concepts/foundation-model-serving-endpoints.md) - Managed endpoints on Databricks that deploy and serve foundation models, supporting both externally-hosted and Databricks-curated models.
* [Foundation Model Serving Modes](/concepts/foundation-model-serving-modes.md) - Two modes for accessing models via Databricks Foundation Model APIs: pay-per-token (usage-based pricing) and provisioned throughput (dedicated capacity recommended for production workloads).
* [Foundation Model Unity Catalog Permissions](/concepts/foundation-model-unity-catalog-permissions.md) - A Databricks feature that allows account admins to restrict which Databricks-hosted foundation models users and groups can access using Unity Catalog permissions on the system.ai schema.
* [Foundation Models APIs on Databricks](/concepts/foundation-models-apis-on-databricks.md) - Databricks-hosted foundation models available via pay-per-token APIs for embedding tasks, also called Databricks-hosted foundation models.
* [Freshness (data quality metric)](/concepts/freshness-data-quality.md) - A metric measuring how recently a table has been updated, using historical commit analysis to predict expected next commit time and flagging tables as stale if commits are unusually late.
* [Freshness (data quality)](/concepts/freshness-data-quality.md) - A data quality metric that measures how recently a table has been updated; anomaly detection builds a per-table model to predict next commit time and flags stale tables.
* [Freshness and Completeness Quality Metrics](/concepts/freshness-and-completeness-quality-metrics.md) - Two core data quality dimensions: freshness measures how recently a table was updated compared to predicted commit patterns; completeness measures whether row counts fall within expected historical ranges over 24 hours.
* [Freshness Monitoring (Databricks Anomaly Detection)](/concepts/freshness-monitoring-databricks-anomaly-detection.md) - A quality dimension that monitors how recently a table has been updated, using historical commit patterns to predict when the next commit should occur and flagging tables as stale if commits are unusually late.
* [FSCK REPAIR TABLE](/concepts/fsck-repair-table.md) - Command to detect and repair metadata inconsistencies in Delta Lake tables on Databricks
* [FSCK REPAIR TABLE Command](/concepts/fsck-repair-table-command.md) - A Delta Lake SQL command that repairs a table by detecting and reporting various types of corruption including missing files, CRC corruption, unreadable files, and invalid partition values.
* [FSDP (Fully Sharded Data Parallel)](/concepts/fsdp-fully-sharded-data-parallel.md) - A distributed training strategy that shards model parameters, gradients, and optimizer states across multiple GPUs to enable training of large models beyond a single GPU's memory capacity.
* [FSDP (Fully Sharded Data Parallel) on Databricks](/concepts/fsdp-fully-sharded-data-parallel-on-databricks.md) - A parallelism technique that shards model parameters, gradients, and optimizer states across GPUs to reduce memory usage for large models.
* [FSDP Checkpoint Consolidation with FullStateDictConfig](/concepts/fsdp-checkpoint-consolidation-with-fullstatedictconfig.md) - A pattern for saving FSDP-sharded models that gathers the full state dict from all ranks onto CPU of rank 0 and writes a consolidated checkpoint (via save_pretrained) to shared storage like Unity Catalog volumes.
* [FSDP on Databricks AI Runtime](/concepts/fsdp-on-databricks-ai-runtime.md) - Notebook-based examples and integration for running FSDP training workloads on Databricks AI Runtime on AWS
* [FSDP vs DDP (Distributed Data Parallel)](/concepts/fsdp-vs-ddp-distributed-data-parallel.md) - Comparison between FSDP and DDP, where DDP is simpler and recommended for smaller models that fit in a single GPU's memory, while FSDP provides better memory efficiency for large models.
* [FSDP vs DeepSpeed](/concepts/fsdp-vs-deepspeed.md) - Comparison between FSDP and DeepSpeed, where DeepSpeed offers advanced memory optimization features as an alternative to FSDP for large model training.
* [Full Supervised Fine-Tuning](/concepts/full-supervised-fine-tuning.md) - Complete fine-tuning approach for LLMs where all model parameters are updated during training
* [Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP)](/concepts/fully-sharded-data-parallel-fsdp.md) - A PyTorch-native distributed training strategy for large model training, presented as an alternative to DeepSpeed
* [Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP) for Large Model Training](/concepts/fully-sharded-data-parallel-fsdp-for-large-model-training.md) - A distributed training technique that shards model parameters, gradients, and optimizer states across GPUs to enable training of large models that exceed single GPU memory capacity.
* [Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP) on Databricks](/concepts/fully-sharded-data-parallel-fsdp-on-databricks.md) - Memory-efficient training for large models using PyTorch FSDP, supported by the serverless_gpu @distributed API.
* [Function Calling Limitations (Public Preview)](/concepts/function-calling-limitations-public-preview.md) - Current limitations include no parallel function calling, max 32 functions, single-turn optimization, and restricted provisioned throughput support for new endpoints only.
* [Function Calling Trace Integration](/concepts/function-calling-trace-integration.md) - MLflow auto-traces function calling responses from Databricks Foundation Models, highlights function instructions in the trace UI, and supports decorating tool functions with @mlflow.trace to create spans for tool execution.
* [Function Calling Workflow](/concepts/function-calling-workflow.md) - The four-step sequence of calling the model with tools, receiving JSON function arguments, executing the function in code, and returning results to the model for summarization.
