* [Functions and Registered Models](/concepts/functions-and-registered-models.md) - Functions in Unity Catalog represent reusable executable logic including UDFs, stored procedures, and registered MLflow models, with models having additional privileges for version management and tagging.
* [GDPR Discovery and Deletion with Classification](/concepts/gdpr-discovery-and-deletion-with-classification.md) - A use case and example notebook showing how to leverage data classification results to assist with data discovery and deletion for GDPR compliance.
* [Gemini Foundation Models Supporting Multimodal Input on Databricks](/concepts/gemini-foundation-models-supporting-multimodal-input-on-databricks.md) - The list of Gemini pay-per-token foundation models on Databricks that support audio and video inputs, including databricks-gemini-3-1-pro, databricks-gemini-3-pro, and others.
* [GenAI Agent Evaluation Workflow](/concepts/genai-agent-evaluation-workflow.md) - End-to-end process of creating an agent, defining custom judges, building a test dataset, and running evaluations to compare agent configurations
* [GenAI Agent Observability](/concepts/genai-agent-observability.md) - The practice of monitoring, tracing, debugging, and evaluating GenAI agent behavior using MLflow tools
* [GenAI App Evaluation Workflow on Databricks](/concepts/genai-app-evaluation-workflow-on-databricks.md) - End-to-end workflow pattern: instrument a GenAI app with tracing, collect end-user feedback, add developer annotations, conduct expert review via labeling sessions, and evaluate with scorers.
* [GenAI App Lifecycle Management](/concepts/genai-app-lifecycle-management.md) - Versioning, tracking, and governance for GenAI applications including prompts, apps, and traces across development and production
* [GenAI Application Evaluation Lifecycle](/concepts/genai-application-evaluation-lifecycle.md) - The iterative workflow of developing, evaluating, and monitoring GenAI applications across multiple phases including development testing and production monitoring
* [GenAI Evaluation and Quality Assessment](/concepts/genai-evaluation-and-quality-assessment.md) - Reviewing assessment scores from human feedback, LLM judges, and programmatic checks; inspecting evaluation datasets and scorers for GenAI agents
* [GenAI Evaluation Dataset Design](/concepts/genai-evaluation-dataset-design.md) - Practice of designing structured evaluation datasets with diverse input templates to systematically test GenAI application behavior across different scenarios.
* [GenAI human feedback and review tools](/concepts/genai-human-feedback-and-review-tools.md) - Databricks provides a Review App for human feedback on GenAI outputs, including a Chat UI for vibe checks and an expert feedback UI for labeling traces, enabling structured human evaluation of generative AI.
* [GenAI Observability](/concepts/genai-observability.md) - The practice of monitoring, debugging, and optimizing generative AI applications through comprehensive trace data and metadata analysis.
* [GenAI production monitoring with LLM judges](/concepts/genai-production-monitoring-with-llm-judges.md) - A Databricks service that continuously evaluates a sample of production GenAI traffic using LLM judges and scorers, powered by production-scale trace ingestion stored in Unity Catalog tables.
* [GenAI Trace Analysis and Debugging](/concepts/genai-trace-analysis-and-debugging.md) - The capability to investigate failing traces, examine span trees, pinpoint root causes, analyze latency, and identify bottlenecks in GenAI agent execution flows within MLflow.
* [GenAI Tracing Autolog on Serverless Clusters](/concepts/genai-tracing-autolog-on-serverless-clusters.md) - On Databricks serverless compute clusters, genAI tracing autologging must be explicitly enabled per integration
* [GENERATE Command (Delta Lake)](/concepts/generate-command-delta-lake.md) - A SQL statement used to generate manifest files for Delta Lake tables, primarily for compatibility with external query engines.
* [GENERATE statement (Delta Lake)](/concepts/generate-command-delta-lake.md) - SQL command to generate manifest files for Delta tables, enabling external query engines to read Delta Lake data.
* [GENERATE Syntax Constraints on Delta Table Names](/concepts/generate-syntax-constraints-on-delta-table-names.md) - When using the GENERATE command, the table name must not include temporal specifications or options specifications.
* [generate_test_cases from Production Sessions](/concepts/generate_test_cases-from-production-sessions.md) - A utility function that extracts test case goals and personas from existing production conversation sessions to reflect real user behavior.
* [Generating Test Cases from Production Conversations](/concepts/generating-test-cases-from-production-conversations.md) - Extracting structured test cases (goals and personas) from existing production conversation sessions using mlflow.genai.simulators.generate_test_cases, enabling test scenarios that reflect real user behavior.
* [Genie Agent Sharing Preview](/concepts/genie-agent-sharing-preview.md) - An account-level preview feature that must be enabled for Genie Space sharing via OpenSharing to work.
* [Genie Code](/concepts/genie-code.md) - A natural language interface within Databricks/MLflow for conversational exploration of GenAI observability and evaluation data
* [Genie Code (AI Assistant for Data Science)](/concepts/genie-code-ai-assistant-for-data-science.md) - An AI assistant integrated across Databricks notebooks and workspace for development, debugging, and operations, drawing on specialized enterprise knowledge.
* [Genie Code (AI-Assisted Development)](/concepts/genie-code-ai-assisted-development.md) - An AI assistant integrated across notebooks and workspace on Databricks, used for development, debugging, and ongoing operations with specialized knowledge of enterprise context.
* [Genie Code (DS Agent)](/concepts/genie-code-ds-agent.md) - An AI-assisted agent on Databricks that performs automated EDA, feature discovery, code generation, AutoML, and model serving diagnostics using natural language.
* [Genie Code AI Assistant for ML](/concepts/genie-code-ai-assistant-for-ml.md) - An AI-powered assistant that accelerates the ML lifecycle by performing automated EDA, generating code for feature pipelines, acting as intelligent AutoML from natural language requests, and diagnosing model serving endpoint issues.
* [Genie Code Best Practice Guidance](/concepts/genie-code-best-practice-guidance.md) - Provides recommendations on scaling configurations, endpoint states, monitoring setup, and links to relevant documentation.
* [Genie Code Deployment Diagnostics](/concepts/genie-code-deployment-diagnostics.md) - Capability to diagnose deployment failures, analyze build logs and endpoint state, and assess pending configuration changes.
* [Genie Code for Agent Observability](/concepts/genie-code-for-agent-observability.md) - A natural language interface for analyzing traces, debugging errors, and exploring evaluation data in MLflow Tracing.
* [Genie Code for Deep Learning](/concepts/genie-code-for-deep-learning.md) - An AI assistant feature that supports deep learning workloads on AI Runtime with code generation, debugging, and optimization suggestions
* [Genie Code for Feature Search](/concepts/genie-code-for-feature-search.md) - A natural-language query feature in Databricks that allows users to find feature tables using `/findTables` commands mentioning 'features' or 'feature tables'.
* [Genie Code for Feature Table Discovery](/concepts/genie-code-for-feature-table-discovery.md) - Using the Genie Code assistant's /findTables command with 'features' or 'feature tables' queries to locate relevant feature tables.
* [Genie Code for Lineage](/concepts/genie-code-for-lineage.md) - A natural-language interface in Databricks that allows users to query table lineages and insights using commands such as /getTableLineages and /getTableInsights.
* [Genie Code for Lineage Queries](/concepts/genie-code-for-lineage-queries.md) - Natural-language assistant in Catalog Explorer that can answer lineage questions via commands like /getTableLineages and /getTableInsights to show dependencies and usage patterns.
* [Genie Code for ML](/concepts/genie-code-for-ml.md) - AI-assisted data science tool on Databricks that automates exploratory data analysis, feature engineering, model training (AutoML), deployment, and endpoint diagnostics via natural language.
* [Genie Code for Model Serving Observability](/concepts/genie-code-for-model-serving-observability.md) - An AI-powered read-only advisor that helps diagnose issues, analyze performance, and provide guidance for Databricks model serving endpoints.
* [Genie Code for Trace Analysis](/concepts/genie-code-for-trace-analysis.md) - A natural language interface that allows users to query, explore, and debug traces using plain English questions instead of writing queries or navigating UI pages.
* [Genie Code Instrumentation Guidance](/concepts/genie-code-instrumentation-guidance.md) - Genie Code provides help adding tracing to code with autolog(), @mlflow.trace, or manual spans, including runnable code snippets that can be pasted into Databricks notebooks.
* [Genie Code Latency and Error Analysis](/concepts/genie-code-latency-and-error-analysis.md) - Ability to investigate high latency, timeout issues, and error patterns using metrics, events, inference tables, and service logs.
* [Genie Code Requirements](/concepts/genie-code-requirements.md) - To use Genie Code for agent observability and evaluation, the workspace needs Partner-powered AI features enabled for both account and workspace, and the workspace must be in a supported region as Genie Code uses Geos for data residency.
* [Genie Space Mount Prerequisites](/concepts/genie-space-mount-prerequisites.md) - To mount a shared Genie Space, a user needs the USE PROVIDER privilege, CREATE CATALOG privilege on the Unity Catalog metastore, and access to a pro or serverless SQL warehouse.
* [Genie Space OpenSharing](/concepts/genie-space-opensharing.md) - A feature that allows sharing a Genie Space with users outside your organization via a point-in-time snapshot of data assets and instructions.
* [Genie Space OpenSharing (Beta)](/concepts/genie-space-opensharing-beta.md) - A Databricks feature (currently in Beta) that allows data providers to share Genie Spaces with recipients across workspaces using the OpenSharing protocol.
* [Genie Space Sharing Limitations](/concepts/genie-space-sharing-limitations.md) - Constraints on sharing Genie Spaces: snapshot-only (no live updates), 256 KB compressed size limit for configuration, and inability to share spaces containing metric views.
* [Genie Space Sharing Recipient Management](/concepts/genie-space-sharing-recipient-management.md) - The ability to add additional recipients to an existing shared Genie Space snapshot, but inability to modify data assets in the share after creation.
* [Genie Space Sharing Requirements](/concepts/genie-space-sharing-requirements.md) - Prerequisites for sharing a Genie Space, including Genie Agent Sharing preview, OpenSharing setup, CREATE SHARE privilege, CAN EDIT permission, SELECT on data assets, and at least one recipient.
* [Genie Space Snapshot](/concepts/genie-space-snapshot.md) - A point-in-time capture of a Genie Space's tables, instructions, curated SQL examples, SQL functions, and configuration, created when the Share button is clicked.
* [GEPA (Gradient-Free Evolutionary Prompt Algorithm)](/concepts/gepa-gradient-free-evolutionary-prompt-algorithm.md) - An evolutionary optimization algorithm used by MLflow to iteratively refine prompt templates without gradient computation
* [GEPA Optimization Algorithm](/concepts/gepa-optimization-algorithm.md) - Generative Evolutionary Prompt Algorithm (GEPA) - the prompt optimization algorithm implemented via GepaPromptOptimizer, researched by Databricks AI Research Team, which iteratively refines prompts using LLM-driven reflection and automated feedback.
* [GepaPromptOptimizer](/concepts/gepapromptoptimizer.md) - An MLflow prompt optimizer that uses a reflection model to iteratively improve prompt templates based on evaluation scores from custom scorers.
* [get_scorer Dynamic Creator](/concepts/get_scorer-dynamic-creator.md) - A function in mlflow.genai.scorers.ragas that allows creating a RAGAS scorer at runtime by passing the metric name as a string, enabling dynamic evaluation workflows.
* [Git Folder Limitation with MLflow 3 Tracing](/concepts/git-folder-limitation-with-mlflow-3-tracing.md) - Known limitation where MLflow 3 real-time tracing does not work by default when deploying agents from notebooks stored in Databricks Git folders, requiring explicit experiment assignment via mlflow.set_experiment() to a non-Git-associated experiment.
* [Git Snapshot Pinning](/concepts/git-snapshot-pinning.md) - Pin code versions in training jobs by git branch or commit SHA using the `git:` block inside `code_source`, with optional remote HEAD fetching via `git.remote`.
* [Git-based code workflows](/concepts/git-based-code-workflows.md) - Approach for submitting training workloads using code stored in git repositories.
* [Global Mode Ray Cluster](/concepts/global-mode-ray-cluster.md) - A shared Ray cluster on Databricks accessible by multiple users with no automatic inactivity timeout, started via setup_global_ray_cluster
* [Global mode Ray clusters](/concepts/global-mode-ray-cluster.md) - Shared Ray clusters accessible by all users attached to a Databricks cluster, created via setup_global_ray_cluster, with no automatic shutdown timeout
* [Global Tags](/concepts/global-tags.md) - Classification tags that detect sensitive data regardless of geographic origin and are available in all Databricks workspace regions.
* [Global vs Per-Row Guidelines](/concepts/global-vs-per-row-guidelines.md) - Two strategies for applying evaluation criteria: uniform Guidelines() across all rows vs row-specific ExpectationsGuidelines() for domain-expert-labeled datasets.
* [Global vs Per-Row Guidelines Judges](/concepts/global-vs-per-row-guidelines-judges.md) - MLflow provides two built-in guidelines judges: `Guidelines()` for uniform global rules across all rows, and `ExpectationsGuidelines()` for per-row guidelines labeled by domain experts in the evaluation dataset.
* [Global vs User-Specific Ray Clusters](/concepts/global-vs-user-specific-ray-clusters.md) - Two lifecycle modes for Ray clusters on Databricks: single-user clusters tied to notebook sessions and shared global clusters accessible by all workspace users.
* [Gloo Errors in Distributed CPU Training](/concepts/gloo-errors-in-distributed-cpu-training.md) - Connection refused errors when using the Gloo backend for distributed training on CPU instances, resolved by setting the GLOO_SOCKET_IFNAME environment variable.
* [GluonTS](/concepts/gluonts.md) - A library for probabilistic time-series forecasting, used with the DeepAR model on Databricks
* [GluonTS Evaluation Metrics](/concepts/gluonts-evaluator.md) - Built-in evaluation toolkit in GluonTS that computes standard forecasting metrics including MASE, RMSE, and quantile losses across individual time series and aggregated results.
* [GluonTS Evaluator](/concepts/gluonts-evaluator.md) - A component of the GluonTS library that computes standard forecasting metrics including MASE, RMSE, and quantile losses across multiple time series.
* [GluonTS for Time Series Forecasting](/concepts/gluonts-for-time-series-forecasting.md) - GluonTS is a probabilistic time-series forecasting toolkit used for end-to-end workflows including data ingestion, resampling, model training, prediction, visualization, and evaluation.
* [GluonTS PandasDataset](/concepts/gluonts-pandasdataset.md) - A GluonTS dataset wrapper that converts Pandas DataFrames into the time series format required by GluonTS models, enabling seamless integration with standard data science workflows.
* [Golden Dataset](/concepts/golden-dataset.md) - A 'golden set' of evaluation examples that must always work correctly, used to prevent regressions when testing fixes and changes to prompts, models, or app logic.
* [Golden Set](/concepts/golden-set.md) - A curated collection of test examples that must always pass correctly, used to prevent regressions when iterating on prompts, models, or application logic.
* [Google Gemini API (on Databricks)](/concepts/google-gemini-api-on-databricks.md) - Databricks-hosted access to Google's Gemini API for Gemini models, uniquely supporting text, image, video, and audio inputs.
* [Google Gemini API Integration on Databricks](/concepts/google-gemini-api-integration-on-databricks.md) - Using the Google Gemini API client on Databricks to send media as inlineData (base64) or fileData (URL reference) within the parts array, with Databricks-specific authentication.
* [Google GenAI SDK Custom Endpoint Configuration](/concepts/google-genai-sdk-custom-endpoint-configuration.md) - Using the google.genai Python SDK with custom base_url and headers in http_options to route API calls through a Databricks serving endpoint instead of directly to Google.
* [Governance of ML and AI Assets in Unity Catalog](/concepts/governance-of-ml-and-ai-assets-in-unity-catalog.md) - Unified governance of data, ML assets, model endpoints, and ML tooling through Unity Catalog, AI Gateway, and Databricks AI Security providing a single security and administration layer.
* [Governed Tag (Databricks)](/concepts/governed-tag-databricks.md) - A tag type in Databricks Unity Catalog used for governing and classifying sensitive data, with permissions, allowed values, and integration with ABAC column-level masks.
* [Governed Tag Permissions in Unity Catalog](/concepts/governed-tag-permissions-in-unity-catalog.md) - The ASSIGN permission on system-governed tags and APPLY TAG privilege required to apply certification status tags to Unity Catalog objects.
* [Governed Tags](/concepts/governed-tags.md) - User-defined or system-defined key-value metadata tags applied to securable objects in Unity Catalog that can be referenced in ABAC GRANT policy conditions to dynamically determine access.
* [Governed Tags (Databricks)](/concepts/governed-tag-databricks.md) - Tags managed under a governance policy that can be assigned to data assets; custom classifiers use governed tags to label detected sensitive data columns in Unity Catalog.
* [Governed Tags (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/governed-tags-unity-catalog.md) - Controlled, administrator-defined tag vocabularies that users can apply to catalog objects to organize and categorize data by topic, team, or domain.
* [Governed Tags and System Tags](/concepts/governed-tags-and-system-tags.md) - Tag metadata used in GRANT policy conditions — governed tags are user-defined, system tags are predefined by Databricks — to determine which securable objects a policy applies to.
* [Governed Tags for ABAC Policies](/concepts/governed-tags-for-abac-policies.md) - Tags applied to securable objects (catalogs, schemas, tables, columns) that policies use to match targets for row filtering or column masking.
* [Governed Tags for ABAC Policy Discovery](/concepts/governed-tags-for-abac-policy-discovery.md) - Using governed tags on columns to automatically identify which columns should participate in row filter and column mask policies via MATCH COLUMNS, enabling attribute-based access control.
* [Governed Tags for Advanced Approvals](/concepts/governed-tags-for-advanced-approvals.md) - Using Databricks governed tag policies to control who can approve deployment jobs, enabling multi-group approval workflows (e.g., separate Legal and Security gates) and preventing model owner self-approval.
* [Governed tags for column classification](/concepts/governed-tags-for-column-classification.md) - A governed tag system in Unity Catalog that associates key-value metadata (e.g., domain, sensitivity) with columns, which can be queried by policy MATCH COLUMNS clauses to dynamically target columns.
* [Governed Tags in Unity Catalog](/concepts/governed-tags.md) - Attributes/ tags associated with securable objects that are used in ABAC policy conditions to identify which data a policy should protect.
* [Governed Tags Integration](/concepts/governed-tags-integration.md) - Custom classifiers assign governed tags to detected columns, enabling ABAC column-level masks and auto-tagging for organization-specific data types.
* [Governed Tags Vocabulary](/concepts/governed-tags-vocabulary.md) - Controlled tag vocabularies that administrators create for users to organize and categorize catalog objects by topic, team, or domain, making data browseable and filterable.
* [GPT-OSS 120B Model Fine-Tuning](/concepts/gpt-oss-120b-model-fine-tuning.md) - The supervised fine-tuning of a 120 billion parameter GPT-OSS model using distributed training techniques on 8 H100 GPUs with Databricks Serverless GPU Compute.
* [GPU Build Failures and Flash-Attention Dependencies](/concepts/gpu-build-failures-and-flash-attention-dependencies.md) - Handling GPU-specific container build failures in model serving, including flash-attn dependency management with custom wheels and CUDA version compatibility.
* [GPU Capacity Limitations in Model Deployment](/concepts/gpu-capacity-limitations-in-model-deployment.md) - A known limitation where model deployment on Databricks may fail due to GPU capacity issues, causing timeout during endpoint creation or update.
* [GPU Memory Optimization for Large Language Models](/concepts/gpu-memory-optimization-for-large-language-models.md) - Techniques such as gradient accumulation fusion and CPU offloading used to reduce GPU memory footprint when training models ranging from 1B to 100B+ parameters.
* [GPU Optimization for Deep Learning on Databricks](/concepts/gpu-optimization-for-deep-learning-on-databricks.md) - Best practices for GPU selection (including A100 GPUs), GPU scheduling, and leveraging Single Node GPU clusters for cost-effective deep learning development.
* [GPU Resource Availability for Model Serving](/concepts/gpu-resource-availability-for-model-serving.md) - Constraints and failure modes related to GPU supply limitations when building and deploying GPU-backed model serving endpoints on Databricks.
* [GPU Resource Conservation](/concepts/gpu-resource-conservation.md) - Best practice of attaching notebooks to CPU clusters for non-GPU operations (e.g., Git cloning, data conversion, EDA) to preserve GPU resources.
* [GPU Resource Management for Deep Learning on Databricks](/concepts/gpu-resource-management-for-deep-learning-on-databricks.md) - Guidance on selecting GPU instances (including A100 GPUs), GPU scheduling optimization, and using cluster policies to guide resource choices for deep learning workloads on Databricks.
* [GPU Resource Management for Model Serving](/concepts/gpu-resource-management-for-model-serving.md) - Handling GPU availability, GPU container builds, and custom GPU serving configurations in Databricks Model Serving.
* [GPU resource preservation strategy](/concepts/gpu-resource-preservation-strategy.md) - Operations that don't require GPUs (e.g., Git cloning, data conversion, EDA) should use a CPU cluster instead of serverless GPU to preserve GPU resources.
* [GPU Resources Monitoring Pane](/concepts/gpu-resources-monitoring-pane.md) - A Databricks notebook side pane that displays per-GPU utilization, memory usage, and temperature metrics for AI Runtime workloads, polling every 10 seconds with 2 hours of history.
* [GPU Runtime Utilities (serverless_gpu.runtime)](/concepts/gpu-runtime-utilities-serverless_gpuruntime.md) - Runtime module providing get_local_rank() and get_global_rank() for managing multi-GPU process identity
* [GPU Training for XGBoost on Databricks](/concepts/gpu-accelerated-xgboost-training.md) - Support for GPU-accelerated XGBoost training on Databricks clusters using the use_gpu parameter, with compute capability requirements for different runtime versions
* [GPU Training for XGBoost on Spark](/concepts/gpu-training-for-xgboost-on-spark.md) - GPU-accelerated XGBoost training on Databricks clusters via the use_gpu parameter, supported from Databricks Runtime 9.1 LTS ML and above.
* [GPU Training with xgboost.spark](/concepts/gpu-training-with-xgboostspark.md) - Enabling GPU-accelerated training by setting use_gpu=True, with recommendation to keep spark.task.resource.gpu.amount=1 to avoid idle GPUs.
* [GPU Utilization Monitoring Dashboard](/concepts/gpu-utilization-monitoring-dashboard.md) - A Databricks dashboard for monitoring average GPU utilization across MLflow experiments, helping identify inefficient resource usage across a workspace.
* [GPU Utilization Monitoring with MLflow](/concepts/gpu-utilization-monitoring-dashboard.md) - Using MLflow system tables dashboards to monitor average GPU utilization across experiments and identify inefficient resource usage
* [GPU workload considerations for model serving](/concepts/gpu-workload-limitations-in-model-serving.md) - GPU serving has longer container build times, a 60-minute deployment timeout, storage constraints for large models, slower autoscaling than CPU, and no GPU capacity guarantee after scale-to-zero — large models should use Foundation Model APIs instead.
* [GPU Workload Limitations for Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/gpu-workload-limitations-in-model-serving.md) - Specific limitations when serving models on GPU: longer container image creation, potential timeouts for very large models exceeding 60-minute deployment, longer autoscaling, and no GPU capacity guarantee when scaling to zero.
* [GPU Workload Limitations for Model Serving](/concepts/gpu-workload-limitations-in-model-serving.md) - Specific limitations when deploying custom models on GPU: longer container build times, potential timeout for large models (60-minute limit), longer autoscaling, and no guaranteed GPU capacity when scaling to zero.
* [GPU Workload Limitations in Model Serving](/concepts/gpu-workload-limitations-in-model-serving.md) - GPU serving endpoints have longer container build times, potential timeout for large models exceeding 60-minute builds, longer autoscaling, and no GPU capacity guarantee when scaling to zero.
* [GPU Workload Type Selection Deprecation](/concepts/gpu-workload-type-selection-deprecation.md) - The move away from requiring customers to manually select GPU workload types for LLM serving endpoints, with legacy API-only support remaining for those who need to continue selecting GPU types.
* [GPU Workload Types for Model Serving](/concepts/gpu-workload-types-for-model-serving.md) - GPU compute configurations available for Databricks Model Serving endpoints, with concurrency determining replica count (replicas = concurrency / 4).
* [GPU Workload Types in Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/gpu-workload-types-in-databricks-model-serving.md) - GPU compute options for model serving endpoints supporting PyTorch 1.13-2.0.1, TensorFlow 2.5-2.13, and MLflow 2.4+; concurrency determines replica count (concurrency / 4).
* [GPU-Accelerated Computer Vision on Databricks](/concepts/gpu-accelerated-computer-vision-on-databricks.md) - Using GPU-optimized AI Runtime on Databricks for computer vision tasks including object detection and image classification.
* [GPU-Accelerated Recommendation Systems on Databricks](/concepts/gpu-accelerated-recommendation-systems-on-databricks.md) - The practice of building and training deep learning based recommendation models using GPU acceleration within the Databricks environment on AWS.
* [GPU-Accelerated XGBoost on Databricks](/concepts/gpu-accelerated-xgboost-training.md) - Enabling GPU training for XGBoost models on Databricks clusters by setting use_gpu=True, with compute capability constraints.
* [GPU-Accelerated XGBoost Training](/concepts/gpu-accelerated-xgboost-training.md) - Using GPU clusters for XGBoost training on Databricks by setting use_gpu=True in sparkdl.xgboost estimators.
* [Gradient Accumulation Fusion](/concepts/gradient-accumulation-fusion.md) - A DeepSpeed feature that fuses gradient accumulation steps to improve training efficiency and throughput
* [Gradient Boosted Trees Regression with MLlib](/concepts/gradient-boosted-trees-regression-with-mllib.md) - Using MLlib pipelines to perform regression with gradient boosted trees, demonstrated with bike rental count prediction.
* [Gradient Checkpointing for Memory-Efficient Training](/concepts/gradient-checkpointing-for-memory-efficient-training.md) - A memory optimization technique that trades computation for memory by not storing intermediate activations during forward pass, instead recomputing them during backward pass, enabling larger batch sizes on limited GPU memory.
* [Gradient Checkpointing with Liger Kernels in Distributed Training](/concepts/gradient-checkpointing-with-liger-kernels-in-distributed-training.md) - A memory optimization technique that trades computation for memory by recomputing intermediate activations during backpropagation, combined with Liger kernel optimizations to fit larger models on GPU.
* [GRANT policies (ABAC Beta)](/concepts/grant-policies-beta.md) - A beta feature in Unity Catalog ABAC supporting dynamic privilege grants through GRANT policies, currently scoped to EXECUTE on models.
* [GRANT Policies (Beta)](/concepts/grant-policies-beta.md) - ABAC policy type for dynamic privilege grants, currently scoped to EXECUTE permission on models in Unity Catalog
* [GRANT policies in Unity Catalog](/concepts/grant-policies-in-unity-catalog.md) - ABAC policy type (Beta) for dynamic privilege grants, currently scoped to EXECUTE on models.
* [GRANT Policies in Unity Catalog (Beta)](/concepts/grant-policies-in-unity-catalog.md) - A type of ABAC policy supporting dynamic privilege grants, currently scoped to EXECUTE on models in Unity Catalog.
* [GRANT Policies vs Direct Grants](/concepts/grant-policies-vs-direct-grants.md) - The interaction model between dynamic tag-based GRANT policies and explicit direct grants, where effective privileges are the union of both mechanisms.
* [GRANT Policy Audit Logging](/concepts/grant-policy-audit-logging.md) - GRANT policy create, alter, and drop operations are logged under the same audit actions (createPolicy, deletePolicy, getPolicy, listPolicies) as row filter and column mask policies.
* [GRANT Policy Best Practices](/concepts/grant-policy-best-practices.md) - Recommendations for designing maintainable GRANT policies: use groups not individuals, attach at smallest scope, use tag inheritance with EXCEPT for exceptions, avoid mixing with direct grants.
* [GRANT Policy Beta Limitations](/concepts/grant-policy-beta-limitations.md) - Current Beta constraints: only EXECUTE on models is supported, policies attach at catalog/schema level only, SHOW GRANTS does not reflect policy-granted privileges, and Delta Sharing is incompatible.
* [GRANT Policy Effective Privilege Union Model](/concepts/effective-privileges-union-model.md) - The effective privileges on a securable object are the union of direct grants and any applicable GRANT policies, meaning a more selective policy does not override existing direct grants.
* [GRANT Policy Interaction with Direct Grants](/concepts/grant-policy-interaction-with-direct-grants.md) - Effective privileges are the union of direct grants and GRANT policies; a principal holds EXECUTE if either mechanism grants it, making mixing the two approaches potentially confusing.
* [GRANT Policy Limitations and Beta Scope](/concepts/grant-policy-limitations-and-beta-scope.md) - Current Beta limitations: only EXECUTE privilege on models is supported, policies can only be attached at catalog/schema level (not model), SHOW GRANTS doesn't reflect policy-granted privileges, and Delta Sharing is not compatible.
* [GRANT Policy Management and Auditing](/concepts/grant-policy-management-and-auditing.md) - SQL commands (CREATE POLICY, ALTER POLICY, SHOW POLICIES, DESCRIBE POLICY, DROP POLICY), Catalog Explorer UI operations, and audit logging for managing ABAC GRANT policies in Unity Catalog.
* [GRANT Policy Management Operations](/concepts/grant-policy-management-operations.md) - SQL and UI operations for creating, editing, deleting, showing, and describing GRANT policies, including the USE CATALOG/SCHEMA prerequisites and SHOW EFFECTIVE POLICIES for inherited policies.
* [GRANT Policy Management via Catalog Explorer and SQL](/concepts/grant-policy-management-via-catalog-explorer-and-sql.md) - How to create, edit, delete, list, and describe GRANT policies using the Catalog Explorer UI, SQL statements (CREATE POLICY, DESCRIBE POLICY, SHOW POLICIES), and the Databricks Python SDK.
* [GRANT Policy Quotas and Limitations (Beta)](/concepts/grant-policy-quotas-and-limitations-beta.md) - The current Beta restrictions on GRANT policies including only EXECUTE on models, no direct model attachment, no SHOW GRANTS visibility, and separate quotas.
* [GRANT Policy SQL Syntax](/concepts/grant-policy-sql-syntax.md) - SQL syntax for creating, describing, showing, and deleting GRANT policies, including CREATE POLICY, DESCRIBE POLICY, SHOW POLICIES, and SHOW EFFECTIVE POLICIES.
* [GRANT Policy Syntax and Management](/concepts/grant-policy-syntax-and-management.md) - SQL syntax (CREATE POLICY, DESCRIBE POLICY, SHOW POLICIES, SHOW EFFECTIVE POLICIES, DROP POLICY) and Catalog Explorer UI for creating, editing, deleting, and inspecting ABAC GRANT policies.
* [GRANT Policy Syntax and Structure](/concepts/grant-policy-syntax-and-structure.md) - The CREATE POLICY SQL syntax for defining GRANT policies, including TO/EXCEPT principals, GRANT EXECUTE FOR MODELS, and WHEN conditions using functions like has_tag_value.
* [GRANT Policy vs Direct Grant](/concepts/grant-policy-vs-direct-grant.md) - Key distinction between dynamic tag-based GRANT policies that match by governed tags versus static direct GRANT statements that identify objects by their three-level namespace.
* [GRANT Policy vs Direct Grants](/concepts/grant-policy-vs-direct-grant.md) - Comparison between dynamic attribute-based GRANT policies that match on tags versus traditional explicit GRANT statements that name specific objects in their three-level namespace.
* [Grant Share to SAP BDC](/concepts/grant-share-to-sap-bdc.md) - The workflow of granting an SAP BDC recipient access to selected shares through the Databricks OpenSharing interface.
* [Granting Data Shares to SAP BDC Recipients](/concepts/granting-data-shares-to-sap-bdc-recipients.md) - The step-by-step process of granting an SAP BDC recipient access to specific OpenSharing shares in Databricks
* [Granting Share Access to Recipients](/concepts/granting-share-access-to-recipients.md) - The process of adding a data recipient to an OpenSharing share so they can access the shared data assets.
* [Graph and Network Analysis](/concepts/graph-and-network-analysis.md) - The practice of modeling and analyzing systems using graphs (vertices connected by edges) to understand relationships, structures, and patterns in domains such as social networks, transportation, fraud detection, and recommendations.
* [Graph and Network Analysis on Databricks](/concepts/graph-and-network-analysis-on-databricks.md) - Overview of Databricks capabilities for graph processing, combining Databricks Runtime ML with NetworkX, GraphFrames, and external tools
* [Graph Processing on Databricks](/concepts/graph-processing-on-databricks.md) - Databricks provides capabilities for graph analysis at any scale: NetworkX for single-node processing of small networks, GraphFrames for distributed processing of large networks, and the ability to install additional open-source or third-party tools.
* [Graph Properties (Centrality, Shortest Path, Diameter, Density)](/concepts/graph-properties-centrality-shortest-path-diameter-density.md) - Key analytical metrics for graphs: shortest path (minimum distance between nodes), centrality (node importance measures like degree centrality and betweenness centrality), diameter (maximum shortest path across the network), and density (actual edges vs possible edges).
* [Graph Properties: Density, Diameter, and Degree Distribution](/concepts/graph-properties-density-diameter-and-degree-distribution.md) - Fundamental structural properties of networks including density, diameter, and degree distribution used to characterize graph topology
* [GraphFrames](/concepts/graphframes.md) - Distributed graph processing library for Apache Spark used on Databricks for large-scale network analysis
* [Groq SDK Integration with MLflow](/concepts/groq-sdk-integration-with-mlflow.md) - Integration enabling MLflow to automatically trace Groq chat completion requests
* [Ground Truth Comparison Strategies](/concepts/ground-truth-comparison-strategies.md) - Two approaches for providing ground truth to the Correctness judge: expected_facts (list of atomic facts) for flexible matching, and expected_response (full expected answer) for stricter comparison.
* [Ground Truth Evaluation Strategies](/concepts/ground-truth-evaluation-strategies.md) - Two approaches for evaluating LLM response correctness: expected_facts allows flexible fact-level checking, while expected_response requires more exact response matching.
* [Ground Truth in LLM Evaluation](/concepts/ground-truth-in-llm-evaluation.md) - A distinguishing feature of LLM judges where some judges require ground truth (expectations) to evaluate correctness or sufficiency, while others operate without it by evaluating based on inputs and outputs alone.
* [Ground truth requirement in LLM evaluation](/concepts/ground-truth-requirement-in-llm-evaluation.md) - Design pattern where some LLM judges (Correctness, RetrievalSufficiency, ToolCallCorrectness) require ground truth expectations as input, while others (RelevanceToQuery, Safety, RetrievalGroundedness) do not.
* [Ground Truth Requirements in LLM Evaluation](/concepts/ground-truth-requirements-in-llm-evaluation.md) - A design distinction among built-in judges where some evaluators require ground truth labels (e.g., Correctness, RetrievalSufficiency) and others evaluate purely based on inputs and outputs.
* [Grounded and Topical AI Applications](/concepts/grounded-and-topical-ai-applications.md) - A mitigation requirement that AI chat and conversational agents must be grounded or topical, meaning users cannot freely query the model for unrestricted outputs.
* [Grouping Runs by Parameter](/concepts/grouping-runs-by-parameter.md) - Feature to group MLflow runs by one or more parameter values for comparative analysis in chart views.
* [gRPC over HTTP/2 in Databricks Connect](/concepts/grpc-over-http2-in-databricks-connect.md) - Databricks Connect communicates with Databricks clusters via gRPC over HTTP/2, which is the underlying transport protocol for Spark Connect.
* [gRPC Protocol in Databricks Connect](/concepts/grpc-protocol-in-databricks-connect.md) - The underlying communication protocol used by Spark Connect that transmits Spark unresolved logical plans and Apache Arrow data over gRPC between the client and the remote Databricks cluster.
* [Guardrails AI Framework](/concepts/guardrails-ai-framework.md) - A community-driven framework for validating LLM outputs using a hub of validators for safety, PII detection, content quality, and more.
* [Guardrails AI scorer configuration](/concepts/guardrails-ai-scorer-configuration.md) - Guardrails AI scorers accept validator-specific parameters as keyword arguments to the constructor for customization
* [Guardrails AI Scorers Integration](/concepts/guardrails-ai-scorers-integration.md) - MLflow integration with Guardrails AI for rule-based output validation without LLM calls such as toxicity detection, PII scanning, and jailbreak detection
* [Guideline Authoring Best Practices](/concepts/guideline-authoring-best-practices.md) - Effective natural-language guidelines for LLM judges should be specific and measurable, use clear pass/fail conditions, explicitly reference context variables, and structure complex requirements with bullet points.
* [Guideline Writing Best Practices](/concepts/guideline-writing-best-practices.md) - Principles for authoring effective natural language evaluation criteria: be specific and measurable, use clear pass/fail conditions, reference context explicitly, and structure complex requirements.
* [Guidelines and Tool Call Judges](/concepts/guidelines-and-tool-call-judges.md) - A category of built-in LLM judges that evaluate responses against natural language criteria (Guidelines, ExpectationsGuidelines) or assess tool call quality (ToolCallCorrectness, ToolCallEfficiency).
* [Guidelines Judge Request/Response Parsing](/concepts/guidelines-judge-requestresponse-parsing.md) - The automatic extraction rules by which the Guidelines judge parses inputs and outputs into request and response context variables from OpenAI-format traces or arbitrary dicts.
* [Guidelines LLM Judge](/concepts/guidelines-llm-judge.md) - A pass/fail LLM-based evaluator that uses natural language criteria to assess GenAI outputs for compliance, tone, accuracy, and requirements.
* [Guidelines LLM Judges](/concepts/guidelines-llm-judges.md) - Pass/fail natural language criteria judges that evaluate whether inputs and outputs comply with specified guidelines, configurable with Databricks-hosted or custom model endpoints.
* [Guidelines Scorer](/concepts/guidelines-scorer.md) - An MLflow scorer that evaluates whether LLM responses adhere to user-defined natural language guidelines (e.g., language, tone, safety constraints).
* [Guidelines Writing Best Practices](/concepts/guideline-writing-best-practices.md) - Principles for writing effective guidelines: be specific and measurable, use clear pass/fail conditions, reference context explicitly, and structure complex requirements.
* [Guidelines-based LLM evaluation](/concepts/guidelines-based-llm-evaluation.md) - Evaluation approach using natural language criteria (via Guidelines and ExpectationsGuidelines judges) to assess whether responses meet specified requirements, with or without per-example expectations.
* [Guidelines() Judge (Global Guidelines)](/concepts/guidelines-judge-global-guidelines.md) - A built-in MLflow scorer that applies uniform natural-language guidelines across all evaluation rows or production traces, automatically extracting request/response data.
* [H100 GPU distributed training on Databricks](/concepts/h100-gpu-distributed-training-on-databricks.md) - Pattern for leveraging H100 GPUs in multi-node distributed training workloads on Databricks AI Runtime, supporting both FSDP and Ray Train backends.
* [H100 vs A10 GPU Selection Guidance](/concepts/h100-vs-a10-gpu-selection-guidance.md) - Guidance on when to use H100 GPUs over A10 GPUs: H100s are recommended for large model training requiring high throughput and large GPU memory due to larger FLOPS and HBM capacity.
* [Hadoop Configuration in Databricks Connect](/concepts/hadoop-configuration-in-databricks-connect.md) - How Hadoop configurations are managed in Databricks Connect, including the distinction between spark.conf.set (per-session) and sparkContext configurations (cluster-wide).
* [Hallucination Scorer](/concepts/hallucination-scorer.md) - An Arize Phoenix scorer that detects hallucinations in LLM outputs by comparing them against a provided context
* [Hallucination scorer (Phoenix)](/concepts/hallucination-scorer-phoenix.md) - An Arize Phoenix scorer used within MLflow GenAI evaluation that detects factual inaccuracies or fabricated information in LLM outputs relative to provided context.
* [HAS_INDEXES](/concepts/has_indexes.md) - A subclass of DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE error indicating the source table has indexes that must be dropped before cloning can succeed.
* [HAS_INDEXES (Delta Clone sub-error)](/concepts/has_indexes.md) - A specific DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE sub-error indicating the source table has indexes that must be dropped before cloning.
* [HAS_INDEXES error condition](/concepts/has_indexes.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE indicating the source table has indexes that must be dropped before a Delta clone operation can proceed.
* [HAS_INDEXES sub-error](/concepts/has_indexes.md) - A specific error under DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE indicating the source table has indexes that must be dropped before cloning into Delta.
* [Health Indicators (Catalog Explorer)](/concepts/health-indicators-catalog-explorer.md) - Visual status badges displayed on schema and table overview pages that summarize table health (healthy/unhealthy/error) for data consumers without requiring navigation to the monitoring UI.
* [Health Indicators (Databricks Anomaly Detection)](/concepts/health-indicators-databricks.md) - Visual status indicators shown in Catalog Explorer for each table within a monitored schema, summarizing table health for data consumers
* [Health Indicators (Databricks)](/concepts/health-indicators-databricks.md) - Visual status indicators shown in Catalog Explorer that summarize table health for data consumers, requiring SELECT or BROWSE permissions to view.
* [Heterogeneous Ray Clusters on Databricks](/concepts/heterogeneous-ray-clusters-on-databricks.md) - Ray on Spark clusters allow different configurations between head and worker nodes, but all worker nodes must share the same configuration; Databricks supports this via cluster policies with different driver and worker instance types.
* [High Throughput vs Low Latency LLM Use Cases](/concepts/high-throughput-vs-low-latency-llm-use-cases.md) - Classification of LLM application scenarios: high throughput for batch/non-user-facing tasks where latency can be sacrificed, and low latency for real-time interactive applications.
* [Historical Trace Backfilling](/concepts/historical-trace-backfilling.md) - The pattern of retrospectively applying evaluation scorers to previously collected trace data over a date range using the backfill_scorers API.
* [History Sharing and Time Travel](/concepts/history-sharing-and-time-travel.md) - Sharing the Delta transaction log history of a table to enable recipients to perform time travel queries, read with Spark Structured Streaming, and access Change Data Feed.
* [History Sharing for Shared Tables](/concepts/history-sharing-for-shared-tables.md) - A prerequisite feature on OpenSharing shares that enables metadata history, enabling Structured Streaming and change data feed capabilities on shared tables.
* [Hive Metastore Coexistence with Unity Catalog](/concepts/hive-metastore-coexistence-with-unity-catalog.md) - The per-workspace legacy Hive metastore can continue to be used alongside Unity Catalog in Databricks, appearing as a top-level catalog named `hive_metastore` in the three-level namespace.
* [Hive metastore deprecation in new workspaces](/concepts/hive-metastore-deprecation-in-new-workspaces.md) - The Hive metastore will not be available in new workspaces created after September 30, 2026; existing Hive tables and views must be upgraded to Unity Catalog.
* [Hive Metastore Fallback](/concepts/hive-metastore-fallback.md) - A mechanism that allows queries to fall back to legacy Hive metastore tables when they cannot be resolved in Unity Catalog, which is disabled when legacy access is turned off.
* [Hive Metastore Federation](/concepts/hive-metastore-federation.md) - A technique to federate an existing Hive metastore or AWS Glue catalog as a foreign catalog in Unity Catalog, enabling table upgrades without data movement.
* [Hive Metastore Federation to Unity Catalog](/concepts/hive-metastore-federation-to-unity-catalog.md) - Hive Metastore federation creates a foreign catalog in Unity Catalog that mirrors the Hive metastore, offering a gradual migration path as an alternative to upgrading all tables at once.
* [Hive Metastore Migration Prerequisites](/concepts/hive-metastore-migration-prerequisites.md) - The criteria that must be met before disabling legacy Hive metastore access, including completing table migration to Unity Catalog and upgrading all jobs to Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS or above.
* [Hive Metastore Object Ownership](/concepts/hive-metastore-object-ownership.md) - The ownership model where creators of objects become owners (or group membership confers ownership), with ability to transfer ownership via ALTER command.
* [Hive Metastore Privilege Model (Legacy)](/concepts/hive-metastore-privilege-model-legacy.md) - The legacy access control system for the built-in Hive metastore in Databricks workspaces, distinct from Unity Catalog's privilege model.
* [Hive Metastore Privilege Types](/concepts/hive-metastore-privilege-types.md) - The set of privileges (SELECT, CREATE, MODIFY, USAGE, READ_METADATA, CREATE_NAMED_FUNCTION, MODIFY_CLASSPATH, ALL PRIVILEGES) used with legacy Hive metastore access control.
* [Hive Metastore Privileges and Securable Objects](/concepts/hive-metastore-privileges-and-securable-objects.md) - The permission model and set of securable objects (e.g., tables, databases, ANY FILE) used in the legacy Hive metastore table access control system.
* [Hive Metastore Resource Limits and Concurrency](/concepts/hive-metastore-resource-limits-and-concurrency.md) - The Databricks-hosted legacy Hive metastore has resource limits on concurrent connections and connections per hour; exceeding these can cause metastore connection errors, and mitigation includes migration to Unity Catalog and smoothing workload concurrency.
* [Hive Metastore Securable Objects](/concepts/hive-metastore-securable-objects.md) - The hierarchical object model (CATALOG → SCHEMA → TABLE/VIEW/FUNCTION, plus ANONYMOUS FUNCTION and ANY FILE) to which privileges can be granted in the legacy Hive metastore.
* [Hive Metastore Securable Objects and Privileges](/concepts/hive-metastore-securable-objects-and-privileges.md) - The permission model defining which Hive metastore objects (tables, databases, views, etc.) can be secured and what privileges can be granted on them using table access control.
* [Hive Metastore Table Access Control](/concepts/hive-metastore-table-access-control.md) - A Databricks security feature that restricts access to Hive metastore tables and views on a cluster based on administrator-defined privileges.
* [Hive Metastore Table Access Control (Legacy)](/concepts/hive-metastore-table-access-control-legacy.md) - A legacy Databricks data governance model that enables programmatic grant and revoke of permissions on Hive metastore objects via Python and SQL, per cluster.
* [Hive Metastore Table Upgrade Methods](/concepts/hive-metastore-table-upgrade-methods.md) - Techniques for upgrading Hive metastore tables to Unity Catalog, including ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED/EXTERNAL, SYNC, and CREATE TABLE AS SELECT.
* [Hive Metastore to Unity Catalog Migration](/concepts/hive-metastore-to-unity-catalog-migration.md) - Strategies for upgrading legacy Hive metastore managed and external Parquet tables to Unity Catalog Delta Lake tables using CONVERT TO DELTA or CTAS.
* [Hive Metastore to Unity Catalog Table Migration](/concepts/hive-metastore-to-unity-catalog-migration.md) - Strategies for converting legacy Hive metastore managed and external Parquet tables to Unity Catalog Delta Lake tables using CTAS or upgrade wizard
* [Hive-to-Unity-Catalog Impact Considerations](/concepts/hive-to-unity-catalog-impact-considerations.md) - Key behavioral changes when migrating: Unity Catalog manages partitions differently (no direct partition manipulation commands), and table history is not migrated with CLONE operations.
* [Holiday Region Covariates](/concepts/holiday-region-covariates.md) - A configurable option in Databricks forecasting that adds holiday region data as covariates (external features) during model training to improve forecast accuracy.
* [Horovod](/concepts/horovod.md) - A distributed deep learning framework that can be used on Databricks for scaling PyTorch and other ML models across multiple nodes.
* [Horovod Custom Installation on Databricks](/concepts/horovod-custom-installation-on-databricks.md) - Process to install a different version of Horovod on Databricks Runtime ML by uninstalling, recompiling from source, and reinstalling as a wheel.
* [Horovod Deprecation on Databricks](/concepts/horovod-deprecation-on-databricks.md) - Horovod and HorovodRunner are deprecated in Databricks Runtime ML after version 15.4 LTS ML, with TorchDistributor and tf.distribute.Strategy recommended as replacements.
* [Horovod Distributed Training Framework](/concepts/horovod.md) - Horovod is a distributed deep learning training framework supporting TensorFlow, Keras, and PyTorch, now deprecated on Databricks.
* [Horovod Extension Build Error Troubleshooting](/concepts/horovod-extension-build-error-troubleshooting.md) - Common ImportError for horovod.{torch|tensorflow} caused by missing required libraries at install time, resolved by reinstalling Horovod after the required framework.
* [Horovod for Distributed Deep Learning on Databricks](/concepts/horovod-for-distributed-deep-learning-on-databricks.md) - The integration of Uber's Horovod distributed training framework within the Databricks environment for scaling deep learning workloads
* [Horovod ImportError Troubleshooting](/concepts/horovod-importerror-troubleshooting.md) - A common error where importing horovod.torch or horovod.tensorflow fails because the extension was not built during installation, typically due to missing required libraries at compile time.
* [Horovod Installation and Compilation Constraints](/concepts/horovod-installation-and-compilation-constraints.md) - Requirement to reinstall Horovod against the same compiler and CUDA version as the deep learning library (TensorFlow/PyTorch) after upgrading or downgrading those libraries.
* [Horovod Installation on Databricks](/concepts/horovod-installation-on-databricks.md) - The process of upgrading or downgrading Horovod on Databricks Runtime ML clusters, requiring manual uninstallation, recompilation with CUDA libraries, and wheel installation.
* [Horovod Installation with Deep Learning Libraries](/concepts/horovod-installation-with-deep-learning-libraries.md) - Horovod must be reinstalled after upgrading or downgrading TensorFlow, Keras, or PyTorch, using the same compiler that the deep learning library was built with, and specifying GPU allreduce backend (NCCL).
* [Horovod Network Interface Configuration](/concepts/horovod-network-interface-configuration.md) - Configuration of OMPI_MCA_btl_tcp_if_include and NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME environment variables to resolve Open MPI TCP connection issues in multi-node clusters.
* [Horovod on Spark](/concepts/horovod-on-spark.md) - A distributed deep learning package providing an estimator API for training Keras and PyTorch models on Apache Spark clusters.
* [Horovod Spark Estimator API with Keras](/concepts/horovod-spark-estimator-api-with-keras.md) - Using the horovod.spark estimator API to run distributed deep learning training with Keras models on Databricks.
* [Horovod Spark Estimator API with PyTorch](/concepts/horovod-spark-estimator-api-with-pytorch.md) - Using the horovod.spark estimator API to run distributed deep learning training with PyTorch models on Databricks.
* [Horovod Timeline](/concepts/horovod-timeline.md) - A performance tracing tool for Horovod that records activity timelines, accessible via Chrome tracing, but with significant performance overhead (~40% throughput reduction).
* [horovod.spark](/concepts/horovodspark.md) - A Spark integration package for Horovod that provides an estimator API for Spark ML pipelines using Keras or PyTorch models.
* [horovod.spark Estimator API](/concepts/horovodspark.md) - Spark ML pipeline estimator API for using Horovod with Keras and PyTorch models in Spark pipelines.
* [horovod.spark Package](/concepts/horovodspark-package.md) - A Spark estimator API package for distributed deep learning training with Horovod, supporting Keras and PyTorch on Databricks.
* [HorovodRunner](/concepts/horovodrunner.md) - A Databricks API for distributed deep learning using Horovod that allows adapting single-node PyTorch code to distributed training with minimal changes.
* [HorovodRunner Deprecation](/concepts/horovodrunner-deprecation.md) - Horovod and HorovodRunner are deprecated in Databricks ML runtime 15.4 LTS and later, with recommended replacements being TorchDistributor for PyTorch and tf.distribute.Strategy for TensorFlow.
* [HorovodRunner deprecation on Databricks](/concepts/horovodrunner-deprecation.md) - Horovod and HorovodRunner are deprecated on Databricks as of ML releases after 15.4 LTS; users must migrate to alternative distributed training APIs.
* [HorovodRunner development workflow](/concepts/horovodrunner-development-workflow.md) - The recommended workflow for developing distributed deep learning models using HorovodRunner on Databricks.
* [HorovodRunner Pickling and Import Constraints](/concepts/horovodrunner-pickling-and-import-constraints.md) - Requirement that all import statements must be included inside the Horovod training method (and any called functions) to avoid pickling errors during distribution to Spark executors.
* [HorovodRunner Pickling and Import Requirements](/concepts/horovodrunner-pickling-and-import-requirements.md) - HorovodRunner pickles the training method on the driver and distributes it to Spark workers, requiring all import statements to be included inside the training method to avoid pickling errors.
* [Hosted Third-Party Foundation Model Catalog](/concepts/hosted-third-party-foundation-model-catalog.md) - A curated catalog of state-of-the-art foundation models from OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, Meta, and Alibaba Cloud, hosted within the Databricks security perimeter and accessible via named endpoints.
* [HTTP vs Job Registry Webhooks](/concepts/http-vs-job-registry-webhooks.md) - Two types of webhooks based on trigger targets: HTTP endpoints (send HTTP requests to a URL) and job triggers (run a Databricks job in the same or different workspace).
* [Hub-and-Spoke Medallion Design](/concepts/hub-and-spoke-medallion-design.md) - An enterprise deployment pattern that combines medallion architecture with a centralized data hub and domain-specific spokes, enabling shared organization-wide assets alongside domain-curated data products.
* [Hugging Face Dataset Caching on Databricks](/concepts/hugging-face-dataset-caching-on-databricks.md) - Strategies for persisting Hugging Face dataset caches across cluster lifecycles using Unity Catalog volumes and HF_DATASETS_CACHE environment variable
* [Hugging Face Datasets on Databricks](/concepts/hugging-face-datasets-on-databricks.md) - Using the Hugging Face Datasets library within Databricks environments to load, prepare, and manage data for fine-tuning LLMs
* [Hugging Face Model Caching on Databricks](/concepts/hugging-face-model-caching-on-databricks.md) - Caching Hugging Face models in DBFS or mount points to reduce model load times and ingress costs across clusters by setting the TRANSFORMERS_CACHE environment variable
* [Hugging Face Trainer](/concepts/hugging-face-trainer.md) - The Trainer utility from Hugging Face transformers library for fine-tuning models, including metrics, data collators, and training configuration.
* [Hugging Face Trainer Utility on Databricks](/concepts/hugging-face-trainer-utility-on-databricks.md) - How to use the Hugging Face Transformers Trainer class for fine-tuning on single-GPU Databricks clusters
* [Hugging Face Transformers Pipelines for Inference](/concepts/hugging-face-transformers-pipelines-for-inference.md) - Using the Hugging Face pipelines class to run pre-trained NLP models for tasks like translation and named-entity recognition on Databricks
* [Hugging Face Transformers Trainer](/concepts/hugging-face-transformers-trainer.md) - A high-level training utility from the Hugging Face transformers library that orchestrates model fine-tuning, evaluation, and checkpointing with minimal boilerplate.
* [Human Feedback Collection for Judge Alignment](/concepts/human-feedback-collection-for-judge-alignment.md) - Best practices and methods for collecting domain expert feedback on judge assessments, including UI review and programmatic feedback, with guidance on diverse reviewers and balanced examples.
* [Human Feedback Collection in MLflow](/concepts/human-feedback-collection-in-mlflow.md) - Mechanism to collect end-user feedback (thumbs up/down) on GenAI app outputs using mlflow.log_feedback with assessment metadata.
* [Human Feedback Collection with mlflow.log_feedback()](/concepts/human-feedback-collection-with-mlflowlog_feedback.md) - Programmatic API for collecting end-user feedback (e.g., thumbs up/down) on GenAI app responses, associated with trace IDs and including rationale and source provenance.
* [Human Feedback for LLM Judge Alignment](/concepts/human-feedback-for-llm-judge-alignment.md) - Human feedback collected via MLflow can be used to create datasets for LLM judges and scorers, keeping automated evaluation systems aligned with human expert judgment and nuanced business requirements.
* [Human Feedback in LLM Monitoring](/concepts/human-feedback-in-llm-monitoring.md) - Incorporating human feedback loops into LLM application monitoring and evaluation, managed as data with near real-time streaming
* [Human Labeling: Sessions and Schemas](/concepts/labeling-sessions-and-schemas.md) - Labeling sessions organize traces for domain expert review, while labeling schemas define the structured questions and response types (ratings, free text) for consistent label collection.
* [Hybrid Deploy Code Approach](/concepts/hybrid-deploy-code-approach.md) - A variation of the deploy code pattern where code is deployed to staging, the model is trained on full production data in staging, and then the trained model artifact is deployed to production, saving training costs in production.
* [Hybrid Reasoning Models](/concepts/hybrid-reasoning-models.md) - A class of AI models (e.g., Claude Opus 4.x, Gemini 2.5/3 Pro) that offer both near-instant responses and extended/deep thinking modes depending on task complexity, blending fast inference with deliberate reasoning.
* [Hybrid Spark and Ray Workload Resource Optimization](/concepts/hybrid-spark-and-ray-workload-resource-optimization.md) - Best practices for running concurrent Spark and Ray workloads on Databricks, including reducing Spark executor memory to avoid OOM errors and enabling Ray-on-Spark autoscaling to dynamically share cluster resources.
* [Hybrid Tracing Patterns](/concepts/hybrid-tracing-patterns.md) - Combining mlflow.start_span() context managers, @mlflow.trace function decorators, and automatic tracing (autolog) for comprehensive application observability.
* [Hyperopt](/concepts/hyperopt.md) - A deprecated Python library for distributed hyperparameter tuning, not included in Databricks Runtime ML after 16.4 LTS ML.
* [Hyperopt Conditional Hyperparameter Spaces](/concepts/hyperopt-conditional-hyperparameter-spaces.md) - Hyperopt supports conditional search spaces where certain hyperparameters are only explored for specific model flavors or algorithms, enabling more targeted optimization.
* [Hyperopt Deprecation and Alternatives](/concepts/hyperopt-deprecation-and-migration.md) - The open-source Hyperopt library is no longer maintained and is removed from Databricks Runtime ML after 16.4 LTS. Databricks recommends Optuna for single-node or RayTune for distributed tuning.
* [Hyperopt deprecation and migration](/concepts/hyperopt-deprecation-and-migration.md) - Hyperopt is no longer maintained and is excluded from Databricks Runtime ML after 16.4 LTS; Databricks recommends Optuna or RayTune as replacements.
* [Hyperopt Deprecation and Migration on Databricks](/concepts/hyperopt-deprecation-and-migration.md) - Hyperopt is deprecated in Databricks Runtime for ML 16.4 LTS and later; recommended replacements are Optuna for single-node optimization and RayTune for distributed hyperparameter tuning.
* [Hyperopt deprecation in Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/hyperopt-deprecation-on-databricks.md) - Hyperopt is excluded from Databricks Runtime for Machine Learning starting from version 16.4 LTS ML, marking its phased removal from the Databricks ML ecosystem.
* [Hyperopt deprecation on Databricks](/concepts/hyperopt-deprecation-on-databricks.md) - Hyperopt is no longer maintained and is removed from Databricks Runtime for Machine Learning after version 16.4 LTS ML
* [Hyperopt Distributed Training Workflow](/concepts/hyperopt-distributed-training-workflow.md) - Guidance on using Hyperopt with distributed machine learning algorithms on Databricks, where trials run on the driver node so the algorithm itself can initiate distributed training.
* [Hyperopt fmin()](/concepts/hyperopt-fmin.md) - The core function in Hyperopt used to execute hyperparameter optimization runs by iteratively generating and evaluating trials.
* [Hyperopt fmin() Workflow](/concepts/hyperopt-fmin-workflow.md) - The standard Hyperopt optimization workflow: define an objective function, define a search space, select a search algorithm, and run fmin() to find optimal hyperparameters.
* [Hyperopt hp.choice() Index Return Behavior](/concepts/hyperopt-hpchoice-index-return-behavior.md) - When using hp.choice() in Hyperopt, the returned value is the index of the chosen option, not the option itself; hyperopt.space_eval() is needed to retrieve actual parameter values.
* [Hyperopt NaN Loss Handling](/concepts/hyperopt-nan-loss-handling.md) - A NaN loss from Hyperopt fmin() usually means the objective function returned NaN; it does not affect other trials and can be safely ignored or mitigated by adjusting the search space or objective function.
* [Hyperopt Search Algorithms](/concepts/hyperopt-search-algorithms.md) - Hyperopt provides two main search algorithms: TPE (Tree of Parzen Estimators) for adaptive Bayesian optimization and random search for non-adaptive sampling over the hyperparameter space.
* [Hyperopt SparkTrials MLflow integration](/concepts/hyperopt-sparktrials-mlflow-integration.md) - SparkTrials logs tuning results as nested MLflow runs, with fmin() calls as parent runs and individual trials as child runs.
* [Hyperopt Tree of Parzen Estimators (TPE) Algorithm](/concepts/hyperopt-tree-of-parzen-estimators-tpe-algorithm.md) - Bayesian hyperparameter optimization algorithm used by Hyperopt that is more efficient than grid search or random search.
* [Hyperopt with HorovodRunner on Databricks](/concepts/hyperopt-with-horovodrunner-on-databricks.md) - Integration of Hyperopt with HorovodRunner to tune distributed deep learning workloads on Databricks, leveraging Spark's barrier mode for stability.
* [Hyperopt with MLlib Distributed Training](/concepts/hyperopt-with-mllib-distributed-training.md) - Example pattern for using Hyperopt to tune hyperparameters of Apache Spark MLlib's distributed training algorithms using the Trials class.
* [Hyperopt-MLflow Integration](/concepts/hyperopt-mlflow-integration.md) - Automated MLflow tracking of Hyperopt tuning trials on Databricks, enabled by calling mlflow.start_run() before fmin() to log all trials as MLflow runs.
* [Hyperparameter Tuning](/concepts/hyperparameter-tuning.md) - The process of optimizing model hyperparameters using automated libraries like Optuna, Ray Tune, and Hyperopt to efficiently find optimal settings for ML models.
* [Hyperparameter tuning for model selection](/concepts/hyperparameter-tuning-for-model-selection.md) - The technique of using hyperparameter optimization frameworks like Hyperopt to compare different model types (e.g., different algorithm families) by tuning each with its own appropriate hyperparameter space to find the best overall model.
* [Hyperparameter Tuning of XGBoost using MLlib Cross-Validation](/concepts/hyperparameter-tuning-of-xgboost-using-mllib-cross-validation.md) - Using Spark MLlib's cross-validation framework to tune XGBoost model hyperparameters at scale
* [Hyperparameter tuning on Databricks](/concepts/hyperparameter-tuning-on-databricks.md) - Integration of Optuna hyperparameter optimization within the Databricks platform for scalable machine learning
* [IAM role for Unity Catalog storage](/concepts/iam-role-for-unity-catalog-storage.md) - An AWS IAM role that grants Unity Catalog access to an S3 bucket used for metastore-level managed storage, requiring a cross-account trust relationship with Databricks.
* [IAM Role Trust for Unity Catalog](/concepts/iam-role-trust-for-unity-catalog.md) - The cross-account IAM role and trust relationship that must be established in AWS to allow Unity Catalog to access an S3 bucket for managed storage.
* [IAM Trust Relationship for Serverless Compute](/concepts/trust-relationship-for-serverless-compute.md) - Requirement that the instance profile's IAM role must have a trust relationship configured for Databricks serverless compute, or the endpoint creation fails.
* [Iceberg Conversion Limitations](/concepts/iceberg-conversion-limitations.md) - Known restrictions when converting Apache Iceberg tables to Delta Lake, including unsupported features like partition evolution and metastore tables.
* [Iceberg data type restrictions in Delta tables](/concepts/iceberg-data-type-restrictions-in-delta-tables.md) - The constraint that IcebergWriterCompatV rejects Delta schemas containing data types not supported by Apache Iceberg
* [Iceberg Metadata Versioning in Delta Uniform](/concepts/iceberg-metadata-versioning-in-delta-uniform.md) - The requirement that Iceberg metadata locations used to refresh a Delta Uniform table must have monotonically increasing version numbers, and UUID consistency must be maintained.
* [Iceberg REST Catalog (IRC) Protocol](/concepts/iceberg-rest-catalog-irc-protocol.md) - The open protocol that allows clients like Spark and Snowflake to read Iceberg tables from a catalog endpoint, used here to access shared data from Databricks OpenSharing.
* [Iceberg REST Catalog Integration for OpenSharing](/concepts/iceberg-rest-catalog-integration-for-opensharing.md) - A method to access OpenSharing data using external Iceberg clients (Snowflake, Trino, Flink, Spark) via the Apache Iceberg REST Catalog API, using vended credentials.
* [Iceberg Table Conversion Limitations](/concepts/iceberg-conversion-limitations.md) - Restrictions and unsupported scenarios when converting Iceberg tables, including partition evolution, metastore tables, and truncated column type limitations.
* [Iceberg to Delta Conversion](/concepts/iceberg-to-delta-conversion.md) - Converting Apache Iceberg tables whose underlying file format is Parquet to Delta Lake, using the Iceberg table's native file manifest, schema and partitioning information.
* [Iceberg to Delta conversion limitations](/concepts/iceberg-conversion-limitations.md) - Technical constraints when converting Iceberg tables including partition evolution and truncated column type restrictions
* [Iceberg to Delta Lake Conversion](/concepts/iceberg-to-delta-lake-conversion.md) - Conversion of Apache Iceberg tables to Delta Lake using CONVERT TO DELTA, with specific version and feature limitations.
* [Iceberg to Delta Lake Conversion Limitations](/concepts/iceberg-conversion-limitations.md) - Constraints and unsupported scenarios when converting Apache Iceberg tables to Delta Lake, including partition evolution, metastore tables, and truncated column types.
* [Iceberg unsupported data types and table properties](/concepts/iceberg-unsupported-data-types-and-table-properties.md) - IcebergWriterCompatV restricts which data types and Iceberg table properties are allowed in the Delta table schema and configuration
* [ICEBERG_MISSING_PARTITION_SPECS](/concepts/iceberg_missing_partition_specs.md) - A subclass of DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE error indicating the source Apache Iceberg table lacks partition specifications, making it incompatible for cloning.
* [ICEBERG_MISSING_PARTITION_SPECS (Delta Clone sub-error)](/concepts/iceberg_missing_partition_specs.md) - A specific DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE sub-error indicating the source Iceberg table lacks partition specs.
* [ICEBERG_MISSING_PARTITION_SPECS error condition](/concepts/iceberg_missing_partition_specs.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE raised when cloning an Apache Iceberg table that has no partition specs defined.
* [ICEBERG_MISSING_PARTITION_SPECS sub-error](/concepts/iceberg_missing_partition_specs.md) - A specific error under DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE indicating the source Apache Iceberg table has no partition specs, making it incompatible for Delta cloning.
* [ICEBERG_UNDERGONE_PARTITION_EVOLUTION](/concepts/iceberg_undergone_partition_evolution.md) - A subclass of DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE error indicating the source Apache Iceberg table has undergone partition evolution, making it incompatible for cloning.
* [ICEBERG_UNDERGONE_PARTITION_EVOLUTION (Delta Clone sub-error)](/concepts/iceberg_undergone_partition_evolution.md) - A specific DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE sub-error indicating the source Iceberg table has undergone partition evolution, which is incompatible with cloning.
* [ICEBERG_UNDERGONE_PARTITION_EVOLUTION error condition](/concepts/iceberg_undergone_partition_evolution.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE raised when cloning an Apache Iceberg table that has undergone partition evolution (changed partitioning over time).
* [ICEBERG_UNDERGONE_PARTITION_EVOLUTION sub-error](/concepts/iceberg_undergone_partition_evolution.md) - A specific error under DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE indicating the source Apache Iceberg table has undergone partition evolution, which is unsupported for Delta cloning.
* [Iceberg-Delta schema and type compatibility constraints](/concepts/iceberg-delta-schema-and-type-compatibility-constraints.md) - IcebergCompatV imposes restrictions on schema changes, including unsupported data types for columns and partition columns, incompatibility with type widening, and a requirement that partition specs cannot be renamed during REPLACE TABLE operations.
* [IcebergCompat Version Mutual Exclusivity](/concepts/icebergcompat-version-mutual-exclusivity.md) - Only one version of IcebergCompat can be enabled on a Delta table at a time; other versions must be explicitly disabled before enabling a new one.
* [IcebergCompatV](/concepts/icebergcompatv.md) - A Delta Lake table setting in Databricks that enables compatibility with Apache Iceberg readers, subject to version-specific validation rules.
* [IcebergCompatV (Iceberg Compatibility Version)](/concepts/icebergcompatv.md) - A Delta Lake table setting that enforces compatibility with Apache Iceberg format, with version constraints between 1 and a maximum supported version.
* [IcebergCompatV managed-table requirement](/concepts/delta-uniform-managed-table-requirement.md) - The IcebergCompatV feature can only be enabled on managed tables in Databricks, not on external/unmanaged tables.
* [IcebergCompatV versioning](/concepts/icebergcompatv-versioning.md) - Delta Lake table property controlling Uniform Iceberg compatibility level (versions 1 to maxVersion), where only one version can be enabled at a time and upgrading requires REORG TABLE.
* [IcebergCompatV versioning in Delta Lake](/concepts/icebergcompatv-versioning.md) - A versioned compatibility feature in Databricks Delta Lake that enforces Apache Iceberg compatibility rules; only one version can be enabled at a time and versions range from 1 to a configurable maxVersion.
* [IcebergCompatV1](/concepts/icebergcompatv.md) - A Delta Lake table compatibility feature that enforces Apache Iceberg compatibility requirements
* [IcebergCompatV1 Incompatible Table Features](/concepts/icebergcompatv1-incompatible-table-features.md) - Some Delta table features cannot coexist with IcebergCompatV1 and will cause a validation failure if present.
* [IcebergCompatV1 Partition Spec Constraint](/concepts/icebergcompatv1-partition-spec-constraint.md) - IcebergCompatV1 does not support replacing a partitioned table with a differently-named partition spec, mirroring a limitation in Iceberg-Spark 1.1.0.
* [IcebergCompatV1 Required Table Features](/concepts/icebergcompatv1-required-table-features.md) - Certain Delta table features must be enabled and cannot be dropped while IcebergCompatV1 is active; disabling the compatibility mode is required first.
* [IcebergCompatV1 Required Table Properties](/concepts/icebergcompatv1-required-table-properties.md) - The requirement that certain table properties must be set to specific values for IcebergCompatV1 to be valid, and violations raise the WRONG_REQUIRED_TABLE_PROPERTY sub-error.
* [IcebergCompatV1 Schema Restrictions](/concepts/icebergcompatv1-schema-restrictions.md) - The constraint that IcebergCompatV1 does not support tables with MapType, ArrayType, or NullType columns in their schema, due to limitations in the Iceberg format.
* [IcebergCompatV1 Unsupported Data Types](/concepts/icebergcompatv1-unsupported-data-types.md) - Schemas containing MapType, ArrayType, or NullType are rejected under IcebergCompatV1 because Iceberg does not support these types.
* [IcebergCompatV2](/concepts/icebergcompatv2.md) - A Delta Lake write protocol feature that enables Iceberg read compatibility on Delta Lake tables, requiring column mapping and Databricks Runtime 14.3 LTS or above.
* [IcebergWriterCompatV](/concepts/icebergwritercompatv.md) - A versioned validation mechanism in Databricks Delta Lake that enforces compatibility constraints so Delta tables can be read by Apache Iceberg writers.
* [IcebergWriterCompatV versioning](/concepts/icebergwritercompatv.md) - A Databricks compatibility mechanism that enforces Delta table schemas and properties conform to Apache Iceberg writer expectations at a given version level
* [IDE Integration with Databricks Connect](/concepts/ide-integration-with-databricks-connect.md) - How to configure and use Databricks Connect with various development environments including JupyterLab, Classic Jupyter Notebook, Visual Studio Code, PyCharm, RStudio Desktop, IntelliJ, Eclipse, and PyDev.
* [Idempotency in data optimization](/concepts/idempotency-in-data-optimization.md) - The property that re-running an optimization on unchanged data produces no additional effect; bin-packing is idempotent while Z-ordering is not.
* [Idempotency of REORG TABLE](/concepts/idempotency-of-reorg-table.md) - The REORG TABLE command is idempotent, meaning running it twice on the same dataset has no effect on the second run.
* [Idempotent Data Loading](/concepts/idempotent-data-loading.md) - COPY INTO automatically skips files that have already been loaded, even if they were modified, ensuring safe retry and avoiding duplicate data.
* [Idempotent Data Loading (COPY INTO)](/concepts/idempotent-data-loading.md) - The property of COPY INTO that skips already-loaded files, making it safe to retry without duplicate data even if source files have been modified.
* [Idempotent Data Migration Pattern for UC Traces](/concepts/idempotent-data-migration-pattern-for-uc-traces.md) - Design pattern for migrating trace data between UC table formats where the migration is idempotent: already-migrated rows are skipped on rerun, making it resilient to partial failures like cluster timeouts
* [Idempotent file ingestion](/concepts/idempotent-file-ingestion.md) - The property that files already loaded via COPY INTO are skipped on subsequent runs, even if modified, enabling safe retries.
