* [Idempotent foreachBatch Writes](/concepts/idempotent-foreachbatch-writes.md) - Pattern using txnAppId and txnVersion to make writes to multiple sinks within foreachBatch idempotent, preventing duplicate writes on failure recovery
* [Idempotent Job Submission](/concepts/idempotent-job-submission.md) - Pattern using --idempotency-key to make job submission safely retryable, returning the existing run if the same key was used before.
* [Idempotent Streaming Writes with foreachBatch](/concepts/idempotent-streaming-writes-with-foreachbatch.md) - Using txnAppId and txnVersion to make foreachBatch writes idempotent when writing to Delta Lake from streams
* [Idempotent Submission Pattern](/concepts/idempotent-submission-pattern.md) - Using the --idempotency-key flag with air run to safely retry submissions, returning the existing run if the same key was already used instead of creating a duplicate.
* [Idempotent Trace Migration](/concepts/idempotent-trace-migration.md) - A migration mechanism that automatically resumes from where it left off if interrupted, skipping already-migrated rows so the same command can be safely rerun.
* [Idempotent Writes with foreachBatch and Transaction Identifiers](/concepts/idempotent-writes-with-foreachbatch-and-transaction-identifiers.md) - Using txnAppId and txnVersion options with foreachBatch to make multi-table writes idempotent, allowing safe retry after failures without duplicating data.
* [Idempotent writes with foreachBatch using txnAppId and txnVersion](/concepts/idempotent-writes-with-foreachbatch-using-txnappid-and-txnversion.md) - Pattern for idempotent multi-sink writes within foreachBatch using Delta Lake's transaction app ID and version to identify and ignore duplicate writes after failures.
* [Identity Federation for Unity Catalog](/concepts/identity-federation-for-unity-catalog.md) - The centralization of user management at the Databricks account level, required as a prerequisite for using UCX and enabling Unity Catalog adoption.
* [Identity Resolution for Allowlisted Artifacts](/concepts/identity-resolution-for-allowlisted-artifacts.md) - The rule by which the identity used for allowlisted libraries and init scripts is determined — the library installer's identity in standard mode and the assigned principal's identity in dedicated access mode.
* [Idle Endpoint Warm-up Delay](/concepts/idle-endpoint-warm-up-delay.md) - Timeout risk when a model serving endpoint scaled to 0 receives a request and must warm up before serving.
* [Idle Endpoint Warm-Up Timeout](/concepts/idle-endpoint-warm-up-timeout.md) - Timeouts that can occur when a scaled-to-zero serving endpoint receives a request and must warm up, potentially exceeding the client-side timeout.
* [Idle Endpoint Warm-Up Timeouts](/concepts/idle-endpoint-warm-up-timeout.md) - Timeouts that can occur when a Model Serving endpoint that has scaled to 0 receives a request and takes too long to warm up.
* [ignoreDeletes and ignoreChanges Legacy Options](/concepts/ignoredeletes-and-ignorechanges-legacy-options.md) - Legacy streaming source options for Delta Lake that handle delete/change operations differently than skipChangeCommits, with specific behaviors for file re-emission and duplicate handling.
* [Image classification with CNN on Databricks](/concepts/image-classification-with-cnn-on-databricks.md) - A tutorial notebook demonstrating how to train a 2-D convolutional neural network for image classification on serverless GPUs using Databricks AI Runtime.
* [Image Classification with CNN on Serverless GPU](/concepts/image-classification-with-cnn-on-databricks.md) - Training a 2-D convolutional neural network for image classification using serverless GPUs in Databricks.
* [Image Classification with CNNs on Databricks](/concepts/image-classification-with-cnn-on-databricks.md) - A tutorial demonstrating how to train a 2-D convolutional neural network for image classification on serverless GPUs using Databricks AI Runtime.
* [Image ETL Pipeline Pattern](/concepts/image-etl-pipeline-pattern.md) - A two-stage reference pipeline for image applications: first ETL images into a Delta table using Auto Loader, then perform distributed inference using pandas UDFs with DL models.
* [Image Input Safety Restrictions for AI Models](/concepts/image-input-safety-restrictions-for-ai-models.md) - A mitigation requirement that image inputs to AI models must be restricted to low risk and topical images to prevent misuse.
* [Image-to-Token Conversion for Foundation Models](/concepts/image-to-token-conversion-for-foundation-models.md) - The mechanism by which each image in a request to a Databricks-hosted foundation model is converted to token usage for billing and pricing purposes.
* [Imbalanced Dataset Handling](/concepts/imbalanced-dataset-handling.md) - AutoML's strategy for reducing class imbalance by downsampling majority classes and applying inverse class weights during training.
* [Imbalanced Dataset Handling in AutoML](/concepts/imbalanced-dataset-handling.md) - Techniques AutoML uses to address class imbalance in classification datasets, including downsampling majority classes and applying class weights.
* [Importing Databricks Dashboards from JSON](/concepts/importing-databricks-dashboards-from-json.md) - The ability to import Databricks dashboards from a JSON file definition, enabling sharing and reuse of dashboard templates like the MLflow system tables dashboard.
* [Importing MLflow Dashboards from JSON](/concepts/importing-mlflow-dashboards-from-json.md) - The workflow of downloading a pre-built dashboard JSON file and importing it into a Databricks workspace to quickly visualize MLflow system table data.
* [ImportNotebook](/concepts/importnotebook.md) - The databricks.automl.import_notebook method imports a notebook saved as an MLflow artifact.
* [ImportNotebookResult](/concepts/importnotebookresult.md) - Result object returned by import_notebook containing the path and URL of the imported notebook.
* [Imputation of Missing Values in AutoML](/concepts/imputation-of-missing-values-in-automl.md) - How AutoML handles null value imputation, including default behavior and custom imputation methods configurable via UI or API.
* [In-memory DataFrame creation and table persistence](/concepts/in-memory-dataframe-creation-and-table-persistence.md) - The workflow of creating a Spark DataFrame from in-memory Scala data, defining a schema with StructType, saving it as a managed table, and querying it on the remote cluster.
* [In-memory Spark to Ray Data Transfer](/concepts/in-memory-spark-to-ray-data-transfer.md) - Using ray.data.from_spark() to efficiently transfer Spark DataFrames to Ray datasets without writing to disk, available on Databricks Runtime ML 15.0+.
* [In-memory Spark-to-Ray Data Transfer on Databricks](/concepts/in-memory-spark-to-ray-data-transfer.md) - Using ray.data.from_spark() to transfer Spark DataFrames to Ray datasets in-memory without writing to disk, available on Databricks Runtime ML 15.0+
* [In-place Table Conversion](/concepts/in-place-table-conversion.md) - The approach of converting Parquet or Iceberg tables to Delta Lake format without copying or rewriting data files, only generating a new transaction log.
* [In-place Table Format Conversion](/concepts/in-place-table-conversion.md) - The process of converting a table from one format (Parquet or Iceberg) to Delta Lake without copying or rewriting the underlying data files, only generating transaction metadata.
* [In-Product Access Request Surfaces](/concepts/in-product-access-request-surfaces.md) - Multiple Databricks surfaces (Catalog Explorer, SQL editor, notebooks, AI/BI dashboards, Genie Spaces) where users can request privileges on Unity Catalog objects.
* [Incident Management (Data Quality)](/concepts/incident-management-data-quality.md) - Workflow for handling detected data quality incidents including assigning ownership, marking false positives, and reviewing recently auto-resolved incidents.
* [Incident Management for Data Quality](/concepts/incident-management-for-data-quality.md) - Workflow for reviewing and managing unhealthy table incidents, including assigning ownership to indicate active investigation and marking incidents as false positives (not an issue), with assignments and dismissals persisting for 7 days.
* [INCOMPATIBLE_TABLE_FEATURE](/concepts/incompatible_table_feature.md) - Error sub-type indicating a table feature is incompatible with IcebergCompatV1
* [INCOMPATIBLE_TABLE_FEATURE (Delta Iceberg error)](/concepts/incompatible_table_feature.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_V1_VIOLATION that occurs when a table has a feature incompatible with IcebergCompatV1
* [Incremental Clone of Parquet and Iceberg to Delta Lake](/concepts/incremental-clone-of-parquet-and-iceberg-to-delta-lake.md) - Alternative approach to incrementally clone Parquet or Iceberg tables to Delta Lake instead of one-time conversion.
* [Incremental Cloning of Parquet and Iceberg to Delta Lake](/concepts/incremental-clone-of-parquet-and-iceberg-to-delta-lake.md) - An alternative approach to CONVERT TO DELTA for incremental, ongoing conversion of Parquet or Iceberg tables to Delta Lake tables.
* [Incremental Cloning to Delta Lake](/concepts/incremental-cloning-to-delta-lake.md) - An alternative to one-time CONVERT TO DELTA for incrementally cloning Parquet and Apache Iceberg tables to Delta Lake.
* [Incremental Cloning vs One-Time Conversion](/concepts/incremental-cloning-vs-one-time-conversion.md) - Distinction between the one-time CONVERT TO DELTA command and incremental cloning for Parquet and Iceberg tables to Delta Lake
* [Incremental Intelligent Scanning](/concepts/incremental-intelligent-scanning.md) - The classification engine intelligently determines when to scan data by leveraging Unity Catalog metadata, performing incremental scans to classify new data without manual configuration, typically scanning new tables and columns within 24 hours.
* [Incremental Model Update Versioning](/concepts/incremental-model-update-versioning.md) - Databricks' approach to shipping model optimizations where endpoint URLs remain stable but the model ID in responses includes a date stamp reflecting the update.
* [Incremental Model Updates (Databricks)](/concepts/incremental-model-update-versioning.md) - Databricks practice of shipping minor model optimizations while keeping the endpoint URL unchanged, with the model ID in the response updated to reflect the date (e.g., 'model-name-030424').
* [Incremental Model Updates and Version History](/concepts/incremental-model-updates-and-version-history.md) - Databricks ships minor model updates with versioned model IDs appended with dates, while keeping endpoint URLs unchanged.
* [Incremental Symlink Manifest Generation](/concepts/incremental-symlink-manifest-generation.md) - A Delta Lake feature for generating symlink manifests incrementally, which is mutually exclusive with persistent deletion vectors.
* [Inference Deployment Strategies on Databricks](/concepts/inference-deployment-strategies-on-databricks.md) - Options for deploying deep learning models for online serving via Model Serving (custom, foundation, external models), batch inference with Spark Pandas UDFs, and streaming inference
* [Inference Monitoring](/concepts/inference-monitoring.md) - Tracking the performance of GenAI applications, machine learning models, and model-serving endpoints by monitoring inference tables that contain model inputs and predictions.
* [Inference Profile](/concepts/inference-profile.md) - A data profile type for monitoring ML model predictions, requiring problem type (classification/regression), prediction column, and model ID column.
* [Inference Profiling](/concepts/inference-profile.md) - A data profiling type for monitoring machine learning model predictions, requiring prediction column, model ID, and optionally label and problem type.
* [Inference Table Augmented DataFrame Logging](/concepts/inference-table-augmented-dataframe-logging.md) - Model serving endpoints created from February 2025 onwards can log the augmented DataFrame (containing looked-up features and function return values) to inference tables for monitoring.
* [Inference Table Logging for Feature Serving](/concepts/inference-tables-for-feature-logging.md) - The ability to log the augmented DataFrame (looked-up feature values and function results) to an inference table for monitoring and debugging, available for endpoints created starting February 2025.
* [Inference Table Monitoring](/concepts/inference-table-monitoring.md) - A use case of data profiling that tracks performance of GenAI apps, ML models, and model-serving endpoints by monitoring inference tables containing model inputs and predictions.
* [Inference Table Profiling](/concepts/inference-table-profiling.md) - A data profiling mode for tracking ML model performance over time by profiling inference tables that contain model inputs and predictions, supporting model version comparison.
* [Inference Table Profiling for ML Models](/concepts/inference-table-profiling.md) - Profiling inference tables that contain ML model inputs and predictions to track model performance over time, compare model versions, and detect data drift relative to training/validation data.
* [Inference Tables](/concepts/inference-tables.md) - A Databricks AI Gateway feature that automatically captures incoming requests and outgoing responses from model serving endpoints for logging and audit.
* [Inference Tables for Feature Logging](/concepts/inference-tables-for-feature-logging.md) - A capability to log the augmented DataFrame containing looked-up feature values and function return values to an inference table for monitoring and debugging, available for endpoints created starting February 2025.
* [Inference Tables for Request Root Cause Analysis](/concepts/inference-tables-for-root-cause-analysis.md) - Using inference tables in Unity Catalog to log, query, and analyze model serving endpoint requests and responses for debugging failures.
* [Inference Tables for Root Cause Analysis](/concepts/inference-tables-for-root-cause-analysis.md) - Using Unity Catalog inference tables to automatically log all requests and responses to model serving endpoints for query-based debugging of failed requests.
* [InferenceLog Analysis](/concepts/inferencelog-analysis.md) - A profile analysis type for ML model monitoring that computes model accuracy, fairness, bias statistics, and per-model-id metrics using label and prediction columns.
* [InferenceLog Analysis for Model Retraining](/concepts/inferencelog-analysis-for-model-retraining.md) - Using drift metrics detected via profile alerts on inference logs to determine when a machine learning model should be retrained.
* [InferenceLog Analysis Metrics](/concepts/inferencelog-analysis-metrics.md) - Additional statistics for model monitoring including model quality metrics (when label_col and prediction_col are provided), fairness and bias statistics for classification models, and automatic slicing by model_id.
* [InferenceLog Model Accuracy and Fairness Statistics](/concepts/inferencelog-model-accuracy-and-fairness-statistics.md) - Additional statistics computed for InferenceLog analysis including model quality metrics (when label_col and prediction_col are provided), automatic slicing by model_id, and fairness/bias statistics for classification models with Boolean slices.
* [InferenceLog Profile](/concepts/inferencelog-profile.md) - A data profile type for ML model monitoring that includes model quality metrics alongside time-series distribution analysis
* [INFORMATION_SCHEMA Grant Visibility Limitation](/concepts/information_schema-grant-visibility-limitation.md) - Users with the MANAGE privilege on an object cannot view all grants for that object via INFORMATION_SCHEMA — only their own grants are shown. This is a known behavior gap slated for correction.
* [Init Script Allowlisting](/concepts/init-script-allowlisting.md) - The process of adding cluster init scripts to the Unity Catalog allowlist from Volumes or object storage, where the identity of the cluster owner is used for permissions in standard access mode.
* [Init Script and JAR Allowlisting](/concepts/init-script-and-jar-allowlisting.md) - The process of adding init scripts or JAR files to the Unity Catalog allowlist via Catalog Explorer or REST API, supporting sources from Unity Catalog volumes or object storage.
* [Initial Snapshot Offset Restriction](/concepts/initial-snapshot-offset-restriction.md) - The constraint that offsets used in Delta skip offset ranges cannot be initial snapshot offsets (isInitialSnapshot must be false)
* [Initial snapshot processing with withEventTimeOrder](/concepts/initial-snapshot-processing-with-witheventtimeorder.md) - Feature to prevent data drops during initial Delta snapshot processing in stateful streaming queries by ordering data by event time into time-bucketed micro-batches.
* [Initial Table Version for Delta Streaming](/concepts/initial-table-version-for-delta-streaming.md) - Setting startingVersion or startingTimestamp to control where a Delta Lake streaming source begins reading
* [INITIAL_SNAPSHOT](/concepts/initial_snapshot.md) - A sub-condition of the DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE error stating that skip offsets must not be initial snapshot offsets
* [INITIAL_SNAPSHOT sub-condition](/concepts/initial_snapshot.md) - A specific reason for the DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE error, indicating that offsets in the skip range must not be initial snapshot offsets (isInitialSnapshot must be false).
* [INITIAL_SNAPSHOT Subcondition](/concepts/initial_snapshot.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE indicating skip offset range is invalid because the offsets are initial snapshot offsets (isInitialSnapshot must be false).
* [Inline Dependency Pinning for Ray Workloads](/concepts/inline-dependency-pinning-for-ray-workloads.md) - Declaring Python dependencies directly in the workload YAML environment section rather than in a separate requirements file, including pinning transitive dependencies like fsspec that conflict with newer library versions.
* [Inline Ray Cluster Bootstrap Pattern](/concepts/inline-ray-cluster-bootstrap-pattern.md) - A pattern for starting a Ray cluster directly within a workload command by running ray start on the head node and having worker nodes join the cluster, enabling distributed training without a separate cluster launcher.
* [Input Rate Limiting and Backlog Metrics](/concepts/input-rate-limiting-and-backlog-metrics.md) - Options (maxFilesPerTrigger, maxBytesPerTrigger) and metrics (numBytesOutstanding, numFilesOutstanding) to control and monitor streaming query processing rate from Delta sources.
* [Input Types for Custom Labeling Schemas](/concepts/input-types-for-custom-schemas.md) - Six supported input types for collecting feedback in custom schemas: InputCategorical, InputCategoricalList, InputText, InputTextList, and InputNumeric — each with configurable constraints.
* [Input types for custom schemas](/concepts/input-types-for-custom-schemas.md) - The supported input types for collecting feedback in custom labeling schemas: InputCategorical, InputCategoricalList, InputText, InputTextList, and InputNumeric.
* [Input types for labeling schemas](/concepts/input-types-for-custom-schemas.md) - Supported input types for custom labeling schemas: InputCategorical, InputCategoricalList, InputText, InputTextList, and InputNumeric, each with specific constraints.
* [Input/Output and Expectation Judges](/concepts/inputoutput-and-expectation-judges.md) - Judges that evaluate agent responses by analyzing conversation inputs/outputs or comparing outputs against predefined expected behaviors
* [Input/Output Tracking](/concepts/inputoutput-tracking.md) - The practice of capturing inputs, outputs, and metadata for each intermediate step in a GenAI application pipeline (retrieval, tool calls, LLM interactions) to enable debugging and analysis.
* [Input/Output-based Evaluation](/concepts/inputoutput-based-evaluation.md) - Evaluation approach where judges assess agent performance by analyzing conversation history (inputs) and agent responses (outputs).
* [Instance Profile (Databricks on AWS)](/concepts/instance-profile-databricks-on-aws.md) - An AWS IAM instance profile attached to a Databricks model serving endpoint to grant access to AWS resources such as S3, allowing models to read/write data during inference.
* [Instance Profile Attachment to Model Serving Endpoints](/concepts/instance-profile-for-model-serving-endpoints.md) - The process and configuration of attaching an AWS IAM instance profile to a Databricks model serving endpoint to grant access to AWS resources.
* [Instance Profile Authentication for Databricks Feature Store](/concepts/instance-profile-authentication-for-feature-store.md) - Using AWS instance profiles attached to Databricks clusters or serving endpoints to authenticate feature store operations with third-party online stores.
* [Instance Profile Authentication for Databricks Online Stores](/concepts/instance-profile-authentication-for-feature-store.md) - Using AWS IAM instance profiles attached to Databricks clusters or serving endpoints to provide read and write authentication to third-party online stores like DynamoDB.
* [Instance Profile Authentication for DynamoDB Online Stores](/concepts/instance-profile-authentication-for-feature-store.md) - Using AWS instance profiles attached to Databricks clusters (for write) or serving endpoints (for read) to authenticate with DynamoDB online stores without explicit secret credentials.
* [Instance Profile Authentication for Feature Store](/concepts/instance-profile-authentication-for-feature-store.md) - Using AWS instance profiles attached to Databricks clusters (for write) or serving endpoints (for read) to authenticate with DynamoDB online stores without explicit credentials.
* [Instance Profile for Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/instance-profile-databricks-on-aws.md) - An AWS IAM instance profile attached to a model serving endpoint to grant access to AWS resources (e.g., S3, feature stores) from a deployed model.
* [Instance Profile for Model Serving Endpoints](/concepts/instance-profile-for-model-serving-endpoints.md) - Attaching AWS IAM instance profiles to Databricks model serving endpoints to grant access to AWS resources such as S3 buckets and feature stores.
* [Instance Profile Lifecycle and Endpoint Behavior](/concepts/instance-profile-lifecycle-and-endpoint-behavior.md) - Modifying or deleting an instance profile in Databricks settings does not affect running endpoints until they are updated or restarted.
* [Instance Profile Lifecycle and Running Endpoints](/concepts/instance-profile-lifecycle-and-endpoint-behavior.md) - Running endpoints continue to use the originally attached instance profile IAM role even if the profile is edited or deleted from the Databricks UI; changes only take effect upon endpoint update.
* [Instance Profile Staleness on Endpoint Update](/concepts/instance-profile-staleness-on-endpoint-update.md) - When an instance profile's IAM role is edited or deleted in Databricks Settings, running serving endpoints continue using the old role until the endpoint is explicitly updated.
* [Intelligent Incremental Scanning](/concepts/intelligent-incremental-scanning.md) - The classification engine uses an agentic AI system with the Data Intelligence Engine to incrementally scan only new or changed data, optimizing cost and avoiding manual configuration.
* [Intelligent Scanning](/concepts/intelligent-scanning.md) - An automated scanning mechanism that prioritizes high-impact tables based on popularity and downstream usage while reducing scan frequency for less critical tables.
* [Intelligent Scanning (Databricks)](/concepts/intelligent-scanning.md) - An automated scan frequency system that prioritizes high-impact tables based on popularity and downstream usage, while reducing scan frequency for less critical tables
* [Intelligent Schema Scanning](/concepts/intelligent-schema-scanning.md) - An approach used by Databricks anomaly detection that prioritizes important tables and skips low-impact ones when monitoring all tables in a schema.
* [IntelliJ IDEA Databricks Integration](/concepts/intellij-idea-databricks-integration.md) - Using Databricks Connect for Scala from the IntelliJ IDEA IDE to run Spark code on Databricks compute.
* [Interactive Data Applications with Databricks Connect](/concepts/interactive-data-applications-with-databricks-connect.md) - The ability to embed Databricks Connect in applications (like JDBC but with full PySpark expressiveness) to build interactive data apps that leverage Databricks serverless compute.
* [Interactive Development with Databricks Connect](/concepts/interactive-development-with-databricks-connect.md) - The ability to develop and debug Spark code from any IDE (VS Code, PyCharm, IntelliJ) with code running remotely on Databricks compute while supporting local debugging.
* [Interactive IDE Development with Databricks Connect](/concepts/interactive-development-with-databricks-connect.md) - A workflow enabling developers to write Spark code in IDEs like VS Code, PyCharm, and IntelliJ IDEA while running and debugging that code on remote Databricks compute clusters.
* [Intermediate Step Instrumentation](/concepts/intermediate-step-instrumentation.md) - Capturing inputs, outputs, and metadata for each intermediate step in a GenAI pipeline (e.g., retrieval, tool calls, LLM interactions) along with user feedback and quality evaluation results.
* [Intermediate Step Trace Capture](/concepts/intermediate-step-trace-capture.md) - The practice of examining inputs, outputs, and metadata for each intermediate step in a GenAI pipeline (retrieval, tool calls, LLM interactions) for debugging and analysis.
* [Internal vs External Identity Provider Models](/concepts/internal-vs-external-identity-provider-models.md) - Two deployment modes for OIDC federation: provider-managed (internal IdP used when sharing within the same organization) and recipient-managed (external IdP where the recipient controls access policies and user identities).
* [Internal vs External Identity Provider Patterns](/concepts/internal-vs-external-identity-provider-models.md) - Two deployment patterns for OIDC federation: provider-managed IdP for intra-organization sharing and recipient-managed IdP for cross-organization sharing, each with different security and control characteristics.
* [Internal vs External Identity Provider Scenarios](/concepts/internal-vs-external-identity-provider-scenarios.md) - Two OIDC sharing models: provider-managed (internal IdP for sharing within large organizations) and recipient-managed (external IdP where recipient controls access).
* [Internal vs External Identity Providers](/concepts/internal-vs-external-identity-providers.md) - Two sharing scenarios: provider-managed (internal IdP for sharing within the same organization) and recipient-managed (external IdP where the recipient controls access policies and MFA).
* [Invalid Partition Value Detection](/concepts/invalid-partition-value-detection.md) - The ability of FSCK REPAIR TABLE to detect data files with partition column values that violate NOT NULL constraints, such as NULL values in non-nullable partition columns.
* [Invalid partition values detection](/concepts/invalid-partition-values-detection.md) - Detection of data files with partition values that violate the table's NOT NULL partition column constraints
* [INVALID_INDEX](/concepts/invalid_index.md) - A sub-condition of the DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE error stating that offset indices must match the expected base index
* [INVALID_INDEX sub-condition](/concepts/invalid_index.md) - A specific reason for the DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE error, indicating that the offset index must match the expected BASE_INDEX value.
* [INVALID_INDEX Subcondition](/concepts/invalid_index.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE indicating the offset index must be BASE_INDEX (<baseIndex>).
* [IP Access List Constraints](/concepts/ip-access-list-constraints.md) - Technical limitations of recipient IP access lists: maximum 100 IP/CIDR values per recipient, only IPv4 addresses supported, only allow lists (no deny lists) available.
* [IP Access Lists for OpenSharing Recipients](/concepts/ip-access-lists-for-opensharing-recipients.md) - A security feature that allows providers to restrict Databricks-to-Open sharing recipients to a defined set of IP addresses, limiting network-level access to shared data.
* [Irreversibility of Unity Catalog Enablement](/concepts/irreversibility-of-unity-catalog-enablement.md) - Once a workspace is enabled for Unity Catalog, the enablement cannot be reversed and identity management permanently shifts to account-level interfaces.
* [Iterative GenAI Development Lifecycle](/concepts/iterative-genai-development-lifecycle.md) - The continuous optimization workflow of developing, testing, monitoring, and collecting human feedback for GenAI applications.
* [Iterative Prompt Improvement Workflow](/concepts/iterative-prompt-improvement-workflow.md) - A workflow pattern for GenAI development: evaluate a prompt, review results, revise the prompt based on evaluation criteria, re-run evaluation, and compare runs in the MLflow UI.
* [Iterative Scorer Development Workflow](/concepts/iterative-scorer-development-workflow.md) - A four-step development process for building code-based scorers: define evaluation data, generate traces, store traces, and iterate on scorers using stored traces.
* [Ivy Dependency Resolution for Databricks Connect UDFs](/concepts/ivy-dependency-resolution-for-databricks-connect-udfs.md) - Using spark.addArtifact() with ivy:// URIs to download third-party Maven dependencies at runtime for UDFs that depend on libraries not present on the Databricks cluster.
* [JAR Build Compatibility for Databricks Serverless](/concepts/jar-build-compatibility-for-databricks-serverless.md) - Specific version requirements (Scala 2.13, JDK 17, Databricks Connect 17.3 for environment version 4) that JARs must match exactly to run on serverless compute.
* [JAR Dependency Management on Serverless](/concepts/jar-dependency-management-on-serverless.md) - Three strategies to make libraries available to JARs on serverless: use provided libraries, attach as environment libraries, or use JDBC connections for external databases.
* [JAR Dependency Strategy for Serverless](/concepts/jar-dependency-strategy-for-serverless.md) - Three approaches to manage library dependencies for JARs on serverless compute: provided libraries, environment libraries, and JDBC connections
* [JAR Library Allowlisting](/concepts/jar-library-allowlisting.md) - The process of adding JAR artifacts to the Unity Catalog allowlist from Volumes or object storage, where the identity of the library installer is used for permissions in standard access mode.
* [JAR Task Configuration in Databricks Jobs](/concepts/jar-task-configuration-in-databricks-jobs.md) - Step-by-step process for creating a Databricks job that runs a JAR on serverless compute: setting main class, selecting compute type, configuring serverless environment version, attaching JAR file, and passing parameters.
* [JAR Task in Lakeflow Jobs](/concepts/jar-task-in-lakeflow-jobs.md) - A Databricks workflow task type that runs a compiled Java/Scala JAR on serverless or classic compute, configured with main class, parameters, and serverless environment.
* [Job Registry Webhook Payload by Job Type](/concepts/job-registry-webhook-payload-by-job-type.md) - Different payload formats for job-triggering webhooks based on job type: notebook/Python wheel jobs get a params dict, Python/JAR/Spark submit jobs get a params list, multi-task jobs get all parameter types populated.
* [Job Reliability Configuration](/concepts/job-reliability-configuration.md) - Settings for workload resilience including `max_retries` (number of automatic retries on failure) and `timeout_minutes` (wall-clock budget per attempt) in AI Runtime YAML configs.
* [Job Webhooks for Manual Model Deployment Approval](/concepts/job-webhooks-for-manual-model-deployment-approval.md) - Using job notifications to call out to external CI/CD systems for manual approval gates before deploying production models, then using aliases to promote the model version
* [Jobs API and Databricks Asset Bundles for AI Runtime](/concepts/jobs-api-and-databricks-asset-bundles-for-ai-runtime.md) - Programmatic creation and management of AI Runtime jobs using the Databricks Jobs API or Asset Bundles with YAML configuration
* [John Snow Labs Healthcare NLP](/concepts/john-snow-labs-healthcare-nlp.md) - A proprietary library (Spark NLP for Healthcare) for clinical and biomedical text mining, offering pre-trained models for entities, drugs, risk factors, anatomy, and demographics, available via Databricks Partner Connect.
* [Joint Multi-Prompt Optimization](/concepts/joint-multi-prompt-optimization.md) - The ability to simultaneously refine multiple prompts together for best overall performance, enabled by passing multiple prompt_uris to the optimize_prompts API.
* [JSON Schema Limitations for Function Calling](/concepts/json-schema-limitations-for-function-calling.md) - Foundation Model APIs support only a subset of JSON schema for function definitions, excluding pattern, complex composition keywords, and limiting to 16 keys maximum.
* [JSON Schema Subset for Function Calling](/concepts/json-schema-subset-for-function-calling.md) - Databricks Foundation Model APIs support a simplified subset of JSON Schema for function definitions, excluding pattern, anyOf/oneOf/allOf, prefixItems, and $ref to improve generation quality.
* [JSON Schema Subset for Function Definitions](/concepts/json-schema-subset-for-function-definitions.md) - The restricted JSON schema specification that Databricks Foundation Model APIs accept for function call definitions, excluding pattern, anyOf, oneOf, allOf, $ref, and limiting keys to 16.
* [Judge Alignment](/concepts/judge-alignment.md) - A process that teaches LLM judges to match human evaluation standards through systematic feedback, improving agreement with human assessments by 30-50 percent.
* [Judge Alignment Three-Step Workflow](/concepts/judge-alignment-three-step-workflow.md) - The standard workflow for aligning judges: generate initial assessments, collect human feedback, then align and deploy using the judge's align() method.
* [Judge Alignment Validation](/concepts/alignment-validation.md) - The process of comparing original and aligned judge performance against human ground truth to measure improvement in accuracy after alignment.
* [Judge Alignment with Human Experts](/concepts/judge-alignment-with-human-experts.md) - A process where base LLM judges are refined by incorporating human expert feedback on application outputs to improve judge accuracy and alignment with human judgment.
* [Judge Instruction Best Practices](/concepts/judge-instruction-best-practices.md) - Guidelines for writing effective judge instructions including specificity about output formats (categorical, boolean, numeric) and breaking down complex evaluations.
* [Judge Model Customization](/concepts/judge-model-customization.md) - The ability to change the underlying LLM that powers a built-in judge by specifying a model in '<provider>:/<model-name>' format, supporting Databricks-hosted models and other LiteLLM-compatible providers.
* [Judge Prompt Template Variables](/concepts/judge-prompt-template-variables.md) - A restricted set of four template variables ({{ inputs }}, {{ outputs }}, {{ expectations }}, {{ trace }}) that can be used in custom judge prompts, with validation to prevent injection of arbitrary variables.
* [Judge Prompt Templates with Variables](/concepts/judge-prompt-templates-with-variables.md) - Custom judge instructions support templated variables such as `{{ inputs }}`, `{{ outputs }}`, `{{ expectations }}`, and `{{ trace }}` that dynamically inject evaluation context from the agent invocation.
* [Judge Registration and Deployment](/concepts/judge-registration-and-deployment.md) - The process of registering an aligned judge for production use with distinguishing metadata such as name, alignment date, and number of traces used.
* [Judge Validation](/concepts/judge-validation.md) - Methodology for comparing original and aligned judge performance against human ground truth to measure accuracy improvement.
* [KD00C SQLSTATE Class](/concepts/kd00c-sqlstate-class.md) - The SQLSTATE class 'KD' (Datasource-specific errors) that encompasses the DELTA_VERSIONS_NOT_CONTIGUOUS error condition in Databricks.
* [KD00E SQLSTATE](/concepts/kd00e-sqlstate.md) - A Databricks-specific SQLSTATE code (KD00E) under the KD class for datasource-specific errors, used by the DELTA_ICEBERG_WRITER_COMPAT_VIOLATION error condition.
* [Knowledge Assistant Limitation with Model Permissions](/concepts/knowledge-assistant-limitation-with-model-permissions.md) - A known limitation where Agent Bricks Knowledge Assistant does not support foundation model Unity Catalog permissions, requiring contact with Databricks account team before enabling the feature if Knowledge Assistant is actively used.
* [Label Mapping for Sequence Classification](/concepts/label-mapping-for-sequence-classification.md) - Mapping string labels to integer IDs and creating id2label/label2id dictionaries for Hugging Face AutoModelForSequenceClassification
* [Label Schema Types: Feedback vs Expectation](/concepts/assessment-types-feedback-vs-expectation.md) - Two fundamental assessment types for labeling schemas: Feedback (subjective opinions, ratings, preferences) and Expectation (objective ground truth, correct answers, expected behavior).
* [Label Schemas](/concepts/label-schemas.md) - Definition of structured feedback fields (categorical or text) used in labeling sessions to collect targeted expert assessments.
* [Label Schemas for Structured Feedback](/concepts/label-schemas-for-structured-feedback.md) - Defining structured feedback schemas using create_label_schema() with InputCategorical and InputText types to standardize expert review criteria and expected responses
* [Labeling and Review in MLflow](/concepts/labeling-and-review-in-mlflow.md) - The capability to view labeling sessions, inspect labeling schemas, and review feedback criteria such as ratings, comments, and expectations for GenAI agent traces.
* [Labeling and Review with Genie Code](/concepts/labeling-and-review-with-genie-code.md) - Genie Code capability to view labeling sessions, see who is assigned to review traces, and inspect labeling schemas to understand feedback criteria such as ratings, comments, and expectations.
* [Labeling Schema](/concepts/labeling-schema.md) - A structured definition of questions, input types, validation rules, and instructions that domain experts answer when labeling traces in the Review App for GenAI evaluation.
* [Labeling schema best practices](/concepts/labeling-schema-best-practices.md) - Guidelines for effective schema design including clear questions, specific prompts, reasonable limits, mutually exclusive options, descriptive naming, and lifecycle management.
* [Labeling schema CRUD management](/concepts/labeling-schema-management-api.md) - API-driven lifecycle management for labeling schemas: create (with overwrite), get, list, update, and delete operations scoped to MLflow experiments.
* [Labeling Schema Input Types](/concepts/labeling-schema-input-types.md) - The five supported input types for labeling schemas: InputCategorical, InputCategoricalList, InputText, InputTextList, and InputNumeric, each with configurable validation rules and constraints.
* [Labeling schema integration with sessions](/concepts/labeling-schema-integration-with-sessions.md) - How labeling schemas are associated with labeling sessions and presented in the Review App to collect structured feedback.
* [Labeling Schema Lifecycle Management](/concepts/labeling-schema-management-api.md) - CRUD operations for labeling schemas using the MLflow API: listing (get_label_schema), creating/updating (create_label_schema with overwrite=True), and deleting (delete_label_schema), with schemas scoped to experiments.
* [Labeling schema management API](/concepts/labeling-schema-management-api.md) - CRUD operations for labeling schemas via the MLflow API: create_label_schema, get_label_schema, and delete_label_schema, with overwrite support for updates.
* [Labeling schema scope and uniqueness](/concepts/labeling-schema-scope-and-uniqueness.md) - Labeling schemas are scoped to MLflow experiments, requiring unique names within each experiment.
* [Labeling Schema Scoping to Experiments](/concepts/labeling-schema-scoping-to-experiments.md) - Labeling schemas are scoped to MLflow experiments, requiring unique names within an experiment and enabling organized, experiment-specific feedback collection.
* [Labeling Schemas](/concepts/labeling-schemas.md) - Definitions of the questions and input types (categorical, numeric, free-text) that domain experts use to provide feedback or expectations on traces within the Review App.
* [Labeling schemas (MLflow Review App)](/concepts/labeling-schema.md) - Structured question definitions that domain experts answer when labeling existing traces in the MLflow Review App for GenAI evaluation.
* [Labeling schemas (MLflow)](/concepts/labeling-schema.md) - Structured question definitions that domain experts answer when labeling existing traces in the MLflow Review App for GenAI evaluation.
* [Labeling Session](/concepts/labeling-session.md) - A structured MLflow run container that collects human-generated assessments (feedback or expectations) from domain experts on GenAI application traces using the MLflow Review App.
* [Labeling session and schema integration](/concepts/labeling-session-and-schema-integration.md) - How labeling schemas are associated with labeling sessions to organize structured human review workflows in the Review App.
* [Labeling Session Dataset Synchronization](/concepts/labeling-session-dataset-synchronization.md) - The mechanism to sync Expectation data from labeling sessions to Evaluation Datasets via an intelligent upsert operation, using trace inputs as unique keys.
* [Labeling Session User Management](/concepts/labeling-session-user-management.md) - The policies, permissions model, and API methods for assigning and managing domain expert reviewers for labeling sessions, including SCIM integration and workspace access.
* [Labeling Session User Management and Permissions](/concepts/labeling-session-user-management-and-permissions.md) - Process for assigning users to labeling sessions, including automatic WRITE permission grants on the MLflow experiment, SCIM provisioning for non-workspace users, and APIs for adding or replacing assigned users.
* [Labeling Sessions](/concepts/labeling-sessions.md) - A special type of MLflow Run that organizes a set of traces for review by specific experts using selected labeling schemas, acting as a queue for the review process.
* [Labeling Sessions (MLflow)](/concepts/labeling-session.md) - Structured containers within MLflow that collect human-generated assessments (labels) on MLflow Traces from domain experts via the MLflow Review App.
* [Labeling Sessions and Labeling Schemas](/concepts/labeling-sessions-and-schemas.md) - Human annotation infrastructure where labeling sessions queue traces for domain expert review and labeling schemas define the structured questions and valid responses for consistent assessment collection.
* [Labeling Sessions and Schemas](/concepts/labeling-sessions-and-schemas.md) - Labeling sessions queue traces for human expert review, while labeling schemas define structured questions and valid responses to ensure consistent assessment collection across domain experts.
* [Labeling Sessions and Schemas (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/labeling-sessions-and-schemas.md) - Infrastructure for collecting domain expert feedback, where sessions queue traces for review and schemas define structured questions for consistent labeling.
* [Labeling Sessions for Expert Review](/concepts/labeling-sessions-for-expert-review.md) - Structured expert review process where domain experts assess trace outputs using defined label schemas (e.g., categorical accuracy, ideal text responses) to establish ground truth.
* [Lakebase Autoscaling](/concepts/lakebase-autoscaling.md) - The latest version of Lakebase with autoscaling compute, scale-to-zero, branching, and instant restore, replacing the original Lakebase Provisioned offering.
* [Lakebase Autoscaling for Online Feature Stores](/concepts/lakebase-autoscaling-for-online-feature-stores.md) - The underlying managed infrastructure powering Databricks Online Feature Stores, providing scalability and high availability for real-time feature serving.
* [Lakebase Synced Table](/concepts/lakebase-synced-table.md) - A Unity Catalog read-only Postgres table that automatically synchronizes data from a Unity Catalog table to a Lakebase database instance for OLTP workloads.
* [Lakeflow Connect Source Lineage](/concepts/lakeflow-connect-source-lineage.md) - Automatic recording of source lineage from external source tables to destination tables in Unity Catalog via managed ingestion pipelines using Lakeflow Connect.
* [Lakeflow Jobs](/concepts/lakeflow-jobs.md) - Databricks' job orchestration platform for running automated workflows, including Python wheel tasks, on AWS.
* [Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines](/concepts/lakeflow-spark-declarative-pipelines.md) - A Databricks pipeline framework supporting incremental (triggered or continuous) data processing, used here to read new OTel spans, apply transformations like PII redaction, and write results to target tables.
* [Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines for Feature Tables](/concepts/lakeflow-spark-declarative-pipelines-for-feature-tables.md) - Streaming tables and materialized views created by Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines can include primary key constraints and be used as feature tables in Unity Catalog.
* [Lakehouse Federation Data Sharing](/concepts/lakehouse-federation-data-sharing.md) - Sharing foreign schemas and tables from external data sources via Lakehouse Federation without copying data, using on-demand query materialization on the provider side.
* [Lakehouse Federation with Online Tables](/concepts/lakehouse-federation-with-online-tables.md) - The ability to query online tables using Lakehouse Federation and a Serverless SQL warehouse, supporting only SELECT operations for interactive or debugging purposes, not for production workloads.
* [Lakehouse Monitoring](/concepts/lakehouse-monitoring.md) - The former name for Data Profiling in Databricks Unity Catalog; now superseded by the data profiling capability within data quality monitoring.
* [Lakehouse Monitoring (Legacy Name)](/concepts/lakehouse-monitoring.md) - The former name of the Data Profiling feature in Databricks Unity Catalog; used for tracking summary statistics and historical metrics of data tables.
* [Landing Zone Architecture](/concepts/landing-zone-architecture.md) - An optional pre-ingestion layer in cloud object storage that receives raw files from external systems before processing into the bronze medallion layer, with patterns for triggers and governance.
* [LangChain Tool for Feature Serving](/concepts/langchaintool-with-feature-serving.md) - A LangChain tool wrapper that calls a Databricks feature serving endpoint to retrieve structured data augmenting the context passed to an LLM.
* [LangChainTool for Feature Lookup](/concepts/langchaintool-for-feature-lookup.md) - A LangChain tool created to query a Databricks feature serving endpoint for relevant structured data in a RAG pipeline
* [LangChainTool with Feature Serving](/concepts/langchaintool-with-feature-serving.md) - A LangChain tool that wraps a Databricks feature serving endpoint to enable an agent to look up structured data during a RAG workflow.
* [Langfuse @observe() Decorator](/concepts/langfuse-observe-decorator.md) - Langfuse's function decorator that automatically creates OpenTelemetry trace spans for instrumented functions, which can be exported to any OTel-compatible backend.
* [Langfuse OTEL Exporter Hijacking](/concepts/langfuse-otel-exporter-hijacking.md) - Setting Langfuse environment variables to dummy values and attaching a custom BatchSpanProcessor with an OTLPSpanExporter to intercept Langfuse-generated OTel spans and redirect them to Databricks instead of the Langfuse server.
* [Langfuse SDK Dummy Configuration](/concepts/langfuse-sdk-dummy-configuration.md) - A technique that disables Langfuse's own trace collection by setting LANGFUSE_HOST, LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, and LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY to dummy values, so only an attached OTLP exporter receives spans.
* [Langfuse SDK Export Redirection](/concepts/langfuse-sdk-export-redirection.md) - Technique of setting dummy Langfuse environment variables (LANGFUSE_HOST, LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY) to prevent traces from being sent to Langfuse servers while still using the SDK's OpenTelemetry TracerProvider for custom export.
* [Langfuse-Databricks Trace Export](/concepts/langfuse-databricks-trace-export.md) - Configuring Langfuse to route OpenTelemetry trace spans to the Databricks MLflow OTLP endpoint for storage and governance in Unity Catalog.
* [Langfuse-to-Databricks Trace Export](/concepts/langfuse-databricks-trace-export.md) - A configuration pattern that redirects Langfuse-instrumented OpenTelemetry spans to Databricks MLflow's OTLP endpoint instead of Langfuse's own servers, enabling trace storage in Unity Catalog tables.
* [Language Support for Databricks Connect](/concepts/language-support-for-databricks-connect.md) - Databricks Connect is available for Python, R, and Scala, with each language having its own dedicated documentation and tutorials.
* [Large Artifact Logging Pattern](/concepts/large-artifact-logging-pattern.md) - Best practice for handling large artifacts in distributed Ray workloads by persisting them as files and logging with MLflow via file path references.
* [Large Dataset Sampling for AutoML](/concepts/large-dataset-sampling-for-automl.md) - AutoML's automatic memory estimation and stratified/random sampling of large datasets to fit single-worker-node training constraints.
* [Large Dataset Sampling in AutoML](/concepts/large-dataset-sampling-in-automl.md) - How Databricks AutoML samples large datasets for classification and regression to fit within single-node memory constraints, using stratified sampling for classification.
* [Large Image File Handling Strategy](/concepts/large-image-file-handling-strategy.md) - Recommendation to store only metadata in Delta tables and load large images (>100 MB on average) directly from object store by path when needed, to avoid performance bottlenecks.
* [Large language models (LLMs) on Databricks](/concepts/large-language-models-llms-on-databricks.md) - Notebook examples for fine-tuning large language models on Databricks AI Runtime, including parameter-efficient methods.
* [Latency-Throughput Trade-off](/concepts/latency-throughput-trade-off.md) - The fundamental tension in LLM serving where increasing concurrent requests improves throughput at the cost of higher per-request latency, requiring workload-specific balancing.
* [Latency-Throughput Trade-off in LLM Serving](/concepts/latency-throughput-trade-off.md) - The fundamental tension in LLM serving where increasing concurrent requests improves throughput at the cost of higher latency, requiring careful control of parallelism based on use case.
* [Learning from Sets Dataset (GroupLens)](/concepts/learning-from-sets-dataset-movielens.md) - A movie rating dataset from GroupLens (Learning from Sets 2019) used for training the two-tower recommendation model, preprocessed with label binarization.
* [Learning from Sets Dataset (MovieLens)](/concepts/learning-from-sets-dataset-movielens.md) - A public MovieLens-derived dataset used for training and evaluating recommendation models, preprocessed with rating binarization
* [Legacy Access Control vs Unity Catalog](/concepts/legacy-access-control-vs-unity-catalog.md) - The access control model described applies only to Databricks workspaces not enabled for Unity Catalog; Unity Catalog-enabled workspaces use Unity Catalog privileges instead.
* [Legacy Anomaly Detection Configurations](/concepts/legacy-anomaly-detection-configurations.md) - Earlier version features including a data quality dashboard at /Shared/Databricks Quality Monitoring/ and customizable job parameters for freshness/completeness evaluation thresholds.
* [Legacy Data Governance Models in Databricks](/concepts/legacy-data-governance-models-in-databricks.md) - Older data governance approaches on Databricks that are superseded by Unity Catalog, including Hive metastore table access control.
* [Legacy data profiling billing query pattern](/concepts/legacy-data-profiling-billing-query-pattern.md) - Pre-February 2026 query pattern for data profiling expenses using sku_name containing JOBS_SERVERLESS and custom_tags['LakehouseMonitoring'] instead of table_id filter.
* [Legacy Delta Configuration Migration](/concepts/legacy-delta-configuration-removal.md) - Recommendation to remove explicit legacy Delta configurations from Spark configurations and table properties when upgrading to a new Databricks Runtime version to enable new optimizations and defaults.
* [Legacy Delta Configuration Removal](/concepts/legacy-delta-configuration-removal.md) - Best practice of removing explicit legacy Delta configurations from Spark configs and table properties when upgrading Databricks Runtime versions to avoid blocking new optimizations.
* [Legacy Feature Store Access Control vs Unity Catalog](/concepts/legacy-access-control-vs-unity-catalog.md) - Databricks Feature Store offers two access control models: legacy fine-grained permissions for workspaces without Unity Catalog, and Unity Catalog privileges for workspaces with Unity Catalog enabled.
* [Legacy Foundation Model Families](/concepts/legacy-foundation-model-families.md) - Legacy model families (e.g., Meta Llama 2/3, DBRX, Mistral) on Databricks that provision inference capacity based on tokens per second ranges rather than model units.
* [Legacy MLflow Model Serving](/concepts/legacy-mlflow-model-serving.md) - The older MLflow-based model serving experience on Databricks that reaches end of life on September 15, 2025, with new endpoint creation blocked after August 22, 2025.
* [Legacy Online Tables](/concepts/legacy-online-tables.md) - The original Databricks online table product being deprecated; users are instructed to migrate to Online Feature Store or Lakebase synced tables.
* [Legacy replaceWhere Partition-Only Overwrite](/concepts/legacy-replacewhere-partition-only-overwrite.md) - Older Delta Lake behavior where replaceWhere only works on partition columns, enabled by setting spark.databricks.delta.replaceWhere.dataColumns.enabled to false.
* [Legacy vs Unity Catalog Access Control for Feature Store](/concepts/legacy-vs-unity-catalog-access-control-for-feature-store.md) - Databricks Feature Store offers two access control paradigms: a legacy permission system with three levels (used in workspaces without Unity Catalog) and Unity Catalog privileges (used in workspaces enabled for Unity Catalog).
* [Legacy Workspace Feature Store](/concepts/legacy-workspace-feature-store.md) - The older feature store system in Databricks, separate from Unity Catalog, still supported for AutoML integration.
* [Library Deprecation and Removal Policy](/concepts/library-deprecation-policy.md) - Policies governing when pre-installed or top-tier libraries are deprecated and removed from Databricks Runtime ML, including triggers such as inactivity, low usage, or replacement by superior packages.
* [Library deprecation criteria](/concepts/library-deprecation-criteria.md) - Conditions under which Databricks removes a top-tier or pre-installed library, including inactivity metrics, lack of maintenance, and low usage.
* [Library Deprecation Policy](/concepts/library-deprecation-policy.md) - Defines conditions under which Databricks removes libraries from top-tier or pre-installed status, including inactivity, low usage, or replacement.
* [Library Maintenance Activity Criteria](/concepts/library-maintenance-activity-criteria.md) - Specific metrics used by Databricks to determine if a library is actively maintained, based on commit frequency and release cadence (e.g., no commits in 2-3 months, no releases in 6-9 months).
* [Library removal notification process](/concepts/library-removal-notification-process.md) - The multi-channel customer notification workflow Databricks follows before removing a library, including release notes, import warnings, and documentation updates.
* [Lifecycle Policy Extension Problem](/concepts/lifecycle-policy-extension-problem.md) - The risk and complications that arise when extending the cloud lifecycle management transition rule to a longer interval, due to the mismatch between the table property and actual archival state during the lag period.
* [Lifecycle policy extension risks with archival support](/concepts/lifecycle-policy-extension-risks-with-archival-support.md) - Extending the cloud lifecycle management transition rule (e.g., from 60 to 90 days) can cause errors because previously archived files are not automatically restored; delta.timeUntilArchived must never exceed the actual age of the most recently archived data.
* [Lifecycle policy extension risks with Delta Lake archival](/concepts/lifecycle-policy-extension-risks-with-delta-lake-archival.md) - Extending the archival interval in cloud lifecycle policies can lead to errors because previously archived files are not automatically restored.
* [Lifecycle Policy Transition Rule Changes](/concepts/lifecycle-policy-transition-rule-changes.md) - Procedures and risks associated with changing the cloud lifecycle management transition rule interval and keeping the delta.timeUntilArchived property in sync.
* [Liger Kernels](/concepts/liger-kernels.md) - GPU-optimized Triton kernels that fuse multiple operations (e.g., linear + loss) to reduce memory usage by up to 80%, optimized for transformer operations like RMSNorm, RoPE, SwiGLU, and CrossEntropy.
* [LightningModule with AUROC Metrics for Recommendation](/concepts/lightningmodule-with-auroc-metrics-for-recommendation.md) - Using PyTorch Lightning's LightningModule pattern with torchmetrics AUROC to encapsulate training, validation, and logging for binary relevance prediction in recommender systems.
* [Limitations of Databricks Autologging](/concepts/limitations-of-databricks-autologging.md) - Known limitations including driver-node-only execution, unsupported XGBoost scikit-learn integration, and interaction with Spark MLlib and Hyperopt integrations
* [Limitations of Databricks Connect Long-lived Sessions](/concepts/limitations-of-databricks-connect-long-lived-sessions.md) - Long-lived sessions are only available on serverless compute (not standard/dedicated), are best-effort (not guaranteed), have state size limits, expire after 2 days of inactivity, and do not preserve streaming query state or EXECUTE IMMEDIATE SQL scripts.
* [Limitations of Databricks Feature Engineering Client](/concepts/limitations-of-databricks-feature-engineering-client.md) - The client library can only run on Databricks (Databricks Runtime or Databricks Runtime ML) and does not support calling Feature Engineering APIs from external/local environments.
* [Limitations of Databricks Utilities in Scala Connect](/concepts/limitations-of-databricks-utilities-in-scala-connect.md) - When using Databricks Connect for Scala, only DBFS and secrets utilities are available through DBUtils.getDBUtils; no other Databricks Utilities functionality is accessible.
* [Limitations of dbutils in Databricks Connect](/concepts/restricted-dbutils-in-databricks-connect.md) - Only the fs and secrets Databricks Utilities are available through dbutils in Databricks Connect; all other dbutils utilities are not accessible
* [Limitations of Delta archival support](/concepts/limitations-of-delta-archival-support.md) - Key constraints include: transaction log must never be archived, no support for access-time or tag-based policies, DROP COLUMN and PURGE restrictions, and LIMIT query incompatibility.
* [Limitations of Distributed XGBoost](/concepts/limitations-of-distributed-xgboost.md) - Restrictions when using distributed XGBoost on Databricks: no mlflow.xgboost.autolog, no baseMarginCol, and incompatibility with autoscaling clusters.
* [Limitations of Distributed XGBoost on Databricks](/concepts/limitations-of-distributed-xgboost.md) - Key restrictions when using distributed XGBoost training: mlflow.xgboost.autolog is unavailable, baseMarginCol cannot be used, and autoscaling clusters are not supported
* [Limitations of External Lineage](/concepts/limitations-of-external-lineage.md) - Constraints on external lineage in Unity Catalog including: lineage not recorded in system.access lineage tables, and resource limits of 10,000 external metadata objects and 100,000 lineage relationships per metastore.
* [Limitations of Multimodal Model Serving on Databricks](/concepts/limitations-of-multimodal-model-serving-on-databricks.md) - Constraints for audio and video inputs on Databricks: only available on Gemini pay-per-token models (not provisioned throughput), and large files increase latency and token usage.
* [Limitations of Route Optimization](/concepts/limitations-of-route-optimization.md) - Route optimization is only available for custom model and feature serving endpoints, not Foundation Model APIs or external models.
* [Limitations of row filters and column masks](/concepts/limitations-of-row-filters-and-column-masks.md) - Comprehensive set of constraints including runtime version requirements, unsupported operations (MERGE, time travel, clones, views), and dedicated access mode restrictions.
* [Limited Databricks Utilities Support in Scala Connect](/concepts/limitations-of-databricks-utilities-in-scala-connect.md) - Only DBFS and secrets utilities are available through Databricks Connect for Scala; no other Databricks Utilities (like widgets, notebook, etc.) are supported for Scala projects.
* [Limited dbutils availability in Databricks Connect](/concepts/limited-dbutils-availability-in-databricks-connect.md) - Only the `fs` and `secrets` Databricks Utilities are available via dbutils in Databricks Connect; all other utilities are unavailable.
* [Limited Utility Availability in Databricks Connect for Scala](/concepts/limited-utility-availability-in-databricks-connect-for-scala.md) - Only DBFS filesystem and secrets utilities are available through Databricks Utilities for Scala; no other utilities are supported
* [Lineage Graph in Catalog Explorer](/concepts/lineage-graph-in-catalog-explorer.md) - Interactive visual interface in Databricks Catalog Explorer for browsing upstream and downstream dependencies of tables, jobs, pipelines, and dashboards with expandable nodes and edge details.
* [Lineage Limitations and Boundary Conditions](/concepts/lineage-limitations-and-boundary-conditions.md) - Notable gaps in lineage capture including renamed objects, RDDs, global temp views, path-referenced tables, UDFs, Jobs API runs_submit workflows, Spark SQL checkpointing, and incomplete coverage for PRIVATE tables in Lakeflow pipelines.
* [Lineage Limitations and Edge Cases](/concepts/lineage-limitations-and-edge-cases.md) - Known gaps in Unity Catalog lineage capture including renamed objects, Spark SQL checkpointing, RDDs, global temp views, path-referenced tables, UDFs, and Jobs API runs_submit workflows.
* [Lineage Limitations in Unity Catalog](/concepts/lineage-limitations-in-unity-catalog.md) - Known constraints including no lineage before September 2024, no preservation on renamed objects, no capture for RDDs, global temp views, spark-submit jobs, and incomplete column coverage for UDFs and path-based references.
* [Lineage Permissions and Access Control](/concepts/lineage-permissions-and-access-control.md) - Lineage visibility is governed by Unity Catalog's BROWSE permission on catalog objects and workspace-level permissions on notebooks, jobs, and dashboards, with cross-workspace masking.
* [Lineage Permissions and Masked Nodes](/concepts/lineage-permissions-and-masked-nodes.md) - Access control model where lineage visibility is governed by Unity Catalog BROWSE/SELECT privileges, and unauthorized tables appear as masked nodes in the lineage graph.
* [Lineage Permissions Model](/concepts/lineage-permissions-model.md) - Access control for lineage data requiring BROWSE privilege on catalog objects and workspace-level permissions on notebooks, jobs, and dashboards, with masked nodes for unauthorized objects.
* [Lineage Relationship Patterns](/concepts/lineage-relationship-patterns.md) - Design patterns for modeling external lineage, including chaining multiple external metadata objects, connecting two Unity Catalog tables through an intermediary, and adding column-level lineage.
* [Lineage Retention and Time Range](/concepts/lineage-retention-and-time-range.md) - Lineage data in Catalog Explorer is retained indefinitely for events after September 1, 2024, with an 'All time' option for newer metastores; lineage system tables retain a rolling one-year window.
* [Lineage Retention Policy](/concepts/lineage-retention-policy.md) - Lineage data displayed in Catalog Explorer is retained indefinitely from September 1, 2024 onward, while system tables retain a rolling one-year window.
* [Lineage System Tables](/concepts/lineage-system-tables.md) - Programmatic access to lineage data via system.access.table_lineage and system.access.column_lineage tables, which retain a rolling one-year window of lineage events.
* [Liquid Clustering](/concepts/liquid-clustering.md) - A Databricks data layout optimization technique recommended for Delta Lake tables to improve query performance.
* [Liquid Clustering (Databricks)](/concepts/liquid-clustering-databricks.md) - A Databricks table clustering feature recommended as a replacement for deprecated Bloom filter indexes.
* [Liquid Clustering Enablement on Existing Tables](/concepts/liquid-clustering-enablement-on-existing-tables.md) - Enabling liquid clustering on an existing Delta table requires using overwrite mode with the overwriteSchema option set to true.
* [Liquid tables](/concepts/liquid-tables.md) - Delta Lake tables that support clustering columns and impose restrictions on certain commands requiring predicates that reference only clustering columns.
* [Liquid tables (Databricks)](/concepts/liquid-tables-databricks.md) - A Databricks table type that supports clustering columns and restricts certain command predicates to only reference those clustering columns.
* [List of Dictionaries Evaluation Pattern](/concepts/list-of-dictionaries-evaluation-pattern.md) - Using a simple list of Python dictionaries for quick prototyping and small-scale GenAI evaluation without formal dataset creation.
* [LitTwoTower: Lightning Module Wrapping TwoTowerModel](/concepts/littwotower-lightning-module-wrapping-twotowermodel.md) - A PyTorch Lightning module that wraps the TwoTowerModel with BCE loss, AUROC metrics, and batch transformation from DataFrame rows to TorchRec Batch objects.
* [LiveSpan Input/Output Management](/concepts/livespan-inputoutput-management.md) - Manual specification of span inputs and outputs via LiveSpan.set_inputs() and LiveSpan.set_outputs() when using context managers, unlike automatic capture with function decorators.
* [Llama 3.2 1B Fine-tuning on Databricks](/concepts/llama-32-1b-fine-tuning-on-databricks.md) - A reference workflow for fully fine-tuning the Llama 3.2 1B Instruct model on 8xH100 GPUs using AI Runtime, DeepSpeed, and TRL, with bfloat16 precision and gradient checkpointing.
* [LLM Endpoint Benchmarking Harness](/concepts/llm-endpoint-benchmarking-harness.md) - A systematic approach to benchmarking LLM endpoints by measuring total latency, throughput, and the throughput-versus-latency curve across varying numbers of parallel requests.
* [LLM Endpoint Benchmarking Methodology](/concepts/llm-endpoint-benchmarking-methodology.md) - A Databricks-recommended approach using a benchmarking harness notebook that measures total latency vs. throughput across varying concurrency levels to characterize endpoint performance.
* [LLM endpoint governance](/concepts/llm-endpoint-governance.md) - Controlling access to hosted and external LLM endpoints, enforcing rate limits, and tracking usage and costs across providers via Unity AI Gateway.
* [LLM Endpoint Latency vs Throughput Trade-off](/concepts/llm-endpoint-latency-vs-throughput-trade-off.md) - The balancing act in LLM serving systems where increasing concurrent requests raises throughput at the cost of higher latency, controlled by managing parallel request load.
* [LLM Evaluation Dataset Schema](/concepts/llm-evaluation-dataset-schema.md) - The structured dataset format used in mlflow.genai.evaluate, requiring inputs, outputs, and expectations (with context) fields
* [LLM Evaluation Quality Dimensions](/concepts/llm-evaluation-quality-dimensions.md) - Specific quality aspects evaluated by LLM judges including relevance, retrieval relevance, safety, groundedness, correctness, sufficiency, guidelines compliance, tool call correctness, and tool call efficiency.
* [LLM Fine-tuning in Production](/concepts/llm-fine-tuning-in-production.md) - Fine-tuning existing LLMs on custom data to improve performance for specific scenarios, represented as distinct pipeline jobs in production architecture
* [LLM fine-tuning on Databricks](/concepts/llm-fine-tuning-on-databricks.md) - Notebook examples for fine-tuning large language models on AI Runtime, including parameter-efficient methods.
* [LLM Foundry YAML Configuration](/concepts/llm-foundry-yaml-configuration.md) - A structured YAML-based configuration system for LLM Foundry that defines model architecture, FSDP settings, training hyperparameters, dataset configuration, MLflow logging, callbacks, and checkpointing in a declarative format.
* [LLM Function Calling](/concepts/llm-function-calling.md) - A technique that lets LLMs generate structured JSON output describing function arguments, enabling reliable integration with external APIs and code.
* [LLM Inference Latency](/concepts/llm-inference-latency.md) - Total response time calculated as TTFT plus the product of TPOT and number of generated tokens, representing end-to-end user-perceived delay.
* [LLM Inference Phases: Prefill and Decoding](/concepts/llm-inference-prefill-and-decoding.md) - LLMs perform inference in two distinct phases: prefill (parallel processing of input tokens) and decoding (auto-regressive generation of output tokens one at a time).
* [LLM Inference Prefill and Decoding](/concepts/llm-inference-prefill-and-decoding.md) - The two-step process by which LLMs perform inference: prefill processing input tokens in parallel, followed by auto-regressive decoding that generates one token at a time.
* [LLM Inference Two-Step Process: Prefill and Decoding](/concepts/llm-inference-prefill-and-decoding.md) - LLM inference consists of two sequential phases: parallel prompt processing (prefill) followed by auto-regressive token generation (decoding).
* [LLM Judge Customization](/concepts/llm-judge-customization.md) - The ability to change the underlying model that powers a built-in LLM judge by specifying a different model via the 'model' argument using LiteLLM-compatible format.
* [LLM Judge Customization Paths](/concepts/llm-judge-customization-paths.md) - Alternatives to built-in judges, including custom LLM judges for more control and Python code-based scorers, enabling users to tailor evaluation to their specific use cases.
* [LLM Judge Model Customization](/concepts/llm-judge-model-customization.md) - The ability to change the underlying model powering a guidelines judge using the model argument with a provider/model-name format compatible with LiteLLM.
* [LLM Judge Model Infrastructure and Privacy](/concepts/llm-judge-model-infrastructure-and-privacy.md) - LLM judges for guidelines evaluation use specially-tuned models hosted by region (EU or US), support custom model selection via LiteLLM-compatible providers, and have privacy controls including opt-out of Abuse Monitoring and partner-powered AI feature disabling.
* [LLM Judge Model Trust and Data Privacy](/concepts/llm-judge-model-trust-and-data-privacy.md) - Operational and trust considerations for LLM judges, including third-party model hosting (Azure OpenAI), regional data residency, abuse monitoring opt-out, and restrictions on using judge outputs for model training.
* [LLM Judge Model Trust and Privacy](/concepts/llm-judge-model-trust-and-privacy.md) - Information about the models powering LLM judges, including Azure OpenAI hosting, regional data residency, opt-out of abuse monitoring, and the ability to disable partner-powered models.
* [LLM Judges](/concepts/llm-judges.md) - A type of MLflow Scorer that uses Large Language Models to assess quality across dimensions like relevance, safety, and correctness.
* [LLM Judges and Scorers](/concepts/llm-judges-and-scorers.md) - Built-in or custom evaluation components used to assess LLM and agent outputs, reusable across both development testing and production monitoring.
* [LLM Model Selection for Conversation Simulation](/concepts/llm-model-selection-for-conversation-simulation.md) - Configuring which LLM powers the simulated user in conversation simulation, supporting provider-prefixed model names and regional hosting constraints.
* [LLM Throughput vs Latency Trade-off](/concepts/llm-throughput-vs-latency-trade-off.md) - The fundamental tension in LLM serving where increasing concurrent requests boosts throughput but also raises latency, requiring careful load control based on use case.
* [LLM-as-a-Judge](/concepts/llm-as-a-judge.md) - The pattern of using a large language model to evaluate the quality of outputs from another LLM-based system, serving as an automated evaluator for dimensions like relevance and safety.
* [LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation](/concepts/llm-as-a-judge-evaluation.md) - Using an LLM to automatically evaluate the quality of another LLM's outputs based on criteria like safety, correctness, and custom guidelines.
* [LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation Metrics](/concepts/llm-as-a-judge-evaluation-metrics.md) - DeepEval provides LLM-based evaluation metrics that require a model parameter to act as the judge for scoring outputs
* [LLM-as-a-Judge for Relevance](/concepts/llm-as-a-judge-for-relevance.md) - The pattern of using a configurable LLM (defaulting to a Databricks-hosted model) to automatically score the relevance of responses and retrieved documents in GenAI applications.
* [LLM-as-a-Judge for Relevance Evaluation](/concepts/llm-as-a-judge-for-relevance-evaluation.md) - The paradigm of using a secondary LLM (judge model) to automatically assess the relevance of outputs or retrieved documents in GenAI applications, as implemented by MLflow's built-in scorers.
* [LLM-as-a-judge in MLflow evaluation](/concepts/llm-as-a-judge-in-mlflow-evaluation.md) - Pattern where a scorer integrates a custom or externally hosted LLM to evaluate response quality, using structured prompts and parsing the LLM's JSON output into Feedback objects with AssessmentSource metadata.
* [LLM-as-a-Judge Paradigm](/concepts/llm-as-a-judge-paradigm.md) - The pattern of using large language models themselves as evaluators to assess quality dimensions of other LLM outputs, as implemented by Databricks-hosted judge models.
* [LLM-as-a-Judge Scoring](/concepts/llm-as-a-judge-scoring.md) - DeepEval scorers use an LLM-as-a-judge approach where a language model (e.g., databricks-gpt-5-mini or gpt-4o) evaluates outputs against configurable thresholds.
* [LLM-as-judge evaluation paradigm](/concepts/llm-as-judge-evaluation-paradigm.md) - The methodology of using one LLM to evaluate the outputs of another LLM-powered application, scoring along quality dimensions such as relevance, safety, groundedness, and correctness.
* [LLM-based vs Non-LLM RAGAS Scorers](/concepts/llm-based-vs-non-llm-ragas-scorers.md) - RAGAS scorers divide into LLM-based metrics that require a model parameter (e.g., Faithfulness) and non-LLM deterministic metrics that need no model (e.g., ExactMatch).
* [LLMOps vs MLOps](/concepts/llmops-vs-mlops.md) - Key differences and commonalities between traditional MLOps and LLMOps workflows for large language models
* [Load Test Cluster Configuration on Databricks](/concepts/load-test-cluster-configuration-on-databricks.md) - Recommended compute configuration for running Locust-based load tests: single-node cluster with Databricks Runtime 15.4 LTS ML and a CPU-optimized instance type with at least 32 cores.
* [Load Test Payload Specification](/concepts/load-test-payload-specification.md) - Guidelines for choosing representative payloads in load tests to accurately reflect production usage patterns and validate them via the Serving UI query interface
* [Load Testing for ML Serving Endpoints](/concepts/load-testing-for-ml-serving-endpoints.md) - The practice of simulating real-world traffic on model serving infrastructure to measure performance, identify bottlenecks, and correctly size endpoints for production workloads.
* [Load Testing for Model Serving Endpoints](/concepts/load-testing-for-model-serving-endpoints.md) - Testing methodology that measures endpoint performance under realistic traffic conditions to determine optimal provisioned concurrency, identify bottlenecks, and validate latency and throughput requirements.
* [load_context and predict Model Lifecycle](/concepts/load_context-and-predict-model-lifecycle.md) - Custom MLflow pyfunc models require two functions: load_context for one-time initialization and predict for per-request inference logic.
* [Loading and Using Prompts from the Registry](/concepts/loading-and-using-prompts-from-the-registry.md) - Prompts can be loaded by name and version using URI syntax (prompts:/catalog.schema.name/version) or explicitly, then formatted with variables and used in LLM applications.
* [Loading and Using Prompts in Applications](/concepts/loading-and-using-prompts-in-applications.md) - The pattern of loading prompt versions from the registry using URI syntax (prompts:/catalog.schema.name/version) and formatting them with runtime variables for use in LLM applications.
* [Loading Data for Machine Learning on Databricks](/concepts/loading-data-for-machine-learning-on-databricks.md) - Best practices and patterns for loading data specifically for ML and DL workloads on Databricks, as distinct from general data ingestion.
* [Loading Fine-tuned Models for Inference via MLflow](/concepts/loading-fine-tuned-models-for-inference-via-mlflow.md) - Loading logged Hugging Face models from MLflow as Spark UDFs for batch inference on Databricks
* [Loading MLflow experiments by ID](/concepts/loading-mlflow-experiments-by-id.md) - Loading MLflow experiment run data into a Spark DataFrame by passing one or more experiment IDs as a comma-separated string to the mlflow-experiment data source.
