* [Loading MLflow experiments by name](/concepts/loading-mlflow-experiments-by-name.md) - Resolving an MLflow experiment name to an ID using mlflow.get_experiment_by_name() and then passing that ID to the mlflow-experiment data source.
* [Loading Parquet Files Directly with Petastorm](/concepts/loading-parquet-files-directly-with-petastorm.md) - The alternative (less preferred) workflow for using Petastorm by saving data in Parquet format to DBFS and loading it via the DBFS mount point, bypassing the Spark Converter API.
* [Loading TFRecords into Spark DataFrames](/concepts/loading-tfrecords-into-spark-dataframes.md) - The process of reading TFRecord files into Apache Spark DataFrames using the spark-tensorflow-connector library.
* [Local Development Environment Integration with Databricks MLflow](/concepts/local-development-environment-integration-with-databricks-mlflow.md) - The workflow for connecting a local IDE (VS Code, PyCharm, Cursor, Jupyter) to a Databricks workspace for MLflow experiment tracking, using environment variables and authentication tokens.
* [Local Model Serving Simulation](/concepts/local-model-serving-simulation.md) - Manual approach to test model serving behavior by loading the model on an all-purpose cluster or local PC with a matching environment before endpoint deployment.
* [Local vs Databricks Environment Constraints for Feature Engineering](/concepts/local-vs-databricks-environment-constraints-for-feature-engineering.md) - The limitation that the client library can only run on Databricks (Runtime or ML Runtime) for actual API calls, but can be installed locally for code development, IDE support, and unit testing.
* [Local vs Remote Execution Model in Databricks Connect](/concepts/local-vs-remote-execution-model-in-databricks-connect.md) - The execution model where all Python code runs locally while PySpark DataFrame operations run on the remote Databricks cluster, with responses sent back to the local caller.
* [LOCATION_NOT_SUPPORTED](/concepts/location_not_supported.md) - Sub-error indicating that specifying a LOCATION is not supported when deep cloning a streaming table because the cloned table uses managed storage.
* [LOCATION_NOT_SUPPORTED sub-error](/concepts/location_not_supported.md) - Sub-error of DELTA_DEEP_CLONE_STREAMING_TABLE_ERROR indicating that specifying a custom LOCATION is forbidden because the cloned streaming table uses managed storage.
* [Locust CPU Resource Constraints](/concepts/locust-cpu-resource-constraints.md) - Understanding that Locust performance is CPU-bound (roughly 4000 requests per second per CPU core) and that auto-detection of CPU cores via the --processes -1 flag maximizes throughput
* [Locust Load Testing for ML Endpoints](/concepts/locust-load-testing-for-ml-endpoints.md) - Using the open-source Locust framework to run distributed load tests against Databricks Model Serving endpoints, configured to auto-detect CPU cores for maximum request throughput.
* [Locust Load Testing Framework](/concepts/locust-load-testing-framework.md) - An open-source framework for load testing production-grade endpoints, capable of modifying connection count and spawn rate while measuring endpoint performance.
* [Locust Load Testing Framework for Model Serving](/concepts/locust-load-testing-framework-on-databricks.md) - Using the open-source Locust framework to run automated, parameterized load tests against Databricks Model Serving endpoints, with support for custom concurrency, spawn rates, and CPU-aware parallel execution.
* [Locust Load Testing Framework on Databricks](/concepts/locust-load-testing-framework-on-databricks.md) - Using the open-source Locust framework to run automated, configurable load tests against Databricks Model Serving endpoints, supporting parameter tuning for client connections, spawn rates, and multi-CPU utilization.
* [Log Probability for Chat Completion](/concepts/log-probability-for-chat-completion.md) - The logprobs parameter for chat completion tasks that provides token log probabilities for classification, model uncertainty assessment, and evaluation metrics.
* [Logged Model Evaluation with MLflow](/concepts/logged-model-evaluation-with-mlflow.md) - Loading logged MLflow models with pyfunc and wrapping them for evaluation using mlflow.genai.evaluate() with named parameters.
* [Logged Models](/concepts/logged-models.md) - A concept in MLflow 3 that tracks a model's progress throughout its lifecycle by persisting metadata, metrics, parameters, and artifacts across environments and runs for comparison and debugging.
* [LoggedModel](/concepts/loggedmodel.md) - MLflow abstraction that packages model artifacts together with metrics, parameters, and lineage; when registered to Unity Catalog, all metadata becomes queryable in the UI and API.
* [Long-Term Trace Analysis and Dashboards](/concepts/long-term-trace-analysis-and-dashboards.md) - Archiving traces to Delta tables enables building custom dashboards, performing in-depth analytics on trace data, and maintaining a durable record of application behavior beyond ephemeral tracking.
* [Lookback window for feature lookups](/concepts/lookback-window-for-feature-lookups.md) - A timedelta parameter in FeatureLookup that excludes feature values older than a specified duration from the training set, applied during training and batch inference but not during online serving.
* [Lookup table optimization for ABAC policy UDFs](/concepts/lookup-table-optimization-for-abac-policy-udfs.md) - Using small lookup tables in policy UDFs enables broadcast hash joins where the lookup table is copied to each executor for fast filtering, but large lookup tables or complex predicates force slower shuffle joins.
* [Lookup tables in ABAC policy UDFs](/concepts/lookup-tables-in-abac-policy-udfs.md) - Using small lookup tables to express user-specific access rules that cannot be captured by the TO/EXCEPT clauses alone, leveraging broadcast hash joins for performance.
* [LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)](/concepts/lora-low-rank-adaptation.md) - A parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique that trains small low-rank adapter matrices while freezing the base model weights, drastically reducing the number of trainable parameters.
* [LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) for Efficient Finetuning](/concepts/lora-low-rank-adaptation.md) - Parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique that adapts pre-trained models by training low-rank matrices targeting specific attention modules (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, etc.), reducing memory footprint.
* [LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) for LLM fine-tuning](/concepts/lora-low-rank-adaptation.md) - Parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique that trains small adapter layers while freezing the base model to reduce compute and memory requirements.
* [LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) for LLMs](/concepts/lora-low-rank-adaptation.md) - A parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique that trains small rank-decomposition matrices on targeted modules (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, etc.) while keeping the base model frozen, supported by Unsloth.
* [LoRA Adapter Merging and Model Export](/concepts/lora-adapter-merging-and-model-export.md) - Process of merging learned LoRA adapter weights into the base model (merge_and_unload) and exporting both tokenizer and merged model for inference and registration.
* [LoRA Adapter Merging Pattern](/concepts/lora-adapter-merging-pattern.md) - The process of merging a trained LoRA adapter with the base model to produce a standalone combined model for deployment
* [LoRA Adapter Merging Workflow](/concepts/lora-adapter-merging-workflow.md) - The process of loading a trained LoRA adapter, merging it with the base model using merge_and_unload(), and registering the combined model for deployment
* [LoRA Configuration Parameters](/concepts/lora-configuration-parameters.md) - Key hyperparameters for configuring LoRA including rank (r=8) balancing performance vs parameters, alpha (32) as scaling factor, dropout (0.1) for regularization, and target module selection for attention and MLP layers.
* [LoRA Finetuning with Unsloth on Llama 3.2](/concepts/lora-finetuning-with-unsloth-on-llama-32.md) - The specific recipe for applying Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct using Unsloth's FastLanguageModel API, including hyperparameters like r=16, lora_alpha=16, and target module selection.
* [Low Shuffle Merge](/concepts/low-shuffle-merge.md) - An optimized implementation of MERGE in Delta Lake that provides better performance for most common workloads and preserves existing data layout optimizations like liquid clustering on unmodified data
* [Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA)](/concepts/low-rank-adaptation-lora.md) - A parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique that adds small, trainable rank-decomposition matrices to attention and feed-forward layers while keeping the base model frozen, reducing memory and compute requirements.
* [Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) for Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning](/concepts/low-rank-adaptation-lora.md) - A technique that reduces the number of trainable parameters by adding small adapter layers to a frozen base model, making fine-tuning of large language models more memory and compute efficient.
* [M2M (Machine-to-Machine) Authentication Flow](/concepts/machine-to-machine-m2m-authentication.md) - Authentication flow for automated workloads using service principals registered in the recipient's IdP, using the OAuth Client Credentials Grant flow with no user interaction required.
* [Machine-to-Machine (M2M) Authentication](/concepts/machine-to-machine-m2m-authentication.md) - Authentication flow for automated workloads where a service principal registered in the recipient's IdP enables applications or scripts to access shared data programmatically using the OAuth Client Credentials Grant flow without user interaction.
* [Machine-to-Machine (M2M) Authentication Flow](/concepts/machine-to-machine-m2m-authentication.md) - OIDC authentication flow for automated workloads where a service principal registered in the IdP programmatically accesses shared data using the OAuth Client Credentials Grant, with no user interaction required.
* [MANAGE ALLOWLIST Privilege](/concepts/manage-allowlist-privilege.md) - A Unity Catalog privilege required to add or remove items from the allowlist; recommended to be granted only to metastore admins and trusted platform administrators.
* [MANAGE Permission in Unity Catalog](/concepts/manage-permission-in-unity-catalog.md) - A privilege required for all policy operations (create, edit, delete, describe) on securable objects in Unity Catalog, distinct from ownership but an alternative requirement.
* [MANAGE Permission Requirement](/concepts/manage-permission-requirement.md) - All ABAC policy operations (create, edit, delete, show, describe) require MANAGE permission on the securable object or object ownership, ensuring only authorized users can modify data access rules.
* [MANAGE Privilege](/concepts/manage-privilege.md) - A special privilege in Unity Catalog that allows users to manage (grant/revoke) privileges on an object without owning it, though currently MANAGE users cannot see all grants via INFORMATION_SCHEMA.
* [MANAGE Privilege for Data Quality Monitoring Alerts](/concepts/manage-privilege-for-data-quality-monitoring-alerts.md) - The MANAGE privilege on a schema or catalog is required to create or manage alert rules for data quality monitoring in Unity Catalog.
* [MANAGE privilege in Unity Catalog](/concepts/manage-privilege-in-unity-catalog.md) - A Unity Catalog privilege that allows users to assign/revoke privileges, transfer ownership, and delete an object without being the owner
* [Managed and Foreign Iceberg Table Sharing](/concepts/foreign-iceberg-table-sharing.md) - Sharing managed Iceberg tables (native to Databricks) and foreign Iceberg tables (federated from external Iceberg catalogs) with Delta Uniform support, history sharing, and specific limitations.
* [Managed assets in Unity Catalog](/concepts/managed-assets-in-unity-catalog.md) - Data assets (tables and volumes) where Unity Catalog controls both governance and the underlying file storage lifecycle
* [Managed Iceberg Table Clone Limitations](/concepts/managed-iceberg-table-cloning.md) - Managed Iceberg tables support only deep cloning and cannot be shallow cloned or have their table format changed during cloning.
* [Managed Iceberg Table Clone Restrictions](/concepts/managed-iceberg-table-cloning.md) - Managed Iceberg tables support only deep cloning (not shallow), and the table format cannot be changed during cloning.
* [Managed Iceberg Table Cloning](/concepts/managed-iceberg-table-cloning.md) - Managed Iceberg tables on Databricks support only deep cloning, not shallow cloning, and the table format cannot be changed during the clone operation.
* [Managed MLflow on Databricks](/concepts/managed-mlflow-on-databricks.md) - Enterprise extension of open source MLflow with governance, managed hosting, production scaling, and integration with Databricks lakehouse and Unity Catalog
* [Managed Storage for Streaming Tables](/concepts/managed-storage-for-streaming-tables.md) - Databricks-managed storage location for streaming tables, where users cannot specify a custom LOCATION during deep clone
* [Managed Storage in Unity Catalog](/concepts/managed-storage-in-unity-catalog.md) - The S3 bucket storage location, configurable at metastore, catalog, or schema level, where Unity Catalog stores managed tables and managed volumes.
* [Managed storage location](/concepts/managed-storage-location.md) - The storage location within a user's cloud account that Unity Catalog controls for organizing, optimizing, and deleting data files of managed assets
* [Managed table requirement for Uniform Iceberg](/concepts/managed-tables-requirement-for-icebergcompat.md) - The IcebergCompatV feature can only be enabled on Managed Tables in Databricks, not on external or unmanaged tables.
* [Managed Tables in Databricks](/concepts/managed-tables-in-databricks.md) - A table type requirement for enabling IcebergCompatV; the feature can only be enabled on managed tables, not external or unmanaged tables.
* [Managed Tables in Unity Catalog](/concepts/managed-tables-in-unity-catalog.md) - Tables whose lifecycle, file layout, storage location, and performance optimization are fully managed by Unity Catalog; always use Delta format and reside in reserved managed storage.
* [Managed Tables requirement for IcebergCompat](/concepts/managed-tables-requirement-for-icebergcompat.md) - IcebergCompatV can only be enabled on managed Delta tables, not external or unmanaged tables.
* [Managed to External Table Rollback](/concepts/managed-to-external-table-rollback.md) - The operation of reverting a Unity Catalog managed table back to an external table, which is time-limited and has specific failure modes.
* [Managed to External Table Rollback in Delta Lake](/concepts/managed-to-external-table-rollback.md) - The operation of converting a Unity Catalog managed table back to an external table via ALTER TABLE UNSET MANAGED, which has constraints and failure modes.
* [Managed to External Table Rollback in Unity Catalog](/concepts/managed-to-external-table-rollback.md) - The operation of reverting a Unity Catalog managed table back to an external table, which may fail under certain conditions
* [Managed vs External Assets in Unity Catalog](/concepts/managed-vs-external-assets-in-unity-catalog.md) - Fundamental distinction between managed objects (Unity Catalog controls storage lifecycle) and external objects (user controls storage and lifecycle) in Unity Catalog.
* [Managed vs External tables in Unity Catalog](/concepts/managed-vs-external-tables-in-unity-catalog.md) - Distinction between tables where Unity Catalog controls storage location and file lifecycle versus tables where the user specifies the storage location
* [Managed vs External volumes in Unity Catalog](/concepts/managed-vs-external-volumes-in-unity-catalog.md) - Distinction between volumes where Unity Catalog determines the storage location and manages file lifecycle versus volumes where the user specifies the storage location
* [Managing MLflow Experiments: Permissions, Rename, Delete](/concepts/managing-mlflow-experiments-permissions-rename-delete.md) - Experiment owners can rename, delete, or change permissions from either the Experiments page or the experiment details page. Notebook experiments are tied to notebooks — deleting one deletes both. Git-folder notebook experiments must be managed at the Git folder level.
* [Managing MLflow Runs](/concepts/managing-mlflow-runs.md) - Capabilities to delete, compare, and tag MLflow runs from the chart view interface for experiment organization.
* [Managing Reasoning Across Multi-Turn Conversations](/concepts/managing-reasoning-across-multi-turn-conversations.md) - Technique for handling reasoning blocks in multi-turn conversations where only the last assistant turn's reasoning is visible to the model; optionally omitting or including reasoning blocks depending on whether the model needs to reason over prior steps.
* [Manual Delta log tampering](/concepts/manual-delta-log-tampering.md) - Manually deleting or removing files from the Delta transaction log directory can corrupt the table by creating version gaps.
* [Manual Iceberg Metadata Conversion (MSCK REPAIR TABLE SYNC METADATA)](/concepts/manual-iceberg-metadata-conversion-msck-repair-table-sync-metadata.md) - A synchronous operation that manually triggers Iceberg metadata generation for the latest Delta Lake table version, used when automatic generation fails due to cluster termination, errors, or writes from non-UniForm clients.
* [Manual Library Installation on Databricks](/concepts/manual-library-installation-on-databricks.md) - The fallback option to continue using a library after it has been removed from Databricks Runtime ML by installing it manually or using an earlier runtime version.
* [Manual MLflow Logging with Hyperopt Trials](/concepts/manual-mlflow-logging-with-hyperopt-trials.md) - When using the Trials class with distributed training algorithms on Databricks, automatic MLflow logging is not supported; users must manually call MLflow to log trials.
* [Manual OAuth token fetch with authorization_details](/concepts/manual-oauth-token-fetch-with-authorization_details.md) - The process of manually requesting an OAuth token from the OIDC token endpoint with an authorization_details payload specifying the endpoint ID, object type, and action permission.
* [Manual Span Instrumentation in LangGraph Nodes](/concepts/manual-span-instrumentation-in-langgraph-nodes.md) - Adding child spans inside LangGraph nodes or tools using mlflow.start_span() to gain detailed per-step insights
* [Manual Tracing](/concepts/manual-tracing.md) - An MLflow tracing approach designed for custom logic and complex workflows, offering Function Decorator APIs and low-level APIs for fine-grained control over what gets traced.
* [Manual Tracing (MLflow)](/concepts/manual-tracing.md) - Approach for tracing custom logic and complex workflows using Function Decorator APIs or low-level APIs for fine-grained control.
* [Manual Tracing APIs](/concepts/manual-tracing-apis.md) - Four manual tracing approaches: trace() for functions, @trace decorator for class methods, withSpan() for code blocks, and explicit startSpan/endSpan for full lifecycle control.
* [Manual Tracing for MLflow](/concepts/manual-tracing.md) - Manual approach to tracing designed for custom logic and complex workflows using function decorators or low-level APIs
* [Manual UDF Dependency Management with withDependencies](/concepts/manual-udf-dependency-management-with-withdependencies.md) - Databricks Connect allows explicit specification of Python dependencies for UDFs via the withDependencies() API, supporting PyPI packages, Unity Catalog volumes, and local packages.
* [Mapping Table Pattern for Access Control](/concepts/mapping-table-pattern-for-access-control.md) - A single lookup table that drives both row-level filtering and column-level masking, replacing complex group-based approaches with row updates for access changes.
* [Mapping Tables for Access Control](/concepts/mapping-tables-for-access-control.md) - A pattern using dedicated mapping tables (access-control lists) to encode which users or groups can access specific rows, integrated via row filters that join against the mapping table.
* [Marian Transformer for Machine Translation on Databricks](/concepts/marian-transformer-for-machine-translation-on-databricks.md) - A practical example of using Spark NLP's Marian Transformer for batch machine translation on Databricks clusters, including pipeline construction and inference.
* [MATCH COLUMNS with AND conditions](/concepts/match-columns-with-and-conditions.md) - Using logical AND inside MATCH COLUMNS to target columns that satisfy multiple tag criteria simultaneously, enabling fine-grained policy targeting across tag dimensions.
* [Materialization Trigger Types](/concepts/materialization-trigger-types.md) - CronSchedule for periodic materialization of aggregation features and TableTrigger for incremental materialization of column-selection features
* [Materialization Triggers](/concepts/materialization-triggers.md) - CronSchedule (for aggregation features, scheduled via Quartz cron) and TableTrigger (for ColumnSelection features, triggered on upstream Delta table commits) control when materialization pipelines run.
* [materialize_features() API](/concepts/materialize_features-api.md) - API function that materializes a list of registered declarative features into offline Delta tables and/or an Online Feature Store, controlled by a trigger (CronSchedule or TableTrigger).
* [Materialized Feature Pipeline Scheduling](/concepts/materialized-feature-pipeline-scheduling.md) - Mechanisms for scheduling feature materialization jobs using CronSchedule (time-based, for aggregation features) and TableTrigger (event-driven, for column selection features), with support for both offline and online store configurations.
* [Materialized Features (Declarative)](/concepts/materialized-features-declarative.md) - Precomputed feature representations in Unity Catalog produced from declarative feature definitions via Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines, used for model training, batch scoring, and online serving.
* [Materialized views in Databricks](/concepts/materialized-views-in-databricks.md) - Pre-computed views that refresh on a schedule or on demand, one of the two table types supported for external metadata.
* [MaterializedFeaturePipelineSchedule](/concepts/materializedfeaturepipelineschedule.md) - Configuration for scheduling feature materialization pipelines using CronSchedule (for aggregation features) or TableTrigger (for column selection features), with ACTIVE/PAUSED state management.
* [Maven Coordinate Allowlisting](/concepts/maven-coordinate-allowlisting.md) - The process of adding Maven artifacts to the Unity Catalog allowlist using groupId:artifactId:version format, supporting wildcard patterns for all versions or all artifacts in a group.
* [Maven Coordinates in Allowlist](/concepts/maven-coordinate-allowlisting.md) - The ability to add Maven coordinates to the Unity Catalog allowlist using groupId:artifactId:version format, with support for wildcards to include all versions or all artifacts in a group.
* [MCP Client Configuration for Databricks](/concepts/mcp-client-configuration-for-databricks.md) - Configuration patterns for connecting MCP-compatible clients (VS Code, Cursor, Claude Desktop) to the MLflow MCP server with Databricks authentication
* [MCP Server Governance](/concepts/mcp-server-governance.md) - Managing access to managed, external, and custom MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers alongside LLM endpoints through the Unity AI Gateway.
* [MCP server governance in Unity AI Gateway](/concepts/mcp-server-governance.md) - Management of access to managed, external, and custom MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers alongside LLM endpoints through Unity AI Gateway.
* [MDS (Mosaic Data Shard) Format](/concepts/mds-mosaic-data-shard-format.md) - A storage format produced by converting Apache Spark DataFrames using Mosaic Streaming, optimized for distributed deep learning training with support for compression and hashing.
* [Medallion Architecture](/concepts/medallion-architecture.md) - A data organization pattern that structures data into bronze (raw), silver (refined), and gold (business-ready) layers to progressively improve data quality as it flows through a lakehouse pipeline.
* [MemAlign Optimizer](/concepts/memalign-optimizer.md) - The default alignment optimizer used by MLflow's judge alignment system, automatically applied when calling align() without specifying an optimizer.
* [Memory Efficiency in Distributed Training](/concepts/memory-efficiency-in-distributed-training.md) - The comparative memory efficiency of FSDP over DDP as a key consideration for choosing training strategies
* [MERGE INTO (Delta Lake)](/concepts/merge-into-delta-lake.md) - Alternative SQL statement used instead of unsupported UPDATE...FROM...JOIN syntax to update a table based on a join with another table or subquery.
* [Merge/Update/Delete restrictions on archived Delta data](/concepts/mergeupdatedelete-restrictions-on-archived-delta-data.md) - MERGE, UPDATE, and DELETE operations fail if they impact data in archived files; files must be restored first.
* [mergeSchema Option](/concepts/mergeschema-option.md) - A Spark/Delta Lake DataFrameWriter option that enables automatic schema migration by merging the provided schema with the existing table schema during writes.
* [mergeSchema Option Pattern](/concepts/mergeschema-option.md) - A Delta Lake DataFrameWriter option (.option('mergeSchema', 'true')) used to enable automatic schema migration when writing, allowing new columns to be added to the table.
* [Metadata Column Access in Unity Catalog](/concepts/metadata-column-access-in-unity-catalog.md) - Using _metadata.file_name and _metadata.file_path as an alternative to input_file_name() for shared clusters in Unity Catalog.
* [Metadata Immutability in MLflow Traces](/concepts/metadata-immutability-in-mlflow-traces.md) - The constraint that trace metadata is immutable once set — attempting to update an existing metadata key is silently ignored.
* [METADATA ONLY mode](/concepts/metadata-only-mode.md) - FSCK REPAIR TABLE mode that only checks metadata integrity without verifying actual data file contents
* [METADATA ONLY verification](/concepts/metadata-only-verification.md) - A verification mode for FSCK REPAIR TABLE that checks only the Delta transaction log metadata (checkpoint CRCs, file listings) without examining actual data file contents.
* [Metadata Quality and Discoverability](/concepts/metadata-quality-and-discoverability.md) - The principle that the quality of admin-side metadata directly determines how useful the data discovery experience is for users.
* [Metadata Quality and Discovery Experience](/concepts/metadata-quality-and-discovery-experience.md) - The quality of admin-side metadata directly determines how useful the discovery experience is for users; consistent certification standards and governed tag vocabularies enable confident filtering and searching.
* [Metadata upgrade scope](/concepts/metadata-upgrade-scope.md) - The set of feature table metadata upgraded to Unity Catalog: primary keys, time series columns, comments, tags, and notebook/job lineage.
* [Metadata vs Tags Immutability in MLflow Traces](/concepts/metadata-vs-tags-immutability-in-mlflow-traces.md) - A key distinction: once a trace is logged, metadata fields are immutable (ideal for stable identifiers like user and session IDs), while tags remain mutable (suitable for updatable annotations).
* [METADATA_CLEANUP_ERROR](/concepts/metadata_cleanup_error.md) - A specific sub-error under DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_TABLE_NOT_MIGRATABLE indicating failure to create a checkpoint or clean up old metadata files before migration.
* [METADATA_CLEANUP_ERROR sub-error](/concepts/metadata_cleanup_error.md) - A specific sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_TABLE_NOT_MIGRATABLE indicating failure to create checkpoint or clean up old metadata files before migrating the table.
* [Metastore](/concepts/metastore.md) - The top-level securable object in Unity Catalog that contains all registered objects (catalogs, credentials, shares, connections, etc.) within a single cloud region.
* [Metastore Admin (Databricks)](/concepts/metastore-admin-databricks.md) - Privileged role required to create, edit, or delete custom classifiers in Databricks Unity Catalog data classification.
* [Metastore Admin (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/metastore-admin-role.md) - An optional role governing data access, ownership, and top-level Unity Catalog securable objects within a single metastore, with privileges from both the role and ownership of the metastore.
* [Metastore admin ownership privileges](/concepts/metastore-admin-ownership-privileges.md) - Default and ownership-based privileges granted to metastore admins as owners of the metastore, including the ability to manage any object in the metastore.
* [Metastore Admin Role](/concepts/metastore-admin-role.md) - An optional, highly privileged Unity Catalog role that governs data access, ownership, and top-level securable objects; required for specific actions like changing ownership or removing default workspace admin permissions.
* [Metastore Admin Role (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/metastore-admin-role.md) - An optional but highly privileged Unity Catalog role that governs data access, ownership, and top-level securable objects within a metastore.
* [Metastore admin role assignment](/concepts/metastore-admin-role-assignment.md) - Process and considerations for assigning the metastore admin role via the account console, with recommendations to use groups and awareness of caching delays.
* [Metastore Admin Role in Unity Catalog](/concepts/metastore-admin-role-in-unity-catalog.md) - The administrative role automatically assigned to the creator of a metastore, with privileges to create catalogs and manage access, and a recommendation to reassign it to a group.
* [Metastore admins (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/metastore-admin-role.md) - Optional but highly privileged role governing data access, ownership, and top-level Unity Catalog securable objects within a single metastore.
* [Metastore Admins in Unity Catalog](/concepts/metastore-admin-role.md) - Optional but highly privileged role governing data access, ownership, and top-level Unity Catalog securable objects within a single metastore; needed for operations like changing ownership or removing default workspace admin permissions.
* [Metastore Connection Limit for SAP BDC](/concepts/metastore-connection-limit-for-sap-bdc.md) - A maximum of five SAP BDC connections can exist per Unity Catalog metastore.
* [Metastore Connection Limits](/concepts/metastore-connection-limits.md) - A limitation restricting the number of SAP BDC connections to five per metastore in Databricks
* [Metastore Connection Limits for SAP BDC](/concepts/metastore-connection-limit-for-sap-bdc.md) - A limit of five SAP BDC connections per Unity Catalog metastore, enforced as a hard upper bound.
* [Metastore Metadata Mismatch](/concepts/metastore-metadata-mismatch.md) - An error (convertMetastoreMetadataMismatchException) thrown when the catalog metadata differs from the Delta Lake transaction log metadata during conversion, which can be overridden with a configuration flag.
* [Metastore Metadata Mismatch in Delta Conversion](/concepts/metastore-metadata-mismatch.md) - An error condition (convertMetastoreMetadataMismatchException) that occurs when catalog metadata differs from the Delta Lake transaction log metadata during or after CONVERT TO DELTA.
* [Metastore Recipient Token Lifetime Configuration](/concepts/metastore-recipient-token-lifetime-configuration.md) - The default token lifetime setting for a Unity Catalog metastore that applies to new recipients when no explicit token lifetime is set, configurable only by account admins.
* [Metastore Storage Root Removal](/concepts/metastore-storage-root-removal.md) - The process of removing the metastore-level storage root to enforce catalog- or schema-level data isolation, which pushes the root location down to existing catalogs and requires new catalogs to specify their own storage location.
* [Metastore-Level Connection Limit](/concepts/metastore-level-connection-limit.md) - A single Unity Catalog metastore can have at most five SAP BDC connections.
* [Metastore-level managed storage](/concepts/metastore-level-managed-storage.md) - An optional S3 bucket used as the default storage location for managed tables and managed volumes in a Unity Catalog metastore, which can be overridden at catalog and schema levels.
* [Metastore-Level Quality Dashboard](/concepts/metastore-level-quality-dashboard.md) - A downloadable Lakeview dashboard template that provides a workspace-wide view of all data quality results across the metastore using the system.data_quality_monitoring.table_results table.
* [Metastore-Level Scoping of Custom Classifiers](/concepts/metastore-level-scoping-of-custom-classifiers.md) - Custom classifiers apply to all catalogs in the metastore that have Data Classification enabled; per-catalog or per-schema scoping is not supported, making this a metastore-wide governance mechanism.
* [Metastore-level SecureConnect enrollment](/concepts/metastore-level-secureconnect-enrollment.md) - A metastore administrator can configure a metastore so that all new recipients automatically use SecureConnect, controlled via Catalog Explorer settings.
* [Methods for Creating Model Serving Endpoints](/concepts/methods-for-creating-model-serving-endpoints.md) - Databricks Model Serving supports four creation methods: Serving UI, REST API, MLflow Deployments SDK, and Workspace Client.
* [Metric Backfill](/concepts/metric-backfill.md) - backfill_scorers() function to evaluate historical traces by applying scorers to past time windows, returning a job ID for tracking
* [Metric naming behavior](/concepts/metric-naming-behavior.md) - Rules for how scorer metric names are derived from function names, Feedback.name fields, and Scorer.name fields
* [Metric naming behavior in scorers](/concepts/metric-naming-behavior.md) - Rules for how metric names are derived from scorers: Feedback.name takes precedence over function/Scorer.name, and all metrics must have distinct names.
* [Metric naming conventions for MLflow scorers](/concepts/metric-naming-behavior.md) - Rules for how evaluation metric names are derived from scorers: function name for decorator-based scorers, Feedback.name for named feedback, class name for class-based scorers, with unique names required when returning multiple Feedback objects.
* [Metric naming conventions for scorers](/concepts/metric-naming-behavior.md) - Metric names in evaluation results are determined by Feedback.name fields (if present) or the scorer function/class name, with distinct naming requirements.
* [Metric naming in MLflow scorers](/concepts/metric-naming-behavior.md) - Rules for how metric names are derived from scorers, with Feedback.name fields taking precedence over function/class names, requiring distinct names for multiple feedbacks.
* [Metric Slicing Expressions](/concepts/metric-slicing-expressions.md) - A technique to define subsets of a table for targeted profiling, generating separate slices for each predicate and its complement, such as 'col_2 > 10' or unique values in a column.
* [Metric Table Location and Querying](/concepts/metric-table-location-and-querying.md) - Convention for naming and locating profile and drift metric tables using output schema and table name, along with direct SQL querying patterns.
* [Metric Tables (Databricks Data Profiling)](/concepts/metric-tables-databricks-data-profiling.md) - Unity Catalog tables created by data profiles that store computed statistics; can be queried via notebooks, SQL query explorer, or Catalog Explorer.
* [Metric Tables and Profile Outputs](/concepts/metric-tables-and-profile-outputs.md) - Unity Catalog tables automatically created by data profiling to store computed metrics, which can be queried via SQL, notebooks, or Catalog Explorer and controlled via Unity Catalog privileges.
* [Metric tables vs dashboard separation](/concepts/metric-tables-vs-dashboard-separation.md) - The architectural separation where profile updates write to metric tables, but the dashboard runs separate SQL queries over those tables, requiring independent refresh operations.
* [Metrics and Performance Analysis for GenAI Agents](/concepts/metrics-and-performance-analysis-for-genai-agents.md) - The ability to compute latency percentiles (P50/P95/P99), track error rates and throughput over time, analyze token usage patterns and costs, and compare performance across different time periods or filters.
* [Metrics and Performance Analysis via Genie Code](/concepts/metrics-and-performance-analysis-via-genie-code.md) - Genie Code capability to compute latency percentiles (P50/P95/P99), track error rates and throughput over time, analyze token usage patterns and costs, and compare performance across time periods or filters.
* [Migrating existing workspaces to Unity Catalog](/concepts/migrating-existing-workspaces-to-unity-catalog.md) - The recommended process of upgrading existing Databricks workspaces to Unity Catalog, including migrating Hive tables, DBFS files, notebooks, jobs, compute access modes, and Runtime versions.
* [Migrating External Distributed Training to Databricks](/concepts/migrating-external-distributed-training-to-databricks.md) - Process to import an external training repository as a Databricks Git folder and launch distributed training via TorchDistributor
* [Migrating from databricks-feature-store to databricks-feature-engineering](/concepts/migrating-from-databricks-feature-store-to-databricks-feature-engineering.md) - The migration path from the deprecated legacy package to the new unified package, requiring only a pip install change without code modifications.
* [Migrating from MLflow 2.x to MLflow 3](/concepts/migrating-from-mlflow-2x-to-mlflow-3.md) - Key differences and migration guide between MLflow 2.x and 3.0, including changes to model logging, artifact storage, and model registry.
* [Migrating LLM Serving Endpoints](/concepts/migrating-llm-serving-endpoints.md) - The process of updating existing Databricks LLM serving endpoints to use provisioned throughput by selecting a new model version, which automatically switches the UI to the new experience.
* [Migrating Single-Node to Distributed Deep Learning with Horovod](/concepts/migrating-single-node-to-distributed-deep-learning-with-horovod.md) - A six-step workflow for converting single-node deep learning code (TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch) to distributed training using Horovod, then wrapping it for HorovodRunner.
* [Migration from Databricks Agent Evaluation to MLflow 3](/concepts/migration-from-databricks-agent-evaluation-to-mlflow-3.md) - The process and code changes required to migrate from the legacy databricks.agents.evals API to the new mlflow.genai API.
* [Migration from databricks-feature-store to databricks-feature-engineering](/concepts/migration-from-databricks-feature-store-to-databricks-feature-engineering.md) - The process and compatibility notes for migrating from the deprecated databricks-feature-store package to the new databricks-feature-engineering package, with backward compatibility for import statements.
* [Migration from External Repositories to Databricks](/concepts/migration-of-external-repositories-to-databricks.md) - Process of importing existing distributed training code from external repos into Databricks Git folders and launching it with TorchDistributor
* [Migration from sparkdl.xgboost to xgboost.spark](/concepts/migration-from-sparkdlxgboost-to-xgboostspark.md) - Guide for replacing deprecated sparkdl.xgboost estimators with xgboost.spark equivalents, including parameter name changes (camelCase to snake_case) and removal of use_external_storage.
* [Migration of Databricks Connect from Runtime 12.2 LTS to 13.3 LTS and above](/concepts/migration-of-databricks-connect-from-runtime-122-lts-to-133-lts-and-above.md) - The process of upgrading a Python project or environment from Databricks Connect for Databricks Runtime 12.2 LTS and below to 13.3 LTS and above.
* [Migration of External Repositories to Databricks](/concepts/migration-of-external-repositories-to-databricks.md) - Process for importing external distributed training repositories as Databricks Git folders and launching them via TorchDistributor.
* [MirroredStrategyRunner](/concepts/mirroredstrategyrunner.md) - Spark-based runner for TensorFlow MirroredStrategy that coordinates distributed training across Spark executors
* [Missing data files and deletion vectors](/concepts/missing-data-files-and-deletion-vectors.md) - Detection of referenced data files or deletion vectors that are missing from storage in Delta Lake tables
* [Missing value handling in on-demand features](/concepts/missing-value-handling-in-on-demand-features.md) - Difference in how missing feature lookup values are returned depending on the environment: None for batch scoring vs float('nan') for online serving.
* [Missing Value Imputation](/concepts/missing-value-imputation.md) - Configurable strategies for imputing null values in AutoML, including default auto-detection and user-specified methods via UI or API.
* [Missing Value Imputation for Forecasting](/concepts/missing-value-imputation-for-forecasting.md) - AutoML allows specifying imputation methods for null values per column; defaults are chosen by column type and content, and custom imputation disables semantic type detection.
* [Missing Value Imputation in AutoML](/concepts/missing-value-imputation-in-automl.md) - Configurable strategies for handling null values in forecasting data, including default automatic selection based on column type and content.
* [Missing Value Imputation in AutoML Forecasting](/concepts/missing-value-imputation-for-forecasting.md) - How Databricks AutoML handles null values in forecasting datasets, including configurable imputation methods via UI or API, with automatic selection based on column type and content.
* [Missing Value Semantics in Spark XGBoost](/concepts/missing-value-semantics-in-spark-xgboost.md) - Difference in how the 'missing' parameter is handled in sparkdl.xgboost vs standard xgboost, particularly regarding sparse matrix zero values not being treated as missing by default.
* [MISSING_REQUIRED_TABLE_FEATURE](/concepts/missing_required_table_feature.md) - Error sub-type indicating a required table feature for IcebergCompatV1 is not supported or enabled
* [MISSING_REQUIRED_TABLE_FEATURE (Delta Iceberg error)](/concepts/missing_required_table_feature.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_V1_VIOLATION that occurs when a required table feature for IcebergCompatV1 is not enabled
* [MISSING_UNIFORM_TBL_PROPERTIES](/concepts/missing_uniform_tbl_properties.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_UNIFORM_ICEBERG_INGRESS_VIOLATION indicating required Delta table properties (tableId, snapshotId, metadataLocation) are missing, possibly due to manual changes to the _delta_log.
* [MISSING_UNIFORM_TBL_PROPERTIES error](/concepts/missing_uniform_tbl_properties.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_UNIFORM_ICEBERG_INGRESS_VIOLATION indicating that one or more required Delta Uniform properties (tableId, snapshotId, metadataLocation) are missing from the Delta table properties, possibly due to manual changes to _delta_log.
* [Mistral AI Python SDK](/concepts/mistral-ai-python-sdk.md) - The official Mistral AI Python client library (mistralai) that provides a chat completion API for interacting with Mistral's large language models.
* [Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Architecture](/concepts/mixture-of-experts-moe-architecture.md) - A neural network architecture where only a subset of parameters (e.g., 10B active out of 122B total) are activated per inference, enabling compute-efficient reasoning and coding, exemplified by models like Qwen3.5.
* [ML Code as Templates](/concepts/ml-code-as-templates.md) - Standardized notebook templates for ML project components (training, batch inference, testing) created by MLOps Stacks to unify project structure and enforce modularized, test-ready code.
* [ML Environment Stages (Dev/Staging/Prod)](/concepts/ml-environment-stages-devstagingprod.md) - The separation of ML workloads into development, staging, and production environments, each corresponding to a Unity Catalog catalog, with distinct purposes for model development, integration testing, and final serving.
* [ML Lifecycle](/concepts/ml-lifecycle.md) - The end-to-end journey for taking an ML project from initial scoping to production, encompassing eight stages: scoping, data exploration, data preparation, model training, evaluation, staging, deployment, and monitoring.
* [ML Lifecycle on Databricks](/concepts/ml-lifecycle-on-databricks.md) - The end-to-end journey from raw data to production model, covering eight key stages: scoping, EDA, data/feature preparation, training, evaluation, registration, deployment, and monitoring/retraining.
* [ML Model Monitoring and Automated Retraining](/concepts/ml-model-monitoring-and-automated-retraining.md) - The practice of profiling input data and model predictions for drift and performance, publishing metrics to dashboards, setting up alerts, and triggering automated retraining cycles via scheduled or event-driven jobs.
* [ML Model Performance Profiling](/concepts/ml-model-performance-profiling.md) - Using Databricks data profiling on inference tables to track ML model inputs, predictions, performance trends, and drift over time.
* [ML Platform](/concepts/ml-platform.md) - The combined infrastructure, tooling, and governance layer that supports the full ML lifecycle on Databricks
* [ML Platform (Databricks)](/concepts/ml-platform.md) - Combined infrastructure, tooling, and governance layer supporting the full ML lifecycle — including data assets, experimentation tools, training infrastructure, deployment/monitoring infrastructure, and MLOps/governance tools.
* [ML vs Deep Learning vs GenAI Distinctions](/concepts/ml-vs-deep-learning-vs-genai-distinctions.md) - The overlapping boundaries between classical machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI, with platform features like Model Serving, ai_query, AI Runtime, MLflow, and AI Search supporting all three paradigms.
* [ML vs Deep Learning vs GenAI on Databricks](/concepts/ml-vs-deep-learning-vs-genai-on-databricks.md) - The relationship and shared platform support between classic ML, deep learning, and generative AI paradigms on Databricks
* [MLflow](/concepts/mlflow.md) - An open source platform for managing the end-to-end machine learning lifecycle, supporting tracking for model tuning in Python, R, and Scala, and used as the tracking backend for MLlib automated logging.
* [MLflow 3](/concepts/mlflow-3.md) - The latest major version of MLflow on Databricks, delivering observability, evaluation, prompt management, and new concepts like Logged Models and Deployment Jobs.
* [MLflow 3 Agent Evaluation SDK](/concepts/mlflow-3-managed-evaluation.md) - The `mlflow[databricks]>=3.1` SDK that provides Agent Evaluation methods as part of the managed MLflow 3 platform.
* [MLflow 3 API Changes and Deprecations](/concepts/mlflow-3-api-changes-and-deprecations.md) - Notable API changes in MLflow 3 including the deprecation of artifact_path (replaced by name), removal of run_uuid (use run_id), and deprecation of mlflow.evaluate in favor of mlflow.models.evaluate.
* [MLflow 3 Artifact Storage Architecture](/concepts/mlflow-3-artifact-storage-architecture.md) - In MLflow 3, model artifacts are stored under a dedicated model artifact path (models/<model_id>/artifacts/) rather than under the run's artifact path, changing how models are loaded and referenced.
* [MLflow 3 Deep Learning Workflow](/concepts/mlflow-3-deep-learning-workflow.md) - A workflow for training deep learning models with PyTorch on Databricks, using MLflow 3 to log checkpoints, track metrics, and register models to Unity Catalog.
* [MLflow 3 Dependency for GenAI Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-3-dependency-for-genai-tracing.md) - Requirement for MLflow 3.x (specifically mlflow[databricks]>=3.1) to access the latest GenAI tracing capabilities, including automatic and manual tracing features.
* [MLflow 3 deployment job](/concepts/mlflow-3-deployment-job.md) - A Databricks job that evaluates and deploys model versions, creating additional metrics viewable on the Catalog Explorer model version page for approver review before deployment approval.
* [MLflow 3 Deployment Jobs](/concepts/mlflow-3-deployment-jobs.md) - Lakeflow Jobs-based workflows that manage the model lifecycle including evaluation, approval, and deployment steps, governed by Unity Catalog with full activity logging.
* [MLflow 3 Evaluation Data Schema](/concepts/mlflow-3-evaluation-data-schema.md) - MLflow 3 uses a new data field mapping: inputs (replaces request), outputs (replaces response), and expectations (replaces expected_response, expected_facts, etc.), organized as structured dicts within each evaluation row.
* [MLflow 3 Experiment Page Models Tab](/concepts/mlflow-3-experiment-page-models-tab.md) - A UI view in MLflow 3 experiments that displays all logged checkpoint models from a run, with metrics, parameters, and visualization tools for model comparison.
* [MLflow 3 for GenAI](/concepts/mlflow-3-for-genai.md) - A comprehensive set of features in MLflow 3 for generative AI application development including tracing, evaluation with LLM judges, human feedback collection, and a centralized Prompt Registry.
* [MLflow 3 for Models](/concepts/mlflow-3-for-models.md) - State-of-the-art experiment tracking, performance evaluation, and production management for traditional machine learning and deep learning models on Databricks.
* [MLflow 3 GenAI Evaluation API](/concepts/mlflow-3-genai-evaluation-api.md) - The new mlflow.genai.evaluate() API that replaces mlflow.evaluate() for agent and LLM evaluation, with cleaner parameter names and explicit scorer selection.
* [MLflow 3 GenAI Module](/concepts/mlflow-3-genai-module.md) - The new mlflow.genai package that unifies GenAI evaluation functionality, replacing the separate Databricks Agent Evaluation API.
* [MLflow 3 GenAI Tracing Platform](/concepts/mlflow-3-genai-tracing-platform.md) - MLflow 3 provides the recommended platform for GenAI tracing with capabilities for automatic and manual tracing of LLM calls, spans, and multi-step workflows on Databricks.
* [MLflow 3 Guidelines and ExpectationsGuidelines Scorers](/concepts/mlflow-3-guidelines-and-expectationsguidelines-scorers.md) - Global guidelines from MLflow 2.x are replaced by explicit Guidelines() scorer instances, and per-example guidelines use the ExpectationsGuidelines scorer with expectations.guidelines fields in the evaluation data.
* [MLflow 3 Human Feedback and Labeling](/concepts/mlflow-3-human-feedback-and-labeling.md) - The human feedback system in MLflow 3 moves from databricks.agents.review_app to mlflow.genai.labeling and mlflow.genai.label_schemas, with explicit schema creation and new dataset management via mlflow.genai.datasets.
* [MLflow 3 Installation](/concepts/mlflow-3-installation.md) - Instructions for installing MLflow 3 by upgrading to version 3.0 or above using pip.
* [MLflow 3 LoggedModel](/concepts/mlflow-3-loggedmodel.md) - A new first-class object in MLflow 3 that elevates models from run artifacts to dedicated tracked entities, capturing metrics, parameters, and traces across multiple development phases and environments.
* [MLflow 3 Managed Evaluation](/concepts/mlflow-3-managed-evaluation.md) - Managed Agent Evaluation integrated with MLflow 3 using the mlflow[databricks]>=3.1 SDK, superseding MLflow 2 Agent Evaluation.
* [MLflow 3 Migration from MLflow 2.x](/concepts/mlflow-3-migration-from-mlflow-2x.md) - Key changes when migrating from MLflow 2.x to 3.0 including new log_model API, artifact storage location changes, default Unity Catalog registry, and deprecated APIs.
* [MLflow 3 Model Artifact Storage](/concepts/mlflow-3-model-artifact-storage.md) - Change in storage location for model artifacts from run-based paths to model-based paths under experiments.
* [MLflow 3 Model Object](/concepts/mlflow-3-model-object.md) - In MLflow 3, models are first-class objects distinct from run artifacts, with code snippets for loading and using them for predictions on Spark and Pandas DataFrames.
* [MLflow 3 Model Registry](/concepts/mlflow-3-model-registry.md) - Enhanced model registry in MLflow 3 that exposes metrics and parameters for registered models, with unified cross-experiment and cross-workspace visibility.
* [MLflow 3 Model Registry in Unity Catalog](/concepts/mlflow-3-model-registry-in-unity-catalog.md) - MLflow 3 defaults to the Unity Catalog-backed Model Registry (databricks-uc) with three-level model naming (<catalog>.<schema>.<model>) and inferred defaults for Unity Catalog workspaces.
* [MLflow 3 Model Registry with Unity Catalog](/concepts/mlflow-3-model-registry-in-unity-catalog.md) - Default registry URI in MLflow 3 is now databricks-uc, using Unity Catalog for model lifecycle management with three-level naming.
* [MLflow 3 Model Visualization](/concepts/mlflow-3-model-visualization.md) - New capabilities in MLflow 3 that extend chart view features from experiment runs to models on the Models tab, allowing visual comparison of logged models.
* [MLflow 3 Models tab in experiment page](/concepts/mlflow-3-models-tab-in-experiment-page.md) - A view showing all Logged Models produced by an experiment's runs, with a Charts tab for visual comparison of models before registering them to Unity Catalog for deployment.
* [MLflow 3 Models Tab Visualization](/concepts/mlflow-models-tab-visualizations.md) - Extension of chart view and visualization features (previously only for runs) to models from the Models tab in MLflow 3.
* [MLflow 3 Prerequisites for GenAI Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-3-prerequisites-for-genai-tracing.md) - System and package requirements for using MLflow 3's GenAI tracing capabilities, including mlflow[databricks]>=3.1 and framework-specific SDKs.
* [MLflow 3 Serverless Compute Autologging Requirement](/concepts/serverless-compute-autologging-requirement.md) - On Databricks serverless compute clusters, autologging for genAI tracing frameworks is not automatically enabled; users must explicitly call the appropriate `mlflow.<library>.autolog()` function.
* [MLflow 3 traditional ML workflow](/concepts/mlflow-3-traditional-ml-workflow.md) - A Databricks notebook-based workflow demonstrating model training as an MLflow Run, producing a Logged Model, and exploring results via the MLflow UI and Unity Catalog.
* [MLflow active experiment](/concepts/mlflow-active-experiment.md) - The mechanism for controlling which experiment MLflow logs runs to, which can be set using mlflow.set_experiment(), mlflow.start_run(), or environment variables.
* [MLflow Active Run Management with fit()](/concepts/active-mlflow-run-management-with-fit.md) - Best practice of wrapping CrossValidator.fit() or TrainValidationSplit.fit() calls inside a 'with mlflow.start_run():' block to ensure tuning results are logged under a dedicated parent run and to simplify adding custom tags, parameters, or metrics.
* [MLflow add_libraries_to_model Utility](/concepts/mlflow-add_libraries_to_model-utility.md) - MLflow utility that prepackages all model dependencies as Python wheel files alongside the model, ensuring exact library versions from the training environment
* [MLflow Agent Evaluation](/concepts/mlflow-agent-evaluation.md) - Comprehensive evaluation framework for AI agents, LLMs, RAG systems, and GenAI applications covering the entire development lifecycle from testing through production monitoring.
* [MLflow Aggregate Metric Analysis Across Experiments](/concepts/mlflow-aggregate-metric-analysis-across-experiments.md) - Using system tables to compute summary statistics (e.g., average GPU utilization) across all experiments in a workspace within a given time window.
* [MLflow and Unity Catalog Model Registration](/concepts/mlflow-and-unity-catalog-model-registration.md) - The workflow for logging fine-tuned LLM artifacts with MLflow and registering them in Unity Catalog for governance, versioning, and deployment as an 'llm/v1/chat' task.
* [MLflow and Unity Catalog Model Registration Pipeline](/concepts/mlflow-and-unity-catalog-model-registration.md) - Workflow for logging fine-tuned models to MLflow Tracking and registering them in Databricks Unity Catalog with versioning, metadata, and deployment readiness.
* [MLflow Annotation Data Formats](/concepts/mlflow-annotation-data-formats.md) - The otel_annotations table structure for storing MLflow tracing entities (metadata, tags, assessments, run links) as typed rows with soft-delete semantics.
* [MLflow Anthropic Autolog](/concepts/mlflow-anthropic-autolog.md) - Automatic trace capture for Anthropic LLM SDK calls via mlflow.anthropic.autolog()
* [MLflow Anthropic Autolog Disable](/concepts/mlflow-anthropic-autolog-disable.md) - Mechanism to globally disable automatic tracing for Anthropic via mlflow.anthropic.autolog(disable=True) or mlflow.autolog(disable=True)
* [MLflow Anthropic Autologging](/concepts/mlflow-anthropic-autolog.md) - Automatic tracing of Anthropic Claude SDK calls via mlflow.anthropic.autolog() for capturing agent interactions and tool calls in MLflow traces.
* [MLflow Application Versioning](/concepts/mlflow-application-versioning.md) - Version management for GenAI apps through logged models (snapshots linking traces, prompts, and code) and prompts (version-controlled templates with variables and aliases like 'production').
* [MLflow Artifact Packaging for Model Serving](/concepts/mlflow-artifact-packaging-for-model-serving.md) - Using MLflow's log_model() artifacts parameter to bundle file dependencies (e.g., model weights, tokenizer caches) directly into the model artifact for deployment on Databricks Model Serving.
* [MLflow Assessment and AssessmentSource API](/concepts/mlflow-assessment-and-assessmentsource-api.md) - MLflow SDK entities (Assessment, AssessmentSource, AssessmentSourceType) used to log structured human feedback on traces with rationale and source provenance.
* [MLflow Assessment and Feedback API](/concepts/mlflow-assessment-and-feedback-api.md) - Programmatic API for logging feedback on traces using mlflow.log_feedback() with AssessmentSource and AssessmentSourceType, enabling structured attribution of human evaluations.
* [MLflow Assessments (Feedback and Expectation)](/concepts/mlflow-assessments-feedback-and-expectation.md) - The human-generated labels captured during a labeling session, stored as Assessments on MLflow Traces; can be either Feedback or Expectation data and later synced to evaluation datasets.
* [MLflow Authentication Methods](/concepts/mlflow-authentication-methods.md) - Authentication approaches for connecting to remote MLflow tracking servers, including Personal Access Tokens (PAT) and OAuth with service principals.
* [MLflow Authentication Methods for Remote Tracking](/concepts/mlflow-authentication-methods.md) - Authentication approaches for remote MLflow tracking server connections, including Personal Access Tokens (PAT) and OAuth using service principals.
* [MLflow Auto-Tracing Limitations](/concepts/mlflow-auto-tracing-limitations.md) - MLflow auto-tracing only supports synchronous calls; async and streaming methods are not traced
* [MLflow Autolog Configuration Parameters](/concepts/mlflow-autolog-configuration-parameters.md) - A set of configurable parameters (log_input_examples, log_model_signatures, log_models, disable, exclusive, disable_for_unsupported_versions, silent) that control what and how Databricks Autologging captures during model training.
* [MLflow Autolog Disable Mechanism](/concepts/mlflow-autolog-disable-mechanism.md) - Method to globally disable automatic tracing for Groq via mlflow.groq.autolog(disable=True) or mlflow.autolog(disable=True)
* [MLflow Autolog for GenAI Tracing](/concepts/autologging-for-genai-tracing.md) - A single-line code pattern (mlflow.<library>.autolog()) that automatically traces generative AI applications across 20+ supported libraries and frameworks.
* [MLflow autolog for generative AI](/concepts/mlflow-autolog-for-generative-ai.md) - The mechanism in MLflow that automatically captures traces for supported GenAI frameworks (including txtai) when the autolog function is called
* [MLflow autolog() Configuration](/concepts/mlflow-autolog-configuration.md) - The default configuration parameters and customization options for mlflow.autolog() including log_models, log_input_examples, log_model_signatures, disable, exclusive, disable_for_unsupported_versions, and silent flags.
* [MLflow Autologging](/concepts/mlflow-autologging.md) - A framework feature that automatically logs ML model parameters, metrics, and artifacts from supported libraries without requiring explicit logging calls in user code.
* [MLflow Autologging for GenAI](/concepts/mlflow-autologging-for-genai.md) - Automatic instrumentation via mlflow.openai.autolog() that captures application execution traces (inputs, outputs) for use in MLflow Evaluation and monitoring.
* [MLflow Automatic Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-automatic-tracing.md) - One-line automatic instrumentation for GenAI libraries to capture LLM calls, tool usage, and agent interactions without significant code changes.
* [MLflow Automatic Tracing (autolog)](/concepts/mlflow-automatic-tracing-autolog.md) - One-line code pattern using mlflow.<library>.autolog() to automatically trace generative AI applications across 20+ supported libraries and frameworks.
* [MLflow Automatically Populated Trace Metadata](/concepts/automatically-populated-trace-metadata.md) - Standard metadata fields that MLflow automatically sets based on the execution environment, which can be overridden using mlflow.update_current_trace().
* [MLflow Bedrock Autolog](/concepts/mlflow-bedrock-autolog.md) - A function (mlflow.bedrock.autolog) that enables automatic tracing of Amazon Bedrock LLM invocations, capturing prompts, responses, latencies, model names, metadata, function calls, and exceptions.
* [MLflow Chart Interaction and Customization](/concepts/mlflow-chart-interaction-and-customization.md) - Interactive features of MLflow charts including hover details, move/resize/fullscreen controls, and edit/delete/download options via a kebab menu.
* [MLflow Chart View](/concepts/mlflow-chart-view.md) - A page in the MLflow UI that shows a collection of charts comparing runs of an experiment, with capabilities to select, filter, sort, group runs and customize or create new charts.
* [MLflow Child Run Pattern](/concepts/mlflow-child-run-pattern.md) - A hierarchical logging approach where a parent run is created in the driver process and each Ray task logs metrics to a nested child run under that parent.
* [MLflow Code-based Scorers](/concepts/mlflow-code-based-scorers.md) - Custom Python functions (decorator-based or class-based) that define flexible evaluation metrics for GenAI applications within MLflow Evaluation.
* [MLflow Combined Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-combined-tracing.md) - Hybrid approach mixing automatic and manual tracing to achieve complete coverage of application traces
* [MLflow Compatibility with databricks-feature-engineering](/concepts/mlflow-compatibility-with-databricks-feature-engineering.md) - A compatibility note that databricks-feature-engineering versions <=0.7.0 are incompatible with mlflow >=2.18.0, requiring an upgrade to databricks-feature-engineering 0.8.0 or above.
* [MLflow Correctness Scorer](/concepts/mlflow-correctness-scorer.md) - An evaluation scorer that quantitatively compares GenAI app outputs against expert-provided expected responses to measure factual alignment.
* [MLflow CrewAI Autolog](/concepts/mlflow-crewai-autolog.md) - Automatic tracing for CrewAI multi-agent workflows via mlflow.crewai.autolog()
* [MLflow Dashboard for GPU Utilization Monitoring](/concepts/mlflow-dashboard-for-gpu-utilization-monitoring.md) - A dashboard pattern for monitoring average GPU utilization and other system metrics across MLflow experiments within a time window, using system tables to identify inefficient resource usage.
* [MLflow Dashboard for Run Details](/concepts/mlflow-dashboard-for-run-details.md) - A dashboard pattern that reproduces MLflow UI run details (tags, parameters, metric graphs) from system table data using customizable SQL queries.
* [MLflow Dashboarding on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-dashboarding-on-databricks.md) - The practice of building Dashboards in Databricks using MLflow metadata from system tables to analyze experiments and runs across an entire workspace.
* [MLflow Dashboards from System Tables](/concepts/mlflow-dashboards-from-system-tables.md) - Building and using Databricks dashboards that query MLflow system tables to visualize experiment results, run details, and system metrics across a workspace.
* [MLflow Databricks Tracking](/concepts/mlflow-databricks-tracking.md) - Setting up MLflow to use the Databricks tracking URI and experiments for capturing and visualizing agent traces
* [MLflow deep learning tracking](/concepts/mlflow-deep-learning-tracking.md) - Using MLflow on Databricks to track deep learning training runs and model development, providing experiment tracking essential for iterative deep learning workflows.
* [MLflow Deployment Jobs](/concepts/mlflow-deployment-jobs.md) - Automated jobs that manage the full lifecycle of ML models on Databricks by automating evaluation, approval, and deployment tasks when new model versions are created.
* [MLflow Deployment SDK](/concepts/mlflow-deployment-sdk.md) - A Python SDK (MLflow 2.9+) for interacting with Databricks Model Serving endpoints programmatically, used to send scoring requests alongside REST API and PowerBI connectors
* [MLflow deployment with CI/CD and MLOps](/concepts/mlflow-deployment-with-cicd-and-mlops.md) - Infrastructure-as-code management of MLflow experiments and models using Declarative Automation Bundles and MLOps Stacks, plus automated staged model deployment through MLflow 3 deployment jobs integrated with Databricks Workflows and Unity Catalog.
* [MLflow Deployments SDK](/concepts/mlflow-deployments-sdk.md) - A Python SDK (mlflow.deployments) for programmatically creating and managing model serving endpoints on Databricks.
* [MLflow Document Entity](/concepts/mlflow-document-entity.md) - A data class in mlflow.entities representing a retrieved document with an id, page_content, and metadata dictionary.
* [MLflow Environment and Version Tracking](/concepts/mlflow-environment-and-version-tracking.md) - Standardized metadata fields for tracking execution environment (development, staging, production) and application version in traces, enabling environment-specific analysis and regression detection.
* [MLflow Eval Harness](/concepts/mlflow-eval-harness.md) - Development-phase evaluation system that uses LLM judges and scorers to assess traces and measure the quality of GenAI applications
* [MLflow evaluate with Judges](/concepts/mlflow-evaluate-with-judges.md) - The pattern of using mlflow.genai.evaluate() with a dataset and one or more scorer/judge instances to perform comprehensive GenAI application evaluation.
* [MLflow Evaluation Dataset](/concepts/mlflow-evaluation-dataset.md) - A curated set of example inputs (labeled or unlabeled) used to systematically test and improve GenAI applications, stored in Unity Catalog with versioning, lineage, sharing, and governance.
* [MLflow Evaluation Dataset for Conversation Testing](/concepts/mlflow-evaluation-dataset-for-conversation-testing.md) - Persisting conversation test cases as reproducible MLflow Evaluation Datasets using create_dataset and merge_records.
* [MLflow Evaluation Dataset for Reproducible Testing](/concepts/mlflow-evaluation-dataset-for-conversation-testing.md) - A mechanism to persist test cases as MLflow Evaluation Datasets using create_dataset and get_dataset, enabling reproducible conversation simulation runs.
* [MLflow Evaluation Dataset for Versioning](/concepts/mlflow-evaluation-dataset-for-versioning.md) - Recommended practice of packaging labeled traces into an MLflow Evaluation Dataset for version tracking and lineage when evaluating GenAI apps.
* [MLflow Evaluation Dataset Integration for Conversation Simulation](/concepts/mlflow-evaluation-dataset-integration-for-conversation-simulation.md) - Mechanism to persist conversation test cases as MLflow Evaluation Datasets for reproducible testing, using create_dataset and get_dataset APIs.
* [MLflow Evaluation Dataset Limitations](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-limitations.md) - Constraints on evaluation datasets including a maximum of 2000 rows per dataset, 20 expectations per record, and incompatibility with customer-managed key (CMK) encrypted catalogs.
* [MLflow Evaluation Dataset UI](/concepts/evaluation-dataset-ui.md) - A visual interface in the MLflow experiment Datasets tab for creating, editing, searching, and managing evaluation datasets and their records without writing code.
* [MLflow Evaluation Datasets](/concepts/evaluation-datasets.md) - Structured test data used as input for GenAI evaluations, available in formats like EvaluationDataset, list of dicts, Pandas DataFrame, or Spark DataFrame.
* [MLflow Evaluation Results Comparison](/concepts/mlflow-evaluation-results-comparison.md) - The ability to compare multiple evaluation runs side-by-side in the MLflow UI to assess the impact of prompt changes on GenAI application quality.
* [MLflow Evaluation Run](/concepts/mlflow-evaluation-run.md) - An MLflow run that organizes and stores the results of evaluating a GenAI app, including traces, feedback, metrics, and metadata.
* [MLflow Evaluation Runs](/concepts/mlflow-evaluation-runs.md) - Test report capturing everything about how a GenAI app performed on a specific dataset, including per-row traces annotated with feedback from each judge.
* [MLflow Evaluation UI](/concepts/mlflow-evaluation-ui.md) - Interactive user interface within MLflow for reviewing, comparing, and analyzing GenAI evaluation runs across different prompt versions.
* [MLflow Experiment](/concepts/mlflow-experiment.md) - A named container that organizes and groups all artifacts related to a single GenAI application, including traces, evaluation runs, app versions, prompts, and quality assessments.
* [MLflow Experiment as a Databricks App Resource](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-as-a-databricks-app-resource.md) - The ability to add an MLflow experiment as a resource within a Databricks App, enabling experiment tracking for AI applications directly from the app.
* [MLflow Experiment as Trace Storage Backend](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-as-trace-storage-backend.md) - MLflow experiments serve as the primary storage location for production traces, with optional custom artifact locations (e.g., Unity Catalog volumes) enabling Unity Catalog volume privilege governance for trace data access.
* [MLflow Experiment Best Practices for Databricks Apps](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-best-practices-for-databricks-apps.md) - Recommended practices for organizing, naming, and managing MLflow experiments used as resources in Databricks Apps.
* [MLflow Experiment Budget Policy Binding](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-budget-policy-binding.md) - The practice of assigning a serverless budget policy to an MLflow experiment so that all serverless workloads spawned from that experiment use the designated policy instead of the workspace default.
* [MLflow Experiment Budget Policy Configuration](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-budget-policy-configuration.md) - The mechanism to assign a serverless budget policy to an MLflow experiment via UI or API to control serverless workloads.
* [MLflow Experiment Configuration for Air Runs](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-configuration-for-air-runs.md) - The YAML fields `experiment_name` (required) and `mlflow_run_name` (optional) control how runs are organized in MLflow experiments.
* [MLflow Experiment Environment Variable Injection](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-environment-variable-injection.md) - Databricks automatically exposes the experiment ID via environment variables when an MLflow experiment is added as an app resource, accessible through the valueFrom field in app.yaml.
* [MLflow Experiment for GenAI](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-for-genai.md) - A container in Databricks used to organize and manage GenAI application traces, runs, and experiment metadata, accessible via the Experiments UI under 'GenAI apps & agents'.
* [MLflow Experiment Integration for AI Runtime](/concepts/mlflow-integration-in-ai-runtime.md) - Each AI Runtime CLI run creates a run within a named MLflow experiment, capturing metrics, parameters, artifacts, and logs viewable in the Databricks workspace MLflow UI.
* [MLflow experiment output schema](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-output-schema.md) - The fixed schema returned by the mlflow-experiment data source, including run_id, experiment_id, metrics (map), params (map), tags (map), start_time, end_time, status, and artifact_uri.
* [MLflow Experiment Permission Levels for Apps](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-permission-levels-for-apps.md) - Three permission levels (Can read, Can edit, Can manage) that control what a Databricks App's service principal can do with a linked MLflow experiment resource.
* [MLflow Experiment Resource](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-resource.md) - A Databricks Apps resource type that connects an MLflow experiment to an app for tracking runs, parameters, metrics, and artifacts.
* [MLflow Experiment Resource (Databricks Apps)](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-resource.md) - A Databricks Apps resource type that binds an MLflow experiment to an app, granting the app's service principal permissions to track runs, log parameters/metrics, and access experiment data via the MLflow Tracking API.
* [MLflow Experiment Resource Best Practices](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-resource-best-practices.md) - Recommended practices for organizing experiments by project or model type, using consistent naming conventions for runs and parameters, and considering retention policies and storage management.
* [MLflow Experiment Resource in Databricks Apps](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-resource-in-databricks-apps.md) - A configuration pattern that allows Databricks Apps to reference and interact with MLflow experiments as first-class resources, enabling experiment tracking capabilities within applications.
* [MLflow Experiment Resource Lifecycle](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-resource-lifecycle.md) - The process of adding and removing MLflow experiment resources from Databricks apps, including how removal revokes service principal access while leaving the experiment itself intact.
* [MLflow Experiment Resource Lifecycle in Databricks Apps](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-resource-lifecycle.md) - The process of adding an MLflow experiment resource to an app (granting service principal access) and removing it (revoking service principal access, with the experiment itself remaining unchanged).
* [MLflow Experiment Scoped Access](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-scoped-access.md) - When an MLflow experiment is added as a resource, the app's access is scoped exclusively to that specific experiment; other experiments must be added as separate resources.
* [MLflow Experiment Trace Location Configuration](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-trace-location-configuration.md) - The API and configuration pattern for setting a Unity Catalog trace location when creating or configuring an MLflow experiment, including catalog_name, schema_name, and table_prefix parameters
* [MLflow Experiment Tracking](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-tracking.md) - The MLflow capability to create, list, get, and manage experiments and runs programmatically via the REST API
* [MLflow Experiment Tracking for Distributed Training](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-tracking-for-distributed-training.md) - Integrating MLflow to log metrics (loss values), artifacts (checkpoints), and run metadata during distributed multi-GPU training sessions for experiment tracking and reproducibility.
* [MLflow Experiment Tracking Lifecycle](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-tracking-lifecycle.md) - Structured workflow for organizing AI/ML development lifecycle using MLflow experiments — including logging runs, parameters, metrics, artifacts, debugging agent traces, evaluating LLM quality, and versioning prompt templates.
* [MLflow Experiment Tracking on Databricks AI Runtime](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-tracking-on-databricks-ai-runtime.md) - Integration of MLflow for logging metrics, parameters, and artifacts from distributed training workloads submitted via the air CLI on Databricks
* [MLflow Experiment Tracking on Serverless GPU](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-tracking-on-serverless-gpu.md) - Logging training metrics (loss, avg_loss), artifacts (checkpoints), and run metadata to MLflow during distributed training on serverless GPU compute for experiment tracking and reproducibility.
* [MLflow Experiment Types: Workspace vs Notebook](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-types-workspace-vs-notebook.md) - MLflow experiments on Databricks come in two flavors — workspace experiments (standalone, created via UI or API, not tied to a notebook) and notebook experiments (auto-created when mlflow.start_run() is called without an active experiment, bound to a specific notebook).
* [MLflow Experiment Workload Creation Policy Tag](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-workload-creation-policy-tag.md) - The mlflow.workload_creation_policy_id experiment tag used to bind a serverless budget policy to an MLflow experiment via API.
* [MLflow experiments for GenAI applications](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-for-genai.md) - An MLflow experiment serves as the container for GenAI application traces and runs, automatically linked when using a Databricks notebook.
* [MLflow Feedback Data Model](/concepts/mlflow-feedback-data-model.md) - A structured entity (a type of Assessment) for capturing user feedback on traces, storing value, source, rationale, and metadata
* [MLflow Feedback Entity (Assessment System)](/concepts/mlflow-feedback-entity-assessment-system.md) - Structured data model for capturing user feedback as assessments on traces, including value, source, rationale, and metadata fields.
* [MLflow Feedback Entity and Assessment Data Model](/concepts/mlflow-feedback-entity-assessment-system.md) - A structured data model where user feedback is captured as Assessment entities attached to traces or spans, containing value, source, rationale, and metadata fields.
* [MLflow Feedback Object](/concepts/mlflow-feedback-object.md) - The structured output returned by MLflow LLM judges containing a 'value' field ('yes' or 'no') indicating relevance and a 'rationale' field explaining the reasoning for the assessment.
* [MLflow for Deep Learning Model Lifecycle](/concepts/mlflow-for-deep-learning-model-lifecycle.md) - Integrated MLflow support in Databricks for tracking model development, autologging, model deployment, and serving — enabling management of deep learning models including custom preprocessing logic across batch, streaming, and online inference.
