* [MLflow for Deep Learning Model Management](/concepts/mlflow-for-deep-learning-model-management.md) - Using MLflow for tracking experiments, autologging, model deployment, and model serving for deep learning models on Databricks.
* [MLflow for Deep Learning Tracking](/concepts/mlflow-deep-learning-tracking.md) - Using MLflow on Databricks to track iterative deep learning training runs, model development, and experiment logging.
* [MLflow for experiment tracking](/concepts/mlflow-for-experiment-tracking.md) - Using MLflow to log, track, and compare hyperparameter tuning runs across different model types
* [MLflow for Experiment Tracking and Model Registry](/concepts/mlflow-for-experiment-tracking-and-model-registry.md) - Databricks-managed MLflow provides reproducible, auditable ML development with tracking, registry, and full audit trails linking model versions to training runs, datasets, environments, and git commits.
* [MLflow for Model Lifecycle Tracking](/concepts/mlflow-for-model-lifecycle-tracking.md) - Using MLflow to track model development, log code snapshots, parameters, metrics, artifacts, and manage the model lifecycle across environments.
* [MLflow Function Calling Agent Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-function-calling-agent-tracing.md) - Combining MLflow's @mlflow.trace decorator with mlflow.bedrock.autolog enables tracing of custom ReAct-style function-calling agents, capturing the full chain of LLM invocations and tool executions as a single trace.
* [MLflow Gemini Autolog](/concepts/mlflow-gemini-autolog.md) - Automatic MLflow tracing for Google Gemini SDK calls via mlflow.gemini.autolog(), capturing prompts, responses, latencies, model names, metadata, function calls, and exceptions.
* [MLflow GenAI App Tracing with OpenAI](/concepts/mlflow-genai-app-tracing-with-openai.md) - Pattern for instrumenting Python GenAI applications using MLflow's automatic OpenAI client tracing and the @mlflow.trace decorator for custom functions like retrievers
* [MLflow GenAI Assessment](/concepts/mlflow-genai-assessment.md) - Quality measurements and ground truth labels (feedback or expectations) attached to a trace, added by end users, domain experts, or automated scorers.
* [MLflow GenAI Data Model](/concepts/mlflow-genai-data-model.md) - Hierarchical data model for organizing generative AI application data within experiments, covering traces, evaluations, human labeling, and versioning.
* [MLflow GenAI Datasets SDK](/concepts/mlflow-genai-datasets-sdk.md) - Programmatic API (mlflow.genai.datasets) for creating, retrieving, deleting, and interacting with evaluation datasets via Python.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluate API](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluate-api.md) - The mlflow.genai.evaluate() API for running evaluation datasets through multiple scorers including third-party integrations like Phoenix
* [MLflow genai evaluate() with DeepEval Scorers](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluate-with-deepeval-scorers.md) - DeepEval scorers can be used as part of the mlflow.genai.evaluate() pipeline to evaluate datasets of inputs and outputs.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation.md) - A framework within MLflow for evaluating generative AI applications, including built-in judges like RetrievalGroundedness for automated scoring of LLM outputs.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation Dataset](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation-dataset.md) - A curated, versioned collection of test cases (inputs and optional ground-truth expectations) used to systematically evaluate and improve a GenAI application's quality.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation Dataset Format](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation-dataset-format.md) - Structure for evaluation datasets in MLflow GenAI, requiring inputs, outputs, and expectations (with context) fields
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation Datasets](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation-datasets.md) - Structured evaluation data for LLM applications, typically a list of dictionaries with an 'inputs' key containing message histories for the AI agent.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation Framework](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation-framework.md) - A framework within MLflow for evaluating generative AI applications using structured datasets, custom scorers, and automated tracing.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation Harness](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation-harness.md) - A structured framework using `mlflow.genai.evaluate()` to systematically test GenAI app quality against curated datasets using scorers, with support for both direct and answer-sheet modes.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation Pipeline](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation-pipeline.md) - The workflow for evaluating GenAI applications using MLflow, combining evaluation datasets, built-in or custom scorers/judges, and the mlflow.genai.evaluate() function to produce quality assessments.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation Results Comparison](/concepts/mlflow-evaluation-results-comparison.md) - The ability to compare multiple evaluation runs side-by-side in the MLflow UI to measure the impact of prompt changes or model updates.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation Scorers (Judges)](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation-scorers-judges.md) - A framework within MLflow for using LLM-based judges (scorers) to programmatically evaluate the quality of GenAI application outputs, including relevance, groundedness, safety, and correctness.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation with LLM-as-Judge](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation-with-llm-as-judge.md) - Using MLflow's make_judge to create LLM-based evaluators that assess agent outputs for relevance and other criteria, integrated with tracing.
* [MLflow GenAI evaluation with third-party scorers](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation-with-third-party-scorers.md) - The integration pattern for using external evaluation scorers, such as Arize Phoenix, within MLflow's mlflow.genai.evaluate() function by passing them in the scorers list parameter.
* [MLflow GenAI Evaluation Workflow](/concepts/mlflow-genai-evaluation-workflow.md) - End-to-end process of defining custom judges, building evaluation datasets, and running evaluations with mlflow.genai.evaluate() to assess GenAI agent quality
* [MLflow GenAI Experiment](/concepts/mlflow-experiment.md) - A named container that organizes all artifacts related to a single GenAI application, including traces, evaluations, prompts, and model versions.
* [MLflow GenAI Feedback Data Model](/concepts/mlflow-genai-feedback-data-model.md) - The structured Feedback entity in MLflow that captures user assessments on traces, including value, source, rationale, and metadata fields.
* [MLflow GenAI Feedback Object](/concepts/mlflow-genai-feedback-object.md) - A structured output returned by LLM judges containing a binary value (yes/no) and a detailed rationale explaining the evaluation result.
* [MLflow GenAI Labeling and Label Schemas](/concepts/mlflow-genai-labeling-and-label-schemas.md) - New mlflow.genai.labeling and mlflow.genai.label_schemas modules for defining and managing evaluation label schemas.
* [MLflow GenAI Labeling Session](/concepts/mlflow-genai-labeling-session.md) - An organized queue of traces prepared for human review by domain experts, using labeling schemas to structure the assessments collected.
* [MLflow genai optimize_prompts](/concepts/mlflow-genai-optimize_prompts.md) - An MLflow function that orchestrates prompt optimization by combining a prediction function, training data, prompt URIs, an optimizer, custom scorers, and an aggregation function.
* [MLflow GenAI Predefined Scorers](/concepts/mlflow-genai-predefined-scorers.md) - A set of built-in evaluation scorers in MLflow 3 including Correctness, Guidelines, ExpectationsGuidelines, RelevanceToQuery, Safety, RetrievalGroundedness, RetrievalRelevance, and RetrievalSufficiency.
* [MLflow GenAI Production Monitoring](/concepts/mlflow-genai-production-monitoring.md) - Automated continuous quality assessment of GenAI app traces using scheduled scorers in production, with configurable sampling and multi-turn conversation evaluation.
* [MLflow GenAI Prompt Optimization](/concepts/mlflow-genai-prompt-optimization.md) - A framework within MLflow for automatically optimizing prompt templates using evolutionary algorithms and evaluation scorers
* [MLflow genai Python SDK for Prompt Management](/concepts/mlflow-genai-python-sdk-for-prompt-management.md) - Python SDK functions (register_prompt, load_prompt, search_prompts) for programmatic management of prompts in the Prompt Registry.
* [MLflow GenAI Scorer](/concepts/mlflow-genai-scorer.md) - A function that evaluates a trace's quality by extracting relevant data fields and passing them to evaluation logic (deterministic code or LLM judge), returning feedback assessments.
* [MLflow GenAI Scorers](/concepts/mlflow-genai-scorers.md) - Modular scoring components (built-in like Safety, and custom like Guidelines) used to evaluate GenAI outputs programmatically within MLflow Evaluation workflows.
* [MLflow GenAI Scorers API](/concepts/mlflow-genai-scorers-api.md) - Python API within Databricks for registering, starting, and backfilling scorers on MLflow traces, including both built-in and custom scoring functions.
* [MLflow GenAI Trace](/concepts/mlflow-genai-trace.md) - A record of the complete execution of a GenAI application, capturing inputs, outputs, and every intermediate step such as LLM calls, retrievals, and tool use.
* [MLflow GenAI Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-genai-tracing.md) - An MLflow subsystem that captures and visualizes the end-to-end execution traces of generative AI models and LLM API calls for debugging and observability.
* [MLflow GenAI Tracing on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-genai-tracing-on-databricks.md) - The integration that captures full traces of GenAI agent workflows—LLM calls, tool invocations, and multi-agent message passing—and records them to a Databricks experiment.
* [MLflow genai.evaluate Function](/concepts/mlflow-genaievaluate-function.md) - The mlflow.genai.evaluate() function runs a GenAI application against an evaluation dataset and applies scorers to judge outputs, logging results to the active MLflow experiment.
* [MLflow Groq Autolog](/concepts/mlflow-groq-autolog.md) - Automatic trace generation for Groq SDK calls via mlflow.groq.autolog()
* [MLflow Guardrails AI scorer integration](/concepts/mlflow-guardrails-integration.md) - MLflow integrates Guardrails AI validators as scorers for rule-based LLM evaluation without requiring LLM calls
* [MLflow Guardrails Integration](/concepts/mlflow-guardrails-integration.md) - MLflow integrates Guardrails AI validators as scorers for rule-based LLM output evaluation without requiring additional LLM calls.
* [MLflow Guidelines Scorer](/concepts/mlflow-guidelines-scorer.md) - A scorer that evaluates LLM outputs against custom natural-language guidelines such as language consistency, humor, child safety, and template adherence.
* [MLflow HTTP Client Timeout Configuration](/concepts/mlflow-http-timeout-configuration.md) - Configuration of MLflow environment variables (MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT, MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_MAX_RETRIES) to control client-side HTTP request timeouts for model serving.
* [MLflow HTTP Request Timeout Configuration](/concepts/mlflow-http-timeout-configuration.md) - Client-side timeout configuration via MLflow environment variables MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT and MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_MAX_RETRIES for model serving requests.
* [MLflow HTTP Timeout Configuration](/concepts/mlflow-http-timeout-configuration.md) - MLflow environment variables (MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT, MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_MAX_RETRIES) that control client-side HTTP request timeouts for MLflow operations.
* [MLflow Human Feedback Assessments](/concepts/mlflow-human-feedback-assessments.md) - MLflow stores human feedback as Assessments — structured objects (Feedback or Expectation types) attached to individual MLflow Traces or spans within a trace, enabling collection of ratings, comments, and ground-truth expectations from developers, domain experts, and end users.
* [MLflow Human Feedback Collection](/concepts/mlflow-human-feedback-collection.md) - System for collecting expert or human feedback on GenAI application traces using labeling schemas, labeling sessions, and the Review App UI.
* [MLflow Human Feedback Collection (Review App)](/concepts/mlflow-human-feedback-collection.md) - A system for collecting structured feedback from domain experts on GenAI traces using labeling schemas, labeling sessions, and the Review App UI.
* [MLflow Instrumentation for GenAI](/concepts/mlflow-instrumentation-for-genai.md) - Methods for adding tracing to GenAI code using autolog(), @mlflow.trace decorator, or manual span creation with runnable code snippets
* [MLflow Instrumentation Guidance](/concepts/mlflow-instrumentation-guidance.md) - The ability to get help adding tracing to GenAI agent code using autolog(), @mlflow.trace decorator, or manual spans, with runnable code snippets for Databricks notebooks.
* [MLflow Integration for AI Runtime](/concepts/mlflow-integration-in-ai-runtime.md) - Automatic tracking of training workload metrics, parameters, artifacts, and logs in MLflow experiments and runs, viewable in the Databricks workspace UI
* [MLflow integration for AI Runtime CLI](/concepts/mlflow-integration-in-ai-runtime.md) - Integration with MLflow to track and manage training runs submitted via the AI Runtime CLI.
* [MLflow Integration for Computer Vision Models](/concepts/mlflow-integration-for-computer-vision-models.md) - AI Runtime computer vision workflows integrate MLflow for experiment tracking and Databricks Model Serving for deployment of trained models.
* [MLflow Integration for Distributed Training Runs](/concepts/mlflow-integration-for-distributed-training-runs.md) - Integration between the AI Runtime CLI and MLflow for tracking distributed training runs, combined with visibility in the Databricks Jobs run page
* [MLflow integration for hyperparameter tuning](/concepts/mlflow-integration-for-hyperparameter-tuning.md) - The practice of using MLflow to track and compare hyperparameter tuning runs across different model types, enabling systematic model selection and experiment tracking.
* [MLflow Integration for LLM Training](/concepts/mlflow-integration-for-llm-training.md) - The integration of MLflow experiment tracking with LLM Foundry to log training metrics, system metrics, and model checkpoints, enabling experiment comparison and reproducibility.
* [MLflow Integration for XGBoost Spark Models](/concepts/mlflow-integration-for-xgboost-spark-models.md) - Limitations of MLflow autologging with distributed XGBoost and recommended use of mlflow.spark.log_model for model logging
* [MLflow Integration in AI Runtime](/concepts/mlflow-integration-in-ai-runtime.md) - Automatic tracking of training runs as MLflow experiments, capturing metrics, parameters, artifacts, and logs visible in the Databricks workspace MLflow UI.
* [MLflow integration in AI Runtime CLI](/concepts/mlflow-integration-in-ai-runtime.md) - AI Runtime CLI integrates with MLflow for tracking runs of distributed training workloads.
* [MLflow Integration in Ray Train on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-integration-in-ray-train-on-databricks.md) - Automatic MLflow logging integration where Databricks injects MLFLOW_RUN_ID and configures the tracking URI so distributed Ray Train jobs can log metrics without explicit credentials.
* [MLflow Integration on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-integration-on-databricks.md) - Databricks AI Runtime integrates MLflow for tracking model training experiments, as demonstrated in computer vision tutorials.
* [MLflow Integration with AI Runtime](/concepts/mlflow-integration-in-ai-runtime.md) - AI Runtime natively integrates with MLflow for experiment tracking, metric visualization, and model logging, with support for autologging, custom run names, and environment variable configuration.
* [MLflow Integration with AI Runtime CLI](/concepts/mlflow-integration-in-ai-runtime.md) - Integration between the AI Runtime CLI and MLflow for tracking and managing training runs.
* [MLflow Integration with Axolotl](/concepts/mlflow-integration-with-axolotl.md) - The integration between Axolotl training framework and MLflow for experiment tracking, logging training metrics, and registering models to Unity Catalog
* [MLflow Integration with Hugging Face](/concepts/mlflow-integration-with-hugging-face.md) - Logging Hugging Face models to MLflow using mlflow.transformers.log_model, including automatic metric logging via MLflowCallback and model artifact management.
* [MLflow Integration with Hugging Face Transformers](/concepts/mlflow-integration-with-hugging-face.md) - The integration between MLflow and Hugging Face that automatically logs training metrics via MLflowCallback and provides mlflow.transformers.log_model for logging trained models and pipelines to the MLflow registry.
* [MLflow integration with Hyperopt on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-integration-with-hyperopt-on-databricks.md) - Using MLflow to track and compare hyperparameter tuning experiments across different model types in Databricks
* [MLflow Integration with Ray on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-integration-with-ray-on-databricks.md) - Using MLflow tracking with Ray Tune, Ray Train, or customized Ray tasks on Databricks by setting authentication environment variables
* [MLflow integration with Ray Train on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-integration-in-ray-train-on-databricks.md) - Automatic MLflow logging setup where AI Runtime injects MLFLOW_RUN_ID and configures the Databricks tracking URI, enabling metric and parameter logging without explicit credentials.
* [MLflow Judge Feedback Object](/concepts/mlflow-feedback-object.md) - The output schema returned by MLflow LLM judges containing a 'value' field ('yes' or 'no') indicating relevance and a 'rationale' field providing an explanation for the judge's decision.
* [MLflow Labeling Schemas](/concepts/mlflow-labeling-schemas.md) - Predefined or custom templates that define the questions, format, and structure for collecting feedback or expectations during labeling sessions.
* [MLflow Labeling Schemas and Sessions](/concepts/mlflow-labeling-schemas-and-sessions.md) - Programmatic API (create_label_schema, create_labeling_session) to define what feedback to collect from human reviewers and organize traces for review.
* [MLflow Labeling Sessions](/concepts/labeling-session.md) - A structured mechanism for gathering human-generated assessments (labels) from domain experts on GenAI application traces, implemented as a special type of MLflow run.
* [MLflow Labeling Sessions and Schemas](/concepts/mlflow-labeling-sessions-and-schemas.md) - Primitives for defining structured human feedback collection — create_label_schema defines what feedback to collect (e.g., categorical ratings), and create_labeling_session groups schemas and traces for reviewer access.
* [MLflow Labeling Sessions for Expert Review](/concepts/mlflow-labeling-sessions-for-expert-review.md) - Structured workflow for sending GenAI app traces to domain experts for review via labeling sessions, enabling authoritative ground-truth feedback collection.
* [MLflow LabelingSession API](/concepts/mlflow-labelingsession-api.md) - The programmatic Python API (mlflow.genai.labeling) for creating, retrieving, deleting labeling sessions, managing users, adding traces, and syncing expectations to evaluation datasets.
* [MLflow LangGraph Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-langgraph-tracing.md) - Automatic capture of LangGraph agent executions as MLflow traces via the LangChain autolog integration
* [MLflow LLM Judge Architecture](/concepts/mlflow-llm-judge-architecture.md) - The framework of built-in LLM judges in MLflow that use a judge LLM (defaulting to a Databricks-hosted model) to automatically assess GenAI application quality across dimensions like relevance, groundedness, safety, and correctness.
* [MLflow LLM Judge Feedback Object](/concepts/mlflow-llm-judge-feedback-object.md) - The structured return type from MLflow LLM judges containing a binary 'value' (yes/no) indicating correctness and a 'rationale' providing detailed explanation of which facts were supported or missing.
* [MLflow LLM Judges](/concepts/mlflow-llm-judges.md) - Built-in LLM-powered evaluators that assess quality dimensions (relevance, groundedness, safety, correctness) of GenAI application outputs
* [MLflow LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation](/concepts/mlflow-llm-as-a-judge-evaluation.md) - Automated evaluation of GenAI application outputs using built-in and custom scorers that leverage an LLM to judge metrics like Safety, Correctness, and custom guidelines.
* [MLflow log_feedback API](/concepts/mlflow-log_feedback-api.md) - The API for programmatically logging user feedback against traces, supporting boolean, numeric, text, and structured feedback values.
* [MLflow Logged Model](/concepts/mlflow-logged-model.md) - A trained model tracked as a first-class entity in MLflow 3, capturing parameters, metrics, metadata, source runs, datasets, and model versions separately from MLflow Runs.
* [MLflow LoggedModel](/concepts/mlflow-loggedmodel.md) - A tracked model artifact in MLflow 3 that represents a specific checkpoint or version of a model, with associated metrics, parameters, and metadata.
* [MLflow Logging (Parameters, Metrics, Artifacts)](/concepts/mlflow-logging-parameters-metrics-artifacts.md) - The MLflow capability to log parameters, metrics, and artifacts to runs via the REST API
* [MLflow Logging in Ray Train Workers](/concepts/mlflow-logging-in-ray-train-workers.md) - Pattern for conditionally starting MLflow runs inside Ray Train workers using the MLFLOW_RUN_ID environment variable injected by the Databricks AI Runtime, enabling per-worker metric logging without explicit credentials.
* [MLflow Logging Limitations for Distributed XGBoost](/concepts/mlflow-logging-limitations-for-distributed-xgboost.md) - Inability to use mlflow.xgboost.autolog with distributed XGBoost; alternative use of mlflow.spark.log_model for logging xgboost.spark models
* [MLflow make_judge](/concepts/mlflow-make_judge.md) - A function in MLflow's GenAI module that allows users to create custom judges/scorers for evaluating LLM outputs based on custom instructions and criteria.
* [MLflow Manual Tracing](/concepts/manual-tracing.md) - Manual approach using function decorator APIs or low-level APIs for custom logic and complex workflow tracing.
* [MLflow MCP Server](/concepts/mlflow-mcp-server.md) - An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes MLflow trace management operations to AI applications and coding assistants
* [MLflow Mistral Auto Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-mistral-auto-tracing.md) - Automatic tracing of Mistral AI API calls via mlflow.mistral.autolog() that captures request/response telemetry without manual instrumentation.
* [MLflow Model Comparison](/concepts/mlflow-model-comparison.md) - Using MLflow to track, compare, and evaluate performance across multiple model types and hyperparameter configurations during model selection.
* [MLflow Model Dependency Logging](/concepts/mlflow-model-dependency-logging.md) - The practice of logging a machine learning model along with its Python and non-Python dependencies as artifacts, ensuring reproducibility and portability across environments.
* [MLflow Model Dependency Management](/concepts/mlflow-model-dependency-management.md) - Process of updating pip requirements of a logged MLflow model in-place without re-logging the model.
* [MLflow Model Flavors](/concepts/mlflow-model-flavors.md) - Built-in conventions that allow a single MLflow Model to be saved in multiple framework-specific formats (python-function, pytorch, sklearn, etc.) for compatibility with different serving and inference platforms.
* [MLflow Model Format](/concepts/mlflow-model-format.md) - A standard format for packaging machine learning models for use across downstream tools such as batch inference on Spark or real-time REST API serving.
* [MLflow Model Logging for GPU Serving with flash-attn](/concepts/mlflow-model-logging-for-gpu-serving-with-flash-attn.md) - Best practices for logging transformer models that require flash-attn, including specifying compatible CUDA, PyTorch, Torchvision versions and using custom wheel references in pip_requirements for mlflow.transformers.log_model.
* [MLflow Model Logging for Serving](/concepts/mlflow-model-logging-for-serving.md) - Methods for logging ML models in MLflow format for deployment: autologging, built-in flavors, pyfunc custom logging, with required signatures and input examples for Unity Catalog.
* [MLflow model logging methods for serving](/concepts/mlflow-model-logging-methods-for-serving.md) - Three approaches to log ML models for Databricks Model Serving: autologging, MLflow built-in flavors, and custom pyfunc logging — each with different trade-offs for manual control and flexibility.
* [MLflow Model Registration and PyFunc Wrapping for Recommendation Models](/concepts/mlflow-model-registration-and-pyfunc-wrapping-for-recommendation-models.md) - Registering a trained two-tower model in MLflow with a PyFunc wrapper to simplify serving by converting dict inputs into prediction lists.
* [MLflow Model Registry](/concepts/mlflow-model-registry.md) - A centralized model store with UI and APIs for managing the full lifecycle of MLflow Models, including registration with Unity Catalog or Workspace Model Registry.
* [MLflow Model Registry (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/mlflow-model-registry-unity-catalog.md) - Databricks' hosted version of the open-source MLflow Model Registry, integrated with Unity Catalog, using the 'databricks-uc' registry URI and three-level naming (catalog.schema.model).
* [MLflow Model Registry in Unity Catalog](/concepts/mlflow-model-registry-in-unity-catalog.md) - A governed model registry in Unity Catalog for managing model versions, aliases (Staging, Production), staging-to-production promotion, rollback, and audit trails.
* [MLflow Model Serving (Databricks)](/concepts/mlflow-model-serving-databricks.md) - Databricks platform feature for deploying custom ML models as serving endpoints, with support for MLflow pyfunc models registered to Unity Catalog or Workspace Registry.
* [MLflow Model Serving and Deployment](/concepts/mlflow-model-serving-and-deployment.md) - Deploying MLflow models as REST endpoints via Model Serving on Databricks, including validation before deployment and automatic updates based on model version availability.
* [MLflow Model Validation Before Deployment](/concepts/model-validation-before-deployment.md) - MLflow provides mlflow.models.predict to validate custom pyfunc models locally before deploying them to Model Serving to ensure they are capable of being served.
* [MLflow Models Tab Visualizations](/concepts/mlflow-models-tab-visualizations.md) - Chart view features available for comparing models from the Models tab in MLflow 3, extending the same visualization tools used for experiment runs.
* [MLflow Nested Run Structure for Hyperparameter Tuning](/concepts/mlflow-nested-run-structure-for-hyperparameter-tuning.md) - How MLlib automated MLflow tracking organizes tuning results: a parent run for the CrossValidator/TrainValidationSplit and child runs for each hyperparameter setting tested.
* [MLflow on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-on-databricks.md) - Integrated MLflow platform on Databricks for experiment tracking, model management in Unity Catalog, model deployment, and evaluation throughout the ML lifecycle.
* [MLflow OpenAI Autolog](/concepts/mlflow-openai-autolog.md) - Automatic instrumentation of OpenAI API calls by MLflow to capture traces, inputs, and outputs without manual logging code.
* [MLflow OpenAI Autolog for Ollama Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-openai-autolog-for-ollama-tracing.md) - Technique using mlflow.openai.autolog() to automatically capture and record Ollama LLM interactions as traces in MLflow, leveraging Ollama's OpenAI API compatibility.
* [MLflow OpenAI Autolog for Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-openai-autolog.md) - Automatic instrumentation of OpenAI client calls via mlflow.openai.autolog() to capture traces without manual instrumentation, enabling trace-based evaluation workflows.
* [MLflow OpenAI Autologging](/concepts/mlflow-openai-autolog.md) - Automatic instrumentation of OpenAI client calls via mlflow.openai.autolog() to capture traces for GenAI evaluation.
* [MLflow OpenAI Autologging and Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-openai-autolog.md) - Automatic instrumentation of OpenAI API calls via mlflow.openai.autolog() and @mlflow.trace decorator for capturing LLM request/response traces during evaluation.
* [MLflow OpenAI Autologging for Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-openai-autolog.md) - Automatically instrumenting OpenAI-based applications with MLflow Tracing via mlflow.openai.autolog(), which captures traces for use as scorer inputs during evaluation.
* [MLflow OpenTelemetry Compatibility](/concepts/opentelemetry-compatibility.md) - MLflow traces conform to the OpenTelemetry trace specification, enabling export to any OpenTelemetry-compatible observability solution.
* [MLflow OpenTelemetry Metrics Export](/concepts/mlflow-opentelemetry-metrics-export.md) - MLflow can export OpenTelemetry metrics such as span durations to a configured metrics endpoint using OTLP protocol.
* [MLflow Parallel Coordinates Plot](/concepts/parallel-coordinates-plot.md) - A chart type in MLflow that visualizes relationships between multiple parameters and metrics across runs, helping identify parameter settings that influence model performance.
* [MLflow Production Monitoring](/concepts/mlflow-production-monitoring.md) - Production-phase monitoring system that reuses the same judges and scorers from development evaluation to ensure consistent quality assessment
* [MLflow Production Monitoring for Long-term Trace Storage](/concepts/mlflow-production-monitoring-for-long-term-trace-storage.md) - Beta feature for storing GenAI agent traces in Delta tables via Production Monitoring, offering durable storage, no trace size limits, automated quality assessment, and ~15-minute sync intervals.
* [MLflow Production Tracing for External Deployments](/concepts/mlflow-production-tracing-for-external-deployments.md) - Framework for capturing execution traces from GenAI agents deployed outside Databricks and sending them to a Databricks workspace for observability in the MLflow UI.
* [MLflow Production Tracing on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-production-tracing-on-databricks.md) - Automatic capture of GenAI application traces when deployed on Databricks, with traces logged to MLflow experiments for real-time viewing and optional long-term Delta table storage via Production Monitoring.
* [MLflow Production Tracing with Unity Catalog](/concepts/mlflow-production-tracing-with-unity-catalog.md) - For large-scale trace analysis in production, use production monitoring to log traces to Delta tables in Unity Catalog, removing the 1,000-trace and 100,000-trace limits.
* [MLflow Project Entry Points](/concepts/mlflow-project-entry-points.md) - Executable commands within an MLflow Project defined in the MLproject file, with optional parameters declared with types and defaults, and commanded via 'command' field in YAML.
* [MLflow Project Environment Specification](/concepts/mlflow-project-environment-specification.md) - The software environment for an MLflow Project is defined via python_env.yaml for virtualenv, conda_env for Conda, or docker_env for Docker; if absent, a minimal Python virtualenv is used.
* [MLflow Projects](/concepts/mlflow-projects.md) - A format for packaging data science code in a reusable and reproducible way, including API and CLI tools for running projects with automatic parameter and git commit tracking.
* [MLflow Projects Deprecation on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-projects-deprecation-on-databricks.md) - MLflow Projects is no longer supported on Databricks; documentation is retired and users must migrate to alternative approaches, especially for Databricks Runtime 13.0 ML and above.
* [MLflow Prompt Optimization API](/concepts/mlflow-prompt-optimization-api.md) - The `mlflow.genai.optimize_prompts()` API for automatically improving prompts using evaluation metrics and training data, part of MLflow's GenAI capabilities.
* [MLflow prompt registration and versioning](/concepts/mlflow-prompt-registration-and-versioning.md) - The process of registering prompt templates in MLflow's prompt registry, creating versioned prompts that can be loaded and used in prediction functions.
* [MLflow Prompt Registry](/concepts/prompt-registry.md) - A centralized system within MLflow for storing, versioning, and managing prompt templates used in GenAI applications, backed by Unity Catalog.
* [MLflow pyfunc (Python Function)](/concepts/mlflow-pyfunc-python-function.md) - MLflow's Python function format that allows deploying any Python code or model as a serveable model on Databricks Model Serving
* [MLflow pyfunc Custom Python Model](/concepts/mlflow-pyfunc-custom-python-model.md) - MLflow's Python function (pyfunc) format allows deploying arbitrary Python code as a model, including preprocessing, postprocessing, branching logic, or unsupported frameworks.
* [MLflow pyfunc load_context Pattern](/concepts/mlflow-pyfunc-load_context-pattern.md) - The design pattern of separating one-time initialization (load_context) from per-request prediction logic (predict) in custom MLflow pyfunc models
* [MLflow PyFunc Model Validation](/concepts/mlflow-pyfunc-model-validation.md) - The practice of using mlflow.models.predict to validate custom pyfunc models before deploying them to a serving endpoint, ensuring the model is capable of being served.
* [MLflow PyFunc Wrapper for Model Serving](/concepts/mlflow-pyfunc-wrapper-for-model-serving.md) - Wrapping a TwoTower model in an MLflow PythonModel (PyFunc) to abstract away TorchRec internals and enable simple dictionary-based serving
* [MLflow PyFunc Wrapping for Model Serving](/concepts/mlflow-pyfunc-wrapping-for-model-serving.md) - Wrapping a PyTorch recommendation model in an MLflow PythonModel to simplify the inference interface from tensor-based batches to dictionary-based list inputs for serving.
* [MLflow PySpark ML Autologging](/concepts/mlflow-pyspark-ml-autologging.md) - The recommended replacement for MLlib automated MLflow tracking, enabled by calling mlflow.pyspark.ml.autolog() and enabled by default with Databricks Autologging on Databricks Runtime 10.1 ML and above.
* [MLflow Python SDK for Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-python-sdk-for-tracing.md) - Python-based APIs in MLflow for instrumenting generative AI applications with tracing, supporting autolog, decorator, and low-level patterns.
* [MLflow REST API](/concepts/mlflow-rest-api.md) - The open-source REST API for MLflow that enables creating, listing, and getting experiments and runs, plus logging parameters, metrics, and artifacts
* [MLflow Review App](/concepts/mlflow-review-app.md) - A structured UI tool within MLflow for collecting domain expert feedback and expectations on GenAI application traces via labeling sessions and schemas.
* [MLflow Review App for Domain Expert Feedback](/concepts/mlflow-review-app-for-domain-expert-feedback.md) - MLflow's Review App provides two approaches for collecting domain expert feedback: interactive testing with a Chat UI (real-time conversational testing of deployed apps) and labeling existing traces (systematic review of captured traces to assess outputs and define ground truth).
* [MLflow Run](/concepts/mlflow-run.md) - A single execution of model code in MLflow that records parameters, metrics, tags, artifacts, and source information for experiment tracking.
* [MLflow Run Comparison](/concepts/mlflow-run-comparison.md) - A feature to compare multiple MLflow runs from single or multiple experiments in tabular format with parameter and metric side-by-side views, diff toggling, and visualization support.
* [MLflow Run Download and Export](/concepts/mlflow-run-download-and-export.md) - The ability to download MLflow run data as CSV files from the UI or programmatically, and to copy runs between workspaces using the MLflow Export-Import community tool.
* [MLflow Run Filtering and Search](/concepts/mlflow-run-filtering-and-search.md) - The ability to search and filter MLflow runs using queries on parameters, metrics, tags, state, creation time, and datasets to locate specific experiment executions.
* [MLflow Run Filtering and Sorting](/concepts/mlflow-run-filtering-and-sorting.md) - Filtering MLflow runs based on parameter values, metric values, or tags, and sorting runs by selected parameters to control what appears in chart views.
* [MLflow Run Filtering, Sorting and Grouping](/concepts/mlflow-run-filtering-sorting-and-grouping.md) - Capabilities in the MLflow UI to filter runs by parameter/metric values or tags, sort runs by parameters, and group runs by parameter values for comparative analysis.
* [MLflow Run Grouping](/concepts/mlflow-run-grouping.md) - Grouping MLflow runs by one or more parameter values using the Group by dropdown to organize chart displays.
* [MLflow Run Lifecycle Management](/concepts/mlflow-run-lifecycle-management.md) - Operations for managing MLflow runs including renaming, deleting (with bulk deletion by creation time), restoring (with bulk restore by deletion time), and a 30-day retention period for deleted runs.
* [MLflow Run Management Actions](/concepts/mlflow-run-management-actions.md) - Operations for managing MLflow runs including deletion, comparison, and tag management through a checkbox-based selection interface in the chart view.
* [MLflow Run Selection and Batch Operations](/concepts/mlflow-run-selection-and-batch-operations.md) - The ability to select multiple runs in the MLflow UI to perform batch operations such as deletion, comparison, or tag management.
* [MLflow Run Selection and Filtering](/concepts/mlflow-run-selection-and-filtering.md) - Mechanisms in the MLflow UI to select, show/hide, filter, sort, and group runs for comparative analysis in charts, including search by parameter/metric values or tags.
* [MLflow Run Selection and Management](/concepts/mlflow-run-selection-and-management.md) - The ability to select, show/hide, delete, compare, and add/remove tags for individual runs within the MLflow experiment tracking UI.
* [MLflow Run Tagging System](/concepts/mlflow-run-tagging-system.md) - Key-value metadata tags that can be added, edited, and deleted on MLflow runs to enable searching and organizing experiment executions.
* [MLflow Run Tracking on AI Runtime](/concepts/mlflow-integration-in-ai-runtime.md) - The integration between AI Runtime CLI training jobs and MLflow for tracking experiment runs, accessible via the Jobs run page.
* [MLflow Run vs Logged Model distinction](/concepts/mlflow-run-vs-logged-model-distinction.md) - MLflow 3 separates the concept of a Run (training/evaluation job execution) from a Logged Model (the persistent model artifact with its own metadata lifecycle), enabling clearer tracking across training, evaluation, and deployment.
* [MLflow Safety Scorer](/concepts/mlflow-safety-scorer.md) - Built-in MLflow scorer that evaluates LLM outputs for safety and harmful content, used as part of GenAI application evaluation.
* [MLflow Scorer Error Handling](/concepts/mlflow-scorer-error-handling.md) - Two approaches for handling errors in custom scorers: returning AssessmentError in a Feedback object, or letting exceptions propagate for MLflow to handle gracefully.
* [MLflow Scorers](/concepts/mlflow-scorers.md) - Pluggable evaluation criteria in MLflow that assess LLM outputs against custom guidelines or built-in policies, including Guidelines scorer and Safety scorer.
* [MLflow Scorers (Guidelines & Safety)](/concepts/mlflow-scorers.md) - Modular evaluation criteria in MLflow for assessing GenAI outputs against custom guidelines and built-in safety checks.
* [MLflow Scorers (Guidelines and Safety)](/concepts/mlflow-scorers.md) - Built-in evaluation criteria in MLflow for assessing GenAI outputs, including Guidelines scorer for custom rules and Safety scorer for content safety checks.
* [MLflow Serverless Workloads](/concepts/mlflow-serverless-workloads.md) - Serverless workloads that MLflow can run against an experiment, including scheduled scorers, synthetic evaluation set generation, and agent evaluation.
* [MLflow Serverless Workloads for Experiments](/concepts/mlflow-serverless-workloads.md) - Serverless workloads triggered by MLflow experiments, including scheduled scorers, synthetic evaluation set generation, and agent evaluation.
* [MLflow set_experiment_trace_location API](/concepts/mlflow-set_experiment_trace_location-api.md) - An MLflow API that links a Unity Catalog schema location to an MLflow experiment, defining where incoming OTLP spans will be stored as trace tables.
* [MLflow Span Analysis and Hierarchy](/concepts/mlflow-span-analysis-and-hierarchy.md) - Techniques for analyzing span trees including parent-child relationships, critical path detection, and per-type timing statistics.
* [MLflow Span Type Labeling via gen_ai.operation.name](/concepts/mlflow-span-type-labeling-via-gen_aioperationname.md) - Setting the gen_ai.operation.name attribute on spans to classify operations (chat, etc.) so MLflow renders the correct span type instead of UNKNOWN.
* [MLflow Spans](/concepts/mlflow-spans.md) - Building blocks of traces representing individual operations with properties like span_id, name, type, timing, status, inputs/outputs, and parent-child relationships.
* [MLflow Standard Metadata Fields for Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-standard-metadata-fields-for-tracing.md) - Predefined metadata keys like mlflow.trace.user and mlflow.trace.session that MLflow recognizes for filtering, grouping, and UI integration in trace analytics.
* [MLflow Supported Tracing Libraries](/concepts/mlflow-supported-tracing-libraries.md) - The collection of 20+ supported libraries that can be automatically instrumented with mlflow.autolog() for tracing.
* [MLflow System Metrics](/concepts/mlflow-system-metrics.md) - System-level metrics (CPU, memory, GPU utilization) recorded by MLflow during runs and exposed via system tables for workspace-wide monitoring and analysis.
* [MLflow System Metrics Monitoring](/concepts/mlflow-system-metrics.md) - Recording and analyzing system-level metrics such as CPU, memory, and GPU utilization across MLflow experiments to detect inefficiencies
* [MLflow System Tables](/concepts/mlflow-system-tables.md) - System tables in Databricks that capture MLflow experiment metadata across all workspaces in a region, enabling large-scale analytics via SQL and dashboards.
* [MLflow System Tables Dashboards](/concepts/mlflow-system-tables-dashboards.md) - Building dashboards from MLflow metadata stored in Databricks system tables to analyze experiments and runs across an entire workspace
* [MLflow System Tables for Experiment Analysis](/concepts/mlflow-system-tables-for-experiment-data.md) - The availability of MLflow experiment and run metadata in Databricks system tables, enabling use of Databricks SQL and lakehouse tooling for custom visualization and analysis.
* [MLflow System Tables for Experiment Data](/concepts/mlflow-system-tables-for-experiment-data.md) - MLflow metadata stored in Databricks system tables, enabling SQL-based querying and lakehouse visualization of experiment data.
* [MLflow System Tables for Visualization](/concepts/mlflow-system-tables-for-visualization.md) - Databricks system tables that expose MLflow metadata for experiments and runs, enabling custom visualizations using Databricks SQL and lakehouse tooling.
* [MLflow Third-Party Scorer Architecture](/concepts/mlflow-third-party-scorer-architecture.md) - MLflow's integration layer that wraps evaluation metrics from third-party frameworks as MLflow scorers usable with mlflow.genai.evaluate()
* [MLflow to Unity Catalog Trace Migration](/concepts/mlflow-to-unity-catalog-trace-migration.md) - A process for copying traces, spans, assessments, tags, and metadata from an MLflow experiment into Unity Catalog Delta tables, removing trace storage limits and enabling fine-grained access control.
* [MLflow Token Usage Tracking](/concepts/mlflow-token-usage-tracking.md) - Mechanism for tracking token consumption in LLM calls within traces, aggregating input_tokens, output_tokens, and total_tokens from provider APIs at both trace and span level.
* [MLflow Trace Analysis](/concepts/mlflow-trace-analysis.md) - Capability to investigate failing traces, examine span trees, pinpoint root causes, analyze latency, and identify bottlenecks in GenAI agent execution
* [MLflow Trace and Autologging](/concepts/mlflow-trace-and-autologging.md) - Automatic tracing of GenAI application calls (e.g., OpenAI) via mlflow.openai.autolog() to capture inputs, outputs, and metadata for evaluation and debugging.
* [MLflow Trace Assessment](/concepts/mlflow-trace-assessment.md) - Structured feedback, scores, or ground truth attached to traces and spans for quality evaluation in MLflow, with configurable type, name, data type, value, and rationale.
* [MLflow Trace Assessments](/concepts/trace-assessments.md) - Evaluations and feedback attached to traces, searchable by name, type (feedback/expectation), span ID, and source, with support for overrides and rationales.
* [MLflow Trace Captured Data for Anthropic](/concepts/mlflow-trace-captured-data-for-anthropic.md) - The specific telemetry automatically captured by MLflow Traces from Anthropic SDK calls, including prompts, responses, latencies, model names, metadata, function calls, and exceptions
* [MLflow Trace Captured Metadata](/concepts/mlflow-trace-captured-metadata.md) - Information automatically captured by MLflow traces for Databricks Foundation Model calls including prompts, completions, latencies, model name/endpoint, temperature/max_tokens parameters, function calls, and exceptions.
* [MLflow Trace Collection Verification](/concepts/mlflow-trace-collection-verification.md) - Programmatic method to verify that traces are being collected from an external deployment by checking MLflow experiment connectivity and searching for recent traces.
* [MLflow Trace Context](/concepts/mlflow-trace-context.md) - The mechanism for adding metadata, tags, and contextual information to MLflow traces to enable debugging, monitoring, and analysis of GenAI applications.
* [MLflow Trace Context Best Practices](/concepts/mlflow-trace-context-best-practices.md) - Guidelines for effectively adding context to traces, including consistent ID formats, session boundaries, environment variables, combining context types, and regular analysis.
* [MLflow Trace Context Metadata](/concepts/mlflow-trace-context-metadata.md) - Standardized metadata fields (mlflow.trace.user, mlflow.trace.session, mlflow.source.type, etc.) that can be attached to traces to track users, sessions, environments, and versions in GenAI applications.
* [MLflow Trace Data Captured for Bedrock](/concepts/mlflow-trace-data-captured-for-bedrock.md) - MLflow automatically captures prompts, completion responses, latencies, model names, metadata (temperature, max_tokens), function calling info, and exceptions from Amazon Bedrock API calls.
* [MLflow Trace Data Export](/concepts/mlflow-trace-data-export.md) - Methods for converting MLflow Trace objects to dictionaries, JSON strings, and Pandas DataFrame rows for serialization and analysis.
* [MLflow trace decorator](/concepts/mlflow-trace-decorator.md) - A decorator (@mlflow.trace) for instrumenting functions with span types (e.g., RETRIEVER) to enable observability and tracing in GenAI application evaluation.
* [MLflow Trace Detail View](/concepts/mlflow-trace-detail-view.md) - A deep-dive view into an individual trace with two main tabs: Summary (root span inputs/outputs, exceptions) and Details & Timeline (span hierarchy/waterfall, per-span details including Chat, Inputs/Outputs, Attributes, Events tabs, and an Assessments panel).
* [MLflow Trace Error Monitoring](/concepts/mlflow-trace-error-monitoring.md) - Patterns for programmatically monitoring and analyzing trace errors using mlflow.search_traces() with status filters, time windows, and error aggregation by function name.
* [MLflow Trace Experiment Linking](/concepts/mlflow-trace-experiment-linking.md) - Using mlflow.tracing.enablement.set_experiment_trace_location to bind an MLflow experiment to a Unity Catalog catalog/schema, defining where incoming OTel traces will be stored.
* [MLflow Trace Export Modes](/concepts/mlflow-trace-export-modes.md) - MLflow supports three trace export modes: MLflow Tracking Server only, OpenTelemetry Collector only, or dual export to both simultaneously.
* [MLflow Trace Generation and Querying](/concepts/mlflow-trace-generation-and-querying.md) - The process of using mlflow.genai.evaluate() to generate traces from an AI application, then retrieving them as a Pandas DataFrame via mlflow.search_traces() for downstream evaluation.
* [MLflow Trace Immutability Model](/concepts/mlflow-trace-immutability-model.md) - Distinction between mutable tags and immutable metadata in MLflow tracing, where metadata keys are set-once and ignored on update attempts.
* [MLflow Trace List View](/concepts/mlflow-trace-list-view.md) - A tabular overview of all captured traces in an MLflow Experiment, displaying sortable columns such as Trace ID, Request, Response, Session, User, Execution time, State, Source, and custom assessment/tag columns.
* [MLflow Trace Management Operations](/concepts/mlflow-trace-management-operations.md) - The set of programmatic operations exposed by the MLflow MCP server for searching, retrieving, analyzing, and managing MLflow traces
* [MLflow Trace Metadata](/concepts/trace-metadata.md) - Immutable, write-once key-value pairs attached to traces at logging time for fixed information such as model version and environment.
* [MLflow Trace Object](/concepts/mlflow-trace-object.md) - Core entity representing a complete record of an LLM or application execution, composed of TraceInfo (metadata) and TraceData (execution data).
* [MLflow Trace object in scoring](/concepts/mlflow-trace-object-in-scoring.md) - Accessing full MLflow Trace objects within custom scorers to retrieve spans, timing, inputs, outputs, and attributes for fine-grained metric calculation.
* [MLflow Trace Object Structure](/concepts/mlflow-trace-object-structure.md) - The MLflow Trace object is composed of two main components: TraceInfo (metadata about the trace) and TraceData (actual execution data including spans and request/response payloads).
* [MLflow Trace Query and Reuse](/concepts/mlflow-trace-query-and-reuse.md) - The pattern of using mlflow.search_traces() to query stored evaluation traces and pass the resulting DataFrame directly to mlflow.genai.evaluate() for rapid metric iteration.
* [MLflow Trace Requirements for Evaluation](/concepts/mlflow-trace-requirements-for-judges.md) - Prerequisites for built-in judges to work: RelevanceToQuery requires 'inputs' and 'outputs' on the Trace's root span, while RetrievalRelevance requires at least one span with span_type 'RETRIEVER'.
* [MLflow Trace Requirements for Judges](/concepts/mlflow-trace-requirements-for-judges.md) - Built-in judges require specific MLflow Trace span data, such as inputs/outputs on the root span for RelevanceToQuery or RETRIEVER-type spans for RetrievalRelevance
* [MLflow Trace Requirements for LLM Judges](/concepts/mlflow-trace-requirements-for-llm-judges.md) - Technical prerequisites for MLflow LLM judges including that inputs and outputs must be on the Trace's root span (for RelevanceToQuery) and that the Trace must contain at least one span with span_type set to RETRIEVER (for RetrievalRelevance).
* [MLflow Trace Search and Filtering](/concepts/mlflow-trace-search-and-filtering.md) - Capabilities to narrow down traces using a search bar (by Request content), structured Filters dropdown (by attributes, assessments, state, tags), Sort dropdown, and Columns dropdown to customize the trace list.
* [MLflow Trace Search and Retrieval API](/concepts/mlflow-trace-search-and-retrieval-api.md) - Using MLflow Python SDK (search_traces, get_trace) with SQL warehouse configuration to query OpenTelemetry traces stored in Unity Catalog.
* [MLflow Trace Search Limitations](/concepts/mlflow-trace-search-limitations.md) - The trace list returns only the most recent 1,000 traces by default; older traces require narrowed time filters or migration to Unity Catalog to remove limits.
* [MLflow Trace Span for Retrieval](/concepts/mlflow-trace-span-for-retrieval.md) - A pattern using @mlflow.trace(span_type='RETRIEVER') to instrument and trace document retrieval functions for observability during RAG evaluation.
* [MLflow Trace Storage in Unity Catalog](/concepts/mlflow-trace-storage-in-unity-catalog.md) - Mechanism for storing MLflow AI/agent traces in Unity Catalog tables, supporting structured querying, annotations, and multiple trace destinations per schema
* [MLflow Trace Structure](/concepts/mlflow-trace-structure.md) - The hierarchical structure of a trace consisting of root and child spans, each containing attributes (model name, token counts, timing), inputs, and outputs.
* [MLflow Trace Tag Management via UI](/concepts/mlflow-ui-trace-tag-editing.md) - Graphical interface in the MLflow UI for updating or deleting tags on traces by navigating to the trace tab and using the pencil icon.
* [MLflow Trace Tags](/concepts/mlflow-trace-tags.md) - Mutable key-value pairs attached to MLflow traces for dynamic metadata like user feedback, review status, and data quality assessments.
* [MLflow Trace Timeout Mechanism](/concepts/mlflow-trace-timeout-mechanism.md) - The MLFLOW_TRACE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS environment variable can halt stuck traces with ERROR status to prevent infinite 'in progress' traces.
* [MLflow Trace Token Tracking](/concepts/mlflow-trace-token-tracking.md) - Token usage tracking for LLM calls within MLflow traces, accessible via span attributes and trace info
* [MLflow Trace UI Label Column](/concepts/mlflow-trace-ui-label-column.md) - Labels added to traces through the MLflow Experiment UI appear as new columns in the Traces tab, enabling trace-level filtering and organization by annotation value.
* [MLflow Trace-based Evaluation](/concepts/mlflow-trace-based-evaluation.md) - Technique of passing a Pandas DataFrame of precomputed MLflow traces directly to mlflow.genai.evaluate() instead of a predict_fn, enabling rapid scorer iteration without re-running the application.
* [MLflow Trace-based Evaluation Requirements](/concepts/mlflow-trace-based-evaluation-requirements.md) - The structural requirements that MLflow traces must meet for built-in judges to function, including specific span types (e.g., RETRIEVER) and root span input/output fields.
* [MLflow Trace-Based Evaluation Workflow](/concepts/mlflow-trace-based-evaluation-workflow.md) - End-to-end workflow: instrument GenAI apps with MLflow tracing, collect human feedback on traces, route traces to expert labeling sessions, and use labeled traces for automated evaluation.
* [MLflow Trace-based Scorer Development Workflow](/concepts/iterative-scorer-development-workflow.md) - A four-step iterative workflow for developing scorers: define evaluation data, generate traces, store traces, then iterate on scorers without rerunning the application.
* [MLflow Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-tracing.md) - Framework for recording and analyzing trace data from LLM and AI application calls
* [MLflow Tracing Approach Selection Guide](/concepts/tracing-approach-selection-guide.md) - Guidance on choosing between automatic, manual, or combined tracing, recommending starting with automatic tracing for fastest results
* [MLflow Tracing Archival API](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-archival-api.md) - Python API functions (enable_databricks_trace_archival and disable_databricks_trace_archival) in mlflow.tracing.archival that control whether traces from an experiment are saved to a Unity Catalog Delta table.
* [MLflow Tracing Concepts and Workflow](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-concepts-and-workflow.md) - The tracing workflow includes understanding how MLflow captures and organizes trace data, using the Trace UI to analyze application behavior, and setting up quality assessment for Gemini-powered applications.
* [MLflow Tracing Enable and Disable API](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-enable-and-disable-api.md) - mlflow.tracing.disable() ceases trace data collection and mlflow.tracing.enable() re-enables tracing for instrumented models.
* [MLflow Tracing Enablement via Autolog](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-enablement-via-autolog.md) - A mechanism to enable or disable tracing for generative AI framework integrations (OpenAI, LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen) using autolog functions.
* [MLflow Tracing Environment Configuration](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-environment-configuration.md) - Required environment variables (DATABRICKS_HOST, DATABRICKS_TOKEN, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI, MLFLOW_EXPERIMENT_NAME) to connect an external application to a Databricks workspace for trace collection.
* [MLflow Tracing for Databricks Foundation Models](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-for-databricks-foundation-models.md) - Automatic tracing capability that captures LLM invocation metadata, prompts, responses, latencies, and exceptions for Databricks Foundation Model API calls via mlflow.openai.autolog().
* [MLflow Tracing for GenAI](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-for-genai.md) - MLflow capability for adding traces to Python and TypeScript generative AI applications
* [MLflow Tracing for GenAI Applications](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-for-genai-applications.md) - Instrumenting generative AI applications with MLflow tracing using @mlflow.trace decorators and automatic OpenAI client tracing via mlflow.openai.autolog()
* [MLflow Tracing for GenAI Apps](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-for-genai-apps.md) - Automatic instrumentation of GenAI applications using MLflow's @mlflow.trace decorator and mlflow.openai.autolog() to capture and visualize traces of LLM calls and retrieval steps.
* [MLflow Tracing for GenAI Observability](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-for-genai-observability.md) - Databricks' tracing framework for monitoring, analyzing, and debugging GenAI application behavior using MLflow
* [MLflow Tracing for GenAI on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-for-genai-on-databricks.md) - Databricks MLflow 3 provides automatic tracing for GenAI frameworks including CrewAI, LangChain, and others
* [MLflow Tracing for Generative AI](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-for-generative-ai.md) - An MLflow feature that uses autolog to enable or disable tracing for generative AI model integrations including OpenAI, LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and AutoGen.
* [MLflow Tracing for LLMs](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-for-llms.md) - Automatic instrumentation of LLM API calls using mlflow.openai.autolog() and @mlflow.trace decorator to capture inputs, outputs, and metadata for observability.
* [MLflow Tracing for txtai](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-for-txtai.md) - Automatic tracing capability for txtai workflows via MLflow's autolog function, capturing traces for LLM invocations, embeddings, and AI Search
* [MLflow Tracing for TypeScript](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-for-typescript.md) - MLflow tracing support for TypeScript generative AI applications, alongside Python support
* [MLflow Tracing in Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-in-databricks.md) - An autolog-based tracing feature for generative AI workloads that enables trace logging for LLM frameworks such as OpenAI, LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and AutoGen.
* [MLflow Tracing in Databricks Notebooks](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-in-databricks-notebooks.md) - Seamless integration that displays interactive trace exploration directly in notebook cell outputs when MLflow Tracking URI is set to 'databricks', available in MLflow 2.20+, with programmatic enable/disable controls.
* [MLflow Tracing Integrations](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-integrations.md) - The set of popular GenAI libraries and frameworks that MLflow Tracing natively supports for automatic observability.
* [MLflow Tracing integrations library](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-integrations.md) - A collection of 20+ pre-built library integrations that provide automatic tracing for popular frameworks used in GenAI applications.
* [MLflow Tracing Latency Overhead](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-latency-overhead.md) - Traces are written asynchronously but still add minimal latency proportional to trace size; testing is recommended before production deployment.
* [MLflow Tracing Limitations and Scaling](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-limitations-and-scaling.md) - The trace list returns at most 1,000 traces; experiments not in Unity Catalog are capped at 100,000 traces; migrating to Unity Catalog removes both limits and enables full time-range search across all traces.
* [MLflow Tracing Limitations for Anthropic](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-limitations-for-anthropic.md) - Current constraints of the MLflow-Anthropic tracing integration: sync-only tracing (async added in 2.21.0), no multi-modal input recording, and serverless cluster autolog limitations
* [MLflow Tracing Multi-threading Behavior](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-multi-threading-behavior.md) - MLflow Tracing uses Python ContextVar, causing each thread to have its own trace context by default; additional steps are needed to combine multi-threaded traces into a single trace.
* [MLflow Tracing Prerequisites (Databricks)](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-prerequisites-databricks.md) - Required software setup for MLflow tracing on Databricks: mlflow[databricks]>=3.1.0, openai, and databricks-connect>=16.1, plus creating an MLflow experiment.
* [MLflow Tracing Prerequisites and Setup for Anthropic](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-prerequisites-and-setup-for-anthropic.md) - Required environment setup including MLflow version (>=3.1 recommended), Anthropic SDK installation, Databricks environment variables, and API key configuration for the Anthropic tracing integration
* [MLflow Tracing Rate Limits and Quotas](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-rate-limits-and-quotas.md) - Databricks workspaces impose rate limits and quotas on MLflow Tracing to ensure service stability and fair usage.
* [MLflow Tracing Recommendation Strategy](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-recommendation-strategy.md) - Recommended workflow of starting with automatic tracing and adding manual tracing when more control is needed.
* [MLflow Tracing Span Hierarchy](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-span-hierarchy.md) - The parent-child span structure generated by MLflow Tracing, where a parent function span contains child spans for individual LLM calls and sub-functions.
* [MLflow Tracing TypeScript SDK](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-typescript-sdk.md) - An npm package (mlflow-tracing) that brings MLflow Tracing observability to TypeScript and JavaScript Node.js applications with minimal code changes.
* [MLflow Tracing UI Panels](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-ui-panels.md) - MLflow provides a Chat tab for rich message visualization (supported by converse/converse_stream APIs) and an Inputs/Outputs tab for raw payload inspection, including configuration parameters.
* [MLflow Tracking](/concepts/mlflow-tracking.md) - Experiment tracking system for logging parameters, metrics, artifacts, and model provenance (dataset, code, environment) across training runs, enabling reproducibility, comparison, and deployment.
* [MLflow Tracking for Deep Learning](/concepts/mlflow-tracking-for-deep-learning.md) - Using MLflow on Databricks to track iterative deep learning training runs and model development as part of the MLflow ecosystem.
* [MLflow tracking URI](/concepts/mlflow-tracking-uri.md) - A configuration parameter that specifies which tracking server MLflow should log experiment data to, set via mlflow.set_tracking_uri() with a URI like 'databricks://remote-workspace-url'.
* [MLflow tracking URI configuration](/concepts/mlflow-tracking-uri-configuration.md) - Setting the tracking URI to point to a remote MLflow tracking server using mlflow.set_tracking_uri() to direct where experiment data is logged.
* [MLflow Transformers Flavor](/concepts/mlflow-transformers-flavor.md) - Logging Hugging Face models to MLflow using the MLflow transformers flavor for model management and versioning
* [MLflow TypeScript SDK for Tracing](/concepts/mlflow-typescript-sdk-for-tracing.md) - TypeScript-based APIs in MLflow for instrumenting generative AI applications with tracing, providing similar capabilities to the Python SDK.
* [MLflow TypeScript Tracing SDK](/concepts/mlflow-typescript-tracing-sdk.md) - MLflow tracing support for TypeScript generative AI applications alongside Python.
* [MLflow UI Trace Tag Editing](/concepts/mlflow-ui-trace-tag-editing.md) - Graphical interface in the MLflow UI for updating or deleting tags on traces via the trace tab, using a pencil icon.
* [MLflow UI Trace Tag Management](/concepts/mlflow-ui-trace-tag-editing.md) - Graphical method within the MLflow UI to view, update, and delete tags on existing traces using a pencil icon in the trace tab.
* [MLflow Unified Evaluation Interface](/concepts/mlflow-unified-evaluation-interface.md) - The ability to combine scorers from multiple third-party frameworks in a single mlflow.genai.evaluate() call with unified results visualization
* [MLflow User and Session Tracking](/concepts/mlflow-user-and-session-tracking.md) - Standardized metadata fields mlflow.trace.user and mlflow.trace.session for associating traces with specific users and grouping traces into multi-turn conversations.
* [MLflow UUID Name Conflict Resolution](/concepts/mlflow-uuid-name-conflict-resolution.md) - MLflow's behavior when fit() is called multiple times within the same active run: it appends a UUID to parameter and tag names to resolve conflicts.
* [MLFLOW_LOCK_MODEL_DEPENDENCIES](/concepts/mlflow_lock_model_dependencies.md) - An environment variable in MLflow 3 that, when set to 'true', causes MLflow to capture both direct and transitive Python dependencies when logging a model.
* [MLflow-DeepEval Integration](/concepts/mlflow-deepeval-integration.md) - MLflow integrates DeepEval metrics as scorers for use with mlflow.genai.evaluate(), enabling standardized LLM evaluation within the MLflow ecosystem.
* [MLflow-DeepEval Scorer Integration](/concepts/mlflow-deepeval-integration.md) - MLflow integrates with DeepEval to allow using DeepEval metrics as MLflow scorers for LLM evaluation workflows.
* [mlflow-experiment data source](/concepts/mlflow-experiment-data-source.md) - A Spark DataFrameReader API on Databricks that loads MLflow experiment run data into a DataFrame for analysis, comparison, and dashboarding.
* [mlflow-skinny vs full mlflow for Model Serving](/concepts/mlflow-skinny-vs-mlflow-on-databricks.md) - A Databricks-specific consideration where Databricks Runtime ML includes mlflow-skinny by default, requiring explicit specification of mlflow in pip_requirements to avoid container build failures in Model Serving.
* [Mlflow-Skinny vs Full Mlflow in Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/mlflow-skinny-vs-mlflow-on-databricks.md) - DBR ML ships with mlflow-skinny by default, but Model Serving requires the full mlflow package in conda.yaml; pip_requirements must specify mlflow explicitly.
* [MLflow-Skinny vs Full MLflow on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-skinny-vs-mlflow-on-databricks.md) - Databricks Runtime ML includes mlflow-skinny by default, not full mlflow; custom pyfunc models must explicitly specify mlflow in pip_requirements to ensure Model Serving container compatibility.
* [mlflow-skinny vs mlflow Compatibility on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-skinny-vs-mlflow-on-databricks.md) - The critical requirement that Databricks Model Serving needs the full mlflow package (not mlflow-skinny) in conda.yaml when deploying custom pyfunc models from Databricks Runtime ML
* [mlflow-skinny vs mlflow on Databricks](/concepts/mlflow-skinny-vs-mlflow-on-databricks.md) - Databricks Runtime ML includes mlflow-skinny by default, but Model Serving requires the full mlflow package; custom pyfunc models must explicitly specify mlflow in pip_requirements
* [mlflow-skinny vs mlflow Serving Compatibility](/concepts/mlflow-skinny-vs-mlflow-serving-compatibility.md) - Databricks Runtime ML ships with mlflow-skinny by default; custom pyfunc models must explicitly specify the full mlflow package in pip_requirements to be deployable on Model Serving.
* [mlflow-tracing Package](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-package.md) - Production-optimized Python package for MLflow tracing with minimal dependencies and better performance, requiring MLflow 3
* [mlflow-tracing Production Package](/concepts/mlflow-tracing-production-package.md) - A lightweight, optimized pip package for production deployments of MLflow tracing, distinct from the development package and required for feedback collection in MLflow 3.
* [mlflow-txtai extension](/concepts/mlflow-txtai-extension.md) - A Python package (mlflow-txtai) that provides the integration bridge between MLflow tracing and txtai workflows
* [mlflow.ag2.autolog()](/concepts/mlflowag2autolog.md) - A one-line API call that enables automatic tracing of all AG2 agent interactions, including tool calls, LLM calls, and message exchanges.
* [mlflow.autolog() Configuration](/concepts/mlflowautolog-configuration.md) - The Python API function used to enable, disable, and customize Databricks Autologging behavior with parameters like log_models, log_input_examples, and log_model_signatures.
* [mlflow.autolog() Default Configuration](/concepts/mlflowautolog-default-configuration.md) - Default parameter values for mlflow.autolog() on Databricks, including log_models, log_model_signatures, and disabling of input examples
* [mlflow.autolog() for GenAI Tracing](/concepts/autologging-for-genai-tracing.md) - One-line automatic tracing for generative AI applications using mlflow.<library>.autolog(), supporting 20+ libraries and frameworks out of the box.
* [mlflow.autolog() for Generative AI Tracing](/concepts/autologging-for-genai-tracing.md) - One-line automatic tracing of generative AI applications using mlflow.<library>.autolog(), supporting 20+ frameworks out of the box.
* [mlflow.genai.evaluate()](/concepts/mlflowgenaievaluate.md) - The Python API function that automatically creates an evaluation run when called with a dataset, prediction function, and scorers.
* [mlflow.langchain.autolog() for LangGraph](/concepts/mlflowlangchainautolog-for-langgraph.md) - The API call that enables automatic trace capture for LangGraph workflows, built on the LangChain autolog integration
* [mlflow.models.predict API](/concepts/mlflowmodelspredict-api.md) - MLflow API for testing offline model predictions in a simulated serving environment before endpoint deployment.
* [mlflow.openai.autolog()](/concepts/mlflowopenaiautolog.md) - Automatic instrumentation for OpenAI SDK calls that captures request details, responses, token counts, and timing without manual instrumentation code.
* [mlflow.pyfunc.log_model for Dependency Management](/concepts/mlflowpyfunclog_model-for-dependency-management.md) - Using mlflow.pyfunc.log_model to log models from libraries without built-in MLflow flavors, with automatic dependency inference and support for extra_pip_requirements and pip_requirements parameters.
* [mlflow.search_traces() API](/concepts/mlflowsearch_traces-api.md) - Primary Python API for programmatically querying and filtering MLflow traces stored in tracking servers, inference tables, or Unity Catalog tables.
* [mlflow.set_trace_tag and mlflow.delete_trace_tag](/concepts/mlflowset_trace_tag-and-mlflowdelete_trace_tag.md) - Post-hoc API methods to set or remove tags on already-completed MLflow traces stored in the backend, using the trace ID.
* [mlflow.set_trace_tag and mlflow.delete_trace_tag APIs](/concepts/mlflowset_trace_tag-and-mlflowdelete_trace_tag.md) - Methods to set or remove tags on a trace that has already been completed and logged, operating on the backend store.
* [mlflow.smolagents.autolog](/concepts/mlflowsmolagentsautolog.md) - The API function that enables automatic trace capture for Smolagents agent runs
* [mlflow.start_span() Context Manager](/concepts/mlflowstart_span-context-manager.md) - A context manager API in MLflow for creating spans around arbitrary code blocks, providing fine-grained control over tracing beyond function-level decorators.
* [mlflow.update_current_trace API](/concepts/mlflowupdate_current_trace-api.md) - Function used during trace execution to add metadata or tags to the currently active trace via fluent or automatic tracing APIs.
* [mlflow.workload_creation_policy_id Tag](/concepts/mlflowworkload_creation_policy_id-tag.md) - An MLflow experiment tag used to specify the serverless budget policy ID that MLflow will use for serverless workloads created from that experiment.
* [MlflowClient Trace Lifecycle Management](/concepts/mlflowclient-trace-lifecycle-management.md) - Low-level API for explicit, fine-grained control over trace creation, span management, and trace completion using the MlflowClient class.
* [MLlib Automated MLflow Tracking](/concepts/mllib-automated-mlflow-tracking.md) - A deprecated Databricks feature that automatically logs hyperparameters and evaluation metrics to MLflow when tuning Apache Spark MLlib models using CrossValidator or TrainValidationSplit, without requiring explicit API calls.
* [MLlib Cross-Validation for XGBoost](/concepts/mllib-cross-validation-for-xgboost.md) - Using MLlib cross-validation tools to tune hyperparameters of XGBoost models in Scala
* [MLlib Cross-Validation for XGBoost Hyperparameter Tuning](/concepts/mllib-cross-validation-for-xgboost-hyperparameter-tuning.md) - Using Spark MLlib's cross-validation functionality to tune XGBoost model hyperparameters
* [MLlib ML Pipeline for XGBoost](/concepts/mllib-ml-pipeline-for-xgboost.md) - Embedding XGBoost models into MLlib ML pipelines to enable distributed model training workflows
* [MLlib Pipelines API](/concepts/mllib-pipelines-api.md) - A high-level API in Apache Spark MLlib for constructing and tuning machine learning workflows as composable pipeline stages.
* [MLOps (Machine Learning Operations)](/concepts/mlops-machine-learning-operations.md) - A set of processes and automated steps for managing code, data, and models to improve performance, stability, and long-term efficiency of ML systems, combining DevOps, DataOps, and ModelOps.
* [MLOps Stacks](/concepts/mlops-stacks.md) - Infrastructure-as-code templates for automating repeatable ML pipeline promotion from development to production on Databricks.
* [MLOps Stacks for Automated ML Pipelines](/concepts/mlops-stacks.md) - Infrastructure-as-code templates enabling automated, repeatable promotion from development to production, integrated with Unity Catalog and AI Gateway for full governance.
* [MLOps Stacks on Databricks](/concepts/mlops-stacks.md) - Infrastructure-as-code templates on Databricks for automating repeatable ML model promotion from development to production with CI/CD.
* [MLOps Three-Workspace Architecture](/concepts/mlops-three-workspace-architecture.md) - The recommended Databricks MLOps pattern using separate development, staging, and production Databricks workspaces to isolate model development, testing, and production deployment.
* [MLproject File Format](/concepts/mlproject-file-format.md) - A YAML-based configuration file that defines an MLflow Project's name, software environment, entry points, and parameters for more explicit project specification.
* [MNIST Dataset](/concepts/mnist-dataset.md) - A large database of handwritten digits used as the example dataset for HorovodRunner distributed training examples.
* [MNIST dataset for distributed training examples](/concepts/mnist-dataset.md) - The MNIST handwritten digit database is used as the example dataset in Databricks' HorovodRunner distributed training demonstrations.
* [MNIST Dataset Loading with torchvision](/concepts/mnist-dataset-loading-with-torchvision.md) - Loading and transforming the MNIST handwritten digit dataset using torchvision.datasets.MNIST with ToTensor and Normalize transformations.
* [MNIST handwritten digit classification](/concepts/mnist-handwritten-digit-classification.md) - A classic benchmark dataset and problem for handwritten digit recognition using deep learning, used as a demonstration example for HorovodRunner with TensorFlow and Keras.
* [MNIST on Databricks with TensorFlow and Keras](/concepts/mnist-on-databricks-with-tensorflow-and-keras.md) - Reference example demonstrating distributed deep learning using TensorFlow, Keras, and HorovodRunner on Databricks for the MNIST digit classification task.
* [MNIST PyTorch on Databricks](/concepts/mnist-pytorch-on-databricks.md) - A reference notebook example showing how to train an MNIST model using PyTorch on Databricks, first on a single node then distributed with HorovodRunner
* [MNIST TensorFlow Keras Example](/concepts/mnist-tensorflow-keras-example.md) - A reference notebook demonstrating distributed deep learning on the MNIST dataset using TensorFlow, Keras, and HorovodRunner on Databricks.
* [MNIST TensorFlow Keras example on Databricks](/concepts/mnist-tensorflow-keras-example.md) - A reference notebook demonstrating deep learning with TensorFlow, Keras, and HorovodRunner for the MNIST dataset on Databricks.
* [MNIST with PyTorch on Databricks](/concepts/mnist-pytorch-on-databricks.md) - Example of training an MNIST model using PyTorch on Databricks, serving as a reference for distributed deep learning
* [MNIST with TensorFlow and Keras on Databricks](/concepts/mnist-with-tensorflow-and-keras-on-databricks.md) - A reference pattern for training an MNIST digit classifier using TensorFlow and Keras, runnable with HorovodRunner for distributed execution
* [Model Access Control in Unity Catalog](/concepts/model-access-control-in-unity-catalog.md) - Using Unity Catalog's GRANT ON FUNCTION syntax and Catalog Explorer to set permissions on registered models, including USE SCHEMA, USE CATALOG, CREATE MODEL, and EXECUTE privileges.
* [Model Accuracy Metrics in InferenceLog Profiling](/concepts/model-accuracy-metrics-in-inferencelog-profiling.md) - For InferenceLog analysis, model quality metrics (confusion_matrix, precision, recall, f1_score, roc_auc_score) are computed when label_col and prediction_col are provided; fairness/bias stats for classification models.
* [Model Aliases](/concepts/model-aliases.md) - Mutable, named references assigned to specific model versions for indicating deployment status (e.g., 'Champion'), enabling decoupled deployment workflows.
* [Model Aliases for Production Rollouts](/concepts/model-aliases-for-production-rollouts.md) - Using aliases like 'Champion' on registered models to manage production model versions and promote models across environments in Unity Catalog
* [Model Allow-List via Unity Catalog Permissions](/concepts/model-allow-list-via-unity-catalog-permissions.md) - A security pattern where admins remove default EXECUTE from system.ai schema and selectively grant EXECUTE only on approved models, creating an explicit allow-list of accessible models.
* [Model Allow-Listing via EXECUTE Grants](/concepts/model-allow-listing-via-execute-grants.md) - The pattern of removing default EXECUTE on the system.ai schema and then selectively granting EXECUTE on approved individual model objects to create an allow-list of permitted foundation models.
* [Model Checkpoint Resume Training](/concepts/model-checkpoint-resume-training.md) - A technique for saving model state during training (via PyTorch Lightning's ModelCheckpoint) and later resuming training from a saved checkpoint.
