* [Model Checkpointing and Resume Training](/concepts/model-checkpointing-and-resume-training.md) - A technique to save model state during training (e.g., after each epoch) and later resume training from a saved checkpoint, enabling long-running or interrupted training workflows.
* [Model Checkpointing for Long-Running AI Runtime Jobs](/concepts/model-checkpointing-for-long-running-ai-runtime-jobs.md) - Recommended mechanism using Unity Catalog volumes via UCVolumeWriter/UCVolumeReader for workloads exceeding the 7-day maximum runtime.
* [Model Checkpointing with Unity Catalog](/concepts/model-checkpointing-with-unity-catalog.md) - Using UCVolumeWriter and UCVolumeReader from serverless_gpu.data to implement manual checkpointing for long-running AI Runtime jobs that may exceed the 7-day maximum runtime.
* [Model Checkpointing with Unity Catalog for AI Runtime](/concepts/model-checkpointing-with-unity-catalog.md) - Recommended checkpointing strategy for long-running AI Runtime jobs using UCVolumeWriter and UCVolumeReader from serverless_gpu.data
* [Model Checkpointing with Unity Catalog Volumes](/concepts/model-checkpointing-with-unity-catalog-volumes.md) - Distributed training on AI Runtime uses UCVolumeWriter and UCVolumeReader for asynchronous checkpoint saves/loads to Unity Catalog volumes, leveraging NVMe-backed local staging for performance.
* [Model Conversion from sparkdl.xgboost to xgboost.spark](/concepts/model-conversion-from-sparkdlxgboost-to-xgboostspark.md) - Utility function to convert saved sparkdl.xgboost models into xgboost.spark format, handling parameter mapping and booster serialization.
* [Model Dependency Management in MLflow](/concepts/model-dependency-management-in-mlflow.md) - Techniques for capturing and restoring model dependencies using requirements.txt, conda.yaml, virtualenv, and PySpark UDF environment managers to ensure reproducible model loading and scoring.
* [Model Deployment Timeout](/concepts/model-deployment-timeout.md) - Timeout during container build and model deployment, with retry limits and duration limits based on CPU/GPU workload size.
* [Model Deployment Timeouts](/concepts/model-deployment-timeouts.md) - Timeouts that occur during container build and model deployment for Databricks Model Serving endpoints, including retry limits and workload-specific time limits.
* [Model Hub in LLMOps](/concepts/model-hub-in-llmops.md) - Using internal or external model hubs to select pretrained foundation models for LLM applications, reducing time and cost of building custom models
* [Model Inference Best Practices on Databricks](/concepts/model-inference-best-practices-on-databricks.md) - Strategies for online, batch, and streaming inference including MLflow deployment, Model Serving, Foundation Model APIs, external models, and Pandas UDFs.
* [Model Inference Configuration from Registry](/concepts/model-inference-configuration-from-registry.md) - Capability to configure batch, streaming (via Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines), and real-time inference (via Model Serving) directly from registered models in the Workspace Model Registry.
* [Model Lineage in Unity Catalog](/concepts/model-lineage-in-unity-catalog.md) - Tracking the upstream datasets used to train or evaluate a model by logging input tables with mlflow.log_input, visible in Catalog Explorer's lineage graph.
* [Model Optimization for Inference Serving](/concepts/model-optimization-for-inference-serving.md) - Techniques to improve inference speed and resource efficiency, including reducing model size and complexity via quantization or pruning, enabling client-side batching, and offloading pre/post-processing from serving infrastructure.
* [Model Optimization Information API](/concepts/model-optimization-information-api.md) - API endpoint that returns whether a model is optimizable for provisioned throughput and its throughput_chunk_size, used to determine suitable throughput ranges.
* [Model Packaging](/concepts/model-packaging.md) - The process of logging a model with feature metadata so it retains references to its features and can automatically retrieve feature values during batch or real-time inference.
* [Model Packaging with Feature Metadata](/concepts/model-packaging.md) - The process of logging a model with references to its feature tables so it can automatically retrieve feature values during batch or real-time inference.
* [Model Preprocessing and Postprocessing with pyfunc](/concepts/model-preprocessing-and-postprocessing-with-pyfunc.md) - Pattern of wrapping model inference with custom preprocessing (input formatting) and postprocessing (output transformation) logic within a single MLflow pyfunc model
* [Model Redirect Bridge](/concepts/model-redirect-bridge.md) - A Databricks technique where a retiring model is temporarily redirected to a compatible replacement (same price, backwards-compatible) to give customers the full transition period even when a partner's retirement timeline is shorter than Databricks' standard.
* [Model Redirection Mechanism](/concepts/model-redirection-mechanism.md) - Databricks practice of temporarily redirecting requests from a retired partner model to a similar replacement model to ensure customers receive full transition time.
* [Model Registration and Deployment from AutoML](/concepts/model-registration-and-deployment-from-automl.md) - Process of registering the best AutoML model to Unity Catalog or Model Registry and deploying it to a custom model serving endpoint on Databricks.
* [Model Registration Methods](/concepts/model-registration-methods.md) - Multiple approaches to register models in the Workspace Model Registry: via the UI from MLflow runs, programmatically via mlflow.log_model() during experiments, via mlflow.register_model() post-experiment, and via the MLflow Client API create_registered_model().
* [Model Registry Permissions and Access Control](/concepts/model-registry-permissions-and-access-control.md) - Role-based access control system for the Workspace Model Registry where users must have at least CAN MANAGE to configure permissions, and model versions inherit permissions from their parent model.
* [Model Requirements for Trace-based Judges](/concepts/model-requirements-for-trace-based-judges.md) - Trace-based judges require a capable model served via Foundation Model APIs, External Model Serving Endpoints, or Custom Model Serving Endpoints, with specific recommended models listed.
* [Model Retirement and Deprecation Policy](/concepts/model-retirement-and-deprecation-policy.md) - Databricks Foundation Model APIs have a formal retirement policy for models, with specific retirement dates and recommended replacement models for migrating workloads.
* [Model Retirement and Migration Policy](/concepts/model-retirement-and-migration-policy.md) - Databricks publishes retirement dates for hosted models with recommended replacement models and migration guidance, including temporary traffic redirection periods.
* [Model Retirement Policy (Databricks)](/concepts/model-retirement-policy-databricks.md) - Databricks' official policy for notifying customers, managing transitions, and retiring deprecated generative AI models across Foundation Model API offerings.
* [Model Retirement Policy on Databricks](/concepts/model-retirement-policy-on-databricks.md) - A lifecycle management policy for Databricks-hosted foundation models that includes scheduled retirement dates, recommended replacement models, and migration guidance for deprecated endpoints.
* [Model Selection for Conversation Simulation](/concepts/model-selection-for-conversation-simulation.md) - Configuring which LLM powers the simulated user, including provider format patterns, region-specific hosting for EU vs US, and the ability to disable partner-powered models.
* [Model selection for throughput optimization](/concepts/model-selection-for-throughput-optimization.md) - Choosing smaller models (e.g., Llama 3.1 8B) for high-volume throughput tasks and larger models (e.g., Llama 3.1 405B) for tasks requiring maximum capability.
* [Model Selection for User Simulation](/concepts/model-selection-for-user-simulation.md) - Choosing which LLM powers the simulated user in conversation simulation via the user_model parameter, supporting provider-prefixed model formats (e.g., 'anthropic:/claude-sonnet-4-20250514'), with specific data residency and privacy considerations for Azure OpenAI and EU workspaces.
* [Model Selection Strategy for Throughput](/concepts/model-selection-strategy-for-throughput.md) - Guidance on selecting smaller models (e.g., Llama 3.1 8B) for high-volume tasks and larger models (e.g., Llama 3.1 405B) for complex tasks to optimize within rate limits.
* [Model Server Debugging Logs](/concepts/model-server-debugging-logs.md) - Two types of logs for troubleshooting model serving endpoints: container build logs (initialization phase) and model server logs (runtime phase).
* [Model Serving](/concepts/model-serving.md) - Databricks' unified solution for deploying, governing, and querying AI/ML models via REST APIs for real-time and batch inference with automatic scaling.
* [Model Serving and Batch Inference for Forecasts](/concepts/model-serving-and-batch-inference-for-forecasts.md) - Post-training capabilities in Databricks Serverless Forecasting, including viewing predictions, generating batch inference notebooks, and deploying to Model Serving endpoints.
* [Model Serving Authentication](/concepts/model-serving-authentication.md) - Authentication mechanism for Databricks Model Serving endpoints requiring a Databricks REST API token, sent via HTTP basic auth with 'token:' as the username and the API token as the password
* [Model Serving Build Logs Troubleshooting](/concepts/model-serving-build-logs-troubleshooting.md) - Process of diagnosing package installation failures during Model Serving deployment by inspecting build logs in the Databricks Serving UI
* [Model Serving Compute Types](/concepts/model-serving-compute-types.md) - Variety of CPU and GPU compute options including CPU_MEDIUM and CPU_LARGE for trading concurrency for more memory; GPU requires explicit code setup for predictions.
* [Model Serving Connection Timeouts](/concepts/model-serving-connection-timeouts.md) - Timeouts related to establishing network connections between clients and model serving endpoints, such as JDBC SocketTimeout configurations.
* [Model Serving Container Build Debugging](/concepts/model-serving-container-build-debugging.md) - Process of diagnosing and resolving failures during container image creation for Databricks Model Serving endpoints, including event log inspection and common build errors.
* [Model Serving Container Security (Databricks)](/concepts/model-serving-container-security-databricks.md) - Security practices for model serving containers including automatic 30-day rebuilds for compliance, no backporting of security patches to existing images, and patching via new model versions.
* [Model Serving Data Protection](/concepts/model-serving-data-protection.md) - Security controls including logical isolation, AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.2+ in transit, and policies against using customer data for model training
* [Model Serving Dependency Management](/concepts/model-serving-dependency-management.md) - Ensuring all required libraries and custom artifacts (whl files) are properly declared as model dependencies to prevent container build and runtime failures.
* [Model Serving Deployment Container and Dependencies](/concepts/model-serving-deployment-container-and-dependencies.md) - How a production-grade container is built during deployment, including how to specify package dependencies via pip_requirements, conda_env, and extra_pip_requirements, and code dependencies via code_path for custom pyfunc models.
* [Model Serving Deployment Timeouts](/concepts/model-deployment-timeouts.md) - Timeouts that occur during the container build and model deployment phases of Databricks Model Serving, including retry logic and duration limits per workload type.
* [Model Serving Endpoint](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint.md) - A REST API endpoint on Databricks that serves custom ML models for real-time inference, accessed via a URL pattern like https://<instance>/serving-endpoints/<endpoint-name>/invocations
* [Model Serving Endpoint (Custom Models)](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-custom-models.md) - A Databricks endpoint that serves custom machine learning models via UI, REST API, MLflow Deployments SDK, or Workspace Client
* [Model Serving Endpoint (Databricks)](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-databricks.md) - A serverless endpoint that hosts one or more ML models for inference, configurable with instance profiles, workload sizes, and scale-to-zero settings.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Concurrency Scaling](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-concurrency-scaling.md) - The relationship between endpoint concurrency, client-side connections, and latency percentiles, where required concurrency scales linearly with concurrent connections for achieving target latency.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Configuration API](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-configuration-api.md) - The REST API endpoints and payload format for creating (POST /api/2.0/serving-endpoints) and updating (PUT /api/2.0/serving-endpoints/{name}/config) model serving endpoints with an instance_profile_arn field.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Configuration for Load Testing](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-configuration-for-load-testing.md) - Recommended endpoint configuration for load testing: start with a Small CPU endpoint with min/max concurrency of 4, enable route optimization, and disable scale-to-zero.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Configuration for Performance Testing](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-configuration-for-performance-testing.md) - Recommended Databricks endpoint configuration settings including CPU instance type, concurrency levels, route optimization, and scale-to-zero settings for load testing.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Configuration Methods](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-configuration-methods.md) - The four interfaces for configuring environment variables on model serving endpoints: Serving UI, REST API, WorkspaceClient SDK, and MLflow Deployments SDK.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Configuration with Resource Access](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-configuration-with-resource-access.md) - The overall pattern of configuring model serving endpoints to access external or private resources such as SaaS APIs and data storage locations
* [Model Serving Endpoint Configuration Workflow](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-configuration-workflow.md) - The overall process of creating or updating model serving endpoints with environment variables through UI, REST API, WorkspaceClient SDK, or MLflow Deployments SDK
* [Model Serving Endpoint Creation Methods](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-creation-options.md) - Three ways to create model serving endpoints in Databricks: the Serving UI, REST API, and MLflow Deployments SDK.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Creation Options](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-creation-options.md) - Three methods to create model serving endpoints: Serving UI, REST API, and MLflow Deployments SDK.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Creator Identity](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-creator-identity.md) - The calling identity recorded as the endpoint's creator, used for Unity Catalog access, immutable after creation, and continuously validated for workspace membership and grants.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Debugging Workflow](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-debugging-workflow.md) - Systematic approach to debugging Databricks Model Serving endpoints, starting with pre-deployment validation, then container build logs, followed by build-success or build-failure debugging paths.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Deployment](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-deployment.md) - The process of registering a custom pyfunc model to Unity Catalog or Workspace Registry and serving it via a Model Serving endpoint on Databricks.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Deployment Constraints](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-deployment-constraints.md) - Databricks Model Serving endpoints require that all model dependencies be statically captured at deployment time; network-based lazy loading and direct Unity Catalog volume access at inference time are not supported for real-time workloads.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Deployment for Custom Models](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-deployment.md) - After logging a custom pyfunc model, it can be registered to Unity Catalog or Workspace Registry and served via a Databricks Model Serving endpoint.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Deployment Timeouts](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-deployment-timeouts.md) - Timeouts that occur when deploying or updating a model serving endpoint, including container build retries and post-build deployment duration limits for CPU and GPU workloads.
* [Model Serving Endpoint for LangChain](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-for-langchain.md) - A Databricks model serving endpoint used to deploy a LangChain application that uses feature lookups for RAG
* [Model Serving Endpoint Scaling](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-scaling.md) - Automatic scaling of serving endpoints based on traffic and provisioned concurrency, including scale-to-zero after 30 minutes of inactivity, route optimization for high QPS, express deployments, and scaling behavior (immediate scale-up, 5-minute scale-down intervals).
* [Model Serving endpoint scaling behavior](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-scaling-behavior.md) - Serving endpoints auto-scale based on traffic: scale up near-instantly under load, scale down every five minutes, with optional scale-to-zero after 30 minutes of inactivity (cold start adds latency). Provisioned concurrency = QPS × execution time.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Status](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-status.md) - The operational states of a Databricks model serving endpoint, including Ready, Not Ready, and Update Failed indicators.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Telemetry](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-telemetry.md) - A Databricks feature that persists OpenTelemetry logs, traces, and metrics from custom model serving endpoints to Unity Catalog tables for monitoring and compliance.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Timeout Types](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-timeout-types.md) - Three categories of timeouts encountered when using Databricks Model Serving: model deployment timeouts, server-side timeouts, and client-side timeouts.
* [Model Serving Endpoint Timeouts](/concepts/model-serving-endpoint-timeouts.md) - Overview of the various timeout categories (deployment, server-side, client-side) encountered when using Databricks Model Serving endpoints.
* [Model Serving Endpoints (Databricks)](/concepts/model-serving-endpoints-databricks.md) - Custom model serving endpoints on Databricks that can be configured with route optimization for improved performance.
* [Model Serving Endpoints for Feature Store Models](/concepts/model-serving-endpoints-for-feature-store-models.md) - How to deploy and query models trained using Databricks Feature Store as model serving endpoints using Unity Catalog for online feature lookup.
* [Model Serving Environment Variables](/concepts/model-serving-environment-variables.md) - Mechanism for configuring access to external and private resources from Databricks model serving endpoints using plain text or secrets-based environment variables.
* [Model Serving Error Classification](/concepts/model-serving-error-classification.md) - Classification of error codes in Databricks Model Serving, distinguishing between customer-caused errors (4xx) and platform errors (5xx).
* [Model Serving Error Code Semantics](/concepts/model-serving-error-code-semantics.md) - Databricks Model Serving maps MlflowException model code errors to 4xx (customer-caused) responses and reserves 5xx for Databricks infrastructure errors, helping users determine responsibility.
* [Model Serving Error Code Semantics (4xx vs 5xx)](/concepts/model-serving-error-code-semantics.md) - Mapping of MLflowException to 4xx customer-caused errors and 5xx Databricks-responsibility errors in model serving endpoints.
* [Model Serving Error Response Mapping](/concepts/model-serving-error-code-semantics.md) - Databricks convention mapping MlflowException model code errors to 4xx responses (customer-caused) while reserving 5xx errors for Databricks infrastructure faults.
* [Model Serving for LLMs](/concepts/model-serving-for-llms.md) - Unified interface to deploy, govern, and query AI models, including handling external API calls, latency, and credential management for third-party LLM APIs
* [Model Serving Instance Profile](/concepts/model-serving-instance-profile.md) - An AWS IAM instance profile attached to a Databricks model serving endpoint, allowing models to access AWS resources (e.g., S3, Feature Store) during inference.
* [Model Serving Instance Profile Lifecycle Constraints](/concepts/model-serving-instance-profile-lifecycle-constraints.md) - Operational constraints around serving endpoint instance profiles: endpoints retain the old IAM role if the profile is edited in Databricks Settings, and deleting a profile does not affect running endpoints.
* [Model Serving Migration](/concepts/model-serving-migration.md) - The process of transitioning from Legacy MLflow Model Serving to the new Model Serving experience, including URL changes, scoring format changes, and endpoint reconfiguration.
* [Model Serving Networking and Security Limitations](/concepts/model-serving-networking-and-security-limitations.md) - Security restrictions for Model Serving endpoints including access control, PrivateLink support, outbound network policies, and security patching policies.
* [Model Serving on Databricks](/concepts/model-serving-on-databricks.md) - Databricks platform supports deploying trained models (e.g., YOLO) to production via Model Serving, a capability demonstrated in AI Runtime tutorials.
* [Model Serving on Databricks: Batch and Real-Time](/concepts/model-serving-on-databricks-batch-and-real-time.md) - Two model deployment patterns: batch inference for efficient large-scale scoring and real-time serving via low-latency API endpoints, with governance through Unity Catalog inference tables.
* [Model Serving Patterns](/concepts/model-serving-patterns.md) - Two primary deployment patterns on Databricks: real-time serving via low-latency REST endpoints (Model Serving) and batch inference via ai_query, Spark UDFs, or mlflow.pyfunc for periodic jobs.
* [Model Serving Pre-deployment Testing Workflow](/concepts/model-serving-pre-deployment-testing-workflow.md) - Databricks-recommended validation steps to catch model issues before endpoint deployment, including offline prediction testing, dependency checks, input validation, and local serving simulation.
* [Model Serving Region Availability](/concepts/model-serving-region-availability.md) - Regional limitations for Databricks Model Serving, including control plane dependencies and per-region feature availability for custom models, AI agents, and Foundation Model APIs.
* [Model Serving Resource and Payload Limits](/concepts/model-serving-resource-and-payload-limits.md) - Default limits that Databricks Model Serving imposes on custom model and AI agent endpoints to ensure reliable performance.
* [Model Serving Security and Data Protection](/concepts/model-serving-security-and-data-protection.md) - Security controls for Model Serving including logical isolation, AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.2+ in transit, no training on customer data, container build log retention (30 days), metrics retention (14 days), and abuse monitoring for Foundation Model APIs.
* [Model Serving Traffic Split](/concepts/model-serving-traffic-split.md) - Ability to route a fraction of inference traffic to models in Unity Catalog while gradually increasing the proportion for controlled rollouts
* [Model Serving with Automatic Feature Lookup](/concepts/model-serving-with-automatic-feature-lookup.md) - Mechanism where models trained using FeatureLookup automatically retrieve feature values from online tables during inference without additional configuration.
* [Model serving with on-demand features](/concepts/model-serving-with-on-demand-features.md) - Workflow for serving models that use on-demand features, covering batch scoring (score_batch) and real-time Model Serving with automatic UDF evaluation.
* [Model sharding](/concepts/model-sharding.md) - The technique of partitioning model parameters, gradients, and optimizer states across multiple devices to overcome single-GPU memory limitations.
* [Model Signature and Input Examples in MLflow](/concepts/model-signature-and-input-examples-in-mlflow.md) - Recommended practice of adding signatures (via infer_signature) and input examples when logging models; signatures are required for logging to Unity Catalog.
* [Model signature requirement for Unity Catalog](/concepts/model-signature-requirement-for-unity-catalog.md) - Models registered in Unity Catalog require a signature; if none exists, the MLFLOW_SKIP_SIGNATURE_CHECK_FOR_UC_REGISTRY_MIGRATION environment variable (MLflow ≥3.4.0) can bypass the requirement during migration.
* [Model Signatures in Unity Catalog](/concepts/model-signatures-in-unity-catalog.md) - Required schema definitions for new model versions in Unity Catalog, inferred automatically via autologging or input examples, and needed for input enforcement, AI functions, and model serving.
* [Model Tags in Unity Catalog](/concepts/model-tags-in-unity-catalog.md) - Key-value pairs applied to registered models and model versions for labeling and categorization, managed via MLflow Client API or Catalog Explorer.
* [Model Traces in Unity Catalog](/concepts/model-traces-in-unity-catalog.md) - Traces panel on the model version page that consolidates inference traces from development, evaluation (mlflow.evaluate()), and online serving endpoints for full observability.
* [Model Truncation for Feature Extraction](/concepts/truncated-model-feature-extraction.md) - The technique of removing the final classification layers of a pre-trained deep learning model so that it outputs feature tensors instead of predictions, enabling use as a feature extractor.
* [Model type comparison with Hyperopt SparkTrials](/concepts/model-type-comparison-with-hyperopt-sparktrials.md) - Using Hyperopt with SparkTrials to tune hyperparameters for and compare multiple model types to select the best overall model
* [Model Type Restriction on Endpoints](/concepts/model-type-restriction-on-endpoints.md) - The constraint that a single Databricks model serving endpoint cannot mix different model types (custom, foundation, external) — all served models must be of the same type.
* [Model Units](/concepts/model-units.md) - A unit of throughput that determines how much work a provisioned throughput endpoint can handle per minute, used to allocate inference capacity for foundation models.
* [Model Validation Before Deployment](/concepts/model-validation-before-deployment.md) - Using mlflow.models.predict to validate custom pyfunc models before deploying them to Model Serving endpoints
* [Model Validation Pipeline](/concepts/model-validation-pipeline.md) - A structured pipeline that loads a model from Unity Catalog, runs validation checks (format, metadata, performance, compliance), and determines whether a model should proceed to deployment by assigning aliases.
* [Model version page in Catalog Explorer](/concepts/model-version-page-in-catalog-explorer.md) - A Unity Catalog page providing an overview of all model performance and evaluation results across linked environments including different workspaces, endpoints, and experiments, used for monitoring, deployment, and approval workflows.
* [Model Version Promotion Across Environments](/concepts/model-version-promotion-across-environments.md) - Copying model versions across registered models in Unity Catalog (e.g., staging to prod) using the copy_model_version API, combined with aliases for deployment tracking.
* [Model Versioning and Stage Transitions](/concepts/model-versioning-and-stage-transitions.md) - A system within the Workspace Model Registry that tracks distinct versions of a registered model and allows transitioning them through lifecycle stages: None, Staging, Production, and Archived, with optional approval workflows.
* [Model-Specific vs Registry-Wide Webhooks](/concepts/model-specific-vs-registry-wide-webhooks.md) - Two scope-based webhook types: model-specific (applies to a single registered model, requires CAN MANAGE permission) and registry-wide (triggered by events on any model, requires workspace admin permissions).
* [Modeling External Lineage Relationships](/concepts/modeling-external-lineage-relationships.md) - Patterns for representing complex data flows through external metadata objects, including connecting two Unity Catalog tables via an intermediary object, modeling multi-hop pipelines, and adding column-level lineage with source-to-target mappings.
* [ModelOps on Databricks](/concepts/modelops-on-databricks.md) - Model development and lifecycle management using MLflow tracking and Model Registry, with monitoring of model performance and input data changes.
* [Models in Unity Catalog](/concepts/models-in-unity-catalog.md) - Databricks' integration of MLflow Model Registry with Unity Catalog for centralized model management, access control, auditing, and lineage.
* [Models Tab vs Catalog Explorer Model Version Page](/concepts/models-tab-vs-catalog-explorer-model-version-page.md) - The Models tab on the MLflow experiment page focuses on comparing logged models for selection, while the Catalog Explorer model version page provides a holistic view for monitoring and deployment across environments.
* [Monitoring dashboard parameters and widgets](/concepts/monitoring-dashboard-parameters-and-widgets.md) - User-editable widgets at the top of the dashboard that control data filtering by date range, data slices, models, and other parameters, varying by analysis type.
* [Monitoring Deep Learning Training with TensorBoard and Cluster Metrics](/concepts/monitoring-deep-learning-training-with-tensorboard-and-cluster-metrics.md) - Using pre-installed TensorBoard and Databricks cluster metrics (network, processor, memory, GPU) to inspect bottlenecks and monitor training processes.
* [Monitoring GPU Utilization with MLflow System Metrics](/concepts/monitoring-gpu-utilization-with-mlflow-system-metrics.md) - Using MLflow-recorded system metrics (GPU, CPU, memory usage) and Databricks dashboards to monitor compute efficiency across experiments, particularly identifying underutilized GPUs.
* [Mosaic LLM Foundry](/concepts/mosaic-llm-foundry.md) - An open-source framework for training, fine-tuning, evaluating, and deploying large language models with built-in support for distributed training strategies like FSDP.
* [Mosaic Streaming](/concepts/mosaic-streaming.md) - An open-source data loading library for single-node or distributed training/evaluation of deep learning models, compatible with PyTorch, PyTorch Lightning, Mosaic Composer, and TorchDistributor.
* [Mosaic Streaming (MDS) format](/concepts/mosaic-streaming-mds-format.md) - A data format used for preparing and streaming training data, demonstrated in the context of distributed training on Databricks.
* [Mount a Shared Genie Space](/concepts/mount-a-shared-genie-space.md) - The process by which recipients of a shared Genie Space can mount the share to create a local Genie Space pre-loaded with the provider's data and instructions.
* [Mounted Genie Space Ownership and Isolation](/concepts/mounted-genie-space-ownership-and-isolation.md) - The recipient has full ownership of the mounted Genie Space — changes made locally do not affect the provider's original space, and the recipient can add data, modify instructions, and reconfigure freely.
* [Multi-Agent Workflow Termination](/concepts/multi-agent-workflow-termination.md) - The pattern of using a TERMINATE keyword in agent messages to signal the end of a multi-agent conversation
* [Multi-cluster Write Limitations for Delta Lake on S3](/concepts/multi-cluster-write-limitations-on-s3.md) - Delta Lake tables on S3 support multi-cluster writes only within a single Databricks workspace due to S3's eventual consistency model; cross-workspace writes risk data loss or corruption.
* [Multi-cluster Write Limitations on S3](/concepts/multi-cluster-write-limitations-on-s3.md) - Delta Lake tables on S3 have multi-cluster write guarantees limited to a single Databricks workspace due to S3's eventual consistency model; modifying the same table from different workspaces risks data corruption.
* [Multi-cluster writes feature toggle](/concepts/multi-cluster-writes-feature-toggle.md) - The spark.databricks.delta.multiClusterWrites.enabled setting controls whether Delta Lake tables on S3 support concurrent writes from multiple clusters; disabling it requires single-cluster access.
* [Multi-Dimensional Access Control with Single Lookup Table](/concepts/multi-dimensional-access-control-with-single-lookup-table.md) - Replacing N groups per access dimension with a single table where each row represents a user's permissions across multiple dimensions (region, department, clearance level), eliminating combinatorial group explosion.
* [Multi-Dimensional Feedback Assessment](/concepts/multi-dimensional-feedback-assessment.md) - Collecting separate ratings for different quality dimensions (accuracy, helpfulness, relevance) and logging each as an individual assessment on a single trace.
* [Multi-Dimensional Feedback Collection](/concepts/multi-dimensional-feedback-collection.md) - Collecting feedback across multiple rating dimensions (e.g., accuracy, helpfulness, relevance) by logging each as a separate assessment with normalized scores, enabling granular quality analysis.
* [Multi-Dimensional User Feedback](/concepts/multi-dimensional-user-feedback.md) - Logging separate assessments for different quality dimensions (accuracy, helpfulness, relevance) to enable granular analysis
* [Multi-domain column masking with sensitivity tiers](/concepts/multi-domain-column-masking-with-sensitivity-tiers.md) - A pattern for applying column-level masks that vary by both domain ownership and sensitivity classification, using AND conditions in MATCH COLUMNS to target specific combinations of governed tags.
* [Multi-Experiment Run Comparison](/concepts/multi-experiment-run-comparison.md) - Ability to display and compare runs from multiple experiments in the MLflow Chart View, enabling cross-experiment analysis.
* [Multi-Experiment Run Comparison in MLflow](/concepts/multi-experiment-run-comparison.md) - The capability to display and compare runs across multiple experiments simultaneously in the MLflow UI.
* [Multi-Feedback Scorer Pattern](/concepts/multi-feedback-scorer-pattern.md) - A single custom scorer returning a list of Feedback objects to assess multiple quality facets in one pass, each with a unique metric name.
* [Multi-framework Auto-tracing](/concepts/multi-framework-auto-tracing.md) - Ability to enable automatic tracing for multiple AI frameworks (e.g., OpenAI and LangChain) simultaneously in the same agent workflow, producing unified traces.
* [Multi-Framework Auto-Tracing in MLflow](/concepts/multi-framework-auto-tracing.md) - The ability to enable automatic tracing for multiple frameworks (e.g., OpenAI and LangChain) simultaneously in the same agent, producing a single unified trace.
* [Multi-Framework Trace Consolidation on Databricks](/concepts/multi-framework-trace-consolidation-on-databricks.md) - Architectural benefit of routing traces from Langfuse and other frameworks into a single Unity Catalog-backed storage, enabling unified querying, comparison, and governance.
* [Multi-GPU distributed training (Databricks)](/concepts/multi-gpu-distributed-training-databricks.md) - Notebook examples for scaling training across multiple GPUs and nodes on Databricks using the Serverless GPU API.
* [Multi-GPU distributed training API](/concepts/multi-gpu-distributed-training-api.md) - Beta-stage API on Databricks for scaling training workloads across multiple GPUs and nodes.
* [Multi-GPU Distributed Training on Databricks](/concepts/multi-gpu-distributed-training-on-databricks.md) - Scaling model training across multiple GPUs and nodes using Databricks AI Runtime, with support for H100 GPUs, parallelism techniques (DDP, FSDP, DeepSpeed), and serverless GPU Python library tutorials.
* [Multi-GPU Distributed Training with Serverless GPU API](/concepts/multi-gpu-distributed-training-with-serverless-gpu-api.md) - API for scaling deep learning training workloads across multiple GPUs and nodes on Databricks, currently in Beta.
* [Multi-GPU Training APIs on Databricks](/concepts/multi-gpu-training-apis-on-databricks.md) - The two parallel approaches for multi-GPU workload training on Databricks: the AI Runtime CLI for laptop-based submission and the in-notebook Python API with @distributed and @ray_launch decorators
* [Multi-GPU workload Python API](/concepts/multi-gpu-workload-python-api.md) - In-notebook Python API using `@distributed` and `@ray_launch` decorators as an alternative to the AI Runtime CLI for distributed training.
* [Multi-Image Input in Vision Model Queries](/concepts/multi-image-input-in-vision-model-queries.md) - The capability to send multiple images in a single Chat Completions API request, allowing vision models to analyze and compare images side by side.
* [Multi-Integration Auto Tracing in MLflow](/concepts/multi-integration-auto-tracing-in-mlflow.md) - Enabling auto-tracing for several GenAI libraries simultaneously to produce a single cohesive trace across combined toolchains.
* [Multi-Judge Evaluation Framework](/concepts/multi-judge-evaluation-framework.md) - The practice of combining multiple custom judges (e.g., issue resolution, expected behaviors, tool call correctness) in a single `mlflow.genai.evaluate()` call to assess different quality dimensions of a GenAI agent simultaneously.
* [Multi-Judge Evaluation Pipelines](/concepts/multi-judge-evaluation-pipelines.md) - The pattern of passing multiple custom judges as scorers to mlflow.genai.evaluate() to evaluate different aspects of an agent (issue resolution, expected behaviors, tool correctness) in a single run.
* [Multi-Level Human Feedback in GenAI Pipelines](/concepts/multi-level-human-feedback-in-genai-pipelines.md) - A pattern for collecting feedback at three distinct stages: end-user (in-app thumbs up/down), developer (UI annotations), and domain expert (structured labeling sessions)
* [Multi-LLM Call Tracing Pattern](/concepts/multi-llm-call-tracing-pattern.md) - Design pattern where automatic tracing captures multiple sequential LLM calls within a single workflow, illustrated by an analysis-then-response pattern with conditional model routing.
* [Multi-Model Serving](/concepts/multi-model-serving.md) - The ability to serve multiple models (e.g., different sizes of Llama) within a single serving endpoint, each with its own configuration.
* [Multi-Model Serving Endpoint](/concepts/multi-model-serving-endpoint.md) - A Databricks model serving endpoint that hosts multiple models simultaneously to enable A/B testing, traffic splitting, and side-by-side comparison.
* [Multi-Model Strategy in Agent Systems](/concepts/multi-model-strategy-in-agent-systems.md) - The practice of using different LLMs for different stages of a multi-prompt pipeline, such as a stronger model for planning and a more cost-efficient model for execution.
* [Multi-Module Artifact Upload for Databricks Connect](/concepts/multi-module-artifact-upload-for-databricks-connect.md) - Strategy for handling UDFs in multi-module SBT projects where each module's classes must be uploaded separately using addCompiledArtifacts() with getClass references from each module.
* [Multi-node FSDP fine-tuning on AI Runtime](/concepts/multi-node-fsdp-fine-tuning-on-ai-runtime.md) - Supervised fine-tuning of LLMs (e.g., Llama-3.1-8B) across multiple nodes using PyTorch Fully Sharded Data Parallel on H100 GPUs
* [Multi-node FSDP Fine-tuning on Databricks](/concepts/multi-node-llm-fine-tuning-with-fsdp.md) - Pattern for supervised fine-tuning of large language models (e.g., Llama-3.1-8B) across multiple nodes (e.g., 16 H100 GPUs on 2 nodes) using PyTorch FSDP and torchrun on Databricks AI Runtime
* [Multi-node LLM fine-tuning with FSDP](/concepts/multi-node-llm-fine-tuning-with-fsdp.md) - A distributed-training pattern using PyTorch FSDP and torchrun to fine-tune Llama-3.1-8B across 16 H100 GPUs (2 nodes), with MLflow logging and Unity Catalog checkpointing.
* [Multi-node LLM fine-tuning with FSDP on Databricks](/concepts/multi-node-llm-fine-tuning-with-fsdp.md) - Distributed supervised fine-tuning of Llama-3.1-8B across 16 H100 GPUs (2 nodes) using torchrun and PyTorch Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP), with MLflow logging and Unity Catalog checkpointing.
* [Multi-Prompt Optimization](/concepts/multi-prompt-optimization.md) - The technique of jointly optimizing multiple chained prompts in an agent system using GEPA (GepaPromptOptimizer) to improve overall pipeline performance.
* [Multi-Series Forecasting](/concepts/multi-series-forecasting.md) - A forecasting approach where data is grouped by identifier columns into multiple independent time series, and Databricks trains a separate model for each series.
* [Multi-Series Forecasting in AutoML](/concepts/multi-series-forecasting-in-automl.md) - Technique in Databricks AutoML where time series identifiers group data into multiple independent time series, with each series trained independently.
* [Multi-Series Forecasting with Time Series Identifiers](/concepts/multi-series-forecasting-with-time-series-identifiers.md) - The ability to forecast multiple independent time series simultaneously by designating columns that identify each series, with a model trained per series.
* [Multi-Tenant App for Delta Sharing](/concepts/multi-tenant-app-for-delta-sharing.md) - Databricks-registered multi-tenant OAuth app (DeltaSharing) with client ID 64978f70-f6a6-4204-a29e-87d74bfea138 used by Power BI and Tableau recipients
* [Multi-threaded tracing in MLflow](/concepts/multi-threaded-tracing-in-mlflow.md) - MLflow Tracing is thread-safe with ContextVar isolation per thread, requiring manual context copying via contextvars.copy_context() and ctx.run() to propagate traces across threads.
* [Multi-threaded Tracing with ContextVar](/concepts/multi-threaded-tracing-in-mlflow.md) - MLflow Tracing is thread-safe using Python's ContextVar mechanism, but requires manual context copying via contextvars.copy_context() when spawning traces across threads with ThreadPoolExecutor.
* [Multi-threading with MLflow Tracing](/concepts/multi-threading-with-mlflow-tracing.md) - Thread-safe tracing in MLflow using ContextVar, requiring manual context copying across threads via contextvars.copy_context() for correct parent-child span relationships.
* [Multi-turn Assessment Storage](/concepts/multi-turn-assessment-storage.md) - Design pattern where multi-turn assessments are stored on the first trace (chronologically) in each session to ensure stability and easy retrieval.
* [Multi-turn Conversation Evaluation](/concepts/multi-turn-conversation-evaluation.md) - A paradigm for assessing conversational AI systems at the session level rather than individual turns, capturing user frustration, conversation completeness, knowledge retention, and dialogue coherence.
* [Multi-turn Conversation Judges](/concepts/multi-turn-conversation-judges.md) - Scorers that evaluate entire conversations by grouping traces via session tags, assessing quality patterns like user frustration and conversation completeness across multiple interactions.
* [Multi-turn Evaluation with Simulated Conversations](/concepts/multi-turn-evaluation-with-simulated-conversations.md) - Using the ConversationSimulator as input to mlflow.genai.evaluate() alongside multi-turn scorers (ConversationCompleteness) and single-turn scorers (Safety) applied per turn.
* [Multi-turn Function Calling](/concepts/multi-turn-function-calling.md) - Function calling across multiple conversational turns, recommended for Claude models on Databricks; Llama models currently optimized for single-turn only.
* [Multi-turn Judge](/concepts/multi-turn-judge.md) - A type of LLM judge that evaluates entire conversational histories across multiple interaction turns rather than scoring individual response turns in isolation.
* [Multi-turn Judges](/concepts/multi-turn-judge.md) - LLM judges that evaluate entire conversations rather than individual turns, analyzing the complete conversation history for quality patterns over multiple interactions.
* [Multi-turn LLM Judges](/concepts/multi-turn-llm-judges.md) - LLM-based evaluators that analyze full conversation history using built-in scorers like ConversationCompleteness and UserFrustration, or custom judges via make_judge with the {{ conversation }} template variable.
* [Multi-turn Scorers in MLflow](/concepts/multi-turn-scorers-in-mlflow.md) - Evaluation scorers that assess conversational AI agents across multiple turns, such as ConversationCompleteness and Safety, applicable within the conversation simulation evaluation workflow.
* [Multi-User Collaboration on AI Runtime](/concepts/multi-user-collaboration-on-ai-runtime.md) - Best practices for sharing code, using Git folders, and collaborating on notebooks in Databricks AI Runtime.
* [Multi-User Collaboration on Databricks](/concepts/multi-user-collaboration-on-ai-runtime.md) - Best practices for sharing code and notebooks on Databricks, including using /Workspace/Shared for shared artifacts and Git folders with remote repos for active development.
* [Multi-user Collaboration on Databricks AI Runtime](/concepts/multi-user-collaboration-on-ai-runtime.md) - Best practices for sharing code via /Workspace/Shared, using Git folders for active development, and collaborating on notebooks for multi-user ML projects.
* [Multilingual Chain-of-Thought Fine-Tuning](/concepts/multilingual-chain-of-thought-fine-tuning.md) - Fine-tuning language models on multilingual datasets like HuggingFaceH4/Multilingual-Thinking to enable chain-of-thought reasoning across languages
* [Multilingual chain-of-thought reasoning](/concepts/multilingual-chain-of-thought-reasoning.md) - Capability of a language model to perform internal reasoning in one language while generating responses in another, enabled by multilingual CoT datasets
* [Multimodal Foundation Models](/concepts/multimodal-foundation-models.md) - Foundation models that accept multiple input types (text, image, video, audio) alongside text, enabling cross-modal reasoning tasks like image understanding, video analysis, and visual Q&A.
* [Multiple ABAC Policy Resolution Rules](/concepts/multiple-abac-policy-resolution-rules.md) - Only one distinct row filter or column mask can resolve per user per table or column at runtime, otherwise Databricks blocks access
* [Multiple Client Options for Embedding Queries](/concepts/multiple-client-options-for-embedding-queries.md) - Various client interfaces supported for querying embedding models on Databricks including OpenAI client, SQL, REST API, MLflow Deployments SDK, Databricks Python SDK, and LangChain.
* [Multiple feedback from a single scorer](/concepts/multiple-feedback-from-a-single-scorer.md) - A pattern where a single custom scorer returns a list of Feedback objects, each with a unique name, to assess multiple quality facets simultaneously in one evaluation pass
* [Multiple Feedback objects from a single scorer](/concepts/multiple-feedback-objects-from-a-single-scorer.md) - A single custom scorer can return a list of Feedback objects, each with a unique name, to assess multiple quality facets (e.g., PII, sentiment, conciseness) simultaneously.
* [Multiple Framework Auto-Tracing](/concepts/multi-framework-auto-tracing.md) - Pattern enabling simultaneous automatic tracing of multiple AI frameworks (e.g., OpenAI and LangChain) in a single agent workflow to produce unified, debuggable traces.
* [Multiple Judge Evaluation](/concepts/multiple-judge-evaluation.md) - Using multiple custom judges together to evaluate different aspects of a GenAI agent simultaneously within a single evaluation run.
* [Multiple Row Filter and Column Mask Conflict Resolution](/concepts/multiple-row-filter-and-column-mask-conflict-resolution.md) - Rules ensuring only one distinct row filter per table and one distinct column mask per column can apply at runtime; conflicts block access and require diagnosis via SHOW EFFECTIVE POLICIES and INFORMATION_SCHEMA views.
* [Multiple Served Entities per Endpoint](/concepts/multiple-served-entities-per-endpoint.md) - A single serving endpoint can serve multiple models or model versions with configurable traffic splits between them.
* [Multiple Source Row Match Error in MERGE](/concepts/multiple-source-row-match-error-in-merge.md) - A MERGE operation fails with DELTA_MULTIPLE_SOURCE_ROW_MATCHING_TARGET_ROW_IN_MERGE error if more than one source row matches the same target row, requiring preprocessing of the source table to eliminate ambiguity.
* [MULTIPLE_RELATIONS constraint for table_changes](/concepts/multiple_relations-constraint-for-table_changes.md) - A validation rule requiring that views used with table_changes reference exactly one relation (table or view), not multiple
* [MULTIPLE_RELATIONS error reason](/concepts/multiple_relations-sub-error.md) - A sub-reason of DELTA_TABLE_CHANGES_VIEW_UNSUPPORTED indicating the view references more than one relation.
* [MULTIPLE_RELATIONS sub-error](/concepts/multiple_relations-sub-error.md) - A specific cause of DELTA_TABLE_CHANGES_VIEW_UNSUPPORTED where the view references more than one relation
* [MUST_REFRESH_SAME_TABLE](/concepts/must_refresh_same_table.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_UNIFORM_ICEBERG_INGRESS_VIOLATION that occurs when attempting to refresh an Iceberg table with metadata from a different table UUID where the version must increase monotonically.
* [MUST_REFRESH_SAME_TABLE error](/concepts/must_refresh_same_table.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_UNIFORM_ICEBERG_INGRESS_VIOLATION that occurs when attempting to refresh an existing Apache Iceberg table UUID with metadata from a different Iceberg table UUID, or when the refresh metadata location has a lower version than the existing location.
* [Mutability Rules for Trace Tags vs Metadata](/concepts/mutability-rules-for-trace-tags-vs-metadata.md) - The distinction that tags are mutable after trace logging while metadata becomes immutable once a trace is logged, influencing how context should be attached.
* [Mutable Tags vs Immutable Metadata](/concepts/mutable-tags-vs-immutable-metadata.md) - In MLflow tracing, tags on logged traces can be mutated after logging while metadata becomes immutable once the trace is logged, making metadata suitable for immutable identifiers like user IDs.
* [Mutable Tags vs Immutable Metadata in MLflow Traces](/concepts/mutable-tags-vs-immutable-metadata.md) - Distinction between tags (mutable after trace logging) and metadata (immutable after trace logging) in MLflow trace context
* [MXFP4 Quantization](/concepts/mxfp4-quantization.md) - Microscaling 4-bit floating point format that reduces memory requirements during model training and inference by representing weights in a compact 4-bit format with per-group scaling factors.
* [Mxfp4 Quantization for Large Model Loading](/concepts/mxfp4-quantization-for-model-loading.md) - A 4-bit quantization format (Mxfp4Config) used to reduce memory footprint when loading large models, with dequantization happening during forward passes to maintain training fidelity.
* [Mxfp4 Quantization for Model Loading](/concepts/mxfp4-quantization-for-model-loading.md) - A 4-bit floating point quantization format used to reduce memory footprint when loading large models (e.g., 120B parameters) into GPU memory during fine-tuning.
* [Named Entity Recognition with Spark NLP and MLflow](/concepts/named-entity-recognition-with-spark-nlp-and-mlflow.md) - Training a named entity recognition (NER) model using Spark NLP and tracking/saving it with MLflow for reusable inference on Databricks.
* [Navigating to the Data Profiling Dashboard](/concepts/accessing-the-data-profiling-dashboard.md) - Two methods to access the data profiling dashboard in Databricks: via the Catalog Explorer UI (Quality tab on a table) or via the Dashboards sidebar listing.
* [NCCL and Gloo Environment Variable Fixes for Distributed Training](/concepts/nccl-and-gloo-environment-variable-fixes-for-distributed-training.md) - Environment variable configurations (NCCL_P2P_DISABLE, NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME, GLOO_SOCKET_IFNAME) to resolve common GPU/CPU distributed training failures on Databricks
* [NCCL Errors in Multi-Node GPU Training](/concepts/nccl-errors-in-multi-node-gpu-training.md) - Common NCCL failures on Databricks including peer access issues on G5 GPUs and network interface misconfiguration, with environment variable workarounds.
* [NCCL Network Communication Failure in Multi-Node GPU Training](/concepts/nccl-network-communication-failure-in-multi-node-gpu-training.md) - Troubleshooting NCCL failures during multi-node GPU training by setting spark.executorEnv.NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME to eth for correct network interface selection.
* [NCCL Network Configuration for Distributed GPU XGBoost](/concepts/nccl-network-configuration-for-distributed-gpu-xgboost.md) - Troubleshooting NCCL communication failures in multi-node GPU training by setting NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME environment variable to eth
* [NCCL Network Configuration for Distributed XGBoost](/concepts/nccl-network-configuration-for-distributed-xgboost.md) - Troubleshooting NCCL communication failures in distributed XGBoost training by setting the NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME environment variable on Databricks clusters.
* [NCCL Network Error Troubleshooting for XGBoost](/concepts/nccl-network-troubleshooting-for-xgboost.md) - Troubleshooting NCCL failures in multi-node GPU training by setting the spark.executorEnv.NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME configuration to eth
* [NCCL Network Troubleshooting for XGBoost](/concepts/nccl-network-troubleshooting-for-xgboost.md) - Troubleshooting NCCL network failures in multi-node GPU training by setting spark.executorEnv.NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME=eth to fix inter-GPU communication issues.
* [NCCL Socket Configuration for Spark XGBoost](/concepts/nccl-socket-configuration-for-xgboost.md) - Troubleshooting NCCL communication failures during multi-node GPU training by setting spark.executorEnv.NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME to eth.
* [NCCL Socket Configuration for XGBoost](/concepts/nccl-socket-configuration-for-xgboost.md) - Troubleshooting NCCL failures during multi-node GPU training by setting spark.executorEnv.NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME=eth to fix network communication issues.
* [Nested Function Tracing](/concepts/nested-function-tracing.md) - When traced functions call other traced functions, MLflow generates a hierarchical trace where parent-child span structure mirrors the call graph.
* [Nested MLflow Runs for Hyperparameter Tuning](/concepts/nested-mlflow-runs-for-tuning.md) - A pattern where CrossValidator or TrainValidationSplit logs tuning results as a parent run (containing the estimator metadata) and child runs (one per hyperparameter setting tested, with its evaluation metric).
* [Nested MLflow Runs for Model Tuning](/concepts/nested-mlflow-runs-for-tuning.md) - The logging structure where CrossValidator or TrainValidationSplit tuning results are organized as parent (main) runs and child runs per hyperparameter setting tested.
* [Nested MLflow Runs for Tuning](/concepts/nested-mlflow-runs-for-tuning.md) - Pattern where CrossValidator or TrainValidationSplit log tuning results as nested MLflow runs with a parent run for the estimator and child runs for each hyperparameter setting tested
* [Nested span tracing for complex workflows](/concepts/nested-span-tracing-for-complex-workflows.md) - Using @mlflow.trace decorators in combination with mlflow.start_span() context managers to create hierarchical parent-child span structures for multi-step pipelines.
* [Nested Span Workflows with Context Managers](/concepts/nested-span-workflows-with-context-managers.md) - Using mlflow.start_span() as a context manager within a @mlflow.trace-decorated function to create nested spans for complex, multi-step workflows like data pipelines.
* [Network Access for Serverless Materialization in Delta Sharing](/concepts/network-access-for-serverless-materialization-in-delta-sharing.md) - Shared view materialization uses serverless compute in the provider's region, which may be blocked by storage-layer firewalls or private links; requires allowlisting serverless compute IPs.
* [NetworkX](/concepts/networkx.md) - Python library for graph and network analysis suitable for small to medium-sized networks on a single compute node
* [No Isolation Shared Clusters](/concepts/no-isolation-shared-clusters.md) - A cluster type that does not respect the legacy Hive metastore disablement setting, requiring the Enforce User Isolation setting to prevent users from bypassing Unity Catalog governance.
* [No Isolation Shared Clusters and Legacy Access](/concepts/no-isolation-shared-clusters-limitation.md) - No Isolation shared clusters do not respect the legacy Hive metastore disablement setting; Enforce User Isolation must be enabled instead.
* [No Isolation Shared Clusters Limitation](/concepts/no-isolation-shared-clusters-limitation.md) - No Isolation shared clusters do not respect the legacy Hive metastore disablement setting, requiring the Enforce User Isolation setting to prevent users from bypassing the disablement.
* [NO_CLUSTERING_COLUMNS error subtype](/concepts/no_clustering_columns-error-subtype.md) - A specific error raised when a command requiring clustering columns is run on a Liquid table that has no clustering columns defined.
* [NO_HINT Sub-Error](/concepts/no_hint-sub-error.md) - A sub-condition of DELTA_SHARING_SECURABLE_DELETE_BLOCKED with no additional context, simply advising the user to use ALTER VIEW instead of deleting.
* [No-isolation shared clusters deprecation](/concepts/no-isolation-shared-clusters-deprecation.md) - No-isolation shared cluster access mode will not be available in new workspaces after September 30, 2026, requiring migration of compute to Unity Catalog access modes.
* [Nodes and Edges](/concepts/nodes-and-edges.md) - Fundamental building blocks of a graph: nodes (vertices) represent entities such as people or cities, and edges (links, relationships, arcs) represent connections or relationships between them.
* [NON_CLUSTERING_COLUMN error subtype](/concepts/non_clustering_column-error-subtype.md) - A specific error within DELTA_UNSUPPORTED_CLUSTERING_COLUMN_PREDICATES raised when a predicate references a column that is not a clustering column.
* [NON_DELTA error sub-condition](/concepts/non_delta-error-sub-condition.md) - A specific error sub-condition under DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE, raised when the source table is not a Delta format table.
* [NON_DELTA source error condition](/concepts/non_delta-error-sub-condition.md) - A specific sub-error of DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE raised when the source table format is not Delta (e.g., Parquet, CSV, JSON)
* [NON_DELTA source format error](/concepts/non_delta-source-sub-error.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE indicating the source table is not in Delta format, so cloning with history is unsupported.
* [NON_DELTA source restriction for clone with history](/concepts/non_delta-source-restriction-for-clone-with-history.md) - The error condition raised when cloning a Delta table with history from a source that is not in Delta format (e.g., Parquet, CSV, JSON).
* [NON_DELTA source sub-error](/concepts/non_delta-source-sub-error.md) - A specific sub-condition of DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE raised when the source table is not in Delta format.
* [NON_DELTA target error](/concepts/non_delta-target-error.md) - A subtype of DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_TARGET indicating the target table is not a Delta format table.
* [NON_DELTA target table restriction](/concepts/non_delta-target-table-restriction.md) - Delta clone with history does not support target tables that are not in Delta format.
* [NON_DETERMINISTIC_EXPRESSIONS constraint for table_changes](/concepts/non_deterministic_expressions-constraint-for-table_changes.md) - A validation rule requiring that views used with table_changes must not contain non-deterministic expressions
* [NON_DETERMINISTIC_EXPRESSIONS sub-error](/concepts/non_deterministic_expressions-sub-error.md) - A specific cause of DELTA_TABLE_CHANGES_VIEW_UNSUPPORTED where the view contains non-deterministic expressions
* [Non-Delta target restriction for clone with history](/concepts/non-delta-target-restriction-for-clone-with-history.md) - Cloning a Delta table with history to a non-Delta format target table is not supported in Databricks.
* [Non-Delta target tables in Delta Clone with History](/concepts/non-delta-target-tables-in-delta-clone-with-history.md) - An error sub-condition (NON_DELTA) where the target table for a Delta Clone with History operation is not in Delta format
* [Non-Express Deployments (Databricks)](/concepts/non-express-deployments-databricks.md) - The traditional model serving deployment approach where model artifacts and environments are packaged into containers at deployment time, which may result in environment mismatches with training.
* [Non-Express vs Express Deployments](/concepts/non-express-vs-express-deployments.md) - The contrast between traditional deployments (packaging artifacts at deployment time, risking environment drift) and express deployments (packaging at registration time for consistency).
* [Non-linear Token Work Scaling](/concepts/non-linear-token-work-scaling.md) - The principle that computational work required for inference grows non-linearly as input or output token counts increase, rather than scaling linearly.
* [Non-LLM Metrics in DeepEval](/concepts/non-llm-metrics-in-deepeval.md) - A category of DeepEval metrics that do not require an LLM as a judge, enabling evaluation without calling a language model.
* [Non-modifying Monitoring](/concepts/non-modifying-monitoring.md) - Design principle stating that data quality monitoring does not modify any tables it monitors nor adds overhead to jobs that populate those tables.
* [Non-Python Package Dependency Logging](/concepts/non-python-package-dependency-logging.md) - The challenge and recommended approach for logging non-Python dependencies (Java, R, native/Linux packages) as artifacts alongside MLflow models, since MLflow does not automatically capture them.
* [NOT EXISTS vs NOT IN Performance for DELETE](/concepts/not-exists-vs-not-in-performance-for-delete.md) - Databricks recommends using NOT EXISTS over NOT IN in DELETE WHERE clauses because NOT IN subqueries can be slow.
* [NOT IN subquery performance warning](/concepts/not-in-subquery-performance-warning.md) - DELETE with NOT IN subqueries can be slow; Databricks recommends using NOT EXISTS instead whenever possible.
* [NOT IN subquery restrictions in Databricks DELETE](/concepts/not-in-subquery-restrictions-in-databricks-delete.md) - In Databricks SQL DELETE FROM statements, NOT IN subqueries are not supported when nested inside an OR expression, and can be slow; NOT EXISTS is recommended as a faster alternative.
* [NOT IN vs NOT EXISTS for DELETE](/concepts/not-in-vs-not-exists-for-delete.md) - Preference for NOT EXISTS over NOT IN subqueries in DELETE statements due to performance considerations
* [NOT_DELTA_TABLE constraint for table_changes](/concepts/not_delta_table-constraint-for-table_changes.md) - A validation rule requiring that views used with table_changes must ultimately reference a Delta table
* [NOT_DELTA_TABLE error reason](/concepts/not_delta_table-sub-error.md) - A sub-reason of DELTA_TABLE_CHANGES_VIEW_UNSUPPORTED indicating the underlying view does not reference a Delta table.
* [NOT_DELTA_TABLE restriction](/concepts/not_delta_table-restriction.md) - A restriction that table_changes requires the target to be a Delta table; views referencing non-Delta tables are rejected.
* [NOT_DELTA_TABLE sub-error](/concepts/not_delta_table-sub-error.md) - A specific cause of DELTA_TABLE_CHANGES_VIEW_UNSUPPORTED where the view does not reference a Delta table
* [NOT_SHARED_VIEW error reason](/concepts/not_shared_view-error-reason.md) - A sub-reason of DELTA_TABLE_CHANGES_VIEW_UNSUPPORTED indicating the view is not an OpenSharing view.
* [NOT_UNIFORM_INGRESS_TABLE Error](/concepts/not_uniform_ingress_table-error.md) - An error raised when a table referenced in a Delta Uniform ingress operation is not actually configured as a uniform ingress table.
* [Notebook Development Workflow for Distributed PyTorch Training](/concepts/notebook-development-workflow-for-distributed-pytorch-training.md) - A four-step workflow to convert single-node PyTorch code to distributed training using TorchDistributor in notebooks
* [Notebook environment requirement](/concepts/notebook-environment-requirement.md) - @scorer-decorated functions used in production monitoring must be defined and registered from a Databricks notebook due to serialization constraints
* [Notebook experiment in Databricks](/concepts/notebook-experiment-in-databricks.md) - Each Databricks notebook has an associated MLflow experiment; its run data can be loaded by calling load() with no arguments on the mlflow-experiment data source.
* [Notebook experiment in Databricks MLflow](/concepts/notebook-experiment-in-databricks-mlflow.md) - The default experiment for MLflow runs when no experiment is explicitly set, where runs are automatically logged to the notebook's associated experiment.
* [Notebook Experiment Lifecycle and Restrictions](/concepts/notebook-experiment-lifecycle-and-restrictions.md) - Notebook experiments share name/ID with their notebook and cannot be independently deleted or renamed. Deleting a notebook experiment via UI or API also deletes the notebook. Git-folder notebook experiments can only be managed at the Git folder level.
* [Notebook-required function serialization](/concepts/notebook-required-scorer-serialization.md) - @scorer-decorated functions for production monitoring must be defined and registered from a Databricks notebook due to serialization requirements
* [Notebook-Required Scorer Serialization](/concepts/notebook-required-scorer-serialization.md) - @scorer-decorated functions used in production monitoring must be defined and registered from a Databricks notebook because the monitoring service serializes function code for remote execution and requires the notebook environment.
* [Notebook-Scoped Libraries and Cluster Policies for Deep Learning](/concepts/notebook-scoped-libraries-and-cluster-policies-for-deep-learning.md) - Customizing deep learning environments at notebook, cluster, and job levels using notebook-scoped libraries, cluster-level libraries, and cluster policies to enforce consistent configurations
* [NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3 on Databricks](/concepts/nvidia-h100-80gb-hbm3-on-databricks.md) - Configuration and use of NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3 GPUs via Databricks Serverless GPU compute for large model training
* [NVIDIA H100 GPU on Databricks](/concepts/nvidia-h100-gpu-on-databricks.md) - The NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3 accelerator available as a serverless GPU option on Databricks, offering 8 chips per node with 80GB memory each for large model training.
* [OAuth Client Credentials Grant (M2M) Flow](/concepts/oauth-client-credentials-grant-m2m-flow.md) - A machine-to-machine authentication flow where an application (nightly job, VM) uses client credentials to obtain short-lived JWTs from an IdP, used as OAuth tokens to access Databricks Delta Sharing endpoints.
* [OAuth Profile File (oauth_config.share)](/concepts/oauth-profile-file-oauth_configshare.md) - A JSON configuration file downloaded from the OIDC profile portal containing endpoint, tokenEndpoint, client credentials, and scope, used to configure the Delta Sharing Python client for OIDC-based access.
* [OAuth Token Authentication for Route-Optimized Endpoints](/concepts/oauth-token-authentication-for-route-optimized-endpoints.md) - Route-optimized endpoints require Databricks in-house OAuth tokens instead of personal access tokens for authentication.
* [OAuth token authentication for serving endpoints](/concepts/oauth-token-authentication-for-serving-endpoints.md) - OAuth tokens are the only supported authentication method for querying route-optimized endpoints; personal access tokens are not supported.
* [Object detection with RetinaNet on Databricks](/concepts/object-detection-with-retinanet-on-databricks.md) - A tutorial notebook demonstrating how to train an object detection model using RetinaNet on serverless GPU with Databricks AI Runtime.
* [Object detection with YOLO11n on Databricks](/concepts/object-detection-with-yolo11n-on-databricks.md) - A tutorial notebook demonstrating how to train a YOLO11n object detection model on the COCO128 dataset using serverless GPU, with MLflow tracking and Model Serving deployment.
* [Object Ownership in Hive Metastore](/concepts/object-ownership-in-hive-metastore.md) - The model where a user who creates a schema, table, view, or function becomes its owner with all privileges and grant authority; workspace admins can transfer ownership.
* [Object Ownership in Unity Catalog](/concepts/object-ownership-in-unity-catalog.md) - Every securable object has an owner who possesses all privileges on that object and can grant privileges to others. Ownership can be transferred by the current owner, a metastore admin, or a user with MANAGE privilege.
* [Objective Function Pattern for Hyperopt](/concepts/objective-function-pattern-for-hyperopt.md) - The pattern for writing Hyperopt objective functions: define a function taking hyperparameters as arguments, train/evaluate a model, and return a dictionary with 'loss' (to minimize) and 'status'.
* [Offline Feature Store](/concepts/offline-feature-store.md) - The storage layer for feature tables materialized as Delta tables, used for feature discovery, model training, and batch inference.
* [Offline-to-Production Evaluation Continuity](/concepts/offline-to-production-evaluation-continuity.md) - The principle that the same evaluation logic and scorers used during development can also run in production monitoring, providing consistent quality assessment across the AI lifecycle.
* [Offline-to-Production Monitoring Consistency](/concepts/offline-to-production-evaluation-continuity.md) - The principle that the same scorers and evaluation logic used in offline development can be reused in production monitoring, providing consistent quality metrics across the AI lifecycle.
* [OfflineStoreConfig](/concepts/offlinestoreconfig.md) - Configuration specifying the Unity Catalog catalog, schema, and table name prefix where materialized features are stored as Delta tables for offline use (training, batch scoring).
* [OIDC Federation for Data Sharing](/concepts/oidc-federation-for-data-sharing.md) - An authentication method using OpenID Connect federation for Databricks-to-Open sharing, supporting both user-to-machine (U2M) and machine-to-machine (M2M) flows, used with tools like Tableau and Power BI.
* [OIDC Federation for Delta Sharing](/concepts/oidc-federation-for-delta-sharing.md) - Authentication method using Open ID Connect federation to allow recipients without a Databricks workspace to access OpenSharing shares
* [OIDC Federation for M2M Delta Sharing](/concepts/oidc-federation-for-m2m-delta-sharing.md) - Using Open ID Connect federation with the OAuth Client Credentials grant flow to let non-Databricks recipients authenticate to Delta Sharing shares without long-lived bearer tokens.
* [OIDC Federation for OpenSharing](/concepts/oidc-federation-for-opensharing.md) - Authentication method that allows data providers to federate identity verification to a recipient's IdP using OIDC, enabling short-lived JWT tokens instead of long-lived bearer tokens for OpenSharing.
* [OIDC Federation Policy](/concepts/oidc-federation-policy.md) - A configuration policy specifying issuer URL, subject claim (oid, groups, azp), subject identifier, and audience(s) that the JWT must match for authentication to succeed.
* [OIDC Federation Policy Configuration](/concepts/oidc-federation-policy-configuration.md) - A policy object in Databricks that maps JWT claims (issuer, subject, audience) from a recipient's IdP to access permissions, specifying the issuer URL, subject claim type (oid, groups, or azp), subject value, and allowed audiences.
* [OIDC Profile Generation Portal](/concepts/oidc-profile-generation-portal.md) - Portal URL provided by Databricks providers that recipients use to obtain profile files or OAuth sign-in endpoints for U2M access
* [OIDC Token Federation (Delta Sharing)](/concepts/oidc-token-federation-delta-sharing.md) - An alternative authentication flow for Open Sharing that offers security and convenience advantages over the bearer token method, recommended as a replacement for long-lived tokens.
* [OIDC Token Federation for Open Sharing](/concepts/oidc-token-federation-for-opensharing.md) - An alternative authentication method to bearer tokens for Databricks-to-Open sharing, offering advantages in security and convenience for recipients without Databricks workspaces.
* [OIDC Token Federation for OpenSharing](/concepts/oidc-token-federation-for-opensharing.md) - An alternative authentication flow to bearer tokens for Databricks-to-Open sharing that offers improved security and convenience by using OpenID Connect federation.
* [OIDC vs Bearer Token Authentication](/concepts/oidc-vs-bearer-token-authentication.md) - Comparison between OIDC federation and long-lived Databricks-issued bearer tokens for OpenSharing, highlighting trade-offs in security, MFA support, and credential management.
* [OLD_ARCHITECTURE_NOT_SUPPORTED](/concepts/old_architecture_not_supported.md) - Sub-error indicating that only streaming tables using the default publishing mode are supported for deep clone operations.
* [OLD_ARCHITECTURE_NOT_SUPPORTED sub-error](/concepts/old_architecture_not_supported.md) - Sub-error indicating that only streaming tables using the default publishing mode are supported for deep clone; legacy architectures are rejected.
* [Ollama](/concepts/ollama.md) - An open-source platform for running large language models (LLMs) locally on personal devices, supporting models like Llama 3.2, Gemma 2, and Mistral.
* [Ollama OpenAI API Compatibility](/concepts/ollama-openai-api-compatibility.md) - Ollama's local endpoint is compatible with the OpenAI API format, allowing the OpenAI SDK client to query it by setting a custom base_url and dummy api_key.
* [Olmo3 7B Instruct Model](/concepts/olmo3-7b-instruct-model.md) - An open-source 7 billion parameter instruction-tuned language model from Allen AI, used as the base for fine-tuning
* [On-Demand Feature Computation for Real-Time Inference](/concepts/on-demand-feature-computation-for-real-time-inference.md) - Databricks Feature Store supports on-demand computation of features for real-time applications, handling all feature computation tasks to simplify client-side code.
* [On-demand Feature Computation with Python UDFs](/concepts/on-demand-feature-computation-with-python-udfs.md) - Pattern for computing features at inference time using registered Python UDFs that transform raw input data into feature values on the fly
* [On-Demand Feature Computation with UDFs](/concepts/on-demand-feature-computation-with-udfs.md) - Python UDFs registered in Unity Catalog can be used as FeatureFunctions to compute features on-demand during model inference
* [On-demand features (Databricks)](/concepts/on-demand-features-databricks.md) - Features computed at inference time using Python UDFs in Unity Catalog, used when feature values depend on request-time inputs.
* [On-demand features limitations](/concepts/on-demand-features-limitations.md) - Constraints on on-demand features: MapType and ArrayType output types are unsupported, and older library versions require extra Unity Catalog permissions.
* [On-Device LLM Inference](/concepts/on-device-llm-inference.md) - The paradigm of running large language models locally on user devices rather than relying on cloud-hosted inference APIs, with privacy and latency benefits.
* [One-vs-All Fairness Scoring for Multi-Class Models](/concepts/one-vs-all-fairness-scoring-for-multi-class-models.md) - A technique for computing fairness metrics across all predicted classes in a multi-class classification model by treating each class in a one-vs-all manner
* [Online Feature Computation and Serving](/concepts/online-feature-computation-and-serving.md) - On-demand computation of features for real-time applications with millisecond-latency serving endpoints.
* [Online Feature Serving](/concepts/online-feature-serving.md) - Real-time feature computation and lookup with millisecond latency, including on-demand computation, available via single-click endpoints in Databricks.
* [Online Feature Store](/concepts/online-feature-store.md) - A high-performance, low-latency serving layer for real-time ML inference, powered by Databricks Lakebase, that syncs feature tables from the offline store.
* [Online Feature Store (Databricks)](/concepts/online-feature-store-databricks.md) - A managed feature store that serves feature tables to model and feature serving endpoints, replacing legacy online tables for ML serving.
* [Online Feature Store Capacity and Compute Sizing](/concepts/online-feature-store-capacity-and-compute-sizing.md) - The capacity setting (CU_1, CU_2, CU_4, CU_8) that controls compute power for an online store, corresponding to Lakebase Provisioned capacity levels.
* [Online Feature Store Capacity and Cost Optimization](/concepts/online-feature-store-cost-optimization.md) - Best practices for managing online store capacity settings (CU_1 through CU_8) and minimizing costs by reusing stores, right-sizing, and deleting unused stores.
* [Online Feature Store Cost Optimization](/concepts/online-feature-store-cost-optimization.md) - Best practices for managing costs including reusing online stores across projects, right-sizing capacity starting with CU_2, and deleting unused stores to avoid continuous charges.
* [Online Feature Store Lifecycle Management](/concepts/online-feature-store-lifecycle-management.md) - Full CRUD operations for online stores and tables including creation, listing, retrieval, capacity updates, and deletion via the FeatureEngineeringClient and Databricks SDK.
* [Online Feature Store Publish Modes](/concepts/online-feature-store-publish-modes.md) - The publish_mode parameter (TRIGGERED, CONTINUOUS, etc.) that controls how and when online tables are synced with offline feature tables, replacing the legacy streaming parameter.
* [Online Feature Store Read Replicas](/concepts/online-feature-store-read-replicas.md) - Read replicas that distribute read traffic across multiple compute instances, reducing latency and improving scalability for high-concurrency workloads, with support for up to 3 replicas.
* [Online Feature Table Publishing](/concepts/online-feature-table-publishing.md) - The process of synchronizing offline feature tables to online stores using the publish_table API, including prerequisites like primary keys and Change Data Feed.
