* [Online Store Authentication for SQL Stores](/concepts/online-store-authentication-for-sql-stores.md) - The pattern of storing user/password credentials in Databricks secrets for SQL-based third-party online stores, as opposed to access keys used for DynamoDB.
* [Online Store Authentication via Secret Prefixes](/concepts/online-store-authentication-via-secret-prefixes.md) - Using write_secret_prefix and read_secret_prefix parameters in online store specifications to reference Databricks secrets for authentication.
* [Online Store Capacity and Cost Optimization](/concepts/online-feature-store-cost-optimization.md) - Best practices for managing online store costs including right-sizing capacity (CU_1 through CU_8), reusing stores across multiple projects, and deleting unused stores that continuously incur costs.
* [Online Store Capacity and Read Replicas](/concepts/online-store-capacity-and-read-replicas.md) - Online stores support configurable compute capacity (CU_1 through CU_8) and up to 3 read replicas for distributing read traffic and improving high-concurrency performance.
* [Online Store Feature Publishing](/concepts/online-store-feature-publishing.md) - The mechanism by which feature values are published to online stores for low-latency serving, with ArrayType and MapType features stored in JSON format.
* [Online Store Publishing](/concepts/online-store-publishing.md) - Tracking which online stores a feature table has been published to, visible in the Workspace Feature Store UI.
* [Online Store Publishing of Features](/concepts/online-store-publishing-of-features.md) - Features published to online stores for real-time serving with ArrayType and MapType stored in JSON format, providing millisecond-latency serving endpoints.
* [Online Table (Databricks)](/concepts/online-table-databricks.md) - Read-only, row-oriented, serverless copy of a Delta Table optimized for low-latency online access, used with Model Serving, Feature Serving, and RAG applications.
* [Online Table Integration with Lakehouse Federation](/concepts/online-table-integration-with-lakehouse-federation.md) - Online tables can be queried via Lakehouse Federation using Serverless SQL warehouses, but only for SELECT operations and intended for interactive/debugging use, not production.
* [Online Table Limitations](/concepts/online-table-limitations.md) - Constraints on online tables including 1 per source table, 1000 column max, 2TB metastore-wide preview limit, 750 MB/sec max read throughput, and restrictions on primary key data types.
* [Online Table Limitations and Constraints](/concepts/online-table-limitations.md) - Comprehensive set of constraints for Databricks online tables including metastore-wide size limits, column count limits, data type restrictions, primary key requirements, and throughput caps.
* [Online Table Overwrite and Merge Modes](/concepts/online-table-overwrite-and-merge-modes.md) - Two modes for updating online feature tables: 'overwrite' completely replaces the online table from offline data, while 'merge' allows conditional updates using filter_condition for selective row replacement.
* [Online Table Permission & Service Principal Model](/concepts/online-table-permission-service-principal-model.md) - A unique auto-generated service principal for each serving endpoint that allows autonomous data access from online tables, independent of the user who created the resource, with the principal lifetime tied to the endpoint lifetime.
* [Online Table Permission Model](/concepts/online-table-permission-model.md) - Permission requirements for creating and managing online tables, including SELECT/USE_CATALOG/CREATE_TABLE privileges, REFRESH privilege, and an auto-created service principal for endpoints.
* [Online Table Permissions and Security Model](/concepts/online-table-permission-model.md) - Permission requirements for creating and managing online tables, including Unity Catalog privileges and the automatic service principal model that enables endpoints to access data independently of the creating user.
* [Online Table Permissions Model](/concepts/online-table-permission-model.md) - Required Unity Catalog privileges (SELECT, USE CATALOG, USE SCHEMA, CREATE TABLE, REFRESH) and the automatic service principal created for endpoints.
* [Online Table Sync Modes](/concepts/online-table-sync-modes.md) - Three synchronization modes for updating online tables from source Delta tables: Snapshot (full copy), Triggered (manual/periodic incremental updates), and Continuous (real-time streaming sync).
* [Online Table Troubleshooting](/concepts/online-table-troubleshooting.md) - Common issues with online tables including missing Create option, unavailable sync modes, pipeline failures due to deleted source tables, firewall blocks, and exceeded metastore size limits.
* [Online Tables](/concepts/online-tables.md) - Unity Catalog tables optimized for low-latency serving of feature data, used to host structured data that RAG applications need to query in real time.
* [Online Tables (Databricks)](/concepts/online-table-databricks.md) - Read-only, row-oriented, serverless copies of Delta Tables optimized for low-latency online access, used with Model Serving, Feature Serving, and RAG applications.
* [Online Tables for RAG Applications](/concepts/online-tables-for-rag-applications.md) - Using online tables to host structured data for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) applications, where a feature serving endpoint provides low-latency lookups integrated with LangChain agents.
* [Online Tables in Unity Catalog](/concepts/online-tables-in-unity-catalog.md) - Real-time synchronised tables in Databricks Unity Catalog that enable low-latency lookups of structured data for serving in applications.
* [Online Tables Limitations](/concepts/online-table-limitations.md) - Boundaries including 2TB metastore-wide limit, 1000 column max, 64KB string limit, 2MB row max, 750 MB/s read throughput, and restrictions on certain source table types.
* [OnlineStoreConfig](/concepts/onlinestoreconfig.md) - Configuration for the online feature store used by model serving, creating Delta tables streamed to an Online Feature Store with the same name prefix.
* [Open Sharing (Databricks-to-Open Sharing)](/concepts/open-sharing-databricks-to-open.md) - An authentication flow in Databricks Delta Sharing that allows data providers to share data with recipients who do not have access to a Unity Catalog-enabled Databricks workspace, using bearer tokens or OIDC federation.
* [Open Sharing (Databricks-to-Open)](/concepts/open-sharing-databricks-to-open.md) - A Delta Sharing authentication flow that allows data providers to share data with recipients who do not have access to a Unity Catalog-enabled Databricks workspace, using bearer tokens or OIDC federation.
* [Open Sharing views in Databricks](/concepts/open-sharing-views-in-databricks.md) - A view type in Databricks that supports Open Sharing protocol, required by table_changes (views that are not Open Sharing views trigger the NOT_SHARED_VIEW error)
* [Open Source Commitment in Databricks ML](/concepts/open-source-commitment-in-databricks-ml.md) - Databricks' commitment to the open-source ML ecosystem by supporting frameworks like scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Hugging Face, and storing artifacts in open formats based on open-source MLflow, Unity Catalog, and Delta Lake.
* [Open source MLflow data portability](/concepts/open-source-mlflow-data-portability.md) - The core MLflow data model, API, and SDK are fully open source, ensuring users can export and use their MLflow data anywhere despite using the managed Databricks platform.
* [Open Source vs Databricks-Managed MLflow](/concepts/open-source-vs-databricks-managed-mlflow.md) - Key differences between the open-source MLflow and the Databricks-managed version, including exclusive enterprise features, Unity Catalog integration, and differences in telemetry collection defaults.
* [Open-Source Foundation of Databricks ML](/concepts/open-source-foundation-of-databricks-ml.md) - Databricks ML platform built on open-source technologies including MLflow, Unity Catalog APIs, and Delta Lake, ensuring portability of models, features, and data.
* [Open-Source ML Ecosystem on Databricks](/concepts/open-source-ml-ecosystem-on-databricks.md) - Full support for popular open-source ML frameworks (scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow, etc.) and open formats (Delta Lake, MLflow, Unity Catalog APIs) ensuring portability.
* [OpenAI and LangChain Credential Injection for Model Serving](/concepts/openai-and-langchain-credential-injection-for-model-serving.md) - Using secrets-based environment variables to securely pass credentials when deploying OpenAI and LangChain MLflow model flavors to Databricks serving endpoints
* [OpenAI API Compatibility in Databricks](/concepts/openai-compatible-api-interface.md) - Foundation Model APIs are compatible with the OpenAI client SDK and API, allowing users to use familiar OpenAI tooling to query models on Databricks.
* [OpenAI client compatibility](/concepts/openai-client-compatibility.md) - Foundation Model APIs are compatible with the OpenAI client SDK and API, allowing users to query Databricks-hosted models using familiar OpenAI tooling.
* [OpenAI High Risk Use Case Mitigation Framework](/concepts/openai-high-risk-use-case-mitigation-framework.md) - A structured set of mitigation requirements that end users must implement when deploying OpenAI models for high-risk use cases on Databricks.
* [OpenAI Responses API (on Databricks)](/concepts/openai-responses-api-on-databricks.md) - Databricks-hosted access to OpenAI's Responses API for GPT-5 and GPT-4o models, supporting text and image inputs.
* [OpenAI-compatible API (Databricks)](/concepts/openai-compatible-api-interface.md) - Databricks Foundation Model APIs are compatible with the OpenAI client SDK and API, allowing users to use familiar OpenAI tooling to query Databricks-hosted models.
* [OpenAI-Compatible API Auto-Tracing](/concepts/openai-compatible-api-auto-tracing.md) - MLflow leverages the OpenAI SDK to automatically trace Databricks Foundation Model calls because these models expose an OpenAI-compatible API, enabling reuse of mlflow.openai.autolog().
* [OpenAI-compatible API interface](/concepts/openai-compatible-api-interface.md) - Foundation Model APIs are compatible with the OpenAI client SDK and API, allowing users to use the OpenAI client for querying supported models.
* [OpenAI-Compatible Client for Databricks](/concepts/openai-compatible-client-for-databricks.md) - Using the OpenAI Python client to query Databricks-hosted chat models with a familiar chat completions interface.
* [OpenSharing](/concepts/opensharing.md) - An open standard for secure data sharing, supporting both Databricks-to-Databricks and Databricks-to-Open sharing models.
* [OpenSharing (Databricks Delta Sharing)](/concepts/opensharing-databricks-delta-sharing.md) - Databricks' implementation of the Delta Sharing protocol for sharing data across workspaces and platforms using Unity Catalog.
* [OpenSharing (Databricks-to-Open) Protocol](/concepts/opensharing-databricks-to-open-protocol.md) - An open sharing protocol that uses bearer tokens and credential files to provide secure read-only access to shared data, with providers managing credential expiration and rotation.
* [OpenSharing (Delta Sharing)](/concepts/opensharing-delta-sharing.md) - An open protocol for sharing datasets across platforms; objects shared via OpenSharing cannot be dropped — only altered — to prevent breaking downstream consumers.
* [OpenSharing Access Control Scope](/concepts/opensharing-access-control-scope.md) - The three specific endpoints affected by IP access lists: OpenSharing OSS Protocol REST API access, OpenSharing activation URL access, and OpenSharing credential file download.
* [OpenSharing Apache Spark Connector](/concepts/opensharing-apache-spark-connector.md) - A connector that enables reading shared data via Apache Spark using the delta-sharing-spark library, supporting batch queries, change data feed (CDF), structured streaming, deletion vectors, column mapping, and row tracking.
* [OpenSharing Audit Log Fields for Pre-signed URL Queries](/concepts/opensharing-audit-log-fields-for-pre-signed-url-queries.md) - Provider audit logs for deltaSharingQueriedTable events contain fields like scannedAddFileSize, numRecords, tableVersion, limitHint, and userAgent to track what was shared.
* [OpenSharing Audit Log Fields for STS Token Queries](/concepts/opensharing-audit-log-fields-for-sts-token-queries.md) - Provider audit logs for generateTemporaryTableCredentials events capture recipient_name, share_name, credential_type, operation, and table_url to track STS-token-based sharing.
* [OpenSharing Audit Log System](/concepts/opensharing-audit-log-system.md) - The system for auditing and monitoring data sharing events in Databricks Delta Sharing, capturing provider and recipient actions via system tables or audit log delivery.
* [OpenSharing Audit Logging Overview](/concepts/opensharing-audit-logging-overview.md) - Audit logging infrastructure for monitoring Delta Sharing (OpenSharing) events, covering both provider and recipient perspectives with logged events and error messages.
* [OpenSharing Audit Logs](/concepts/opensharing-audit-logs.md) - Audit logs that record actions taken by data providers and recipients in Delta Sharing, stored in system.access.audit or delivered to an S3 bucket
* [OpenSharing BI Tool Connectors (Power BI and Tableau)](/concepts/opensharing-bi-tool-connectors-power-bi-and-tableau.md) - Specialized connectors for Power BI Desktop and Tableau that allow users to discover, analyze, and visualize OpenSharing datasets through a graphical interface using credential files.
* [OpenSharing Catalog](/concepts/opensharing-catalog.md) - A special catalog type in Unity Catalog created from a share, providing read-only access to shared data assets (tables, views, volumes, notebooks) with a 3-level namespace structure (catalog.schema.table).
* [OpenSharing Credential File](/concepts/opensharing-credential-file.md) - A downloadable file used in Databricks-to-Open sharing with bearer tokens to authenticate and read shared data; can only be downloaded once and must be stored securely.
* [OpenSharing Egress Cost Management](/concepts/opensharing-egress-cost-management.md) - Strategies and tools provided by Databricks to monitor, manage, and minimize cloud vendor data egress fees when sharing data across regions using OpenSharing.
* [OpenSharing Egress Pipeline Notebooks](/concepts/opensharing-egress-pipeline-notebooks.md) - Two Databricks Marketplace notebooks (IP Ranges Mapping Pipeline and Egress Cost Analysis Pipeline) that use Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines to monitor egress usage patterns and costs by joining logs with cloud provider IP ranges and OpenSharing system tables.
* [OpenSharing Error Handling](/concepts/opensharing-error-handling.md) - Categorized error messages and error codes (e.g., PERMISSION_DENIED, SHARE_DOES_NOT_EXIST, TABLE_DOES_NOT_EXIST) logged for failed OpenSharing actions on both provider and recipient sides.
* [OpenSharing Error Logging](/concepts/opensharing-error-logging.md) - Standardized error messages logged for failed OpenSharing operations in both provider and recipient audit logs, including errors for missing entities, permission denials, and configuration issues
* [OpenSharing Error Message Catalog](/concepts/opensharing-error-message-catalog.md) - Standardized error messages logged in OpenSharing audit logs for both providers and recipients, covering permission, existence, and configuration failures.
* [OpenSharing for SAP BDC](/concepts/opensharing-for-sap-bdc.md) - The Delta Sharing-based framework used to enable bidirectional data sharing between Databricks and SAP Business Data Cloud, requiring the metastore to have OpenSharing enabled.
* [OpenSharing in Delta Sharing](/concepts/opensharing-in-delta-sharing.md) - The sharing protocol underlying Databricks Delta Sharing that, when active on a securable object, blocks its deletion to prevent breaking active sharing recipients.
* [OpenSharing IP Access List](/concepts/opensharing-ip-access-list.md) - An IP-based allow list that data providers can assign to recipients using the Databricks-to-Open sharing protocol to restrict access to shared data from specific IP addresses or CIDR ranges.
* [OpenSharing IP Access List Audit Logging](/concepts/opensharing-ip-access-list-audit-logging.md) - Audit events triggered by IP access list operations, including recipient create/update operations and denial of access to OpenSharing REST API, activation URL, and credential file download.
* [OpenSharing Limitations and Constraints](/concepts/opensharing-limitations-and-constraints.md) - A collection of known limitations across OpenSharing clients, including memory constraints, unsupported features for streaming tables and materialized views, column mapping/CDF limitations, and connector-specific restrictions.
* [OpenSharing Profile Path Syntax](/concepts/opensharing-profile-path-syntax.md) - The URL-like path format '<profile-path>#<share-name>.<schema-name>.<table-name>' used to locate shared tables in Spark DataFrame read operations.
* [OpenSharing Protocol](/concepts/opensharing-protocol.md) - Databricks protocol for sharing data with recipients who don't have a Unity Catalog-enabled Databricks workspace
* [OpenSharing Provider Object](/concepts/opensharing-provider-object.md) - A securable object in a recipient's Unity Catalog metastore that represents a data provider organization sharing data via OpenSharing on Databricks.
* [OpenSharing Python and Pandas Connector](/concepts/opensharing-python-and-pandas-connector.md) - A Python connector (delta-sharing library) that enables listing and reading shared tables as pandas DataFrames, with support for change data feed queries.
* [OpenSharing Python Client for Delta Sharing](/concepts/opensharing-python-client-for-delta-sharing.md) - The open-source Python library (delta-sharing >=1.3.1) used by non-Databricks recipients to list shared tables and read shared data via the SharingClient, configured with the oauth_config.share profile.
* [OpenSharing Recipient](/concepts/opensharing-recipient.md) - A person or group outside the data provider's organization who receives access to shared data; represented as a 'recipient' object in the provider's Databricks account.
* [OpenSharing Recipient Activation Link](/concepts/opensharing-recipient-activation-link.md) - A URL generated by Databricks when creating a recipient object that allows the recipient to download a credential file once for authenticating and accessing shared Delta Sharing data.
* [OpenSharing Recipient Creation Workflow](/concepts/opensharing-recipient-creation-workflow.md) - Step-by-step process for data providers to create an OIDC-based recipient in Databricks, configure federation policy, and share an OIDC profile portal URL with the recipient.
* [OpenSharing Recipient Object](/concepts/opensharing-recipient-object.md) - A named object in Databricks Unity Catalog that represents the identity of a user or group with whom data is shared via Delta Sharing/OpenSharing. It supports multiple authentication types: DATABRICKS (Databricks-to-Databricks), TOKEN (bearer token for non-Databricks users), OAUTH_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS, and OIDC_FEDERATION.
* [OpenSharing SecureConnect](/concepts/opensharing-secureconnect.md) - A Databricks feature that allows providers to share data from cloud storage behind firewalls or private endpoints without needing to allowlist each recipient's network individually.
* [OpenSharing Share](/concepts/opensharing-share.md) - A securable object in Unity Catalog used to share data and AI assets (tables, views, models, notebooks, volumes, etc.) with one or more recipients.
* [OpenSharing Share (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/opensharing-share.md) - A securable object in Unity Catalog that bundles data assets (tables, views, volumes, notebooks, AI models) for sharing with recipients via Delta Sharing / OpenSharing.
* [OpenSharing Share Object](/concepts/opensharing-share.md) - A securable object in Unity Catalog used to share data and AI assets (tables, views, volumes, notebooks, models, FeatureSpecs, Genie Spaces) with one or more recipients. Shares can contain assets from only one metastore and support dynamic addition/removal of assets.
* [OpenSharing Sharing Identifier](/concepts/opensharing-sharing-identifier.md) - A unique string in the format '<cloud>:<region>:<uuid>' that identifies a Unity Catalog metastore, used to establish Databricks-to-Databricks sharing connections.
* [OpenSharing using OIDC Federation](/concepts/opensharing-using-oidc-federation.md) - Pattern for data recipients to use OpenID Connect (OIDC) federation with an Iceberg REST Catalog client to access Delta Sharing/OpenSharing shares created in Databricks.
* [OpenSharing view compatibility](/concepts/opensharing-view-compatibility.md) - table_changes on views is only supported for OpenSharing views; non-OpenSharing views trigger NOT_SHARED_VIEW.
* [OpenSharing views](/concepts/opensharing-views.md) - A specific type of view in Databricks that supports open sharing protocols; referenced as a requirement for table_changes on views
* [OpenSharing with ABAC Policies](/concepts/opensharing-with-abac-policies.md) - The capability to share Databricks tables and views protected by Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) row filters and column masks through OpenSharing, requiring Databricks Runtime 16.4+ or serverless compute.
* [OpenSharing with ABAC-Secured Tables](/concepts/opensharing-with-abac-secured-tables.md) - How Delta Sharing (OpenSharing) works with tables governed by ABAC row filter and column mask policies
* [OpenSharing with OIDC Federation](/concepts/opensharing-with-oidc-federation.md) - An authentication method for Databricks-to-Open sharing using OpenID Connect (OIDC) token federation, supporting both User-to-Machine (U2M) and Machine-to-Machine (M2M) flows.
* [OpenTelemetry Collector Configuration for MLflow](/concepts/opentelemetry-collector-configuration-for-mlflow.md) - MLflow uses the standard OTLP exporter to send traces and metrics to an OpenTelemetry Collector, supporting configuration of endpoint URL, protocol (gRPC vs HTTP), headers, and service name through environment variables.
* [OpenTelemetry Compatibility](/concepts/opentelemetry-compatibility.md) - MLflow Tracing's compatibility with OpenTelemetry, an industry-standard observability specification, enabling export of trace data to existing observability stacks.
* [OpenTelemetry Compatibility in MLflow Tracing](/concepts/opentelemetry-compatibility.md) - MLflow Tracing's integration with the OpenTelemetry standard, allowing export of trace data to existing observability stacks.
* [OpenTelemetry Compatibility with MLflow Tracing](/concepts/opentelemetry-compatibility.md) - MLflow Tracing's compatibility with the OpenTelemetry observability standard, enabling export of trace data to existing observability stacks and services.
* [OpenTelemetry Export for MLflow Traces](/concepts/opentelemetry-export-for-mlflow-traces.md) - Compatibility between MLflow Tracing and the OpenTelemetry industry-standard observability specification, allowing trace data to be exported to existing observability stacks.
* [OpenTelemetry for Model Serving](/concepts/opentelemetry-for-model-serving.md) - Persists system logs, application logs, metrics, and traces to Unity Catalog Delta tables using OpenTelemetry for long-term retention and compliance.
* [OpenTelemetry Instrumentation for Custom Models](/concepts/opentelemetry-instrumentation-for-custom-models.md) - Using the OpenTelemetry SDK to instrument MLflow pyfunc models with custom counters, spans, attributes, and logging for endpoint telemetry capture.
* [OpenTelemetry MLflow Span Attribute Mapping](/concepts/opentelemetry-mlflow-span-attribute-mapping.md) - How OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic convention attributes map to MLflow span types and UI rendering in Databricks managed MLflow.
* [OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) Endpoint in Databricks MLflow](/concepts/opentelemetry-protocol-otlp-endpoint-in-databricks-mlflow.md) - Databricks exposes an OTLP-compatible API at /api/2.0/otel/v1/traces that accepts OpenTelemetry spans and stores them in Unity Catalog tables.
* [OpenTelemetry Trace Span Schema in Unity Catalog](/concepts/opentelemetry-trace-span-schema-in-unity-catalog.md) - The detailed schema of the trace_unified view including span structures, events, links, status, resource, and instrumentation scope fields.
* [OpenTelemetry Trace Storage in Unity Catalog](/concepts/opentelemetry-trace-storage-in-unity-catalog.md) - The foundational capability to store OTel trace data (spans, logs, annotations) as Delta tables in Unity Catalog, enabling SQL-based querying and integration with Databricks governance and processing tools.
* [OpenTelemetry Traces in Unity Catalog](/concepts/opentelemetry-traces-in-unity-catalog.md) - Storage of OTel spans, logs, and annotations as managed Delta tables in Unity Catalog with a defined schema and trace_unified view.
* [Optimized Writes for Partitioned Tables](/concepts/optimized-writes-for-partitioned-tables.md) - Technique to reduce the number of small output files produced by MERGE on partitioned tables by enabling optimized writes, addressing a common performance bottleneck.
* [Optuna](/concepts/optuna.md) - A lightweight Python framework for hyperparameter tuning with dynamic search spaces, parallelization via Joblib, and MLflow integration.
* [Optuna as Hyperopt replacement on Databricks](/concepts/optuna-as-hyperopt-replacement-on-databricks.md) - Databricks recommends Optuna for single-node hyperparameter optimization as a replacement for Hyperopt
* [Optuna for Hyperparameter Tuning](/concepts/optuna-for-hyperparameter-tuning.md) - Optuna provides adaptive hyperparameter tuning for machine learning models on Databricks, helping automate the search for optimal hyperparameters including batch size and learning rate combinations.
* [Optuna for single-node optimization on Databricks](/concepts/optuna-for-single-node-optimization-on-databricks.md) - Optuna is recommended by Databricks as the replacement for Hyperopt for single-node hyperparameter optimization workloads.
* [Optuna Hyperparameter Tuning on Databricks](/concepts/optuna-hyperparameter-tuning-on-databricks.md) - Using Optuna for adaptive hyperparameter tuning to optimize deep learning model performance, including batch size and learning rate tuning.
* [Optuna objective function](/concepts/optuna-objective-function.md) - A user-defined function that defines the hyperparameter search space and returns a metric value for Optuna to minimize or maximize
* [Optuna Trial object](/concepts/optuna-trial-object.md) - Core abstraction in Optuna that provides suggest methods to generate hyperparameter values during optimization
* [OSS Spark Iceberg REST Catalog Configuration](/concepts/oss-spark-iceberg-rest-catalog-configuration.md) - Configuration for open-source Apache Spark to connect to an Iceberg REST Catalog, requiring specific JARs (Iceberg Spark runtime and AWS bundle), SparkSession extensions, and OAuth2 authentication properties.
* [Output Reducers](/concepts/output-reducers.md) - Custom aggregation functions for streaming traces that consolidate yielded elements into a single span output, with patterns for token aggregation, metrics, and error collection.
* [Output Reducers for Streaming Traces](/concepts/output-reducers.md) - A mechanism to aggregate elements yielded by a traced generator or iterator into a single span output using a custom reducer function, with support for token aggregation, metrics aggregation, and error collection.
* [Override Feature Values in Online Scoring](/concepts/override-feature-values-in-online-scoring.md) - Users can override automatically looked-up feature values during REST API scoring by including the desired feature values in the API payload, as long as the data types match the model's expectations.
* [OVERWRITE_REQUIRED sub-error](/concepts/overwrite_required-sub-error.md) - A DELTA_METADATA_MISMATCH sub-error that occurs when schema or partitioning changes require setting overwriteSchema to true
* [overwriteSchema Option](/concepts/overwriteschema-option.md) - A Spark/Delta Lake DataFrameWriter option that allows overwriting the schema or changing partitioning of an existing Delta table.
* [overwriteSchema Option for Delta Tables](/concepts/overwriteschema-option.md) - A Spark DataFrameWriter option used to overwrite an existing Delta table's schema or change its partitioning.
* [overwriteSchema Option Pattern](/concepts/overwriteschema-option.md) - A Delta Lake DataFrameWriter option (.option('overwriteSchema', 'true')) used to force overwrite the table schema or change partitioning when writing, with restrictions when using replaceWhere.
* [Packaging Custom Libraries as Python Wheels for MLflow](/concepts/packaging-custom-libraries-as-python-wheels-for-mlflow.md) - Method of packaging custom or unavailable libraries as Python wheel files and including them when logging MLflow models via extra_pip_requirements or conda_env
* [Pandas Compatibility Issue in AutoML Model Serving](/concepts/pandas-dependency-compatibility-for-model-serving.md) - Known incompatibility between pandas version used by AutoML training and the model serving environment, resolved by editing the logged model's dependencies to include pandas==1.5.3.
* [Pandas Compatibility Issue with AutoML Model Serving](/concepts/pandas-dependency-compatibility-for-model-serving.md) - Common error and resolution for pandas version incompatibility when serving AutoML models with Databricks Model Serving
* [Pandas DataFrame Evaluation Pattern](/concepts/pandas-dataframe-evaluation-pattern.md) - Using Pandas DataFrames for GenAI evaluation when working with CSV files or existing data science workflows.
* [Pandas DataFrame Scoring Format](/concepts/pandas-dataframe-scoring-format.md) - A JSON-serialized format for scoring requests to Databricks Model Serving endpoints using Pandas DataFrame orientations, supporting both 'dataframe_split' (recommended) and 'dataframe_records' keys
* [Pandas Dependency Compatibility for AutoML Model Serving](/concepts/pandas-dependency-compatibility-for-model-serving.md) - Known pandas version incompatibility error when serving AutoML-built models, resolved by pinning pandas==1.5.3 via a helper script that edits the model's requirements.txt and conda.yaml.
* [Pandas Dependency Compatibility for Model Serving](/concepts/pandas-dependency-compatibility-for-model-serving.md) - Known issue where AutoML-built models can fail in Model Serving due to incompatible pandas versions, resolved by adding pandas==1.5.3 dependency via a provided script
* [pandas UDF for Distributed Inference](/concepts/pandas-udf-for-distributed-inference.md) - Using pandas User-Defined Functions (UDFs) in PySpark to parallelize deep learning model inference across a Spark cluster, treating inference as an embarrassingly parallel workload.
* [Pandas UDFs for Distributed Featurization](/concepts/pandas-udfs-for-distributed-featurization.md) - Using pandas UDFs (and Scalar Iterator pandas UDFs) on Databricks to apply pre-trained deep learning models across a cluster for scalable, distributed feature computation.
* [Pandas UDFs for Distributed Model Inference on Spark](/concepts/pandas-udfs-for-distributed-model-inference-on-spark.md) - Wrapping Hugging Face models in Pandas UDFs to distribute batch inference across worker nodes in a Spark cluster
* [Pandas Version Compatibility for AutoML Model Serving](/concepts/pandas-dependency-compatibility-for-model-serving.md) - Known issue where mismatched pandas versions between AutoML training and model serving endpoints cause 'No module named pandas.core.indexes.numeric' error, resolved by pinning pandas==1.5.3.
* [PandasDataset](/concepts/pandasdataset.md) - A GluonTS utility that converts a Pandas DataFrame into the internal dataset format required for model training and prediction.
* [Parallel Coordinates Chart in MLflow](/concepts/parallel-coordinates-chart-in-mlflow.md) - A visualization type in MLflow used to understand the effect of parameter settings on model performance and investigate relationships between parameters and metrics.
* [Parallel Coordinates Plot](/concepts/parallel-coordinates-plot.md) - A visualization type in MLflow for understanding the effect of parameter settings on model performance by investigating relationships between parameters and metrics.
* [Parallel Coordinates Plot for MLflow](/concepts/parallel-coordinates-plot.md) - A parallel coordinates visualization in MLflow that helps understand relationships between parameter settings and model performance metrics.
* [Parallel Requests Limits in Model Serving](/concepts/parallel-requests-limits-in-model-serving.md) - Workspace-enforced maximum on parallel inference requests, with mitigation via route optimized endpoints or client reduction and quota increase requests.
* [Parallelism vs adaptivity trade-off](/concepts/parallelism-vs-adaptivity-trade-off.md) - In Hyperopt, higher parallelism speeds up trials but reduces adaptivity since each iteration has fewer past results to inform new hyperparameter suggestions.
* [Parameter passing between Databricks notebooks](/concepts/parameter-passing-between-databricks-notebooks.md) - Techniques for passing string parameters and structured data (via temporary views, DBFS, or JSON) between notebooks using dbutils.notebook.run() and widgets.
* [Parameter Passing in Python Wheel Tasks](/concepts/parameter-passing-in-python-wheel-tasks.md) - How positional arguments (JSON array of strings) and keyword arguments (key-value pairs) are passed to a Python wheel task as command-line arguments.
* [Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT)](/concepts/parameter-efficient-fine-tuning-peft.md) - A category of techniques that fine-tune LLMs by updating only a small subset of parameters, reducing computational cost
* [Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) for LLMs](/concepts/parameter-efficient-fine-tuning-peft.md) - Techniques for fine-tuning large language models with fewer computational resources by updating only a subset of model parameters, as demonstrated in Databricks AI Runtime notebooks.
* [Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for LLMs](/concepts/parameter-efficient-fine-tuning-peft.md) - Techniques for fine-tuning large language models that update only a subset of parameters to reduce computational cost, as demonstrated in Databricks AI Runtime notebooks.
* [Parameterized Partition Sharing](/concepts/parameterized-partition-sharing.md) - A technique using the CURRENT_RECIPIENT() function to dynamically filter table partitions based on recipient properties, enabling a single share to serve multiple recipients with data boundaries maintained.
* [Parameterized Partition Sharing (Recipient Properties)](/concepts/parameterized-partition-sharing.md) - A technique for sharing table partitions that match data recipient properties (e.g., databricks.accountId, databricks.metastoreId, databricks.name, or custom properties). Enables a single share to dynamically deliver different data to different recipients while maintaining data boundaries, using the CURRENT_RECIPIENT() SQL function.
* [Parquet and Foreign Iceberg to Delta Clone](/concepts/parquet-and-foreign-iceberg-to-delta-clone.md) - The ability to incrementally clone Parquet and foreign Iceberg tables to Delta Lake format on Databricks, supporting both deep and shallow cloning.
* [Parquet Table Caching](/concepts/parquet-table-caching.md) - Caching support for Parquet tables in Databricks using the CACHE SELECT command.
* [Parquet to Delta Lake Conversion](/concepts/parquet-to-delta-lake-conversion.md) - Process of converting a directory of Parquet data files or a Parquet external table into a Delta Lake table using CONVERT TO DELTA.
* [Parquet to Delta Lake Migration](/concepts/parquet-to-delta-lake-migration.md) - The process and strategies for converting existing Apache Parquet data lakes into Delta Lake format on Databricks.
* [Parquet-based Delta Tables](/concepts/parquet-based-delta-tables.md) - Delta Lake tables stored in Parquet columnar format, which are required for features like persistent deletion vectors and serve as the standard storage format for Delta Lake.
* [Parquet-to-Delta Conversion](/concepts/parquet-to-delta-conversion.md) - The process of converting an existing Apache Parquet table to Delta Lake by listing files, creating a transaction log, and inferring schema by reading Parquet file footers.
* [Partial Column and Row Caching](/concepts/partial-column-and-row-caching.md) - The ability to cache only a subset of columns and rows from a table using column lists and WHERE predicates in the CACHE SELECT statement.
* [Partial reveal masking without regex](/concepts/partial-reveal-masking-without-regex.md) - Revealing parts of sensitive values using string operations like RIGHT and CONCAT instead of expensive regex-based masking, improving performance on large text fields.
* [Partial reveal without regex](/concepts/partial-reveal-without-regex.md) - A performance-optimized pattern for revealing part of a sensitive value (e.g. last digits of an SSN) using simple string functions like RIGHT and CONCAT instead of expensive regex-based masking on large text fields.
* [Partition Discovery in Delta Conversion](/concepts/partition-discovery-in-delta-conversion.md) - How CONVERT TO DELTA handles partitioning information, requiring PARTITIONED BY for path-based conversions and optionally loading from the metastore for registered tables.
* [Partition Evolution in Iceberg](/concepts/partition-evolution-in-iceberg.md) - A feature of Apache Iceberg tables where the partitioning scheme can change over time; tables that have undergone partition evolution are incompatible as sources for Delta clone operations.
* [Partition Hint and Predicate Rewrite Conflicts](/concepts/partition-hint-and-predicate-rewrite-conflicts.md) - Conflict sub-errors related to partition hints and filter predicates that could not be applied for row-level conflict detection in Delta Lake.
* [Partition Hints for Delta Lake Conflict Resolution](/concepts/partition-hints-for-delta-lake-conflict-resolution.md) - A technique using partition hints to narrow the scope of read operations in Delta Lake, reducing the chance of concurrent delete conflicts to specific partitions.
* [Partition Inference in CONVERT TO DELTA](/concepts/partition-inference-in-convert-to-delta.md) - CONVERT TO DELTA automatically infers partitioning information for Hive metastore tables but requires explicit partitioning for Unity Catalog external tables.
* [Partition Inference in Delta Conversion](/concepts/partition-inference-in-delta-conversion.md) - Automatic partitioning inference for Hive metastore tables during CONVERT TO DELTA, and the requirement to provide partitioning info for Unity Catalog external tables.
* [Partition Sharing and Parameterized Partition Filtering](/concepts/partition-sharing-and-parameterized-partition-filtering.md) - Sharing only a subset of a table's data via partition specifications, including dynamic filtering using recipient properties (CURRENT_RECIPIENT) to deliver different data to different recipients from a single share.
* [Partition Specification in CONVERT TO DELTA](/concepts/partition-specification-in-convert-to-delta.md) - The requirement and behavior of the PARTITIONED BY clause when converting partitioned Parquet tables to Delta Lake, including validation against actual directory structure.
* [Partition Value Validation in Delta Tables](/concepts/partition-value-validation-in-delta-tables.md) - Detection of data files with partition column values that violate table constraints, such as NULL values in NOT NULL partition columns, reported by FSCK REPAIR TABLE.
* [Partition-Aware Conflict Detection in Delta Lake](/concepts/partition-aware-conflict-detection-in-delta-lake.md) - Strategies for detecting and resolving concurrent delete conflicts on partitioned Delta tables, including partition hints and source table requirements for MERGE operations.
* [Partition-Aware Transaction Conflicts in Delta Lake](/concepts/partition-based-transaction-conflicts-in-delta-lake.md) - Conflict scenarios in Delta Lake specifically related to partitioned tables, including inability to detect conflicts on partitioned tables and conflicts limited to specific partition values.
* [Partition-based Conflict Detection in Delta Lake](/concepts/partition-based-conflict-detection-in-delta-lake.md) - Using partition hints and partition pruning to narrow the scope of transaction conflict detection, with specific error subclasses for partitioned tables.
* [Partition-based Transaction Conflicts in Delta Lake](/concepts/partition-based-transaction-conflicts-in-delta-lake.md) - Conflict detection scenarios specific to partitioned Delta tables, including cases where partition hints narrow the conflict scope or where partitioned tables lack merge source information for row-level detection.
* [Partition-Hinted vs Unhinted Conflict Resolution](/concepts/partition-hinted-vs-unhinted-conflict-resolution.md) - Two related sub-errors (WITH_PARTITION_HINT and WITHOUT_HINT) describing partition-level conflicts in Delta Lake concurrent operations, where the presence or absence of partition hints affects the error message.
* [Partition-Level Conflict Detection](/concepts/partition-level-conflict-detection.md) - Conflict detection that operates at the partition level, where concurrent modifications to specific partitions invalidate reads or writes targeting those partitions
* [Partition-Level Conflict Hints in Delta Lake](/concepts/partition-level-conflict-hints-in-delta-lake.md) - A mechanism where partition hints can narrow the scope of concurrent delete/read conflicts to specific partitions, but can still fail when those partitions are affected by concurrent deletions.
* [Partitioned Conversion and Metastore Metadata](/concepts/partitioned-conversion-and-metastore-metadata.md) - When converting partitioned data, PARTITIONED BY is required for path-based tables; mismatches between catalog metadata and the Delta transaction log throw a convertMetastoreMetadataMismatchException.
* [Partitioned Table Conflict Detection Limitations](/concepts/partitioned-table-conflict-detection-limitations.md) - A scenario in Delta Lake where row-level conflict detection cannot be performed on partitioned tables under certain conditions, requiring a retry of the operation.
* [Partitioned table merge conflicts in Delta Lake](/concepts/partitioned-table-merge-conflicts-in-delta-lake.md) - A specific conflict sub-error (PARTITIONED_TABLE_WITHOUT_MERGE_SOURCE) that occurs when row-level conflict detection cannot be performed on a partitioned table during a MERGE operation.
* [Partitioned Table Row-Level Conflict Detection](/concepts/partitioned-table-row-level-conflict-detection.md) - A limitation where row-level conflict detection cannot be performed on certain partitioned Delta tables, requiring a retry.
* [PARTITIONING_MISMATCH sub-error](/concepts/partitioning_mismatch-sub-error.md) - A DELTA_METADATA_MISMATCH sub-error that occurs when provided partition columns do not match the table's partition columns
* [Partner Model Retirement Policy](/concepts/partner-model-retirement-policy.md) - Special retirement policy for third-party models from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google available through Databricks Foundation Model APIs, where Databricks may redirect models to bridge partner-led deprecation gaps.
* [Partner Model Retirement Redirection](/concepts/partner-model-retirement-redirection.md) - A Databricks policy that temporarily redirects deprecated third-party models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) to similar versions when partners provide shorter notice than Databricks' standard transition period.
* [Partner-powered AI Features on Databricks](/concepts/partner-powered-ai-features-on-databricks.md) - A prerequisite for using Genie Code, requiring partner-powered AI features to be enabled at both account and workspace level, with supported region and geo availability requirements.
* [Partner-powered AI Features Prerequisite](/concepts/partner-powered-ai-features-prerequisite.md) - Requirement that both the account and workspace must have partner-powered AI features enabled for Genie Code to function.
* [Passing structured data between Databricks notebooks](/concepts/passing-structured-data-between-databricks-notebooks.md) - Techniques for sharing non-string data between notebooks using temporary views, DBFS file storage, and JSON serialization.
* [PATH Configuration for Databricks Connect Binaries](/concepts/path-configuration-for-databricks-connect-binaries.md) - Databricks Connect requires its binaries to take precedence in the system PATH; missing or conflicting PATH entries can prevent commands like spark-shell from running correctly.
* [PATH_BASED target error](/concepts/path_based-target-error.md) - A subtype of DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_TARGET indicating the target table is a path-based table (not supported for clone with history).
* [PATH_BASED target table restriction](/concepts/path_based-target-table-restriction.md) - Delta clone with history does not support path-based (location-only) target tables.
* [Path-based target restriction for clone with history](/concepts/non-delta-target-restriction-for-clone-with-history.md) - Cloning a Delta table with history to a path-based target table is not supported in Databricks.
* [Path-based target tables in Delta Clone with History](/concepts/path-based-target-tables-in-delta-clone-with-history.md) - An error sub-condition (PATH_BASED) where the target table for a Delta Clone with History operation is path-based rather than metastore-registered
* [Pay-per-token Compliance](/concepts/pay-per-token-compliance.md) - HIPAA compliance only for pay-per-token Foundation Model API workloads; other standards (PCI-DSS, FedRAMP, IRAP, CCCS, UK Cyber Essentials Plus) not supported.
* [Pay-per-token deployment mode](/concepts/pay-per-token-deployment-mode.md) - A consumption-based pricing model for Foundation Model APIs that supports HIPAA compliance but not other standards like PCI-DSS, FedRAMP, IRAP, CCCS, or UK Cyber Essentials Plus.
* [Pay-per-token endpoint rate limits](/concepts/pay-per-token-endpoint-rate-limits.md) - Foundation Model APIs enforce token-based (ITPM, OTPM) and query-based (QPH) rate limits on pay-per-token endpoints, with the most restrictive limit applying at any time.
* [Pay-per-Token Endpoints](/concepts/pay-per-token-endpoints.md) - A consumption-based pricing model for foundation model inference where users are charged per token processed, suitable for exploration and variable workloads.
* [Pay-per-token Foundation Model APIs](/concepts/pay-per-token-foundation-model-apis.md) - Preconfigured endpoints for querying foundation models on a per-token pricing basis, recommended for getting started and low-throughput production workloads.
* [Pay-Per-Token Foundation Models on Databricks](/concepts/pay-per-token-foundation-models-on-databricks.md) - A pricing model for foundation models on Databricks where users are charged per token consumed, supported by the Anthropic Messages API for hosted Claude models.
* [Pay-per-Token Pricing](/concepts/pay-per-token-pricing.md) - Usage-based pricing model for Databricks Foundation Model APIs where users pay per token consumed for inference on curated model architectures.
* [Pay-per-Token Pricing (Databricks)](/concepts/pay-per-token-pricing-databricks.md) - A consumption-based pricing model for base foundation models available for immediate use without performance guarantees.
* [Pay-per-token Pricing Mode](/concepts/pay-per-token-pricing-mode.md) - A consumption-based pricing model for Foundation Model APIs where users are charged per token processed, suitable for experimentation and variable workloads.
* [Pay-per-token serving mode](/concepts/pay-per-token-serving-mode.md) - Preconfigured endpoints for accessing foundation models on a per-token billing basis, recommended for getting started and prototyping but not designed for high-throughput applications.
* [Pay-per-token vs Provisioned Throughput Deployment Modes](/concepts/pay-per-token-vs-provisioned-throughput-deployment-modes.md) - Two distinct deployment modes for Foundation Model APIs with different compliance support: pay-per-token supports only HIPAA, while provisioned throughput supports the full range of compliance standards.
* [Pay-per-Token vs Provisioned Throughput Endpoints](/concepts/pay-per-token-vs-provisioned-throughput-endpoints.md) - Two endpoint types in Databricks Foundation Model APIs: preconfigured pay-per-token endpoints and user-created provisioned throughput endpoints supporting A/B testing.
* [Pay-per-Token vs Provisioned Throughput Modes](/concepts/pay-per-token-vs-provisioned-throughput-modes.md) - Two pricing and deployment modes for Foundation Model APIs: pay-per-token for variable usage and provisioned throughput for production workloads, with the latter supporting all models in a model architecture family.
* [Pay-per-token vs Provisioned Throughput Serving Modes](/concepts/pay-per-token-vs-provisioned-throughput-modes.md) - Two distinct serving models in Databricks Foundation Model APIs: pay-per-token for consumption-based billing and provisioned throughput for production workloads with guaranteed capacity across model architecture families.
* [Per-Cluster Hive Metastore Disablement](/concepts/per-cluster-hive-metastore-disablement.md) - A gradual approach to disabling Hive metastore access on a cluster-by-cluster basis using Spark configurations, useful during incremental Unity Catalog migration.
* [Per-Table Access Control Configuration](/concepts/per-table-access-control-configuration.md) - Users with CAN MANAGE permission can configure permissions on an individual feature table via the table's drop-down menu in the Databricks Feature Store UI.
* [Percent Null](/concepts/percent-null.md) - A supplementary data quality metric that tracks the expected percentage of null values per column over the last 24 hours, flagging incompleteness when observed nulls exceed predicted bounds.
* [Percent Null (Completeness)](/concepts/percent-null.md) - An additional completeness sub-metric tracking the expected percentage of null values per column over 24 hours to detect anomalous null increases
* [Percent Null Completeness Check](/concepts/percent-null.md) - An additional quality detail of completeness that monitors the expected percentage of rows with null values for a given column over the last 24 hours, flagging a table as incomplete if the observed null percentage exceeds the predicted upper bound.
* [Performance and Cost Monitoring via Traces](/concepts/performance-and-cost-monitoring-via-traces.md) - Using MLflow Tracing to capture operational metrics like latency, cost, and resource utilization at each step of GenAI application execution to identify bottlenecks and optimize efficiency.
* [Performance and Cost Optimization via Traces](/concepts/performance-and-cost-monitoring-via-traces.md) - Using MLflow Tracing to capture operational metrics like latency, cost, and resource utilization at each execution step to identify bottlenecks and optimize cost efficiency in GenAI applications.
* [Performance and Cost Optimization via Tracing](/concepts/performance-and-cost-monitoring-via-traces.md) - Using MLflow Tracing to capture and monitor operational metrics like latency, cost, and resource utilization at each execution step to identify bottlenecks and optimize efficiency.
* [Performance monitoring with MLflow traces](/concepts/performance-monitoring-with-mlflow-traces.md) - Pattern for profiling GenAI trace performance by calculating latency percentiles (P50, P95, P99) and identifying outlier traces using execution_time_ms.
* [Performance optimization for row filters and column masks](/concepts/performance-optimization-for-row-filters-and-column-masks.md) - Best practices to minimize query performance impact when using row filters and column masks, including UDF simplicity, deterministic expressions, and avoiding Python UDFs.
* [Performance Profiling with Trace Percentiles](/concepts/performance-profiling-with-trace-percentiles.md) - Technique for analyzing GenAI trace performance by calculating latency percentiles (P50, P95, P99) and identifying outlier traces.
* [Performance Tuning for GPU-based Inference on Spark](/concepts/performance-tuning-for-gpu-based-inference-on-spark.md) - Strategies for optimizing Hugging Face inference performance on Spark clusters including batch size tuning, DataFrame repartitioning, and GPU utilization monitoring
* [Permission Inheritance and Override](/concepts/permission-inheritance-and-override.md) - Feature store-wide permissions are inherited by individual feature tables and can only be removed at the store level; per-table settings can add more permissive rules but cannot override inherited restrictions.
* [Permission Inheritance and Override in Feature Store](/concepts/permission-inheritance-in-feature-store.md) - Permissions set at the Feature Store level apply to all tables and can only be overridden to add more permissive access, not remove; inherited permissions are marked as such
* [Permission Inheritance in Feature Store](/concepts/permission-inheritance-in-feature-store.md) - Permissions set at the Feature Store level are inherited by individual feature tables; they can be overridden to add permissions but not to remove inherited permissions.
* [Permission Validation at Endpoint Creation](/concepts/permission-validation-at-endpoint-creation.md) - The endpoint creator's permission to the specified instance profile is validated at endpoint creation time, not at update time.
* [Permissions for Custom Classifiers](/concepts/permissions-for-custom-classifiers.md) - Role and privilege requirements for managing custom classifiers: metastore admin role, ASSIGN on the governed tag, and SELECT on example table columns.
* [Permissions for Governed Tags](/concepts/permissions-for-governed-tags.md) - The ASSIGN permission on system.certification_status governed tag required to apply certification status to objects, along with APPLY TAG, USE SCHEMA, and USE CATALOG privileges on the target object.
* [Persistent Deletion Vectors](/concepts/persistent-deletion-vectors.md) - A mode of deletion vectors in Delta Lake where the deletion metadata persists in the table metadata, supported only on Parquet-based Delta tables and incompatible with incremental symlink manifest generation.
* [Persistent Deletion Vectors Constraint](/concepts/persistent-deletion-vectors-constraint.md) - Persistent deletion vectors are only supported on Parquet-based Delta tables and cannot coexist with incremental symlink manifest generation.
* [Persisting Test Cases as MLflow Evaluation Datasets](/concepts/persisting-test-cases-as-mlflow-evaluation-datasets.md) - Storing conversation test cases in reusable MLflow Evaluation Datasets via mlflow.genai.datasets for reproducible testing and versioning across simulation runs.
* [Petastorm](/concepts/petastorm.md) - An open source data access library for converting Apache Spark DataFrames to TensorFlow Datasets or PyTorch DataLoaders, enabling single-node and distributed deep learning training from Parquet data.
* [Petastorm Cache Directory Configuration](/concepts/petastorm-cache-directory-configuration.md) - The mechanism to specify where Petastorm caches converted Spark DataFrames in Parquet format, configurable via Spark config or notebook, requiring a DBFS path starting with file:///dbfs/.
* [Petastorm Deprecation and Mosaic Streaming](/concepts/petastorm-deprecation-and-mosaic-streaming.md) - The petastorm package is deprecated on Databricks, with Mosaic Streaming recommended as the replacement for loading large datasets from cloud storage.
* [Photon Compatibility with Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/photon-compatibility-with-databricks-runtime-ml.md) - Photon can be enabled on Databricks Runtime 15.2 ML+ to improve Spark SQL, DataFrames, feature engineering, and xgboost4j performance, but does not benefit Python-based ML packages like PyTorch and TensorFlow.
* [Photon Compatibility with ML Workloads](/concepts/photon-compatibility-with-ml-workloads.md) - Photon acceleration improves performance for Spark SQL, DataFrames, feature engineering, and xgboost4j but not for Spark RDDs, Pandas UDFs, Python packages like XGBoost/PyTorch/TensorFlow, and may cause memory issues with Spark MLlib.
* [Photon on Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/photon-on-databricks-runtime-ml.md) - A performance optimization engine for Spark SQL, DataFrames, and feature engineering workloads, available on Databricks Runtime 15.2 ML and above
* [Pickling Errors in Distributed PyTorch](/concepts/pickling-errors-in-distributed-pytorch.md) - A common distributed training issue where library imports are not distributed to executors, solved by placing import statements inside the training function.
* [PII Redaction Pipeline for OpenTelemetry Traces](/concepts/pii-redaction-pipeline-for-opentelemetry-traces.md) - An incremental pipeline pattern that uses AI Functions and Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines to redact personally identifiable information from OpenTelemetry trace data stored in Unity Catalog, writing sanitized results to separate tables with broader access controls.
* [Pipeline Parallelism in DeepSpeed](/concepts/pipeline-parallelism-in-deepspeed.md) - An advanced parallelism technique in DeepSpeed that partitions model layers across multiple devices to enable training of models that exceed single-device memory capacity.
* [Pipeline Update Retry Behavior in Orchestrated Workflows](/concepts/pipeline-update-retry-behavior-in-orchestrated-workflows.md) - How retry configurations on both the pipeline level and the orchestrator level interact, potentially causing cascading retries.
* [Placeholder Model Version Technique](/concepts/placeholder-model-version-technique.md) - A technique using a dummy PythonModel to fill gaps in version numbers, ensuring version parity between workspace and Unity Catalog model registries
* [Placeholder Scorer Pattern](/concepts/placeholder-scorer-pattern.md) - Using a minimal temporary scorer (e.g., returning a constant value) as a required argument to mlflow.genai.evaluate() to bootstrap trace generation before developing real metrics.
* [Placeholder Scorers](/concepts/placeholder-scorers.md) - Minimal scorer functions used solely to satisfy mlflow.genai.evaluate() requirements during initial trace generation, later replaced with real evaluation logic.
* [Placeholder Scorers for Trace Generation](/concepts/placeholder-scorers-for-trace-generation.md) - The pattern of defining a minimal placeholder scorer (e.g., a no-op function returning a constant) when initially generating traces via mlflow.genai.evaluate(), since evaluate() requires at least one scorer.
* [Plain Text Environment Variables in Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/plain-text-environment-variables-in-databricks-model-serving.md) - Setting non-sensitive environment variables directly in model serving endpoint configurations via the UI, REST API, or SDK.
* [Plain Text Environment Variables in Model Serving](/concepts/plain-text-environment-variables-in-model-serving.md) - Setting non-sensitive configuration variables directly on a model serving endpoint via UI, REST API, or SDK without encryption
* [Poetry Tilde Notation for Databricks Connect](/concepts/poetry-tilde-notation-for-databricks-connect.md) - When using Poetry, the '~X.Y' (tilde) notation is recommended to pin the major.minor version while allowing the latest patch of databricks-connect.
* [Point-in-Time Correct Training Sets](/concepts/point-in-time-correct-training-sets.md) - The mechanism by which create_training_set computes point-in-time aggregated features, ensuring temporal consistency between labels and features
* [Point-in-time correctness](/concepts/point-in-time-correctness.md) - A join strategy that ensures training datasets reflect only feature values that existed as of the timestamp of each label observation, preventing data leakage.
* [Point-in-Time Feature Joins](/concepts/point-in-time-feature-joins.md) - The ability to join feature values based on temporal alignment, ensuring historical consistency for training datasets.
* [Point-in-time Joins](/concepts/point-in-time-joins.md) - A feature of Databricks Feature Store that enables time-accurate feature lookups, ensuring features are joined to training or inference data at the correct historical timestamp.
* [Point-in-Time Lookups](/concepts/point-in-time-lookups.md) - A time-aware join technique for time series feature tables that ensures training data uses only feature values known up to the timestamp of each target observation.
* [Point-in-Time Training Sets](/concepts/point-in-time-training-sets.md) - Using create_training_set to compute point-in-time aggregated features for ML model training by joining labeled data with feature definitions across entity keys and time.
* [Policies (ABAC)](/concepts/policies-abac.md) - Core mechanism in Unity Catalog ABAC that uses governed tag attributes in conditions to determine access; attached at catalog, schema, or table level and evaluated dynamically.
* [Policies in Unity Catalog ABAC](/concepts/policies-in-unity-catalog-abac.md) - Reusable access rule definitions attached to catalog, schema, or table levels that dynamically evaluate governed tags to determine access
* [Policy Function Inputs](/concepts/policy-function-inputs.md) - Parameters provided to row filter or column mask UDFs, which can be column values matched by tags, columns matched by custom expressions, or constant values.
* [Policy Lifecycle Management (CREATE/ALTER/DROP/SHOW/DESCRIBE)](/concepts/policy-lifecycle-management-createalterdropshowdescribe.md) - SQL statements and UI workflows for creating, editing, deleting, listing, and describing ABAC row filter and column mask policies on securable objects.
* [Policy Quotas and Audit Logging](/concepts/policy-quotas-and-audit-logging.md) - GRANT policies have separate quotas from row filter/column mask policies, and CRUD operations are logged under standard policy audit actions (createPolicy, deletePolicy, getPolicy, listPolicies).
* [Policy Retention on Table Replacement](/concepts/policy-retention-on-table-replacement.md) - The behavior where row filters and column masks are automatically retained when running REPLACE TABLE, preventing accidental loss of data access policies unless explicitly dropped or updated.
* [Policy Scope and Attachment Levels](/concepts/policy-scope-and-attachment-levels.md) - GRANT policies in Unity Catalog can be attached at the catalog or schema level (not at the individual model level) and apply to all securable objects within that scope that match the condition.
* [Policy Scope and Inheritance](/concepts/policy-scope-and-inheritance.md) - ABAC policies can be attached at the catalog, schema, or table level, and effective policies include inherited policies from parent scopes (e.g., catalog-level policies affecting a table).
* [Policy Scoping at Catalog or Schema Level](/concepts/policy-scoping-at-catalog-or-schema-level.md) - Attaching ABAC policies at the highest applicable scope (catalog or schema) rather than at the table level
* [Policy-based column masking with UDFs](/concepts/policy-based-column-masking-with-udfs.md) - Using User-Defined Functions (UDFs) as the masking logic in Unity Catalog column mask policies, enabling reusable masking strategies such as partial masking and full redaction.
* [Population Stability Index (PSI)](/concepts/population-stability-index-psi.md) - A numeric metric in [0, inf) measuring how different two distributions are: PSI < 0.1 indicates no significant change, < 0.2 moderate change, and >= 0.2 significant population change.
* [Population Stability Index (PSI) for Data Drift](/concepts/population-stability-index-psi.md) - PSI is a numeric metric in [0, ∞) comparing two distributions; <0.1 no significant change, <0.2 moderate change, >=0.2 significant change. Used in Databricks drift metrics tables.
* [Portability of Databricks Connect APIs](/concepts/portability-of-databricks-connect-apis.md) - All Databricks Connect APIs are available in Databricks notebooks as part of the corresponding Databricks Runtime, enabling seamless code transition from local development to deployment without code changes.
* [Portable Spark Session Pattern](/concepts/portable-spark-session-pattern.md) - A try/except pattern for writing code that works in environments both with and without the DatabricksSession class, falling back to standard SparkSession.
* [Portable SparkSession Pattern](/concepts/portable-sparksession-pattern.md) - A coding pattern that gracefully falls back from DatabricksSession to a regular SparkSession when Databricks Connect is not available, enabling portable PySpark code.
* [Portable SparkSession pattern for Databricks Connect](/concepts/portable-sparksession-pattern.md) - A code pattern that falls back from DatabricksSession to SparkSession when Databricks Connect is unavailable, enabling portable code
* [Post-completion Tag Management (mlflow.set_trace_tag / mlflow.delete_trace_tag)](/concepts/post-completion-tag-management-mlflowset_trace_tag-mlflowdelete_trace_tag.md) - MLflow API methods for setting or deleting tags on traces that have already been completed and logged in the backend store.
* [Post-hoc Tag Management](/concepts/post-hoc-tag-management.md) - Setting, updating, and deleting tags on already-completed and logged traces using mlflow.set_trace_tag and mlflow.delete_trace_tag.
* [Post-hoc trace tag management](/concepts/post-hoc-tag-management.md) - The ability to set or delete tags on an MLflow trace that has already been completed and logged, using mlflow.set_trace_tag and mlflow.delete_trace_tag functions or the MLflow UI.
* [Pre-admission checking and token credit-back](/concepts/pre-admission-checking-and-token-credit-back.md) - Databricks pre-admits requests by verifying input tokens against ITPM and reserving max_tokens against OTPM; unused reserved tokens are credited back after the response.
* [Pre-deployment Validation for Model Serving](/concepts/pre-deployment-validation-for-model-serving.md) - Practices for validating model serving configurations before deployment to catch common issues early, preventing build and runtime failures.
* [Pre-generated Conversation Evaluation](/concepts/pre-generated-conversation-evaluation.md) - Approach for evaluating existing conversation traces (from production or pre-recorded test data) by retrieving traces grouped by session ID and passing them to mlflow.genai.evaluate.
* [Pre-Installed Library Removal Criteria](/concepts/pre-installed-library-removal-criteria.md) - Conditions under which a pre-installed library is removed from Databricks Runtime ML, including lack of active maintenance (no commits in 3 months, no releases in 9 months, archived repository, or announced maintenance stop) or no functional stable release.
* [Pre-signed URL Sharing](/concepts/pre-signed-url-sharing.md) - A Delta Sharing mechanism that provides temporary read access via pre-signed URLs, logged as deltaSharingQueriedTable and deltaSharingQueriedTableChanges events
* [Pre-signed URL Sharing Audit Details](/concepts/pre-signed-url-sharing-audit-events.md) - Audit log fields for pre-signed URL-based sharing, including deltaSharingQueriedTableChanges and deltaSharingQueriedTable events with detailed metadata about shared files.
* [Pre-signed URL Sharing Audit Events](/concepts/pre-signed-url-sharing-audit-events.md) - Audit events (deltaSharingQueriedTableChanges, deltaSharingQueriedTable) logged when data recipients query shared data via pre-signed URL-based sharing, with detailed response.result fields.
* [Pre-signed URL Sharing in OpenSharing](/concepts/pre-signed-url-sharing.md) - A credential mechanism for OpenSharing that provides temporary read access to shared data via pre-signed URLs, logged through deltaSharingQueriedTable and deltaSharingQueriedTableChanges events.
* [Pre-signed URL vs STS Token Auditing](/concepts/pre-signed-url-vs-sts-token-auditing.md) - OpenSharing supports two credential types for temporary data access — pre-signed URLs and scoped-down STS tokens — each producing distinct audit log fields in provider logs.
* [Pre-trained Model Truncation for Feature Extraction](/concepts/truncated-model-feature-extraction.md) - The technique of removing the final classification layers from a pre-trained deep learning model so that the model outputs feature tensors instead of predictions, which can then be used as inputs to new models.
* [Predicate-based row deletion](/concepts/predicate-based-row-deletion.md) - Core mechanism of DELETE FROM that filters which rows to remove using a WHERE predicate; deleting all rows when no predicate is provided.
* [Predict Function Contract for Optimization](/concepts/predict-function-contract-for-optimization.md) - The requirement that predict_fn must use mlflow.entities.model_registry.PromptVersion.format() to load prompts from the registry, rather than hardcoding prompt strings, for optimization to work correctly.
* [Predict Function Wrapper for Logged Models](/concepts/predict-function-wrapper-for-logged-models.md) - MLflow 3 requires wrapping logged MLflow models in a predict function that maps named input parameters to the model's expected input format, serving as a translation layer between the evaluation framework and the model.
* [predict_fn Patterns in MLflow Evaluation](/concepts/predict_fn-patterns-in-mlflow-evaluation.md) - Common patterns for defining or wrapping prediction functions used with mlflow.genai.evaluate(), including direct calls, wrappers, endpoint evaluation, and logged model evaluation.
* [Predictive Equality](/concepts/predictive-equality.md) - A fairness metric that compares false positive rates between protected and unprotected groups
* [Predictive I/O](/concepts/predictive-io.md) - A modern replacement for Bloom filter indexes in Databricks that optimizes data skipping during queries.
* [Predictive I/O (Databricks)](/concepts/predictive-io.md) - A Databricks optimization feature recommended as a replacement for deprecated Bloom filter indexes.
* [Predictive Optimization for Delta Lake](/concepts/predictive-optimization-for-delta-lake.md) - Automatic optimization feature on Databricks that runs OPTIMIZE and VACUUM commands on Unity Catalog managed tables without manual intervention.
* [Predictive Optimization for VACUUM](/concepts/predictive-optimization-for-vacuum.md) - When predictive optimization is enabled, Databricks automatically triggers VACUUM operations, making manual VACUUM commands unnecessary in most cases.
* [Predictive Parity](/concepts/predictive-parity.md) - A fairness metric that compares the model's precision (positive predictive value) between protected and unprotected groups
* [Prefill-Decode Architecture](/concepts/prefill-decode-architecture.md) - The two-phase LLM inference process where input tokens are processed in parallel (prefill) followed by auto-regressive token-by-token generation (decode).
* [Prefix-Based Secret Naming for Online Stores](/concepts/prefix-based-secret-naming-for-online-stores.md) - A naming convention using a unique prefix combined with -access-key-id/-secret-access-key (DynamoDB) or -user/-password (SQL stores) to organize credentials in Databricks secret scopes.
* [prepare_model and prepare_data_loader](/concepts/prepare_model-and-prepare_data_loader.md) - Ray Train utility functions that automatically wrap a PyTorch model in DDP (Distributed Data Parallel), move it to the correct device, and inject a DistributedSampler into DataLoaders for sharded training.
* [prepare_model and prepare_data_loader in Ray Train](/concepts/prepare_model-and-prepare_data_loader.md) - Ray Train utility functions that handle DDP wrapping, GPU device placement, and distributed dataset sampling for PyTorch training workflows.
* [Preprocessing and Postprocessing with Custom PyFunc](/concepts/preprocessing-and-postprocessing-with-pyfunc.md) - Using custom pyfunc models to add preprocessing (before model inference) and postprocessing (after model inference) logic to model serving pipelines
* [Preprocessing and Postprocessing with PyFunc](/concepts/preprocessing-and-postprocessing-with-pyfunc.md) - Using custom pyfunc models to add preprocessing (input formatting) and postprocessing (output formatting) logic around model inference.
* [Prerequisite Permission Validation](/concepts/prerequisite-permission-validation.md) - Automatic checking of prerequisite privileges (e.g., USE CATALOG, USE SCHEMA) when users request a privilege like SELECT, with missing prerequisites generating additional requests.
* [Prerequisites and Limitations for UniForm Iceberg Reads](/concepts/prerequisites-and-limitations-for-uniform-iceberg-reads.md) - Enabling Iceberg reads requires Unity Catalog registration, column mapping, specific Delta protocol versions (minReaderVersion >= 2, minWriterVersion >= 7), and Databricks Runtime 14.3+ — with limitations including no deletion vectors, read-only Iceberg client access, and no support for materialized views or streaming tables.
* [Prerequisites for Disabling Hive Metastore Access](/concepts/prerequisites-for-disabling-legacy-hive-metastore.md) - Conditions that must be met before disabling legacy Hive metastore, including completed migration, Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS+, and user readiness.
* [Prerequisites for Disabling Legacy Hive Metastore](/concepts/prerequisites-for-disabling-legacy-hive-metastore.md) - Criteria that must be met before disabling the legacy Hive metastore, including completed Unity Catalog migration and Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS or above
* [Prerequisites for MLflow 3 GenAI Tracing](/concepts/prerequisites-for-mlflow-3-genai-tracing.md) - Installation requirements including `mlflow[databricks]>=3.1`, provider SDKs, and LLM API key configuration for automatic tracing on Databricks.
* [Prerequisites for MLflow Bedrock Tracing](/concepts/prerequisites-for-mlflow-bedrock-tracing.md) - Using MLflow tracing with Amazon Bedrock requires installing mlflow[databricks]>=3.1 and boto3, configuring Databricks credentials (DATABRICKS_HOST, DATABRICKS_TOKEN), and configuring AWS credentials for Bedrock access.
* [Prerequisites for MLflow CrewAI Tracing](/concepts/prerequisites-for-mlflow-crewai-tracing.md) - Environment setup requirements including MLflow 3+, crewai library, API keys, and Databricks credentials
* [Prerequisites for Publishing Feature Tables to Online Stores](/concepts/prerequisites-for-publishing-feature-tables-to-online-stores.md) - Feature tables must satisfy primary key constraints, non-nullable primary key columns, and Change Data Feed (CDF) enablement before they can be published to an online store for real-time serving.
* [Prerequisites for Row Filters and Column Masks](/concepts/prerequisites-for-row-filters-and-column-masks.md) - The required privileges (EXECUTE, USE SCHEMA, USE CATALOG), compute resource constraints (SQL warehouse, shared/dedicated access mode versions), and UDF registration requirements for applying row filters and column masks.
* [Prerequisites for Unity Catalog Trace Migration](/concepts/prerequisites-for-unity-catalog-trace-migration.md) - The required environment setup including a Unity Catalog-enabled workspace, Databricks Runtime 15.3+, the databricks-agents package (>=1.10.1), and specific read/write permissions on source and destination.
* [Prerequisites for upgrading feature tables to Unity Catalog](/concepts/prerequisites-for-upgrading-feature-tables-to-unity-catalog.md) - The underlying workspace Delta table must be upgraded to Unity Catalog before calling upgrade_workspace_table, and users must notify producers/consumers of the new table name.
* [Presto and Athena Delta Lake integration](/concepts/presto-and-athena-delta-lake-integration.md) - Method for reading Delta Lake tables from Presto and Amazon Athena using generated manifest files as a bridge.
* [Prevent access until sensitive columns are tagged](/concepts/prevent-access-until-sensitive-columns-are-tagged.md) - A governance pattern that uses a default 'unverified' tag to block access to unclassified data, then automatically switches to column masking once a data steward updates the classification tag.
* [Primary Key Constraint for Feature Tables](/concepts/primary-key-constraint-for-feature-tables.md) - Feature tables require a primary key constraint, which can be defined at table creation or added to existing Delta tables via ALTER TABLE DDL statements.
* [Primary Key Constraints as Feature Tables](/concepts/primary-key-constraints-as-feature-tables.md) - Any Delta table in Unity Catalog with a primary key constraint is automatically treated as a feature table and appears in the Features UI.
* [Primary Key Constraints for Feature Tables](/concepts/primary-key-constraints-for-feature-tables.md) - Feature tables require a primary key constraint defined via NOT NULL columns and ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT DDL; only the table owner can declare it.
* [Primary Key Requirement for Shared Data](/concepts/primary-key-requirement-for-shared-data.md) - Requirement that any data asset shared with SAP BDC must have a primary key or identity column, which can be added before sharing or defined in the CSN schema.
* [Primary Key Requirements for Shared Data Assets](/concepts/primary-key-requirement-for-shared-data.md) - Any data asset shared via SAP BDC must have a primary key or identity column defined before sharing
* [Principal targeting in ABAC policies: TO/EXCEPT vs identity functions](/concepts/principal-targeting-in-abac-policies-toexcept-vs-identity-functions.md) - Two approaches for specifying which principals a policy applies to: using TO/EXCEPT clauses (simpler, eliminates UDF execution for exempt users) or identity functions like current_user() and is_account_group_member() inside the UDF (flexible, resolved once per query, not per row).
* [Principal Targeting with TO and EXCEPT Clauses](/concepts/principal-targeting-with-to-and-except-clauses.md) - Using policy TO and EXCEPT clauses to define which users/groups a row filter or column mask applies to, keeping UDF logic simple and avoiding complex conditional logic inside functions.
* [Private PyPI Mirror Configuration for Model Serving](/concepts/private-pypi-mirror-configuration-for-model-serving.md) - Workspace-level configuration of private package repositories (Nexus, Artifactory) as default package source for Model Serving deployments
* [Privilege Escalation Prevention for Views and Functions](/concepts/privilege-escalation-prevention-for-views-and-functions.md) - Only a metastore admin can transfer ownership of a view, function, or model to any user/group. Current owners and MANAGE users are restricted to transferring ownership to themselves or a group they belong to, preventing privilege escalation.
* [Privilege Grant and Revocation](/concepts/privilege-grant-and-revocation.md) - Granting, revoking, and inspecting privileges on Unity Catalog objects using Catalog Explorer and SQL
