* [Privilege Hierarchy and Inheritance](/concepts/privilege-hierarchy-and-inheritance.md) - The hierarchical inheritance of privileges where granting on CATALOG cascades to all schemas and their objects, and DENY on a parent hides objects from listing.
* [Privilege Inheritance Hierarchy](/concepts/privilege-inheritance-hierarchy.md) - The downward inheritance model where privileges granted or denied on a parent object (e.g., CATALOG) automatically apply to all child objects (schemas, tables, etc.).
* [Privileges and Ownership](/concepts/privileges-and-ownership.md) - The traditional RBAC-style model controlling who can access what securable objects via grants and ownership in Unity Catalog.
* [Privileges and Ownership Model](/concepts/privileges-and-ownership.md) - Controls who can access what objects in Unity Catalog through grants on securable objects, with a hierarchy of privileges from account-level to table-level.
* [Privileges for External Lineage](/concepts/privileges-for-external-lineage.md) - The set of Unity Catalog privileges required to create external metadata objects and configure lineage relationships, including CREATE EXTERNAL METADATA, MODIFY, SELECT, and MODIFY privileges depending on the role of the object.
* [Privileges for External Lineage Management](/concepts/privileges-for-external-lineage.md) - The specific Unity Catalog privileges required to create external metadata objects (CREATE EXTERNAL METADATA), specify lineage relationships (MODIFY on the external object), and define upstream/downstream relationships to UC objects (write/read privileges).
* [Privileges for Prompt Management](/concepts/privileges-for-prompt-management.md) - Required Unity Catalog permissions (CREATE FUNCTION, EXECUTE, MANAGE) for creating prompts and evaluation datasets, ensuring proper access control.
* [Probabilistic Time Series Forecasting on Databricks](/concepts/probabilistic-time-series-forecasting-on-databricks.md) - An end-to-end workflow for probabilistic time series forecasting on Databricks, covering data ingestion, resampling, model training, prediction, visualization, and evaluation.
* [Probabilistic Time-Series Forecasting](/concepts/probabilistic-time-series-forecasting.md) - A forecasting approach that produces probability distributions over future values rather than point estimates
* [Producers and Consumers in Feature Store](/concepts/producers-and-consumers-in-feature-store.md) - A lineage concept distinguishing notebooks and jobs that write to a feature table (producers) from models, endpoints, notebooks, and jobs that read from it (consumers).
* [Production Feedback Collection with mlflow-tracing](/concepts/production-feedback-collection-with-mlflow-tracing.md) - The optimized production deployment pattern using the mlflow-tracing package with minimal dependencies and the log_feedback API, requiring MLflow 3 and supporting both trace ID and client request ID approaches.
* [Production ML Monitoring with Inference Tables](/concepts/production-ml-monitoring-with-inference-tables.md) - Continuous monitoring of production ML systems using inference tables to log model inputs/outputs automatically, combined with data quality monitoring, drift detection, and anomaly alerts.
* [Production Monitoring](/concepts/production-monitoring.md) - Capability to reuse LLM judges and scorers from development to monitor GenAI applications in production, ensuring consistent evaluation throughout the application lifecycle.
* [Production monitoring custom scorer functions](/concepts/production-monitoring-custom-scorer-functions.md) - The overall pattern for using user-defined scoring functions in Databricks production monitoring, including serialization and registration requirements
* [Production Monitoring for AI Agents](/concepts/production-monitoring-for-ai-agents.md) - Reusing the same judges and scorers from development in production to maintain consistent evaluation of AI application quality.
* [Production Monitoring for GenAI Applications](/concepts/production-monitoring-for-genai-applications.md) - The broader framework for setting up, managing, and backfilling production monitoring of GenAI applications using scorers in Databricks MLflow.
* [Production Monitoring for Long-term Trace Storage](/concepts/production-monitoring-for-long-term-trace-storage.md) - Optional beta feature that stores MLflow traces in Delta tables for durable long-term retention, removes trace size limits, enables automated quality assessment via MLflow scorers, and syncs approximately every 15 minutes.
* [Production Monitoring Scorer Restrictions](/concepts/production-monitoring-scorer-restrictions.md) - Class-based Scorer subclasses are not supported for production monitoring; only @scorer-decorated functions are allowed.
* [Production monitoring with custom scorer functions](/concepts/production-monitoring-with-custom-scorer-functions.md) - Production monitoring supports built-in LLM judges and @scorer-decorated functions, but not class-based Scorer subclasses
* [Production Quality Monitoring (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/production-quality-monitoring-mlflow-genai.md) - Scheduled evaluation of production traces using scorers via mlflow.genai.Scorer.start() to detect quality issues in deployed applications.
* [Production Quality Monitoring on Databricks](/concepts/production-quality-monitoring-on-databricks.md) - Framework for continuous quality assessment of GenAI application traces using configurable scorers with sampling and filtering
* [Production Safety Monitoring](/concepts/production-safety-monitoring.md) - Continuous monitoring of safety for deployed AI applications, set up using MLflow's production monitoring capabilities to track safety evaluations over time.
* [Profile Alerts](/concepts/profile-alerts.md) - Databricks SQL alerts configured on profile metrics tables to notify when data statistics move out of expected ranges or when data drift is detected.
* [Profile analysis types](/concepts/profile-analysis-types.md) - Three types of data profiling analysis in Databricks: Snapshot, Timeseries, and InferenceLog, each with different dashboard selectors and default visualizations.
* [Profile and Dashboard Refresh Independence](/concepts/profile-and-dashboard-refresh-independence.md) - The mechanism by which profile metric tables and the profiling dashboard are updated independently — refreshing the profile updates metric tables but not the dashboard, and vice versa.
* [Profile Dashboard (Databricks)](/concepts/profile-dashboard-databricks.md) - An automatically generated, fully customizable dashboard in Databricks that visualizes data profiling results from profile and drift metric tables.
* [Profile Metric Table](/concepts/profile-metric-table.md) - An output table created by data profiling containing summary statistics for a table, stored as a Delta table in Unity Catalog, with metrics computed for time windows and data slices.
* [Profile Metric Tables](/concepts/profile-metric-tables.md) - Delta tables automatically created by Databricks data profiling that store summary statistics and drift metrics, queryable via SQL and usable for dashboards and alerts.
* [Profile Metrics Organization](/concepts/profile-metrics-organization.md) - Metrics computed by a profile run are organized into sections on the dashboard, with lists of metrics and statistics shown in tables and charts on the left side.
* [Profile Metrics Table](/concepts/profile-metrics-table.md) - A table storing data profiling statistics (e.g., fraction of missing values) that can be queried to trigger alerts on data quality changes.
* [Profile Refresh and Scheduling](/concepts/profile-refresh-and-scheduling.md) - Mechanisms to refresh data profiling metrics on-demand or on a cron schedule, with serverless compute execution.
* [Profile refresh mechanism](/concepts/profile-refresh-mechanism.md) - The two-step refresh process where profile calculations update metric tables, and a separate dashboard refresh runs queries over those tables to update visualizations.
* [Profile Refresh Operations](/concepts/profile-refresh-operations.md) - The process of triggering metric table creation/update via create_refresh API, running on serverless compute, with status monitoring via list_refreshes and get_refresh.
* [Profile Refresh Scheduling](/concepts/profile-refresh-scheduling.md) - Configuration for automated scheduled refreshes or manual refresh of data profiles, including frequency and time settings, with support for incremental processing via change data feed.
* [Profile Refresh vs Dashboard Refresh](/concepts/profile-refresh-vs-dashboard-refresh.md) - The distinction between refreshing the underlying profile metrics (via UI/API/schedule) and refreshing the dashboard's visualizations (via dashboard Refresh button); both must be done independently.
* [Profile Scheduling and Notifications](/concepts/profile-scheduling-and-notifications.md) - Configuration options for data profiles including scheduled refreshes using cron expressions and failure notifications via email (up to 5 addresses).
* [Profile Statistics Computation with Time Windows and Slices](/concepts/profile-statistics-computation-with-time-windows-and-slices.md) - Statistics are computed for specified time intervals (windows) and optionally for data slices defined by slicing expressions, with metrics aggregated across all combinations of time windows, slices, and grouping columns.
* [Profile Types in Databricks Data Profiling](/concepts/profile-types-in-databricks-data-profiling.md) - Databricks data profiling supports three analysis modes — Snapshot, Timeseries, and InferenceLog — each producing different visualization widgets and selectors on the dashboard.
* [Profiling Analysis Modes](/concepts/profiling-analysis-modes.md) - The three types of data profiling analysis in Databricks: time series, inference (for ML models), and snapshot, each suited to different monitoring use cases.
* [Profiling Granularity Options](/concepts/profiling-granularity-options.md) - The set of predefined aggregation granularities (from 5 minutes to 1 year) available for time-based profiles in Databricks data profiling.
* [Programmatic and Batch Evaluation Patterns](/concepts/programmatic-and-batch-evaluation-patterns.md) - DeepEval scorers can be used both programmatically as standalone scoring functions and in batch mode via mlflow.genai.evaluate() for dataset-level evaluation.
* [Programmatic Endpoint Configuration with Instance Profile ARN](/concepts/programmatic-endpoint-configuration-with-instance-profile-arn.md) - Using the REST API to attach an instance profile to a model serving endpoint by specifying the instance_profile_arn field in the served_entities configuration during endpoint creation or update.
* [Programmatic Instance Profile Assignment via Serving Endpoints API](/concepts/programmatic-instance-profile-assignment-via-serving-endpoints-api.md) - The Databricks Serving Endpoints API supports the instance_profile_arn field for attaching instance profiles during both endpoint creation and updates using REST API calls.
* [Programmatic Prompt Management with MLflow Python SDK](/concepts/programmatic-prompt-management-with-mlflow-python-sdk.md) - The MLflow Python SDK provides functions like mlflow.genai.register_prompt(), mlflow.genai.load_prompt(), and mlflow.genai.search_prompts() for programmatic prompt lifecycle management.
* [Prompt Aliases](/concepts/prompt-aliases.md) - Mutable pointers (e.g., 'production', 'staging') that reference specific prompt versions, enabling safe deployment strategies like A/B testing and gradual rollouts.
* [Prompt and Model Versioning](/concepts/prompt-and-model-versioning.md) - Version-controlled prompt templates with Git-like history and dynamic variables, plus logged models that snapshot application configurations, link to traces and evaluation runs, and can package code for deployment.
* [Prompt Chaining](/concepts/prompt-chaining.md) - An architectural pattern where the output of one prompt (e.g., a plan) is fed as input into a subsequent prompt (e.g., an answer) to decompose complex tasks into sequential steps.
* [Prompt Discovery and Search](/concepts/prompt-discovery-and-search.md) - Searching prompts using filter strings on catalog and schema with the mlflow.genai.search_prompts() function.
* [Prompt Iteration and Comparison Workflow](/concepts/prompt-iteration-and-comparison-workflow.md) - Workflow for iteratively improving GenAI application prompts, re-running evaluations, and comparing results side-by-side in the MLflow UI to measure improvement.
* [Prompt Iteration via Evaluation](/concepts/prompt-iteration-via-evaluation.md) - Iterative development workflow where GenAI prompts are refined based on quantitative evaluation results, with runs compared in the MLflow UI.
* [Prompt Iteration Workflow](/concepts/prompt-iteration-workflow.md) - A methodology for iteratively improving LLM prompts by defining evaluation criteria, running evaluations, reviewing results in the MLflow UI, refining prompts, and comparing runs.
* [Prompt Iteration Workflow for GenAI](/concepts/prompt-iteration-workflow-for-genai.md) - The iterative process of defining system prompts, evaluating outputs against criteria, refining prompts, and comparing results in the MLflow UI.
* [Prompt Optimization for Text Classification](/concepts/prompt-optimization-for-text-classification.md) - The pattern of using prompt optimization to align a generic classification prompt to a specific set of label categories and formatting constraints
* [Prompt Registry](/concepts/prompt-registry.md) - A centralized system for registering, versioning, and managing prompt templates used in GenAI applications, with support for tracking changes via commit messages.
* [Prompt Registry API (mlflow.genai)](/concepts/prompt-registry-api-mlflowgenai.md) - Python SDK functions including register_prompt(), load_prompt(), and search_prompts() for programmatic prompt lifecycle management.
* [Prompt Registry in Unity Catalog](/concepts/prompt-registry-in-unity-catalog.md) - A centralized prompt management system for browsing, versioning, and aliasing GenAI prompts stored in Unity Catalog
* [Prompt Registry load_prompt](/concepts/prompt-registry-load_prompt.md) - Loading specific prompt versions by URI ('prompts:/...') or by name and version number, with optional missing parameter handling.
* [Prompt Registry Location Tag](/concepts/prompt-registry-location-tag.md) - An MLflow experiment tag 'mlflow.promptRegistryLocation' that links an experiment to a Unity Catalog schema, allowing SDKs and tools to infer the prompt storage location automatically.
* [Prompt Registry register_prompt](/concepts/prompt-registry-register_prompt.md) - Registering a new prompt in the Prompt Registry with template variables, commit messages, and metadata tags.
* [Prompt Registry search_prompts with Unity Catalog constraints](/concepts/prompt-registry-search_prompts-with-unity-catalog-constraints.md) - Searching prompts in Unity Catalog registries is restricted to catalog.schema filter pairs; name patterns, tag filters, and combined filters are unsupported, requiring programmatic post-filtering.
* [Prompt Registry Version Control](/concepts/prompt-registry-version-control.md) - MLflow Prompt Registry's automatic registration of optimized prompts, enabling version control and retrieval of prompt versions via URIs like prompts:/{prompt_location}@latest.
* [Prompt Template Formats](/concepts/prompt-template-formats.md) - Two supported prompt storage formats in the Prompt Registry: simple string prompts and conversation-style (chat) prompts, both using double-brace syntax for template variables.
* [Prompt Template Variables](/concepts/prompt-template-variables.md) - Double-brace syntax ({{variable_name}}) for defining dynamic placeholders in prompt templates that are filled at runtime.
* [Prompt Templates with Variable Substitution](/concepts/prompt-templates-with-variable-substitution.md) - Prompts support double-brace syntax ({{variable_name}}) for defining template variables that are filled in at runtime using the prompt.format() method.
* [Prompt Templates with Variables](/concepts/prompt-templates-with-variables.md) - Prompts use double-brace syntax ({{variable_name}}) to define template variables that are filled in at runtime with dynamic data.
* [Prompt Types: Text vs Chat](/concepts/prompt-types-text-vs-chat.md) - Two prompt types supported in the MLflow Prompt Registry: Text (single completion-style template) and Chat (list of role-based messages for conversational models).
* [Prompt Version Aliases](/concepts/prompt-version-aliases.md) - Assigning aliases (e.g. 'production', 'staging') to specific prompt versions to manage deployment stages without referencing version numbers.
* [Prompt Version Evaluation](/concepts/prompt-version-evaluation.md) - A systematic methodology for comparing different prompt versions using consistent evaluation datasets, custom scorers, and composite metrics to identify the best-performing prompt.
* [Prompt Version Lifecycle](/concepts/prompt-version-lifecycle.md) - The end-to-end workflow for prompt management: creating versions, evaluating against datasets, selecting the best performer, and deploying to production using aliases.
* [Prompt Version Management](/concepts/prompt-version-management.md) - Registering, versioning, and iterating prompt templates in MLflow's prompt registry with semantic commit messages.
* [Prompt Version Management (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/prompt-version-management-mlflow-genai.md) - Version-controlled templates for LLM prompts with Git-like history, variables, aliases, and links to evaluation runs for quality tracking.
* [Prompt Version Management in MLflow](/concepts/prompt-version-management.md) - Systematic approach to creating, registering, versioning, and comparing different prompt templates for GenAI applications using MLflow's prompt registry.
* [Prompt Versioning](/concepts/prompt-versioning.md) - Git-like version control for prompt templates, providing immutable snapshots with auto-incrementing version numbers, commit messages, and rollback capabilities.
* [Prompt Versioning and Immutability](/concepts/prompt-versioning-and-immutability.md) - Prompts in MLflow are immutable once created; edits produce new versions with full history preserved, enabling rollbacks and comparison.
* [Prompts and Logged Models](/concepts/prompts-and-logged-models.md) - Prompts are version-controlled LLM templates with variables and aliases; logged models are application snapshots linking traces, prompts, and evaluation runs, serving as a metadata hub or deployable artifact.
* [Protocol and metadata conflicts in Delta Lake](/concepts/protocol-and-metadata-conflicts-in-delta-lake.md) - Transaction conflicts triggered by concurrent operations that change the table protocol version, schema, or partitioning metadata, requiring a retry.
* [Provided Libraries on Databricks Serverless](/concepts/provided-libraries-on-databricks-serverless.md) - The curated set of libraries automatically available on serverless compute (environment version 4), which should not be bundled in JARs
* [Provided Libraries on Databricks Serverless Compute](/concepts/provided-libraries-on-databricks-serverless-compute.md) - A curated set of libraries (Databricks Connect, Scala, logging, HTTP, Jackson, Guava, etc.) are pre-installed on serverless compute; bundling duplicate versions causes NoSuchMethodError.
* [Provided Libraries on Serverless Compute](/concepts/provided-libraries-on-serverless-compute.md) - A curated set of required libraries (Databricks Connect, Scala libraries, Jackson, Guava, etc.) that are pre-installed on serverless compute and must be declared 'provided' in your build.
* [Provider and Recipient Auto-Creation](/concepts/provider-and-recipient-auto-creation.md) - When an SAP BDC connection is created on Databricks, two objects (a Provider and a Recipient) are automatically generated to represent the SAP BDC account for bidirectional sharing.
* [Provider Audit Log Events](/concepts/provider-audit-logs.md) - Audit events recorded for data providers in OpenSharing, including query response details, error messages, and actions taken by recipients on shared data.
* [Provider Audit Logs](/concepts/provider-audit-logs.md) - Audit logs recording actions taken by the data provider and actions taken by recipients on the provider's shared data, including query result details and error messages.
* [Provider Credential Rotation](/concepts/provider-credential-rotation.md) - The process of rotating bearer tokens or credentials for OpenSharing providers using Databricks REST API PATCH request, without dropping and recreating the provider object.
* [Provider Error Events in OpenSharing](/concepts/provider-error-events-in-opensharing.md) - OpenSharing logs specific error messages for providers, covering failures such as disabled features, missing catalogs, permission denials, invalid names, and duplicate resources.
* [Provider Internal ID Binding](/concepts/provider-internal-id-binding.md) - Catalogs bind to a provider's internal ID (not its name), so recreating a provider with the same name after deletion breaks the catalog's connection to shared data.
* [Provider Management Privileges in Unity Catalog](/concepts/provider-management-privileges-in-unity-catalog.md) - The permission hierarchy for managing provider objects: USE PROVIDER for viewing, CREATE PROVIDER for creating, and owner privileges for updating and deleting providers in Unity Catalog.
* [Provider Native APIs (Databricks)](/concepts/provider-native-apis-databricks.md) - Direct access to provider-specific API surfaces (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) on Databricks Model Serving, bypassing the unified OpenAI-compatible API to use latest provider features.
* [Provider vs Recipient Audit Perspectives](/concepts/provider-vs-recipient-audit-perspectives.md) - Distinction between provider audit logs (recording both provider and recipient actions on shared data) and recipient audit logs (recording share access and provider object management)
* [Provider-Recipient Role Model in Delta Sharing](/concepts/provider-recipient-role-model-in-delta-sharing.md) - The two-party governance model in Delta Sharing where providers configure data access rules and recipients verify or contact providers when access issues arise.
* [Provider-Recipient roles in Delta Sharing error resolution](/concepts/provider-recipient-roles-in-delta-sharing-error-resolution.md) - The separation of responsibilities between data providers and data recipients when resolving Delta Sharing access errors
* [Provider-Side ABAC Policy Exemption via EXCEPT Clause](/concepts/provider-side-abac-policy-exemption-via-except-clause.md) - The mechanism by which share owners are exempted from ABAC row filter and column mask policies on the provider side using the EXCEPT clause in CREATE POLICY statements, enabling them to share protected data.
* [Provider-side OpenSharing Audit Events](/concepts/provider-side-opensharing-audit-events.md) - Audit events visible to data providers in OpenSharing, including query result details for pre-signed URL shares and STS-token shares, and error messages for failed operations.
* [Provider-side resolution for Delta Sharing access errors](/concepts/provider-side-resolution-for-delta-sharing-access-errors.md) - Troubleshooting guidance where the data provider must verify that the recipient or recipient property exists and is correctly configured.
* [Provisioned Concurrency](/concepts/provisioned-concurrency.md) - The amount of parallel processing capacity allocated to a model serving endpoint; increasing provisioned concurrency allows more requests to be processed simultaneously, directly impacting throughput.
* [Provisioned Concurrency and Parallel Request Limits](/concepts/provisioned-concurrency-and-parallel-request-limits.md) - Managing Databricks Model Serving concurrency quotas, parallel request limits, and the 'too many concurrent requests' 429 error, including mitigation strategies like autoscaling, route-optimized endpoints, and quota increases.
* [Provisioned Concurrency and Parallel Requests in Model Serving](/concepts/provisioned-concurrency-and-parallel-requests-in-model-serving.md) - Managing provisioned concurrency quotas and parallel request limits in Databricks Model Serving, including error handling and scaling strategies.
* [Provisioned Concurrency for Serving Endpoints](/concepts/provisioned-concurrency-for-serving-endpoints.md) - Controls how many simultaneous requests a model serving endpoint can process, with autoscaling support. Calculated as Target QPS times Average Latency. Used to handle baseline traffic and spikes while controlling costs.
* [Provisioned Concurrency Quotas in Model Serving](/concepts/provisioned-concurrency-quotas-in-model-serving.md) - Workspace-level limits on provisioned concurrency for model serving endpoints, including how to free quota by deleting/stopping endpoints and requesting increases.
* [Provisioned Throughput](/concepts/provisioned-throughput.md) - A deployment option for production workloads using base or fine-tuned models with performance guarantees, available through Foundation Model APIs.
* [Provisioned Throughput (Databricks)](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-databricks.md) - A deployment option for production workloads using base or fine-tuned models with performance guarantees, as opposed to pay-per-token pricing.
* [Provisioned Throughput and Tokens Per Second](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-and-tokens-per-second.md) - A model for provisioning inference capacity on Databricks based on tokens per second, with a throughput ceiling reached when the endpoint's provisioned capacity is saturated.
* [Provisioned Throughput Benchmarking](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-benchmarking.md) - The methodology of measuring tokens per second under varying concurrent request loads to find the saturation point where throughput plateaus due to provisioned capacity limits.
* [Provisioned Throughput Benchmarking for LLM Endpoints](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-benchmarking-for-llm-endpoints.md) - The practice of load-testing an LLM serving endpoint by measuring tokens per second across varying concurrency levels to understand capacity limits and latency-throughput curves.
* [Provisioned Throughput Compliance](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-compliance.md) - Provisioned throughput workloads support the full range of compliance standards (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, FedRAMP, IRAP, CCCS, UK Cyber Essentials Plus) in supported regions.
* [Provisioned throughput deployment mode](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-deployment-mode.md) - A reserved-capacity pricing model for Foundation Model APIs supporting the full range of compliance standards including HIPAA, PCI-DSS, FedRAMP, IRAP, CCCS, and UK Cyber Essentials Plus.
* [Provisioned Throughput Endpoint](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-endpoint.md) - A Databricks serving endpoint type that reserves dedicated inference capacity for foundation models, configured using model units or tokens per second.
* [Provisioned Throughput Endpoint Enforcement Gap](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-enforcement-gap.md) - A limitation where provisioned throughput endpoints do not automatically enforce foundation model Unity Catalog permissions and must be manually deleted by admins, unlike pay-per-token and batch inference endpoints which enforce automatically.
* [Provisioned Throughput Endpoint REST API](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-endpoint-rest-api.md) - Creating provisioned throughput serving endpoints via REST API by specifying min/max provisioned throughput fields and querying model optimization info.
* [Provisioned throughput endpoints](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-endpoints.md) - Alternative to pay-per-token endpoints offering no TPM restrictions, higher rate limits (up to 200 QPS), and predictable performance for production workloads.
* [Provisioned Throughput Enforcement Gap](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-enforcement-gap.md) - A limitation where provisioned throughput endpoints for disallowed models continue serving until manually deleted, unlike pay-per-token and batch inference endpoints which enforce permissions automatically.
* [Provisioned Throughput for LLM Endpoints](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-for-llm-endpoints.md) - A capacity planning model where inference capacity is provisioned based on tokens per second, with endpoint scaling limited by provisioned throughput leading to a throughput plateau.
* [Provisioned Throughput for LLM Inference on Databricks](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-for-llm-inference-on-databricks.md) - A capacity provisioning model for Databricks Foundation Model APIs where inference capacity is allocated in tokens per second for specific supported models (e.g., Meta Llama, GTE, BGE).
* [Provisioned Throughput for LLM Serving](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-for-llm-serving.md) - A Databricks offering that simplifies LLM serving endpoints by allowing scale-out configuration in tokens per second instead of concurrency, and removing the need for manual GPU workload type selection.
* [Provisioned Throughput Foundation Model APIs](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-foundation-model-apis.md) - Optimized inference mode for foundation model workloads requiring performance guarantees, compliance certifications like HIPAA, and support for fine-tuned models.
* [Provisioned Throughput Mode](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-mode.md) - A reserved-capacity deployment mode for foundation models, recommended by Databricks for production workloads, supporting all models of a given architecture family.
* [Provisioned Throughput Multi-Model Endpoint](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-multi-model-endpoint.md) - A Foundation Model API endpoint configured with provisioned throughput that serves multiple generative AI models with a shared traffic split.
* [Provisioned Throughput Permissions Enforcement Gap](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-enforcement-gap.md) - A limitation where provisioned throughput endpoints do not automatically enforce foundation model Unity Catalog permissions and require manual deletion of disallowed endpoints.
* [Provisioned throughput serving mode](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-serving-mode.md) - Dedicated endpoints with performance guarantees, HIPAA compliance, fine-tuned model support, and optimized inference for production workloads on Databricks.
* [Provisioned Throughput vs Pay-per-Token](/concepts/provisioned-throughput-vs-pay-per-token.md) - Two pricing models for Foundation Model APIs: pay-per-token for immediate use of base models, and provisioned throughput for production workloads with performance guarantees.
* [Publish Data Share to SAP BDC Notebook](/concepts/publish-data-share-to-sap-bdc-notebook.md) - A Databricks notebook that uses the SAP BDC SDK to describe a share in Core Schema Notation (CSN) for consumption by SAP BDC users
* [Publish Modes for Online Feature Stores](/concepts/publish-modes-for-online-feature-tables.md) - Synchronization modes (TRIGGERED, CONTINUOUS) that control how and when online tables are updated with changes from offline feature tables.
* [Publish Modes for Online Feature Tables](/concepts/publish-modes-for-online-feature-tables.md) - The publish_mode parameter (TRIGGERED, CONTINUOUS) determines how and when an online table is synced with changes from the offline feature table, replacing the older streaming parameter.
* [Publish Table API](/concepts/publish-table-api.md) - The API operation (publish_table) that creates an online table, syncs feature data from an offline feature table to the online store, and sets up infrastructure for ongoing synchronization.
* [Publish Table to Online Store](/concepts/publish-table-to-online-store.md) - The process of synchronizing a Delta table from Unity Catalog to a Databricks Online Feature Store for low-latency feature serving.
* [Publishing Feature Tables to Online Stores](/concepts/publishing-feature-tables-to-online-stores.md) - The publish_table API that synchronizes data from offline feature tables to an online store, including prerequisites like primary key constraints, non-nullable keys, and Change Data Feed.
* [Pull Request Driven ML Development](/concepts/pull-request-driven-ml-development.md) - Workflow where data scientists file pull requests to update ML code, triggering automated unit tests and integration tests in an isolated staging workspace before merging to main.
* [Purge Phase After Deletion](/concepts/purge-phase-after-deletion.md) - After the 7-day recovery window ends, Unity Catalog permanently deletes data files within 48 hours, and the object can no longer be recovered.
* [PyArrow Compatibility Constraint for horovod.spark](/concepts/pyarrow-compatibility-constraint-for-horovodspark.md) - horovod.spark does not support pyarrow versions 11.0 and above, requiring manual installation of a compatible version on Databricks Runtime 15.0 ML and above.
* [PyArrow Compatibility Constraint with horovod.spark](/concepts/pyarrow-compatibility-constraint-for-horovodspark.md) - horovod.spark does not support pyarrow versions 11.0 and above, requiring manual installation of pyarrow below version 11.0 for Databricks Runtime 15.0 ML and above.
* [PyFunc Code Path Sharing](/concepts/pyfunc-code-path-sharing.md) - Using the code_path parameter in mlflow.pyfunc.log_model to include shared utility modules that can be imported and used inside custom pyfunc model implementations.
* [PyFunc Context Artifact Access Pattern](/concepts/pyfunc-context-artifact-access-pattern.md) - Custom PyFunc models access packaged artifacts through the context.artifacts dictionary inside load_context(), enabling static loading of model weights, tokenizers, and other files at deployment time.
* [PyFunc Model Preprocessing and Postprocessing](/concepts/pyfunc-model-preprocessing-and-postprocessing.md) - Using MLflow pyfunc's predict method to encapsulate preprocessing logic (input formatting) and postprocessing logic (output transformation) within a custom model class.
* [PyFunc Preprocessing and Postprocessing Patterns](/concepts/pyfunc-preprocessing-and-postprocessing-patterns.md) - Custom MLflow pyfunc models can encapsulate preprocessing logic (e.g., tokenization), model inference, and postprocessing (e.g., sigmoid transformation) within the predict function, enabling end-to-end serving.
* [PySpark and Databricks Connect Conflict](/concepts/pyspark-and-databricks-connect-conflict.md) - The databricks-connect package conflicts with PySpark, requiring PySpark to be uninstalled before installing Databricks Connect.
* [PySpark and Databricks Connect Mutual Exclusivity](/concepts/pyspark-and-databricks-connect-mutual-exclusivity.md) - The databricks-connect package conflicts with PySpark; both cannot be installed in the same environment without causing stream corruption or class-not-found errors, requiring full uninstall and reinstall when switching.
* [PySpark Sandbox Restrictions](/concepts/pyspark-sandbox-restrictions.md) - Security restrictions applied to user code when Python and SQL table access control is enabled, including low-privilege users, filesystem restrictions, and network port limitations.
* [pyspark.ml.connect](/concepts/pysparkmlconnect.md) - A Spark ML module for distributed training and inference of Spark ML models, enabled by default in Databricks Runtime 17.0+ on Standard compute resources.
* [pysparklyr](/concepts/pysparklyr.md) - R package that manages the installation and configuration of Databricks Connect dependencies, including automatically determining the correct Databricks Runtime version from a cluster ID.
* [pysparklyr Package](/concepts/pysparklyr.md) - An R package that manages installation of Databricks Connect dependencies and Python environments, bridging R and PySpark for Databricks connectivity.
* [Python 3.11 Shared Memory Issues with StreamingDataset](/concepts/python-311-shared-memory-issues-with-streamingdataset.md) - Transient issues with StreamingDataset on Python 3.11 shared memory implementation in Databricks Runtime 15.4 LTS ML, resolved by upgrading to Databricks Runtime 16.4 LTS ML with Python 3.12.
* [Python and SQL Table Access Control](/concepts/python-and-sql-table-access-control.md) - A version of table access control that allows users to run SQL, Python, and PySpark DataFrame API commands while enforcing low-privilege user restrictions and network/firewall constraints.
* [Python Base Environment for UDF Execution](/concepts/python-base-environment-for-udf-execution.md) - UDFs execute in the Python environment of the Databricks compute (cluster or serverless), not on the client, with specific package sets defined by the Databricks Runtime version or serverless environment version.
* [Python Environment Compatibility for Databricks Connect](/concepts/python-environment-compatibility-for-databricks-connect.md) - The requirement that the minor version of Python on the development machine must match the minor Python version of the Databricks cluster, with recommended use of virtual environments.
* [Python UDF performance penalty in ABAC policies](/concepts/python-udf-performance-penalty-in-abac-policies.md) - Python UDFs used in ABAC policies must be wrapped in SQL UDFs and are generally slower than SQL UDFs because the optimizer cannot inline or optimize them, and the Python function executes for every row.
* [Python UDFs in Unity Catalog](/concepts/python-udfs-in-unity-catalog.md) - Python user-defined functions registered as first-class objects in Unity Catalog's three-level namespace, usable for on-demand feature computation.
* [Python Version Compatibility for UDFs](/concepts/python-version-compatibility-for-udfs.md) - The Python version on the Databricks Connect client must exactly match the Python version on the Databricks compute cluster for UDF serialization and deserialization to work correctly.
* [Python Version Compatibility in Databricks Connect](/concepts/python-version-compatibility-in-databricks-connect.md) - Databricks Connect requires the local Python version to match at least the same minor release as the cluster's Python version, configurable via the PYSPARK_PYTHON environment variable.
* [Python Virtual Environment for Databricks Connect](/concepts/python-virtual-environment-for-databricks-connect.md) - Databricks strongly recommends using a Python virtual environment (venv or Poetry) to isolate the correct versions of Python and Databricks Connect.
* [Python Wheel Files](/concepts/python-wheel-files.md) - A standard packaging format (.whl) for distributing Python applications, containing code, metadata, and dependencies.
* [Python Wheel Task](/concepts/python-wheel-task.md) - A Databricks Jobs task type that runs applications packaged as Python wheel files, enabling fast and reliable code execution.
* [Python/Scala UDF Wrapper Pattern for Column Masks](/concepts/pythonscala-udf-wrapper-pattern-for-column-masks.md) - The requirement to wrap Python or Scala UDFs in a SQL UDF before applying them as row filters or column masks, because only SQL UDFs can be directly assigned as filters or masks.
* [PyTorch DDP Implementation](/concepts/pytorch-ddp-implementation.md) - Using PyTorch's torch.nn.parallel.DistributedDataParallel module for distributed training of neural networks
* [PyTorch DDP on Databricks](/concepts/pytorch-ddp-on-databricks.md) - Using PyTorch's torch.nn.parallel.DDP module to train a multilayer perceptron (MLP) on Databricks with serverless GPU resources.
* [PyTorch Distributed Checkpoint (DCP)](/concepts/pytorch-distributed-checkpoint-dcp.md) - A PyTorch API for saving and loading model and optimizer state in distributed training contexts, supporting sharded state dicts and async checkpointing across multiple devices.
* [PyTorch Distributed Checkpoint (DCP) with AppState Wrapper](/concepts/pytorch-distributed-checkpoint-dcp-with-appstate-wrapper.md) - A pattern for checkpointing distributed model training using PyTorch's distributed checkpoint API combined with a Stateful AppState wrapper that automatically handles FSDP state dict management and checkpoint save/load operations.
* [PyTorch Distributed Checkpoint Stateful Protocol](/concepts/pytorch-distributed-checkpoint-stateful-protocol.md) - A pattern using AppState as a Stateful wrapper around model and optimizer to enable distributed checkpointing with dcp.save/dcp.load, automatically managing state_dict serialization.
* [PyTorch Distributed Training Error Troubleshooting](/concepts/pytorch-distributed-training-error-troubleshooting.md) - Common errors encountered with PyTorch DataParallel and DistributedDataParallel on Databricks and their solutions
* [PyTorch Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP)](/concepts/fsdp-fully-sharded-data-parallel.md) - A distributed training strategy that shards model parameters, gradients, and optimizer states across multiple GPUs to reduce per-GPU memory and enable training of large models that don't fit on a single GPU.
* [PyTorch Installation on Databricks Runtimes](/concepts/pytorch-installation-on-databricks-runtimes.md) - Methods for installing PyTorch on Databricks Runtime vs Databricks Runtime for ML, including GPU and CPU cluster differences
* [PyTorch Lightning DDP integration](/concepts/pytorch-lightning-ddp-integration.md) - PyTorch Lightning provides a high-level interface that automatically handles DDP configuration for multi-GPU training.
* [PyTorch Lightning DDP multi-GPU training](/concepts/pytorch-lightning-ddp-multi-gpu-training.md) - Using PyTorch Lightning's high-level interface that automatically handles DDP configuration for multi-GPU training on Databricks AI Runtime.
* [PyTorch Lightning for DDP](/concepts/pytorch-lightning-for-ddp.md) - A high-level interface that automatically handles DDP configuration for multi-GPU training
* [PyTorch Lightning for Distributed Recommendation Training](/concepts/pytorch-lightning-for-distributed-recommendation-training.md) - Use of PyTorch Lightning to implement distributed training of recommendation models, enabling multi-GPU and multi-node scaling on Databricks AI Runtime.
* [PyTorch Lightning for Distributed Training](/concepts/pytorch-lightning-for-distributed-training.md) - A lightweight PyTorch wrapper used for scaling deep learning model training across multiple GPUs in a distributed setting, demonstrated here for recommendation models.
* [PyTorch Lightning Trainer for Distributed Training](/concepts/pytorch-lightning-trainer-for-distributed-training.md) - Using Lightning's Trainer API with DDP strategy to distribute two-tower model training across multiple GPUs.
* [PyTorch Lightning with TorchRec for Distributed Training](/concepts/pytorch-lightning-with-torchrec-for-distributed-training.md) - Combining PyTorch Lightning's Trainer API with TorchRec's embedding modules for scalable, multi-GPU training of recommendation models using DDP strategy.
* [PyTorch MNIST distributed training](/concepts/pytorch-mnist-distributed-training.md) - A reference notebook and example demonstrating how to train an MNIST model with PyTorch and scale it using HorovodRunner on Databricks.
* [PyTorch MNIST Distributed Training Example](/concepts/pytorch-mnist-distributed-training.md) - Reference notebook demonstrating how to train an MNIST model with PyTorch, first on a single node and then distributed with HorovodRunner on Databricks
* [PyTorch on Databricks](/concepts/pytorch-on-databricks.md) - Using PyTorch within the Databricks environment for GPU-accelerated tensor computation and building deep learning networks, supporting both single-node and distributed training.
* [QLoRA (Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation)](/concepts/qlora-quantized-low-rank-adaptation.md) - A parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique that combines 4-bit quantization with low-rank adapter matrices to enable efficient LLM fine-tuning on consumer-grade hardware
* [Qualitative and Quantitative Trace Selection](/concepts/quantitative-and-qualitative-trace-selection.md) - Methods for selecting representative traces for evaluation datasets, combining quantitative analysis (filtering by tags, latency, token usage) with qualitative review (examining inputs, outputs, and edge cases).
* [Quality and Evaluation Support in Genie Code](/concepts/quality-and-evaluation-support-in-genie-code.md) - Genie Code capability to review assessment scores from human feedback, LLM judges, and programmatic checks, inspect evaluation datasets and registered scorers, and get help setting up mlflow.genai.evaluate().
* [Quality Dimensions for GenAI Evaluation](/concepts/quality-dimensions-for-genai-evaluation.md) - The set of common quality axes evaluated by built-in judges including relevance, safety, groundedness, correctness, sufficiency, guideline adherence, and tool call correctness.
* [Quantitative and Qualitative Trace Selection](/concepts/quantitative-and-qualitative-trace-selection.md) - Methodologies for selecting representative traces for evaluation datasets using measurable filters (tags, latency, token usage) and human judgment (reviewing edge cases, low-quality outputs, patterns).
* [Query Route-Optimized Serving Endpoints](/concepts/query-route-optimized-serving-endpoints.md) - Route-optimized endpoints require a different URL and OAuth-based query pattern compared to non-optimized endpoints.
* [Query Timeout (3600 seconds)](/concepts/query-timeout-3600-seconds.md) - Long queries exceeding 3600 seconds are not supported in Databricks Connect for Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS and above.
* [Querying Feature Serving endpoints](/concepts/querying-feature-serving-endpoints.md) - Methods to query endpoints using REST API, MLflow Deployments SDK, or the Serving UI, passing dataframe_records with lookup keys and dynamic inputs.
* [Querying Model Serving Endpoints with Databricks SDK](/concepts/querying-model-serving-endpoints-with-databricks-sdk.md) - Using the Databricks Python SDK's WorkspaceClient.serving_endpoints.query() method to send inference requests with dataframe_records payloads.
* [Querying Vision Models on Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/querying-vision-models-on-databricks-model-serving.md) - How to send query requests to foundation models optimized for vision tasks via Databricks Model Serving endpoints using a unified API.
* [Queuing and Autoscaling in Model Serving](/concepts/queuing-and-autoscaling-in-model-serving.md) - How traffic surges cause request queuing when autoscaling cannot provision capacity fast enough, leading to increased latency and HTTP 429 errors. Mitigation includes setting minimum provisioned concurrency, enabling route optimization, and client-side retry logic.
* [Queuing in Model Serving](/concepts/queuing-in-model-serving.md) - When incoming requests exceed provisioned concurrency, requests queue up, causing increased end-to-end latency due to additional wait time before processing begins.
* [Qwen2-0.5B Language Model](/concepts/qwen2-05b-language-model.md) - A 0.5 billion parameter large language model from the Qwen2 family, used as the base model for parameter-efficient fine-tuning with LoRA in this tutorial.
* [R .Renviron Databricks configuration pattern](/concepts/r-renviron-databricks-configuration-pattern.md) - Best practice pattern for storing Databricks workspace URL, personal access token, and cluster ID in a .Renviron file for use in R projects with Databricks Connect.
* [RAG Application Architecture on Databricks](/concepts/rag-application-architecture-on-databricks.md) - The four-step pipeline for building structured RAG apps on Databricks: create a feature serving endpoint, build a LangChain tool, use it in a LangChain agent, and host via Model Serving.
* [RAG Application Evaluation with RetrievalRelevance](/concepts/rag-application-evaluation-with-retrievalrelevance.md) - A pattern for evaluating Retrieval-Augmented Generation applications by annotating a retriever function with @mlflow.trace(span_type='RETRIEVER') and passing RetrievalRelevance as a scorer to mlflow.genai.evaluate.
* [RAG evaluation with MLflow](/concepts/rag-evaluation-with-mlflow.md) - The practice of evaluating Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipelines using MLflow's evaluation tools, including defining retriever functions, constructing prompts with context, and scoring responses.
* [RAG Ground Truth Evaluation with Expected Facts](/concepts/rag-ground-truth-evaluation-with-expected-facts.md) - A pattern for evaluating retrieval-augmented generation systems by providing expected_facts in the evaluation dataset to ground truth judge assessments.
* [RAG Pipeline (txtai)](/concepts/rag-pipeline-txtai.md) - Retrieval Augmented Generation pipeline in txtai that combines embeddings retrieval with LLM generation, supporting custom prompt templates and traceable via MLflow
* [RAG Pipeline Tracing with MLflow](/concepts/rag-pipeline-tracing-with-mlflow.md) - Instrumenting a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline with MLflow traces using RETRIEVER, CHAT_MODEL, TOOL, and CHAIN span types, including document metadata, token usage, and tool definitions.
* [RAG Recommendation as Accuracy Mitigation](/concepts/rag-recommendation-as-accuracy-mitigation.md) - A consistent recommendation across all documented models to use Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) when accuracy is critical, to mitigate the risk of hallucinations and factual omissions.
* [RAG with Fine-tuned Open Source Model Architecture](/concepts/rag-with-fine-tuned-open-source-model-architecture.md) - Production architecture for retrieval-augmented generation applications that fine-tune an open source model on custom data
* [RAG with Third-Party LLM API Architecture](/concepts/rag-with-third-party-llm-api-architecture.md) - Production architecture for retrieval-augmented generation applications that connect to third-party LLM APIs via Databricks External Models
* [RAGAS (Retrieval Augmented Generation Assessment)](/concepts/ragas-retrieval-augmented-generation-assessment.md) - An evaluation framework for LLM applications that assesses retrieval quality, answer generation, agent behavior, and text similarity.
* [RAGAS Metric Categories](/concepts/ragas-metric-categories.md) - The five categories of RAGAS metrics: RAG metrics, Agent and tool use metrics, Natural language comparison metrics, General purpose metrics, and Other tasks.
* [RAGAS Scorers in MLflow](/concepts/ragas-scorers-in-mlflow.md) - MLflow integration that enables RAGAS metrics to be used as built-in scorers for evaluating LLM traces via mlflow.genai.evaluate() or direct invocation.
* [RAGAS Scorers Integration](/concepts/ragas-scorers-integration.md) - MLflow integration with RAGAS providing deep RAG evaluation with fine-grained context metrics and deterministic text comparison scores like BLEU and ROUGE
* [Rank-0 Only Logging Pattern for Distributed Workloads](/concepts/rank-0-only-logging-pattern-for-distributed-workloads.md) - On multi-node workloads, all nodes share the same MLflow run ID; logging should only be done from the rank-0 process to avoid duplicate metric recording.
* [Rate Limit Error Handling (429 Errors)](/concepts/rate-limit-error-handling-429-errors.md) - Error responses returned when rate limits are exceeded, including fields like limit_type, limit, current usage, and retry_after, along with strategies for handling them.
* [Rate limit error handling and 429 responses](/concepts/rate-limit-error-handling-429-errors.md) - When rate limits are exceeded, the API returns a 429 error with details including limit_type, limit value, current usage, and suggested retry_after time.
* [Rate Limit Error Handling Best Practices](/concepts/rate-limit-error-handling-best-practices.md) - Best practices for handling 429 rate limit errors including exponential backoff retry logic, token usage monitoring, and error response parsing.
* [Rate Limit Failure](/concepts/rate-limit-failure.md) - Request failures caused by exceeding the rate limits of a model serving endpoint, which can appear similar to timeouts.
* [Rate Limit Induced Request Failure](/concepts/rate-limit-induced-request-failure.md) - Request failures caused by exceeding the rate limit of a model serving endpoint, leading to additional request rejections.
* [Rate Limiting in Delta Streaming](/concepts/rate-limiting-in-delta-streaming.md) - Using maxFilesPerTrigger and maxBytesPerTrigger to control micro-batch input size and manage streaming processing performance
* [Rate Limiting in Evaluation](/concepts/rate-limiting-in-evaluation.md) - Technique for wrapping predict functions with rate-limiting logic when evaluating models subject to API rate limits, using libraries like ratelimit.
* [Rate Limiting in GenAI Evaluation](/concepts/rate-limiting-in-genai-evaluation.md) - Techniques for controlling API call rates during evaluation, including wrapping predict functions with rate-limit decorators and configuring parallel worker count via environment variables.
* [Rate Limits and Model Serving Endpoint Throttling](/concepts/rate-limits-and-model-serving-endpoint-throttling.md) - How exceeding rate limits on model serving endpoints can cause request failures and timeouts, including references to resource and payload limits.
* [Rate Limits and Timeouts in Model Serving](/concepts/rate-limits-and-timeouts-in-model-serving.md) - How exceeding rate limits on model serving endpoints can lead to request failures and timeouts, with references to resource and payload limits.
* [Ray and Spark Cluster Resource Configuration](/concepts/ray-spark-cluster-resource-configuration.md) - Best practices for configuring CPU and GPU resources when co-locating Ray and Spark worker nodes, including num_cpus_worker_node and num_gpus_worker_node settings.
* [Ray and Spark Integration on Databricks](/concepts/ray-spark-integration-on-databricks.md) - Running Ray and Spark in the same execution environment on Databricks, supported by Delta Lake and Unity Catalog for data management and lineage tracking.
* [Ray Client Remote Connection](/concepts/ray-client-remote-connection.md) - Connecting to a remote Ray cluster from a Databricks notebook using the Ray client with a connection string obtained from setup_ray_cluster
* [Ray Client Remote Connection on Databricks](/concepts/ray-client-remote-connection.md) - Using the Ray client (Ray 2.3.0+) to connect to a remote Ray cluster on Databricks via a connection string, with limitations on Ray Dataset API
* [Ray Cluster Bootstrap on Databricks Nodes](/concepts/ray-cluster-bootstrap-on-databricks-nodes.md) - Pattern for starting a Ray cluster on Databricks compute nodes using the workload command, including head node initialization and worker node joining logic.
* [Ray cluster bootstrap pattern for multi-node training](/concepts/ray-cluster-bootstrap-pattern-for-multi-node-training.md) - Pattern for starting a Ray cluster inline within a workload command, with a head node branch and worker node join logic, enabling single-node and multi-node scaling.
* [Ray cluster lifecycle on Databricks](/concepts/ray-cluster-lifecycle-on-databricks.md) - Creating, managing, and shutting down Ray clusters within Databricks compute using the setup_ray_cluster and shutdown_ray_cluster APIs, including auto-shutdown after inactivity
* [Ray cluster log collection on Databricks](/concepts/ray-log-collection-on-databricks.md) - Configuring log output destinations for Ray clusters using the collect_log_to_path argument, with recommendations for /dbfs/ or Unity Catalog Volume paths to preserve logs after cluster termination
* [Ray Cluster on Databricks](/concepts/ray-cluster-on-databricks.md) - Creating and running Ray compute clusters on top of Databricks Spark clusters using the ray.util.spark.setup_ray_cluster API
* [Ray cluster requirements and installation on Databricks](/concepts/ray-cluster-requirements-and-installation-on-databricks.md) - Prerequisites for creating Ray clusters on Databricks including Databricks Runtime 12.2 LTS ML+, access modes, and Ray installation methods for different runtime versions
* [Ray Cluster Resource Configuration for Spark](/concepts/ray-cluster-resource-configuration-for-spark.md) - Best practices for configuring CPU and GPU allocations when running Ray and Spark together on shared worker nodes, including num_cpus_worker_node and num_gpus_worker_node settings.
* [Ray Cluster Scaling Best Practices](/concepts/ray-cluster-scaling-best-practices.md) - Recommended configurations for Ray worker nodes include minimum 4 CPU cores and 10GB heap memory per node, and setting num_cpus_worker_node to match Spark worker node CPU count for optimal resource utilization.
* [Ray Core MLflow Integration](/concepts/ray-core-mlflow-integration.md) - Patterns for integrating MLflow tracking with Ray Core distributed applications, including driver-process logging and child-run approaches.
* [Ray Core to Spark Data Exchange Patterns](/concepts/ray-core-to-spark-integration-patterns.md) - Three patterns for exchanging data between Ray Core applications and Spark: persisting to temporary storage, using Spark Connect, and using third-party libraries like deltalake and deltaray.
* [Ray Core to Spark Integration Patterns](/concepts/ray-core-to-spark-integration-patterns.md) - Three patterns for writing data from Ray Core applications to Spark: persist to temporary location, connect via Spark Connect, or use third-party libraries like deltalake or deltaray.
* [Ray Data to Unity Catalog via write_databricks_table()](/concepts/ray-data-to-unity-catalog-via-write_databricks_table.md) - Write Ray datasets directly to Unity Catalog tables using ray.data.Dataset.write_databricks_table(), which stages data through Unity Catalog Volumes and requires the _RAY_UC_VOLUMES_FUSE_TEMP_DIR environment variable.
* [Ray Delta Sharing Read API](/concepts/ray-delta-sharing-read-api.md) - Reading data from Databricks Delta Sharing (OpenSharing) tables using ray.data.read_delta_sharing_tables as a more reliable alternative to SQL warehouse queries for external Ray applications
* [Ray GPU Cluster Configuration](/concepts/ray-gpu-cluster-configuration.md) - Configuring Ray clusters on Databricks to leverage GPU resources by specifying num_gpus_per_node and num_gpus_head_node parameters
* [Ray GPU cluster configuration on Databricks](/concepts/ray-gpu-cluster-configuration.md) - Configuring GPU resources for Ray clusters on Databricks, including head/worker node GPU allocation and best practices for mixed GPU/CPU workloads
* [Ray Head Node Resource Configuration](/concepts/ray-head-node-resource-configuration.md) - Databricks restricts Ray head node resources by default (0 CPU, 0 GPU, 128 MB memory) since it's used for coordination, but allows configuration of CPU, GPU, and memory for the head node via setup_ray_cluster API parameters.
* [Ray Job CLI Integration via Databricks Driver Proxy](/concepts/ray-job-cli-with-databricks-driver-proxy.md) - Connecting Ray Job CLI to a Databricks-hosted Ray cluster through the driver proxy endpoint
* [Ray Job CLI integration with Databricks](/concepts/ray-job-cli-integration-with-databricks.md) - Using the Ray Job CLI to submit jobs to Ray clusters running on Databricks via the driver proxy, alongside recommendations for using Lakeflow Jobs instead
* [Ray Job CLI with Databricks Driver Proxy](/concepts/ray-job-cli-with-databricks-driver-proxy.md) - Connecting the Ray Job CLI to a Databricks-hosted Ray cluster through the driver proxy endpoint for external job submission
* [Ray Log Collection on Databricks](/concepts/ray-log-collection-on-databricks.md) - Collecting and preserving Ray cluster logs using collect_log_to_path with DBFS or Unity Catalog Volume paths
* [Ray Memory Monitor Issues on Databricks](/concepts/ray-memory-monitor-issues-on-databricks.md) - Known issues in Ray 2.9.3 where the memory monitor can inadvertently kill Ray tasks, with workaround of disabling the monitor via RAY_memory_monitor_refresh_ms environment variable
* [Ray on Databricks](/concepts/ray-on-databricks.md) - An open-source framework for parallel compute processing that scales ML workflows and AI applications within the Databricks environment.
* [Ray on Databricks Autoscaling](/concepts/ray-on-databricks-autoscaling.md) - Ray clusters on Databricks support integration with Databricks autoscaling, allowing Ray worker nodes to scale up and down based on workload demand using min/max worker node parameters.
* [Ray on Databricks Best Practices](/concepts/ray-on-databricks-best-practices.md) - Collection of best practices for running Ray on Databricks including GPU allocation, transformers trainer MLflow integration, pickling errors, memory monitor, batch processing, and library scoping
* [Ray on Databricks Overview](/concepts/ray-on-databricks-overview.md) - Running Ray compute clusters on top of Databricks Spark clusters using Databricks Runtime ML
* [Ray on Spark Cluster Architecture](/concepts/ray-on-spark-cluster-architecture.md) - Ray on Spark creates a Ray cluster via a background Apache Spark job where each Spark task creates a Ray worker node, and the Ray head node runs on the Spark driver, with API version differences between Ray <2.10 and >=2.10.
* [Ray Serve on Databricks Limitations](/concepts/ray-serve-on-databricks-limitations.md) - Challenges of using Ray Serve for real-time inference on Databricks clusters due to network security constraints, with recommendation to use Databricks Model Serving instead.
* [Ray to Unity Catalog Table Writing](/concepts/ray-to-unity-catalog-table-writing.md) - Writing Ray datasets directly to Unity Catalog tables using ray.data.Dataset.write_databricks_table() via Unity Catalog Volumes as an intermediary
* [Ray Train distributed training on AI Runtime](/concepts/ray-train-distributed-training-on-ai-runtime.md) - Distributed data-parallel fine-tuning using Ray Train's TorchTrainer across multiple H100 GPUs on a single node
* [Ray Train Distributed Training on Databricks](/concepts/ray-train-distributed-training-on-databricks.md) - Pattern for distributed data-parallel fine-tuning using Ray Train's TorchTrainer across GPUs on a single node, with one worker per GPU, running on Databricks AI Runtime
* [Ray Train MLflow Logging](/concepts/ray-train-mlflow-logging.md) - Method for logging Ray Train models to MLflow by reloading from checkpoints and using MLflow model flavors such as PyTorch or TensorFlow.
* [Ray Train Resource Allocation](/concepts/ray-train-resource-allocation.md) - Configuration guidance for setting resources_per_worker in Ray Train to avoid resource contention on Databricks clusters.
* [Ray Train TorchTrainer](/concepts/ray-train-torchtrainer.md) - A Ray Train API that launches one worker per GPU, wraps models in DDP, and shards datasets across distributed workers for data-parallel training.
* [Ray Tune](/concepts/ray-tune.md) - A distributed hyperparameter tuning library that uses Ray as a backend for parallel compute, available in Databricks Runtime ML.
* [Ray Tune MLflow Integration](/concepts/ray-tune-mlflow-integration.md) - Integration of Ray Tune distributed hyperparameter tuning with MLflow experiment tracking using child runs and the MLflowLoggerCallback.
* [Ray Worker Node Memory Allocation](/concepts/ray-worker-node-memory-allocation.md) - Memory allocation for Ray worker nodes is determined by physical memory per Spark worker, number of local Ray nodes, and configurable object store memory, with heap and object store split ratios and OS shared memory caps.
* [Ray-Spark Cluster Resource Configuration](/concepts/ray-spark-cluster-resource-configuration.md) - Best practices for configuring CPU and GPU resource allocations when running Ray and Spark on the same Databricks cluster, including num_cpus_worker_node and num_gpus_worker_node settings.
* [Ray-Spark Integration on Databricks](/concepts/ray-spark-integration-on-databricks.md) - Architecture and patterns for running Ray and Spark in the same execution environment on Databricks, leveraging Delta Lake and Unity Catalog for data management.
* [Ray-Spark Resource Allocation Best Practices on Databricks](/concepts/ray-spark-resource-allocation-on-databricks.md) - Configure Ray worker nodes to match Spark worker node resources by setting num_cpus_worker_node and num_gpus_worker_node appropriately, ensuring one Ray worker per Spark worker for full resource utilization.
* [Ray-Spark Resource Allocation on Databricks](/concepts/ray-spark-resource-allocation-on-databricks.md) - Best practices for configuring CPU and GPU resources when running Ray and Spark workers on the same Databricks cluster, including num_cpus_worker_node settings
* [ray.data.from_spark() — In-Memory Spark-to-Ray Transfer](/concepts/raydatafrom_spark-in-memory-spark-to-ray-transfer.md) - Transfer Spark DataFrames to Ray datasets in-memory using ray.data.from_spark(), avoiding intermediate storage writes; available on Databricks Runtime ML 15.0+ with a required cluster config.
* [RayTune](/concepts/raytune.md) - Recommended replacement on Databricks for distributed hyperparameter tuning, similar to deprecated Hyperopt SparkTrials functionality
* [RayTune as distributed Hyperopt replacement on Databricks](/concepts/raytune.md) - Databricks recommends RayTune as a replacement for Hyperopt's distributed hyperparameter tuning functionality
* [RayTune for distributed hyperparameter tuning on Databricks](/concepts/raytune.md) - RayTune is recommended by Databricks as the replacement for Hyperopt for distributed hyperparameter tuning, providing a similar experience.
* [RDD to DataFrame API Migration in Databricks Connect](/concepts/rdd-to-dataframe-api-migration-in-databricks-connect.md) - The migration from RDD APIs to DataFrame APIs required when upgrading Databricks Connect, as RDD and SparkContext usage must be replaced.
* [RDD to DataFrame Migration Pattern](/concepts/rdd-to-dataframe-migration-pattern.md) - Replacing sc.parallelize with spark.read.json() pattern by using json.loads and spark.createDataFrame for Unity Catalog shared clusters.
* [Read Authentication for Feature Lookup with Served MLflow Models](/concepts/read-authentication-for-feature-lookup-with-served-models.md) - Methods and requirements for authenticating read operations when Databricks-hosted MLflow models look up features from third-party online stores.
* [Read Authentication for Feature Lookup with Served Models](/concepts/read-authentication-for-feature-lookup-with-served-models.md) - Authentication required when Databricks-hosted MLflow models look up feature values from third-party online stores during serving, supporting instance profiles or Databricks secrets with a read_secret_prefix.
* [Read Authentication for Served MLflow Models](/concepts/read-authentication-for-served-mlflow-models.md) - Required read credentials (via instance profile on serving endpoints or secrets with read_secret_prefix) for Databricks-hosted MLflow models to look up feature values from third-party online stores.
* [Read Predicates and Filters in Delta Lake Transactions](/concepts/read-predicates-and-filters-in-delta-lake-transactions.md) - The use of filter predicates and partition hints to narrow data scope for transaction reads, enabling row-level conflict detection and preventing full-table read conflicts.
* [Read Replicas for Online Feature Stores](/concepts/read-replicas-for-online-feature-stores.md) - Additional read-only compute instances that can be added to an online store to distribute read traffic, reduce latency, and improve availability by failing over if the primary instance fails.
* [Read Replicas for Online Stores](/concepts/read-replicas-for-online-stores.md) - A feature that distributes read traffic across multiple compute instances to reduce latency and improve scalability for high-concurrency workloads.
* [Read Replicas in Online Feature Stores](/concepts/read-replicas-in-online-feature-stores.md) - Read replicas distribute read traffic across multiple compute instances for high-concurrency workloads, supporting up to 3 replicas (4 total instances including primary), and are configured via the read_replica_count parameter during create or update.
* [Read vs Write Authentication Separation](/concepts/read-vs-write-authentication-separation.md) - Architectural pattern where read and write authentication for online stores use separate credentials, instance profiles, and secret scopes to enforce least-privilege access.
* [Read-Only Provider Data in Mounted Genie Spaces](/concepts/read-only-provider-data-in-mounted-genie-spaces.md) - The provider's tables in a mounted Genie Space are read-only — recipients can query them but cannot write to them.
* [Read/Write Secret Prefix Separation Pattern](/concepts/readwrite-secret-prefix-separation-pattern.md) - The pattern of using separate secret scopes (read-scope vs write-scope) and a shared prefix name to manage independent read-only and read-write credentials for the same online store.
* [Reading Databricks Tables via Databricks Connect](/concepts/reading-databricks-tables-via-databricks-connect.md) - The pattern of using spark.read.table() through Databricks Connect to query and display remote Databricks tables from a local Scala application.
* [Reading Databricks tables with Spark.read.table](/concepts/reading-databricks-tables-with-sparkreadtable.md) - The pattern of using SparkSession.read.table() via Databricks Connect to query existing tables on a Databricks cluster and display results.
* [Reading Delta Sharing Tables with the deltasharing Format Keyword](/concepts/reading-delta-sharing-tables-with-the-deltasharing-format-keyword.md) - Using .format('deltasharing') in Apache Spark DataFrames to read shared tables from an OpenSharing provider.
* [Reading Shared Tables and Streaming](/concepts/reading-shared-tables-and-streaming.md) - Technical operations for querying shared tables including batch reads, time-travel queries, change data feed (CDF), and Apache Spark Structured Streaming, with specific version and option requirements.
* [Reading tables with Databricks Connect](/concepts/reading-tables-with-databricks-connect.md) - Using spark.read.table() to query and display tables on a remote Databricks cluster from a local environment
* [Reasoning Models (Foundation Model API)](/concepts/reasoning-models-foundation-model-api.md) - Foundation models optimized for step-by-step reasoning tasks, accessed via Databricks' unified chat completions endpoint, with support for revealing internal thought processes before delivering final answers.
* [Reasoning Tokens](/concepts/reasoning-tokens.md) - Special tokens that let a model 'think' through a prompt internally via step-by-step reasoning before producing visible output tokens; some models expose these tokens to users while others discard them.
* [Reasoning-Only vs Hybrid Models](/concepts/reasoning-only-vs-hybrid-models.md) - Classification of reasoning models into reasoning-only (dedicated reasoning models) and hybrid (models that can toggle reasoning on/off), controlling how step-by-step thinking is enabled during inference.
* [Reasons for Collecting User Feedback in GenAI](/concepts/reasons-for-collecting-user-feedback-in-genai.md) - Five key motivations: real-world quality signals, continuous improvement, training data creation, quality monitoring, and model fine-tuning.
* [Recently Resolved Incidents](/concepts/recently-resolved-incidents.md) - A section in the Databricks data quality monitoring dashboard showing tables that auto-recovered from unhealthy to healthy status, helping distinguish transient issues from persistent problems.
* [Recipient (Delta Sharing)](/concepts/recipient-delta-sharing.md) - A named object in OpenSharing that represents the identity of a user or group of users who consume shared data, created with a specific authentication type.
* [Recipient and share concepts](/concepts/recipient-and-share-model.md) - Fundamental OpenSharing abstractions where a provider creates a 'recipient' to represent the data consumer and a 'share' representing the tables, volumes, and views to be shared.
* [Recipient and Share Model](/concepts/recipient-and-share-model.md) - The core abstraction in OpenSharing where a data provider creates a 'recipient' representing authorized users and a 'share' representing the tables, volumes, and views to be shared with that recipient.
* [Recipient Audit Log Events](/concepts/recipient-audit-logs.md) - Audit events recorded for data recipients in OpenSharing, covering share access attempts and management of provider objects.
* [Recipient Audit Logs](/concepts/recipient-audit-logs.md) - Audit logs recording events related to accessing shares and managing provider objects from the data consumer side, including recipient-specific error messages.
* [Recipient Creation for OIDC Federation](/concepts/recipient-creation-for-oidc-federation.md) - Step-by-step process in Databricks Catalog for creating an Open recipient with OIDC authentication method, configuring federation policy, and sharing the OIDC profile portal URL with the recipient.
* [Recipient Error Events in OpenSharing](/concepts/recipient-error-events-in-opensharing.md) - OpenSharing logs specific error messages for recipients, including permission denied on shares, missing shares, and missing tables within shares.
* [Recipient IdP App Registration for Delta Sharing](/concepts/recipient-idp-app-registration-for-delta-sharing.md) - The process of registering an OAuth application in a recipient's identity provider (e.g., Microsoft Entra ID) to enable OIDC-based authentication for Delta Sharing, including capturing issuer URL, subject claim, subject, and audience.
* [Recipient IP Access List Lifecycle](/concepts/recipient-ip-access-list-lifecycle.md) - The complete set of operations for managing IP access lists on recipients: assigning IPs/CIDRs at creation or to existing recipients, viewing the list, and removing entries, each with specific required permissions.
* [Recipient Lifecycle Management](/concepts/recipient-lifecycle-management.md) - Administration of OpenSharing recipients including viewing recipient lists (SHOW RECIPIENTS), viewing details (DESCRIBE RECIPIENT), viewing granted shares (SHOW GRANTS TO RECIPIENT), updating properties/owner/name/comments (ALTER RECIPIENT), and deleting recipients (DROP RECIPIENT), which invalidates tokens and revokes data access.
* [Recipient Object (Delta Sharing)](/concepts/recipient-object-delta-sharing.md) - A Unity Catalog object created by a data provider to represent a non-Databricks recipient, configured with authentication type TOKEN, and used to grant access to shared data shares.
* [Recipient Object (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/recipient-object-unity-catalog.md) - A Unity Catalog object that represents a data recipient in Open Sharing, configured with authentication type TOKEN, with an activation link and credential file generated upon creation.
* [Recipient Object in Unity Catalog](/concepts/recipient-object-unity-catalog.md) - A Unity Catalog object representing a data recipient in Delta Sharing, created with authentication type TOKEN for bearer token flows, which controls access to shared data shares.
* [Recipient Object with OIDC Federation](/concepts/recipient-object-with-oidc-federation.md) - A Databricks Unity Catalog recipient object configured for OIDC-based authentication, generated with a profile URL or downloadable profile file that contains no sensitive information.
* [Recipient Properties](/concepts/recipient-properties.md) - Custom key-value pairs (metadata) that can be attached to an OpenSharing recipient object for additional configuration or identification.
* [Recipient Properties (OpenSharing)](/concepts/recipient-properties.md) - Custom key-value metadata that can be attached to a recipient object in OpenSharing for organizing or annotating recipients.
* [Recipient Properties for Data Filtering](/concepts/recipient-properties-for-data-filtering.md) - Predefined (databricks.accountId, databricks.metastoreId, databricks.name) and custom properties (e.g., 'country') attached to recipient objects that enable partition filtering of shared tables and dynamic view row/column-level access control, allowing the same share to serve multiple recipients with different data boundaries.
* [Recipient Property-based Partition Filtering](/concepts/recipient-property-based-partition-filtering.md) - A technique that uses recipient properties (like databricks.accountId) to dynamically deliver only the relevant partition of a table to each recipient, enabling multi-tenant data sharing with a single share object.
* [Recipient Token Lifecycle Management](/concepts/recipient-token-lifecycle-management.md) - The process of creating, rotating, updating, and expiring bearer tokens for OpenSharing recipients, including token lifetime defaults, expiration policies, and mandatory rotation procedures.
* [Recipient Token Lifetime](/concepts/recipient-token-lifetime.md) - The configurable expiration period for bearer tokens assigned to open sharing recipients, with a maximum validity of one year from creation, configurable per recipient or at the metastore level.
* [Recipient Token Lifetime Configuration](/concepts/recipient-token-lifetime.md) - Configurable expiration settings for recipient bearer tokens, set at creation time or via metastore-level defaults, with a maximum validity of one year and the ability to update or rotate tokens.
* [Recipient Token Lifetime Management](/concepts/recipient-token-lifetime.md) - Configuration of expiration periods for Delta Sharing recipient bearer tokens, including per-recipient settings and metastore-wide defaults, with a maximum validity of one year.
* [Recipient Token Rotation](/concepts/recipient-token-rotation.md) - The process of expiring an existing recipient token and generating a new token and activation URL, used when tokens are about to expire, compromised, or when metastore token lifetime is modified.
* [Recipient Token Rotation (Delta Sharing)](/concepts/recipient-token-rotation.md) - The process of setting an existing bearer token to expire and replacing it with a new token and activation URL, used when tokens are compromised, about to expire, or when the metastore token lifetime changes.
* [Recipient Token Security Best Practices](/concepts/recipient-token-security-best-practices.md) - Security guidelines for managing bearer tokens including immediate expiration on compromise, secure channel communication, credential rotation, and responding to token exposure incidents.
* [Recipient-level SecureConnect toggle](/concepts/recipient-level-secureconnect-toggle.md) - Recipient owners and users with the USE_RECIPIENT privilege can toggle SecureConnect on or off for individual recipients, disabled by default unless the metastore setting is enabled.
* [Recipient-Local Views Pattern](/concepts/recipient-local-views-pattern.md) - A pattern where providers share only base tables (not views) and recipients create local views over the shared tables in a separate schema, allowing ABAC policies on the shared tables to be respected when accessed through those views.
* [Recipient-Side ABAC Enforcement for Shared Data](/concepts/recipient-side-abac-enforcement-for-shared-data.md) - Recipients of OpenSharing can apply their own ABAC policies (row filters, column masks) to shared tables to enforce access control on their side, independent of provider-side policies.
* [Recipient-side OpenSharing Audit Events](/concepts/recipient-side-opensharing-audit-events.md) - Audit events visible to data recipients in OpenSharing, focused on accessing shares and managing provider objects, with specific error messages for permission and existence failures.
