* [Recipient-side resolution for Delta Sharing access errors](/concepts/provider-side-resolution-for-delta-sharing-access-errors.md) - Troubleshooting guidance where the data recipient must contact the data provider to resolve property misconfiguration issues.
* [Reconciliation queries in Databricks](/concepts/reconciliation-queries-in-databricks.md) - Queries that reconcile or validate external metadata against a source table, with restrictions on projections and aliasing.
* [Reconciliation Query Projections](/concepts/reconciliation-query-projections.md) - Query projections used in metadata reconciliation that have specific support limitations in Databricks
* [Recovery Window and UNDROP](/concepts/recovery-window-and-undrop.md) - A 7-day soft-delete window following deletion of a managed table or volume during which the object can be recovered using the UNDROP SQL command.
* [Redirect URL Destination](/concepts/redirect-url-destination.md) - A special destination type that redirects users to an external access request system, disabling the in-product request form and all other destinations for that object.
* [REDIRECT_READY_ALREADY_EXISTS](/concepts/redirect_ready_already_exists.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED indicating the table already has a RedirectReady state, likely due to a concurrent ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED completing the migration first.
* [REDIRECT_READY_ALREADY_EXISTS error](/concepts/redirect_ready_already_exists.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED indicating the table already has a RedirectReady state, possibly due to a concurrent migration command completing first.
* [REDIRECT_READY_ALREADY_EXISTS Sub-Error](/concepts/redirect_ready_already_exists.md) - Sub-error indicating another concurrent ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED command already migrated the table to managed state
* [RedirectReady state in Delta table migration](/concepts/redirectready-state-in-delta-table-migration.md) - An intermediate state during the ALTER TABLE SET MANAGED migration indicating the table is ready for redirect; duplicate RedirectReady state suggests concurrent migration already finished.
* [Refresh operation on Delta Uniform tables](/concepts/refresh-operation-on-delta-uniform-tables.md) - A metadata refresh operation for Delta Uniform tables, which may require specific arguments to function correctly
* [REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM](/concepts/refresh-sync-uniform.md) - A Databricks SQL identifier/command used to synchronize Uniform format metadata for Delta tables
* [REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM Command](/concepts/refresh-sync-uniform.md) - A Databricks SQL command to synchronize the UniForm (Iceberg/Hudi) metadata of a Delta table.
* [Regex masking performance on large text fields](/concepts/regex-masking-performance-on-large-text-fields.md) - Using regexp_replace inside column masks on serialized documents (XML/JSON stored as STRING) is expensive because it walks the full string per row; the recommended alternative is to materialize sensitive fields as typed scalar columns and mask those individually.
* [Regional metastore deployment](/concepts/regional-metastore-deployment.md) - The requirement that each Databricks region must have its own Unity Catalog metastore, which can be linked to multiple workspaces within that same region.
* [Regional Metastore Requirement](/concepts/regional-metastore-requirement.md) - Each AWS region in which an organization operates requires its own Unity Catalog metastore; workspaces can only link to a metastore in the same region.
* [Regional Model Availability (Databricks Foundation Model APIs)](/concepts/regional-model-availability-databricks-foundation-model-apis.md) - Restrictions on which foundation models are available in US-only vs. EU-and-US regions, with cross-Geo processing as an option for non-EU/non-US workspaces.
* [Regional Model Availability (US vs EU)](/concepts/regional-model-availability-us-vs-eu.md) - Certain foundation models have geographic restrictions: some models are US-only (e.g., Anthropic Claude Opus 4.1, Meta Llama 3.1 405B), while others are available in both EU and US regions (e.g., OpenAI GPT-5.x series, Anthropic Claude models).
* [Regional Model Availability Restrictions](/concepts/regional-model-availability-us-vs-eu.md) - Certain foundation models (e.g., Anthropic Claude Opus 4.1, Meta Llama 3.1 405B, BGE Large) are restricted to US regions only, while others are available in both EU and US regions.
* [Regional Tags](/concepts/regional-tags.md) - Classification tags that detect sensitive data specific to certain countries or regions, available only in Databricks workspaces deployed in the corresponding region.
* [Registering and Deploying AutoML Forecasting Models](/concepts/registering-and-deploying-automl-forecasting-models.md) - Process to register AutoML-trained forecasting models to Unity Catalog or Model Registry and deploy them to custom model serving endpoints on Databricks.
* [Registry URI Configuration in MLflow](/concepts/registry-uri-configuration-in-mlflow.md) - The mechanism of setting the MLflow registry URI to 'databricks' (workspace) or 'databricks-uc' (Unity Catalog) to target different model registries
* [Registry Webhook Events](/concepts/registry-webhook-events.md) - The set of MLflow Model Registry events that can trigger webhooks, including model version creation, stage transitions, transition requests, comments, tags, and registered model creation.
* [Regress](/concepts/regress.md) - The databricks.automl.regress method configures an AutoML run to train a regression model.
* [Relationship Between RPS, Latency, and Concurrency](/concepts/relationship-between-rps-latency-and-concurrency.md) - Throughput (requests per second) is inversely related to latency and directly related to concurrency; there is an optimal ratio of client-side to server-side concurrency that maximizes RPS while meeting latency targets.
* [Relevance Scorer](/concepts/relevance-scorer.md) - An Arize Phoenix scorer that measures how relevant an LLM's output is to the given query and context
* [Relevance scorer (Phoenix)](/concepts/relevance-scorer.md) - An Arize Phoenix scorer used within MLflow GenAI evaluation that measures how relevant an LLM's response is to the given query and context.
* [RelevanceToQuery](/concepts/relevancetoquery.md) - An MLflow LLM judge that evaluates whether a GenAI app's response directly addresses the user's input without deviating into unrelated topics
* [RelevanceToQuery Judge](/concepts/relevancetoquery.md) - An MLflow built-in LLM judge that evaluates whether a GenAI application's response directly addresses the user's input question without deviating into unrelated topics.
* [Remote Code Execution Model](/concepts/remote-code-execution-model.md) - The execution model in Databricks Connect where general Python/Scala code runs locally on the client, DataFrame APIs execute on remote Databricks compute, and UDFs are serialized and run on the cluster.
* [Remote connection in Databricks Connect](/concepts/remote-connection-in-databricks-connect.md) - Using the remote() method to connect to Databricks compute that is not attached to the current notebook or job, allowing connections to separate clusters via configuration kwargs or methods like host() and token().
* [Remote Connections from Databricks Notebooks](/concepts/remote-connections-from-databricks-notebooks.md) - Databricks Connect's remote() function allows notebooks to connect to Databricks compute not attached to the notebook or job, using configuration kwargs or methods like host() and token() to create a new session.
* [Remote Databricks compute connection via remote()](/concepts/remote-databricks-compute-connection-via-remote.md) - DatabricksSession.builder.remote() allows connecting to Databricks compute that is not attached to the current notebook or job, using keyword arguments or configuration methods like host(), token(), etc.
* [Remote DataFrame and table operations](/concepts/remote-dataframe-and-table-operations.md) - Patterns for creating, reading, writing, and querying DataFrames and tables on a remote Databricks cluster using Databricks Connect
* [Remote DataFrame Execution Model](/concepts/remote-dataframe-execution-model.md) - The execution model where DataFrame transformations run on remote Databricks compute while general Python/Scala code runs locally, with results materialized via commands like collect(), show(), and toPandas().
* [Remote DataFrame Operations](/concepts/remote-dataframe-operations.md) - The ability to create, manipulate, and persist DataFrames on a remote Databricks cluster using local Python code through Databricks Connect
* [Remote DataFrame Operations with Databricks Connect](/concepts/remote-dataframe-operations-with-databricks-connect.md) - Using Databricks Connect to read tables, create DataFrames, write to tables, and run SQL queries on a remote Databricks cluster from a local environment.
* [Remote DataFrame Testing Pattern](/concepts/remote-dataframe-testing-pattern.md) - A testing pattern where PySpark DataFrame operations (like count, show) execute on remote Databricks compute, and tests assert on results using standard pytest assertions.
* [Remote MLflow tracking server](/concepts/remote-mlflow-tracking-server.md) - Setting up a connection to a remote MLflow tracking server to log experiments from local development or across different Databricks workspaces.
* [Remote MLflow Tracking Server Configuration](/concepts/remote-mlflow-tracking-server-setup.md) - Process of setting up MLflow to log experiments to a remote tracking server by configuring both the tracking URI and experiment path.
* [Remote MLflow tracking server setup](/concepts/remote-mlflow-tracking-server-setup.md) - Configuration required to connect to a remote MLflow tracking server, including setting the tracking URI and experiment path to log experiments across different environments.
* [Remote Model Registry with Feature Store](/concepts/remote-model-registry-with-feature-store.md) - Ability to specify a remote model registry URI alongside a remote feature store URI, enabling training models using local or remote feature tables and registering them in local or remote model registries.
* [renv Environment Management for Databricks Connect](/concepts/renv-environment-management-for-databricks-connect.md) - Using the renv R package to create reproducible R environments when setting up Databricks Connect projects in RStudio.
* [renv for Databricks Connect projects](/concepts/renv-for-databricks-connect-projects.md) - Using renv (R package for reproducible environments) to manage project-specific R package dependencies when developing with Databricks Connect in RStudio.
* [renv Package Integration with Databricks Connect](/concepts/renv-package-integration-with-databricks-connect.md) - The use of the renv R package for creating reproducible R project environments when working with Databricks Connect, ensuring consistent package dependencies across sessions.
* [REORG for Upgrading Iceberg Read Support](/concepts/reorg-for-upgrading-iceberg-read-support.md) - A SQL command (REORG TABLE ... APPLY UPGRADE UNIFORM) used to enable Iceberg reads on tables with deletion vectors, upgrade from IcebergCompatV1, or rewrite data for compatibility with Hive-style Parquet readers.
* [REORG TABLE](/concepts/reorg-table.md) - A Databricks SQL command to reorganize Delta Lake tables by rewriting files to purge soft-deleted data, upgrade to Iceberg format, or perform checkpointing.
* [REORG TABLE APPLY (PURGE)](/concepts/reorg-table-apply-purge.md) - A Delta Lake SQL command to purge deletion vectors from a table, enabling compatibility with symlink manifest generation.
* [REORG TABLE APPLY (PURGE) Command](/concepts/reorg-table-apply-purge.md) - A Delta Lake command used to rewrite a table to remove deletion vectors, enabling operations like symlink manifest generation that are incompatible with deletion vectors.
* [REORG TABLE APPLY (UPGRADE UNIFORM)](/concepts/reorg-table-apply-upgrade-uniform.md) - A Databricks SQL command used to upgrade a Delta table to a specific IcebergCompatV version, rewrite data for Iceberg compatibility, and resolve several DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_VIOLATION errors.
* [REORG TABLE APPLY command](/concepts/reorg-table.md) - A Databricks SQL command used to resolve IcebergCompatV violations by rewriting, purging deletion vectors, or upgrading the Uniform format version on Delta tables.
* [REORG TABLE command](/concepts/reorg-table.md) - A Databricks SQL command used to resolve IcebergCompat violations by rewriting data, upgrading uniform format, purging deletion vectors, or enabling IcebergCompat versions.
* [REORG TABLE for Iceberg compat upgrades](/concepts/reorg-table-for-iceberg-compat-upgrades.md) - The command pattern REORG TABLE APPLY (UPGRADE UNIFORM ('ICEBERG_COMPAT_VERSION = <version>')) used to rewrite a Delta table and enable a new IcebergCompat version, including PURGE sub-commands for deletion vectors.
* [REPLACE ON Complex-Match Overwrite](/concepts/replace-on-complex-match-overwrite.md) - Delta Lake overwrite option for user-defined matching conditions including NULL-safe equality, used when REPLACE USING's equality semantics are insufficient.
* [REPLACE USING Dynamic Data Overwrite](/concepts/replace-using-dynamic-data-overwrite.md) - Compute-independent, atomic overwrite that replaces rows when specified columns compare equal under equality, supporting partitioned, unpartitioned, and liquid-clustered tables.
* [REPLACE WHERE Selective Overwrite](/concepts/replace-where-selective-overwrite.md) - Delta Lake option to atomically replace data matching an arbitrary boolean expression, with optional constraint validation to ensure all written rows fall within the predicate.
* [REPLACE_TABLE_CHANGE_PARTITION_NAMES](/concepts/replace_table_change_partition_names.md) - Error sub-type indicating IcebergCompatV1 does not support replacing partitioned tables with a differently-named partition spec
* [REPLACE_TABLE_CHANGE_PARTITION_NAMES (Delta Iceberg error)](/concepts/replace_table_change_partition_names.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_V1_VIOLATION that occurs when replacing a partitioned table with a differently-named partition spec, unsupported by IcebergCompatV1 due to Iceberg-Spark 1.1.0 limitations
* [REPLACE_TABLE_CHANGE_PARTITION_NAMES error](/concepts/replace_table_change_partition_names.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_V1_VIOLATION triggered when replacing a partitioned table with a differently-named partition spec, mirroring a limitation in Iceberg-Spark 1.1.0.
* [Replacing spark.catalog API Calls for Unity Catalog](/concepts/replacing-sparkcatalog-api-calls-for-unity-catalog.md) - Workarounds for spark.catalog.tableExists, listTables, and setDefaultCatalog using SQL equivalents when running on shared clusters with Unity Catalog.
* [Representative Payload Design for Load Testing](/concepts/representative-payload-design-for-load-testing.md) - The principle that load test payloads must accurately reflect real production traffic characteristics, such as request size and data structure, to produce valid performance measurements. Example: a fraud-detection endpoint should send one transaction per request, not batches.
* [Representative Payload Design for Load Tests](/concepts/representative-payload-design-for-load-tests.md) - Ensuring load test validity by using payloads that accurately represent production traffic, matching the size and structure of real requests such as a single credit card transaction for fraud detection models.
* [Representative Payload Selection for Load Testing](/concepts/representative-payload-selection-for-load-testing.md) - The practice of choosing input payloads that accurately mirror real production traffic patterns to ensure valid and meaningful load test results.
* [RequestSource](/concepts/requestsource.md) - A data source that defines a schema for inference-time request payload data, used with ColumnSelection for pass-through features without pre-materialized tables
* [RequestSource and Inference-Time Features](/concepts/requestsource-and-inference-time-features.md) - A data source type for features provided at inference time via HTTP request payload (or extracted from labeled DataFrame during training); uses FieldDefinition with ScalarDataType and is combined with ColumnSelection only.
* [RequestSource Features](/concepts/requestsource-features.md) - Features passed directly in the request payload at inference time rather than looked up from an online store; these columns are added to the MLflow model signature during log_model.
* [Required table features and properties for Iceberg compat](/concepts/required-table-features-and-properties-for-iceberg-compat.md) - IcebergCompatV requires specific table features to be enabled and specific table properties to be set to particular values; disabling required features or misconfiguring properties triggers violation errors.
* [Requirements for Express Deployments](/concepts/requirements-for-express-deployments.md) - Specific conditions that must be met for express deployments: custom models only, serverless notebook registration, mlflow>=3.1, UC registration, CPU serving, and max 1GB environment size.
* [REQUIRES_WITH_HISTORY](/concepts/requires_with_history.md) - Sub-error indicating that the WITH HISTORY clause is required when performing a DEEP CLONE on a streaming table.
* [REQUIRES_WITH_HISTORY sub-error](/concepts/requires_with_history.md) - Sub-error stating that WITH HISTORY clause is mandatory when performing a deep clone of a streaming table.
* [Resource and Environment Management for Deep Learning](/concepts/resource-and-environment-management-for-deep-learning.md) - Techniques for customizing deep learning environments on Databricks at notebook, cluster, and job levels, including cluster policies to guide data scientists.
* [Resource Key Customization](/concepts/resource-key-customization.md) - The ability to specify a custom resource key when adding a resource to a Databricks App, which controls how the resource is referenced in app configuration and environment variable injection.
* [Resource Management in Combined Ray-Spark Environments](/concepts/resource-management-in-combined-ray-spark-environments.md) - When combining Ray and Spark, resource conflicts can be managed by configuring CPU/memory/GPU allocation, e.g., using setup_ray_cluster to split resources between the two frameworks.
* [Response-Only Training](/concepts/response-only-training.md) - A training technique that masks out user/instruction tokens during supervised fine-tuning so the model only learns from assistant responses, improving training efficiency.
* [Response-Only Training with Chat Templates](/concepts/response-only-training-with-chat-templates.md) - A training strategy that masks or ignores user/prompt tokens during supervised fine-tuning, so the model only learns to predict assistant responses, improving training efficiency and focus.
* [Responses API](/concepts/responses-api.md) - An OpenAI-compatible API on Databricks for multi-turn conversations using 'input' instead of 'messages', supporting custom tools, function calls, and grammar-based outputs for GPT-5 series models.
* [Responses API (Databricks)](/concepts/responses-api.md) - An OpenAI-compatible multi-turn conversation API that uses 'input' instead of 'messages', supporting custom tools, grammar-based outputs, and reasoning configs.
* [Responses API Limitations on Pay-Per-Token Models](/concepts/responses-api-limitations-on-pay-per-token-models.md) - Restrictions on pay-per-token foundation models when using the Responses API, including unsupported parameters (background, store, previous_response_id, service_tier) and a specific set of supported tool types.
* [REST API for Model Serving](/concepts/rest-api-for-model-serving.md) - Direct HTTP requests to Databricks model serving endpoints for querying chat models with structured JSON payloads.
* [RESTORE Operation Metrics](/concepts/restore-operation-metrics.md) - Output columns returned by a RESTORE operation showing table size, file counts, and files changed during the restore
* [RESTORE TABLE Command](/concepts/restore-table-command.md) - Delta Lake SQL command to restore a table to a previous state using timestamp or version
* [RESTORE TABLE TO TIMESTAMP AS OF](/concepts/restore-table-to-timestamp-as-of.md) - Restoring a Delta table to the state it was in at a specific point in time
* [RESTORE TABLE TO VERSION AS OF](/concepts/restore-table-to-version-as-of.md) - Restoring a Delta table to a specific version number obtained from DESCRIBE HISTORY
* [Restricted dbutils in Databricks Connect](/concepts/restricted-dbutils-in-databricks-connect.md) - Only 'fs' (file system) and 'secrets' Databricks Utilities are available through dbutils in Databricks Connect for Python; all other utilities are unavailable.
* [Restricted SQL Function Allowlist](/concepts/shared-view-function-allowlist.md) - A security mechanism in Databricks-to-Databricks view sharing that limits shared views to only use a predefined subset of built-in SQL functions and operators, preventing arbitrary code execution.
* [Restricting Workspace Admin Privileges](/concepts/restricting-workspace-admin-privileges.md) - Account admins can restrict workspace admin privileges using the RestrictWorkspaceAdmins setting to limit the scope of workspace-level administrative capabilities.
* [RestrictWorkspaceAdmins Setting](/concepts/restrictworkspaceadmins-setting.md) - A setting that account admins can use to restrict workspace admin privileges in Unity Catalog.
* [Retired Model Discovery Query](/concepts/retired-model-discovery-query.md) - A SQL query against Databricks system tables (system.serving) to find workloads using deprecated models and identify the owners for migration planning.
* [Retired Model Discovery via System Tables](/concepts/retired-model-discovery-via-system-tables.md) - SQL query pattern for identifying workloads using retired models by querying Databricks system tables endpoint_usage and served_entities.
* [Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)](/concepts/retrieval-augmented-generation-rag.md) - A technique recommended across all supported models to improve factual accuracy by retrieving relevant context from external sources and injecting it into the model prompt.
* [Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) on Databricks](/concepts/retrieval-augmented-generation-rag-on-databricks.md) - A recommended pattern for improving LLM accuracy by retrieving relevant context from documents before generating responses, consistently advised across all Databricks-hosted foundation models.
* [Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) Recommendation](/concepts/retrieval-augmented-generation-rag-recommendation.md) - Databricks recommends using retrieval augmented generation (RAG) in scenarios where accuracy is especially important to mitigate the risk of LLMs omitting facts or producing false information.
* [Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) with Foundation Models](/concepts/retrieval-augmented-generation-rag-with-foundation-models.md) - Databricks recommends using retrieval augmented generation (RAG) alongside foundation models to improve factual accuracy and reduce hallucination risks in scenarios where correctness is critical.
* [Retrieval Quality Judges](/concepts/retrieval-quality-judges.md) - A family of built-in LLM judges (RetrievalRelevance, RetrievalGroundedness, RetrievalSufficiency) that evaluate the quality of retrieved context in RAG applications.
* [Retrieval-based evaluation judges](/concepts/retrieval-focused-evaluation.md) - A family of built-in LLM judges (RetrievalRelevance, RetrievalGroundedness, RetrievalSufficiency) focused specifically on evaluating the quality of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems.
* [Retrieval-Focused Evaluation](/concepts/retrieval-focused-evaluation.md) - A family of built-in judges (RetrievalRelevance, RetrievalGroundedness, RetrievalSufficiency) that specifically evaluate the quality of retrieved context in RAG-based applications.
* [RetrievalGroundedness judge](/concepts/retrievalgroundedness-judge.md) - An MLflow GenAI judge that evaluates whether a generated response is factually grounded in the retrieved context documents, detecting hallucination in RAG applications.
* [RetrievalRelevance](/concepts/retrievalrelevance.md) - An MLflow LLM judge that evaluates whether each document returned by a retriever is relevant to the user's input request, requiring RETRIEVER spans in traces
* [RetrievalRelevance Judge](/concepts/retrievalrelevance.md) - An MLflow built-in LLM judge that evaluates whether each document returned by a retriever in a RAG application is relevant to the user's input request.
* [RetrievalSufficiency Judge](/concepts/retrievalsufficiency-judge.md) - An MLflow GenAI scorer that evaluates whether retrieved documents contain enough context to sufficiently answer a user query.
* [RETRIEVER Spans](/concepts/retriever-spans.md) - Specialized MLflow span type for document retrieval operations that must output a list of Document objects with page_content, metadata, and optional ID fields.
* [Retrieving Labeling Session Feedback Responses](/concepts/retrieving-labeling-session-feedback-responses.md) - The mechanism for accessing reviewer assessments after a labeling session completes, available via the MLflow Experiments UI or programmatically through the MLflow API.
* [Retry Logic with Exponential Backoff](/concepts/retry-logic-with-exponential-backoff.md) - Best practice for retrying requests after rate limit errors using exponential backoff with jitter to handle 429 errors gracefully.
* [Retry with exponential backoff for rate limits](/concepts/retry-with-exponential-backoff-for-rate-limits.md) - Best practice pattern for handling 429 rate limit errors by retrying with exponential backoff, optionally adding jitter to avoid thundering herd problems.
* [Returning Complex Structured Types from Pandas UDFs](/concepts/returning-complex-structured-types-from-pandas-udfs.md) - Using array<struct<...>> return type annotations in Pandas UDFs to represent complex NLP pipeline outputs like entity recognition results
* [Reusable TraceAnalyzer class](/concepts/reusable-traceanalyzer-class.md) - Design pattern for a utility class that encapsulates advanced trace analysis including error summary, LLM usage aggregation, retrieval metrics, span hierarchy, and evaluation data export.
* [Reusing Stored Traces for Scorer Iteration](/concepts/reusing-stored-traces-for-scorer-iteration.md) - Technique of passing a Pandas DataFrame of precomputed traces directly to mlflow.genai.evaluate() as the input dataset, allowing scorer iteration without re-running the original application.
* [Review App Content Rendering](/concepts/review-app-content-rendering.md) - The automatic rendering of different trace content types in the Review App, including retrieved documents from RETRIEVER spans, OpenAI format chat messages, and dicts as pretty-printed JSON.
* [Review App for Human Feedback](/concepts/review-app-for-human-feedback.md) - Integrated application for collecting domain expert feedback on AI agent outputs, producing evaluation data for further iteration.
* [Review App Integration with Labeling Schemas](/concepts/review-app-integration-with-labeling-schemas.md) - Labeling schemas are associated with labeling sessions in the Review App; they control the question shown, input method, validation rules, and optional instructions, and are not used for vibe checks in the Chat UI.
* [Review App Trace Content Rendering](/concepts/review-app-content-rendering.md) - The automatic rendering behavior of the MLflow Review App for different trace content types, including RETRIEVER span documents, OpenAI-format messages, and dictionary inputs/outputs.
* [Review Apps for Expert Feedback](/concepts/review-apps-for-expert-feedback.md) - Feature in MLflow 3 for collecting expert-labeled datasets to align automated judges and scorers with human judgement
* [Revoking Share Access from Recipients](/concepts/revoking-share-access-from-recipients.md) - The process of removing a recipient's access to an OpenSharing share using Catalog Explorer, SQL, or CLI.
* [Risks of Direct File Deletion in Delta Lake on S3](/concepts/risks-of-direct-file-deletion-in-delta-lake-on-s3.md) - Using shell commands like rm -rf to drop Delta Lake tables on S3 can cause stale data and corruption; Delta Lake tables should be dropped using proper table operations.
* [Risks of using rm -rf to drop Delta Lake tables](/concepts/risks-of-using-rm-rf-to-drop-delta-lake-tables.md) - Using rm -rf to delete a Delta Lake table on S3 can lead to stale data issues; Databricks recommends using proper drop table operations instead.
* [Rolling Window Backtesting](/concepts/rolling-window-backtesting.md) - A time series evaluation technique using DateSplitter to create multiple train/test windows for robust model validation.
* [Rolling Window Backtesting for Time Series](/concepts/rolling-window-backtesting.md) - A model evaluation technique that creates multiple train/test splits using rolling windows over a time series, allowing assessment of forecast performance across different time periods.
* [Route Optimization (Databricks Model Serving)](/concepts/route-optimization-for-model-serving.md) - A feature that improves network path for inference requests on Databricks serving endpoints, enabling higher QPS and lower latency.
* [Route Optimization for Model Serving](/concepts/route-optimization-for-model-serving.md) - A Databricks-specific optimization that improves the network path for inference requests, resulting in faster, more direct communication between clients and models. Supports workloads above 200 QPS and sub-50ms overhead.
* [Route Optimization for Serving Endpoints](/concepts/route-optimization-for-serving-endpoints.md) - A performance feature recommended for endpoints with high QPS and throughput requirements that optimizes request routing.
* [Route Optimized Endpoints](/concepts/route-optimized-endpoints.md) - A model serving endpoint type that removes the workspace-level parallel requests limit (429 errors), recommended as a solution for high-concurrency workloads.
* [Route-Optimized Endpoint Configuration for Load Testing](/concepts/route-optimized-endpoint-configuration-for-load-testing.md) - Prerequisite configuration for Databricks Model Serving endpoints under test: start with a Small CPU endpoint (min/max concurrency=4), enable route optimization, and disable scale-to-zero to ensure consistent baseline measurements.
* [Route-optimized endpoint URL](/concepts/route-optimized-endpoint-url.md) - The dedicated URL format for route-optimized endpoints — https://<unique-id>.serving.cloud.databricks.com/<workspace-id>/serving-endpoints/<endpoint-name>/invocations — which provides the benefits of route optimization.
* [Route-Optimized Model Serving Endpoints](/concepts/route-optimized-model-serving-endpoints.md) - A Databricks Model Serving feature that enables OAuth-based authentication and requires specific endpoint configuration (fixed concurrency, disabled scale-to-zero) for effective load testing.
* [Route-optimized serving endpoints](/concepts/route-optimized-serving-endpoints.md) - Serving endpoints on Databricks with route optimization enabled for improved query performance, requiring dedicated URLs and OAuth authentication.
* [Row Filter and Column Mask Policies](/concepts/row-filter-policies.md) - ABAC-supported policy types for row and column-level security on tables, materialized views, and streaming tables in Unity Catalog.
* [Row Filter and Column Mask UDFs](/concepts/row-filter-and-column-mask-udfs.md) - User-defined functions used by row filter and column mask policies to implement filtering or masking logic, with SQL UDFs recommended for performance over Python UDFs.
* [Row Filter Policies](/concepts/row-filter-policies.md) - ABAC policy type that enforces row-level security on tables, materialized views, and streaming tables by filtering rows based on attributes
* [Row filter policies and column mask policies](/concepts/row-filter-policies.md) - ABAC policy types providing row-level and column-level security on tables, materialized views, and streaming tables in Unity Catalog.
* [Row filter policies in Unity Catalog](/concepts/row-filter-policies.md) - ABAC policy type that supports row-level security on tables, materialized views, and streaming tables.
* [Row filtering with column-only predicates](/concepts/row-filtering-with-column-only-predicates.md) - Filtering rows using simple boolean logic that references only table columns, enabling predicate pushdown for query optimization, including patterns for matching multiple country/region columns with a single policy.
* [Row filtering with region-based policies](/concepts/row-filtering-with-region-based-policies.md) - Applying row-level filters via Unity Catalog policies that restrict data visibility based on geographic region, using UDFs that compare a tagged column value against an allowed region constant.
* [Row filters (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/row-filters-unity-catalog.md) - SQL UDFs applied to tables that restrict which rows a user can see at query time based on per-row evaluation.
* [Row Filters and Column Masks](/concepts/row-filters-and-column-masks.md) - Per-table UDF-based row and column level filters that control what data users see at query time in Unity Catalog.
* [Row Filters in Unity Catalog](/concepts/row-filters-in-unity-catalog.md) - A row-level security feature in Databricks Unity Catalog that uses SQL UDFs to filter rows returned from a table based on user attributes or group membership.
* [Row Level Security in Delta Lake](/concepts/row-level-security-in-delta-lake.md) - A Delta Lake table feature for filtering rows based on user identity, which is incompatible with REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM.
* [Row-Level Conflict Detection in Delta Lake](/concepts/row-level-conflict-detection-in-delta-lake.md) - A mechanism in Delta Lake that detects conflicts at the row level when concurrent transactions modify the same data, with specific sub-conditions and limitations.
* [Row-Level Conflict Resolution](/concepts/row-level-conflict-resolution.md) - A mechanism in Delta Lake that detects and resolves conflicts at the individual row level during concurrent transactions
* [Row-Level Conflict Resolution in Delta Lake](/concepts/row-level-conflict-resolution-in-delta-lake.md) - A mechanism to resolve concurrent write conflicts at the row level rather than aborting the entire transaction, with specific failure modes like exceeded time or incompatible table features.
* [Row-Level Security (RLS) Policies](/concepts/row-level-security-rls-policies.md) - Databricks security feature for filtering rows that is incompatible with external metadata sources
* [Row-level Security (RLS) policies in Delta Lake](/concepts/row-level-security-rls-policies-in-delta-lake.md) - A Delta table security feature that filters rows at query time per user permissions, unsupported by external metadata sources.
* [Row-level security with Delta Uniform](/concepts/row-level-security-with-delta-uniform.md) - Row-level security features on Delta tables prevent REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM from succeeding
* [RStudio debugging with Databricks Connect](/concepts/rstudio-debugging-with-databricks-connect.md) - Databricks Connect enables interactive debugging of Spark code in RStudio Desktop, including setting breakpoints, inspecting variables in the Environment view, and stepping through code execution.
* [Rule-based LLM Evaluation without LLM Calls](/concepts/rule-based-llm-evaluation-without-llm-calls.md) - Guardrails AI scorers perform rule-based validation of LLM outputs without requiring additional LLM inference calls, offering a lightweight evaluation approach.
* [Run Evaluation From Dataset](/concepts/run-evaluation-from-dataset.md) - The workflow to trigger mlflow.genai.evaluate() from a stored evaluation dataset, generating a Python code snippet from the UI.
* [Run management in AI Runtime CLI](/concepts/run-management-in-ai-runtime-cli.md) - Commands for listing, inspecting status, and cancelling runs using air list runs and air cancel commands, with filtering options like --active and --limit.
* [Run Management in MLflow UI](/concepts/run-management-in-mlflow-ui.md) - Features for managing MLflow runs including selecting runs to display, deleting, comparing, adding/editing tags, filtering by parameter/metric values or tags, sorting by parameters, and grouping by parameter values.
* [Run Navigation and Log Retrieval Commands](/concepts/run-navigation-and-log-retrieval-commands.md) - The `air get run`, `air list runs`, and `air logs` commands provide navigation between Jobs, MLflow, and previous workloads, as well as streaming/downloading run logs.
* [Running Evaluations from Datasets](/concepts/run-evaluation-from-dataset.md) - The workflow and UI integration for triggering mlflow.genai.evaluate() directly from a saved evaluation dataset, including auto-generated Python code snippets.
* [Running MLflow Projects on Databricks](/concepts/running-mlflow-projects-on-databricks.md) - The process of executing MLflow Projects remotely on Databricks clusters using the MLflow CLI with 'mlflow run <uri> -b databricks --backend-config <cluster-spec>' and Databricks CLI authentication.
* [Runtime Requirements for Feature Store AutoML](/concepts/runtime-requirements-for-feature-store-automl.md) - Classification and regression experiments with feature store require Databricks Runtime 11.3 LTS ML+, forecasting requires 12.2 LTS ML+.
* [S3 Bucket Naming Restrictions for Unity Catalog](/concepts/s3-bucket-naming-restrictions-for-unity-catalog.md) - The requirement that S3 buckets used with Unity Catalog must not use dot notation in their names due to compatibility issues with features like OpenSharing.
* [S3 Bucket Versioning and Delta Lake](/concepts/s3-bucket-versioning-and-delta-lake.md) - Databricks recommends against enabling S3 bucket versioning for Delta Lake tables because Delta Lake implements its own versioning and garbage collection, and S3 versioning can cause performance degradation and storage bloat.
* [S3 Commit Service for Delta Lake](/concepts/s3-commit-service-for-delta-lake.md) - Databricks provides an S3 commit service to help manage multi-cluster writes and consistency for Delta Lake tables stored on S3.
* [S3 eventual consistency and Delta Lake](/concepts/s3-eventual-consistency-and-delta-lake.md) - The phenomenon where AWS S3's eventual consistency model can cause non-contiguous Delta log versions when a table is deleted and recreated at the same location.
* [S3 eventual consistency impact on Delta Lake](/concepts/s3-eventual-consistency-impact-on-delta-lake.md) - Amazon S3's eventually consistent model can cause data corruption or loss when multiple clusters or systems concurrently modify the same Delta Lake table stored on S3.
* [S3 Eventual Consistency Impact on Delta Tables](/concepts/s3-eventual-consistency-and-delta-lake.md) - A cause specific to AWS S3 where eventual consistency can lead to a non-contiguous delta log when a table is deleted and recreated at the same location.
* [S3 Eventually Consistent Model](/concepts/s3-eventually-consistent-model.md) - Amazon S3's eventual consistency model can cause data corruption and data loss when multiple systems or clusters modify the same Delta Lake table stored on S3 simultaneously.
* [Safe Span Management with Context Managers](/concepts/safe-span-management-with-context-managers.md) - Pattern for using Python context managers (contextlib.contextmanager) to ensure spans are always properly closed, even when exceptions occur.
* [Safety Judge (MLflow)](/concepts/safety-judge-mlflow.md) - A built-in MLflow judge that evaluates text content to identify potentially harmful, offensive, or inappropriate material, returning a pass/fail assessment with detailed rationale.
* [Safety Judge Scorer](/concepts/safety-judge-scorer.md) - A pre-built safety evaluation scorer in Databricks that can be registered, started, and configured with sampling to assess content safety of model outputs.
* [Safety Scorer (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/safety-scorer-in-mlflow.md) - Pre-built safety evaluation judge from mlflow.genai.scorers that can be registered and configured with sampling rates to assess safety of model outputs.
* [Safety Scorer in MLflow](/concepts/safety-scorer-in-mlflow.md) - A built-in MLflow GenAI scorer that evaluates content safety, registered and started with a sampling configuration.
* [Safety Scorer in MLflow GenAI](/concepts/safety-scorer-in-mlflow.md) - A built-in MLflow GenAI scorer that evaluates the safety of model outputs, registered and started with optional sampling configuration before use.
* [Sampling Large Datasets for AutoML](/concepts/sampling-large-datasets-for-automl.md) - Automatic memory-based sampling of large datasets in AutoML, using stratified sampling for classification (sampleBy) and simple sampling for regression (sample).
* [Sampling Large Datasets in AutoML](/concepts/sampling-large-datasets-in-automl.md) - How AutoML handles large datasets by sampling, using stratified sampling for classification and simple sampling for regression to fit within single-node memory limits.
* [Sampling-based restoration detection](/concepts/sampling-based-restoration-detection.md) - Databricks samples files presumed to be archived to detect if they have been restored, and if so, assumes all files are restored for that query.
* [Sampling-based restored data detection](/concepts/sampling-based-restoration-detection.md) - Databricks samples files presumed archived to detect whether they have been restored; if sampling indicates restoration, it assumes all files are available.
* [SAP BDC as Authoritative Metadata Source](/concepts/sap-bdc-as-authoritative-metadata-source.md) - SAP Business Data Cloud is the source of truth for semantic metadata; metadata synced into Databricks is read-only and cannot be directly modified by users or OpenSharing recipients.
* [SAP BDC Connection](/concepts/sap-bdc-connection.md) - A named connection object that links a Databricks workspace to an SAP Business Data Cloud account for OpenSharing, enabling bidirectional data sharing.
* [SAP BDC Connection (Databricks)](/concepts/sap-bdc-connection.md) - A bidirectional connection between Databricks and SAP Business Data Cloud that enables Delta Sharing via the OpenSharing protocol, with automatic creation of provider and recipient objects.
* [SAP BDC Connection Deletion Prerequisites](/concepts/sap-bdc-connection-deletion-prerequisites.md) - Before deleting an SAP BDC connection, all mounted shares received from SAP BDC must be unmounted from Unity Catalog catalogs, or deletion is blocked.
* [SAP BDC Connection Deletion Workflow](/concepts/sap-bdc-connection-deletion-workflow.md) - A two-step process requiring first unmounting all shares from Unity Catalog catalogs, then deleting the connection; after deletion, both Databricks loses access to SAP data products and SAP loses access to shared assets.
* [SAP BDC Connection Lifecycle](/concepts/sap-bdc-connection-lifecycle.md) - The process of creating, updating ownership, and deleting an SAP BDC connection on Databricks including prerequisites and cleanup steps
* [SAP BDC Connection Lifecycle Management](/concepts/sap-bdc-connection-lifecycle.md) - The process of creating, transferring ownership, and deleting SAP BDC connections on Databricks, including prerequisite cleanup steps for deletion.
* [SAP BDC Connection Limitations](/concepts/sap-bdc-connection-limitations.md) - A hard limit of five SAP BDC connections per Databricks metastore, enforced at the metastore level.
* [SAP BDC Connection on Databricks](/concepts/sap-bdc-connection.md) - A configuration enabling bidirectional data sharing between SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks via OpenSharing
* [SAP BDC Connection Ownership](/concepts/sap-bdc-connection-ownership.md) - The ownership model for SAP BDC connections, where the creator becomes owner, ownership can only be transferred to individual users (not groups or service principals), and the owner is required to delete connections.
* [SAP BDC Connector](/concepts/sap-bdc-connector.md) - The connection infrastructure between a Databricks workspace and an SAP Business Data Cloud account required before sharing data.
* [SAP BDC Metadata Consumption Interfaces](/concepts/sap-bdc-metadata-consumption-interfaces.md) - Multiple Databricks interfaces for consuming synced SAP BDC metadata: Catalog Explorer, SQL (DESCRIBE TABLE, INFORMATION_SCHEMA), Genie Spaces natural language queries, ABAC governance policies, and audit logs.
* [SAP BDC OpenSharing Connection](/concepts/sap-bdc-opensharing-connection.md) - A bidirectional Delta Sharing connection between Databricks and SAP Business Data Cloud that enables data exchange via the OpenSharing protocol.
* [SAP BDC OpenSharing Recipients](/concepts/sap-bdc-opensharing-recipients.md) - Recipient entities automatically created in Databricks when an SAP BDC connection is established, used to manage share access
* [SAP BDC Provider and Recipient Objects](/concepts/sap-bdc-provider-and-recipient-objects.md) - Two automatically created Unity Catalog objects (provider and recipient) that represent the SAP BDC account for sharing and receiving data.
* [SAP BDC Provider Object](/concepts/sap-bdc-provider-object.md) - An object automatically created upon SAP BDC connection establishment that represents the SAP BDC account as a data provider within Unity Catalog.
* [SAP BDC Recipient](/concepts/sap-bdc-recipient.md) - A Delta Sharing recipient automatically created when a Databricks-to-SAP BDC connection is established, used to grant data share access.
* [SAP BDC Recipient Object](/concepts/sap-bdc-recipient-object.md) - An object automatically created upon SAP BDC connection establishment that represents the SAP BDC account as a data recipient within Unity Catalog.
* [SAP BDC SDK](/concepts/sap-bdc-sdk.md) - A Python SDK (sap-bdc-connect-sdk) used to describe Delta Shares in Core Schema Notation for consumption by SAP BDC users.
* [SAP BDC Semantic Metadata Ingestion](/concepts/sap-bdc-semantic-metadata-ingestion.md) - Automatic syncing of SAP Business Data Cloud table and column semantic metadata (comments, constraints, tags) into Databricks Unity Catalog for mounted SAP BDC shares.
* [SAP BDC Semantic Metadata Sync](/concepts/sap-bdc-semantic-metadata-sync.md) - Automatic synchronization of SAP semantic metadata (table and column comments, primary keys, foreign keys, governance tags) into Unity Catalog when a share from SAP BDC is mounted to a catalog.
* [SAP BDC Semantic Metadata Synchronization](/concepts/sap-bdc-semantic-metadata-sync.md) - When an SAP BDC share is mounted to a Unity Catalog catalog, SAP semantic metadata—including table/column comments, primary keys, foreign keys, and governance tags—automatically syncs into Unity Catalog.
* [SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Connector](/concepts/sap-business-data-cloud-bdc-connector.md) - A Databricks connector that establishes a connection between a Databricks workspace and an SAP BDC account for data sharing
* [SAP PersonalData Governance Tags](/concepts/sap-personaldata-governance-tags.md) - System-reserved governance tags in the sap.PersonalData namespace that automatically sync from SAP BDC into Unity Catalog to classify tables containing personal or sensitive information.
* [SAP Semantic Metadata Synchronization](/concepts/sap-bdc-semantic-metadata-sync.md) - When an SAP BDC share is mounted to a Unity Catalog catalog, SAP semantic metadata (table/column comments, primary keys, foreign keys, governance tags) syncs automatically into Unity Catalog.
* [SavedModel Format Requirement for Keras Callbacks](/concepts/savedmodel-format-requirement-for-keras-callbacks.md) - When using horovod.spark with custom Keras callbacks, models must be saved in TensorFlow SavedModel format (.tf for TF 2.x, save_weights_only=True for TF 1.x).
* [Saving Spark DataFrames to TFRecords](/concepts/saving-spark-dataframes-to-tfrecords.md) - The process of writing Apache Spark DataFrames to the TFRecord binary format using the spark-tensorflow-connector library.
* [sbt-assembly Plugin for Building Databricks JARs](/concepts/sbt-assembly-plugin-for-building-databricks-jars.md) - Using sbt-assembly (version 2.3.1) to build a fat JAR for Databricks serverless compute; configured via project/plugins.sbt and built with sbt commands.
* [Scala Databricks Utilities limitation](/concepts/scala-databricks-utilities-limitation.md) - Only DBFS and secrets utilities are available through Databricks Utilities for Scala; no other Databricks Utilities functionality is supported.
* [Scalable Metadata Handling](/concepts/scalable-metadata-handling.md) - Delta Lake handles metadata at scale, allowing data lakes to manage billions of files and partitions without performance degradation.
* [Scalable metadata handling in Delta Lake](/concepts/scalable-metadata-handling-in-delta-lake.md) - Delta Lake's ability to efficiently manage metadata at scale for large data lake environments, preventing metadata bottlenecks common in traditional data lakes.
* [Scalar Iterator pandas UDFs](/concepts/scalar-iterator-pandas-udfs.md) - A newer variant of pandas UDFs that offers flexible APIs, support for any deep learning library, and high performance for featurization tasks in distributed Spark environments.
* [Scalar UDFs on Dedicated Access Mode](/concepts/scalar-udfs-on-dedicated-access-mode.md) - Scalar UDFs are unavailable on compute resources using dedicated access mode (formerly single user) in Databricks Connect for Scala.
* [Scale to Zero in Model Serving](/concepts/scale-to-zero-in-model-serving.md) - Configuration option allowing endpoints to scale down when not in use, introducing cold-start latency and not recommended for production
* [Scale-to-Zero and GPU Capacity Planning](/concepts/scale-to-zero-and-gpu-capacity-planning.md) - The scale-to-zero behavior and capacity planning considerations for custom LLM serving endpoints on Databricks, including fixed replica counts, cold start latency, and cloud provider GPU availability constraints.
* [Scale-to-Zero for Model Serving Endpoints](/concepts/scale-to-zero-in-model-serving.md) - An optional configuration that allows endpoints to scale down to zero when idle, with the trade-off of cold-start latency and no guaranteed capacity when scaling back up.
* [Scaled Dot Product Attention (SDPA)](/concepts/scaled-dot-product-attention-sdpa.md) - An attention implementation alternative to Flash Attention offering broader GPU compatibility in Axolotl configurations
* [Scheduled Jobs with AI Runtime](/concepts/scheduled-jobs-with-ai-runtime.md) - Recurring job execution on serverless GPU using AI Runtime, with restrictions on environment panel dependencies and auto-recovery.
* [Scheduled Jobs with Serverless GPU](/concepts/scheduled-jobs-with-serverless-gpu.md) - Recurring notebook jobs on serverless GPU compute, with limitations on environment panel usage and auto-recovery, plus a 7-day maximum runtime.
* [Scheduled Scorers (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/scheduled-scorers-mlflow-genai.md) - A serverless workload in Databricks MLflow that periodically evaluates GenAI applications in production, subject to budget policy controls.
* [Scheduled Streaming Table Deep Clone Restriction](/concepts/scheduled-streaming-table-deep-clone-restriction.md) - Scheduled streaming tables cannot be used as the source of a deep clone operation
* [Scheduled Streaming Tables](/concepts/scheduled-streaming-tables.md) - Streaming tables configured to run on a schedule, which are not supported for deep clone operations in Databricks
* [SCHEDULED_TABLE_NOT_SUPPORTED](/concepts/scheduled_table_not_supported.md) - Sub-error indicating that scheduled streaming tables cannot be used as the source for a deep clone operation.
* [SCHEDULED_TABLE_NOT_SUPPORTED sub-error](/concepts/scheduled_table_not_supported.md) - Sub-error indicating that scheduled (automated refresh) streaming tables cannot be deep cloned.
* [Scheduling AI Runtime Jobs](/concepts/scheduling-ai-runtime-jobs.md) - Method for running recurring GPU-accelerated notebook jobs on serverless GPU with specific limitations on dependencies and auto-recovery
* [Schema and Data Type Compatibility with Apache Iceberg](/concepts/schema-and-data-type-compatibility-with-apache-iceberg.md) - IcebergCompatV imposes restrictions on supported data types and schema changes, including unsupported data types, partition data types, and type widening that are incompatible with Apache Iceberg.
* [Schema and data type constraints for Iceberg compat](/concepts/schema-and-data-type-constraints-for-iceberg-compat.md) - IcebergCompatV imposes restrictions on schema changes and data types, including unsupported data types, unsupported partition data types, type widening incompatibility, and partition spec rename restrictions.
* [Schema and Type Compatibility Between Delta and Iceberg](/concepts/schema-and-data-type-compatibility-with-apache-iceberg.md) - Constraints on schema changes and data types when enabling IcebergCompatV, including unsupported data types, unsupported partition data types, and incompatibility with type widening.
* [Schema Mismatch with Open Source Spark and Delta Sharing](/concepts/schema-mismatch-with-open-source-spark-and-delta-sharing.md) - Schema mismatch errors occur in OSS Spark when Delta table schema or partition columns change after DataFrame creation; fixable by setting spark.delta.sharing.client.useStructuralSchemaMatch to true (requires client 1.2.3+ and Spark 4.0+).
* [SCHEMA_MISMATCH sub-error](/concepts/schema_mismatch-sub-error.md) - A DELTA_METADATA_MISMATCH sub-error that occurs when the data schema does not match the Delta table schema, resolvable via mergeSchema or auto-merge configuration
* [Schema-Level Data Sharing](/concepts/schema-level-data-sharing.md) - Sharing an entire schema (database) in a share, which provides recipients access to all current and future tables, streaming tables, views, materialized views, models, and volumes in that schema. Tables shared this way always include full history. Has limitations: unsupported assets are filtered out, aliases/partitions are removed, and schema-level aliasing is not supported.
* [Schema-Level Sharing](/concepts/schema-level-sharing.md) - Sharing an entire schema in Unity Catalog so recipients automatically gain access to all current and future tables, views, volumes, and models within that schema.
* [Schema-level Sharing in OpenSharing](/concepts/schema-level-sharing-in-opensharing.md) - Sharing an entire schema (database) so recipients automatically access all current and future tables, views, models, and volumes within it, with specific limitations on unsupported asset types.
* [Schema-to-session integration](/concepts/schema-to-session-integration.md) - Labeling schemas are associated with labeling sessions in the Review App, and schemas defined in an experiment are automatically available when creating sessions.
* [Scoped Resource Access in Databricks Apps](/concepts/scoped-resource-access-in-databricks-apps.md) - The principle that adding a resource (like an MLflow experiment) to an app grants access only to that specific resource instance, not to all resources of that type in the workspace.
* [Scorer class](/concepts/scorer-class.md) - A Pydantic-based base class for defining scorers with internal state and custom logic, offering more flexibility than the @scorer decorator but not supported for production monitoring.
* [Scorer class (Pydantic base class)](/concepts/scorer-class.md) - An alternative, Pydantic-based base class for defining scorers with internal state and custom fields, supporting more flexibility than the @scorer decorator.
* [Scorer class for advanced customization](/concepts/scorer-class.md) - An alternative to the decorator pattern, using a Pydantic-based Scorer base class that supports internal state and custom fields, but not supported for production monitoring.
* [Scorer decorator pattern](/concepts/scorer-decorator-pattern.md) - The @scorer decorator for defining lightweight, stateless custom evaluation functions in MLflow's GenAI evaluation framework
* [Scorer error handling strategies](/concepts/scorer-error-handling-strategies.md) - Two approaches for handling errors in MLflow scorers: letting exceptions propagate naturally or handling them explicitly with AssessmentError.
* [Scorer Immutability](/concepts/scorer-immutability.md) - Scorers are immutable objects; every lifecycle operation returns a new scorer instance rather than modifying the original, preventing accidental modifications in production.
* [Scorer Immutable Update Pattern](/concepts/scorer-immutable-update-pattern.md) - Scorer instances are immutable — update() and stop() return new Scorer instances with modified configuration rather than mutating the original
* [Scorer inputs](/concepts/scorer-inputs.md) - The four optional input parameters available to scorers: inputs, outputs, expectations, and trace from MLflow traces
* [Scorer inputs and outputs](/concepts/scorer-inputs-and-outputs.md) - Scorers receive inputs (request), outputs (response), expectations (ground truth), and trace (complete MLflow trace), and return simple values or rich Feedback objects.
* [Scorer Lifecycle](/concepts/scorer-lifecycle.md) - The lifecycle states and transitions for production scorers in Databricks MLflow, centered around MLflow experiments.
* [Scorer Lifecycle Management](/concepts/scorer-lifecycle-management.md) - Lifecycle methods (register, start, update, stop) that control MLflow scorers running continuous quality assessment on production traces
* [Scorer Lifecycle Management (Register/Start Pattern)](/concepts/scorer-lifecycle-management-registerstart-pattern.md) - Two-step API pattern (.register() then .start()) for associating scorers with an MLflow experiment and activating continuous quality monitoring.
* [Scorer metric naming conventions](/concepts/scorer-naming-conventions.md) - Rules for how metric names are derived in MLflow evaluation runs from Feedback.name fields, function names, or Scorer.name fields.
* [Scorer Naming Conventions](/concepts/scorer-naming-conventions.md) - Rules for how metric names are derived from code-based scorers, based on function names, Feedback object names, or class names depending on return type.
* [Scorer Naming Conventions (MLflow)](/concepts/scorer-naming-conventions.md) - Rules determining how metric names are derived from decorator function names, Feedback object name fields, or Scorer class name fields.
* [Scorer Properties (sample_rate and filter_string)](/concepts/scorer-properties-sample_rate-and-filter_string.md) - Instance attributes sample_rate (0.0-1.0) and filter_string that reflect the current sampling configuration of an active or stopped scorer
* [Scorer Registration and Activation Lifecycle](/concepts/scorer-registration-and-activation-lifecycle.md) - The pattern of registering a scorer with a name and starting it with a sampling configuration before use, as seen with both built-in and custom scorers.
* [Scorer Registration and Lifecycle](/concepts/scorer-registration-and-lifecycle.md) - Two-phase lifecycle pattern for scorers: .register(name=...) to assign a name, then .start(sampling_config=...) to activate the scorer with a sampling configuration.
* [Scorer Registration and Registry](/concepts/scorer-registration-and-registry.md) - API functions to register, retrieve, list, and delete scorers via get_scorer(), list_scorers(), delete_scorer(), and Scorer.register()
* [Scorer Sampling Configuration](/concepts/scorer-sampling-configuration.md) - Mechanism to control what fraction of traces are evaluated by a scorer using ScorerSamplingConfig and sample_rate parameters.
* [Scorer State Management](/concepts/scorer-state-management.md) - Management of scorer states including registration, start (active monitoring), stop (sample rate set to 0, kept registered), update, and deletion from the server.
* [Scorers — Replacing Custom Metrics and Judges](/concepts/scorers-replacing-custom-metrics-and-judges.md) - Scorers are the unified evaluation building block in MLflow 3, replacing both @metric decorators and LLM judge functions, with predefined scorers (Correctness, Safety, Guidelines, etc.) and a @scorer decorator for custom logic.
* [Scorers (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/scorers-mlflow-genai.md) - A unified interface within MLflow GenAI evaluation framework that defines evaluation criteria for models, agents, and applications, receiving traces and returning quality assessments as feedback.
* [Scorers and LLM Judges](/concepts/scorers-and-llm-judges.md) - Evaluation components (built-in, guidelines-based, custom-prompt, code-based) used to assess GenAI trace quality, supporting both single-turn and multi-turn judging.
* [Scorers in MLflow Evaluation](/concepts/scorers-in-mlflow-evaluation.md) - Quality metrics (built-in or custom) applied by mlflow.genai.evaluate() to assess GenAI app outputs, including LLM judges for relevance, safety, and more.
* [ScorerSamplingConfig](/concepts/scorersamplingconfig.md) - Data class holding sampling rate and optional filter string configuration for controlling which traces a scorer evaluates
* [Scoring Format Change](/concepts/scoring-format-change.md) - The difference in request/response format between Legacy MLflow Model Serving and new Model Serving, requiring application updates during migration.
* [SDK-Based Dataset Management in MLflow](/concepts/sdk-based-dataset-management-in-mlflow.md) - Programmatic creation, updating, and management of evaluation datasets using the MLflow Python SDK, including methods like create_dataset(), merge_records(), search_traces(), and to_df().
* [SDPA (Scaled Dot Product Attention)](/concepts/sdpa-scaled-dot-product-attention.md) - Flash Attention alternative used for broader GPU compatibility in transformer model training
* [Search by Certification Status](/concepts/search-by-certification-status.md) - A search capability in Databricks that allows users to filter and query Unity Catalog objects by their certification status using the certificationStatus keyword or filter menu.
* [Searching and Discovering Prompts](/concepts/searching-and-discovering-prompts.md) - Prompts can be discovered and filtered using mlflow.genai.search_prompts() with filter strings for catalog and schema, with optional max_results limiting.
* [Searching and Filtering Traces and Spans](/concepts/searching-and-filtering-traces-and-spans.md) - API methods (search_spans, search_assessments, search_traces) for finding specific spans and assessments by name, regex pattern, type, span ID, or combined criteria.
* [Searching by Certification Status in Databricks](/concepts/searching-by-certification-status-in-databricks.md) - Using the 'certificationStatus' keyword in Databricks search to filter for certified or deprecated data assets across supported object types.
* [Searching OTel Traces via MLflow SDK](/concepts/searching-otel-traces-via-mlflow-sdk.md) - Using the span.attributes.* prefix in mlflow.search_traces() filter_string to query ingested OTel traces by attribute values like session.id, user.id, or token counts.
* [Secret Naming Conventions for Online Stores](/concepts/secret-naming-conventions-for-online-stores.md) - Standardized naming pattern for Databricks secrets targeting online stores, using scope-prefix-key combinations with store-specific keys (e.g., access-key-id, secret-access-key, user, password).
* [Secret Scope Management for Feature Store](/concepts/secret-scope-management-for-feature-store.md) - Creating and managing Databricks secret scopes for accessing multiple online stores, including naming conventions (prefix-based) and avoiding scope limits by sharing scopes.
* [Secret Scope Separation for Read and Write Access](/concepts/secret-scope-separation-for-read-and-write-access.md) - The pattern of creating two separate Databricks secret scopes — one for read-only access and one for read-write access — to manage online store credentials with least-privilege principles.
* [Secrets-Based Environment Variables for Model Serving](/concepts/secrets-based-environment-variables-in-model-serving.md) - Using Databricks secrets to securely inject sensitive credentials like API keys into model serving endpoints at runtime
* [Secrets-Based Environment Variables in Databricks Model Serving](/concepts/secrets-based-environment-variables-in-databricks-model-serving.md) - Securely storing and referencing API keys, tokens, and other credentials as Databricks secrets, then injecting them into model serving endpoints via the {{secrets/scope/key}} syntax.
* [Secrets-based Environment Variables in Model Serving](/concepts/secrets-based-environment-variables-in-model-serving.md) - Using Databricks secrets to securely inject credentials like API keys into model serving endpoints at serving time
* [Securable AI Assets in Unity Catalog](/concepts/securable-ai-assets-in-unity-catalog.md) - ML models, Unity Catalog functions, HTTP connections, and hosted foundation models managed as securable objects with grant/revoke privileges.
* [Securable AI Objects in Unity Catalog](/concepts/securable-ai-objects-in-unity-catalog.md) - AI resources—including models, functions, HTTP connections, and hosted foundation models—that can have permissions granted and revoked using standard Unity Catalog privileges.
* [Securable Object Lifecycle in Delta Sharing](/concepts/securable-object-lifecycle-in-delta-sharing.md) - The constraint that a securable object (e.g., view, table) cannot be dropped while it is actively shared via OpenSharing, shares, or clean rooms, with ALTER VIEW recommended as an alternative.
* [Securable objects in Unity Catalog](/concepts/securable-objects-in-unity-catalog.md) - Objects in the Unity Catalog hierarchy (catalog, schema, table, etc.) that can have attributes and have policies attached to them for access control.
* [Securable Types in Databricks](/concepts/securable-types-in-databricks.md) - Objects such as tables, views, or schemas that can be shared via Delta Sharing and are protected from deletion while actively shared; the error message uses placeholders for securable type and full name.
* [Secure API Key Management for MLflow Tracing](/concepts/secure-api-key-management-for-mlflow-tracing.md) - Best practices for managing API keys in production GenAI tracing environments using AI Gateway or Databricks secrets instead of hardcoding credentials.
* [SecureConnect](/concepts/secureconnect.md) - Databricks feature enabling data sharing through firewalls by routing traffic through Databricks-internal IP addresses
* [SecureConnect and IP Access List Interaction](/concepts/secureconnect-and-ip-access-list-interaction.md) - When SecureConnect is enabled on the provider side, the IP access list also enforces access to shared data storage; without SecureConnect, storage URLs are reachable from any client IP regardless of the IP access list.
* [SecureConnect billing and usage attribution](/concepts/secureconnect-billing-and-usage-attribution.md) - Providers are billed for data transfer through SecureConnect, with per-recipient usage attributed via the recipient_id field in the billing system table for cost breakdown.
* [SecureConnect Billing for Data Transfer](/concepts/secureconnect-billing-for-data-transfer.md) - Databricks SecureConnect feature that bills data transfer through Databricks rather than the cloud vendor, providing an alternative billing model for egress costs.
* [SecureConnect IP access lists (ACLs)](/concepts/secureconnect-ip-access-lists-acls.md) - For open recipients, IP ACLs can restrict which client IP addresses are allowed to reach SecureConnect, covering both OpenSharing endpoint access and storage access.
* [SecureConnect Limitations](/concepts/secureconnect-limitations.md) - Known constraints of SecureConnect including lack of mTLS support for classic compute and OIDC recipients, and unsupported serverless-to-Open credential scenarios
* [SecureConnect limitations and supported scenarios](/concepts/secureconnect-limitations-and-supported-scenarios.md) - SecureConnect supports sharing to AWS, Azure, and GCP recipients but has limitations including incompatibility with Cloudflare R2 storage, unavailability on AWS GovCloud, and AWS PrivateLink-to-S3 incompatibility with FIPS endpoints in US regions.
* [SecureConnect managed proxy architecture](/concepts/secureconnect-managed-proxy-architecture.md) - Databricks routes recipient data requests through a managed proxy, eliminating the need for providers to update storage firewalls when adding new recipients.
* [SecureConnect PrivateLink and NCC configuration](/concepts/secureconnect-privatelink-and-ncc-configuration.md) - For shared storage behind private endpoints not reachable from the public network, account admins can configure a Network Connectivity Configuration (NCC) and attach it to the OpenSharing metastore, with a note that AWS PrivateLink to S3 is incompatible with FIPS endpoints in US regions.
* [SecureConnect Recipient Firewall Configuration](/concepts/secureconnect-recipient-firewall-configuration.md) - The network configuration steps a Databricks recipient must take to access shares from a provider using SecureConnect, including allowlisting inbound IPs
* [SecureView barrier and predicate pushdown on protected tables](/concepts/secureview-barrier-and-predicate-pushdown-on-protected-tables.md) - The SecureView barrier introduced by row filters and column masks blocks certain predicates from being pushed to the storage layer, preventing partition pruning and liquid clustering optimizations. Simple equality predicates can still be pushed down, but functions and implicit casts are blocked, potentially forcing full table scans.
* [Security and Data Management for Autologging](/concepts/security-and-data-management-for-autologging.md) - How model training information captured by Autologging is stored in MLflow Tracking and secured using MLflow Experiment permissions.
* [Selective Caching with Predicate Pushdown](/concepts/selective-caching-with-predicate-pushdown.md) - Technique of caching only a subset of columns and rows from a table using column lists and WHERE predicates, enabling subsequent queries to avoid scanning original files.
* [Selective Column and Row Caching](/concepts/selective-column-and-row-caching.md) - The ability to cache only a subset of columns (via column list) and rows (via WHERE predicate) in the disk cache, minimizing scan overhead.
* [Selective Feature Publishing](/concepts/selective-feature-publishing.md) - Publishing only a subset of features from an offline feature table to an online store using the 'features' argument in publish_table(), while primary keys and timestamp keys are always included.
* [Selective Time-Range Migration](/concepts/selective-time-range-migration.md) - The ability to migrate only traces created after a specific timestamp using the start_time_ms parameter in epoch milliseconds, with automatic deduplication of already-migrated traces.
* [Semantic Type Annotations](/concepts/semantic-type-annotations.md) - A mechanism to manually control semantic type assignment in Databricks AutoML using Python metadata annotations, supporting types: categorical, numeric, datetime, text, and the 'native' keyword to disable detection.
* [Semantic Type Annotations for AutoML](/concepts/semantic-type-annotations-for-automl.md) - Manual annotations (categorical, numeric, datetime, text, native) that users can apply to columns to override AutoML's automatic semantic type detection.
* [Semantic Type Annotations in AutoML](/concepts/semantic-type-annotations-in-automl.md) - How users can manually assign semantic types (categorical, numeric, datetime, text, or native) to columns using metadata annotations to override or disable automatic detection.
* [Semantic Type Detection](/concepts/semantic-type-detection.md) - AutoML's ability to infer semantic types (categorical, numeric, datetime, text) from raw Spark/pandas data types, with support for manual annotations.
* [Semantic Type Detection in AutoML](/concepts/semantic-type-detection.md) - AutoML's ability to detect semantic types (datetime, numeric, categorical, text) beyond raw Spark/pandas data types, including manual annotation support.
* [Separation of Duties for ABAC](/concepts/separation-of-duties-in-abac.md) - The organizational model distributing ABAC responsibilities across specialized roles: tag taxonomy creators, data stewards (tagging), governance admins (policy creation), data creators, and data consumers.
* [Separation of Duties in ABAC](/concepts/separation-of-duties-in-abac.md) - ABAC enables distributing governance tasks across specialized teams: taxonomy creators, data stewards (tagging), governance admins (policy creation), data creators, and data consumers.
* [Separation of Duties in ABAC Deployment](/concepts/separation-of-duties-in-abac-deployment.md) - A five-step workflow distributing ABAC responsibilities across specialized roles: tag taxonomy creation, data asset tagging, policy creation, data object creation, and governed data access — each with distinct permission requirements.
* [Sequential LLM Call Tracing in Workflows](/concepts/sequential-llm-call-tracing-in-workflows.md) - Pattern where automatic tracing captures multiple LLM calls within a single workflow (e.g., analyze-then-respond), producing a trace with a parent span for the overall function and child spans for each LLM invocation.
* [Sequential Multi-LLM Call Tracing in MLflow](/concepts/sequential-multi-llm-call-tracing-in-mlflow.md) - Tracing workflows that make multiple sequential LLM calls (e.g., analysis followed by response generation) within a single traced function.
* [Sequential Multi-LLM Call Tracing Pattern](/concepts/multi-llm-call-tracing-pattern.md) - A pattern where automatic tracing captures multiple sequential LLM calls in a single workflow, creating a trace with a parent span and child spans for each LLM invocation.
* [Served Entities Configuration](/concepts/served-entities-configuration.md) - The served_entities array in a serving endpoint config where each entity specifies a name, entity_name, entity_version, and throughput limits.
* [Served Entity Configuration](/concepts/served-entity-configuration.md) - Named configuration objects that define which model versions, workload sizes, and scaling settings are assigned to each model on a multi-model endpoint.
* [Served Entity Grants](/concepts/served-entity-grants.md) - Unity Catalog grants (READ, EXECUTE) that the endpoint creator must hold on each served model and its transitive function dependencies, validated at endpoint creation and update time.
* [Server-side Batch Pipeline Redaction](/concepts/server-side-batch-pipeline-redaction.md) - Recommended approach using Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines to materialize redacted copies of OTel span, log, and annotation tables via incremental streaming.
* [Server-Side Request Timeout](/concepts/server-side-timeout.md) - Default timeout limits for requests sent to model serving endpoints, varying by endpoint type (CPU vs GPU).
* [Server-Side Request Timeouts in Model Serving](/concepts/server-side-timeout.md) - Timeouts caused by the serving endpoint taking too long to respond to a request, with default limits varying by endpoint type (CPU vs GPU).
* [Server-Side Timeout](/concepts/server-side-timeout.md) - Request processing time limits enforced by the model serving endpoint, varying by workload type (CPU/GPU small, medium, large).
* [Server-Side Timeouts](/concepts/server-side-timeout.md) - Default request timeout durations on the server side of Databricks Model Serving endpoints, varying by endpoint type (CPU vs GPU with different sizes).
* [Server-side Timeouts for Model Serving Endpoints](/concepts/server-side-timeout.md) - Default request timeout durations on the server side for CPU and GPU model serving endpoints, distinguishing them from client-side timeouts.
* [Serverless Autologging Restriction](/concepts/serverless-autologging-restriction.md) - On Databricks serverless compute clusters, autologging is not automatically enabled and requires explicit invocation
* [Serverless Budget Policy](/concepts/serverless-budget-policy.md) - A Databricks policy that controls cost and resource allocation for serverless workloads, which can be assigned to MLflow experiments to govern serverless compute usage.
* [Serverless Budget Policy (Databricks)](/concepts/serverless-budget-policy.md) - A policy that controls which budget policy is used for serverless workloads in Databricks, including those run by MLflow experiments.
* [Serverless Budget Policy for MLflow Experiments](/concepts/serverless-budget-policy.md) - A policy that controls which Databricks serverless budget policy MLflow uses for serverless workloads associated with an MLflow experiment.
* [Serverless Budget Policy Permissions](/concepts/serverless-budget-policy-permissions.md) - The requirement that users and service principals must be entitled to use a budget policy before they can assign it to an MLflow experiment.
* [Serverless Compute Autologging Requirement](/concepts/serverless-compute-autologging-requirement.md) - On Databricks serverless compute clusters, autologging for GenAI tracing frameworks is not automatically enabled and must be explicitly enabled by calling the appropriate mlflow.<library>.autolog() function.
* [Serverless Compute Cluster Autolog Requirements](/concepts/serverless-compute-autologging-requirement.md) - On Databricks serverless compute clusters, autologging for GenAI tracing frameworks is not automatically enabled and requires explicit calls to mlflow.<library>.autolog().
* [Serverless compute connection in Databricks Connect](/concepts/serverless-compute-connection-in-databricks-connect.md) - Configuration for connecting to serverless compute using DATABRICKS_SERVERLESS_COMPUTE_ID=auto, serverless_compute_id=auto in profiles, or builder.serverless()/remote(serverless=True).
* [Serverless Compute for Databricks Connect Testing](/concepts/serverless-compute-for-databricks-connect-testing.md) - Databricks recommends using serverless compute with Databricks Connect for all testing because it provides Databricks Runtime features, Unity Catalog integration, faster execution, and better end-to-end validation than local OSS PySpark testing.
* [Serverless Compute for Databricks JARs](/concepts/serverless-compute-for-databricks-jars.md) - How to build, configure, and run Java/Scala JARs as tasks on Databricks serverless compute using Lakeflow Jobs, including version requirements and limitations.
* [Serverless Compute for Model Serving](/concepts/serverless-compute-for-model-serving.md) - The underlying serverless compute architecture that powers Model Serving, enabling automatic scaling, cost savings, and high availability without manual infrastructure management.
* [Serverless Compute JAR Compatibility Requirements](/concepts/serverless-compute-jar-compatibility-requirements.md) - JARs targeting Databricks serverless compute must exactly match the runtime Scala and JDK versions, use Databricks Connect, and only use public Spark APIs.
* [Serverless Compute JAR Limitations](/concepts/serverless-compute-jar-limitations.md) - APIs and techniques unavailable on serverless compute: RDD API, Spark internal APIs, and native libraries
