* [Serverless Compute Limitations for JAR Tasks](/concepts/serverless-compute-limitations-for-jar-tasks.md) - Restrictions when running JARs on Databricks serverless compute: no RDD API, no Spark internal APIs, no native libraries; code must use public Spark APIs only.
* [Serverless Compute Query Timeout](/concepts/serverless-compute-query-timeout.md) - A configurable timeout property (spark.databricks.execution.timeout) for Databricks notebooks running on serverless compute, which defaults to 9000 seconds.
* [Serverless compute query timeout configuration](/concepts/serverless-compute-query-timeout.md) - For notebooks on serverless compute, queries default to 9000 second timeout, configurable via spark.databricks.execution.timeout
* [Serverless compute query timeout in Databricks notebooks](/concepts/serverless-compute-query-timeout.md) - For notebooks running on serverless compute, queries time out after 9000 seconds by default, configurable via the Spark property spark.databricks.execution.timeout.
* [Serverless Compute Tracing Requirements](/concepts/serverless-compute-tracing-requirements.md) - On serverless compute clusters, GenAI tracing autologging is not automatically enabled and must be explicitly enabled by calling mlflow.<library>.autolog() functions.
* [Serverless Compute Trust Relationship (Databricks)](/concepts/serverless-compute-trust-relationship-databricks.md) - A required trust policy configuration on an AWS IAM role that allows Databricks serverless compute to assume the role when running model serving endpoints.
* [Serverless Compute Trust Relationship for IAM Roles](/concepts/serverless-compute-trust-relationship-for-iam-roles.md) - IAM roles used with Databricks model serving endpoints must have a trust relationship configured for serverless compute, or endpoints will fail with a specific error.
* [Serverless Compute Trust Relationship for Instance Profiles](/concepts/serverless-compute-trust-relationship-for-instance-profiles.md) - The requirement that an instance profile's IAM role must have a trust relationship configured for Databricks serverless compute; otherwise the endpoint creation fails with a specific error.
* [Serverless Compute with Databricks Connect](/concepts/serverless-compute-with-databricks-connect.md) - Support for connecting Databricks Connect to serverless compute resources, available starting from Databricks Connect version 15.1 with specific workspace requirements.
* [Serverless Environment Version Compatibility](/concepts/serverless-environment-version-compatibility.md) - JARs must match the serverless runtime's exact Scala, JDK, and Databricks Connect versions to avoid runtime failures
* [Serverless Environment Versioning](/concepts/serverless-environment-versioning.md) - Serverless compute uses versioned environments (e.g., version 4) that define exact Scala, JDK, and Databricks Connect versions your JAR must match to run without failures.
* [Serverless GPU @distributed Decorator](/concepts/serverless-gpu-distributed-decorator.md) - A Databricks serverless_gpu library decorator that launches PyTorch Lightning training across multiple GPUs on a single node
* [Serverless GPU API](/concepts/serverless-gpu-api.md) - Databricks API for scaling training across multiple GPUs and nodes without managing infrastructure.
* [Serverless GPU API for Distributed Training](/concepts/serverless-gpu-api-for-distributed-training.md) - A Databricks API for scaling machine learning training across multiple GPUs and nodes in a serverless manner, still in Beta.
* [Serverless GPU Cluster on Databricks](/concepts/serverless-gpu-cluster-on-databricks.md) - A Databricks compute configuration that provides on-demand GPU resources without managing clusters, suitable for running deep learning workloads like time series forecasting.
* [Serverless GPU Clusters for ML Workflows](/concepts/serverless-gpu-clusters-for-ml-workflows.md) - Databricks provides serverless GPU clusters for running machine learning workloads such as time-series forecasting with minimal infrastructure management.
* [Serverless GPU Compute](/concepts/serverless-gpu-compute.md) - On-demand, auto-scaling GPU compute in Databricks that powers AI Runtime workloads and auto-terminates after 60 minutes of inactivity.
* [Serverless GPU Compute (Databricks)](/concepts/serverless-gpu-compute-databricks.md) - Databricks managed compute that automatically provisions, scales, and orchestrates GPU resources (e.g., 8xH100) for distributed training without requiring users to manage infrastructure.
* [Serverless GPU Compute for Classic Machine Learning](/concepts/serverless-gpu-compute-for-classic-machine-learning.md) - Using serverless GPU clusters on Databricks to accelerate classic machine learning workloads such as time series forecasting with deep learning models.
* [Serverless GPU Compute on Databricks](/concepts/serverless-gpu-compute-on-databricks.md) - On-demand GPU compute resource that auto-terminates after 60 minutes of inactivity, configurable via accelerator selection like 8xH100
* [Serverless GPU Compute Specification](/concepts/serverless-gpu-compute-specification.md) - Configuration of GPU resources for AIR workloads, including accelerator type (e.g., GPU_1xA10), count of accelerators, and distributed multi-node support.
* [Serverless GPU Distributed Training API](/concepts/serverless_gpu-distributed-decorator.md) - A Databricks API using the @distributed decorator that handles provisioning GPUs, setting up the distributed environment, and managing the lifecycle of remote compute resources for multi-GPU training.
* [Serverless GPU Environment](/concepts/serverless-gpu-environment.md) - Managed compute environment for AI Runtime workloads specifying num_accelerators, accelerator_type, and environment version for running training jobs
* [Serverless GPU Environments in AI Runtime](/concepts/serverless-gpu-environments-in-ai-runtime.md) - Pre-configured serverless GPU compute environments (e.g., GPU_1xA10) with selectable environment versions that run training workloads without managing infrastructure.
* [Serverless GPU for Machine Learning](/concepts/serverless-gpu-for-machine-learning.md) - On-demand GPU compute resources on Databricks that enable training and fine-tuning of computer vision models without managing underlying infrastructure.
* [Serverless GPU Infrastructure](/concepts/serverless-gpu-infrastructure.md) - Fully managed, flexible access to GPUs on Databricks without cluster configuration, driver selection, or autoscaling policies.
* [Serverless GPU Python Library for Distributed Training](/concepts/serverless-gpu-python-library-for-distributed-training.md) - A serverless GPU Python library on Databricks that simplifies distributed training workflows across multiple GPUs.
* [Serverless GPU Training on Databricks](/concepts/serverless-gpu-training-on-databricks.md) - AI Runtime supports serverless GPU compute for single-node computer vision tasks (Public Preview), with a separate Beta API for distributed multi-GPU training.
* [Serverless JAR Jobs on Databricks](/concepts/serverless-jar-jobs-on-databricks.md) - Creating and running JAR tasks (Java/Scala Spark code) on serverless compute within Databricks Lakeflow Jobs.
* [Serverless Notebook Environment for Model Registration](/concepts/serverless-notebook-environment-for-model-registration.md) - The requirement that models must be logged and registered in Databricks Serverless Notebooks (client version 3 or 4) to use express deployments.
* [Serverless Notebook Environments](/concepts/serverless-notebook-environments.md) - Databricks compute environments (client versions 3 or 4) required for express model deployments, where model artifacts are packaged during registration.
* [Serverless Notebook Environments (Databricks)](/concepts/serverless-notebook-environments.md) - Databricks compute environments (client versions 3 or 4) used for model training and registration that enable express deployment features through environment consistency.
* [Serverless Usage Policies for Model Serving](/concepts/serverless-usage-policies-for-model-serving.md) - Custom tagging mechanism for attributing serverless usage costs to specific projects or teams via budget policies attached to endpoints.
* [Serverless vs Classic Compute for SecureConnect](/concepts/serverless-vs-classic-compute-for-secureconnect.md) - Serverless compute recipients do not need egress firewall configuration for SecureConnect, while classic compute and open recipients must allowlist inbound IPs
* [Serverless vs Classic Compute Forecasting](/concepts/serverless-vs-classic-compute-forecasting.md) - The distinction between Databricks' serverless forecasting (fully-managed compute) and classic compute forecasting (traditional compute-backed), with differences in setup, scaling, and management overhead.
* [Serverless Workload Types in MLflow](/concepts/serverless-workload-types-in-mlflow.md) - The three types of MLflow serverless workloads affected by budget policies: scheduled scorers, synthetic evaluation set generation, and agent evaluation.
* [serverless_gpu @distributed decorator](/concepts/serverless_gpu-distributed-decorator.md) - A Python decorator from Databricks' serverless_gpu library that handles provisioning GPUs, setting up the distributed training environment, and managing the lifecycle of remote compute resources for multi-GPU training.
* [serverless_gpu Distributed Training](/concepts/serverless_gpu-distributed-training.md) - A Databricks library that provides a @distributed decorator to run LLM training across multiple GPUs (e.g., 8xH100) with automatic rank management and device mapping.
* [serverless_gpu distributed training library](/concepts/serverless_gpu-distributed-training-library.md) - A Python library enabling distributed GPU training on Databricks Serverless GPU compute with decorators like @distributed to specify GPU count and type.
* [serverless_gpu Distributed Training on Databricks](/concepts/serverless_gpu-distributed-training.md) - A Python library providing a @distributed decorator pattern for scaling LLM finetuning across multiple GPUs (e.g., 8×H100) on Databricks serverless compute.
* [serverless_gpu Python Library](/concepts/serverless_gpu-python-library.md) - A first-party Databricks Python library that provides decorators and runtime utilities for executing distributed GPU workloads seamlessly from Databricks notebooks.
* [serverless_gpu.data.DataLoader](/concepts/serverless_gpudatadataloader.md) - A drop-in PyTorch DataLoader subclass tuned for serverless GPU I/O on Databricks, with higher default num_workers and prefetch_factor, plus MLflow timing logs.
* [serverless_gpu.runtime Module](/concepts/serverless_gpuruntime-module.md) - A runtime module within the serverless_gpu library that provides utilities like get_local_rank() and get_global_rank() for identifying GPU processes in a distributed setting.
* [Service Principal Authentication for Endpoint Testing](/concepts/service-principal-authentication-for-endpoint-testing.md) - Using Databricks service principals with OAuth tokens, secret scopes, and Can Query permissions to authenticate load tests against model serving endpoints.
* [Service Principal Authentication for Load Testing](/concepts/service-principal-authentication-for-load-testing.md) - Setting up a Databricks Service Principal with OAuth token generation and secret-scoped credentials to authenticate load test clients against route-optimized model serving endpoints.
* [Service Principal Authentication for ML Endpoint Load Tests](/concepts/service-principal-authentication-for-endpoint-testing.md) - Setting up a Databricks service principal with Can Query permissions and OAuth tokens stored in Databricks secrets to authenticate Locust load tests against model serving endpoints.
* [Service Principal Authentication for Model Serving Load Tests](/concepts/service-principal-authentication-for-endpoint-testing.md) - Setting up a Databricks service principal with Can Query permissions on the endpoint, storing its client ID and secret in a Databricks secret scope, and generating OAuth tokens for authenticating Locust load test traffic.
* [Service principal authentication for serving endpoints](/concepts/service-principal-authentication-for-serving-endpoints.md) - Using Databricks service principals to programmatically fetch OAuth tokens for production applications that query route-optimized serving endpoints.
* [Service Principal Authentication in Delta Sharing](/concepts/service-principal-authentication-in-delta-sharing.md) - Registering an OAuth application as a Service Principal (SP) in the recipient's IdP to authenticate M2M access to Delta Sharing shares without sharing long-lived secrets between parties.
* [Service Principal Authorization for Databricks App Resources](/concepts/service-principal-authorization-for-databricks-apps.md) - The authorization model where Databricks grants an app's associated service principal specific permissions on linked resources, scoping access to only those explicitly added resources.
* [Service Principal Authorization for Databricks Apps](/concepts/service-principal-authorization-for-databricks-apps.md) - The authorization model where Databricks Apps use a service principal, which is granted specific permissions to resources (like MLflow experiments) when those resources are added to the app.
* [Serving Endpoint ACLs](/concepts/serving-endpoint-acls.md) - Access control management for model serving endpoints, requiring CAN MANAGE permission to modify permissions on an endpoint.
* [Serving Endpoint Configuration for Custom LLMs](/concepts/serving-endpoint-configuration-for-custom-llms.md) - The configuration decisions for creating a custom LLM serving endpoint on Databricks, including workload_type (GPU selection), workload_size (Small/Medium/Large), and programmatic creation via Databricks SDK.
* [Serving Endpoint Deployment Container and Dependencies](/concepts/serving-endpoint-deployment-container-and-dependencies.md) - Production-grade container built during deployment that includes libraries captured in the MLflow model; application-level dependencies must be explicitly specified via pip_requirements, conda_env, or extra_pip_requirements.
* [Serving Endpoint Instance Profile for Feature Lookup](/concepts/serving-endpoint-instance-profile-for-feature-lookup.md) - Configuration of Databricks model serving endpoints with instance profiles to enable MLflow models to authenticate and read features from third-party online stores.
* [Serving Endpoint OpenAPI Schema](/concepts/serving-endpoint-openapi-schema.md) - A formal OpenAPI specification (JSON) that describes a serving endpoint's query interface, request/response format, and data types for reproducibility and client code generation.
* [Serving Endpoint REST API (Databricks)](/concepts/serving-endpoint-rest-api-databricks.md) - The Databricks REST API (endpoints /api/2.0/serving-endpoints) for creating and updating model serving endpoint configurations, including fields like instance_profile_arn.
* [Serving Endpoint REST API Instance Profile Configuration](/concepts/serving-endpoint-rest-api-instance-profile-configuration.md) - The instance_profile_arn field in the Databricks Serving Endpoint REST API (POST/PUT /api/2.0/serving-endpoints) used to attach an AWS instance profile during endpoint creation or update.
* [Serving Endpoint Update Semantics](/concepts/serving-endpoint-update-semantics.md) - How configuration updates to custom model endpoints work: old config keeps serving until new one is ready, concurrent updates are blocked, and updates can be cancelled from the UI.
* [Serving Endpoints (Databricks)](/concepts/serving-endpoints-databricks.md) - REST API endpoints for Databricks Model Serving that use a 'serving-endpoints' URL path, with full API support for creation, configuration, and lifecycle management.
* [Serving UI](/concepts/serving-ui.md) - The Databricks workspace interface for creating, viewing, and managing model serving endpoints, including canceling in-progress configuration updates.
* [Serving UI Advanced Configuration for Instance Profiles](/concepts/serving-ui-advanced-configuration-for-instance-profiles.md) - Instance profiles can be added to model serving endpoints through the Databricks Serving UI under Advanced configurations, with the endpoint creator's permission validated at creation time.
* [Serving UI token fetch](/concepts/serving-ui-token-fetch.md) - Fetching a short-lived OAuth token (valid for 1 hour) directly from the Databricks Serving UI for development and testing of route-optimized endpoints.
* [Session and User Association in MLflow Traces](/concepts/session-and-user-association-in-mlflow-traces.md) - Associating traces with sessions and users via the session.id and user.id OpenTelemetry span attributes for trace-level metadata and the session tab in MLflow UI.
* [Session ID Tracing](/concepts/session-id-tracing.md) - Tagging MLflow traces with session IDs via mlflow.update_current_trace to group individual turns into coherent conversations.
* [Session ID Tracking](/concepts/session-id-tracking.md) - The mechanism of tagging MLflow traces with session IDs to group individual turns into coherent conversation sessions for multi-turn evaluation.
* [Session temporary target restriction for clone with history](/concepts/session-temporary-target-restriction-for-clone-with-history.md) - Cloning a Delta table with history to a session temporary target table is not supported in Databricks.
* [Session temporary target tables in Delta Clone with History](/concepts/session-temporary-target-tables-in-delta-clone-with-history.md) - An error sub-condition (SESSION_TEMPORARY) where the target table for a Delta Clone with History operation is a session-scoped temporary table
* [Session Tracking with Trace Tags](/concepts/session-tracking-with-trace-tags.md) - Mechanism for instrumenting agents with session IDs on traces (mlflow.trace.session tag) to group individual turns into conversation sessions for evaluation.
* [Session User Identity Model for ABAC on Views](/concepts/session-user-identity-model-for-abac-on-views.md) - ABAC policies on underlying tables are evaluated using the session user's identity when querying through views and functions
* [SESSION_TEMPORARY target error](/concepts/session_temporary-target-error.md) - A subtype of DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_TARGET indicating the target table is a session temporary table (not supported for clone with history).
* [SESSION_TEMPORARY target table restriction](/concepts/session_temporary-target-table-restriction.md) - Delta clone with history does not support session-scoped temporary target tables.
* [Setting Initial Streaming Starting Point with Version or Timestamp](/concepts/setting-initial-streaming-starting-point-with-version-or-timestamp.md) - Using startingVersion or startingTimestamp options to begin a Delta Lake streaming read from a specific table version or point in time, skipping the full table snapshot.
* [Setting the active experiment in MLflow](/concepts/active-experiment-in-mlflow.md) - How to control where MLflow runs are logged by setting the active experiment using mlflow.set_experiment(), mlflow.start_run(), or environment variables.
* [SFTConfig and SFTTrainer](/concepts/sftconfig-and-sfttrainer.md) - Components from the TRL library that simplify supervised fine-tuning configuration and training, handling dataset loading, model initialization, checkpointing, and metric logging.
* [SFTTrainer](/concepts/sfttrainer.md) - A TRL trainer class for supervised fine-tuning that handles dataset formatting, tokenization, and training loop with optional DeepSpeed optimization
* [SFTTrainer (Supervised Fine-Tuning Trainer)](/concepts/sfttrainer-supervised-fine-tuning-trainer.md) - A TRL library component that simplifies supervised fine-tuning configuration by automatically applying optimizations like Liger kernels, mixed precision, gradient checkpointing, and chat format setup.
* [SFTTrainer (TRL)](/concepts/sfttrainer-trl.md) - A supervised fine-tuning trainer from the Transformer Reinforcement Learning (TRL) library that streamlines instruction tuning of language models with built-in support for LoRA, gradient checkpointing, and mixed precision.
* [SFTTrainer and TRL Library](/concepts/sfttrainer-and-trl-library.md) - Supervised Fine-Tuning Trainer from the Transformer Reinforcement Learning (TRL) library used to fine-tune causal language models with support for LoRA, gradient checkpointing, and mixed precision
* [SFTTrainer with Chat Template Processing](/concepts/sfttrainer-with-chat-template-processing.md) - The use of TRL's SFTTrainer with Unsloth's chat template utilities (get_chat_template, standardize_sharegpt, train_on_responses_only) for supervised finetuning on conversational datasets like mlabonne/FineTome-100k.
* [SFTTrainer with train_on_responses_only](/concepts/sfttrainer-with-train_on_responses_only.md) - A training approach using TRL's SFTTrainer combined with Unsloth's train_on_responses_only utility to mask instruction parts and train only on assistant responses.
* [SGC recommender system with PyTorch Lightning](/concepts/sgc-recommender-system-with-pytorch-lightning.md) - A tutorial pattern using Simplified Graph Convolution (SGC) for recommendations, implemented with PyTorch Lightning for distributed training on Databricks
* [Shallow Clone](/concepts/shallow-clone.md) - A cloning strategy that copies only the source table's metadata and references the source data files without copying the data.
* [Shallow Clone (Databricks)](/concepts/shallow-clone.md) - A metadata-only copy of a source Delta, Iceberg, or Parquet table that references the source data files without copying them, creating a lightweight pointer.
* [Shallow Clone (Delta Lake)](/concepts/shallow-clone.md) - A metadata-only copy of a source Delta, Parquet, or Foreign Iceberg table that references the source's data files without copying them.
* [Shallow Clone (Delta Table)](/concepts/shallow-clone.md) - A copy of a source table's definition that references the original data files without copying them, providing a lightweight metadata-only clone.
* [Share Deletion Implications](/concepts/share-deletion-implications.md) - Deleting an OpenSharing share immediately revokes recipient access to all shared data; only the share owner can perform deletion.
* [Share Details and Metadata](/concepts/share-details-and-metadata.md) - Information associated with a share including owner, creator, creation/update timestamps, comments, contained data assets, and associated recipients.
* [Share Owner Asset Permission Requirements](/concepts/share-owner-asset-permission-requirements.md) - The requirement that share owners must have sufficient permissions on all assets in the share for recipients to access them, and object creators must grant access to new assets.
* [Share Owner Exemption from ABAC Policies](/concepts/share-owner-exemption-from-abac-policies.md) - The share owner must be listed in the EXCEPT clause of ABAC policies on the provider side to share tables and views protected by row filters and column masks through OpenSharing.
* [Share Owner Permissions for Shared Assets](/concepts/share-owner-permissions-for-shared-assets.md) - A 'does not exist' error on a shared asset indicates the share owner lacks sufficient permissions on the asset; requires verifying permissions on all shared objects.
* [Share Ownership and ABAC Security](/concepts/share-ownership-and-abac-security.md) - The security implication that transferring share ownership to an over-privileged user may allow recipients over-privileged access when ABAC policies are in effect on underlying tables or schemas.
* [Share Update Operations](/concepts/share-update-operations.md) - Operations that can be performed on an existing OpenSharing share including renaming, removing assets, adding comments, renaming table aliases, enabling history access, managing partitions, and changing ownership.
* [Share Viewing Interfaces](/concepts/share-viewing-interfaces.md) - Three interfaces for viewing OpenSharing shares and their details: Catalog Explorer (UI), Databricks Unity Catalog CLI, and SQL commands in notebooks or SQL query editor.
* [Share-to-Catalog Mounting](/concepts/share-to-catalog-mounting.md) - The process of creating a new catalog from a share or mounting a share to an existing OpenSharing catalog, which makes shared data assets accessible in a recipient's Unity Catalog workspace.
* [Shareable Asset Types in OpenSharing](/concepts/shareable-asset-types-in-opensharing.md) - The broad range of data and AI assets that can be included in an OpenSharing share, including Delta tables, streaming tables, Iceberg tables, views, volumes, notebooks, Python UDFs, AI models, FeatureSpecs, and Genie Spaces.
* [Shared Cluster Compatibility in Unity Catalog](/concepts/shared-cluster-compatibility-in-unity-catalog.md) - Certain Spark APIs like spark.catalog, sc.parallelize, sc.emptyRDD, and input_file_name() are unsupported or behave differently on shared clusters when using Unity Catalog.
* [Shared Data Permission Model](/concepts/shared-data-permission-model.md) - A hierarchical privilege model for OpenSharing catalogs where catalog owners can grant read-only privileges (SELECT, READ VOLUME, USE CATALOG) while write/update privileges cannot be granted on shared assets.
* [Shared External Table Conversion Caveat](/concepts/shared-external-table-conversion-caveat.md) - The issue that multiple external tables sharing the same underlying Parquet directory require all such tables to be converted to Delta Lake, or they become inaccessible after the first conversion.
* [Shared Infrastructure Requirements for Cross-workspace Feature Store](/concepts/shared-infrastructure-requirements-for-cross-workspace-feature-store.md) - Prerequisite that both local and remote workspaces must share the same external Hive metastore and have access to the same DBFS storage for cross-workspace feature store operations.
* [Shared Mode Execution (Ray + Spark)](/concepts/shared-mode-execution-ray-spark.md) - Running both Ray and Spark in the same execution environment on Databricks allows teams to use Spark for data-intensive tasks and Ray for heavy computation stages within a single application.
* [Shared Platform Features for ML, Deep Learning, and GenAI](/concepts/shared-platform-features-for-ml-deep-learning-and-genai.md) - Databricks platform features — Model Serving, ai_query, AI Runtime, MLflow experiment tracking, and AI Search — that support classic ML, deep learning, and generative AI under a unified infrastructure.
* [Shared Storage for Model Checkpointing](/concepts/shared-storage-for-model-checkpointing.md) - Using shared storage for data loading and model checkpointing in distributed deep learning training on Databricks.
* [Shared View Function Allowlist](/concepts/shared-view-function-allowlist.md) - The security mechanism that restricts Databricks-to-Databricks shared views to only a specific subset of built-in SQL functions and operators, preventing potentially dangerous operations on shared data.
* [Shared View Materialization Failure (DS_MATERIALIZATION_QUERY_FAILED)](/concepts/shared-view-materialization-failure-ds_materialization_query_failed.md) - Error when sharing views, materialized views, or streaming tables because the provider lacks read-write access to the asset or the materialization workspace is misconfigured.
* [Shared Views in Databricks-to-Databricks Sharing](/concepts/shared-views-in-databricks-to-databricks-sharing.md) - Shared views have specific restrictions including limited built-in function support, a 20-view query limit across at most 5 provider-shares, and a 5-minute query timeout for on-the-fly materialization without direct access.
* [ShareGPT chat template standardization](/concepts/sharegpt-chat-template-standardization.md) - A data preprocessing step in Unsloth that converts conversational datasets into standardized chat templates (e.g., Llama 3.1 format) before training.
* [ShareGPT Dataset Format Standardization](/concepts/sharegpt-dataset-format-standardization.md) - A preprocessing step that normalizes conversational datasets (e.g., FineTome-100k) into a standardized multi-turn conversation format with 'from' and 'value' fields, making them compatible with chat-template-based tokenization.
* [Sharing Identifier](/concepts/sharing-identifier.md) - A unique string identifying a Unity Catalog metastore, formatted as 'cloud:region:uuid', used by data providers to set up Databricks-to-Databricks sharing.
* [Sharing Tables with Deletion Vectors and Column Mapping](/concepts/sharing-tables-with-deletion-vectors-and-column-mapping.md) - Sharing Delta tables that have storage optimizations like deletion vectors or column mapping enabled, requiring history sharing and specific recipient runtime versions.
* [Short-lived JWT Token Authentication](/concepts/short-lived-jwt-token-authentication.md) - Security model where JWTs are issued and lifetime-enforced by the recipient's IdP, not by Databricks, eliminating the need to manage shared credentials or long-lived bearer tokens.
* [Short-lived JWT Tokens for Delta Sharing](/concepts/short-lived-jwt-tokens-for-delta-sharing.md) - JWT tokens issued by the recipient's IdP with lifetimes enforced by that IdP, validated by Databricks against the federation policy, eliminating the need for recipients to manage long-lived shared credentials.
* [SHOW ARCHIVED FILES syntax](/concepts/show-archived-files-syntax.md) - A SQL command that returns URIs of archived files required to satisfy a given query, enabling targeted restoration.
* [SHOW EFFECTIVE POLICIES](/concepts/show-effective-policies.md) - A SQL command that lists all policies affecting a securable object, including inherited policies from parent scopes like catalog-level policies affecting a table.
* [Simple vs Complex Deployment Job Patterns](/concepts/simple-vs-complex-deployment-job-patterns.md) - Two common deployment job patterns: a simple 3-step pipeline (evaluation, approval, deployment) and a complex staged rollout with gradual deployment percentages, metrics collection, and rollback logic.
* [Single Node Clusters for PyTorch](/concepts/single-node-clusters-for-pytorch.md) - Using single-node Databricks clusters to test and migrate single-machine PyTorch workflows
* [Single Node GPU Cluster for Deep Learning Development](/concepts/single-node-gpu-clusters-for-deep-learning.md) - Best practice of using a single-node GPU cluster (driver only) for fast, cost-effective iterative deep learning model development before scaling to distributed training.
* [Single Node GPU Clusters for Deep Learning](/concepts/single-node-gpu-clusters-for-deep-learning.md) - Best practice recommendation to use Single Node (driver-only) GPU clusters for fast, cost-effective deep learning model development, as a single node with multiple GPUs avoids network communication overhead of distributed training.
* [Single Node GPU Clusters for Deep Learning Development](/concepts/single-node-gpu-clusters-for-deep-learning-development.md) - Using a single driver-only GPU cluster for fast, cost-effective iterative deep learning model development before scaling to distributed training
* [Single Node to Distributed Code Adaptation](/concepts/single-node-to-distributed-code-adaptation.md) - The process of modifying single-node PyTorch training code to work in a distributed environment using HorovodRunner, following Databricks' recommended development workflow
* [Single Node to Distributed Training Adaptation Pattern](/concepts/single-node-to-distributed-training-adaptation-pattern.md) - Recommended development workflow of first training a model on a single node, then adapting the code for distributed training using HorovodRunner
* [Single Run Details Dashboard](/concepts/single-run-details-dashboard.md) - A pre-built example dashboard that replicates the MLflow run details page using system table queries, filterable by experiment ID, run ID, and metric name
* [Single-Brace Format Compatibility](/concepts/single-brace-format-compatibility.md) - MLflow's conversion mechanism between its native double-brace template syntax and the single-brace Python f-string syntax used by LangChain, LlamaIndex, and other GenAI frameworks.
* [Single-GPU Fine-tuning](/concepts/single-gpu-fine-tuning.md) - Techniques and configurations for fine-tuning Hugging Face transformer models on a single GPU, including batch size reduction, FP16 training, and gradient accumulation.
* [Single-GPU Fine-Tuning with Hugging Face on Databricks](/concepts/single-gpu-fine-tuning-with-hugging-face-on-databricks.md) - The end-to-end workflow for fine-tuning Hugging Face transformer models on a single GPU node in Databricks, covering cluster setup, tokenization, training configuration, MLflow logging, and inference loading.
* [Single-Machine Preference for Neural Networks](/concepts/single-machine-preference-for-neural-networks.md) - Databricks recommendation to train neural networks on a single machine when possible, because distributed code is more complex and slower due to communication overhead
* [Single-Machine Training Preference on Databricks](/concepts/single-machine-training-preference-on-databricks.md) - Databricks recommends training neural networks on a single machine when possible, as distributed training adds complexity and communication overhead
* [Single-node AI Runtime](/concepts/single-node-ai-runtime.md) - Databricks AI Runtime configured for single-node ML tasks, currently in Public Preview
* [Single-node GPU Training on Databricks](/concepts/single-node-gpu-training-on-databricks.md) - AI Runtime supports single-node GPU tasks for model training and fine-tuning, currently in Public Preview.
* [Single-Node Multi-GPU Fine-tuning Pipeline](/concepts/single-node-multi-gpu-fine-tuning-pipeline.md) - A complete reference pattern for fine-tuning large language models (like Qwen2.5-3B) using Ray Train on a single node with 8 H100 GPUs, covering Ray cluster setup, DDP training, and metric logging.
* [Single-Node Ray Cluster Bootstrap](/concepts/single-node-ray-cluster-bootstrap.md) - A pattern where the workload command starts a Ray head on the local node with all GPUs, runs the training driver, then stops the cluster — with a fallback branch for multi-node scaling.
* [Single-Node vs Multi-GPU Workload Tiers](/concepts/single-node-vs-multi-gpu-workload-tiers.md) - Databricks distinguishes between single-node tasks (Public Preview) and multi-GPU distributed training (Beta), each with different stability and availability guarantees.
* [Single-node-to-distributed development workflow](/concepts/single-node-to-distributed-development-workflow.md) - A recommended development pattern for distributed training: first train and validate on a single node, then adapt the code for distributed execution using HorovodRunner.
* [Single-User vs Global Ray Clusters](/concepts/single-user-vs-global-ray-clusters.md) - Two modes for Ray clusters on Databricks: user-scoped clusters with auto-shutdown and shared global clusters without timeout
* [skipChangeCommits](/concepts/skipchangecommits.md) - Option to ignore data modification transactions (UPDATE, DELETE, MERGE, OVERWRITE) in a Delta Lake streaming source, processing only appends.
* [skipChangeCommits for handling source table modifications](/concepts/skipchangecommits.md) - Option to skip transactions that modify/delete records in a Delta source table during streaming, processing only appends; replaces legacy options ignoreChanges and ignoreDeletes.
* [skipChangeCommits for Source Table Changes](/concepts/skipchangecommits.md) - Option to ignore transactions that delete or modify existing records in a Delta source table, processing only appends during streaming reads
* [skipChangeCommits for Streaming Sources](/concepts/skipchangecommits.md) - An option to ignore transactions that delete or modify existing records when streaming from a Delta Lake source
* [Slicing Expressions in Data Profiling](/concepts/slicing-expressions-in-data-profiling.md) - Mechanism to compute metrics for data subsets defined by boolean expressions or column values, identified by slice_key and slice_value columns.
* [Small-world Networks](/concepts/small-world-networks.md) - A real-world network pattern characterized by closely linked subgroups and a short average path length between any two nodes; commonly observed in social and other real-world networks and can lead to data skew in distributed graph processing.
* [Smolagents](/concepts/smolagents.md) - A lightweight agent framework by HuggingFace emphasizing minimalism and composability
* [Snapshot Profile](/concepts/snapshot-profile.md) - A data profile type that profiles the full contents of a table over time, with a 4TB maximum table size limit
* [Snowflake Catalog Integration for Iceberg](/concepts/snowflake-catalog-integration-for-iceberg.md) - Snowflake SQL configuration to create a CATALOG INTEGRATION and linked database using the ICEBERG_REST catalog source with OAuth authentication and vended credentials access delegation.
* [Source Table Types for CLONE](/concepts/source-table-types-for-clone.md) - CLONE supports Delta, Parquet, Foreign Iceberg, and Managed Iceberg tables as source tables, each with different cloning capabilities.
* [Source Transformation and Filtering](/concepts/source-transformation-and-filtering.md) - Pre-processing source data before aggregation using filter_condition (SQL WHERE clause) and transformation_sql (SQL SELECT expression) with schema_json requirement, plus a convenience from_sql() factory method.
* [Sources of Human Feedback in MLflow](/concepts/sources-of-human-feedback-in-mlflow.md) - MLflow collects human feedback from three distinct sources — developers (during development via direct trace annotation), domain experts (via Review App with Chat UI or labeling existing traces), and end users (in production from live applications).
* [Span Attributes and Search](/concepts/span-attributes-and-search.md) - Spans carry typed attributes accessible via get_attribute() and SpanAttributeKey constants, and can be searched by name (exact or regex), span type, span ID, or combination of criteria using trace.search_spans().
* [Span customization parameters](/concepts/span-customization-parameters.md) - The @mlflow.trace decorator accepts name, span_type, and attributes parameters to customize span metadata, with optional dynamic updates via mlflow.get_current_active_span().
* [Span Customization via @mlflow.trace](/concepts/span-customization-via-mlflowtrace.md) - Customizing created spans through decorator parameters (name, span_type, attributes) and dynamically via mlflow.get_current_active_span().
* [Span Customization via Decorator Parameters](/concepts/span-customization-parameters.md) - The @mlflow.trace decorator accepts name, span_type, and attributes parameters to customize spans, and allows dynamic attribute updates via mlflow.get_current_active_span().
* [Span Hierarchy and Analysis](/concepts/span-hierarchy-and-analysis.md) - Spans form a parent-child hierarchy within a trace, and can be analyzed for relationships, critical paths, timing statistics, and categorized by span type (LLM, CHAT_MODEL, RETRIEVER, TOOL, etc.).
* [Span Hierarchy and Critical Path Analysis](/concepts/span-hierarchy-and-critical-path-analysis.md) - Technique for analyzing trace span trees including parent-child relationships, identifying root spans, computing duration statistics, and finding the critical path (longest duration path from root to leaf).
* [Span Search and Analysis](/concepts/span-search-and-analysis.md) - API for searching spans by name (exact or regex), span type, span ID, and combined criteria; supports building span hierarchy trees, computing timing statistics by type, and identifying critical paths.
* [Span Types and Lifecycle](/concepts/span-types-and-lifecycle.md) - Spans capture input arguments, return values, exception info, and latency; they can be typed (LLM, TOOL, etc.) and organized hierarchically to represent nested function calls.
* [Span-level Labeling in MLflow](/concepts/span-level-labeling-in-mlflow.md) - The ability to attach assessments to individual spans within a trace, with the root span representing the entire trace, enabling granular feedback on specific components.
* [SpanEvent Objects](/concepts/spanevent-objects.md) - MLflow SpanEvent objects that record specific occurrences during a span's lifetime, supporting timestamped events, custom attributes, and exception-derived events.
* [SpanStatus API](/concepts/spanstatus-api.md) - MLflow SpanStatus objects for defining span completion status (OK/ERROR), with caveats about context manager overwriting status upon exit.
* [Spark Barrier Execution Mode](/concepts/spark-barrier-execution-mode.md) - Spark execution mode that enables synchronous, long-running distributed training by providing task executors with topology awareness via BarrierTaskContext.
* [Spark Barrier Execution Mode Integration](/concepts/spark-barrier-execution-mode-integration.md) - HorovodRunner integrates Horovod with Spark's barrier execution mode, using BarrierTaskContext to collect executor IPs and trigger MPI jobs via mpirun for stable long-running deep learning training.
* [Spark Caching Anti-pattern with Delta Lake](/concepts/spark-caching-anti-pattern-with-delta-lake.md) - Databricks recommends against using Spark caching with Delta Lake because it loses data skipping benefits and can return stale data if the table is accessed via a different identifier
* [Spark Caching Incompatibility with Delta Lake](/concepts/spark-caching-anti-pattern-with-delta-lake.md) - Databricks recommends against using Spark caching with Delta Lake because it loses data skipping benefits and can become stale if the table is accessed via different identifiers.
* [Spark Cluster Integration for ML Training](/concepts/spark-cluster-integration-for-ml-training.md) - The pattern of leveraging Apache Spark clusters to orchestrate distributed machine learning training jobs with TensorFlow.
* [Spark Connect](/concepts/spark-connect.md) - An open-source gRPC-based protocol within Apache Spark that enables remote connectivity to Spark clusters using the DataFrame API with a decoupled client-server architecture.
* [Spark Connect API Limitations on Serverless](/concepts/spark-connect-api-limitations-on-serverless.md) - Serverless compute uses Spark Connect, which prohibits RDD APIs, Spark internal APIs, and native libraries; code must use only public Spark APIs (DataFrame/Dataset/SQL).
* [Spark Connect Architecture for JAR Execution](/concepts/spark-connect-architecture-for-jar-execution.md) - Serverless compute uses Spark Connect; JARs run against a thin client library exposing public Spark APIs while the Spark engine runs server-side; code bypassing public API cannot use Catalyst or Photon optimization.
* [Spark Connect Compatibility for JARs](/concepts/spark-connect-compatibility-for-jars.md) - When running JARs on serverless compute, the JAR must use only public Spark APIs (no RDDs, no Catalyst/Spark internal APIs, no native libraries) because serverless uses Spark Connect under the hood.
* [Spark Connect Connection String](/concepts/spark-connect-connection-string.md) - Advanced method to configure Databricks Connect by passing a Spark Connect connection string via the `remote` function or the `SPARK_REMOTE` environment variable.
* [Spark Connect Integration with Ray Tasks](/concepts/spark-connect-integration-with-ray-tasks.md) - Using DatabricksSession and Spark Connect within Ray remote tasks to directly interact with Spark DataFrames and tables from Ray workers
* [Spark Connect Limitations for JAR Tasks](/concepts/spark-connect-limitations-for-jar-tasks.md) - Serverless compute uses Spark Connect; RDD APIs, Spark internal APIs, and native libraries (.so, .dll, JNI) are unavailable. Code must use DataFrame/Dataset APIs via SparkSession.
* [Spark Connect Protocol](/concepts/spark-connect-protocol.md) - The communication protocol used by Databricks Connect to connect a local PySpark shell to a remote Spark server on a Databricks cluster, as seen in the connection URI scheme.
* [Spark Connect with Ray](/concepts/spark-connect-with-ray.md) - Using Spark Connect from within Ray remote tasks to allow Ray workers to interact directly with a Spark cluster running on the driver node.
* [Spark Connect with Ray Tasks](/concepts/spark-connect-with-ray.md) - Using Databricks Connect (Spark Connect) to allow Ray Core tasks to directly interact with a Spark cluster from within remote Ray tasks, with considerations for threading locks and resource allocation.
* [Spark Connect with Ray Tasks on Databricks](/concepts/spark-connect-with-ray.md) - Use DatabricksSession and Spark Connect to enable Ray Core tasks to directly interact with a Spark cluster from worker nodes, though tasks become sequential due to a threading lock on the single Spark driver.
* [Spark Context and RDD Limitations](/concepts/spark-context-and-rdd-limitations.md) - Spark Context and RDDs are not available when using Databricks Connect for Scala; users must use higher-level DataFrame APIs.
* [Spark DataFrame Evaluation Pattern](/concepts/spark-dataframe-evaluation-pattern.md) - Using Spark DataFrames for large-scale GenAI evaluation when data resides in Delta Lake or Unity Catalog.
* [Spark DataFrame to Hugging Face Dataset Conversion](/concepts/spark-dataframe-to-hugging-face-dataset-conversion.md) - Converting Apache Spark DataFrames to Hugging Face Dataset objects using the Dataset.from_spark method for model training
* [Spark DataFrame to MDS Conversion Workflow](/concepts/spark-dataframe-to-mds-conversion-workflow.md) - A recommended four-step workflow: load/preprocess data with Spark, convert to MDS format via dataframe_to_mds, load with StreamingDataset, and serve with StreamingDataLoader.
* [Spark DataFrame to ML Format Conversion](/concepts/spark-dataframe-to-ml-format-conversion.md) - Converting Apache Spark DataFrames into pandas DataFrames using toPandas() and optionally to NumPy format using to_numpy() for ML pipelines.
* [Spark Dataset Converter API](/concepts/spark-dataset-converter-api.md) - The Petastorm API method that materializes a Spark DataFrame in Parquet format and loads it as a tf.data.Dataset or torch.utils.data.DataLoader for deep learning frameworks.
* [Spark ML Distributed Training with pyspark.ml.connect](/concepts/spark-ml-distributed-training-with-pysparkmlconnect.md) - A module in Databricks Runtime 17.0+ for distributed training and inference of Spark ML models, enabled by default in Standard compute resources.
* [Spark ML Text Processing](/concepts/spark-ml-text-processing.md) - Using Spark ML's built-in text processing tools (tokenization, stop-word removal, word2vec, feature hashing) to create features from text columns within ML pipelines.
* [Spark MLlib Compatibility Limitations with Databricks Connect](/concepts/spark-mllib-limitations-with-databricks-connect.md) - Databricks Connect has limited compatibility with Apache Spark MLlib because MLlib uses RDDs while Databricks Connect only supports the DataFrame API, restricting certain sparklyr ML functions.
* [Spark MLlib limitations with Databricks Connect](/concepts/spark-mllib-limitations-with-databricks-connect.md) - Databricks Connect has limited compatibility with Apache Spark MLlib because MLlib uses RDDs while Databricks Connect only supports the DataFrame API; users needing full MLlib functions should use Databricks notebooks or the brickster package's db_repl function.
* [Spark NLP Library](/concepts/spark-nlp-library.md) - An open-source library for scaling deep learning NLP methods on Apache Spark, providing annotators and pre-trained transformers for tasks like tokenization, named entity recognition, summarization, and translation.
* [Spark NLP Transformers](/concepts/spark-nlp-transformers.md) - Pre-trained deep learning models available in Spark NLP (including BERT, T5, and Marian) for tasks such as text generation, summarization, named entity recognition, and machine translation.
* [Spark Shell and SBT with Databricks Connect](/concepts/spark-shell-and-sbt-with-databricks-connect.md) - Instructions for using Databricks Connect with the Spark shell (pyspark and spark-shell) and configuring SBT build files to link against Databricks Connect JARs.
* [spark_connect Function for Databricks Connect](/concepts/spark_connect-function-for-databricks-connect.md) - The sparklyr::spark_connect function with method='databricks_connect' parameter, which establishes a connection from an R environment to a remote Databricks cluster for distributed data operations.
* [SPARK_REMOTE Environment Variable](/concepts/spark_remote-environment-variable.md) - Environment variable used to point Databricks Connect or Spark Connect clients to a remote Spark server instance
* [SPARK_REMOTE environment variable authentication](/concepts/spark_remote-environment-variable-authentication.md) - A configuration method for Databricks Connect where the SPARK_REMOTE environment variable provides connection and authentication details without explicit code configuration.
* [SPARK_REMOTE Environment Variable Incompatibility](/concepts/spark_remote-environment-variable-limitation.md) - The Databricks SDK for Python does not recognize the SPARK_REMOTE environment variable used by Databricks Connect; authentication must use DATABRICKS_HOST and DATABRICKS_TOKEN instead.
* [SPARK_REMOTE Environment Variable Limitation](/concepts/spark_remote-environment-variable-limitation.md) - The Databricks SDK for Python does not recognize the SPARK_REMOTE environment variable for authentication, unlike the Databricks Connect client itself
* [Spark-TensorFlow Integration](/concepts/spark-tensorflow-integration.md) - The pattern of combining Apache Spark's distributed data processing with TensorFlow's deep learning capabilities for scalable ML workflows
* [spark-tensorflow-connector](/concepts/spark-tensorflow-connector.md) - A TensorFlow ecosystem library that enables conversion between Apache Spark DataFrames and TFRecord files.
* [spark-tensorflow-distributor](/concepts/spark-tensorflow-distributor.md) - An open-source native TensorFlow package for distributed training on Spark clusters
* [sparkdl.xgboost Deprecation and Migration to xgboost.spark](/concepts/sparkdlxgboost-deprecation-and-migration-to-xgboostspark.md) - The sparkdl.xgboost module is deprecated since Databricks Runtime 12.0 ML; users should migrate to the xgboost.spark module.
* [sparkdl.xgboost Module](/concepts/sparkdlxgboost-module.md) - A deprecated PySpark module providing XgboostRegressor and XgboostClassifier estimators for distributed XGBoost training within Spark ML pipelines, superseded by xgboost.spark in Databricks Runtime 12.0 ML.
* [sparkdl.xgboost Parameter Differences from Standard xgboost](/concepts/sparkdlxgboost-parameter-differences-from-standard-xgboost.md) - Key parameter discrepancies between sparkdl.xgboost and the standard xgboost package, including missing, baseMarginCol, weightCol, and validationIndicatorCol.
* [sparkdl.xgboost Parameter Differences from xgboost Package](/concepts/sparkdlxgboost-parameter-differences-from-xgboost-package.md) - Important semantic differences in parameters between sparkdl.xgboost and the standard xgboost package, including handling of missing values in sparse vectors and unsupported parameters like gpu_id, output_margin, and validate_features
* [sparkdl.xgboost PySpark Estimators](/concepts/sparkdlxgboost-pyspark-estimators.md) - PySpark ML pipeline estimators (XgboostRegressor and XgboostClassifier) from the sparkdl.xgboost module for distributed XGBoost training on Databricks
* [sparklyr](/concepts/sparklyr.md) - R interface to Apache Spark that serves as the primary integration point for Databricks Connect from R, enabling dplyr and dbplyr workflows against Databricks clusters.
* [sparklyr Databricks Connect Integration](/concepts/sparklyr-databricks-connect-integration.md) - The integration of sparklyr with Databricks Connect for Databricks Runtime 13.0+, enabling R users to run Spark DataFrame operations on remote Databricks clusters.
* [sparklyr Databricks Integration](/concepts/sparklyr-databricks-integration.md) - The sparklyr R package's method = 'databricks_connect' integration that provides an R interface to Databricks Connect for remote cluster operations.
* [SparkSession as DatabricksSession Fallback](/concepts/sparksession-as-databrickssession-fallback.md) - A pattern for using org.apache.spark.sql.SparkSession directly (instead of DatabricksSession) when the DatabricksSession class is unavailable, relying on SPARK_REMOTE for authentication.
* [SparkSession with SPARK_REMOTE](/concepts/sparksession-with-spark_remote.md) - Alternative pattern to connect to Databricks clusters using the standard SparkSession builder when DatabricksSession is unavailable, relying on the SPARK_REMOTE environment variable
* [SparkSession with SPARK_REMOTE authentication](/concepts/sparksession-with-spark_remote.md) - An alternative approach to connecting to Databricks clusters using the standard SparkSession class together with the SPARK_REMOTE environment variable for authentication, useful when DatabricksSession is unavailable.
* [SparkSession-based Databricks Connect](/concepts/sparksession-based-databricks-connect.md) - A fallback pattern for using Databricks Connect with the standard SparkSession class when DatabricksSession is unavailable, relying on SPARK_REMOTE for authentication.
* [SparkTrials](/concepts/sparktrials.md) - A Databricks API that distributes Hyperopt hyperparameter tuning trials across Spark workers for parallel execution.
* [SparkTrials for Distributed Hyperopt](/concepts/sparktrials-for-distributed-hyperopt.md) - A Trials subclass for Hyperopt that distributes hyperparameter tuning trials across a Spark cluster, accepting parallelism and timeout parameters for concurrent model fitting.
* [SparkTrials Incompatibility with Autoscaling Clusters](/concepts/sparktrials-incompatibility-with-autoscaling-clusters.md) - SparkTrials should not be used on autoscaling clusters because Hyperopt sets the parallelism value at execution start and cannot adapt if the cluster later rescales.
* [SparkTrials parallelism configuration](/concepts/sparktrials-parallelism-configuration.md) - SparkTrials accepts a parallelism parameter (default: number of Spark executors, max: 128) and a timeout parameter to control distributed tuning execution.
* [SparkTrials Parallelism for CPU vs GPU Clusters](/concepts/sparktrials-parallelism-for-cpu-vs-gpu-clusters.md) - SparkTrials parallelism configuration differs between CPU clusters (multiple executor threads per node) and GPU clusters (one executor thread per node to avoid GPU conflicts).
* [SparkTrials vs Trials in Hyperopt](/concepts/sparktrials-vs-trials-in-hyperopt.md) - Key distinction: SparkTrials distributes individual trials across workers for non-distributed algorithms, while Trials runs all trials on the driver for use with distributed training algorithms.
* [Sparse Data Optimization for XGBoost on Spark](/concepts/sparse-data-optimization-for-xgboost-on-spark.md) - Optimization for training on sparse feature datasets using enable_sparse_data_optim=True and missing=0.0 with pyspark.ml.linalg.SparseVector input.
* [Sparse Data Optimization in xgboost.spark](/concepts/sparse-data-optimization-in-xgboostspark.md) - Performance optimization for datasets with sparse features by setting enable_sparse_data_optim=True and missing=0.0, requiring SparseVector feature columns.
* [SQL Alerts on MLflow Metadata](/concepts/sql-alerts-on-mlflow-metadata.md) - Using Databricks SQL alerts with MLflow system tables to monitor experiment reliability and be notified when constraints are violated.
* [SQL Function Allowlist for Shared Views](/concepts/sql-function-allowlist-for-shared-views.md) - The restricted subset of Databricks built-in SQL functions and operators permitted in view definitions shared via Databricks-to-Databricks sharing
* [SQL Warehouse Integration for Trace Search](/concepts/sql-warehouse-integration-for-trace-search.md) - Performance optimization using a Databricks SQL warehouse to accelerate queries on large trace datasets in inference or Unity Catalog tables.
* [SQL-only Table Access Control](/concepts/sql-only-table-access-control.md) - A restrictive version of table access control that limits users to SQL commands only, configured via Spark conf spark.databricks.acl.sqlOnly true.
* [SQLSTATE 0AKDC](/concepts/sqlstate-0akdc.md) - The SQLSTATE code associated with the DELTA_CLONE_INCOMPATIBLE_SOURCE error, categorized under class 0A (Feature Not Supported).
* [SQLSTATE 0AKDC (Feature Not Supported)](/concepts/sqlstate-0akdc-feature-not-supported.md) - A Databricks SQLSTATE class indicating a feature is not supported by the source or operation context.
* [SQLSTATE 42601](/concepts/sqlstate-42601.md) - A SQL state code indicating a syntax error or access rule violation, used as the parent error class for the DELTA_UNIFORM_COMPATIBILITY_MISSING_OR_INVALID_LOCATION error.
* [SQLSTATE 42616](/concepts/sqlstate-42616.md) - A SQL standard error code in Class 42 (Syntax Error or Access Rule Violation) used by the DELTA_STREAMING_SKIP_OFFSETS_INVALID_OFFSET_RANGE error condition in Databricks.
* [SQLSTATE 42704](/concepts/sqlstate-42704.md) - A SQL standard error code (class 42) indicating a syntax error or access rule violation, associated with undefined objects
* [SQLSTATE 42711](/concepts/sqlstate-42711.md) - A SQL standard error code for duplicate column definition violations, referenced by the DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND error class in Databricks.
* [SQLSTATE 42711 (Databricks Error Code)](/concepts/sqlstate-42711.md) - The SQL standard error state code (class 42, syntax error or access rule violation) associated with the DELTA_DUPLICATE_COLUMNS_FOUND error in Databricks.
* [SQLSTATE 42711 (duplicate column error)](/concepts/sqlstate-42711.md) - The SQL standard error code 42711, classified under class 42 (syntax error or access rule violation), which maps to the Delta Lake duplicate column error condition.
* [SQLSTATE 42809](/concepts/sqlstate-42809.md) - The SQL standard state code for syntax error or access rule violation, associated in Databricks with the DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_UNSET_MANAGED_FAILED error class.
* [SQLSTATE 428GU](/concepts/sqlstate-428gu.md) - The SQL standard error code assigned to the DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA error, classified under Class 42 (Syntax Error or Access Rule Violation).
* [SQLSTATE 55006](/concepts/sqlstate-55006.md) - ANSI/ISO SQL standard state 'object not in prerequisite state', which this Databricks error maps to, indicating the object is in a state that prevents the requested operation.
* [SQLSTATE 55006 — Object Not in Prerequisite State](/concepts/sqlstate-55006-object-not-in-prerequisite-state.md) - A SQL standard error class (class 55) indicating the target object cannot be modified because it is not in the required prerequisite state, used here when a shared securable is blocked from deletion.
* [SQLSTATE 55019](/concepts/sqlstate-55019.md) - A SQL state code (class 55: Object not in prerequisite state) associated with table migration failures in Databricks.
* [SQLSTATE 55019 (Object Not in Prerequisite State)](/concepts/sqlstate-55019-object-not-in-prerequisite-state.md) - A SQL standard state code indicating that an object is not in the prerequisite state required for the requested operation.
* [SQLSTATE class 0A (Feature not supported)](/concepts/sqlstate-class-0a-feature-not-supported.md) - A SQLSTATE error class indicating a feature is not supported, which is the parent class of the DELTA_TABLE_CHANGES_VIEW_UNSUPPORTED error.
* [SQLSTATE KD00C](/concepts/sqlstate-kd00c.md) - The SQL state class KD00C used for Datasource-specific errors in Databricks, under which the DELTA_VERSIONS_NOT_CONTIGUOUS error falls.
* [SQLSTATE KD00E](/concepts/sqlstate-kd00e.md) - A Databricks-specific SQLSTATE error class for datasource-specific errors, used as the parent classification of DELTA_ICEBERG_WRITER_COMPAT_VIOLATION
* [SSL/TLS Certificate Configuration for Databricks Connect](/concepts/ssltls-certificates-for-databricks-connect.md) - Configuration of the GRPC_DEFAULT_SSL_ROOTS_FILE_PATH environment variable to support custom SSL/TLS certificates for Databricks workspace FQDN resolution.
* [SSL/TLS Certificates for Databricks Connect](/concepts/ssltls-certificates-for-databricks-connect.md) - Configuring custom SSL/TLS certificates for Databricks Connect by setting the `GRPC_DEFAULT_SSL_ROOTS_FILE_PATH` environment variable to the certificate path on the local development machine.
* [Stage-based Model Version Serving](/concepts/stage-based-model-version-serving.md) - A migration pattern that creates separate serving endpoints for Staging and Production model versions, aligned with the MLflow model registry stage concept.
* [Stage-Level Scheduling for GPU Parallelism](/concepts/stage-level-scheduling-for-gpu-parallelism.md) - Using Spark's ResourceProfile and stage-level scheduling to control the number of tasks running per GPU for increased parallelism
* [Staging and Testing ML Models](/concepts/staging-and-testing-ml-models.md) - The practice of testing model versions in staging with integration tests, A/B or shadow tests on production data, and stakeholder sign-off before promoting to production using model aliases.
* [Standard Access Mode](/concepts/standard-access-mode.md) - A Databricks cluster access mode that enables table access control by default.
* [Standard Access Mode Compute](/concepts/standard-access-mode-compute.md) - A Databricks compute access mode (formerly shared access mode) where the allowlist restricts what libraries and init scripts can run, using the library installer's identity for libraries and the cluster owner's identity for init scripts.
* [Standard access mode compute (Databricks)](/concepts/standard-access-mode-compute.md) - A cluster access mode where identity of the library installer or cluster owner is used for access control; allowlisted libraries and init scripts are required to run on this mode with Unity Catalog.
* [Standard Access Mode Compute in Unity Catalog](/concepts/standard-access-mode-compute-in-unity-catalog.md) - A compute access mode (formerly shared access mode) that enforces user isolation and requires allowlisted libraries and init scripts for non-default artifacts.
* [Standard Access Mode for Clusters](/concepts/standard-access-mode.md) - A Databricks cluster access mode that enables table access control by default, as opposed to legacy modes that require explicit configuration.
* [Standardized Metadata Fields for MLflow Traces](/concepts/standardized-metadata-fields-for-traces.md) - Predefined metadata keys like mlflow.trace.user, mlflow.trace.session, and mlflow.source.type that MLflow recognizes for filtering, grouping, and UI features in trace analysis.
* [Standardized Metadata Fields for Traces](/concepts/standardized-metadata-fields-for-traces.md) - Predefined metadata field names (like mlflow.trace.user, mlflow.trace.session, mlflow.source.type) that MLflow recognizes for automatic UI filtering, grouping, and environment detection.
* [Starting version and timestamp for Delta streaming sources](/concepts/starting-version-and-timestamp-for-streaming-reads.md) - Options (startingVersion, startingTimestamp) to control the starting point of Delta streaming reads without processing the entire table history.
* [Starting Version and Timestamp for Streaming Reads](/concepts/starting-version-and-timestamp-for-streaming-reads.md) - Options (startingVersion, startingTimestamp) to control the initial table version from which a Delta Lake streaming source begins processing changes.
* [State management in Scorer class](/concepts/state-management-in-scorer-class.md) - When using the Scorer class, state must be managed with instance attributes (not mutable class attributes) to avoid sharing state across scorer instances.
* [Stateful Scorers in Production](/concepts/stateful-scorers-in-production.md) - Technique for implementing stateful scorers by managing state inside the body of a @scorer-decorated function, since class-based Scorer subclasses are not supported.
* [Stateful scorers in production using @scorer](/concepts/stateful-scorers-in-production.md) - For stateful scoring logic in production, use the @scorer decorator and manage state inside the function body instead of class-based subclasses.
* [Stateful scorers with @scorer](/concepts/stateful-scorers-with-scorer.md) - To implement stateful scorers in production, use the @scorer decorator and manage state inside the function body, since class-based approaches are unsupported
* [Stateful scorers with @scorer decorator](/concepts/stateful-scorers-with-scorer-decorator.md) - For stateful scorers in production, use the @scorer decorator and manage state inside the function body
* [Static Dependency Capture Principle](/concepts/static-dependency-capture-principle.md) - The principle that real-time model serving workloads perform best when all file and artifact dependencies are statically packaged into the model at deployment time, rather than loaded dynamically from networks or external sources.
* [Statistical Parity](/concepts/statistical-parity.md) - A fairness metric that measures the difference in predicted outcomes (selection rates) between protected and unprotected groups
* [Statistics Collection during Delta Conversion](/concepts/statistics-collection-during-delta-conversion.md) - The process of collecting statistics during CONVERT TO DELTA to improve query performance, which can be bypassed with the NO STATISTICS option.
* [Stopping the PySpark Shell](/concepts/stopping-the-pyspark-shell.md) - Multiple methods to terminate the Databricks Connect PySpark shell including Ctrl+D, Ctrl+Z, quit(), or exit().
* [Storage Billing After Object Deletion](/concepts/storage-billing-after-object-deletion.md) - Databricks bills for default storage only during the 7-day recovery window; for customer-provided storage the cloud provider bills directly and charges may persist based on bucket policies.
* [Storage Credential (IAM Role) for Unity Catalog](/concepts/storage-credential-iam-role-for-unity-catalog.md) - An IAM role created in AWS that grants Unity Catalog access to an S3 bucket used for managed storage, requiring cross-account trust relationship policies.
* [Storage path conflict](/concepts/storage-path-conflict.md) - A conflict that occurs when an external location overlaps with the default Unity Catalog storage path of any workspace in a Databricks account, interfering with internal processing and data governance.
* [Streaming Data Approaches for Distributed Training](/concepts/streaming-data-approaches-for-distributed-training.md) - Methods for handling very large datasets that do not fit in memory when training distributed machine learning models, including PyTorch IterableDataset, Hugging Face datasets streaming, and Ray Data.
* [Streaming Data for Distributed Deep Learning](/concepts/streaming-data-for-distributed-deep-learning.md) - Streaming approaches for very large datasets that do not fit in memory, including PyTorch IterableDataset, Hugging Face Datasets streaming, and Ray Data.
* [Streaming Datasets on AI Runtime](/concepts/streaming-datasets-on-ai-runtime.md) - Strategies for handling very large datasets that don't fit in memory, including UCVolumeDataset, PyTorch IterableDataset, Hugging Face datasets streaming, and Ray Data.
* [Streaming Feature Computation Pipelines](/concepts/streaming-feature-computation-pipelines.md) - Feature tables can be updated incrementally using streaming DataFrames with `write_table` in merge mode, supporting real-time feature computation.
* [Streaming Feature Publishing](/concepts/streaming-feature-publishing.md) - Continuous streaming of features from Databricks Feature Store to an online store by setting the streaming=True parameter in publish_table().
* [Streaming from Delta Lake Tables with skipChangeCommits](/concepts/streaming-from-delta-lake-tables-with-skipchangecommits.md) - The skipChangeCommits option allows streaming queries to ignore upstream modifications (UPDATE, DELETE, MERGE, OVERWRITE) on a source Delta table, processing only append transactions.
* [Streaming Offset Event Time Restriction](/concepts/streaming-offset-event-time-restriction.md) - The constraint that streaming offsets used in Delta skip offset ranges must not contain event time (eventTimeMillis must be empty)
* [Streaming Output Tracing](/concepts/streaming-output-tracing.md) - Tracing of generator and iterator functions with @mlflow.trace, where spans start when the iterator is consumed and end when exhausted.
* [Streaming Response Feedback Collection](/concepts/streaming-response-feedback-collection.md) - Technique for collecting user feedback when using SSE/WebSocket streaming responses, where the trace ID is only available after stream completion
* [Streaming Response Feedback Handling](/concepts/streaming-response-feedback-handling.md) - Special approach for collecting user feedback on streaming responses where trace IDs are only available after stream completion, requiring deferred feedback UI design.
* [Streaming Table Default Publishing Mode Requirement](/concepts/streaming-table-default-publishing-mode-requirement.md) - Only streaming tables using the default publishing mode can be deep cloned; older architectures are not supported
* [Streaming table managed storage](/concepts/streaming-table-managed-storage.md) - Streaming tables in Databricks use managed storage, preventing specification of a custom LOCATION during deep clone operations.
* [Streaming Table Managed Storage Location Restriction](/concepts/streaming-table-managed-storage.md) - Specifying a custom LOCATION when deep cloning a streaming table is not supported because the cloned table uses managed storage
* [Streaming Table Publishing Modes](/concepts/streaming-table-publishing-modes.md) - Different publishing modes for streaming tables in Databricks, where only the default mode supports deep clone operations
* [Streaming Table Sharing](/concepts/streaming-table-sharing.md) - Sharing streaming tables (append-only Delta tables with incremental processing) with restrictions on partition filters, CDF support for open recipients, and predicate pushdown.
* [Streaming Tables and Materialized Views as Feature Tables](/concepts/streaming-tables-and-materialized-views-as-feature-tables.md) - Streaming tables and materialized views created by Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines with primary key constraints can be used as feature tables.
* [Streaming tables in Databricks](/concepts/streaming-tables-in-databricks.md) - Incrementally updated Delta tables that ingest streaming data, one of the two table types supported for external metadata.
* [Streaming Trace Behavior in MLflow](/concepts/streaming-trace-behavior-in-mlflow.md) - MLflow tracing for streaming Bedrock calls (converse_stream, invoke_model_with_response_stream) creates a span only when chunks are consumed (e.g., iterated in a for-loop), not when the response object is first returned.
* [Streaming Trace Support for Foundation Models](/concepts/streaming-trace-support-for-foundation-models.md) - MLflow Tracing supports streaming API responses from Databricks Foundation Models, automatically capturing and rendering the concatenated streaming output in the span UI.
* [Streaming Upserts with foreachBatch and MERGE](/concepts/streaming-upserts-with-foreachbatch-and-merge.md) - Using MERGE operations inside foreachBatch to continuously apply streaming changes (upserts) to Delta Lake tables with idempotency guarantees
* [StreamingDataLoader](/concepts/streamingdataloader.md) - A PyTorch DataLoader variant that provides an additional checkpoint/resumption interface by tracking the number of samples seen by the model per rank.
* [StreamingDataset](/concepts/streamingdataset.md) - A PyTorch IterableDataset variant that features elastically deterministic shuffling, enabling fast mid-epoch resumption during distributed training.
* [STRUCT_ALL_VOID_FIELDS](/concepts/struct_all_void_fields.md) - A sub-reason for DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA where a struct column has all fields of VOID type.
* [STRUCT_ALL_VOID_FIELDS error condition](/concepts/struct_all_void_fields.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA where a struct column has all fields of VOID type
* [STRUCT_NO_FIELDS](/concepts/struct_no_fields.md) - A sub-reason for DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA where a struct column has no fields defined.
* [STRUCT_NO_FIELDS error condition](/concepts/struct_no_fields.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA where a struct column has zero fields defined
* [Structured Outputs in Foundation Model APIs](/concepts/structured-outputs-in-foundation-model-apis.md) - A feature allowing specification of output format via JSON Schema in Databricks Foundation Model API requests, supported across both Responses and Chat Completions APIs.
* [Structured RAG with Online Tables](/concepts/structured-rag-with-online-tables.md) - Using Databricks online tables to serve structured data (e.g., customer metadata, product catalogs) as real-time context for retrieval-augmented generation applications.
* [Structured Streaming on Shared Tables](/concepts/structured-streaming-on-shared-tables.md) - Using Spark Structured Streaming with OpenSharing shared tables as a source for incremental processing, requiring history sharing to be enabled.
* [STS Temporary Credentials for Data Access](/concepts/sts-temporary-credentials-for-data-access.md) - Model serving endpoints use AWS STS temporary security credentials to authenticate data access through the instance profile; these credentials cannot bypass network restrictions.
* [STS Temporary Credentials for Model Data Access](/concepts/sts-temporary-credentials-for-model-data-access.md) - AWS STS temporary security credentials are used to authenticate data access from model serving endpoints, but they cannot bypass network restrictions.
* [STS Temporary Credentials for Model Serving](/concepts/sts-temporary-credentials-for-data-access.md) - AWS Security Token Service (STS) temporary credentials are used by Databricks model serving endpoints to authenticate data access through an instance profile, but cannot bypass network restrictions.
* [STS Temporary Credentials for Model Serving Data Access](/concepts/sts-temporary-credentials-for-model-data-access.md) - AWS STS temporary security credentials are used to authenticate data access from model serving endpoints, but they cannot bypass network restrictions.
* [STS Token Sharing](/concepts/sts-token-sharing.md) - A Delta Sharing mechanism using scoped-down AWS STS tokens for temporary read access, logged as generateTemporaryTableCredentials and generateTemporaryVolumeCredentials events
* [STS Token Sharing Audit Details](/concepts/sts-token-sharing-audit-details.md) - Audit log fields for STS token-based sharing, including generateTemporaryTableCredentials and generateTemporaryVolumeCredentials events with credential and table metadata.
* [STS Token Sharing Audit Events](/concepts/sts-token-sharing-audit-events.md) - Audit events (generateTemporaryTableCredentials, generateTemporaryVolumeCredentials) logged when data recipients query shared data via scoped-down STS token-based sharing, with request_params details.
* [STS Token Sharing in OpenSharing](/concepts/sts-token-sharing.md) - A credential mechanism for OpenSharing that uses scoped-down STS tokens for temporary data access, logged through generateTemporaryTableCredentials and generateTemporaryVolumeCredentials events.
* [SUBQUERY constraint for table_changes](/concepts/subquery-constraint-for-table_changes.md) - A validation rule requiring that views used with table_changes must not contain subqueries
* [Subquery Restrictions in DELETE WHERE](/concepts/subquery-restrictions-in-delete-where.md) - Restrictions on subquery types within DELETE WHERE predicates, including nested subqueries and NOT IN within OR
* [Subquery Restrictions in DML Statements](/concepts/subquery-restrictions-in-dml-statements.md) - DELETE and other DML statements in Databricks SQL forbid nested subqueries and NOT IN subqueries inside OR expressions.
* [Subquery Restrictions in UPDATE WHERE Clause](/concepts/subquery-restrictions-in-update-where-clause.md) - Limitations on subqueries in UPDATE WHERE clauses: nested subqueries and NOT IN subqueries inside OR are not allowed; NOT IN can be rewritten as NOT EXISTS for better performance.
* [Subquery support in Databricks DELETE WHERE clause](/concepts/subquery-support-in-databricks-delete-where-clause.md) - The WHERE predicate in DELETE FROM supports subqueries including IN, NOT IN, EXISTS, NOT EXISTS, and scalar subqueries, with specific restrictions on nested subqueries and NOT IN inside OR expressions.
* [Subquery Support in DELETE WHERE Clause](/concepts/subquery-support-in-delete-where-clause.md) - Types of subqueries allowed (IN, NOT IN, EXISTS, NOT EXISTS, scalar) and not allowed (nested, NOT IN inside OR) in Delta Lake DELETE statements.
* [Subset Caching with CACHE SELECT](/concepts/subset-caching-with-cache-select.md) - Technique of caching only a subset of columns and/or rows from a table by specifying column names and a WHERE predicate in CACHE SELECT.
* [suggest_float with log scale](/concepts/suggest_float-with-log-scale.md) - Optuna method for suggesting continuous float hyperparameters, optionally on a logarithmic scale for wide-ranging values
* [Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT)](/concepts/supervised-fine-tuning-sft.md) - A training technique that fine-tunes a pre-trained language model on labeled instruction-following data to improve task performance
* [Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) of Instruction-Tuned LLMs](/concepts/supervised-fine-tuning-sft-of-instruction-tuned-llms.md) - A training technique where a causal language model is fine-tuned on instruction-response pairs (e.g., Alpaca dataset) by tokenizing prompt-response sequences with EOS tokens and training with standard causal LM loss, often using FSDP for 8B+ parameter models.
* [Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) of LLMs](/concepts/supervised-fine-tuning-sft.md) - A training approach where a pre-trained language model is further trained on labeled conversational data to improve its performance on specific tasks, using techniques like bfloat16 precision, cosine learning rate scheduling, and gradient accumulation.
* [Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) with ChatML](/concepts/supervised-fine-tuning-sft-with-chatml.md) - The process of fine-tuning a causal language model on structured conversational datasets using the ChatML format for instruction-following behavior.
* [Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) with SFTTrainer](/concepts/supervised-fine-tuning-sft-with-trl.md) - Training paradigm using the TRL library's SFTTrainer to fine-tune language models on instruction-following datasets, with configurable hyperparameters and MLflow tracking.
* [Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) with TRL](/concepts/supervised-fine-tuning-sft-with-trl.md) - The training paradigm used to fine-tune language models on instruction-following datasets, implemented via the SFTTrainer class from the transformers reinforcement learning (TRL) library.
