* [Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) with TRL Library](/concepts/supervised-fine-tuning-sft-with-trl.md) - A technique for fine-tuning large language models using the TRL SFTTrainer, which provides tools for supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning training of LLMs.
* [Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) with TRL SFTTrainer](/concepts/supervised-fine-tuning-sft-with-trl.md) - A supervised fine-tuning method for language models using the Transformer Reinforcement Learning (TRL) library's SFTTrainer, which streamlines the process of fine-tuning causal language models on instruction-following datasets.
* [Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) Workflow](/concepts/supervised-fine-tuning-sft-workflow.md) - End-to-end pipeline for fine-tuning a causal language model on a conversational dataset, including loading data, applying chat templates, configuring training hyperparameters, and saving artifacts.
* [Supported Bedrock APIs for MLflow Tracing](/concepts/supported-bedrock-apis-for-mlflow-tracing.md) - MLflow supports auto-tracing for four Bedrock APIs: converse, converse_stream, invoke_model, and invoke_model_with_response_stream.
* [Supported Data Feature Types for AutoML](/concepts/supported-feature-types-in-automl.md) - The data types that Databricks AutoML supports for classification, including numeric, boolean, string, timestamps, arrays, and decimal types.
* [Supported Data Feature Types for AutoML Forecasting](/concepts/supported-feature-types-in-automl.md) - The specific data types (numeric, boolean, string, timestamp, array, decimal) that AutoML accepts for forecasting tasks on Databricks.
* [Supported Data Feature Types in AutoML](/concepts/supported-data-feature-types-in-automl.md) - The data types supported by AutoML for training, including numeric, Boolean, string, timestamp, array, and decimal types, with exclusions like images.
* [Supported Data Types for Feature Lookup](/concepts/supported-data-types-for-feature-lookup.md) - Automatic feature lookup supports IntegerType, FloatType, BooleanType, StringType, DoubleType, LongType, TimestampType, DateType, ShortType, ArrayType, and MapType.
* [Supported Data Types for Feature Stores](/concepts/supported-data-types-for-feature-stores.md) - Databricks Feature Store supports PySpark data types including IntegerType, FloatType, ArrayType, MapType, StructType, and others, with specific version requirements for complex types.
* [Supported Feature Data Types](/concepts/supported-feature-data-types.md) - The set of PySpark data types supported by Databricks Feature Store for storing features such as vectors, tensors, embeddings, and text.
* [Supported Feature Types for AutoML](/concepts/supported-feature-types-in-automl.md) - The set of data types supported by Databricks AutoML including numeric types, Boolean, String, timestamps, ArrayType of Numeric, and DecimalType, with images explicitly unsupported.
* [Supported Feature Types for Forecasting](/concepts/supported-feature-types-in-automl.md) - The set of data types (numeric, boolean, string, timestamps, arrays, decimals) supported by Databricks AutoML for forecasting tasks.
* [Supported Feature Types in AutoML](/concepts/supported-feature-types-in-automl.md) - The set of data types (numeric, boolean, string, timestamp, array, decimal) that AutoML supports for classification feature columns.
* [Supported Feature Types in Databricks AutoML](/concepts/supported-feature-types-in-automl.md) - The exhaustive list of Spark data types (numeric, boolean, string, timestamp, array, decimal) that AutoML accepts as feature columns.
* [Supported Frameworks for Databricks Autologging](/concepts/supported-ml-frameworks-for-autologging.md) - The list of ML frameworks (scikit-learn, Spark MLlib, TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch Lightning, XGBoost, LightGBM, Gluon, Fast.ai, statsmodels, PaddlePaddle, OpenAI, LangChain) for which Databricks Autologging natively captures training metrics.
* [Supported ML Frameworks for Autologging](/concepts/supported-ml-frameworks-for-autologging.md) - The list of machine learning frameworks compatible with Databricks Autologging, including scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch Lightning, XGBoost, and others.
* [Supported ML Frameworks for Databricks Autologging](/concepts/supported-ml-frameworks-for-autologging.md) - The comprehensive list of machine learning frameworks supported by Databricks Autologging including scikit-learn, Apache Spark MLlib, TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch Lightning, XGBoost, LightGBM, Gluon, Fast.ai, statsmodels, PaddlePaddle, OpenAI, and LangChain.
* [Supported Model Providers and Architectures](/concepts/supported-model-providers-and-architectures.md) - The range of third-party model providers available through Databricks Foundation Model APIs including OpenAI, Google (Gemini), Anthropic (Claude), Meta (Llama), Alibaba Cloud (Qwen), and specialized embedding model providers.
* [Supported Models for Function Calling on Databricks](/concepts/supported-models-for-function-calling-on-databricks.md) - A curated list of models (Meta Llama, Mixtral, Claude, Gemini, and Databricks DBRX) available through Foundation Model APIs or External Models for function calling.
* [Supported Object Types for Unity Catalog Certification Tags](/concepts/supported-object-types-for-unity-catalog-certification-tags.md) - The list of Unity Catalog objects that support the certification status tag, including catalogs, schemas, tables, views, volumes, functions, registered models, dashboards, Genie Spaces, apps, and notebooks.
* [Supported PySpark Data Types for Features](/concepts/supported-pyspark-data-types-for-features.md) - The list of PySpark data types (IntegerType, FloatType, BooleanType, StringType, DoubleType, LongType, TimestampType, DateType, ShortType, ArrayType, BinaryType, DecimalType, MapType, StructType) supported by Databricks Feature Store for storing feature values.
* [Supported PySpark Data Types in Feature Store](/concepts/supported-data-types-for-feature-stores.md) - Feature Store supports PySpark types including IntegerType, FloatType, ArrayType, MapType, StructType, and others, with ArrayType and MapType stored as JSON in online stores.
* [Supported PySpark UDF Function Types](/concepts/supported-pyspark-udf-function-types.md) - Databricks Connect supports a wide range of PySpark UDF functions including scalar UDFs, pandas UDFs, UDTFs, mapInPandas/mapInArrow, grouped apply functions, and streaming foreach/foreachBatch.
* [Supported scenarios and limitations of Databricks Feature Engineering](/concepts/supported-scenarios-and-limitations-of-databricks-feature-engineering.md) - Scenarios where the Feature Engineering client can be used (Databricks Runtime) and where it cannot (local/external environments)
* [Supported SQL Functions for Shared Views](/concepts/supported-sql-functions-for-shared-views.md) - The comprehensive list of SQL operators, mathematical functions, string functions, date/time functions, array/map functions, aggregation functions, window functions, encryption functions, geospatial (H3) functions, and type conversion functions allowed in Databricks-to-Databricks view sharing.
* [Supported Table Types for External Metadata in Databricks](/concepts/supported-table-types-for-external-metadata-in-databricks.md) - Only streaming tables and materialized views are supported for external metadata operations; other table types trigger the TABLE_TYPE error.
* [Supported Tool Types for Pay-Per-Token Models](/concepts/supported-tool-types-for-pay-per-token-models.md) - The complete list of tool types supported by pay-per-token foundation models via the Responses API: function, custom, apply_patch, shell, image_generation, mcp, and web_search.
* [Suspended Custom Classifier](/concepts/suspended-custom-classifier.md) - A state where a custom classifier stops producing detections due to rule generation failure, invalid example columns, or invalid governed tags; requires editing or recreation to resolve.
* [Symlink format manifest](/concepts/symlink-format-manifest.md) - A manifest format that uses symlinks to allow Presto/Athena to read Delta tables by generating a list of Parquet files.
* [Symlink Manifest Generation](/concepts/symlink-manifest-generation.md) - A Delta Lake feature for generating symlink manifests for external query engines; mutually exclusive with deletion vectors in several configurations
* [Symlink Manifest Generation (Incremental)](/concepts/symlink-manifest-generation.md) - A mechanism for generating symlink manifests in Delta Lake, used for integrating with external query engines, which has constraints when combined with deletion vectors.
* [symlink_format_manifest mode](/concepts/symlink_format_manifest-mode.md) - A mode for the GENERATE command that produces manifest files enabling Presto and Athena to read Delta tables.
* [SYNC Command for Hive-to-Unity-Catalog Migration](/concepts/sync-command-for-hive-to-unity-catalog-migration.md) - SQL command (SYNC TABLE / SYNC SCHEMA) that copies Hive metastore external tables to Unity Catalog external tables, with support for ongoing synchronization when source tables change.
* [Syncing Expectations to Evaluation Datasets](/concepts/syncing-expectations-to-evaluation-datasets.md) - A workflow that synchronizes collected Expectation labels from a labeling session to an Evaluation Dataset via an intelligent upsert, using trace inputs as unique keys.
* [Synthetic Data Generation for Evaluation](/concepts/synthetic-data-generation-for-evaluation.md) - Databricks' capability to automatically generate a representative evaluation set from documents, enabling quick evaluation of agents with good coverage of test cases without requiring pre-existing labeled data.
* [Synthetic Data Generation for GenAI Evaluation](/concepts/synthetic-data-generation-for-evaluation.md) - Databricks can automatically generate representative evaluation datasets from documents, enabling quick evaluation of GenAI agents with broad test case coverage without manual curation.
* [Synthetic Evaluation Data Generation](/concepts/synthetic-evaluation-data-generation.md) - Databricks' ability to automatically generate a representative evaluation set from source documents, enabling quick coverage of test cases without manually curating examples.
* [Synthetic Evaluation Set Generation (Databricks)](/concepts/synthetic-evaluation-set-generation-databricks.md) - A serverless workload that generates synthetic evaluation datasets for GenAI agent evaluation, controlled via budget policies on experiments.
* [System Tables for Audit Logs](/concepts/system-tables-for-audit-logs.md) - The system.access.audit table where audit logs are stored when system tables are enabled, requiring account admin or granted permissions to read
* [System Tables for Unity Catalog Audit](/concepts/system-tables-for-unity-catalog-audit.md) - The system.access.audit system table requirement for accessing OpenSharing audit logs, requiring account admin enablement and appropriate permissions for non-admin users.
* [System Tags](/concepts/system-tags.md) - Predefined tags provided by Databricks (e.g., ai.model_creator) that can be referenced in GRANT policy conditions alongside user-created governed tags.
* [System Tags (Databricks)](/concepts/system-tags-databricks.md) - Tags automatically assigned by the Databricks system to columns when sensitive data is detected, such as those indicating PII or compliance-related data.
* [system.ai Schema](/concepts/systemai-schema.md) - The Unity Catalog schema under the system catalog where foundation model objects reside and EXECUTE permissions are managed to control model access.
* [System.ai Schema Default EXECUTE Permission](/concepts/systemai-schema-default-execute-permission.md) - The default permission model where all users have EXECUTE on the system.ai schema, granting access to all Databricks-hosted foundation models until an admin revokes it.
* [system.ai Schema Permission Model](/concepts/systemai-schema.md) - The Unity Catalog schema (system.ai) that governs access to all Databricks-hosted foundation models through EXECUTE permissions, applied consistently across pay-per-token, provisioned throughput, and batch inference workloads.
* [system.billing.usage system table](/concepts/systembillingusage-system-table.md) - A Databricks system table used to query billable usage records, including data quality monitoring costs.
* [system.data_classification.mask_value](/concepts/systemdata_classificationmask_value.md) - A built-in, type-aware masking UDF in Databricks that returns safe placeholder values based on column data types (e.g., 0 for integers, SHA-256 hash for strings, DATE '1970-01-01' for dates).
* [system.data_classification.results System Table](/concepts/data-classification-system-table.md) - A system table that stores all classification results across the entire metastore, including sample values from tables, accessible only to account admins by default and requiring serverless compute.
* [system.data_quality_monitoring.table_results](/concepts/systemdata_quality_monitoringtable_results.md) - A system table in Databricks that stores anomaly detection output results, including event time, table status, freshness metrics, completeness metrics, and downstream impact information.
* [system.data_quality_monitoring.table_results System Table](/concepts/systemdata_quality_monitoringtable_results.md) - The Databricks system table that stores anomaly detection output results, containing fields like event_time, catalog_name, schema_name, table_name, status, freshness metrics, completeness metrics, and downstream impact data.
* [system.mlflow.experiments_latest](/concepts/systemmlflowexperiments_latest.md) - A system table that records experiment names, soft-deletion events, and lifecycle information for MLflow experiments.
* [system.mlflow.run_metrics_history](/concepts/systemmlflowrun_metrics_history.md) - A system table that records the name, value, timestamp, and step of all metrics logged on runs, enabling detailed timeseries analysis.
* [system.mlflow.runs_latest](/concepts/systemmlflowruns_latest.md) - A system table that records run-lifecycle information, params, tags, and aggregated metric statistics (min, max, latest) for MLflow runs.
* [Table Access Control (Databricks)](/concepts/table-access-control-databricks.md) - A workspace-level and cluster-level setting that must be enabled to enforce Hive metastore privileges on Databricks clusters and SQL warehouses.
* [Table Access Control (TACL)](/concepts/table-access-control-tacl.md) - A workspace-level and cluster-level setting that must be enabled for Hive metastore privilege enforcement in Databricks.
* [Table Access Control Cluster Configuration](/concepts/table-access-control-cluster-configuration.md) - The per-cluster toggle that controls whether Hive metastore table access control is enforced, with default being open access to all data.
* [Table ACLs Blocking Delta Schema Evolution](/concepts/table-acls-blocking-delta-schema-evolution.md) - A restriction where enabling Table ACLs on a cluster disables automatic schema migration for Delta tables, requiring explicit ALTER TABLE commands.
* [Table alias in DELETE](/concepts/table-alias-in-delete.md) - DELETE FROM supports an optional table alias that must not include a column list, used to reference the target table in subqueries.
* [Table Alias in DELETE FROM](/concepts/table-alias-in-delete-from.md) - Using table aliases in DELETE FROM statements to simplify references in subqueries
* [Table aliases in Databricks DML statements](/concepts/table-aliases-in-delete-statements.md) - Databricks SQL allows defining a table alias in DELETE FROM statements, but the alias must not include a column list, enabling clearer references in subqueries and complex predicates.
* [Table Aliases in DELETE Statements](/concepts/table-aliases-in-delete-statements.md) - Databricks SQL allows optional table aliasing in DELETE FROM syntax but the alias must not include a column list.
* [Table deletion and recreation at same S3 location](/concepts/table-deletion-and-recreation-at-same-s3-location.md) - A specific operational pattern on AWS S3 that can lead to Delta log gaps due to S3's eventual consistency model when a table is dropped and re-created at the same path.
* [Table History Sharing](/concepts/table-history-sharing.md) - Sharing a table's history (Delta log) in a share to allow recipients to perform time travel queries, read with Spark Structured Streaming, run transactions, and improve performance for Databricks-to-Databricks shares. Requires Databricks Runtime 12.2 LTS or above. Required when sharing tables with deletion vectors or column mapping.
* [Table Metadata and Protocol Change Conflicts](/concepts/table-metadata-and-protocol-change-conflicts.md) - Conflict scenarios where concurrent operations change table metadata (schema, partitioning) or upgrade the table protocol, triggering the DELTA_CONCURRENT_APPEND error.
* [Table Metadata Change Transaction Conflicts](/concepts/table-metadata-change-transaction-conflicts.md) - A conflict type where concurrent operations that change table metadata (schema, partitioning, protocol) cause read transactions to fail, typically resolved by retrying the operation.
* [Table Metadata Conflicts in Delta Lake](/concepts/table-metadata-conflicts-in-delta-lake.md) - Conflicts arising from concurrent schema or partitioning changes to a Delta table while a transaction is in progress, requiring transaction retry.
* [Table Name Restrictions in DESCRIBE HISTORY](/concepts/table-name-restrictions-in-describe-history.md) - The table name in DESCRIBE HISTORY must not include a temporal specification or options specification.
* [Table Protocol Upgrade Conflicts](/concepts/table-protocol-upgrade-conflicts.md) - A conflict condition where a concurrent operation upgraded the table protocol, invalidating the current transaction's assumptions and requiring a retry.
* [Table provenance tracking](/concepts/table-provenance-tracking.md) - Delta Lake tracks metadata for each write operation including the operation type, user, and other provenance information.
* [Table provenance tracking in Delta Lake](/concepts/table-provenance-tracking.md) - Delta Lake automatically records operation type, user identity, and other metadata for every write to a table
* [Table Quality Details (Databricks)](/concepts/table-quality-details-databricks.md) - A detailed view showing trends and anomaly reasons for specific tables, including predicted vs observed values and upstream root cause analysis
* [Table Quality Details UI](/concepts/table-quality-details-ui.md) - A detailed view per table showing trend graphs of predicted vs observed values for each quality check, along with identified upstream root causes for failures.
* [Table Types in Unity Catalog](/concepts/table-types-in-unity-catalog.md) - Unity Catalog supports managed tables (storage managed by Unity Catalog), external tables (user-specified storage path), and foreign tables (from foreign catalogs), each with different lifecycle and governance characteristics.
* [TABLE_ALL_VOID_COLUMNS](/concepts/table_all_void_columns.md) - A sub-reason for DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA where every column in the table schema has VOID type.
* [TABLE_ALL_VOID_COLUMNS error condition](/concepts/table_all_void_columns.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA where every column in the table schema is of VOID type
* [table_changes function](/concepts/table_changes-function.md) - A Delta Lake function that reads Change Data Feed (CDF) records, but is unsupported on views.
* [table_changes function (Databricks)](/concepts/table_changes-function.md) - A Databricks function used to retrieve change data feed (CDF) records from Delta tables, which is unsupported on views.
* [table_name restrictions for DESCRIBE HISTORY](/concepts/table-name-restrictions-in-describe-history.md) - The table name used with DESCRIBE HISTORY must not include a temporal specification or options specification.
* [TABLE_NO_COLUMNS](/concepts/table_no_columns.md) - A sub-reason for DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA where the table schema has no columns defined at all.
* [TABLE_NO_COLUMNS error condition](/concepts/table_no_columns.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CANNOT_WRITE_EMPTY_SCHEMA where the table schema has no columns defined at all
* [Table-Level Filtering and Masking](/concepts/table-level-filtering-and-masking.md) - Fine-grained data access controls using row filters and column masks applied via UDFs to control what data users see within tables at query time.
* [Table-Level Row Filters and Column Masks](/concepts/row-filters-and-column-masks.md) - Per-table UDF-based filters and masks that control what data users see at query time within tables.
* [Table-prefix Format Benefits](/concepts/table-prefix-format-benefits.md) - The table-prefix UC trace format provides faster time-range queries, richer attribute types, a dedicated annotations table, and support for multiple trace destinations per schema compared to the older schema-linked format
* [Table-wide vs filtered Delta operations](/concepts/table-wide-vs-filtered-delta-operations.md) - A category of transaction conflicts caused by operations that modify an entire table (no filter predicates) rather than a subset of data, including WHOLE_TABLE_READ, WHOLE_TABLE_REPLACE, and EMPTY_READ_PREDICATES sub-errors.
* [Table-wide vs Filtered Operations in Delta Lake](/concepts/table-wide-vs-filtered-operations-in-delta-lake.md) - The distinction between operations that modify an entire table versus filtered subsets, where whole-table modifications are more prone to concurrent transaction conflicts.
* [Tag Inheritance](/concepts/tag-inheritance.md) - Unity Catalog securables inherit governed tags from parent catalogs and schemas by default, but column tags do not inherit from parent tables and must be applied directly.
* [Tag Mutability vs Metadata Immutability](/concepts/tag-mutability-vs-metadata-immutability.md) - Design distinction in MLflow tracing where tags are mutable (updateable after logging) while metadata is write-once and immutable once set.
* [Tag-based access gating](/concepts/tag-based-access-gating.md) - A governance pattern that blocks access to untagged or unclassified data using a default restrictive tag, then applies masking policies once classification tags are applied.
* [Tag-based access gating (unverified classification pattern)](/concepts/tag-based-access-gating.md) - A governance pattern that blocks access to untagged or unclassified data using a default restrictive tag, then applies column masking after classification is complete, using has_tag_value conditions in policies.
* [Tag-based access gating workflow](/concepts/tag-based-access-gating.md) - A governance pattern that uses a default restrictive tag (e.g. 'classification : unverified') to block access to unclassified data, then transitions to column masking once a data steward completes classification and updates the tag.
* [Tag-based metadata in Unity Catalog](/concepts/tag-based-metadata-in-unity-catalog.md) - Tags for labeling models and model versions in Unity Catalog, supporting search in Catalog Explorer (up to 50 tags per object), replacing stage labels from the Workspace Model Registry.
* [Tag-based Policy Conditions and Built-in Functions](/concepts/abac-policy-conditions-and-built-in-functions.md) - The has_tag() and has_tag_value() functions used in ABAC policy WHEN and MATCH COLUMNS clauses to match tables and columns based on governed tag attributes.
* [Task Parallelism (Ray)](/concepts/task-parallelism-ray.md) - Ray is designed for task parallelism, enabling multiple independent tasks to run concurrently, making it efficient for computation-focused workloads like reinforcement learning, hyperparameter search, and simulation modeling.
* [Task-Specific AI Functions](/concepts/task-specific-ai-functions.md) - Specialized built-in SQL functions in Databricks for specific AI tasks like ai_translate, designed for targeted batch inference workloads
* [Task-Specific AI Functions (Databricks)](/concepts/task-specific-ai-functions.md) - Specialized built-in AI functions such as ai_translate for targeted batch inference tasks without needing to specify a model.
* [Task-Specific AI Functions vs General-Purpose AI Functions](/concepts/task-specific-ai-functions-vs-general-purpose-ai-functions.md) - Databricks distinguishes between task-specific AI functions (e.g., ai_translate for translation) and the general-purpose ai_query function for applying AI models in SQL.
* [Telemetry Data Querying and Analysis](/concepts/telemetry-data-querying-and-analysis.md) - Patterns for querying persisted endpoint telemetry data using SQL, including filtering by severity, timestamp, trace IDs, and custom attributes for root cause analysis.
* [Template Variables for Judge Prompts](/concepts/template-variables-for-judge-prompts.md) - The four allowed template variables in make_judge prompts — inputs, outputs, expectations, and trace — that provide access to agent data during evaluation.
* [Temporal Specification Restriction on DESCRIBE HISTORY](/concepts/temporal-specification-restriction-on-describe-history.md) - The DESCRIBE HISTORY command does not accept temporal or options specifications in the table name parameter.
* [Tensor Input Scoring Format](/concepts/tensor-input-scoring-format.md) - A JSON format for scoring requests to Databricks Model Serving endpoints that accept tensor inputs, supporting both 'instances' (row format with same 0-th dimension) and 'inputs' (columnar format for heterogeneous tensors)
* [TensorBoard Known Issues and Workarounds on Databricks](/concepts/tensorboard-known-issues-and-workarounds-on-databricks.md) - Specific issues encountered when using TensorBoard on Databricks including iframe limitations, port availability, blank projector tabs, and DBFS logging errors.
* [TensorBoard Log Directory Best Practices](/concepts/tensorboard-log-directory-best-practices.md) - Recommendations for writing TensorBoard logs to cloud storage instead of ephemeral cluster storage, and organizing logs by experiment and run subdirectories.
* [TensorBoard Multi-Framework Support](/concepts/tensorboard-multi-framework-support.md) - TensorBoard supports visualization for TensorFlow, PyTorch, Hugging Face Transformers, and other ML programs through callbacks, functions, and file writers.
* [TensorBoard on Databricks](/concepts/tensorboard-on-databricks.md) - How to start and use TensorBoard visualization tools within Databricks notebooks using magic commands or the notebook module.
* [TensorBoard Process Lifecycle on Databricks](/concepts/tensorboard-process-lifecycle-on-databricks.md) - Managing TensorBoard server processes on Databricks clusters including starting, listing, and killing processes, and handling port range limitations.
* [TensorFlow 2 Distributed Training](/concepts/tensorflow-2-distributed-training.md) - The general distributed training capability introduced as a major feature in TensorFlow 2, including distribution strategies.
* [TensorFlow Deprecation from Top-Tier](/concepts/tensorflow-deprecation-from-top-tier.md) - Starting with Databricks Runtime 18.0 ML, TensorFlow and spark-tensorflow-connector are no longer top-tier libraries.
* [TensorFlow deprecation in Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/tensorflow-deprecation-in-databricks-runtime-ml.md) - The removal of TensorFlow and spark-tensorflow-connector from the top-tier library list starting with Databricks Runtime 18.0 ML.
* [TensorFlow Ecosystem](/concepts/tensorflow-ecosystem.md) - A collection of libraries and tools extending TensorFlow, including the spark-tensorflow-connector for Spark integration.
* [TensorFlow on Databricks](/concepts/tensorflow-on-databricks.md) - Using TensorFlow and TensorBoard within Databricks Runtime ML for deep learning and general numerical computations on CPUs, GPUs, and GPU clusters.
* [TensorFlow on Spark clusters](/concepts/tensorflow-on-spark-clusters.md) - Pattern for integrating TensorFlow 2 distributed training with Apache Spark clusters via the TensorFlow ecosystem package
* [TensorFlow SavedModel Format Requirement](/concepts/tensorflow-savedmodel-format-requirement.md) - When using horovod.spark with custom Keras callbacks, models must be saved in the TensorFlow SavedModel format using the .tf suffix (TF 2.x) or save_weights_only=True (TF 1.x).
* [tensorflow.distribute.Strategy](/concepts/tensorflowdistributestrategy.md) - TensorFlow 2's native API for distributed training strategies, serving as the foundation for spark-tensorflow-distributor
* [Test Case Definition](/concepts/test-case-definition.md) - Scenarios for simulated conversations defined by a required goal and optional persona and context fields that guide the simulated user's behavior.
* [Test Case Definition for Conversation Simulation](/concepts/test-case-definition-for-conversation-simulation.md) - Structured specification of a conversation scenario using goal, persona, and context fields to guide the simulator in generating realistic dialogues.
* [Test Case Generation from Production Conversations](/concepts/test-case-generation-from-production-conversations.md) - Process of extracting test scenarios (goals and personas) from existing production conversation sessions using mlflow.search_sessions and generate_test_cases.
* [Test Cases for Conversation Simulation](/concepts/test-cases-for-conversation-simulation.md) - Structured specifications (goal, persona, context) defining each simulated conversation scenario, where goal describes the user's intent, persona shapes communication style, and context provides additional parameters to the agent function.
* [Testing ABAC policy UDF performance at scale](/concepts/testing-abac-policy-udf-performance-at-scale.md) - Guidelines for testing policy UDFs on at least 1 million rows before production deployment, including a template SQL query using range() and WITH test_data to isolate policy overhead.
* [Testing Private Repository Connectivity with Serverless Notebooks](/concepts/testing-private-repository-connectivity-with-serverless-notebooks.md) - Using serverless notebooks to validate connectivity, authentication, and package availability from private PyPI mirrors before deploying to Model Serving
* [Text Embedding Models](/concepts/text-embedding-models.md) - Compact models like GTE, BGE, and Qwen3-Embedding that map text to dense vector representations for semantic search, retrieval, clustering, and classification tasks, typically used alongside LLMs in RAG pipelines.
* [Text Embedding Models for RAG](/concepts/text-embedding-models.md) - Embedding models like GTE Large, BGE Large, and Qwen3-Embedding available via Databricks APIs that map text to dense vectors for semantic search, retrieval, clustering, and classification, often used with LLMs for RAG.
* [Text Embedding Models for Semantic Search](/concepts/text-embedding-models.md) - Models like Qwen3-Embedding, GTE, and BGE that map text to dense vector representations for retrieval, clustering, classification, and semantic similarity tasks, often used to power RAG pipelines.
* [Text Embedding Models on Databricks](/concepts/text-embedding-models.md) - A class of models (GTE, BGE, Qwen3-Embedding) available on Databricks for converting text into dense vector representations used in semantic search, clustering, and RAG pipelines.
* [Text vs Chat Prompt Types](/concepts/text-vs-chat-prompt-types.md) - MLflow Prompt Registry supports two prompt types: 'Text' for single completion-style templates and 'Chat' for role-based multi-message prompts targeting conversational models.
* [Textractor Pipeline (txtai)](/concepts/textractor-pipeline-txtai.md) - A txtai pipeline component for extracting text from documents and data sources, traceable via MLflow autolog
* [tf.data.TFRecordDataset](/concepts/tfdatatfrecorddataset.md) - A TensorFlow dataset class that reads records from TFRecord files, enabling efficient consumption of TFRecord data for training.
* [tf.distribute.Strategy](/concepts/tfdistributestrategy.md) - Core TensorFlow 2 API for distributing training across multiple devices and machines
* [tf.distribute.Strategy API](/concepts/tfdistributestrategy-api.md) - TensorFlow's native API for distributed training, recommended by Databricks as the replacement for HorovodRunner when using TensorFlow.
* [tf.distribute.Strategy for Distributed TensorFlow](/concepts/tfdistributestrategy-for-distributed-tensorflow.md) - Databricks-recommended replacement for Horovod when performing distributed training with TensorFlow.
* [TFRecord File Format](/concepts/tfrecord-file-format.md) - A simple record-oriented binary file format for storing ML training data, commonly used with TensorFlow.
* [TFRecord Format](/concepts/tfrecord-format.md) - A record-oriented binary format used by TensorFlow applications for training data, serving as a data source for distributed deep learning workflows.
* [Thinking Budget Tokens](/concepts/thinking-budget-tokens.md) - A configurable parameter (budget_tokens) that controls how many tokens the model can allocate to its internal reasoning/thinking process before generating the final answer.
* [Third-Party Client API Timeouts](/concepts/third-party-client-api-timeouts.md) - Timeouts caused by third-party client APIs (e.g., OpenAI Python client) used within custom PyFunc model pipelines, and how to configure their timeout parameters.
* [Third-Party Client API Timeouts in Model Pipelines](/concepts/third-party-client-api-timeouts.md) - Client-side timeouts caused by third-party API clients (e.g., OpenAI) used within custom PyFunc model pipelines, and configuration of their timeout parameters.
* [Third-Party Client API Timeouts in Model Serving Pipelines](/concepts/third-party-client-api-timeouts.md) - Client-side timeouts caused by third-party client APIs (e.g., OpenAI) used within custom PyFunc model pipelines, and how to configure their timeout parameters.
* [Third-Party Online Store Authentication for Feature Serving](/concepts/third-party-online-store-authentication-for-feature-serving.md) - Authentication methods (instance profiles and Databricks secrets) for publishing to and reading from third-party online stores in Databricks Feature Store on AWS.
* [Third-Party Online Stores for Feature Serving](/concepts/third-party-online-stores-for-feature-serving.md) - Databricks supports publishing feature tables to third-party online stores like Amazon DynamoDB (v0.3.8+), which must be configured with read-only credentials using Databricks secrets.
* [Third-Party OpenTelemetry Span Search](/concepts/third-party-opentelemetry-span-search.md) - Ability to search traces ingested from third-party OpenTelemetry tools (e.g., Langfuse) using the span.attributes.* prefix in filter strings.
* [Third-Party Scorers in MLflow GenAI](/concepts/third-party-scorers-in-mlflow-genai.md) - MLflow's extensibility mechanism allowing third-party evaluation scorers (like Arize Phoenix) to be used in GenAI evaluations
* [Three-Level Namespace](/concepts/three-level-namespace.md) - The catalog.schema.table naming convention used by Unity Catalog metastores to organize and reference data objects.
* [Three-level namespace (catalog.schema.table)](/concepts/three-level-namespace-catalogschematable.md) - The hierarchical naming convention in Unity Catalog that organizes data objects as catalog.schema.table, providing a structured way to manage and query data.
* [Three-Stage MLOps Environment Architecture](/concepts/three-stage-mlops-environment-architecture.md) - The recommended practice of creating separate execution environments (Development, Staging, Production) for ML code and model development with clearly defined transitions between stages.
* [Three-Step Alignment Workflow](/concepts/three-step-alignment-workflow.md) - The structured process for aligning judges: generate initial assessments, collect human feedback, then align and deploy using the align() method.
* [Throughput Plateau in Provisioned Throughput Systems](/concepts/throughput-plateau-in-provisioned-throughput-systems.md) - The phenomenon where endpoint throughput plateaus as concurrent requests increase beyond provisioned capacity, causing latency to grow unbounded while throughput stagnates.
* [Throughput vs Context Trade-off](/concepts/throughput-vs-context-trade-off.md) - The capacity trade-off where a fixed number of model units can handle either many small requests simultaneously or fewer long-context requests before exhausting capacity.
* [Time per Output Token (TPOT)](/concepts/time-per-output-token-tpot.md) - A performance metric measuring the time to generate each subsequent output token per user, directly influencing perceived model speed.
* [Time Series Aggregation](/concepts/time-series-aggregation.md) - Method for handling multiple values per timestamp in a time series, defaulting to averaging, with configurable options like sum.
* [Time Series Aggregation for Forecasting](/concepts/time-series-aggregation-for-forecasting.md) - The strategy used by Databricks AutoML to handle multiple values per timestamp in a time series, defaulting to averaging but configurable to summation via notebook source code edits.
* [Time Series Aggregation in AutoML Forecasting](/concepts/time-series-aggregation-in-automl-forecasting.md) - How AutoML handles multiple values at the same timestamp in a time series, defaulting to averaging and allowing customization to sum via notebook edits.
* [Time Series Cross-Validation](/concepts/time-series-cross-validation.md) - Chronological splitting method for forecasting data that incrementally extends the training set and validates on subsequent time points.
* [Time Series Cross-Validation for AutoML](/concepts/time-series-cross-validation-for-automl.md) - AutoML uses chronological time series cross-validation to split forecasting data into train, validation, and test sets, incrementally extending the training dataset over time.
* [Time Series Feature Engineering from Date Columns](/concepts/time-series-feature-engineering-from-date-columns.md) - Techniques for deriving predictive features such as weekend indicators from date/time columns to enrich forecasting models.
* [Time series feature publishing modes](/concepts/time-series-feature-publishing-modes.md) - Two strategies for publishing time series feature tables to online stores: snapshot mode (latest value per primary key) and window mode (all values within a time-to-live window, with automatic expiry).
* [Time series feature table](/concepts/time-series-feature-table.md) - A feature table that includes a TIMESTAMP primary key column, enabling point-in-time AS OF joins so that each training row uses the latest feature value prior to the label's timestamp.
* [Time Series Feature Table (Point-in-time Lookup)](/concepts/time-series-feature-table-point-in-time-lookup.md) - Feature tables that include a timestamp column to ensure training datasets use only feature values known up to the time of each target observation, preventing lookahead bias.
* [Time Series Feature Tables](/concepts/time-series-feature-tables.md) - Feature tables that include timestamp dimensions requiring timestamp lookup keys for point-in-time correct feature lookups in AutoML
* [Time Series Feature Tables in AutoML](/concepts/time-series-feature-tables.md) - For time series feature tables, AutoML requires a corresponding timestamp lookup key mapped to a timestamp column in the training dataset.
* [Time Series Identifier Columns](/concepts/time-series-identifier-columns.md) - Columns that identify individual time series in multi-series forecasting; Databricks groups data by these columns and trains a separate model per series.
* [Time Series Preprocessing in AutoML](/concepts/time-series-preprocessing-in-automl.md) - The automatic data preparation steps in Databricks Serverless Forecasting, including missing value imputation, data splitting, and one-hot encoding of categorical features.
* [Time Series Profile](/concepts/time-series-profile.md) - A data profiling analysis mode that computes metrics over time windows, limited to the last 30 days, useful for tracking data distribution changes across successive time periods.
* [Time Series vs Snapshot Profiling](/concepts/time-series-vs-snapshot-profiling.md) - Two analysis modes in data profiling: time series profiles compute metrics over rolling time windows (max 30 days) and support incremental processing, while snapshot profiles analyze a single point-in-state of a table (max 4TB).
* [Time to First Token (TTFT)](/concepts/time-to-first-token-ttft.md) - A latency metric measuring how quickly users see the first output token after submitting a query, driven by prompt processing and first token generation time.
* [Time Travel and Cloning Restrictions with ABAC](/concepts/time-travel-and-cloning-restrictions-with-abac.md) - Time travel queries and clone operations fail on tables with ABAC policies unless the principal is exempted via the EXCEPT clause
* [Time Travel on Streaming Tables](/concepts/time-travel-on-streaming-tables.md) - The ability to query historical versions of a streaming table, which is not supported when performing deep clone operations
* [Time Travel Restriction for Streaming Table Deep Clone](/concepts/time-travel-restriction-for-streaming-table-deep-clone.md) - Time travel (e.g., VERSION AS OF, TIMESTAMP AS OF) is not supported when deep cloning a streaming table
* [Time travel unsupported on streaming table deep clones](/concepts/time-travel-unsupported-on-streaming-table-deep-clones.md) - Time travel queries cannot be used in conjunction with deep clone operations on streaming tables in Databricks.
* [Time Window Aggregation in Data Profiling](/concepts/time-window-aggregation-in-data-profiling.md) - The mechanism by which Databricks profiling computes metrics over specified time intervals (windows), with Snapshot analysis using single points and TimeSeries/InferenceLog using granularity-based windows.
* [Time Window Aggregation Strategies](/concepts/time-window-aggregation-strategies.md) - Best practices and trade-offs for choosing window types (tumbling, sliding, rolling) and durations in feature engineering, balancing noise versus stability.
* [Time Window Aggregation Types](/concepts/time-window-aggregation-types.md) - Different window strategies (tumbling, sliding, rolling) for computing time-series aggregations with trade-offs between scalability and recency
* [Time Window Analysis Types](/concepts/time-window-analysis-types.md) - Three analysis modes (Snapshot, TimeSeries, InferenceLog) that determine how time intervals and granularities structure metric computation in data profiling.
* [Time Window for Unity Catalog Rollback](/concepts/time-window-for-unity-catalog-rollback.md) - The constraint that rolling back a managed table to external status is only supported within a limited number of days (numDays) after the migration to Unity Catalog managed table.
* [Time Window Granularity in Profiling](/concepts/time-window-granularity-in-profiling.md) - How profiling metrics are computed over configurable time intervals (windows) based on granularities specified in create_monitor, with Snapshot, TimeSeries, and InferenceLog analysis types.
* [Time Window Types](/concepts/time-window-types.md) - Three distinct lookback window semantics for time-series aggregations: Rolling (real-time, up-to-date), Tumbling (fixed non-overlapping partitions), and Sliding (overlapping windows with configurable slide interval).
* [Time Windows](/concepts/time-windows.md) - Three types of lookback windows (Rolling, Tumbling, Sliding) that control aggregation time ranges for feature computation in Databricks Feature Engineering
* [Time Windows for Aggregation](/concepts/time-windows.md) - Three types of time windows — rolling, tumbling, and sliding — that control lookback behavior for time-series aggregations in declarative features, each with distinct semantics for overlapping, non-overlapping, and real-time windows.
* [Time Windows for Feature Aggregation](/concepts/time-windows.md) - Three types of time windows (Rolling, Tumbling, Sliding) that control lookback behavior for time-window-based aggregations in declarative feature engineering.
* [TIME_TRAVEL_NOT_SUPPORTED](/concepts/time_travel_not_supported.md) - Sub-error indicating that time travel is not supported when performing a deep clone on a streaming table.
* [TIME_TRAVEL_NOT_SUPPORTED sub-error](/concepts/time_travel_not_supported-sub-error.md) - Sub-error indicating that time travel syntax (e.g., VERSION AS OF, TIMESTAMP AS OF) is incompatible with deep clone on streaming tables.
* [TIME_TRAVELLED_BY_TIMESTAMP error](/concepts/time_travelled_by_timestamp-error.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE indicating the source table used time travel by timestamp, which is incompatible with cloning with history.
* [TIME_TRAVELLED_BY_TIMESTAMP error condition](/concepts/time_travelled_by_timestamp-error.md) - A specific sub-error of DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE raised when the source table uses timestamp-based time travel, which is not supported for clone with history
* [TIME_TRAVELLED_BY_TIMESTAMP error sub-condition](/concepts/time_travelled_by_timestamp-error.md) - A specific error sub-condition under DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE, raised when the source table uses timestamp-based time travel.
* [TIME_TRAVELLED_BY_TIMESTAMP sub-error](/concepts/time_travelled_by_timestamp-error.md) - A specific sub-condition of DELTA_CLONE_WITH_HISTORY_UNSUPPORTED_SOURCE raised when the source table uses timestamp-based time travel, which is not supported for clone with history.
* [TIME_WINDOW_EXCEEDED](/concepts/time_window_exceeded.md) - Sub-error indicating the time window for rolling back a managed table to external has elapsed (beyond numDays)
* [TIME_WINDOW_EXCEEDED error](/concepts/time_window_exceeded-error.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_UNSET_MANAGED_FAILED indicating the rollback window (measured in days after migration to Unity Catalog) has expired.
* [Time-Based Feature Engineering for Forecasting](/concepts/time-based-feature-engineering-for-forecasting.md) - Creating derived temporal features such as is_weekend from date columns to improve forecasting model performance
* [Time-Series Feature Engineering for Covariates](/concepts/time-series-feature-engineering-for-covariates.md) - The process of deriving predictive features from time-series data, such as binary weekend indicators from date columns, to use as covariates.
* [Time-travelled source restriction for clone with history](/concepts/time-travelled-source-restriction-for-clone-with-history.md) - The error condition raised when cloning a Delta table with history from a source that was accessed via time travel by timestamp, which is unsupported.
* [Time-Windowed Aggregation Functions](/concepts/time-windowed-aggregation-functions.md) - Feature computation patterns using TumblingWindow, SlidingWindow, and RollingWindow for point-in-time aggregations over time-series data.
* [TimeSeries Profile](/concepts/timeseries-profile.md) - A data profile type that compares data distributions across time windows using a timestamp column and granularities
* [TimeSeries Profiling](/concepts/timeseries-profiling.md) - A data profiling type that partitions data into time-based windows using a timestamp column and metric granularities for trend analysis.
* [timestamp_lookup_key](/concepts/timestamp_lookup_key.md) - A parameter in FeatureLookup that specifies which DataFrame column contains the timestamps against which to perform point-in-time lookups on a time series feature table.
* [TO and EXCEPT Clauses for Principal Targeting](/concepts/to-and-except-clauses-for-principal-targeting.md) - Using TO and EXCEPT clauses in row filter and column mask policies to define which principals the policy applies to
* [TO and EXCEPT Clauses for Principal Targeting in ABAC](/concepts/to-and-except-clauses-for-principal-targeting-in-abac.md) - Using TO and EXCEPT clauses in row filter, column mask, and GRANT policies to define which users and groups policies apply to, keeping UDF logic simpler.
* [to_predict_fn for Deployed Endpoint Evaluation](/concepts/to_predict_fn-for-deployed-endpoint-evaluation.md) - Using mlflow.genai.to_predict_fn() to evaluate deployed Model Serving chat endpoints, Custom Agents, and custom endpoints.
* [TO/EXCEPT Principal Targeting in ABAC](/concepts/toexcept-principal-targeting-in-abac.md) - Using TO and EXCEPT clauses in row filter, column mask, and GRANT policies to define which users and groups a policy applies to
* [Token Credit-Back Mechanism](/concepts/token-credit-back-mechanism.md) - System that reserves output tokens against OTPM limits during pre-admission checking and credits back unused tokens from the actual response, making them immediately available for other requests.
* [Token Impact of Function Definitions](/concepts/token-impact-of-function-definitions.md) - The use of tool definitions increases input token consumption and billing because prompt injection and other techniques are applied to improve tool-call quality.
* [Token Management for OpenSharing Recipients](/concepts/token-management-for-opensharing-recipients.md) - For recipients using bearer token authentication (Databricks-to-Open sharing), providers can view/copy authentication links, rotate bearer tokens, and manage activation links. Tokens are invalidated when a recipient is deleted.
* [Token Processing Asymmetry](/concepts/token-processing-asymmetry.md) - The concept that generating output tokens is significantly more resource-intensive than processing input tokens during model inference.
* [Token Rotation and Security Management](/concepts/token-rotation-and-security-management.md) - The process of rotating a recipient's bearer token to generate a new token and activation URL, used when tokens are about to expire, compromised, or when security best practices require credential refresh.
* [Token Usage Aggregation at Trace Level in MLflow](/concepts/token-usage-aggregation-at-trace-level-in-mlflow.md) - Setting token counts (gen_ai.usage.input_tokens and gen_ai.usage.output_tokens) on the root span so MLflow aggregates them at the trace level for the UI summary.
* [Token Usage Impact of Function Calling](/concepts/token-usage-impact-of-function-calling.md) - Using function calling increases input and output token consumption due to prompt injection and tool definitions, directly affecting billing costs.
* [Token Usage in MLflow Traces](/concepts/token-usage-in-mlflow-traces.md) - Mechanism for tracking LLM token consumption (input, output, total tokens) within trace metadata and individual span attributes.
* [Token usage monitoring and optimization](/concepts/token-usage-monitoring-and-optimization.md) - Best practices for tracking input/output tokens, minimizing prompt length, controlling output via max_tokens, and logging token usage to avoid rate limits.
* [Token usage tracking across spans](/concepts/token-usage-tracking-across-spans.md) - Method for tracking and aggregating LLM token usage (input, output, total tokens) across individual spans and spans of type CHAT_MODEL in MLflow traces.
* [Token Usage Tracking in MLflow Traces](/concepts/token-usage-in-mlflow-traces.md) - Mechanism for tracking LLM token consumption via aggregated token counts (input, output, total) derived from provider APIs and span-level attributes.
* [Token Usage Tracking in MLflow Tracing](/concepts/token-usage-in-mlflow-traces.md) - Mechanism for tracking LLM token counts (input, output, total) within traces, with provider-specific methods for token tracking.
* [Token Usage Tracking in Tracing](/concepts/token-usage-tracking-in-tracing.md) - MLflow Tracing can track token usage from LLM provider APIs, exposing input_tokens, output_tokens, and total_tokens via trace metadata and span attributes.
* [Token-Based Rate Limiting (ITPM/OTPM)](/concepts/token-based-rate-limiting-itpmotpm.md) - Rate limits controlling the maximum number of input tokens per minute (ITPM) and output tokens per minute (OTPM) that can be processed within a 60-second window for pay-per-token endpoints.
* [Tokens Per Second as a Scaling Metric](/concepts/tokens-per-second-as-a-scaling-metric.md) - Using tokens per second (instead of concurrency) as the unit for configuring scale-out ranges for LLM serving endpoints, providing LLM-native scaling semantics.
* [Tool call evaluation judges](/concepts/tool-call-evaluation-judges.md) - Built-in LLM judges (ToolCallCorrectness, ToolCallEfficiency) that assess whether tool calls and arguments are correct for a user query, and whether they are made without redundancy.
* [Tool Choice Parameter](/concepts/tool-choice-parameter.md) - A configuration option ('auto', 'required', 'none', or a specific function name) that controls whether and which functions an LLM will invoke during a chat completion.
* [Tool Registration Pattern](/concepts/tool-registration-pattern.md) - The pattern of registering a tool with the LLM (for suggestion) and the execution agent (for execution) using register_for_llm and register_for_execution
* [Top-Tier Libraries](/concepts/top-tier-libraries.md) - A subset of Databricks Runtime ML libraries receiving faster updates, advanced support, testing, and embedded optimizations.
* [Top-tier Libraries in Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/top-tier-libraries.md) - A subset of libraries designated for faster update cadence, advanced support, testing, and embedded optimizations, updated with each runtime release.
* [Top-Tier Library Removal Criteria](/concepts/top-tier-library-removal-criteria.md) - Specific conditions that trigger removal of a library from the top-tier list: no commits in 2 months and no releases in 6 months, significant usage drop, or replacement by newer packages.
* [Top-Tier Library Support in Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/top-tier-libraries.md) - A subset of ML libraries in Databricks Runtime ML that receive faster update cadence, advanced support, testing, and embedded optimizations from Databricks.
* [Top-tier Library Support Policy](/concepts/top-tier-library-support-policy.md) - Databricks designates a subset of libraries as 'top-tier' with faster update cadence, advanced support, testing, and embedded optimizations, updated with each runtime release.
* [Top-Tier Removal and Re-addition Policy](/concepts/top-tier-removal-and-re-addition-policy.md) - Conditions under which Databricks may remove a library from top-tier status (2 months no commits, 6 months no releases, significant usage drop, or replacement by newer packages) and the possibility of reinstatement when active maintenance resumes.
* [TorchDistributor](/concepts/torchdistributor.md) - An open-source PySpark module that enables distributed PyTorch training on Spark clusters by initializing environment and communication channels via torch.distributed.run.
* [TorchDistributor (Databricks)](/concepts/deepspeed-distributor.md) - A base distributed training framework on Databricks for PyTorch models, upon which the DeepSpeed distributor is built.
* [TorchDistributor API Methods](/concepts/torchdistributor-api-methods.md) - The supported API methods for TorchDistributor, enabling launching PyTorch training as Spark jobs with parameters like num_processes, local_mode, and use_gpu
* [TorchDistributor Common Errors and Environment Variables](/concepts/torchdistributor-common-errors-and-environment-variables.md) - Troubleshooting guide for CUDA peer access, NCCL internal errors, and Gloo connection failures when using TorchDistributor
* [TorchDistributor Development Workflow](/concepts/torchdistributor-development-workflow.md) - A four-step workflow for converting single-node PyTorch training to distributed training using TorchDistributor: prepare single-node code, convert to distributed, move imports inside training function, and launch with distributor.run().
* [TorchDistributor for Distributed PyTorch](/concepts/torchdistributor-for-distributed-pytorch.md) - Databricks-recommended replacement for Horovod when performing distributed training with PyTorch.
* [TorchDistributor Foundation](/concepts/torchdistributor-foundation.md) - The DeepSpeed distributor is built on top of TorchDistributor, which is Databricks' native Spark-based PyTorch distributed training solution.
* [TorchDistributor Import Scoping for Pickling](/concepts/torchdistributor-import-scoping-for-pickling.md) - Pattern of placing import statements inside the training function to avoid pickling errors during distributed PyTorch training with TorchDistributor
* [TorchDistributor Migration from External Repositories](/concepts/torchdistributor-migration-from-external-repositories.md) - Process to migrate existing distributed PyTorch training code from external repositories to Databricks using Git folders and TorchDistributor.run(train_file).
* [TorchDistributor Requirements and Compatibility](/concepts/torchdistributor-requirements-and-compatibility.md) - TorchDistributor requires Spark 3.4 and Databricks Runtime 13.0 ML or above for compatibility.
* [TorchDistributor Troubleshooting: Gloo Connection Refused](/concepts/torchdistributor-troubleshooting-gloo-connection-refused.md) - Error when using Gloo backend for distributed CPU training, resolved by setting the GLOO_SOCKET_IFNAME=eth0 environment variable.
* [TorchDistributor Troubleshooting: Import Pickling Errors](/concepts/torchdistributor-troubleshooting-import-pickling-errors.md) - Common error when library imports are not distributed to executors; resolved by placing all import statements inside the training function passed to TorchDistributor.run().
* [TorchDistributor Troubleshooting: NCCL and GPU Communication Errors](/concepts/torchdistributor-troubleshooting-nccl-and-gpu-communication-errors.md) - CUDA peer access errors on G5 GPUs and NCCL internal errors in multi-node training, resolved by setting NCCL_P2P_DISABLE=1 and NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME=eth0 environment variables.
* [TorchRec EmbeddingBagCollection](/concepts/torchrec-embeddingbagcollection.md) - TorchRec module that manages multiple EmbeddingBag tables for large-scale categorical features, pooling sparse features into dense embeddings for downstream neural networks.
* [Torchrun Multi-Node Launcher](/concepts/torchrun-multi-node-launcher.md) - A PyTorch distributed launcher that reads rendezvous environment variables (NUM_NODES, NODE_RANK, LOCAL_WORLD_SIZE, MASTER_ADDR, MASTER_PORT) to launch one training process per GPU across multiple nodes without a separate launcher script.
* [total_row_count and daily_row_count Structs](/concepts/total_row_count-and-daily_row_count-structs.md) - Structs in the anomaly detection results table that track overall and daily row count metrics per table.
* [ToxicLanguage Scorer](/concepts/toxiclanguage-scorer.md) - A Guardrails AI scorer for toxicity detection in LLM outputs, configurable with threshold and validation method parameters.
* [Trace (MLflow Evaluation)](/concepts/trace-mlflow-evaluation.md) - A per-input record within an evaluation run that captures the input prompt, model output, and any associated feedback from scorers.
* [Trace Addition to Labeling Sessions](/concepts/trace-addition-to-labeling-sessions.md) - The workflow of selecting MLflow Traces (via UI or API) and adding them to a labeling session so domain experts can review and label them.
* [Trace Analysis and Debugging with Genie Code](/concepts/trace-analysis-and-debugging-with-genie-code.md) - Genie Code capability to investigate failing traces, find errors, examine span trees, pinpoint root causes, analyze latency, and identify bottlenecks in an agent's execution flow.
* [Trace Append Semantics](/concepts/trace-append-semantics.md) - The behavior where archived traces are appended to an existing Delta table rather than overwriting or replacing previous entries.
* [Trace Archival](/concepts/trace-archival.md) - The process of saving MLflow traces and their assessments to a Unity Catalog Delta table for long-term storage and advanced analysis.
* [Trace Archival Permissions](/concepts/trace-archival-permissions.md) - The requirement for users to have the necessary write permissions on the target Unity Catalog Delta table to enable trace archiving.
* [Trace Archiving to Delta Tables](/concepts/trace-archiving-to-delta-tables.md) - The ability to save MLflow traces and their associated assessments to a Unity Catalog Delta table for long-term storage and advanced analysis.
* [Trace Assessments](/concepts/trace-assessments.md) - Evaluations and feedback attached to traces, searchable by name, type (feedback/expectation), span ID, and source, with details like value, rationale, and metadata.
* [Trace Assessments and Feedback](/concepts/trace-assessments-and-feedback.md) - Logging human feedback, LLM judge evaluations, ground truth expectations, and span-specific assessments to MLflow traces using log_feedback() and log_expectation().
* [Trace Assessments and Feedback Logging](/concepts/trace-assessments-and-feedback.md) - Pattern for attaching human feedback, LLM judge evaluations, ground truth expectations, and span-specific assessments to traces using mlflow.log_feedback() and mlflow.log_expectation().
* [Trace Context Enrichment (MLflow)](/concepts/trace-context-enrichment-mlflow.md) - The practice of adding metadata and tags to MLflow traces to track execution details, analyze user behavior, debug issues across environments, and monitor application performance.
* [Trace Context Metadata](/concepts/trace-context-metadata.md) - The practice of adding standard and custom metadata fields (tags and metadata) to MLflow traces to track execution details, user behavior, and environment information.
* [Trace Context Metadata in MLflow](/concepts/trace-context-metadata.md) - Adding standardized and custom metadata/tags to MLflow traces for tracking execution details, user behavior, and application performance
* [Trace Data Export and Conversion](/concepts/trace-data-export-and-conversion.md) - MLflow traces support export and conversion to multiple formats including Python dictionaries (to_dict), JSON strings (to_json), and pandas DataFrame rows (to_pandas_dataframe_row), with corresponding from_dict/from_json reconstruction methods.
* [Trace Data Export and Serialization](/concepts/trace-data-export-and-serialization.md) - Methods for converting MLflow Trace objects to dictionaries, JSON strings, and Pandas DataFrame rows for data portability and analysis.
* [Trace Data Serialization and Export](/concepts/trace-data-serialization-and-export.md) - Methods for converting Trace objects to dictionaries, JSON strings, or Pandas DataFrame rows, and reconstructing them from serialized formats.
* [Trace Debugging Workflows in MLflow](/concepts/trace-debugging-workflows-in-mlflow.md) - Common observability and debugging patterns using the MLflow Tracing UI, including identifying slow traces (sort by execution time), finding user-specific traces (filters/tags), locating error traces (filter by State=ERROR), and identifying negative feedback traces (assessment filters).
* [Trace Grouping by Users and Sessions](/concepts/trace-grouping-by-users-and-sessions.md) - MLflow supports grouping per-request traces by user sessions to maintain multi-turn interaction context and help understand end-user journeys across requests.
* [Trace Input/Output Tracking](/concepts/inputoutput-tracking.md) - The practice of capturing inputs, outputs, and metadata for each intermediate step (retrieval, tool calls, LLM interactions) in a GenAI application to enable debugging and analysis.
* [Trace Lifecycle (start/end span pattern)](/concepts/trace-lifecycle-startend-span-pattern.md) - Every trace follows a strict lifecycle where start_trace and start_span calls must have corresponding end_trace or end_span calls, managed in LIFO order.
* [Trace Logging Redirection](/concepts/trace-logging-redirection.md) - The step of stopping writes to the source experiment and redirecting trace logging to a new Unity Catalog-backed destination experiment before running migration, preventing data loss.
* [Trace Management for Labeling Sessions](/concepts/trace-management-for-labeling-sessions.md) - The process of selecting, adding, and retrieving MLflow Traces within labeling sessions for expert review, supporting both UI and API workflows.
* [Trace Metadata](/concepts/trace-metadata.md) - Immutable write-once key-value pairs attached to MLflow traces for fixed information such as model version, environment, or system configuration.
* [Trace Metadata (MLflow)](/concepts/trace-metadata.md) - Immutable, write-once key-value pairs attached to MLflow traces for fixed information like model version or environment; cannot be updated after logging.
* [Trace Metadata Filters](/concepts/trace-metadata-filters.md) - Structured search queries in the MLflow UI that allow filtering traces by metadata fields such as user, session, source type, and app version using a SQL-like syntax on metadata fields.
* [Trace Metadata in MLflow](/concepts/trace-metadata.md) - Write-once, immutable key-value pairs attached to MLflow traces during execution for fixed information such as model version or environment.
* [Trace Performance Profiling](/concepts/trace-performance-profiling.md) - Techniques for analyzing trace execution time distributions, computing percentile latencies (P50, P95, P99), and identifying performance outliers.
* [Trace Query Performance Optimization](/concepts/trace-query-performance-optimization.md) - Techniques for diagnosing and improving slow trace queries including materialized views, SQL warehouse sizing, and filtering strategies.
* [Trace Return Types](/concepts/trace-return-types.md) - mlflow.search_traces() returns results as either pandas DataFrames or lists of MLflow Trace objects, enabling further analysis or evaluation dataset creation.
* [Trace Reuse Pattern](/concepts/trace-reuse-pattern.md) - Technique of passing a Pandas DataFrame of previously stored traces directly to mlflow.genai.evaluate() to enable rapid iteration on scorers without re-running the full application.
* [Trace Search Query Syntax](/concepts/trace-search-query-syntax.md) - SQL-like filter language used with mlflow.search_traces() to filter traces by fields like status, execution time, tags, and metadata.
* [Trace Selection Strategies](/concepts/trace-selection-strategies.md) - Quantitative and qualitative methods for identifying representative traces from production logs to curate evaluation datasets, including filtering by quality scores, token usage, latency, and manual review of patterns.
* [Trace span hierarchy](/concepts/trace-span-hierarchy.md) - The parent-child structure of spans within an MLflow trace, where a root span (e.g., an application entry point function) can contain child spans (e.g., individual LLM API calls), providing hierarchical visibility into application execution.
* [Trace span hierarchy (root and child spans)](/concepts/trace-span-hierarchy.md) - The tree structure of MLflow traces where a root span (e.g., the application entry point) contains child spans (e.g., individual LLM calls), enabling multi-step visibility.
* [Trace Span Requirements for MLflow Judges](/concepts/mlflow-trace-requirements-for-llm-judges.md) - MLflow judges require specific trace span configurations: RelevanceToQuery needs inputs and outputs on the root span, while RetrievalRelevance requires at least one span with span_type set to RETRIEVER.
* [Trace Spans](/concepts/trace-spans.md) - Immutable building blocks representing individual operations within a trace, with properties including span_id, name, type, timing, status, inputs/outputs, and parent-child relationships.
* [Trace Spans (Root and Child)](/concepts/trace-spans-root-and-child.md) - The hierarchical span structure in MLflow Tracing where a root span captures the top-level function call (e.g., app entry point) and child spans capture sub-operations (e.g., individual LLM API calls), forming a tree of execution.
* [Trace State Management Across Application Components](/concepts/trace-state-management-across-application-components.md) - Pattern for managing active trace state using a stack-based approach, allowing nested traces and clean propagation across application components.
* [Trace Structure Control](/concepts/trace-structure-control.md) - The ability to define exactly which code segments are traced, create custom span hierarchies, and control span boundaries and relationships in GenAI applications.
* [Trace Tags](/concepts/trace-tags.md) - Mutable key-value pairs attached to MLflow traces for organization, search, and filtering; can be updated after logging.
* [Trace Tags (MLflow)](/concepts/trace-tags.md) - Mutable key-value pairs attached to MLflow traces for organization, search, and filtering; can be added or updated post-logging.
* [Trace Tags and Metadata](/concepts/trace-tags-and-metadata.md) - Two distinct annotation systems on traces: mutable tags (user-defined, updatable post-creation) and immutable trace_metadata (set at creation); used for environment, user ID, and other contextual information.
* [Trace Tags in MLflow](/concepts/trace-tags.md) - Mutable key-value pairs attached to MLflow traces for organization, search, and filtering, updatable during or after trace execution.
* [Trace Unified View Pattern](/concepts/trace-unified-view-pattern.md) - A join pattern that aggregates OTel spans, annotations, and tags into a single denormalized trace record, enabling unified querying across redacted trace data.
* [Trace-Associated Feedback Collection](/concepts/trace-associated-feedback-collection.md) - The pattern of linking user feedback to specific MLflow traces using either MLflow-generated trace IDs or custom client request IDs, enabling feedback to be permanently associated with interactions.
* [Trace-Based Dataset Creation](/concepts/trace-based-dataset-curation.md) - The practice of building evaluation datasets by selecting and merging existing MLflow traces (real historical application interactions) using the UI or SDK, enabling datasets grounded in production scenarios.
* [Trace-Based Dataset Curation](/concepts/trace-based-dataset-curation.md) - The practice of building evaluation datasets by selecting and curating traces from an application's historical interactions captured by MLflow Tracing, using either the UI or SDK.
* [Trace-based Evaluation Datasets](/concepts/trace-based-evaluation-datasets.md) - Using archived traces to build evaluation datasets for MLflow evaluation, enabling reuse of production trace data for model assessment.
* [Trace-based Evaluation Iteration](/concepts/trace-based-evaluation-iteration.md) - Technique of passing a Pandas DataFrame of pre-computed MLflow traces directly to mlflow.genai.evaluate() to iterate on scorers without rerunning the application.
* [Trace-Based Evaluation via Expert Review](/concepts/trace-based-evaluation-via-expert-review.md) - The practice of having domain experts label existing production traces to understand high-quality responses, align LLM judges with business requirements, and create evaluation datasets from real interactions.
* [Trace-based Feature Flag Analysis](/concepts/trace-based-feature-flag-analysis.md) - Comparing performance metrics of traces grouped by feature flag states (control vs treatment) by filtering traces on metadata tags with mlflow.search_traces().
* [Trace-Based Feedback Loop](/concepts/trace-based-feedback-loop.md) - The mechanism of using MLflow Traces to store both judge assessments and human feedback, which are then retrieved and used as training data for judge alignment.
* [Trace-based Judges](/concepts/trace-based-judges.md) - A type of custom judge that analyzes execution traces to validate tool calls, enabled by including {{ trace }} in judge instructions.
* [Trace-Based Performance and Cost Monitoring](/concepts/trace-based-performance-and-cost-monitoring.md) - Using MLflow Tracing to capture and monitor latency, cost, and resource utilization at each step of GenAI application execution to identify bottlenecks and optimize efficiency.
* [Trace-Driven Evaluation Datasets](/concepts/trace-driven-evaluation-datasets.md) - Practice of using archived trace data to construct MLflow evaluation datasets for further analysis and monitoring.
* [Trace-level Input/Output Rendering in MLflow](/concepts/trace-level-inputoutput-rendering-in-mlflow.md) - Using gen_ai.input.messages and gen_ai.output.messages attributes with JSON-serialized message arrays for rich UI rendering with role-labeled bubbles.
* [TraceAnalyzer Pattern](/concepts/traceanalyzer-pattern.md) - A reusable object-oriented utility class for advanced trace analysis, providing methods for error summaries, LLM usage aggregation, retrieval metrics, span hierarchy visualization, and evaluation data export.
* [TraceAnalyzer Reusable Utility Class](/concepts/traceanalyzer-reusable-utility-class.md) - Advanced reusable Python class for trace analysis providing error summary, LLM usage aggregation, retrieval metrics extraction, span hierarchy building, and evaluation data export.
* [Traced Gemini Interactions](/concepts/traced-gemini-interactions.md) - MLflow supports tracing multiple types of Gemini SDK interactions: synchronous text generation, multi-turn chat conversations, and embeddings API calls.
* [TraceData](/concepts/tracedata.md) - Execution data component of a trace containing spans, full request/response payloads, and intermediate outputs.
* [TraceData (MLflow)](/concepts/tracedata.md) - Execution data component of an MLflow Trace, containing spans, full request/response data, and intermediate outputs from non-root spans.
* [TraceData and Span Objects](/concepts/tracedata-and-span-objects.md) - TraceData holds the actual execution spans, each with properties like span_id, name, type, parent_id, timing, status, inputs/outputs, and attributes.
* [TraceData and Spans](/concepts/tracedata.md) - TraceData holds the execution data of a trace including spans (individual operations), full request/response data, and intermediate outputs from non-root spans.
* [TraceInfo](/concepts/traceinfo.md) - Metadata component of a trace containing identifiers, status, timing, tags, token usage, and assessments.
* [TraceInfo (MLflow)](/concepts/traceinfo.md) - Metadata component of an MLflow Trace, containing trace ID, status (OK/ERROR/IN_PROGRESS), request/response previews, storage location, timing, tags, token usage, and assessments.
* [TraceInfo Metadata](/concepts/traceinfo-metadata.md) - TraceInfo contains metadata about a trace including trace ID, request/response previews, timing information, tags, trace metadata (immutable), token usage, assessments, and storage location.
* [Traces](/concepts/traces.md) - Execution logs capturing inputs, outputs, and every intermediate step (LLM calls, retrievals, tool use) of a GenAI application, used for debugging, monitoring, and creating evaluation datasets.
* [Traces (MLflow GenAI)](/concepts/traces.md) - Execution logs that capture the full lifecycle of a GenAI application call, including inputs, outputs, and intermediate steps like LLM calls and retrievals.
* [Traces in MLflow Evaluation](/concepts/traces-in-mlflow-evaluation.md) - Per-input evaluation records within an evaluation run, each containing inputs, outputs, and associated feedback for one example in the evaluation dataset.
* [Traces-Based Evaluation Results](/concepts/traces-based-evaluation-results.md) - Evaluation results in MLflow 3 are stored as traces with assessments, accessed via mlflow.search_traces() instead of direct result tables like results.tables['eval_results'].
* [Tracing Approach Selection Guide](/concepts/tracing-approach-selection-guide.md) - Recommendation to start with automatic tracing and add manual tracing later when more control is needed
* [Tracing for Generative AI Applications](/concepts/tracing-for-generative-ai-applications.md) - The practice of adding trace instrumentation to generative AI applications for observability and debugging.
* [Tracing Limitations for Groq](/concepts/tracing-limitations-for-groq.md) - Only synchronous Groq SDK calls are traced; async and streaming methods are not supported
* [Tracking Additional Content with Autologging](/concepts/tracking-additional-content-with-autologging.md) - The pattern and best practice for adding extra metrics, parameters, files, and metadata to MLflow runs created by Databricks Autologging by calling mlflow.autolog() with exclusive=False and wrapping training in an mlflow.start_run() context.
* [Tracking URI in MLflow](/concepts/tracking-uri-in-mlflow.md) - Configuration parameter that specifies the location of the MLflow tracking server, used to direct experiment logging to a specific server instance.
* [Traffic Routing Across Multiple Models on a Single Endpoint](/concepts/traffic-routing-across-multiple-models-on-a-single-endpoint.md) - The ability to serve multiple custom models or model versions from a single serving endpoint, with configurable traffic-split percentages between served entities.
* [Traffic Splitting Between Models](/concepts/traffic-splitting-between-models.md) - Configuring percentage-based distribution of inference requests across different models hosted on the same serving endpoint.
* [train_on_responses_only in Unsloth](/concepts/train_on_responses_only-in-unsloth.md) - A technique that masks out user/instruction tokens during training so the model only learns from assistant responses, improving fine-tuning quality for chat models.
* [train.yaml Workload Configuration](/concepts/trainyaml-workload-configuration.md) - A YAML configuration file used with the AI Runtime CLI to define machine learning workloads with inline dependencies.
* [Training and Inference API](/concepts/training-and-inference-api.md) - Client methods for creating point-in-time correct training sets (create_training_set), logging models with feature lineage (log_model), and performing batch inference (score_batch)
* [Training and Inference API Methods](/concepts/training-and-inference-api-methods.md) - Core methods for ML lifecycle: create_training_set (point-in-time correct feature computation), log_model (model logging with feature lineage), and score_batch (offline batch inference with automatic feature lookup).
* [Training and Inference API with Point-in-Time Correctness](/concepts/training-and-inference-api-with-point-in-time-correctness.md) - API methods (create_training_set, log_model, score_batch) that ensure point-in-time correct feature computation, lineage tracking, and automatic feature lookup during batch inference.
* [Training and Inference Lifecycle](/concepts/training-and-inference-lifecycle.md) - End-to-end ML workflow using declarative features: create_training_set for point-in-time correct datasets, log_model for lineage tracking, and score_batch for offline batch inference with automatic feature lookup.
* [Training and Inference Pipeline API](/concepts/training-and-inference-pipeline-api.md) - End-to-end ML pipeline API with create_training_set() for point-in-time correct feature computation, log_model() for lineage tracking, and score_batch() for batch inference with automatic feature lookup.
* [Training Configuration with Hugging Face TrainingArguments](/concepts/training-configuration-with-hugging-face-trainingarguments.md) - Setting up TrainingArguments including batch size, precision, evaluation strategy, and gradient accumulation for single-GPU fine-tuning
