* [Training Script Pattern with MLflow Tracking on Databricks](/concepts/training-script-pattern-with-mlflow-tracking-on-databricks.md) - Pattern for training a PyTorch model on Databricks that includes MLflow experiment logging, Unity Catalog checkpointing, and single-GPU training with SGD optimizer.
* [Training Set (Feature Store)](/concepts/training-set-feature-store.md) - A dataset composed of a list of features and a DataFrame containing raw training data, labels, and primary keys used to look up features during model training.
* [Training workflow with on-demand features](/concepts/training-workflow-with-on-demand-features.md) - End-to-end process for training ML models that incorporate on-demand features, including create_training_set, log_model, and Unity Catalog registration.
* [Training workload YAML format](/concepts/training-workload-yaml-format.md) - Configuration file format (train.yaml) that defines the distributed training job parameters for the AI Runtime CLI
* [Training/Serving Skew Elimination](/concepts/trainingserving-skew-elimination.md) - Automatic alignment of feature computations between model training and inference to prevent performance degradation caused by inconsistent feature engineering.
* [TrainingArguments Configuration](/concepts/trainingarguments-configuration.md) - Configuration of Hugging Face TrainingArguments for fine-tuning, including output directory, evaluation strategy, learning rate, and memory optimization settings.
* [TrainingArguments for Hugging Face](/concepts/trainingarguments-for-hugging-face.md) - A configuration class in the transformers library used to specify training parameters such as output directory, evaluation strategy, learning rate, batch size, and mixed precision training.
* [TrainValidationSplit](/concepts/trainvalidationsplit.md) - A Spark MLlib model tuning tool that, when used with MLlib automated MLflow tracking, automatically logs hyperparameter settings and evaluation metrics as nested MLflow runs.
* [Transfer Learning](/concepts/transfer-learning.md) - A machine learning technique that reuses knowledge from a pre-trained model on one problem domain to improve learning in a related domain, with featurization being one simple and powerful method for achieving it.
* [Transfer Learning Workflow on Databricks](/concepts/transfer-learning-workflow-on-databricks.md) - A four-step workflow: start with a pre-trained model, truncate its last layers, apply the modified model to a new dataset to compute features, then train a downstream model using those features.
* [Transformer Block Auto-Detection for FSDP Wrapping](/concepts/transformer-block-auto-detection-for-fsdp-wrapping.md) - A technique for automatically identifying transformer decoder layer class names in a model to configure FSDP's layer wrapping policy for sharding.
* [TrialInfo](/concepts/trialinfo.md) - Summary object for each individual trial in an AutoML run, with a method to load the model generated for that trial.
* [TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning)](/concepts/trl-transformer-reinforcement-learning.md) - A Hugging Face library for training language models using reinforcement learning and supervised fine-tuning, providing SFTTrainer and other abstractions.
* [TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning) Library](/concepts/trl-transformer-reinforcement-learning.md) - A Hugging Face library for training language models with reinforcement learning and supervised fine-tuning, providing SFTTrainer and SFTConfig for simplified training configuration.
* [TRL (Transformers Reinforcement Learning)](/concepts/trl-transformer-reinforcement-learning.md) - A Hugging Face library providing tools for training language models with reinforcement learning and supervised fine-tuning
* [TRL (Transformers Reinforcement Learning) SFTTrainer](/concepts/trl-transformer-reinforcement-learning.md) - A library and trainer class from Hugging Face for supervised fine-tuning of transformer models, supporting integration with LoRA, FSDP, and distributed training.
* [TRL SFTTrainer](/concepts/trl-sfttrainer.md) - A library component from Transformer Reinforcement Learning (TRL) that simplifies supervised fine-tuning configuration, integrates with LoRA via PEFT, and supports Liger kernel optimizations.
* [TRL SFTTrainer for Supervised Fine-Tuning](/concepts/trl-sfttrainer-for-supervised-fine-tuning.md) - A trainer from the Transformers Reinforcement Learning library that simplifies supervised fine-tuning of large language models with built-in support for LoRA, FSDP, and distributed training.
* [Troubleshooting ABAC Policy Conflicts](/concepts/troubleshooting-abac-policy-conflicts.md) - Diagnostic approach using SHOW EFFECTIVE POLICIES and INFORMATION_SCHEMA views to identify conflicts, with resolution strategies including refining conditions, adjusting principals, and restructuring policies.
* [Troubleshooting Ray on Databricks](/concepts/troubleshooting-ray-on-databricks.md) - Common issues and solutions when running Ray on Databricks, including pickling errors, OOM memory monitor bugs, transformers MLflow integration conflicts, and library scoping limitations.
* [TruLens Scorers Integration](/concepts/trulens-scorers-integration.md) - MLflow integration with TruLens for analyzing agent execution traces with goal-plan-action alignment metrics like logical consistency and plan adherence
* [Truncated Model Feature Extraction](/concepts/truncated-model-feature-extraction.md) - The technique of removing the final classification layers from a pre-trained deep learning model so the modified model outputs a feature tensor instead of a prediction.
* [Truncated Table History Affecting Rollback](/concepts/truncated-table-history-affecting-rollback.md) - A condition where the Delta table history has been truncated (e.g., via VACUUM or retention policies), making it impossible to find all necessary commits to rollback a table to its original state.
* [Truncated Table History and Rollback Failure in Delta Lake](/concepts/truncated-table-history-and-rollback-failure-in-delta-lake.md) - When Delta table history is truncated (e.g., VACUUM or log cleanup), the necessary commits to rollback a managed-to-external migration may be missing, causing TRUNCATED_HISTORY error.
* [TRUNCATED_HISTORY](/concepts/truncated_history.md) - Sub-error indicating the Delta table history has been truncated, making rollback from managed to external impossible
* [TRUNCATED_HISTORY rollback error](/concepts/truncated_history.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_UNSET_MANAGED_FAILED indicating the Delta table history has been truncated, preventing the system from finding all necessary commits to rollback.
* [Trust Relationship for Serverless Compute](/concepts/trust-relationship-for-serverless-compute.md) - The IAM role associated with an instance profile must have a trust relationship configured for Databricks serverless compute to avoid errors when attaching the profile to a serving endpoint.
* [Tuple-count vs size-on-disk balancing](/concepts/tuple-count-vs-size-on-disk-balancing.md) - The distinction between optimizations that balance files by number of records (Z-ordering) versus those that balance by byte size (bin-packing), and the implications for task skew.
* [TurnRelevancy Scorer](/concepts/turnrelevancy-scorer.md) - A conversational DeepEval scorer that evaluates relevance in multi-turn dialogue, supporting parameters like window_size and strict_mode.
* [Two-Step Connection Establishment](/concepts/two-step-connection-establishment.md) - Creating a Databricks–SAP BDC connection requires a two-party workflow: the Databricks admin shares a connection identifier with the SAP BDC admin, who generates a connection link that the Databricks admin uses to finalize the connection.
* [Two-Tower Models for Recommendation Systems](/concepts/two-tower-models-for-recommendation-systems.md) - Deep learning approach for building recommender systems using dual neural network architectures (two-tower models) to represent users and items.
* [Two-Tower Models for Recommender Systems](/concepts/two-tower-models-for-recommender-systems.md) - A deep learning architecture for recommendation systems that uses separate neural networks (towers) to encode users and items into a shared embedding space.
* [Two-Tower Recommendation Model](/concepts/two-tower-recommendation-model.md) - A deep learning architecture for recommendation systems that uses two separate neural network towers (one for queries/users, one for items/candidates) to learn embeddings for matching.
* [Two-Tower Recommender System](/concepts/two-tower-recommender-system.md) - A recommendation model architecture demonstrated as a use case for distributed training with DDP on Databricks
* [Two-tower recommender system training](/concepts/two-tower-recommender-system-with-ddp.md) - A recommendation model architecture trained using PyTorch Lightning and DDP on Databricks, with data prepared in Mosaic Streaming format.
* [Two-tower recommender system with DDP](/concepts/two-tower-recommender-system-with-ddp.md) - Training a two-tower recommendation model using PyTorch Lightning and DDP on Databricks, including data preparation with Mosaic Streaming (MDS) format.
* [Two-Tower Recommender Training with DDP](/concepts/two-tower-recommender-system-with-ddp.md) - Distributed training of a two-tower recommendation model using PyTorch Lightning DDP, including data preparation with Mosaic Streaming (MDS) format
* [txtai](/concepts/txtai.md) - An all-in-one embeddings database for semantic search, LLM orchestration, and language model workflows
* [txtai Agent](/concepts/txtai-agent.md) - An agent framework in txtai that uses tools (like semantic search) and LLMs to autonomously research and answer questions, traceable via MLflow
* [Type Widening inDelta-Iceberg Compatibility](/concepts/type-widening-indelta-iceberg-compatibility.md) - Type changes applied to a Delta table's schema (e.g., changing a column type) can be incompatible with IcebergCompatV and trigger a violation.
* [Typed Dataset APIs in Databricks Connect](/concepts/typed-dataset-apis-in-databricks-connect.md) - Support for running typed transformations like map(), filter(), mapPartitions() and aggregations via Databricks Connect for Scala, requiring compiled artifacts to be uploaded to the remote cluster.
* [U2M (User-to-Machine) Authentication Flow](/concepts/user-to-machine-u2m-authentication.md) - Authentication flow where a human user authenticates via their IdP (with optional MFA) and uses tools like Power BI or Tableau to access shared data, using the OAuth Authorization Code Grant flow.
* [UC-only workspaces](/concepts/uc-only-workspaces.md) - New Databricks workspaces provisioned without legacy features such as DBFS root, Hive metastore, no-isolation shared clusters, and old Databricks Runtime versions.
* [UCVolumeDataset](/concepts/ucvolumedataset.md) - A PyTorch IterableDataset that streams unstructured data (images, audio, text) from Unity Catalog volumes with automatic local caching and distributed partitioning.
* [UCVolumeDataset and UCVolumeWriter](/concepts/ucvolumedataset-and-ucvolumewriter.md) - Databricks utilities for streaming file-based data from Unity Catalog volumes and checkpointing distributed training across ranks.
* [UCX (Unity Catalog Migration Toolkit)](/concepts/ucx-unity-catalog-migration-toolkit.md) - A Databricks Labs open-source project providing tools and workflows to upgrade non-Unity-Catalog workspaces to Unity Catalog on Databricks.
* [UCX (Unity Catalog Upgrade Utilities)](/concepts/ucx-unity-catalog-upgrade-utilities.md) - A Databricks Labs open-source project providing automation tools to help upgrade non-Unity-Catalog workspaces to Unity Catalog at scale.
* [UCX Assessment Workflow](/concepts/ucx-assessment-workflow.md) - The first UCX migration workflow that assesses Unity Catalog compatibility of groups, storage, access controls, and tables, producing an assessment dashboard and Delta tables for migration planning.
* [UCX Group Migration Workflow](/concepts/ucx-group-migration-workflow.md) - A UCX workflow that upgrades workspace-local groups to account-level groups to support Unity Catalog, replicating permissions and removing unnecessary groups.
* [UCX Table Migration Workflow](/concepts/ucx-table-migration-workflow.md) - A UCX workflow that upgrades tables from the Hive metastore to the Unity Catalog metastore, using SYNC for external tables and DEEP CLONE for managed tables.
* [UDF Dependency Management on Databricks Connect](/concepts/dependency-management-in-databricks-connect.md) - The pattern where application dependencies are installed locally while UDF dependencies must be separately managed and installed on the Databricks cluster for remote execution.
* [UDF Dependency Sources](/concepts/udf-dependency-sources.md) - Dependencies for Databricks Connect UDFs can come from three sources: PyPI packages (PEP 508), Unity Catalog volumes (dbfs: paths), and local files (local: paths including .whl, .tar.gz, folders, and .py files).
* [UDF Execution in Databricks Connect](/concepts/udf-execution-in-databricks-connect.md) - User-defined functions (UDFs) defined locally are serialized and transmitted to the Databricks cluster for execution, requiring UDF dependencies to be installed on Databricks while application dependencies run locally.
* [UDF Serialization in Databricks Connect](/concepts/udf-serialization-in-databricks-connect.md) - User-defined functions are serialized by the Databricks Connect client and sent to the Databricks compute server for execution, requiring matching Python versions between client and server.
* [UDF-based Policy Logic](/concepts/udf-based-policy-logic.md) - ABAC row filter and column mask policies rely on user-defined functions (UDFs) in Unity Catalog that implement the filtering or masking logic, requiring EXECUTE permission on the UDF.
* [Unavailable Databricks Utilities Subset](/concepts/unavailable-databricks-utilities-subset.md) - Certain Databricks Utilities (credentials, library, notebook workflow, widgets) are not available in Databricks Connect for Scala.
* [Unified Development-Production Tracing](/concepts/unified-development-production-tracing.md) - A design principle where tracing instrumentation works consistently across development (IDE, notebooks) and production environments without reconfiguration.
* [Unified Development-to-Production Tracing](/concepts/unified-development-to-production-tracing.md) - MLflow Tracing offers a single instrumentation that works consistently across development (IDE, notebook) and production environments, eliminating the need to switch between multiple tools.
* [Unified ML Platform on Databricks](/concepts/unified-ml-platform-on-databricks.md) - Databricks provides a single platform for the entire ML workflow: data pipelines, feature engineering, model training, batch inference, model serving, and monitoring.
* [Unified OpenAI-compatible API (Databricks)](/concepts/unified-openai-compatible-api-databricks.md) - The standard, provider-agnostic API interface on Databricks Model Serving, contrasted with provider native APIs which offer access to provider-specific features.
* [Unified Streaming and Batch Processing](/concepts/unified-streaming-and-batch-processing.md) - Delta Lake enables a unified approach to stream and batch data processing, allowing users to treat streaming and batch data pipelines with the same table abstractions and semantics.
* [Unified streaming and batch processing in Delta Lake](/concepts/unified-streaming-and-batch-processing-in-delta-lake.md) - Delta Lake's capability to unify streaming and batch data processing under a single storage layer, simplifying data pipeline architectures.
* [Uniform (Apache Iceberg) Format](/concepts/uniform-apache-iceberg-format.md) - A Databricks feature that makes Delta tables readable as Apache Iceberg tables, requiring all files to be Iceberg-compatible and enforced via IcebergCompatV.
* [Uniform (Apache Iceberg) format in Databricks](/concepts/uniform-apache-iceberg-format.md) - A Databricks feature that makes Delta Lake tables readable as Apache Iceberg tables; enabling it requires all files to be Iceberg-compatible and triggers the DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_VIOLATION if files lack compatibility tags.
* [Uniform (Delta-Uniform)](/concepts/delta-uniform.md) - A Databricks feature that makes Delta Lake tables readable by Apache Iceberg clients by storing Iceberg metadata alongside Delta metadata.
* [Uniform Apache Iceberg Ingress Table](/concepts/uniform-apache-iceberg-ingress-table.md) - A Databricks table type that allows ingesting Iceberg metadata into Delta format, supporting only CREATE and REFRESH operations.
* [Uniform Iceberg Ingress Table](/concepts/uniform-ingress-table.md) - A Delta Uniform table that ingests Apache Iceberg data, supporting only CREATE and REFRESH operations; violations of this constraint raise the OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED error.
* [Uniform Ingress Table](/concepts/uniform-ingress-table.md) - A Delta table configured to act as an ingress point for Apache Iceberg data, supporting only CREATE and REFRESH operations
* [Union of Direct Grants and GRANT Policies](/concepts/union-of-direct-grants-and-grant-policies.md) - Effective privileges on an object are the union of direct grants and any applicable GRANT policies, meaning a more selective GRANT policy does not override a broader direct grant.
* [Unit Testing with Databricks Feature Engineering Clients](/concepts/unit-testing-with-feature-engineering-clients.md) - Using mock frameworks (e.g., unittest.mock) to unit-test code that calls FeatureEngineeringClient or FeatureStoreClient APIs locally.
* [Unit Testing with Feature Engineering Clients](/concepts/unit-testing-with-feature-engineering-clients.md) - The ability to install databricks-feature-engineering or databricks-feature-store locally to unit test code using mock frameworks like unittest.mock for validating feature table operations.
* [Unity AI Gateway](/concepts/unity-ai-gateway.md) - An enterprise control plane for governing AI traffic across an organization, managing and monitoring LLM endpoints and MCP servers centrally.
* [Unity Catalog](/concepts/unity-catalog.md) - The unified governance layer for data and AI in Databricks, providing centralized access control, lineage, auditing, and data discovery across workspaces.
* [Unity Catalog ABAC (Attribute-Based Access Control)](/concepts/unity-catalog-attribute-based-access-control-abac.md) - Centralized, tag-driven policies that dynamically filter and mask data across the catalog, recommended by Databricks over per-table filters for scalability.
* [Unity Catalog ABAC Column Mask Policies](/concepts/unity-catalog-abac-column-mask-policies.md) - Attribute-based access control policies that mask column values by applying a UDF to matched columns, identified via governed tags or custom expressions using has_tag and has_tag_value.
* [Unity Catalog ABAC Row Filter Policies](/concepts/unity-catalog-abac-row-filter-policies.md) - Attribute-based access control policies that filter rows from query results based on a user-defined function (UDF) returning boolean values, excluding rows where the function returns FALSE.
* [Unity Catalog Access Control Complementary Models](/concepts/unity-catalog-access-control-complementary-models.md) - The four complementary access control models (privileges, ABAC, table-level filtering, workspace bindings) that work together for fine-grained security.
* [Unity Catalog Access Control for Feature Tables](/concepts/unity-catalog-access-control-for-feature-tables.md) - Governance of feature table access managed through Unity Catalog's privilege system, controlling who can read or write feature tables
* [Unity Catalog Access Control Models](/concepts/unity-catalog-access-control-models.md) - Four complementary access control models in Unity Catalog: privileges/ownership, ABAC, table-level filtering/masking, and workspace-level restrictions.
* [Unity Catalog Access Requests](/concepts/unity-catalog-access-requests.md) - Configurable destinations (email, Slack, Teams, webhooks) for receiving access requests on Unity Catalog securable objects.
* [Unity Catalog Admin Roles](/concepts/unity-catalog-admin-roles.md) - Account admin, workspace admin, and metastore admin roles with distinct scopes of administrative privilege in Unity Catalog.
* [Unity Catalog Admin Roles (Account, Workspace, Metastore)](/concepts/unity-catalog-admin-roles-account-workspace-metastore.md) - The three most important administrative roles in Unity Catalog — account admins, workspace admins, and metastore admins — each operating at different scopes within the Databricks architecture.
* [Unity Catalog AI Asset Governance](/concepts/unity-catalog-ai-asset-governance.md) - Managing AI assets—models, functions, connections, and hosted foundation models—as securable objects with standard Unity Catalog privileges.
* [Unity Catalog AI asset model](/concepts/unity-catalog-ai-asset-model.md) - AI assets (models, functions, connections, hosted foundation models) managed as securable objects in Unity Catalog with standard grant/revoke privileges.
* [Unity Catalog Allowlist](/concepts/unity-catalog-allowlist.md) - A security control in Unity Catalog that specifies which JARs, Maven coordinates, and init scripts are permitted to run on standard access mode compute in Databricks.
* [Unity Catalog Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC)](/concepts/unity-catalog-attribute-based-access-control-abac.md) - Centralized, tag-driven policies that dynamically filter and mask data across the catalog, recommended over per-table row/column filters.
* [Unity Catalog Auto-enablement](/concepts/unity-catalog-auto-enablement.md) - Automatic enablement of Unity Catalog for all Databricks workspaces created after November 8, 2023
* [Unity Catalog Certification Status System Tag](/concepts/certification-status-system-tag.md) - A system-governed tag (system.certification_status) in Databricks Unity Catalog that marks data assets as certified or deprecated to indicate data quality and lifecycle status.
* [Unity Catalog Column Naming and Casing Rules](/concepts/unity-catalog-column-naming-and-casing-rules.md) - Column names in Unity Catalog can use special characters if escaped with backticks; column casing is preserved but queries are case-insensitive.
* [Unity Catalog Compute Requirements (Access Modes & Runtimes)](/concepts/unity-catalog-compute-requirements-access-modes-runtimes.md) - Clusters must run Databricks Runtime 11.3 LTS or above and be configured with standard or dedicated access mode to access Unity Catalog data.
* [Unity Catalog Data Classification](/concepts/unity-catalog-data-classification.md) - A Databricks feature that scans tables in the background and automatically applies system governed tags (class.*) to detected sensitive columns such as PII, financial data, and contact information.
* [Unity Catalog Data Lineage](/concepts/unity-catalog-data-lineage.md) - Automatically captured metadata showing where data in Databricks originated, how it was transformed, and which downstream assets consume it, down to the column level.
* [Unity Catalog Data Quality Monitoring](/concepts/unity-catalog-data-quality-monitoring.md) - A capability in Unity Catalog that helps ensure the quality of data assets through anomaly detection and data profiling, without modifying tables or adding job overhead.
* [Unity Catalog Data Quality Monitoring Alerts](/concepts/unity-catalog-data-quality-monitoring-alerts.md) - Automated notifications that trigger when anomaly detection identifies unhealthy tables in Unity Catalog, supporting both UI-based and SQL-based configuration.
* [Unity Catalog Dataset Storage](/concepts/unity-catalog-dataset-storage.md) - MLflow evaluation datasets are stored in Unity Catalog, which provides built-in versioning, lineage, sharing, and governance for the datasets.
* [Unity Catalog Delta Table](/concepts/unity-catalog-delta-table.md) - The target storage format and catalog system used for archiving traces, requiring the full three-level name (catalog.schema.table) for write access.
* [Unity Catalog Delta Table as Trace Sink](/concepts/unity-catalog-delta-table-as-trace-sink.md) - Using a Unity Catalog-managed Delta table (specified with catalog.schema.table notation) as the storage target for archived MLflow traces, with automatic table creation and append behavior.
* [Unity Catalog Delta Table for Trace Storage](/concepts/unity-catalog-delta-table-for-trace-storage.md) - The requirement and mechanism for storing archived traces in a Unity Catalog-managed Delta table, which is auto-created if it doesn't exist.
* [Unity Catalog Delta Tables as Trace Storage](/concepts/unity-catalog-delta-tables-as-trace-storage.md) - Unity Catalog Delta tables act as the durable storage target for archived MLflow traces, supporting automatic table creation, append-only writes, and cross-catalog/schema organization.
* [Unity Catalog Delta Tables for Trace Storage](/concepts/unity-catalog-delta-tables-for-trace-storage.md) - Target storage format for archived traces, requiring write permissions and supporting automatic table creation or append behavior.
* [Unity Catalog Enablement](/concepts/unity-catalog-enablement.md) - The process of enabling Unity Catalog in Databricks workspaces, which is automatic for workspaces created after November 8, 2023, and requires manual upgrade for older workspaces.
* [Unity Catalog External Table Conversion](/concepts/unity-catalog-external-table-conversion.md) - Converting Parquet or Iceberg tables stored in external locations managed by Unity Catalog to Delta Lake using CONVERT TO DELTA.
* [Unity Catalog Feature Lookup](/concepts/unity-catalog-feature-lookup.md) - Model serving endpoints use Unity Catalog to look up table-backed features from online stores at inference time, using entity keys from the request payload.
* [Unity Catalog Feature Store vs Workspace Feature Store](/concepts/unity-catalog-feature-store-vs-workspace-feature-store.md) - The two types of feature stores on Databricks: the newer Unity Catalog-based feature store and the legacy Workspace Feature Store
* [Unity Catalog Feature Table Access Control](/concepts/unity-catalog-feature-table-access-control.md) - Access control for Databricks Feature Store feature tables is managed through Unity Catalog privileges
* [Unity Catalog Feature Tables](/concepts/unity-catalog-feature-tables.md) - The modern feature store system in Databricks, managed through Unity Catalog, that AutoML can query to augment training data.
* [Unity Catalog File Format Support](/concepts/unity-catalog-file-format-support.md) - Managed tables in Unity Catalog require Delta or Iceberg format; external tables support Delta, CSV, JSON, Avro, Parquet, ORC, or text.
* [Unity Catalog for AI Governance](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-ai-governance.md) - Fine-grained access control, security policies, and governance for all data and AI assets across the Databricks platform.
* [Unity Catalog for Evaluation Data](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-evaluation-data.md) - Storage of MLflow evaluation datasets in Unity Catalog, providing built-in versioning, lineage, sharing, and governance capabilities for GenAI evaluation artifacts.
* [Unity Catalog for Machine Learning](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-machine-learning.md) - Unified governance of data, ML assets, and model endpoints, providing access management, audit logging, and lineage for the full ML lifecycle.
* [Unity Catalog for ML](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-ml.md) - Unified governance layer on Databricks that manages data, features, models, and functions with access control, lineage tracking, and discovery for ML assets.
* [Unity Catalog for ML Artifact Storage](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-ml-artifact-storage.md) - Using Databricks Unity Catalog volumes as a storage location for saving and versioning machine learning artifacts such as model checkpoints, enabling integration with catalog-level access control and governance.
* [Unity Catalog for ML Data Access](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-ml-data-access.md) - Using Unity Catalog as a unified governance solution to access data on clusters via both Spark and local file APIs for ML workloads.
* [Unity Catalog for ML Governance](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-ml-governance.md) - A unified governance layer for data, features, models, AI endpoints, and inference tables, built on open-source Unity Catalog APIs with fine-grained access controls.
* [Unity Catalog for ML Model Management](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-ml-models.md) - A Databricks feature for registering, versioning, and managing machine learning models as first-class assets within a governed catalog-schema hierarchy, enabling deployment to model serving endpoints.
* [Unity Catalog for ML Models](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-ml-models.md) - Databricks Unity Catalog provides a namespace (catalog/schema/model name) for organizing, registering, and versioning machine learning models, enabling model deployment to serving endpoints or batch inference.
* [Unity Catalog for Model Governance](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-model-governance.md) - Using Models in Unity Catalog to manage model versioning, governance, deployment status, access control, and catalog-per-environment isolation across the MLOps workflow.
* [Unity Catalog for Model Management](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-ml-models.md) - Databricks Unity Catalog namespace used to organize, store, register, and deploy fine-tuned models with MLflow
* [Unity Catalog for Model Registration](/concepts/unity-catalog-for-model-registration.md) - A Databricks feature that enables registering, versioning, and managing ML models in a unified catalog for deployment and inference
* [Unity Catalog Foundation Model Deployment](/concepts/unity-catalog-foundation-model-deployment.md) - The recommended workflow for deploying foundation models from the system.ai catalog in Unity Catalog using Databricks Catalog Explorer.
* [Unity Catalog governance](/concepts/unity-catalog-governance.md) - Central management of metadata, access control, auditing, and lineage for all registered securable objects
* [Unity Catalog Governance for Feature Tables](/concepts/unity-catalog-governance-for-feature-tables.md) - Feature tables inherit Unity Catalog's access control, permissions, and governance policies, including model permission inheritance from training data
* [Unity Catalog Governance for ML](/concepts/unity-catalog-governance-for-ml.md) - Unified governance of data, ML assets, model endpoints, and security across the ML lifecycle via Unity Catalog
* [Unity Catalog governance for ML assets](/concepts/unity-catalog-governance-for-ml-assets.md) - Centralized governance of ML models, feature tables, vector indexes, and tools through Unity Catalog, with fine-grained access control and authentication passthrough for agent, data, and tool access.
* [Unity Catalog Governed Tags for Data Classification](/concepts/unity-catalog-governed-tags-for-data-classification.md) - The system of governed tags (PII, GDPR, HIPAA, DPDPA, etc.) that are automatically applied by the classification engine to detected sensitive columns.
* [Unity Catalog Integration for Evaluation Datasets](/concepts/unity-catalog-integration-for-evaluation-datasets.md) - Infrastructure layer where MLflow evaluation datasets are stored in Unity Catalog tables, providing built-in versioning, lineage tracking, sharing across workspaces, and governance controls via Unity Catalog permissions.
* [Unity Catalog Integration for Feature Store](/concepts/unity-catalog-integration-for-features.md) - Built-in governance, cross-workspace sharing, and discovery of feature tables and models through Unity Catalog.
* [Unity Catalog Integration for Features](/concepts/unity-catalog-integration-for-features.md) - Feature tables and models registered in Unity Catalog enable built-in governance, point-in-time joins, and cross-workspace feature sharing and discovery.
* [Unity Catalog Integration for MLflow Models](/concepts/unity-catalog-integration-for-mlflow-models.md) - Saving, registering, and managing MLflow models within Unity Catalog volumes and the Unity Catalog model registry, including viewing parameters and metrics across experiments and workspaces.
* [Unity Catalog Integration for Model Storage](/concepts/unity-catalog-integration-for-model-storage.md) - Using Databricks Unity Catalog catalogs, schemas, and volumes to store model checkpoints and artifacts, requiring USE CATALOG and USE SCHEMA privileges.
* [Unity Catalog Integration for Prompts](/concepts/unity-catalog-integration-for-prompts.md) - Integration between the Prompt Registry and Unity Catalog for governance, access control, audit trails, and prompt-to-experiment lineage tracking.
* [Unity Catalog Limitations and Known Issues](/concepts/unity-catalog-limitations-and-known-issues.md) - Unity Catalog has various limitations including restrictions on workspace-level groups in GRANT statements, bucketing, R workload dynamic views, Python/Scala UDF support by runtime version, cloning constraints, and cross-region write reliability.
* [Unity Catalog managed functions as AI agent tools](/concepts/unity-catalog-managed-functions-as-ai-agent-tools.md) - Unity Catalog functions used as agent tools or for data transformations, enabling governed access to external APIs and data processing within AI workflows.
* [Unity Catalog Managed Table Migration Rollback Constraints](/concepts/unity-catalog-managed-table-migration-rollback-constraints.md) - Constraints governing the ability to roll back a table from managed to external in Unity Catalog, including time windows and history retention requirements.
* [Unity Catalog Managed Table Migration Rollback Time Window](/concepts/unity-catalog-managed-table-migration-rollback-time-window.md) - The time-limited window (in days) after migrating a table to Unity Catalog managed during which rollback via ALTER TABLE UNSET MANAGED is supported.
* [Unity Catalog Managed Tables](/concepts/unity-catalog-managed-tables.md) - Databricks recommendation to use Unity Catalog managed tables for Delta Lake workloads, with automatic optimization and governance.
* [Unity Catalog Metastore](/concepts/unity-catalog-metastore.md) - The top-level container for data in Unity Catalog that registers metadata about securable objects and permissions, exposing a three-level namespace.
* [Unity Catalog Metastore Auto-Assignment](/concepts/unity-catalog-metastore-auto-assignment.md) - An account admin setting that automatically assigns an existing Unity Catalog metastore to new workspaces created in the same AWS region, eliminating the need for manual workspace-to-metastore assignment.
* [Unity Catalog Metastore Deletion](/concepts/unity-catalog-metastore-deletion.md) - Permanent deletion of a Unity Catalog metastore that makes all managed objects inaccessible, auto-deletes managed table data and metadata after 30 days, but does not affect external table data in cloud storage.
* [Unity Catalog Metastore Recipient Token Lifetime Configuration](/concepts/metastore-recipient-token-lifetime-configuration.md) - The ability for account admins to set a default token lifetime for all new OpenSharing recipients at the metastore level, which does not automatically apply to existing recipients.
* [Unity Catalog Migration and Default Registry Behavior](/concepts/unity-catalog-migration-and-default-registry-behavior.md) - Guidance on when and how to use the Workspace Model Registry versus Unity Catalog for model management, including automatic routing based on workspace configuration and explicit registry URI targeting via mlflow.set_registry_uri().
* [Unity Catalog Migration Path](/concepts/unity-catalog-migration-path.md) - The recommended upgrade strategy to replace the legacy Hive metastore table access control with Unity Catalog for simplified, centralized data governance across workspaces.
* [Unity Catalog ML Governance](/concepts/unity-catalog-ml-governance.md) - Applying Unity Catalog's fine-grained access controls, lineage tracking, and governance to machine learning assets including data, features, models, and endpoints for a complete audit trail.
* [Unity Catalog ML Workflow Migration Strategy](/concepts/unity-catalog-ml-workflow-migration-strategy.md) - Recommended approach for migrating existing Databricks ML workflows to Unity Catalog by creating parallel pipelines instead of cut-over
* [Unity Catalog Model Registration](/concepts/unity-catalog-model-registration.md) - The process of registering machine learning models trained with MLflow into Databricks Unity Catalog for centralized governance, versioning, and deployment.
* [Unity Catalog Model Registration for Custom LLMs](/concepts/unity-catalog-model-registration-for-custom-llms.md) - The process of registering custom LLMs to Unity Catalog using MLflow with the databricks_model_serving environment pack to enable express deployments for custom LLM serving.
* [Unity Catalog model registration for LLMs](/concepts/unity-catalog-model-registration-for-llms.md) - The pattern of registering fine-tuned LLMs into Databricks Unity Catalog for governance, versioning, and deployment, including merging LoRA adapters into the base model.
* [Unity Catalog Model Registration for ML Models](/concepts/unity-catalog-model-registration.md) - The process of registering fine-tuned models in Databricks Unity Catalog for governance, version control, discoverability, and deployment via Model Serving.
* [Unity Catalog Model Registration with MLflow](/concepts/unity-catalog-model-registration-with-mlflow.md) - The workflow for registering finetuned LLMs into Databricks Unity Catalog via MLflow, including model versioning, metadata tagging, and merging LoRA adapters into the base model for deployment.
* [Unity Catalog Model Registry](/concepts/unity-catalog-model-registry.md) - A Databricks feature for storing model checkpoints, registering trained models, and managing model lifecycle using a catalog schema naming convention within Unity Catalog volumes.
* [Unity Catalog Model Registry for ML Models](/concepts/unity-catalog-model-registry-for-ml-models.md) - A Unity Catalog namespace for organizing, storing, registering, and deploying ML models, using catalog/schema/volume structure for model checkpoints and registered model serving.
* [Unity Catalog Object Hierarchy](/concepts/unity-catalog-object-hierarchy.md) - The hierarchical structure of securable objects in Unity Catalog, with the metastore at the top and a three-level namespace (catalog.schema.table) for data assets.
* [Unity Catalog Object Hierarchy and Privilege Inheritance](/concepts/unity-catalog-object-hierarchy-and-privilege-inheritance.md) - The hierarchical structure of securable objects in Unity Catalog where privileges flow from parent to child objects.
* [Unity Catalog Only Mode](/concepts/unity-catalog-only-mode.md) - A workspace admin setting that disables direct access to the legacy Hive metastore, forcing all queries to go through Unity Catalog.
* [Unity Catalog Only Mode Spark Configuration](/concepts/unity-catalog-only-mode-spark-configuration.md) - Spark settings spark.databricks.unityCatalogOnlyMode and spark.databricks.sql.initial.catalog.namespace to enforce Unity Catalog exclusively on a cluster.
* [Unity Catalog Overview](/concepts/unity-catalog.md) - The unified governance layer for data and AI in Databricks, providing centralized access control, lineage, auditing, and data discovery
* [Unity Catalog Permission Hierarchy](/concepts/unity-catalog-permissions-model.md) - Hierarchy of securable objects in Unity Catalog with privilege inheritance, where privileges on parent objects flow down to child objects.
* [Unity Catalog permission model for ML models](/concepts/unity-catalog-permission-model-for-ml-models.md) - Account-level Unity Catalog privileges replace workspace-level permissions for models, with a mapping from registry permissions (e.g., Can Read, Can Edit) to Unity Catalog privileges (e.g., EXECUTE, MANAGE), requiring USE CATALOG and USE SCHEMA for all actions.
* [Unity Catalog Permissions for OpenSharing](/concepts/unity-catalog-permissions-for-opensharing.md) - The required permissions (metastore admin, USE SHARE, SET SHARE PERMISSION, USE RECIPIENT, and ownership) needed to manage shares and recipients.
* [Unity Catalog Permissions for Prompts](/concepts/unity-catalog-permissions-for-prompts.md) - Storing and managing prompts in the MLflow Prompt Registry requires specific Unity Catalog schema privileges: CREATE FUNCTION, EXECUTE, and MANAGE.
* [Unity Catalog Permissions for Trace Archival](/concepts/unity-catalog-permissions-for-trace-archival.md) - The requirement that users must have necessary write permissions to the specified Unity Catalog Delta table when archiving traces; the table is created if it does not exist or appended to if it already exists.
* [Unity Catalog Permissions Hierarchy](/concepts/unity-catalog-permissions-model.md) - The object hierarchy and privilege inheritance model in Unity Catalog that governs how access flows from parent to child objects.
* [Unity Catalog Permissions Model](/concepts/unity-catalog-permissions-model.md) - The object hierarchy, privilege inheritance, and access flow from parent to child objects in Unity Catalog
* [Unity Catalog Policies](/concepts/unity-catalog-policies.md) - Policies attached at hierarchy levels (catalog, schema, table) that are evaluated dynamically to enforce access rules based on attributes.
* [Unity Catalog Policy Lifecycle Management](/concepts/unity-catalog-policy-lifecycle-management.md) - Create, edit, delete, show, and describe operations for ABAC policies using Catalog Explorer UI, SQL statements (CREATE POLICY, SHOW POLICIES, DESCRIBE POLICY), Python SDK, REST APIs, and Terraform.
* [Unity Catalog Privilege Management](/concepts/unity-catalog-privilege-management.md) - The overall system for granting, revoking, and inspecting permissions on Unity Catalog securable objects (catalogs, schemas, tables, views, volumes, etc.) using SQL commands, Catalog Explorer, CLI, or Terraform.
* [Unity Catalog Privileges and Ownership](/concepts/privileges-and-ownership.md) - Grant-based model controlling who can access what on securable objects using a hierarchical permission system with privilege inheritance.
* [Unity Catalog Privileges and Ownership Model](/concepts/privileges-and-ownership.md) - Grant-based model controlling who can access what securable objects, with privilege inheritance through the object hierarchy.
* [Unity Catalog Privileges for ML Workflows](/concepts/unity-catalog-privileges-for-ml-workflows.md) - Access control requirements including USE CATALOG, USE SCHEMA, CREATE MODEL, EXECUTE, and CREATE MODEL VERSION privileges needed for model operations in Unity Catalog
* [Unity Catalog Prompt Registry Constraints](/concepts/unity-catalog-prompt-registry-constraints.md) - Unity Catalog imposes specific constraints on prompt operations: catalog+schema required in searches, no pattern/tag filtering, and strict version-deletion ordering for prompt removal.
* [Unity Catalog Provider Import for OpenSharing](/concepts/unity-catalog-provider-import-for-opensharing.md) - A UI-based workflow in Databricks Catalog Explorer that allows recipients to import provider credentials, create catalogs from shares, and use Unity Catalog access controls without manually handling credential files.
* [Unity Catalog Region Support](/concepts/unity-catalog-region-support.md) - All regions support Unity Catalog, with further details available in Databricks' supported regions documentation.
* [Unity Catalog Regional Metastore Requirement](/concepts/unity-catalog-regional-metastore-requirement.md) - The architectural requirement that each AWS region must have its own Unity Catalog metastore, with workspaces linked only to metastores in their same region.
* [Unity Catalog Requirement for Databricks Connect](/concepts/unity-catalog-requirement-for-databricks-connect.md) - To use Databricks Connect, the Databricks account and workspace must have Unity Catalog enabled.
* [Unity Catalog Requirement for Delta Uniform](/concepts/unity-catalog-requirement-for-delta-uniform.md) - Unity Catalog must be enabled to read Apache Iceberg tables via Delta Uniform; otherwise a UNITY_CATALOG_NOT_ENABLED error is raised.
* [Unity Catalog requirement for Delta Uniform Iceberg reads](/concepts/unity-catalog-requirement-for-delta-uniform.md) - Unity Catalog must be enabled for reading Apache Iceberg tables through Delta Uniform; the UNITY_CATALOG_NOT_ENABLED error is raised when this prerequisite is not met.
* [Unity Catalog Resource Quotas](/concepts/unity-catalog-resource-quotas.md) - Unity Catalog enforces resource quotas on all securable objects; users can monitor usage via dedicated APIs and contact the Databricks account team if they expect to exceed limits.
* [Unity Catalog Schema as Prompt Registry Backend](/concepts/unity-catalog-schema-as-prompt-registry-backend.md) - Prompts are stored in Unity Catalog schemas, which require specific permissions (CREATE FUNCTION, EXECUTE, MANAGE) and are linked to MLflow experiments via a tag.
* [Unity Catalog Schema for Prompts](/concepts/unity-catalog-schema-for-prompts.md) - Prompts are stored in Unity Catalog schemas, requiring CREATE FUNCTION, EXECUTE, and MANAGE privileges for user access.
* [Unity Catalog Schema Permissions for Prompts](/concepts/unity-catalog-schema-permissions-for-prompts.md) - Storing prompts requires a Unity Catalog schema with CREATE FUNCTION, EXECUTE, and MANAGE privileges, and the schema acts as the storage location for registered prompts.
* [Unity Catalog Schema Requirements for Prompts](/concepts/unity-catalog-schema-requirements-for-prompts.md) - Prompts are stored in Unity Catalog schemas requiring specific permissions (CREATE FUNCTION, EXECUTE, MANAGE) and are linked to MLflow experiments via the mlflow.promptRegistryLocation tag.
* [Unity Catalog Securable Object Naming Constraints](/concepts/unity-catalog-securable-object-naming-constraints.md) - Object names in Unity Catalog are limited to 255 characters, stored as lowercase, and cannot contain periods, spaces, forward slashes, or ASCII control characters.
* [Unity Catalog Securable Objects](/concepts/unity-catalog-securable-objects.md) - The object hierarchy in Unity Catalog including catalogs, schemas, tables, external locations, and storage credentials that can have privileges assigned
* [Unity Catalog Shallow Clone](/concepts/unity-catalog-shallow-clone.md) - Shallow clone support for Unity Catalog managed tables in Databricks SQL and Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS and above, with no support in Databricks Runtime 12.2 LTS and below.
* [Unity Catalog Shallow Clone Support](/concepts/unity-catalog-shallow-clone.md) - Shallow clone is supported for Unity Catalog managed tables in Databricks SQL and Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS and above, but not in 12.2 LTS and below.
* [Unity Catalog Storage for Evaluation Datasets](/concepts/unity-catalog-storage-for-evaluation-datasets.md) - Evaluation datasets are stored as tables in Unity Catalog, providing built-in versioning, lineage tracking, cross-team sharing, and governance, requiring CREATE TABLE permissions on a schema.
* [Unity Catalog storage validation](/concepts/unity-catalog-storage-validation.md) - The process by which Unity Catalog validates external locations against every storage configuration registered to the Databricks account, not just the workspace where the location was created.
* [Unity Catalog Supported Objects for Tagging](/concepts/unity-catalog-supported-objects-for-tagging.md) - The list of Unity Catalog object types that support the certification status system tag, including catalogs, schemas, tables, views, volumes, functions, registered models, dashboards, Genie Spaces, Databricks Apps, and notebooks.
* [Unity Catalog System Governed Tags](/concepts/unity-catalog-system-governed-tags.md) - System-reserved tags (in the sap.* namespace) that are automatically assigned by the Databricks system and must not be manually created, modified, or deleted by users.
* [Unity Catalog System Tables for Data Quality](/concepts/unity-catalog-system-tables-for-data-quality.md) - The `system.data_quality_monitoring.table_results` system table that stores all anomaly detection scan results, usable for building dashboards or setting up SQL alerts across the metastore.
* [Unity Catalog Tagging Permissions](/concepts/unity-catalog-tagging-permissions.md) - The permission model required to assign governed tags in Unity Catalog, including the ASSIGN permission on the system tag and object-level privileges like APPLY TAG, USE SCHEMA, and USE CATALOG.
* [Unity Catalog Telemetry Table Structure](/concepts/unity-catalog-telemetry-table-structure.md) - Automatically generated Delta tables (_otel_logs, _otel_spans, _otel_metrics) in Unity Catalog that store endpoint telemetry with configurable table prefix.
* [Unity Catalog Three-Level Namespace](/concepts/unity-catalog-three-level-namespace.md) - The catalog.schema.table hierarchical naming convention used by Unity Catalog to organize and access data objects.
* [Unity Catalog Trace Format (Schema-linked vs Table-prefix)](/concepts/unity-catalog-trace-format-schema-linked-vs-table-prefix.md) - Two formats for storing MLflow traces in Unity Catalog: the older Beta schema-linked format (catalog.schema) and the newer Public Preview table-prefix format (catalog.schema.table_prefix)
* [Unity Catalog Trace Location](/concepts/unity-catalog-trace-location.md) - Mechanism to link an MLflow experiment to a Unity Catalog catalog and schema using set_experiment_trace_location(), which determines where ingested OTLP spans are stored as tables.
* [Unity Catalog Trace Storage](/concepts/unity-catalog-trace-storage.md) - Storing OpenTelemetry trace spans in Unity Catalog tables alongside MLflow traces, enabling SQL querying, access controls, lineage, and centralized governance.
* [Unity Catalog Trace Storage for OpenTelemetry](/concepts/unity-catalog-trace-storage.md) - Architecture and approach for storing OpenTelemetry trace data in Unity Catalog tables, enabling query via MLflow SDK and Databricks SQL.
* [Unity Catalog Upgrade Process](/concepts/unity-catalog-upgrade-process.md) - The structured step-by-step process for upgrading a Databricks workspace from legacy Hive metastore and DBFS to Unity Catalog governance.
* [Unity Catalog volume automation via DBUtils](/concepts/unity-catalog-volume-automation-via-dbutils.md) - Using DBUtils.getDBUtils with Databricks Connect for Scala to automate Unity Catalog volumes — creating, reading, and deleting files within volume paths.
* [Unity Catalog Volume Checkpointing](/concepts/unity-catalog-volume-checkpointing.md) - Save and load model checkpoints asynchronously to Unity Catalog volumes using UCVolumeWriter and UCVolumeReader with Torch Distributed Checkpoint API, optimized with NVMe-backed local staging.
* [Unity Catalog Volume file operations via Databricks Utilities Scala](/concepts/unity-catalog-volume-file-operations-via-databricks-utilities-scala.md) - Example pattern showing how to use dbutils.fs.put, dbutils.fs.head, and dbutils.fs.rm on Unity Catalog volume paths (e.g., /Volumes/main/default/my-volume/) in Databricks Connect for Scala.
* [Unity Catalog Volume File Operations via dbutils](/concepts/unity-catalog-volume-file-operations-via-dbutils.md) - Example workflow for creating, reading, and deleting files in Unity Catalog volumes using dbutils.fs methods like put, head, and rm
* [Unity Catalog Volume File Operations with dbutils.fs](/concepts/unity-catalog-volume-file-operations-with-dbutilsfs.md) - Through Databricks Connect, dbutils.fs can perform file operations like put, head, and rm on Unity Catalog volume paths
* [Unity Catalog Volume for Checkpoint Storage](/concepts/unity-catalog-volume-for-checkpoint-storage.md) - A Databricks pattern for storing distributed training checkpoints in Unity Catalog volumes, enabling versioned, governed storage of model artifacts that integrates with MLflow and the Databricks ecosystem.
* [Unity Catalog Volume Operations with DBUtils Scala](/concepts/unity-catalog-volume-operations-with-dbutils-scala.md) - Pattern for creating, reading, and deleting files in Unity Catalog volumes using DBUtils.fs in Scala
* [Unity Catalog Volumes for Checkpointing](/concepts/unity-catalog-volumes-for-checkpointing.md) - Using Unity Catalog volumes as persistent storage for model checkpoints during distributed training workloads on Databricks
* [Unity Catalog Volumes for ML Data](/concepts/unity-catalog-volumes-for-ml-data.md) - Using Unity Catalog volumes as persistent, cluster-independent storage for machine learning training data and Hugging Face dataset caches on Databricks
* [Unity Catalog Volumes for MLflow Dependencies](/concepts/unity-catalog-volumes-for-mlflow-dependencies.md) - Using Unity Catalog volumes as the recommended storage location for custom Python wheel files referenced in MLflow model logging
* [Unity Catalog Volumes for Model Artifacts](/concepts/unity-catalog-volumes-for-model-artifacts.md) - Using Unity Catalog volumes as a repository for model dependency files, with the requirement that these files be packaged into the MLflow model artifact for real-time serving on Databricks.
* [Unity Catalog Volumes for Model Checkpointing](/concepts/unity-catalog-volumes-for-model-checkpointing.md) - Using Unity Catalog Volumes to store and load PyTorch model checkpoints via paths like /Volumes/{catalog}/{schema}/{volume}/{model_name}, integrated with torch.distributed.checkpoint.
* [Unity Catalog vs Legacy Access Control for Feature Store](/concepts/unity-catalog-vs-legacy-access-control-for-feature-store.md) - Distinction between Unity Catalog-based privilege management and the older fine-grained permission model for workspaces not enabled for Unity Catalog
* [Unity Catalog Workspace Bindings](/concepts/unity-catalog-workspace-bindings.md) - Mechanism to restrict which workspaces can access specific catalogs, external locations, and storage credentials.
* [Unity Catalog Workspace-Catalog Binding](/concepts/unity-catalog-workspace-bindings.md) - Workspace-level restrictions that limit which workspaces can access specific catalogs, external locations, and storage credentials.
* [Unity Catalog Write Limitations for Non-Spark Writers](/concepts/unity-catalog-write-limitations-for-non-spark-writers.md) - The limitation that Unity Catalog does not share credentials for writing to tables from non-Spark writers, requiring data to be persisted and read with Spark or using Databricks Connect within Ray tasks.
* [Unity Catalog-Backed Experiment](/concepts/unity-catalog-backed-experiment.md) - An MLflow experiment bound to a Unity Catalog trace location, backed by four Delta tables (otel_spans, otel_annotations, otel_logs, otel_metrics) sharing a common table prefix.
* [Unresolved Logical Plan Size Limit (128 MB)](/concepts/unresolved-logical-plan-size-limit-128-mb.md) - Databricks Connect for Scala cannot create DataFrames with an unresolved logical plan larger than 128 MB, a limit on plan size not data size.
* [UNRESOLVED_COLUMN error subtype](/concepts/unresolved_column-error-subtype.md) - A specific error raised when a column referenced in a predicate cannot be resolved against the available clustering columns of a Liquid table.
* [Unsloth](/concepts/unsloth.md) - An optimization framework for efficiently fine-tuning large language models like Llama
* [Unsloth Chat Templates and ShareGPT Data Standardization](/concepts/unsloth-chat-templates-and-sharegpt-data-standardization.md) - Unsloth provides utilities like get_chat_template and standardize_sharegpt to convert conversational datasets into a standardized chat format (e.g., Llama 3.1) for training.
* [Unsloth Distributed Finetuning on Databricks](/concepts/unsloth-distributed-finetuning-on-databricks.md) - Methodology for fine-tuning large language models like Llama-3.2-3B across multiple GPUs using the Unsloth library within the Databricks environment on AWS.
* [Unsloth Fine-Tuning Library](/concepts/unsloth-fine-tuning-library.md) - An optimized library for faster LLM fine-tuning that supports LoRA/QLoRA, reduced memory usage, and integrated chat templates, included in Databricks AI v5 runtime.
* [Unsloth Library](/concepts/unsloth-library.md) - An optimized library for parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) of large language models, providing faster training and reduced memory usage through techniques like LoRA.
* [Unsloth LoRA Configuration](/concepts/unsloth-lora-configuration.md) - The specific pattern of applying LoRA adapters via `FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()` in Unsloth, targeting q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj with rank 16 and zero dropout for optimized training.
* [Unsloth Optimized LLM Finetuning](/concepts/unsloth-optimized-llm-finetuning.md) - An optimization library that accelerates LLM fine-tuning through memory-efficient kernels, LoRA/QLoRA support, and reduced memory footprint.
* [Unsupported Delta Clone Targets](/concepts/unsupported-delta-clone-targets.md) - The three categories of target tables that cannot receive a Delta clone with history: non-Delta tables, path-based tables, and session temporary tables.
* [Unsupported features for Delta Lake on S3 with multi-cluster writes](/concepts/unsupported-features-for-delta-lake-on-s3-with-multi-cluster-writes.md) - Server-Side Encryption with Customer-Provided Keys (SSE-C) and S3 paths with credentials lacking STS access are not supported when multi-cluster writes are enabled on Delta Lake tables stored in S3.
* [Unsupported Operations on ABAC-Protected Tables](/concepts/unsupported-operations-on-abac-protected-tables.md) - Operations like time travel queries, deep/shallow cloning, OpenSharing, and AI Search index creation that fail on ABAC-secured tables unless the principal is exempt from all applicable policies.
* [Unsupported reader features for Delta Uniform refresh](/concepts/unsupported-reader-features-for-delta-uniform-refresh.md) - Some reader table features are incompatible with REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM and cause the UNSUPPORTED_READER_FEATURES sub-error
* [Unsupported source type for Delta Uniform refresh](/concepts/unsupported-source-type-for-delta-uniform-refresh.md) - REFRESH SYNC UNIFORM cannot be applied to certain source table types, raising UNSUPPORTED_TYPE or WRONG_TYPE sub-errors
* [UNSUPPORTED_DATA_TYPE (Delta Iceberg error)](/concepts/unsupported_data_type-in-icebergcompatv1.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_V1_VIOLATION that occurs when a table schema contains MapType, ArrayType, or NullType, which are unsupported by IcebergCompatV1
* [UNSUPPORTED_DATA_TYPE in IcebergCompatV1](/concepts/unsupported_data_type-in-icebergcompatv1.md) - Error sub-type indicating IcebergCompatV1 does not support schemas containing MapType, ArrayType, or NullType columns
* [UNSUPPORTED_EXPRESSION error subtype](/concepts/unsupported_expression-error-subtype.md) - A specific error raised when an unsupported expression is used in a predicate for a command on a Liquid table.
* [UNSUPPORTED_OPERATOR constraint for table_changes](/concepts/unsupported_operator-constraint-for-table_changes.md) - A validation rule requiring that views used with table_changes must not contain operators that are not allowed for change data capture
* [UPDATE FROM JOIN (Unsupported in Databricks SQL)](/concepts/update-from-join-unsupported-in-databricks-sql.md) - Databricks SQL does not support the UPDATE...FROM...JOIN syntax used in other SQL dialects; users must use MERGE INTO instead.
* [Update Model Serving Endpoints](/concepts/update-model-serving-endpoints.md) - Process and constraints for updating model serving endpoint configurations, including compute configuration changes and the rule that external_model endpoints cannot be converted to or from non-external_model types.
* [UPDATE SET * and INSERT * with EXCEPT](/concepts/update-set-and-insert-with-except.md) - Shorthand syntax to update all target columns from corresponding source columns (or insert all source columns), with the ability to exclude specific columns using the EXCEPT clause (Databricks Runtime 12.2 LTS+).
* [UPDATE Statement (Delta Lake)](/concepts/update-statement-delta-lake.md) - SQL statement to update column values in Delta Lake tables, optionally filtered by a predicate using WHERE clause.
* [upgrade_workspace_table method](/concepts/upgrade_workspace_table-method.md) - API method that migrates metadata (primary keys, time series columns, comments, tags, lineage) from a workspace feature table to a target Unity Catalog table.
* [UpgradeClient (Databricks Feature Engineering)](/concepts/upgradeclient-databricks-feature-engineering.md) - A Python client class from databricks.feature_engineering used to upgrade workspace feature table metadata to Unity Catalog.
* [Upgrading Jobs to Unity Catalog](/concepts/upgrading-jobs-to-unity-catalog.md) - Migration patterns for updating existing Databricks job notebooks to work with Unity Catalog after upgrading from legacy workspaces.
* [Upsert from Streaming Queries using foreachBatch and MERGE](/concepts/upsert-from-streaming-queries-using-foreachbatch-and-merge.md) - Combining foreachBatch with Delta Lake MERGE operations to perform upserts (inserts and updates) from streaming queries, enabling SCD/CDC patterns and deduplication.
* [upstream_jobs Array](/concepts/upstream_jobs-array.md) - An array column in the anomaly detection results table that records information about upstream jobs feeding each scanned table.
* [USAGE Privilege Requirement](/concepts/usage-privilege-requirement.md) - The requirement that users must have the USAGE privilege on a schema (or meet one of several alternative conditions) in addition to any action-specific privilege to operate on objects within that schema.
* [Use Cases for Custom PyFunc Models](/concepts/use-cases-for-custom-pyfunc-models.md) - Scenarios where custom pyfunc is needed: unsupported frameworks, preprocessing/postprocessing, per-request branching logic, and fully custom code deployment.
* [Use cases for DeepSpeed distributor](/concepts/use-cases-for-deepspeed-distributor.md) - Scenarios where DeepSpeed distributor is beneficial: low GPU memory, large model training, and large input data such as batch inference.
* [User and Session Tracking in GenAI Traces](/concepts/user-and-session-tracking-in-llm-traces.md) - Using standardized metadata fields (mlflow.trace.user and mlflow.trace.session) to track user identity and conversation sessions for behavior analysis, personalization, and multi-turn conversation tracking.
* [User and Session Tracking in LLM Traces](/concepts/user-and-session-tracking-in-llm-traces.md) - Using standardized mlflow.trace.user and mlflow.trace.session metadata fields to associate traces with specific users and group them into multi-turn conversations for behavioral analytics.
* [User and Session Tracking in MLflow Traces](/concepts/user-and-session-tracking-in-llm-traces.md) - Using standardized mlflow.trace.user and mlflow.trace.session metadata fields to associate traces with specific users and group traces from multi-turn conversations for behavior analysis and personalization.
* [User and Session Tracking with Standard Metadata Fields](/concepts/user-and-session-tracking-with-standard-metadata-fields.md) - MLflow's standard metadata fields `mlflow.trace.user` and `mlflow.trace.session` for associating traces with users and grouping multi-turn conversations
* [User Assignment and Permissions for Labeling](/concepts/user-assignment-and-permissions-for-labeling.md) - The process of assigning domain experts to labeling sessions, including automatic WRITE permission grants on the underlying MLflow experiment and identity provisioning for external users.
* [User Feedback Collection in MLflow Tracing](/concepts/user-feedback-collection-in-mlflow-tracing.md) - MLflow provides a structured way to capture user feedback as assessments on traces, enabling quality tracking and evaluation dataset creation for GenAI applications.
* [User Proxy Agent](/concepts/user-proxy-agent.md) - An AutoGen agent that acts as an intermediary to execute tool calls and manage conversation termination, typically with human_input_mode='NEVER' for automated workflows
* [User-to-Machine (U2M) Authentication](/concepts/user-to-machine-u2m-authentication.md) - Authentication flow where a human user authenticates via their IdP (with MFA if configured) and accesses shared data through tools like Power BI or Tableau using the OAuth Authorization Code Grant flow.
* [User-to-Machine (U2M) Authentication Flow](/concepts/user-to-machine-u2m-authentication-flow.md) - OIDC-based authentication flow for interactive applications like Power BI and Tableau accessing Delta Sharing data
* [Using Columns Clause for Conditional Masking](/concepts/using-columns-clause-for-conditional-masking.md) - A pattern enabling column masks to reference additional columns or static parameters via the USING COLUMNS clause, allowing conditional logic based on values beyond the masked column itself.
* [Using existing Delta tables as feature tables](/concepts/using-existing-delta-tables-as-feature-tables.md) - Process of converting an existing Delta table in Unity Catalog into a feature table by adding NOT NULL constraints and a primary key constraint via ALTER TABLE DDL statements.
* [Using Views as Feature Tables](/concepts/using-views-as-feature-tables.md) - Simple SELECT views over Delta tables in Unity Catalog can serve as feature tables for offline training, but cannot be published to online stores or used for model serving.
* [V1ToV2SqlMigration](/concepts/v1tov2sqlmigration.md) - A Databricks migration utility class that copies trace spans and annotations from schema-linked UC trace tables to table-prefix format using Spark SQL; idempotent and safe to rerun
* [VACUUM after PURGE](/concepts/vacuum-after-purge.md) - After running APPLY (PURGE), old files containing soft-deleted data may still exist and VACUUM should be used to physically delete them.
* [VACUUM and Bloom filter index file cleanup](/concepts/vacuum-and-bloom-filter-index-file-cleanup.md) - Underlying Bloom filter index files are cleaned only when the Delta table is vacuumed after all indexes are dropped
* [VACUUM and File Visibility in Delta Lake](/concepts/vacuum-and-file-visibility-in-delta-lake.md) - The caveat that files not tracked by the Delta Lake transaction log are invisible to Delta Lake and can be deleted by VACUUM, meaning writes during conversion must go through Delta Lake.
* [VACUUM Command (Databricks)](/concepts/vacuum-command-databricks.md) - A Databricks SQL command that removes unused data files from Delta, Iceberg, and other table directories to free storage space.
* [VACUUM DRY RUN](/concepts/vacuum-dry-run.md) - A preview mode for VACUUM that returns up to 1000 files that would be deleted without actually removing them.
* [VACUUM FULL vs VACUUM LITE](/concepts/vacuum-full-vs-vacuum-lite.md) - Two modes of VACUUM operation on Delta tables: FULL scans all files in the table directory, while LITE uses the transaction log to identify deletable files more efficiently.
* [VACUUM Retention Threshold](/concepts/vacuum-retention-threshold.md) - The configurable time window (default 7 days) that determines which data files are safe to delete; files modified or logically removed within this window are retained.
* [VACUUM Safety Check](/concepts/vacuum-safety-check.md) - A Delta Lake safety mechanism that prevents dangerous VACUUM operations with too-short retention intervals, controllable via spark.databricks.delta.retentionDurationCheck.enabled.
* [Vacuumed Data File Errors in Delta Sharing](/concepts/vacuumed-data-file-errors-in-delta-sharing.md) - Querying a shared table fails with 404 'The specified key does not exist' when a pre-signed URL references a data file that has been vacuumed from a historical table version.
* [VALIDATE Clause](/concepts/validate-clause.md) - A validation clause in COPY INTO that checks data can be parsed, schema matches, and constraints are met without writing to the table
* [validate_serving_input](/concepts/validate_serving_input.md) - MLflow API to validate that model input conforms to the JSON format expected by Databricks Model Serving endpoints before deployment.
* [Validation of Judge Alignment](/concepts/validation-of-judge-alignment.md) - A method to quantitatively compare original versus aligned judge performance against human ground truth, measuring accuracy improvement.
* [VARIANT-based masking for multiple column types](/concepts/variant-based-masking-functions.md) - Using a single VARIANT-accepting and VARIANT-returning masking UDF to handle columns of different types (INT, DOUBLE, DECIMAL, DATE, struct) without needing separate policies per type.
* [VARIANT-based masking functions](/concepts/variant-based-masking-functions.md) - Using the VARIANT type to write a single masking UDF that handles multiple target column types (INT, DOUBLE, DECIMAL, DATE, STRUCT) by accepting and returning VARIANT, which Databricks automatically casts to the target column's type.
* [VARIANT-based masking functions for multiple column types](/concepts/variant-based-masking-functions.md) - Using a single VARIANT-accepting UDF to mask columns of different data types (INT, DOUBLE, DECIMAL, DATE, etc.) and even struct columns, reducing the number of UDFs and policies needed.
* [Vector Index for LLM Applications](/concepts/vector-index-for-llm-applications.md) - Using vector indexes and Databricks AI Search for fast similarity searches to provide context and domain knowledge in LLM queries
* [Vended Credentials Access Delegation](/concepts/vended-credentials-access-delegation.md) - An access delegation mode (ACCESS_DELEGATION_MODE = VENDED_CREDENTIALS) used in Snowflake's Iceberg REST Catalog integration to delegate credential management for accessing shared data.
* [VERIFY ALL FILES mode](/concepts/verify-all-files-mode.md) - FSCK REPAIR TABLE mode that checks all files by reading their contents, including data files and deletion vectors
* [VERIFY ALL FILES verification](/concepts/verify-all-files-verification.md) - A comprehensive verification mode for FSCK REPAIR TABLE that checks both metadata and actual data file contents, including data file readability and deletion vector integrity.
* [Version Compatibility for Databricks Connect](/concepts/version-compatibility-for-databricks-connect.md) - Specific version requirements for Databricks Connect including supported Databricks Runtime versions (7.3 LTS to 12.2 LTS), Python minor version matching, and exact package version alignment.
* [Version compatibility for feature table upgrade](/concepts/version-compatibility-for-feature-table-upgrade.md) - Upgrading tags and time series columns is not supported in Databricks Runtime 13.2 ML and below; older client versions (0.1.2 and below) throw errors on comment mismatch instead of skipping.
* [Version Number Synchronization](/concepts/version-number-synchronization.md) - The process of ensuring Unity Catalog model version numbers match the corresponding workspace model version numbers by inserting placeholder versions for gaps
* [Version Pinning with Dot-Asterisk Notation](/concepts/version-pinning-with-dot-asterisk-notation.md) - Databricks recommends using 'X.Y.*' (pip) to pin the major.minor version while allowing the latest patch version of databricks-connect to be installed.
* [VERSION_MISMATCH](/concepts/version_mismatch.md) - Sub-error indicating the managed and external DeltaLog versions do not match, often due to a concurrent UNSET MANAGED command
* [VERSION_MISMATCH error](/concepts/version_mismatch.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED raised when the managed DeltaLog version does not match the expected version, often due to a concurrent migration succeeding first.
* [VERSION_MISMATCH error (Delta managed migration)](/concepts/version_mismatch.md) - Sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_SET_MANAGED_FAILED indicating the managed DeltaLog version doesn't match expectations, typically due to a concurrent migration
* [VERSION_MISMATCH in Delta Logs](/concepts/version_mismatch.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ALTER_TABLE_UNSET_MANAGED_FAILED where the managed DeltaLog and external DeltaLog versions do not match, often due to concurrent rollback operations.
* [VERSION_MISMATCH Sub-Error](/concepts/version_mismatch-sub-error.md) - Sub-error raised when the managed DeltaLog version does not match the expected version, often due to concurrent migration
* [View constraints on table_changes (Databricks)](/concepts/view-constraints-on-table_changes-databricks.md) - Databricks enumerates specific view features that make a view incompatible with the table_changes function: multiple relations, non-deterministic expressions, non-Delta tables, non-shared views, subqueries, and unsupported operators.
* [View determinism requirement for table_changes](/concepts/view-determinism-requirement-for-table_changes.md) - Views used with table_changes must be deterministic; non-deterministic expressions cause a NON_DETERMINISTIC_EXPRESSIONS error.
* [View Restrictions in Delta Change Tracking](/concepts/view-restrictions-in-delta-change-tracking.md) - Constraints that prevent using table_changes on views, including multi-relation references, non-deterministic expressions, subqueries, unsupported operators, and non-Delta/non-shared views
* [View structural constraints for table_changes](/concepts/view-structural-constraints-for-table_changes.md) - Views used with table_changes must reference a single relation, avoid subqueries, and avoid unsupported operators.
* [View-based On-Read PII Redaction](/concepts/view-based-on-read-pii-redaction.md) - Alternative approach that applies ai_mask in a Unity Catalog view to redact PII at query time without duplicating storage.
* [Viewing and Searching MLflow Experiments](/concepts/viewing-and-searching-mlflow-experiments.md) - Experiments are viewed from the Experiments page (sidebar under AI/ML), from the workspace for workspace experiments, or from the notebook sidebar for notebook experiments. Filter experiments by name, location, or by searching tags like tags.mlflow.note.content for descriptions.
* [Viewing Share Grants and Recipients](/concepts/viewing-share-grants-and-recipients.md) - Inspecting which recipients have access to a share or which shares a recipient can access using Catalog Explorer, SQL, or CLI.
* [Views as Feature Tables](/concepts/views-as-feature-tables.md) - Simple SELECT views over a single Delta table can serve as feature tables for offline training/evaluation but cannot be published to online stores or served.
* [Views in Unity Catalog](/concepts/views-in-unity-catalog.md) - Views are read-only objects defined by stored SQL queries, including standard views, materialized views (pre-computed cached results with REFRESH privilege), and metric views (reusable metric definitions).
* [vLLM Engine Integration for Model Serving](/concepts/vllm-engine-integration-for-model-serving.md) - The use of vLLM as the inference engine powering custom LLM deployments on Databricks, including local testing, entrypoint configuration, and OpenAI-compatible API at /invocations.
* [VOID type in Delta Lake](/concepts/void-type-in-delta-lake.md) - An unsupported column type in Delta Lake schemas; when all columns or all struct fields are of VOID type, the table is considered unwritable.
* [VOID Type in Delta Lake Schemas](/concepts/void-type-in-delta-lake.md) - The VOID data type in Delta Lake represents columns or fields that have no valid type, making schemas containing it effectively empty and unwritable.
* [Volume Export for Large Delta Tables](/concepts/volume-export-for-large-delta-tables.md) - Technique to export oversized Delta tables to Parquet files in Unity Catalog volumes for direct loading via PyTorch or Hugging Face datasets, avoiding Spark overhead.
* [Volumes for Unstructured Data](/concepts/volumes-for-unstructured-data.md) - Volumes are securable objects in Unity Catalog for unstructured data in cloud storage, supporting file-level read/write access without SQL query operations, available as managed or external volumes.
* [VPC Gateway Endpoints for In-Region Access](/concepts/vpc-gateway-endpoints-for-in-region-access.md) - Best practice recommendation for recipients to use VPC gateway endpoints or interface endpoints for S3 instead of NAT gateways when accessing shared data in-region to reduce costs and enhance security.
* [Webhook Lifecycle Management](/concepts/webhook-lifecycle-management.md) - Management operations for webhooks: create (in TEST_MODE or ACTIVE), test with mock events, update (status and properties), list, and delete via REST API or Python client.
* [Webhook Payload Structure](/concepts/webhook-payload-structure.md) - The JSON payload sent to webhook endpoints, which includes event type, webhook ID, timestamp, model name, version, and event-specific fields. Sensitive info like artifact paths are excluded.
* [Webhook Security and Verification](/concepts/webhook-security-and-verification.md) - Security measures including HMAC-SHA256 signature (X-Databricks-Signature header), optional Authorization header, and client-side verification to validate webhook origin.
* [Weighted Edges](/concepts/weighted-edges.md) - Edges in a graph can carry weights representing properties such as capacity, distance, or strength of a relationship, enabling richer analysis like shortest-path with costs.
* [WHEN MATCHED Clause](/concepts/when-matched-clause.md) - Specifies actions (DELETE or UPDATE) to perform when a source row matches a target table row based on the merge condition, with optional conditional evaluation and ordering of multiple clauses.
* [WHEN NOT MATCHED (INSERT) Clause](/concepts/when-not-matched-insert-clause.md) - Inserts a new row into the target Delta table when a source row does not match any target row, supporting INSERT * (all columns), INSERT with specific columns, and optional EXCEPT clause.
* [WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE Clause](/concepts/when-not-matched-by-source-clause.md) - A merge clause that acts on target rows that have no match in the source table, supporting DELETE and UPDATE actions (Databricks Runtime 12.2 LTS+), with conditions limited to target table columns.
* [When to Avoid Delta Lake Conversion](/concepts/when-to-avoid-delta-lake-conversion.md) - Scenarios where maintaining Parquet format may be preferable, such as upstream/downstream system incompatibility with Delta Lake.
* [When to use DDP](/concepts/when-to-use-ddp.md) - Conditions for using DDP: model fits in single GPU memory, scaling via data throughput, and need for simplest distributed training approach.
* [When to use DDP vs FSDP/DeepSpeed](/concepts/when-to-use-ddp-vs-fsdpdeepspeed.md) - Decision criteria for choosing DDP over alternative distributed training techniques like FSDP or DeepSpeed based on model size and memory constraints.
* [When to use FSDP](/concepts/when-to-use-fsdp.md) - Decision criteria for adopting FSDP: when models are too large for single GPU memory, when training models in the 20B to 120B+ parameter range, and when more memory efficiency than DDP is needed.
* [When to use FSDP vs DDP vs DeepSpeed](/concepts/when-to-use-ddp-vs-fsdpdeepspeed.md) - Decision framework for choosing between FSDP, DDP, and DeepSpeed based on model size and memory efficiency needs
