* [When to Use Third-Party vs Built-in Scorers](/concepts/when-to-use-third-party-vs-built-in-scorers.md) - Decision framework for choosing between built-in MLflow LLM judges and third-party scorers based on evaluation needs, domain specificity, and cost considerations
* [Whole Table Operation Conflicts in Delta Lake](/concepts/whole-table-conflicts-in-delta-lake.md) - Conflicts arising when a transaction reads or replaces the entire table while another concurrent operation modifies it, including sub-types WHOLE_TABLE_READ and WHOLE_TABLE_REPLACE.
* [Whole Table Read vs Filtered Read in Delta Lake](/concepts/whole-table-read-vs-filtered-read-in-delta-lake.md) - A pattern where reading an entire Delta table (without filters) increases the likelihood of transaction conflicts with concurrent delete operations, mitigated by adding filter predicates.
* [Whole Table Read/Replace Conflicts](/concepts/whole-table-readreplace-conflicts.md) - Conflict scenarios where a transaction reads or replaces the entire table while a concurrent modification occurs, with recommendations to narrow data scope or retry.
* [Whole Table Scan Conflicts in Delta Lake](/concepts/whole-table-scan-conflicts-in-delta-lake.md) - Transaction conflicts arising when a Delta Lake operation reads or replaces an entire table concurrently with other modifications, resolvable by narrowing query scope with filters or retrying.
* [Whole-Table Conflicts in Delta Lake](/concepts/whole-table-conflicts-in-delta-lake.md) - Transaction conflicts that arise when an operation attempts to read or replace an entire table while a concurrent modification is in progress.
* [Whole-Table Modification Conflicts in Delta Lake](/concepts/whole-table-conflicts-in-delta-lake.md) - A class of transaction conflicts in Delta Lake where either the current or concurrent transaction attempts to operate on the entire table without filters, causing a conflict.
* [Whole-Table Operation Pattern Conflicts](/concepts/whole-table-operation-pattern-conflicts.md) - A class of Delta Lake conflicts that arise when a transaction attempts to modify or read the entire table (without filters), conflicting with concurrent operations; resolution requires adding filters to narrow the data scope.
* [Whole-Table Read vs Filtered Read Conflict Patterns](/concepts/whole-table-versus-filtered-read-conflicts.md) - A class of Delta Lake transaction conflicts arising when a transaction reads an entire table (without filters) concurrently with a delete operation, resolvable by adding query filters to narrow the data scope.
* [Whole-table versus filtered read conflicts](/concepts/whole-table-versus-filtered-read-conflicts.md) - A class of Delta Lake concurrency conflicts where reading an entire table (without filters) collides with concurrent delete operations, with solutions involving narrowing the read scope via predicates or partition hints.
* [Whole-Table vs Filtered Delta Lake Operations](/concepts/whole-table-vs-filtered-delta-lake-operations.md) - Conflict patterns arising when one transaction modifies an entire table (no filters, full replace) while another attempts concurrent modifications, requiring narrowing data scope via filters.
* [Windows Path Space Issue in Databricks Connect](/concepts/windows-path-space-issue-in-databricks-connect.md) - On Windows, Databricks Connect fails with a filename syntax error if installed into a directory path containing spaces; workarounds include using a space-free path or the short name form.
* [WITH HISTORY clause for Databricks deep clone](/concepts/with-history-clause-for-deep-clone.md) - When deep cloning a streaming table in Databricks, the WITH HISTORY clause is required; a DEEP CLONE without it will fail with REQUIRES_WITH_HISTORY.
* [WITH HISTORY Clause for Deep Clone](/concepts/with-history-clause-for-deep-clone.md) - A required clause when performing a deep clone of a streaming table in Databricks to preserve the table's history
* [With History Requirement for Deep Clone of Streaming Tables](/concepts/with-history-requirement-for-deep-clone-of-streaming-tables.md) - The WITH HISTORY clause is required when performing a DEEP CLONE on a streaming table
* [WITH SCHEMA EVOLUTION for MERGE](/concepts/with-schema-evolution-for-merge.md) - A modifier that automatically updates the target Delta table schema to match the source table schema during a MERGE operation (Databricks Runtime 15.2+).
* [withEventTimeOrder for Initial Snapshot Processing](/concepts/witheventtimeorder-for-initial-snapshot-processing.md) - Feature to avoid data drops during initial snapshot processing by ordering data by event time instead of file modification time, using time bucket filtering.
* [withEventTimeOrder for Initial Snapshots](/concepts/witheventtimeorder-for-initial-snapshots.md) - An option to preserve event-time ordering during initial snapshot processing in stateful streaming queries on Delta tables
* [Workflow Architecture Patterns for Ray and Spark](/concepts/workflow-architecture-patterns-for-ray-and-spark.md) - Recommended patterns for integrating Ray and Spark pipelines include isolating ETL in a subtask, using Spark for data handling with Ray for computation, running Ray inside Spark functions (UDFs/foreachBatch), and concurrent Spark/Ray operations.
* [Workload Discovery for Deprecated Models](/concepts/workload-discovery-for-deprecated-models.md) - A SQL-based method to identify workloads, endpoints, and owners still using deprecated or retired models by querying Databricks system tables.
* [Workload YAML Configuration](/concepts/workload-yaml-configuration.md) - The YAML schema accepted by `air run --file` to define GPU training jobs, with fields for experiment name, environment, compute, code source, command, parameters, secrets, and reliability settings.
* [Workload YAML configuration (train.yaml)](/concepts/workload-yaml-configuration-trainyaml.md) - Pattern for defining distributed training workloads declaratively using a train.yaml file submitted via `air run -f train.yaml`.
* [Workload YAML configuration for distributed training](/concepts/workload-yaml-configuration-for-distributed-training.md) - YAML schema defining experiment name, environment dependencies, compute resources, code source, and inline command for launching distributed ML workloads on Databricks.
* [Workload YAML Configuration for Ray on Databricks](/concepts/workload-yaml-configuration-for-ray-on-databricks.md) - YAML configuration format for the Databricks AI Runtime CLI that defines compute resources, dependencies, code sources, and commands for distributed Ray training workloads.
* [Workload YAML for Distributed Training](/concepts/workload-yaml-for-distributed-training.md) - Declarative YAML configuration files (e.g., train.yaml) used with the air CLI to define and submit end-to-end distributed training workloads on Databricks
* [Workload YAML pattern for distributed training](/concepts/workload-yaml-pattern-for-distributed-training.md) - Configuration-driven approach where distributed training workloads are defined in YAML files (e.g., train.yaml) and submitted via `air run -f`, separating launcher scripts from training code.
* [Workspace Admin (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/workspace-admin-unity-catalog.md) - An admin role operating within a single Databricks workspace, managing workspace membership, jobs, and workspace objects, with default metastore privileges on auto-enabled workspaces.
* [Workspace Admin Privileges in Feature Store](/concepts/workspace-admin-privileges-in-feature-store.md) - Workspace administrators automatically have CAN MANAGE permission on all feature tables and can set permissions on the entire Feature Store
* [Workspace Admin Privileges in Unity Catalog](/concepts/workspace-admin-privileges-in-unity-catalog.md) - How workspace admin roles differ depending on whether Unity Catalog was auto-enabled or manually assigned, including the ability to create objects and grant access.
* [Workspace Admin Role (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/workspace-admin-unity-catalog.md) - A highly privileged role operating within a single Databricks workspace, managing workspace membership, jobs, and workspace objects.
* [Workspace admins (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/workspace-admin-unity-catalog.md) - Admin role operating within a single workspace, managing workspace membership, jobs, and workspace objects, with default privileges on automatically-provisioned metastores.
* [Workspace Admins in Unity Catalog](/concepts/workspace-admin-unity-catalog.md) - Workspace-scoped admin role managing workspace membership, jobs, and objects, with additional default privileges on the attached metastore when Unity Catalog is auto-enabled.
* [Workspace Built-in Hive Metastore](/concepts/built-in-hive-metastore.md) - A managed Hive metastore service that deploys per-Databricks-workspace and serves as a central metadata repository for all clusters in that workspace.
* [Workspace Catalog (Unity Catalog)](/concepts/workspace-catalog-unity-catalog.md) - A catalog provisioned for a workspace during automatic Unity Catalog enablement, where workspace admins are default owners and all workspace users receive USE CATALOG privilege.
* [Workspace Catalog Default Ownership and Privileges](/concepts/workspace-catalog-default-privileges.md) - When a workspace is auto-enabled for Unity Catalog, a workspace catalog is provisioned with workspace admins as default owners, granting them privilege management and ownership transfer capabilities over objects in that catalog.
* [Workspace Catalog Default Privileges](/concepts/workspace-catalog-default-privileges.md) - When a workspace is automatically enabled for Unity Catalog, a workspace catalog is created where workspace admins are default owners and all workspace users get USE CATALOG on the catalog plus several privileges on the default schema.
* [Workspace Catalog in Unity Catalog](/concepts/workspace-catalog-in-unity-catalog.md) - An automatically created catalog that is provisioned for each new workspace when metastore auto-assignment is enabled, giving all workspace users privileges to create objects within it.
* [Workspace Catalog Ownership](/concepts/workspace-catalog-ownership.md) - Workspace admins are default owners of the workspace catalog when one is provisioned, granting them the ability to manage privileges and transfer ownership within that catalog.
* [Workspace Default Serverless Budget Policy](/concepts/workspace-default-serverless-budget-policy.md) - The fallback budget policy applied to MLflow serverless workloads when no specific policy is assigned to an experiment; if disabled, workloads fail with a 403 error.
* [Workspace default storage path](/concepts/workspace-default-storage-path.md) - The default storage path configured for a Databricks workspace during deployment using a storage configuration object, used for internal Unity Catalog processing.
* [Workspace Deletion and Catalog Retention](/concepts/workspace-deletion-and-catalog-retention.md) - Deleting a Databricks workspace does not automatically delete its default Unity Catalog catalog; the catalog must be dropped manually from another workspace assigned to the same metastore.
* [Workspace Feature Store (Deprecated)](/concepts/workspace-feature-store-deprecated.md) - Legacy version of Databricks Feature Store for workspaces not enabled for Unity Catalog, now deprecated in favor of the Unity Catalog-backed Feature Store.
* [Workspace Feature Store UI](/concepts/workspace-feature-store-ui.md) - Databricks UI for managing, searching, and governing feature tables with lineage tracking and access control.
* [Workspace Model Registry](/concepts/workspace-model-registry.md) - A Databricks-hosted, legacy version of the MLflow Model Registry for managing the full lifecycle of ML models, providing model lineage, versioning, stage transitions, webhooks, and email notifications.
* [Workspace Model Registry Quota Limits](/concepts/workspace-model-registry-quota-limits.md) - Quota limits imposed on the total number of registered models and model versions per workspace in the Workspace Model Registry, effective May 2024, with recommendations for cleanup and retention strategies.
* [Workspace Model Registry Webhooks](/concepts/workspace-model-registry-webhooks.md) - Event-driven notifications that trigger actions (HTTP calls or job runs) when MLflow Model Registry events occur in a Databricks workspace.
* [Workspace-based and Git-based Code Workflows](/concepts/workspace-based-and-git-based-code-workflows.md) - Two code workflow modes supported by the AI Runtime CLI: sourcing code from Databricks workspaces or from git repositories.
* [Workspace-based code workflows](/concepts/workspace-based-code-workflows.md) - Approach for submitting training workloads using code stored in the Databricks workspace.
* [Workspace-based vs git-based code workflows](/concepts/workspace-based-vs-git-based-code-workflows.md) - AI Runtime CLI supports both Databricks workspace-based code and git-based code workflows for submitting training jobs.
* [Workspace-Catalog Binding](/concepts/workspace-catalog-binding.md) - Workspace-level restrictions that limit which workspaces can access specific catalogs, external locations, and storage credentials.
* [Workspace-Level Restrictions](/concepts/workspace-level-restrictions.md) - Control over where users can access data by binding securable objects like catalogs and external locations to specific workspaces.
* [Workspace-Metastore Assignment](/concepts/workspace-metastore-assignment.md) - The process of enabling a Databricks workspace for Unity Catalog by assigning it to a Unity Catalog metastore, either during workspace creation or later via the account console.
* [Workspace-Metastore Attachment](/concepts/workspace-metastore-linking.md) - The process of linking a Unity Catalog metastore to Databricks workspaces in the same region, giving all linked workspaces a unified view of metastore data.
* [Workspace-Metastore Linking](/concepts/workspace-metastore-linking.md) - The process of attaching a Databricks workspace to a Unity Catalog metastore, allowing multiple workspaces in the same region to share the same view of data with unified access control.
* [WorkspaceClient (Databricks SDK for Python)](/concepts/workspaceclient-databricks-sdk-for-python.md) - The class used to access Databricks Utilities (dbutils) from external Python environments via the Databricks SDK.
* [WorkspaceClient and dbutils](/concepts/workspaceclient-dbutils.md) - The WorkspaceClient class from the Databricks SDK for Python exposes a 'dbutils' variable that provides access to Databricks Utilities (fs and secrets) in Databricks Connect.
* [WorkspaceClient Authentication](/concepts/workspaceclient-authentication.md) - Methods for initializing the Databricks SDK WorkspaceClient including hard-coded credentials, configuration profiles, and environment variables
* [WorkspaceClient Authentication Methods](/concepts/workspaceclient-authentication.md) - Three supported authentication approaches for initializing the Databricks SDK WorkspaceClient: hard-coded credentials, configuration profiles, and environment variables (DATABRICKS_HOST and DATABRICKS_TOKEN).
* [WorkspaceClient dbutils](/concepts/workspaceclient-dbutils.md) - The WorkspaceClient class from the Databricks SDK for Python exposes a dbutils variable that provides access to limited Databricks Utilities through Databricks Connect
* [Wrapping built-in LLM judges in custom scorers](/concepts/wrapping-built-in-llm-judges-in-custom-scorers.md) - Technique of wrapping MLflow's predefined LLM judges (like is_context_relevant) inside a custom code-based scorer to preprocess trace data or post-process judge feedback before returning results.
* [Wrapping predefined LLM judges in custom scorers](/concepts/wrapping-built-in-llm-judges-in-custom-scorers.md) - The pattern of wrapping MLflow's built-in LLM judge functions inside custom code-based scorers to preprocess trace data or post-process judge feedback
* [Write Authentication for Feature Publishing](/concepts/write-authentication-for-feature-publishing.md) - Authentication required when publishing feature tables from the Databricks Feature Store to third-party online stores, supporting instance profiles or Databricks secrets with a write_secret_prefix.
* [Write Authentication for Publishing Feature Tables](/concepts/write-authentication-for-publishing-feature-tables.md) - Required write credentials (via instance profile or secrets with write_secret_prefix) for publishing feature tables to third-party online stores from Databricks Feature Store.
* [Write Authentication for Publishing Feature Tables to Third-Party Online Stores](/concepts/write-authentication-for-publishing-feature-tables-to-third-party-online-stores.md) - Methods and requirements for authenticating write operations when publishing Databricks feature tables to external online stores like DynamoDB or SQL databases.
* [Writing Ray Data to Unity Catalog](/concepts/writing-ray-data-to-unity-catalog.md) - Writing Ray datasets back to Unity Catalog tables using ray.data.Dataset.write_databricks_table(), which stages data in Unity Catalog Volumes before writing with Spark.
* [Writing Ray Data to Unity Catalog Tables](/concepts/writing-ray-data-to-unity-catalog.md) - The ray.data.Dataset.write_databricks_table function for writing Ray datasets to Unity Catalog tables via temporary storage in Unity Catalog Volumes.
* [WRONG_REQUIRED_TABLE_PROPERTY](/concepts/wrong_required_table_property.md) - Error sub-type indicating a required table property for IcebergCompatV1 is set to an incorrect value
* [WRONG_REQUIRED_TABLE_PROPERTY (Delta Iceberg error)](/concepts/wrong_required_table_property.md) - A sub-error of DELTA_ICEBERG_COMPAT_V1_VIOLATION that occurs when a required table property for IcebergCompatV1 is set to an incorrect value
* [XGBoost classification and regression on Databricks](/concepts/xgboost-classification-and-regression-on-databricks.md) - Using XGBoost for both classification (with ML pipeline) and regression (with cross-validation) tasks in Scala notebooks on Databricks.
* [XGBoost Classification on Databricks](/concepts/xgboost-classification-on-databricks.md) - Example notebook showing XGBoost classification embedded in an MLlib ML pipeline on Databricks
* [XGBoost Classification with ML Pipeline Notebook](/concepts/xgboost-classification-with-ml-pipeline-notebook.md) - An example Databricks notebook demonstrating XGBoost classification embedded in an MLlib ML pipeline
* [XGBoost hyperparameter tuning with MLlib cross-validation](/concepts/xgboost-hyperparameter-tuning-with-mllib-cross-validation.md) - Using MLlib's CrossValidator to tune XGBoost model hyperparameters (e.g., n_estimators, max_depth) in a distributed Scala environment.
* [XGBoost integration with MLlib ML pipelines](/concepts/xgboost-integration-with-mllib-ml-pipelines.md) - Embedding an XGBoost model as a stage within an MLlib Pipeline for end-to-end machine learning workflows on Databricks.
* [XGBoost on Databricks Runtime ML](/concepts/xgboost-on-databricks-runtime-ml.md) - Using XGBoost within Databricks Runtime ML which includes pre-installed ML libraries and optimizations
* [XGBoost Parameter Mapping for PySpark](/concepts/xgboost-parameter-mapping-for-pyspark.md) - Parameter differences between the standard xgboost package and sparkdl.xgboost, including unsupported parameters and alternative column-based parameters like weightCol, validationIndicatorCol, and baseMarginCol.
* [XGBoost Parameter Mapping: sparkdl vs Native xgboost](/concepts/xgboost-parameter-mapping-sparkdl-vs-native-xgboost.md) - The mapping and semantic differences between sparkdl.xgboost parameters and native Python xgboost package parameters, including missing, weightCol, baseMarginCol, and unsupported parameters like gpu_id and output_margin.
* [XGBoost Regression with Cross-Validation](/concepts/xgboost-regression-with-cross-validation.md) - Example notebook demonstrating XGBoost regression with MLlib cross-validation on Databricks
* [XGBoost Regression with Cross-Validation Notebook](/concepts/xgboost-regression-with-cross-validation.md) - An example Databricks notebook demonstrating XGBoost regression tuned via MLlib cross-validation
* [XGBoost with MLlib Integration](/concepts/xgboost-with-mllib-integration.md) - Using XGBoost models within Apache Spark MLlib pipelines for distributed training on Databricks
* [XGBoost with MLlib ML Pipeline](/concepts/xgboost-with-mllib-integration.md) - Embedding XGBoost models into Apache Spark MLlib ML pipelines for distributed training
* [XGBoost with MLlib Pipeline API](/concepts/xgboost-with-mllib-pipeline-api.md) - Embedding XGBoost models as stages within MLlib ML pipelines for structured workflows
* [xgboost.spark Module](/concepts/xgboostspark-module.md) - PySpark estimators (SparkXGBRegressor, SparkXGBClassifier, SparkXGBRanker) that support inclusion of XGBoost in SparkML Pipelines, available in xgboost>=1.7.
* [xgboost.spark Module (PySpark XGBoost Estimators)](/concepts/xgboostspark-module.md) - A Python module in xgboost>=1.7 providing SparkXGBRegressor, SparkXGBClassifier, and SparkXGBRanker for integrating XGBoost into SparkML Pipelines
* [xgboost.spark Module API](/concepts/xgboostspark-module.md) - New PySpark estimators (SparkXGBRegressor, SparkXGBClassifier, SparkXGBRanker) that integrate XGBoost into SparkML Pipelines
* [YAML Job Configuration for AI Runtime](/concepts/yaml-job-configuration-for-ai-runtime.md) - The declarative YAML-based job configuration format used by the AI Runtime CLI to define training jobs that can be checked into source control.
* [YAML-based job configuration](/concepts/yaml-based-job-configuration.md) - Declarative approach to defining training jobs using YAML configuration files that can be checked into source control.
* [YAML-based job configuration for AI Runtime](/concepts/yaml-based-job-configuration-for-ai-runtime.md) - Declarative YAML configuration format used by the AI Runtime CLI to define training jobs, enabling source-control integration.
* [YAML-based job configuration for training](/concepts/yaml-based-job-configuration-for-training.md) - Pattern of defining distributed training jobs declaratively in YAML files so they can be checked into source control, used by the AI Runtime CLI.
* [YAML-based LLM Fine-tuning Configuration](/concepts/yaml-based-llm-fine-tuning-configuration.md) - A structured YAML configuration pattern used by LLM Foundry to specify model architecture, optimizer, scheduler, FSDP settings, dataset loading, callbacks, and logging parameters for LLM fine-tuning.
* [Z-Ordering (Delta Lake)](/concepts/z-ordering-delta-lake.md) - A non-idempotent, incremental clustering technique that co-locates related data by number of tuples, improving data skipping performance.
* [ZeRO (Zero Redundancy Optimizer)](/concepts/zero-zero-redundancy-optimizer.md) - A memory optimization technique in DeepSpeed that shards optimizer states, gradients, and parameters across devices to enable training of large models
* [ZeRO Stages (Stage 1, 2, 3)](/concepts/zero-stages-stage-1-2-3.md) - Three stages of optimizer state sharding in DeepSpeed's ZeRO, providing increasing levels of memory savings by partitioning optimizer states, gradients, and model parameters respectively.
* [Zero-Downtime Endpoint Updates](/concepts/zero-downtime-endpoint-updates.md) - Databricks performs zero-downtime updates by keeping the existing endpoint configuration active until the new one is ready, with billing for both configurations during transition.
* [Zero-downtime endpoint updates and maintenance](/concepts/zero-downtime-endpoint-updates.md) - Databricks keeps existing endpoints serving while new configurations deploy, then performs zero-downtime transitions; during maintenance models are reloaded, and failures cause rollback ensuring no service interruption.
